PN CN115093237-A
TI Preparation of compact chromium oxide product with            high thermal shock resistance by spraying and            granulating to obtain granules, using isostatic            pressing to press granules into green body, and firing            prepared blank into product.
AU QIN J
   LI S
   WU G
   LI J
   ZHANG T
   WANG H
   FENG Z
   ZHAO Z
   FAN M
AE SINOSTEEL LUOYANG INST REFRACTORIES RES (SIST-C)
GA 2022C4615D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing compact chromium oxide product with                high thermal shock resistance comprises using                carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide mixed                gas to control oxygen partial pressure at                10-6-10-8atm, introducing carbon dioxide /carbon                dioxide/ carbon monoxide mixed gas into an electric                kiln filled with chromium oxides blank, controlling                the temperature of the oxygen in the temperature                range, growing and homogenizing the crystalline                grain of the chromium dioxide grain, reducing the                driving force of the sinter, making the sintered                machine to evaporate-agglomerate as the main body                to promote the neck development of the crystallic                grain of chromium nitride.
   USE - Preparation method of compact chromium oxide                product used for high erosion part of glass melt                contact e.g. melting pool and pool wall of                clarification tank, pool bottom paving brick,                liquid hole brick, wall brick of main passage and                working passage and bottom brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides compact chromium oxide                product with high thermal shock resistance, and has                no obvious crack after air cooling at                950&#8451; for 2 times. The method is simple                and easy to control and control different sintering                stage, exerting the function of regulating                microstructure.

PN CN114792813-A
TI Preparing carbon-free self-supporting anode film            material for lithium-carbon dioxide battery comprises            e.g. adding ammonium molybdate and sodium molybdate or            mixture of two substances, heating, nitriding, cooling,            then passing inert gas and passivating the            material.
AU QI G
   WANG B
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 2022A64386
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-free self-supporting anode                film material for a lithium-carbon dioxide battery                comprises adding ammonium molybdate and sodium                molybdate or mixture of two substances into 35-50                ml deionized water as molybdenum source, then                slowly adding concentrated nitric acid or                concentrated hydrochloric acid at a volume ratio of                5:(1-2) of deionized water and concentrated nitric                acid, and stirring for 10-20 minutes until the                solid is completely dissolved to obtain mixed                solution A, preserving heat at 180-200 &#8451;                for 10-24 hours for hydrothermal growth, and                naturally cooled to room temperature, taking out                molybdenum trioxide film and cutting into pole                pieces, calcining and passing pure ammonia gas,                heating to 500-650&#176;C, incubating for                nitriding, cooling down to room temperature with                the furnace, then passing inert gas and passivating                the material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon-free                self-supporting anode film material for                lithium-carbon dioxide battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent electrochemical                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon-free self-supporting anode film material for                lithium-carbon dioxide battery.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN CN114478510-A; CN114478510-B
TI Preparing nifuratel comprises e.g. reacting            glycidyl methyl sulfide with carbon dioxide, then            reacting with tert-butyl carbazate, and carrying out            condensation reaction with 5-nitrofurfural.
AU YANG Y
   HUANG W
   MA X
AE YANCHENG KAILI PHARMACEUTCAL CO LTD (YANC-Non-standard)
GA 202271114B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nifuratel (I) comprises (1) taking                glycidyl methyl sulfide (II) as starting material,                carrying out synthesis reaction with carbon dioxide                to obtain 4-(methylthio)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one (III),                (2) reacting (III) and tert-butyl carbazate (IV) to                obtain N-(tert-butyl                formate-amino)-5-methylthiomethyl-2-oxazolidinone                (V), and (3) carrying out condensation reaction of                (V) and 5-nitrofurfural (VI) to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nifuratel                (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces nifuratel with high yield                and purity, is environmentally friendly and                pollution-free, simultaneously solves the problem                of carbon dioxide waste gas pollution, and solves                the deficiencies in the existing nifuratel                synthesis process, e.g. environmental pollution,                safety hazards, waste of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nifuratel of formula (I) comprises                (1) taking glycidyl methyl sulfide of formula (II)                as starting material, carrying out synthesis                reaction with carbon dioxide to obtain                4-(methylthio)-1,3-dioxolan-2-one of formula (III),                (2) reacting (III) and tert-butyl carbazate of                formula (IV) to obtain N-(tert-butyl                formate-amino)-5-methylthiomethyl-2-oxazolidinone                of formula (V), and (3) carrying out condensation                reaction of (V) and 5-nitrofurfural of formula (VI)                to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (1)                  taking (II) as the starting material, adding a                  supported hydroxy ionic liquid catalyst, sealing,                  then continuously filling with carbon dioxide                  gas, and maintaining it under a certain pressure,                  microwave heating for the addition reaction, and                  placing it on ice after completing the reaction,                  cooling to 6degrees Celsius in the bath,                  partially solidifying the liquid, releasing                  excess carbon dioxide, and removing the liquid to                  obtain (III); (2) adding absolute ethanol into                  (III) obtained in step (1) to dissolve, stirring,                  first adding indium trichloride catalyst, then                  adding (IV), stirring and microwave heating and                  refluxing to carry out addition reaction,                  completing the reaction, placing it in an ice                  bath and cooling to 15degrees Celsius to obtain                  (V); (3) adding indium trichloride catalyst to                  step (2), then adding (VI), stirring, and heating                  at low temperature with microwave under the                  condition of avoiding light, precipitating a                  yellow solid, completing the reaction, filtering                  to obtain (I); (4) adding the (VI) obtained in                  (3) into the hot glacial acetic acid, heating to                  reflux for 40 minutes, then cooling to                  20-25degrees Celsius, then adding 20-25degrees                  Celsius pure water, stirring and mixing, then                  allowing to stand for 2-3 hours, filtering,                  washing with 75% ethanol and anhydrous ethanol                  successively and drain to obtain pure product of                  (I). In step (1), the pressure is 1.5-1.8 MPa,                  the microwave power is 800-1000 W, the heating                  temperature is 90-95degrees Celsius, and the                  reaction time is 3-3.5 hours. In step (S2), the                  microwave power is 450-600 W, and the reaction                  time is carried out for 2-2.5 hours. In step                  (S3), the microwave is 100-150W, the heating                  temperature is 20-27degrees Celsius, and the                  reaction time is 1.2-1.5 hour.Preferred Components: In step (1), the molar                  ratio of the (II) to the supported hydroxy ionic                  liquid catalyst is 1:0.013-0.016; the supported                  hydroxy ionic liquid is                  1-hydroxyethyl-1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene                  bromide, 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)-3-methylimidazolium                  bromide or 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine bromide.                  In step (2), the molar ratio of (III) to the                  indium trichloride catalyst is 1:0.007-0.011; the                  molar ratio of the (III) to the tert-butyl                  carbazate is 1:1.03-1.08. In step (3), the                  supplemented indium trichloride catalyst is 1.5                  times that of the Lewis acid catalyst in step                  (2); the molar ratio of the 5-nitrofurfural to                  methylthiomethyl ethylene carbonate is                  1.02-1.06:1.
DC B03 (Other heterocyclics.)
MC B07-A02A; B07-E02; B14-Y; N03-G; N04; N07-K
IP C07D-317/36; C07D-413/12

TI Service-oriented architecture service based intelligent control sky window for automobile, has sunroof opening and closing calling control module for calling vehicle according to external instructions, and sunroof opening and closing function module for driving sky window to open or close.
AU ZHANG H
AE IAT AUTOMOBILE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (IATA-C)
GA 2022595563
AB    NOVELTY - The window has a vehicle body domain controller provided with a sunroof opening and closing calling control module. The sunroof opening and closing calling control module calls a vehicle according to external instructions. An interior temperature monitoring function module, an interior humidity monitoring function module and an interior carbon dioxide gas concentration monitoring function module are used for obtaining interior temperature information, interior humidity information, and interior carbon dioxide concentration information, where the interior humidity information and the interior carbon dioxide concentration information are used for calling the sunroof opening and closing calling control module. The sunroof opening and closing function module drives a sky window to open or close.
   USE - SOA service based intelligent control sky window for an automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The window automatically opens or closes the sky window to temperature in the vehicle, humidity, and carbon dioxide concentration to convenient for the driver.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a service-oriented architecture (SOA) service based intelligent control sky window control method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a SOA service based intelligent control sky window control method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN WO2022051839-A1
TI Carbonation pod for depositing carbon dioxide generating material into beverage carbonator, has granular carbonate material that reacts with granular acid material when mixed together in water to generate carbon dioxide gas.
AU HATHERELL D
AE BONNE O INC (BONN-Non-standard)
GA 202241448Y
AB    NOVELTY - The carbonation pod has a dissolvable pod shell that is flexible and rapidly water-dissolvable. The dissolvable pod shell has a first compartment and a second compartment. The first compartment is sealed from the second compartment. A water dissolvable granular carbonate material is sealed within the first compartment. A water dissolvable granular acid material is sealed within the second compartment. The granular carbonate material is reactive with the granular acid material when mixed together in water to generate carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Carbonation pod for depositing carbon dioxide generating material into carbonation chamber of beverage carbonator used in carbonating beverage such as soda and sparkling water.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonation pod has a dissolvable pod shell that is flexible and rapidly water-dissolvable, a granular carbonate material and granular acid material. The first compartment is sealed from the second compartment to enhance the convenience of the consumer, and reduce the manufacturing cost of the beverage carbonator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a beverage carbonation system;
   (2) a beverage carbonating method; and
   (3) a method of carbonating a beverage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the perspective view of a beverage carbonator.

PN CN113637998-A
TI Preparation of organic molecule-modified copper oxide material used in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves adding potassium bicarbonate solution of polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride to electrolytic cell, and performing electrochemical cyclic voltammetric electrolysis.
AU LING B
   LIU Z
   LIU S
   HUANG J
   CHEN J
   HUANG Z
   LIANG J
   LU W
   JIANG S
   LIU W
   FENG G
   WANG G
   WANG H
   ZHOU H
   LIN H
   CHEN M
AE ZHONGSHAN POWER SUPPLY BUREAU GUANDONG (CSPG-C)
   GUANGDONG POWER GRID CO LTD (CSPG-C)
GA 2021D7791U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of organic molecule-modified copper oxide material involves (1) mixing an ethanol solution of dimethylformamide with a copper nitrate solution, performing a hydrothermal reaction, drying to obtain copper oxide, (2) dissolving copper oxide in water and ethanol solution, dripping on the gas diffusion layer to obtain a gas diffusion layer loaded with copper oxide, and (3) adding the potassium bicarbonate solution of polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride to the electrolytic cell, clamping the gas diffusion layer loaded with copper oxide on the electrode as a working electrode, and performing electrochemical cyclic voltammetric electrolysis in a three-electrode system.
   USE - Preparation of organic molecule-modified copper oxide material used in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare formic acid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures preparation of a copper oxide material with good selectivity and low over-potential. The Faraday efficiency of the formic acid obtained by reducing carbon dioxide by the organic molecular modified copper oxide material can reach 71%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   1. usage of the organic molecule-modified copper oxide material in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide; and
   2. preparation method of formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride to the copper oxide is (2-3):1. The concentration of the copper oxide water and ethanol solution is 1-2 mg/mL. The loading amount of the copper oxide on the gas diffusion layer is 0.8-1.2 mg/cm2. The concentration of the polydiallyl dipotassium ammonium chloride in the potassium bicarbonate solution of polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride is 18-22 mmol/L.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); ; J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Hardpack frozen dessert comprises mixture comprising base component, and solute component comprising sweetener and carbon dioxide distributed within mixture.
AU DUNN M L
   STEELE F M
AE UNIV BRIGHAM YOUNG (UYYO-C)
   UNIV BRIGHAM YOUNG (UYYO-C)
   UNIV BRIGHAM YOUNG (UYYO-C)
GA 202013955J
AB    NOVELTY - Hardpack frozen dessert comprises (A) a mixture comprising (i) a base component, and (ii) a solute component comprising at least one sweetener, where the solute component is sufficient to provide at least one of (a) a solute molarity of the mixture of at least 1 molar, (b) a solute molality of the mixture of at least 0.78 molar, and (c) an Aw of the mixture of 0.91 or less, and (B) carbon dioxide distributed within the mixture, where the dessert provides a carbonation sensation upon eating.
   USE - As hardpack frozen dessert i.e. ice cream, ice milk, gelato, frozen yogurt, or frozen custard, sorbet or fruit-based frozen dessert.
   ADVANTAGE - The hardpack frozen dessert retains the carbonation sensation over a shelf life at -15 degrees C for at least 2 months.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing hardpack frozen dessert, which involves (a) providing a mixture, comprising (i) a base component, and (ii) a solute component, (b) applying carbon dioxide to the mixture, such that the carbon dioxide is distributed within the mixture to produce a mass of carbonated mixture, and (c) chilling the carbonated mixture at a temperature of -15 degrees C or less for sufficient time to achieve a temperature of -15 degrees C or less throughout the mass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The sweetener is included in an amount that does not exceed 40 g sucrose equivalent per 100 g mixture. The sweetener is included in an amount that provides 10-20 g sucrose equivalent per 100 g mixture. The sweetener comprises a sugar alcohol. The sugar alcohol comprises glycerol, erythritol, xylitol, mannitol, sorbitol, maltitol, lactitol, or their combinations. The sweetener comprises allulose. The base component comprises a milk product. The hardpack frozen dessert has sufficient carbon dioxide to provide an overrun of at least 20%, where the overrun is 50-150%. Preferred Product: The frozen dessert has a drip through melt rate that achieves at least 20 wt.% melt within 35 minutes. The frozen dessert is packaged in a hermetically sealed container. Preferred Device: The hermetically sealed container is configured to moderate temperature of the hardpack frozen dessert. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is applied as a gas to the mixture at a rate of 1-4 cc carbon dioxide per gram mixture. The carbon dioxide is applied in an amount sufficient to achieve an overrun of 50-150%. The carbon dioxide is applied as a pressurized gas to a moving stream of the mixture. The carbon dioxide is applied as a carbon dioxide-enriched atmosphere in contact with a surface of the mixture. The carbon dioxide is applied by sparging the mixture with the carbon dioxide. The method further involves packaging the carbonated mixture.

PN CN109824905-A; CN109824905-B
TI Preparing high temperature resistant            polycarbosilane precursor used in preparing silicon            carbide fiber, involves injecting raw material            polydimethylsilane into aluminum hydroxide, replacing            air in reaction system with carbon dioxide.
AU WU B
   HOU Z
AE JIANGXI JIAJIE XINDA NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019500436
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature resistant                polycarbosilane precursor involves injecting raw                material polydimethylsilane into aluminum                hydroxide, replacing air in reaction system with                carbon dioxide, then performing temperature rising                and heat preservation treatment, and cooling to                room temperature, dissolving product obtained in                xylene, adjusting pH to 3-4, then heating and                stirring, filtering in vacuum, and taking solution                phase. The poly zirconium oxide is added to                solution phase, heated to 60-80 degrees C and                stirred uniformly, and dried in vacuum when                viscosity of system is increased. The air is                replaced in reaction system with carbon dioxide in                vacuum dried product, and continuously carbon                dioxide gas is maintained, and then heated. The                temperature is maintained, and cooled to room                temperature. The product obtained is dissolved in                tetrahydrofuran, and fraction is collected at                118-120 degrees C by distillation under reduced                pressure to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing high temperature                resistant polycarbosilane precursor used in                preparing silicon carbide fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high temperature                resistant polycarbosilane precursor that has narrow                molecular weight distribution, good spinnability                and ceramic conversion, high strength, good high                temperature resistance and outstanding                comprehensive performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The aluminum hexahydrate                  solution is heated, and excessive amount of                  sodium carbonate solution is slowly added                  dropwise under stirring to form precipitate,                  after precipitate is aged, washed, and then                  vacuum dried, grounded and sieved to obtain                  aluminum hydroxide. The concentration of aluminum                  chloride hexahydrate solution is 20-35 g/L,                  concentration of sodium carbonate solution is                  10-20 g/L, and ratio of sodium carbonate to                  aluminum chloride hexahydrate is (2.5-5):1.

AU MA Q

AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2019400180
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing double-hole nickel based catalyst comprises (i) mixing triblock copolymer P123, nickel source and aluminum source in an organic solvent, and adding concentrated nitric acid and polystyrene microsphere dispersion to obtain mixed solution; and (ii) drying and roasting the mixed solution.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the conversion rate of the raw materials, facilitates the mass transfer of the reactant molecules and the problem of carbon deposition of the catalyst by utilizing macroporous channel, reduces the carbon content of the catalyst to 5 wt.% and solves the problem of catalyst carbon deposition. The catalyst is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using the catalyst in methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction, comprising (a) subjecting the double-hole nickel based catalyst to a reduction treatment to obtain an activated double-hole nickel based catalyst; and (b) subjecting methane and carbon dioxide to a reforming reaction in the presence of activated double-hole nickel based catalyst, where the temperature of the reforming reaction is 600-800 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst mesoporous pore and macroporous pore, where the pore diameter of mesoporous pore is 4-10 nm and macroporous pore is 40-100 nm. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of triblock copolymer P123, nickel source, aluminum source and polystyrene microsphere dispersion is 1-2 g:0.05-0.5 g:2-4 g:1-2 ml. The concentration of the triblock copolymer P123 in organic solvent is 7-9x 10-3 mol/l. The nickel sources comprises nickel nitrate and the aluminum source comprises aluminum isopropoxide. The mass ratio of concentrated nitric acid and organic solvent is 1-5:5-15. The amount of nickel in the catalyst is 1-20 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the drying is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 48-96 hours. The roasting is carried out at 600-800 degrees C for 3-5 hours under air atmosphere.

PT P
PN CN109250884-A; CN109250884-B
TI Performing a highly-efficient deamination process of biogas slurry combined with thermal hydrolysis of digested sludge involves subjecting dehydrated sludge to slurrying and anaerobic digestion to obtain biogas and digested sludge.
AU DAI X
   ZHANG Y
   YANG D
   DONG L
   DAI L
   LI P
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2019104466
AB    NOVELTY - Performing a highly-efficient deamination process of biogas slurry combined with thermal hydrolysis of digested sludge involves subjecting dehydrated sludge to slurrying and anaerobic digestion to obtain biogas and digested sludge, purifying obtained biogas by using biogas to obtain natural gas, and releasing pressurized carbon dioxide. The digested sludge is efficiently deaminated by pre-dewatering digested sludge to obtain pre-dewatered biogas residue and biogas slurry. The pre-dewatered biogas residue is thermally hydrolyzed to produce a thermally hydrolyzed sludge and a pressure-relieving vapor containing carbon dioxide and ammonia nitrogen. The thermally hydrolyzed sludge is treated as a bio-carbon soil by high-drying dehydration and solar drying. The biogas slurry is sequentially subjected to carbon dioxide removal, ammonia removal, ammonia absorption, and carbonization treatment.
   USE - Method for performing a highly-efficient deamination process of biogas slurry combined with thermal hydrolysis of digested sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform a highly-efficient deamination process of biogas slurry combined with thermal hydrolysis of digested sludge, which utilizes carbon dioxide in biogas fully to recover ammonium bicarbonate, and achieves efficient removal and full recovery of ammonia nitrogen in digested sludge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The ammonia nitrogen concentration of biogas slurry is 2000-3500mg/L, total alkalinity is 8000-15000mg calcium carbonate/L, and a dehydrating agent containing no ferric chloride is added during the pre-dehydration. The pre-dewatered biogas residue is thermally hydrolyzed at a temperature of 160-170 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, and alkalization of bicarbonate in biogas residue releases a large amount of carbon dioxide and ammonia nitrogen with a release pressure, where steam is released and pH is increased to above 9. The thermally hydrolyzed sludge is directly subjected to high-dry dehydration treatment without adding a drug, and a mud cake having a solid content of not less than 40% to produce high-temperature high-COD thermal hydrolyzate. The mud cake is subjected to solar drying treatment as a bio-carbon soil, and high-temperature high-COD thermal hydrolyzate is refluxed for producing methane. The method involves pumping biogas slurry having a temperature of 40-50 degrees C into horizontal rotating disk to remove carbon dioxide tank, and then introducing a small amount of sludge to carry out post-pressure steam after the thermal hydrolysis, removing carbon dioxide and portion of bicarbonate negative ion in biogas slurry under vacuum and dish stirring condition, and pH is increased naturally. The decarburized biogas slurry is pumped into the deamination tower, and subjected to post-pressure steam after the thermal hydrolysis, where temperature of biogas slurry is 70-80 degrees C and vacuum pressure is -0.07 to 0.08Mpa, and then subjected to deamination treatment to obtain deaminated biogas slurry and ammonia-containing steam. The ammonia-containing vapor is cooled and absorbed into concentrated ammonia water by ammonia, and concentrated ammonia water and pressurized carbon dioxide released by biogas purification process are carbonized in a carbonization tower to obtain an ammonium bicarbonate product. The ammoniated biogas slurry increases percentage of methane by absorbing carbon dioxide in the biogas, and adjusts pH value of biogas slurry to be weakly alkaline getting absorbed by the biogas, and is then subjected to subsequent water treatment. The ammonia nitrogen removal rate of biogas slurry in the deamination tower is not less than 80%. Preferred Components: The dehydrating agent is an organic high-molecular flocculant. The organic high-molecular flocculant is polyacrylamide.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.)
MC H09-F03; A04-D04A2; A10-E05B; A12-M01; A12-W11E; A12-W11J; D04-A01B; D04-A01F3; D04-A01J; D04-A01P1; D04-B07; D04-B10A; E11-Q02B; E31-N05C; E32-A04
IP C02F-001/20; C02F-011/04; C02F-011/14; C02F-011/10; C02F-011/147

PT P
PN CN108927194-A
TI Nitrogen-doped ruthenium-based bio-carbon catalyst useful for synthesis of methane from carbon dioxide in which in-situ nitrogen-doped carbon used as carrier, and metal ruthenium is used as active component.
AU WANG S
   LIU Y
   ZHU L
   QIU K
   ZHUO Y
   FANG M
   ZHOU J
   LUO Z
   NI M
   CEN K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201899537X
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped ruthenium-based bio-carbon catalyst in which in-situ nitrogen-doped carbon used as carrier, and metal ruthenium is used as active component. The loading amount of the metal ruthenium is 0.5 5wt.%, and the nitrogen doping amount is 5-20.wt%. The nitrogen-doped nitrogen form comprises pyridine nitrogen and pyrrole nitrogen. The nitrogen-doped ruthenium-based bio-carbon catalyst has microporous and mesoporous.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesis of methane from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves carbon dioxide conversion and methane selectivity in the carbon dioxide synthesis methane reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst comprising (i) mixing biomass rawmaterial, bicarbonate activator with nitrogen source, and performing in-situ nitrogen doping pyrolysis to obtain a black powder, (ii) refluxing the black powder in a dilute nitric acid solution to obtain an in situ nitrogen-doped bio-carbon, and (iii) dispersing the in situ nitrogen-doped biochar into the strontium salt solution, performing sonication and mechanical agitation, evaporating again, and roasting self-reduction enthalpy under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain the nitrogen-doped ruthenium-based bio-carbon catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface pyridinium content is 15-45% of the total nitrogen content, and the surface pyrrole nitrogen content is 25-65% of the total nitrogen content. The specific surface area of the catalyst is 50-800 m2/g, where the specific surface area of the micropores is 50-80% of the total specific surface area, the pore volume is 0.05-0.5 cm3/g, and the average pore diameter is 3-15 nm. The biomass material comprises pin, rice husk and/or lauan. The bicarbonate activating agent is sodium hydrogencarbonate and/or potassium hydrogencarbonate. The nitrogen source comprises urea, melamine and/or ovalbumin. In the step (iii), the cerium salt is cerium chloride, cerium nitrate and/or cerium acetate, where the concentration of the cerium salt solution is 0.025-0.75 mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (i), in situ nitrogen-doped pyrolysis carbonization comprises mixing and grinding the biomass raw material, hydrogencarbonate activator and nitrogen source at a mass ratio of 1:1-8:1-10, heating up to 450-800 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 0.5-5 hours. In the step (ii), the reflux process comprises dispersing the black powder in a dilute nitric acid solution and stirring under reflux for 4-8 hours, then rinsing with deionized water to neutral, heating and drying to obtain the in situ nitrogen-doped bio-carbon, where the dilute nitric acid solution has a mass concentration of 1-20%. In the step (iii), the sonication time is 20-100 minutes, the mechanical stirring time is 6-12 hour, and the self-reduction time in the nitrogen atmosphere is 1-4 hour.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108728153-A; CN108728153-B
TI Manufacturing low-carbon olefin involves adding methane and water vapour to obtain steam reforming synthesis gas understeam reforming reaction conditions, and methane and carbon dioxide contact to obtain dry reforming synthesis gas.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   SUN X
   YAN Z
   XIA G
   LI M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201887231S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing low-carbon olefin involves adding methane and water vapor to obtain steam reforming synthesis gas understeam reforming reaction conditions, and methane and carbon dioxide contact to obtain dry reforming synthesis gas under dry reforming reaction conditions. The partial steam reforming synthesis gas and dry reforming synthesis gas is used to obtain Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feedstock in reaction temperature is used for producing low carbon olefin under Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to obtain Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream is used to separate light olefins, methane and carbon dioxide. The separated methane and separated carbon dioxide are sent.
   USE - Method for manufacturing low-carbon olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare manufacture low-carbon olefin that reduces system energy consumption and greenhouse gas emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a low carbon olefin production system, which comprises water vapor reforming reaction unit, dry reforming reaction unit, synthesis gas mixing unit, Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction unit, Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction product separation unit, and circulation unit, where water vapor reforming reaction unit is used for converting methane and water vapour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The methane and steam temperature is 700-950 degrees C. The reaction pressure is 0.1-5 mega pascal. The gas hourly volume space velocity of feed is 10,000-100,000 per hours. The ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 1:0.5-5. The low carbon olefin reaction temperature is 200-400 degrees C. The pressure preferably is 0.5-3 mega pascal. The ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide is 0.4-3:1, preferably 1.5-2.2:1. The Fischer Tropsch synthesis reaction volume space velocity of feed is 5000-50000 per hours. The Group VIII has valence state lower than highest oxidation state of metal element. The pressure reduction in reactor is 0-3 mega pascal, preferably 0.1-1 mega pascal. The volume space velocity of first gas is 5000-30000 per hours, preferably 10000-20000 per hours. The reaction time is 1-20 hours, preferably 2-10 hours. The ratio of inert gas to hydrocarbon which is gaseous at reduction activation temperature is 1-200:1, preferably 15-30:1. The calcining temperature is 300-900 degrees C, preferably 400-600 degrees C. The calcining duration is 1-10 hours. The calcining temperature is 700-1050 degrees C in air atmosphere. The duration of calcination is 0.5-5 hours. The ratio of methane employed to methane employed is 1:0.8-1.5, preferably 1:0.9-1.3. Preferred Components: The Group VIII metal element is iron, cobalt and nickel, preferably iron. The second metal element is Group IVB metal elements and/or alkaline metal element, preferably Group IVB metal element and alkali metal element. The third metalelement is cerium. The 1-4C alkane and 2-4C olefin is selected from methane, ethane, ethylene, propylene, propane, butane, and butene. The inert gas in first gas and second gas are the same or different and selected from species which is selected from nitrogen and zero group element gas, preferably nitrogen and/or argon. The auxiliary element is selected from alkali metal elements and rare earth metal element, preferably lithium, potassium and cerium. The alumina contains theta-alumina. The raw material gas is selected from shale gas, coal gas, natural gas, refinery gas and coke oven gas. Preferred Compositions: The content of second metal element is 0-15 wt.%, preferably 2-11 wt.%. The content of thirdmetal element is 0-10 wt.%, preferably 0.5-6 wt.%. The content of VIII group metal is 3-30 wt.%, preferably 8-20 wt.%. The content of VIII group metal is 3-30 wt.%, preferably 10-15 wt.%. The content of theta-alumina is 50 wt.%.

TI Cracking aerosol composition used as e.g. hair restorer and hair styling product, comprises liquefied petroleum gas, water, lower alcohol, carbon dioxide, emulsifier, emulsification adjuvant and thickener.
AU IIZUKA Y
AE TOYO AEROSOL IND CO (TOAO-C)
   TOYO AEROSOL IND CO (TOAO-C)
   TOYO AEROSOL IND CO (TOAO-C)
   TOYO AEROSOL IND CO (TOAO-C)
   TOYO AEROSOL KOGYO KK (TOAO-C)
GA 201885688B
AB    NOVELTY - A cracking aerosol composition comprises 45-73 %mass liquefied petroleum gas, water, 4-12 %mass lower alcohol, 0.1-3.5 %mass carbon dioxide, 0.02-1.2 %mass emulsifier, an emulsification adjuvant, and a thickener. The emulsifier comprises two types of nonionic surfactants with different hydrophilic-lipophilic balance values. The mass ratio of the two types of nonionic surfactant is 1:10-10:1. The composition is loaded in an aerosol container comprising a pressure-resistant container equipped with an aerosol valve.
   USE - Cracking aerosol composition. Uses include but are not limited to hair restorer, massaging agent, hair styling product, hair treatment agent, shampoo agent and conditioner agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The cracking aerosol composition has excellent emulsion stability due to the presence of the nonionic surfactant, favorable usability, does not cause sticky feel and skin irritation, while applying to the human body, and produces bubble breakage sounds in a viscous flowable discharge, by the action of carbon dioxide gas and liquefied petroleum gas. The emulsifying action by the emulsifier can be sufficiently exhibited even due to the presence of carbon dioxide, so that the favorable emulsified state of the composition can be obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The two types of nonionic surfactants includes a nonionic surfactant (n1) chosen from polyoxyethylene coconut fatty acid sorbitan and polyoxyethylene sorbitol monolaurate, and having a hydrophilic-lipophilic balance value of 15-18, and a nonionic surfactant (n2) chosen from polyoxyethylene sorbitan tetraoleate and polyoxyethylene hydrogenated castor oil, and having a hydrophilic-lipophilic balance value of 10-15. Preferred Property: The difference between hydrophilic-lipophilic balance value of surfactant (n1) and surfactant (n2) is 1-5. The viscosity of the stock solution containing water, lower alcohol, emulsifier, emulsification adjuvant and thickener at 20 degrees C is 50-1000 mPa.second.

   HAN C
   LIANG F
   LI H
   MA X
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202292450X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sodium-helium heat exchanger                (3) formed as a shell-and-tube heat exchanger. A                sodium unit is set on a shell side of the                sodium-helium heat exchanger. A helium gas unit is                at a pipe side of the sodium-helium heat exchanger.                A quick reactor core (1), a sodium pump (2) and the                sodium-helium heat exchanger are connected together                to form a loop of the system. The loop is arranged                with an electric heating device and supplied with                sodium medium, where the electric heating device                heats the medium to greater than 200 degrees                centigrade. A shell side outlet of the                sodium-helium heat exchanger is fixed with an inlet                of the sodium pump. An outlet of the sodium pump is                fixed with an inlet of the quick reactor                core.
   USE - Quick reactor carbon dioxide power generation                system for use with a steam turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures that the sodium does not                generate chemical reaction in the loop when the                sodium-helium heat exchanger is leaked and even if                helium gas enters into the sodium loop, thus                avoiding occurrence of nuclear safety accidents and                realizing supercritical carbon dioxide power                generation circulation under high temperature                parameter conditions with high efficiency.

PN WO2022136267-A1
TI Carbon dioxide laser for use in industrial            production lines to perform e.g. operations on metal            workpieces, has optical element comprising frequency            selective structure, where Q-factor of laser changes            upon receipt of signal.
AU RYAN D J
   AHERN R R
AE ALLTEC ANGEWANDTE LASERLICHT TECHNOLOGIE (ALLT-Non-standard)
GA 202284900T
AB    NOVELTY - The laser (100) has an optical element (105)                comprising a frequency selective structure having a                periodic pattern of features, where a frequency                response of the optical element is configured to                change upon receipt of a signal, and a Q-factor of                the laser changes upon the receipt of the signal. A                laser cavity comprises a carbon dioxide gain                medium. A radio frequency excitation source excites                the gain medium to produce infrared electromagnetic                radiation. A control system provides the signal to                the element to Q-switch the laser. A separate                control signal is provided to the source to excite                the medium.
   USE - Carbon dioxide laser for use in industrial                production lines to perform operations on metal                workpieces. Uses include but are not limited to                etching, engraving, machining, cutting and                welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The optical element allows a user to control                the frequency response of the optical system with                the optical element. The optical element has                improved energy efficiency, and is cheaper to                manufacture. The frequency selective structure has                a periodic pattern of features with a periodicity                that varies by less than about 10 percent, thus                allowing the element to be used to Q-switch the                laser by changing frequency response to reflect,                transmit and absorb electromagnetic radiation                within a given range of frequencies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a laser marking system; and
   (2) a method for Q-switching a carbon dioxide                laser to produce infrared electromagnetic                radiation; and
   (3) a method for marking a target with                infrared electromagnetic radiation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a laser                comprising a tunable optical element.
   Carbon dioxide laser (100)
   Optical element (105)
   Fully reflective rear mirror (110)
   Output coupler (115)
   Passive optical component (120)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting a carbon dioxide circulating blasting device at an inlet end of a horizontal well. The carbon dioxide circulation blasting device is used for circulating blasting crack of a target ore layer. The horizontal well is controlled to open. Carbon dioxide gas is inverted and collected to reduce carbon dioxide pressure in the target orelayer. A leaching agent is controlled for injecting a sub-system agent into the target mining layer. A liquid pumping pump is controlled after leaching reaction by the pumping well to the ground surface to extract a gas-liquid mixture. Uranium is extracted from the leaching solution based on uranium mine water smelting process, extracting uranium from the leaching solution based on the uranium mine water smelting process.
   USE - Low-permeability sandstone uranium mine seepage-increasing exploitation method.

GA 2021C5875N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves converting an electrolyzer feed stream comprising water to an electrolyzer product stream comprising hydrogen and oxygen, where a portion of the electricity used in the electrolysis step is from renewable sources. The air contacted with an adsorbent and carbon dioxide is removed from an air stream (3). A portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream is reacted with a stream comprising carbon dioxide to produce a reverse water gas shift product stream comprising carbon monoxide. The portion of the hydrogen from the electrolyzer product stream is reacted with a stream comprising a portion of a reverse water gas shift product stream. The portion of the oxygen produced by electrolysis is reacted with a stream. A direct air capture uses a solid supported amine adsorbent and uses aqueous potassium hydroxide to remove carbon dioxide from the air. A hydrocarbyl synthesis feedstock has a molar hydrogen to carbon monoxide ratio between 1.90 and 2.20.
   USE - Integrated method for conversion of feed streams comprising such as electricity, air, and water to products such as fuels e.g. diesel fuel, kerosene, jet fuel, gasoline or gasoline blendstocks and chemicals such as methanol, ammonia, solvents, waxes or olefins.

AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical system (100) includes a reactor (110) comprising an aqueous electrolyte solution (120) in which carbon dioxide and nitrate are dissolved, first and second electrodes (130) and (140) at least partially immersed in the aqueous electrolyte solution, a catalyst comprising metal nanoclusters adsorbed on the surface of the carbon support coated on the surface of the first electrode, and a power supply unit (160) for applying an electrical signal to the first and second electrodes, so that carbon dioxide and nitrate are co-reduced to generate urea.
   USE - Electrochemical system used for synthesizing urea by co-reducing carbon dioxide and nitrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal nanocluster catalyst has improved chemical selectivity for reaction which generates urea by reducing carbon dioxide and nitrate, and high urea generation efficiency under room temperature and pressure conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) metal nanocluster catalyst, which comprises graphatized mesoporous carbon, a binder bonded to the graphatized mesoporous carbon through pi - pi interaction, and copper nanoclusters adsorbed to the surface of the graphatized mesoporous carbon by the binder. The binder includes an organic compound having a structure including at least 3 or more aromatic rings and at least 3 or more nitrogen atoms; and
   (2) preparation of the metal nanocluster catalyst, which involves dispersing the carbon support, Nafion (sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer), and binder in a solvent to form carbon support-binder structure, coating the carbon support-binder structure on a conductive substrate, and coating a solution containing a transition metal ion on the carbon support-binder structure.

PN DE102019215620-A1
TI Method for producing product mixture comprises generating carbon-containing fuel by electrolysis of carbon dioxide in electrolyser including catholyte space and anolyte space and flowing catholyte through catholyte space.
AU KRAUSE R
   MELTZER K
   RELLER C
   SCHMID G
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202138476R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a product mixture (3a) comprises generating a carbon-containing fuel by electrolysis of carbon dioxide in an electrolyser (5) including a catholyte space (11) and an anolyte space (21), flowing a catholyte (12) containing hydrogen carbonate and/or carbonate through the catholyte space at a cell pressure (pk) of 1.5 bar, passing the catholyte flowing out of the catholyte space through a desorber at a desorption pressure lower than the cell pressure and circulating from the desorber back into the catholyte space, and heating the catholyte in the desorber to a temperature higher than that of the catholyte space so that the physically and/or chemically dissolved carbon dioxide is desorbed from the catholyte.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a product mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the release of gaseous carbon dioxide from the electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for producing the product mixture including carbon-containing fuel, comprising an electrolyzer for generating the fuel by electrolysis of carbon dioxide, where the electrolyzer includes a catholyte space and an anolyte space, the catholyte space is designed for a catholyte to flow through at a cell pressure of at least 1.5 bar, and a desorber through which the catholyte flowing out of the catholyte space at a desorption pressure which is lower than the cell pressured can be conducted and from which the catholyte can be circulated back into the catholyte space, where the desorber is designed to raise the catholyte in the desorber to a temperature which is higher than that of the catholyte space to warm up.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the electrolyzer.
   Product mixture (3a)
   Electrolyser (5)
   Catholyte space (11)
   Catholyte (12)
   Anolyte space (21)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The cell pressure (pk) is at least 5 bar. The desorption pressure (pd) is at least 0.5 bar lower than the cell pressure (pk). The temperature (Td) of the catholyte in the desorber is 90-130 degrees C. The solvent of the catholyte is partially evaporated in the desorber. The solvent in the desorber is evaporated in a multi-stage process. The evaporated solvent is recondensed and then fed to the anolyte space of the electrolyzer. The carbon dioxide desorbed from the catholyte in the desorber is at least partially returned to the electrolyser as an educt of the electrolysis. Preferred Components: The electrolyzer comprises a gas diffusion cathode. The electrolyzer is designed as a falling-film electrolyzer. The carbonaceous fuel is carbon monoxide or a gaseous hydrocarbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The desorber is designed at the same time as an evaporator for the solvent of the catholyte. The desorber is designed as a multi-stage evaporator. The electrolyzer is designed as a falling-film electrolyzer.

Preferred Components: The green co-solvent                  is water, ethanol, dimethyl carbonate or their                  combinations. PN CN107712041-A
TI Preparation of fruit and vegetable preservative involves taking water, Cicada slough, Cassia, silkworm cocoons and creeping jenny, mixing, standing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction and fermenting.
AU LIN Z
   LIU R
   LIU Q
AE CHANGZHOU WURONG CHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018168565
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fruit and vegetable preservative involves taking 15-20 pts. wt. water, 8-10 pts. wt. Cicada slough, 6-8 pts. wt. Cassia, 4-5 pts. wt. silkworm cocoons and 2-3 pts. wt. creeping jenny, mixing, standing for 45-60 minutes, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain extract, mixing Aloe vera gel, extract and catalyst at mass ratio of 10-15:7-8:1-2, placing in a container, protecting by carbon dioxide and reacting at 80-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain a reactant.
   USE - Method for preparing fruit and vegetable preservative.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has slow-release function in food preservation; inhibits bacteria; and prolongs the shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of fruit and vegetable preservative comprises: (A) taking 15-20 pts. wt. water, 8-10 pts. wt. Cicada slough, 6-8 pts. wt. Cassia, 4-5 pts. wt. silkworm cocoons and 2-3 pts. wt. creeping jenny, mixing, standing for 45-60 minutes and performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain extract; (B) mixing Aloe vera gel, extract and catalyst at mass ratio of 10-15:7-8:1-2, placing in a container, protecting by carbon dioxide and reacting at 80-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain a reactant; (C) mixing the reactants, grape seeds and watermelon seeds at mass ratio of 10-15:3-4:1-2, crushing to obtain ground material, placing ground material into the fermenter, adding 0.02-0.05 wt.% Trichoderma powder, sealing and fermenting at 30-40 degrees C for 2-3 days to obtain fermentation mixture; and (D) mixing the fermentation mixture, grape seed oil and emulsifier at mass ratio of 50-60:20-25:10-15 and stirring.

PN CN107722577-A; CN107722577-B
TI Preparing microporous composition by mixing thermotropic liquid crystal polymer, filler, processing additive and pore-forming agent, adding premix and glass fibers, melt-plasticizing and injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into extruder.
AU WANG L
AE WANG L (WANG-Individual)
   SUZHOU KELISITU MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018171774
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing microporous liquid crystal polymer composition involves (a) mixing thermotropic liquid crystal polymer, filler, processing additive and pore-forming agent, (b) adding the premix and glass fibers to a twin-screw extruder, transferring the mixture through front two areas of the extruder and melt-plasticizing, (c) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into a zone (III) of the extruder, dissolving supercritical carbon dioxide in the polymer melt, adjusting the melt pump to generate pressure difference, extruding and rapid cooling to obtain the composition.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing microporous liquid crystal polymer composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the microporous liquid crystal polymer composition with excellent high-temperature-resistance and mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing microporous liquid crystal polymer composition involves (a) preliminarily mixing thermotropic liquid crystal polymer, filler, processing additive and pore-forming agent to obtain a premix, (b) adding the premix and glass fibers to a twin-screw extruder, transferring the mixture through front two areas of the twin-screw extruder and carrying out melt-plasticizing to obtain polymer melt, (c) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into a zone (III) of the twin-screw extruder at a temperature of 50 degrees C, a pressure of 10 MPa and output flow rate of 1-10 ml/min, uniformly dissolving supercritical carbon dioxide in the polymer melt, adjusting the melt pump to generate pressure difference, extruding and rapid cooling to obtain the microporous liquid crystal polymer composition. The glass fiber has a length of smaller than 1 mm. The twin-screw extruder is a separate injection system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler is 1000-1500-mesh talc. The pore-forming agent is potassium A molecular sieve, sodium A molecular sieve, sodium Y molecular sieve, calcium X molecular sieve, ZSM-type molecular sieve, calcium carbonate, zinc oxide or pentaerythritol stearate. Preferred Properties: The glass fiber has a diameter of 20-50 microns. The pore-forming agent has a particle size of 3 microns.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The processing additive is lubricant and surface accelerator. The surface accelerator is silane coupling agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermotropic liquid crystal polymer is p-hydroxybenzoic acid, 6-hydroxy-2-naphthoic acid, terephthalic acid, resorcinol or 4,4'-dihydroxybiphenyl. The lubricant is silicone powder or silicone masterbatch. Preferred Composition: The microporous liquid crystal polymer composition comprises 100 parts mass thermotropic liquid crystal polymer, 10-30 parts mass glass fiber, 5-30 parts mass filler, 0-10 parts mass processing additive and 0-5 parts mass pore-forming agent. Preferred Properties: The thermotropic liquid crystal polymer has a melting point of 300-380 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: In the step (c), the injecting step is carried out at an output flow rate of 5 ml/minute.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

PN CN107641197-A; CN107641197-B
TI New catalyst i.e. hexadecaene containing compound            useful for copolymerization reaction of carbon dioxide            and cyclohexene oxide which is useful for preparing            poly(cyclohexenylene carbonate).
AU ZHANG X
   WANG Y
   ZHAO K
   ZHANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201811646J
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst i.e.                16,20-dimethyl-3-l3,33-l3-dioxa-38-thia-11-l4-aza-17-l4-aza-19-l4-aza-25-l4-aza-18-zincadecacyclo[33.2.1.03,18.04,9.011,18.012,17.018,25.018,33.019,24.027,32]octatriaconta-1(37),4,6,8,10,12,14,16,19(24),20,22,25,27(32),28,30,35-hexadecaene                (I), is new.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for copolymerization                reaction of carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide                which is useful for preparing poly(cyclohexenylene                carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent reaction                selectivity, requires mild reaction conditions and                ensures that the copolymerization reaction is                simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst i.e.                16,20-dimethyl-3-l3,33-l3-dioxa-38-thia-11-l4-aza-17-l4-aza-19-l4-aza-25-l4-aza-18-zincadecacyclo[33.2.1.03,18.04,9.011,18.012,17.018,25.018,33.019,24.027,32]octatriaconta-1(37),4,6,8,10,12,14,16,19(24),20,22,25,27(32),28,30,35-hexadecaene                of formula (I), is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for method of preparing                poly(cyclohexenylene carbonate) by copolymerizing                carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide using the                catalyst (I), which involves (a) adding the                catalyst into a vacuum-dried reactor, filling with                carbon dioxide until atmospheric pressure is                reached, adding cyclohexene oxide into the reactor,                heating the reactor at 60-180&#176; C, filling                with carbon dioxide to maintain the pressure at 1-6                MPa, evenly stirring the contents for 6-60 hours                and cooling to room temperature to stop the                reaction and (b) distilling off the unreacted                cyclohexene oxide and performing post-production                treatment to obtain the product.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The catalyst (I) is                  prepared by (a) dissolving                  2,5-bis(2-alkanal-phenoxymethyl)thiophene and                  2-amino-6-methylpyridine in ethanol to obtain                  solution respectively, dropping                  2,5-bis(2-alkanal-phenoxymethyl)thiophene                  solution to 2-amino-6-methylpyridine solution,                  boiling the mixed solution under reflux, washing                  the reaction product with ether and drying to                  obtain 2,5-bis                  (2-alkanal-phenoxymethyl)thienyl-2-amino-6-methylpyridine                  ligand, (b) adding the 2,5-bis                  (2-alkanal-phenoxymethyl)thienyl-2-amino-6-methylpyridine                  ligand into a container, vaccuming the container,                  filling with nitrogen, adding toluene to the                  ligand until complete dissolution, adding a                  toluene solution containing diethylzinc in a                  ice-salt bath, cooling, stirring for 0.5-1 hour,                  continuing stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1                  hour and concentrating under reduced pressure to                  recover solvent and unreacted diethylzinc and                  obtain the product. Preferred Method: In step                  (a), the 2-amino-6-methylpyridine and                  2,5-bis(2-alkanalphenoxymethyl)thiophene are in a                  molar ratio of (1.8-2.2):1. In step (b), 2,5-bis                  (2-alkanalphenoxymethyl)thienyl-2-amino-6-methylpyridine                  ligand and diethyl zinc are in a molar ratio of                  1:(1-1.2). In step (a), the catalyst and                  cyclohexene oxide are in a mass ratio of                  (0.001-0.01):1. In step (b), performing                  post-production treatment by dissolving the                  residue in methylene chloride, filtering, washing                  the filtrate with dilute acid, dilute alkali and                  distilled water, then dropping into methanol                  while stirring, precipitating white solid,                  filtering and vacuum drying to obtain the                  product.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.)
MC A05-E06A; A10-D; A11-A03; A11-C; E05-L03D; E11-F07D; E11-F11

PT P
PN CN114130156-A
TI Green and environment-friendly waste gas treatment by conveying organic waste gas to be processed to dry dust remover, filtering particulate matter gas, purifying organic matter in gas, and starting desorption fan to desorb adsorption bed.
AU CHEN Y
   DING H
   XIA C
AE FUXIN JIANGSU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (FUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202238839H
AB    NOVELTY - Green and environment-friendly waste gas treatment comprises conveying organic waste gas to be processed to dry dust remover, and filtering particulate matter gas; removing the organic particulate matter gas, entering the activated carbon adsorption bed, and purifying the organic matter in the gas; after the adsorption bed is saturated, starting desorption fan to desorb the adsorption bed, entering desorption gas in a preheater in a catalytic bed through a heat exchanger in the catalytic bed, firstly heating, burning organic substance under the action of the catalyst, decomposing into carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O), and discharging a lot of heat, passing the high temperature gas through the heat exchanger again, and heat exchanging with the incoming cold air; and recycling a part of heat by boiler waste heat recovery, generating steam, and using, and entering the other part in adsorption bed to desorb the activated carbon.
   USE - The method is for green and environment-friendly waste gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need external energy, has more convenient operation, reduces the operation cost, avoids the secondary pollution, and is good for energy saving.

AU FORGERON D
