PN JP2021054705-A
TI Apparatus for producing production gas containing carbon monoxide, has reducing agent temperature control portions that adjusts temperature of reducing agent when raw material gas is brought into contact with reducing agent.
AU NAKAMA Y

AE SEKISUI CHEM IND CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202135277C
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a reactors (4a, 4b) that are connected to the raw material gas supply unit and the reducing gas supply unit. A reaction unit is provided with the reducing agent arranged in the reactor and is capable of switching between the raw material gas and the reducing gas supplied to each reactor. A gas merging section produces a mixed gas by merging the gases after passing through the reactor. The reactors have reducing agent temperature control portions that are provided for adjusting the temperature of the reducing agent when the raw material gas or the reducing gas is brought into contact with the reducing agent. The housing has tubes. The reducing agent temperature control unit transfers a medium supplied to the space defined by tubes and the housing and the medium to the space. The first heat exchanger exchanges heat with the medium before being supplied to the space of the reactor.
   USE - Apparatus for producing production gas containing carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus continuously and stably produces a produced gas containing carbon monoxide from a raw material gas containing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The second heat exchanger exchanges heat with raw material gas and reducing gas, before supplied to the reactor. The metal oxide contains metal elements belonging to groups 3 to 12. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a gas-making system. The gas production apparatus is connected to the raw material gas generation unit through raw material gas supply unit; and
   (2) a gas-making method. The reducing agent oxidized by contact with the carbon dioxide is prepared, and the raw material gas is used. The raw material gas and the reducing gas are alternately brought into contact with the reducing agent to convert the carbon dioxide into the carbon monoxide, and oxidized reducing agent is reduced to carry out the reaction. The raw material gas or the reducing gas is brought into contact with the reducing agent. The temperature of the reducing agent is adjusted by supplying a temperature-controlled medium to the space partitioned from the reducing agent. The reducing agent is heated by supplying the heated medium to the space of each reactor when the raw material gas or the reducing gas is brought into contact with the reducing agent.

PN CN112375620-A
TI Preparation of supercritical particles of microbial fermented oil by extracting microalgae powder, adding core material, wall material and co-surfactant, reacting, stirring, pre-expanding, spraying into aqueous medium and forming.
AU HU Y
AE HU Y (HUYY-Individual)
GA 2021203918
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation process of supercritical particles of microbial fermented oil involves (i) adding a certain amount of microalgae powder to supercritical reactor, (ii) extracting the microalgae powder, and adjusting pressure, temperature, flow and decompression separation to obtain oil, (iii) adding a certain amount of core material, wall material comprising Acacia gum, and alpha and beta -cyclodextrin, and co-surfactant comprising Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate) into special reactor, (iv) opening gas cylinder, passing carbon dioxide gas into reactor, replacing air in reactor and increasing pressure, (v) opening outlet valve of reactor, adjusting flow rate of the fluid, and turning on magnetic stirrer under reactor to stir at high speed to form supercritical microemulsion, and (vi) pre-expanding the above supercritical microemulsion, quickly spraying through a nozzle at a certain flow rate into aqueous medium in supercritical collection kettle, and forming.
   USE - Preparation process of supercritical particles of microbial fermented oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The process combines the supercritical rapid expansion technology to collect the nanoparticles, and provides way for the preparation of microbial fermentation oil embedding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation process of supercritical particles of microbial fermented oil involves (i) adding a certain amount of microalgae powder to supercritical reactor, (ii) extracting the microalgae powder by supercritical carbon dioxide gas, and adjusting pressure, temperature, flow and decompression separation to obtain oil, which is used as a core material, (iii) adding a certain amount of core material, wall material comprising Acacia gum, and alpha and beta -cyclodextrin, and co-surfactant comprising Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate) into a special reactor, (iv) opening the gas cylinder when the temperature of the reactor reaches the set temperature, and passing carbon dioxide gas into reactor, replacing the air in the reactor and increasing the pressure to reach the set pressure in the reactor, (v) opening the outlet valve of the reactor, adjusting the flow rate of the fluid, and turning on the magnetic stirrer under the reactor to stir at high speed to form a supercritical microemulsion, where process is in a supercritical state, and the surface tension is 0 after the two phases are mixed, and (vi) pre-expanding the above supercritical microemulsion under the set pressure and temperature condition when the system pressure and flow rate are stable, quickly spraying through a nozzle at a certain flow rate into the aqueous medium in the supercritical collection kettle, and forming a liposome suspension after dispersion and precipitation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The microalgae powder is broken in advance. In step (ii), the extraction pressure is 25-60 MPa, preferably 35-45 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-100 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C, and the extraction time is 50-130 minutes, preferably 60-90 minutes. In step (v), the magnetic stirring power is 15-35 Kw, preferably 25-27 Kw, and the magnetic stirring time is 10-40 minutes, preferably 20-35 minutes. In step (vi), the pre-expansion pressure is 10-45 MPa, preferably 25-30 MPa, and the pre-expansion temperature is 325-365 K, preferably 335-345 K. In step (vi), the supercritical microemulsion is sprayed into the water phase medium at a flow rate of 0.5-10 l/minute, preferably a flow rate of 2-6 l/minute. Preferred Properties: The average particle size of the liposome is 50-1000 nm, and the particle size distribution of the sample prepared under optimal conditions is narrow and uniform, and the average particle size is below 100 nm.

PN CN109384256-A
TI Preparation of calcium sulfate involves adding desulfurized ash material to reactor containing excess dilute sulfuric acid and reacting sulfuric acid, calcium carbonate and calcium hydroxide to form calcium sulfate, carbon dioxide and water.
AU TIAN H
   TIAN Z
   ZONG X
   HUANG L
   YANG S
AE UNIV HUANGGANG NORMAL (UHGN-C)
   TIAN H (TIAN-Individual)
GA 201921296L
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of calcium sulfate involves adding desulfurized ash material to reactor containing excess dilute sulfuric acid, reacting sulfuric acid, calcium carbonate, and calcium hydroxide to form calcium sulfate, carbon dioxide gas and water, reacting with calcium sulfate pentahydrate to form calcium sulfate, emitting sulfur dioxide gas, hydrolyzing certain amount of sulfur dioxide gas into sulfurous acid in reaction vessel, oxidizing to sulfate ion and chloride ion by introducing chlorine dioxide gas into reactor or dripping chlorine dioxide aqueous solution, introducing emitted sulfur dioxide gas into the absorption tower in which acid solution composed of sulfuric acid and chlorine dioxide is used as absorption liquid to generate sulfate ions and chloride ions, testing pH value in reaction kettle, neutralizing reaction liquid in kettle with ash calcium powder until pH is neutral, filtering, washing three times with tap water, dehydrating and drying.
   USE - Preparation of calcium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of calcium sulfate by simple, fast, environmentally-friendly and industrial process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of calcium sulfate involves testing content of calcium carbonate, calcium sulfate pentahydrate, calcium hydroxide in the desulfurized ash to be treated, weighing certain amount of desulfurized ash slag, calculating the amount of sulfuric acid required for reacting, making the sulfuric acid excess 10-30%, dripping desulfurized ash material to the reactor containing excess dilute sulfuric acid, simultaneously opening the induced draft fan and the spray absorption tower, reacting sulfuric acid, calcium carbonate, and calcium hydroxide to form calcium sulfate, carbon dioxide gas and water, reacting with calcium sulfate pentahydrate to form calcium sulfate, emitting sulfur dioxide gas, hydrolyzing certain amount of sulfur dioxide gas into sulfurous acid in reaction vessel, oxidizing to sulfate ion and a chloride ion by introducing chlorine dioxide gas into the reactor or dripping chlorine dioxide aqueous solution, introducing emitted sulfur dioxide gas into the absorption tower containing absorption liquid composed of an acid solution composed of sulfuric acid and chlorine dioxide by an induced draft fan to generate sulfate ions and chloride ions, testing the pH value in the reactor after the reaction in the reactor is completed, neutralizing reaction liquid in the reactor with ash calcium powder until the pH is neutral, filtering by plate and frame filter press, washing three times with tap water and dehydrating and drying. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for preparing calcium sulfate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of amount of sulfuric acid and the total amount of calcium ions in desulfurized ash residue is (1.1-1.3):1. The sulfuric acid is diluted to a dilute sulfuric acid having a mass fraction of 1-30%. The chlorine dioxide gas is diluted with air or carbon dioxide to volume ratio of less than or equal to 10%. The chlorine dioxide aqueous solution is commercially available or self-formulated aqueous solution of chlorine dioxide at a concentration of 5-20 g/l. Preferred Process: The corresponding salt is obtained by recycling the recovered acid solution containing sulfate ion and chloride ion as an absorption liquid or directly neutralizing with calcium ions.

PN CN108069452-A; CN108069452-B
TI Preparing chain-shaped calcite calcium carbonate comprises crushing limestone, calcining, terminating carbonation reaction, dehydrating calcium carbonate suspension, crushing and sieving.
AU MO Y
   ZHU Y
   WEI J
   MAN Z
   LI Y
   LIU S
   QIN X
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201842327J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chain-shaped calcite calcium carbonate comprises (i) crushing limestone, calcining the limestone, sieving by 2 meshes standard sieve, calcining the remaining residue limestone in an furnace, and controlling the digestion rate, (ii) mixing the obtained quicklime with water, stirring and digesting, (iii) passing the lime slag through 150 meshes sieve, sand grinding, and adjusting the solid content, (iv) adding lime slurry into the carbonization tower, starting carbonization tower to stir and circulating cooling water until the temperature of the lime slurry in the tower drops to 12-27 degrees C, closing the circulating cooling water, and introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the carbonation reaction until the pH of the slurry in the carbonization tower is 7, stopping the gasification, and terminating the carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate suspension, and (v) dehydrating the calcium carbonate suspension by filter press drying, crushing and sieving.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chain-shaped calcite calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides good quality product and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing chain-shaped calcite calcium carbonate comprises (i) crushing limestone, calcining the limestone, sieving by 2 meshes standard sieve, calcining the remaining residue limestone in an high temperature furnace at 900-1100 degrees C for 300-700 minutes, controlling the digestion rate of obtain calcined lime to less than or equal to t60 for less than or equal to 1.5 minutes, and the residual carbon dioxide content less than or equal to 0.7%, (ii) mixing the obtained quicklime with water in a mass ratio of 1:5-10, stirring and digesting for 15-25 minutes in a high speed mixer to obtain lime slag, (iii) passing the lime slag through 150 meshes sieve, sand grinding for 0-10 minutes, the lime particle size distribution-D (10) is not less than 1 mu m, D (97) is not greater than 20 mu m, and adjusting the solid content of the obtained lime to 8-15%, (iv) adding lime slurry into the carbonization tower, starting carbonization tower to stir and circulating cooling water until the temperature of the lime slurry in the tower drops to 12-27 degrees C, closing the circulating cooling water, and introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into the carbonation reaction until the pH of the slurry in the carbonization tower is 7, stopping the gasification, and terminating the carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate suspension, and (v) dehydrating the calcium carbonate suspension by filter press drying, crushing and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crusher is a jaw crusher with a discharge diameter of 41 mm. The calcium carbonate suspension has a solid content of not less than 80% after being dehydrated by pressure filtration. Preferred Method: The stirring speed of the high speed mixer is 300-500 rotations/minute. The stirring speed of the carbonizing tower is 80-120 rotations/minute. In the step (iv), the carbonation reaction is carried out before 15 minutes, the concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is controlled to be 10-25%, the flow rate is 0.5-2 m3/h, the reaction time is 15 minutes, the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is controlled, the concentration is 50-100%, preferably 50-65%, and the flow rate is 1-8 m3/hours, preferably 1-4 m3/h. The amount of carbon dioxide intake air in the reaction stage of 15 minutes before the control of the calcium carbonate reaction is less than the reaction time of 15 minutes to reaction ending stage of carbon dioxide. The temperature of the slag in the carbonization tower during the reaction of the calcium carbonate is not greater than 50 degrees C. The drying temperature is 70-90 degrees C and the drying time is 6-10 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-D03A

PN CN107980467-A
TI Improving sweetness and yield of cherry by applying lime milk on trunk, irrigating with K84, applying chicken manure, illuminating with LED light, stocking bees, spraying flowers with borax, watering and spraying carbon dioxide gas.
AU WANG C
   WANG R
   LI M
   ZHAO X
   WANG S
AE HEXIAN NIEXINGXU VEGETABLE GROWING CO (HEXI-Non-standard)
GA 2018394355
AB    NOVELTY - Management method for improving sweetness and yield of cherry involves applying lime milk to the surface of the trunk after the leaves are fallen during late November-early February for preventing insect pest, irrigating the cherry tree with K84, G418 and multi-effect mixture solution, then spraying the soil disinfectant, applying chicken manure, pig manure and plant ash on the land, installing LED light in the orchard, illuminating, stocking bees, spraying the flowers with mixed solution comprising borax, pollination solution and gibberellin during the early, middle and late flowering stages, fertilizing cherry tree root with urea and potassium dihydrogen phosphate, spraying mixed solution comprising humic acid, gallium chloride, sodium tetraborate and urea, watering and spraying carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas.
   USE - The management method is useful for improving sweetness and yield of cherry.
   ADVANTAGE - The management method increases starch content in the cherry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Management method for improving sweetness and yield of cherry involves (a) applying lime milk to the surface of the trunk after the leaves are fallen during late November-early February for preventing insect pest, (b) irrigating each cherry tree with 10 mg/ml K84, 10 mg/ml G418 and 2-3 l 10-14.5 %mass multi-effect mixture solution and then spraying the soil disinfectant, (c) applying 1400-2600 kg/acre chicken manure, 1300-2500 kg/acre pig manure and 2000-3000 kg/acre plant ash on the land, (d) installing LED light in the orchard and illuminating with light intensity of 150-300 mu mol/m2/second during 8:00-10:00 am and 3:00-5:00 pm, (e) stocking 5000-6000 bees per acre, spraying the flowers with 1.5-2.5 l/tree mixed solution comprising 0.1-0.5% borax, 0.01-0.03% pollination solution and 10-15 ppm gibberellin during the early, middle and late flowering stages and fertilizing cherry tree root with 1.5-2 kg urea and 1-1.5 kg potassium dihydrogen phosphate, (f) spraying mixed solution comprising 15-20 g humic acid, 10-15 g gallium chloride, 13-19 g sodium tetraborate and 30-50 g urea once in every morning for 10 days 3 times and watering once a day with 6-10 l water for 20-30 minutes during young fruit period and (g) watering each morning and evening for 10-20 minutes and spraying 15-18 mu mol/l carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas every day at 7:00-9:00 pm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Plant: The cherry tree is a cherry tree in the fruiting period. The cherry tree is planted in an orchard with excellent ventilation and a soil pH of 6-7.5. Preferred Method: The steps (f) and (g) involves watering using an atomizing nozzle. The step (b) preparing soil disinfectant using garden spring solid powder and water in a mass ratio of 1:500 and directly spraying on the ground, where the amount of garden spring solid powder applied is 1.5-2.5 kg/acre. The step (e) involves spraying the flowers with mixed solution comprising 0.1-0.5 %mass borax, 0.01-0.03 %mass pollination solution and 10-15 ppm gibberellin. The step (g) spraying carbon dioxide from the top of the tree.

PN CN108439406-A; CN108439406-B
TI Device for preparing carbon monoxide, has carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system, electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system, pressure swing adsorption gas separation system, thermal management and gas monitoring system.
AU GUAN C
   XIAO G
   WANG J
AE SHANGHAI INST APPLIED PHYSICS CHINESE AC (CAYW-C)
GA 201868225L
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon monoxide-preparing device has carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system, electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system, pressure swing adsorption gas separation system and thermal management and gas monitoring system that is connected to carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system, electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system and pressure swing adsorption gas separation system, respectively. The carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system precipitates purified coal-fired boiler flue gas through alkaline earth metal oxide, filters and dries precipitate and decomposes to obtain carbon dioxide by heating. The electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system introduces carbon dioxide obtained by carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system into solid oxide electrolysis cell system, applies direct current and electrolyzes to obtain mixed gas.
   USE - Device for preparing carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device ensures simple and economical preparation of carbon monoxide, and is safe to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon monoxide-preparing device has carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system, an electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system, a pressure swing adsorption gas separation system and a thermal management and gas monitoring system that is connected to carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system, electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system and pressure swing adsorption gas separation system, respectively. The carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system precipitates purified coal-fired boiler flue gas through an alkaline earth metal oxide, filters and dried precipitate and decomposes to obtain carbon dioxide by heating. The electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system introduces carbon dioxide obtained by carbon dioxide absorption and regeneration system into solid oxide electrolysis cell system, applies direct current and electrolyzes to obtain mixed gas. The pressure swing adsorption gas separation system separates the mixed gas into carbon monoxide and unreacted carbon dioxide. In the CO2 absorption and regeneration system, the thermal management and gas monitoring system monitors system temperature and reaction progress. In electrolytic carbon dioxide processing system, the thermal management and gas monitoring system preheats air and recovers mixed gas and heat. In the pressure swing adsorption gas separation system, the thermal management and gas monitoring system detects flow, composition and purity of gas before and after the separation of the mixed gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparation of carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler of the absorption tower comprises alkaline earth metal oxide comprising calcium oxide, strontium oxide, barium oxide and magnesium oxide. The filler of regeneration tower comprises alkaline earth metal carbonate comprises calcium carbonate, strontium carbonate, barium carbonate and magnesium carbonate.

GA 2018284836
AB    NOVELTY - A gas treatment equipment (100) comprises a carbon dioxide absorption unit (101) for adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in a gas containing carbon dioxide using a carbon dioxide absorber (1), and producing carbonate, a carbonate decomposition unit (102), an oxygen carrier oxidation unit (104), and a carbon dioxide recovery unit (111) for recovering carbon dioxide produced by the decomposition unit. The carbon dioxide absorber and the oxygen carrier circulate through the absorption unit, decomposition unit and oxygen carrier regeneration unit.
   USE - Gas treatment equipment e.g. circulating fluidized bed gasifier for recovering carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide (claimed) e.g. waste gas discharged from industrial boiler and waste gas discharged during coal-fired electric power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment enables recovery of high-concentration carbon dioxide economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas treatment equipment comprises a carbon dioxide absorption unit for adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in a gas containing carbon dioxide using a carbon dioxide absorber as gas treatment medium, and producing carbonate, a carbonate decomposition unit for reducing oxygen carrier to produce reduced-type oxygen carrier and oxygen, and decomposing carbonate with the reaction heat produced by combustion reaction of oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, an oxygen carrier oxidation unit for oxidizing reduced-type oxygen carrier to an oxidized-type oxygen carrier with an oxidizing gas, and a carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide produced by the decomposition unit. The carbon dioxide absorber and the oxygen carrier circulate through the absorption unit, decomposition unit and oxygen carrier regeneration unit. The carbon dioxide absorber contains an alkali metal oxide and/or alkaline earth metal oxide. The oxygen carrier contains a transition metal oxide, and carries out state change to oxidized and reduced states. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas treatment method.

PN JP2019178051-A
TI Apparatus used for manufacturing carbon dioxide, consists of gas circulation line, heat exchanger, reactor, splitter, raw material powder supply line, carbon dioxide recovery line, and reaction temperature-reducing unit.
AU SHIMA H
   KOMATSU T
   TAKADA Y
   TAKAYAMA Y
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 201986439K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus (10) consists of a gas circulation line (11) for circulating a carbon dioxide-containing gas (G), a heat exchanger (12,15), a reactor (13), and a splitter (14) arranged on the gas circulation line in this order, a raw material powder supply line (21) connected to the gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery line (22), and a reaction temperature-reducing unit (23). The heat exchanger performs heat exchange between the gas circulation line and a high-temperature atmosphere region maintained at a temperature higher than the gas circulation line. The raw material powder supply line is connected between the reactor and the heat exchanger or connected to the reactor, and introduces a raw material powder (M) for generating carbon dioxide into the gas circulation line. The reactor generates carbon dioxide by a decarboxylation reaction carried out by contacting the generated raw material powder with the carbon dioxide-containing gas.
   USE - Apparatus is used for manufacturing carbon dioxide (claimed) used as food material and for growing plant and algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus provides high-concentration carbon dioxide with reduced content of toxic substances, by economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus consists of a gas circulation line for circulating a carbon dioxide-containing gas, a heat exchanger, a reactor, and a splitter arranged on the gas circulation line in this order, a raw material powder supply line connected to the gas circulation line, a carbon dioxide recovery line, and a reaction temperature-reducing unit. The heat exchanger performs heat exchange between the gas circulation line and a high-temperature atmosphere region (H) maintained at a temperature higher than the gas circulation line. The raw material powder supply line is connected between the reactor and the heat exchanger or connected to the reactor, and introduces a raw material powder for generating carbon dioxide into the gas circulation line. The reactor generates carbon dioxide by a decarboxylation reaction carried out by contacting the generated raw material powder with the carbon dioxide-containing gas heated by the heat exchanger. The splitter separates the generated raw material powder and the carbon dioxide-containing gas. The reaction temperature-reducing unit lowers the reaction temperature of the decarboxylation reaction in the reactor. The carbon dioxide recovery line recovers the carbon dioxide-containing gas having a carbon dioxide concentration of 95 vol.% or more from the system of the gas circulation line. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of carbon dioxide, which involves heating the carbon dioxide-containing gas using the heat exchanger in the high-temperature atmosphere region, carrying out decarboxylation reaction for generating carbon dioxide by contacting the carbon dioxide-containing gas directly with the generated raw material powder, separating the generated raw material powder and the carbon dioxide-containing gas, recovering the carbon dioxide-containing gas in an amount corresponding to the amount of carbon dioxide produced during the reaction, and refluxing the remaining gas containing carbon dioxide to the gas circulation line; and
   (2) method for designing carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus, which involves determining the reaction pressure and the supply amount of the generated raw material powder to the gas circulation line based on the internal pressure of the gas circulation line and the temperature of the carbon dioxide-containing gas after heating, during the decarboxylation reaction, such that the generation amount of carbon dioxide becomes plus minus 10% with respect to the maximum value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of carbon dioxide manufacturing apparatus.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Manufacture of carbon dioxide further involves setting the reaction pressure of the decarboxylation reaction to a pressure lower than the atmospheric pressure using the reaction temperature-reducing unit.

biomass scrubber to remove alkali metal salt contained in the biomass, washing the solid biomass raw material to a drying furnace for drying, the washing water containing alkali metal salt solution into the washing water tank for storing, after the alkali metal concentration adjusting device and pressurizing washing water pressurizing device, dividing into two paths, one path is injected conversion device, the other path of the injected hydrogen storage active carbon preparing device, (2) after drying the solid biomass raw material enters the pyrolysis gasification furnace, spraying oxygen with the furnace bottom and the furnace side into steam pyrolysis and gasification reaction to obtain high temperature crude synthesis gas and biomass carbon, the main component of the high temperature crude synthesis gas is methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and generation of biomass carbon enters the hydrogen storage activated carbon preparation device, under the catalysis of the alkali metal containing salt solution, activation reaction occurs with oxygen and carbon dioxide, obtaining high-purity hydrogen storage activated carbon, (3) methane and oxygen in the high temperature crude synthesis gas in the synthesis gas reformer device react to generate carbon monoxide, removing hydrogen, then enters in the steam generator heat exchange for cooling, pressurizing synthesis gas compression device and enters heat exchanger device, exchanging in conversion device under the containing alkali metal salt washing liquid of the catalytic effect of the injected water vapor in the raw synthesis gas of carbon monoxide and sprayed for reaction, and are converted into carbon dioxide and hydrogen, then entering the carbon dioxide removing device for PN US2021047568-A1
TI Method for flexible pyrolysis by flowing inlet stream of carbon dioxide in supercritical state into reaction chamber containing coal, removing carbon dioxide, obtaining effluent stream and pyrolysis product condensate, and reconditioning.
AU GOODRICH B L
   WALTER J C
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 202122101K
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises: flowing an inlet stream of carbon dioxide in a supercritical state into a reaction chamber containing coal; removing supercritical carbon dioxide containing dissolved pyrolysis products from the reaction chamber as an outlet stream; directing a first portion of the outlet stream through a first recuperator/collector stage in which the outlet stream is cooled by a return stream of carbon dioxide en route to the reaction chamber, to obtain a first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a first stage pyrolysis product condensate; directing at least a portion of the first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream to obtain a second stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a second stage pyrolysis product condensate; and reconditioning carbon dioxide from the first stage or the second stage carbon dioxide effluent steams by passing the some carbon dioxide as the return stream through the first recuperator/collector stage to obtain the inlet carbon dioxide stream.
   USE - The method is used for flexible pyrolysis and is used for generating pitch from coal and for pyrolyzing coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls pyrolysis reaction to alter the pyrolysis products generated, improves the relative amount of pitch produced from a given feedstock, and significantly reduces energy consumption during reaction product-supercritical fluid separation. The method maintains carbon dioxide in the supercritical state throughout the entire method, and recycles carbon dioxide and reuses it for additional pyrolysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises: flowing an inlet stream of carbon dioxide in a supercritical state into a reaction chamber containing coal; removing, after a contact time, supercritical carbon dioxide containing dissolved pyrolysis products from the reaction chamber as an outlet stream; directing a first portion of the outlet stream through a first recuperator/collector stage in which the outlet stream is cooled by a return stream of carbon dioxide en route to the reaction chamber, the first recuperator/collector stage including a recuperator followed by a condensate collector, to obtain a first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a first stage pyrolysis product condensate; directing at least a portion of the first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream through a second recuperator/collector stage to obtain a second stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a second stage pyrolysis product condensate; and reconditioning at least some carbon dioxide from the first stage carbon dioxide effluent stream or the second stage carbon dioxide effluent steam by passing the some carbon dioxide as the return stream through the first recuperator/collector stage to obtain the inlet carbon dioxide stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: directing a second portion of the outlet stream through the second recuperator/collector stage; maintaining the reaction chamber at a pyrolysis temperature and pressure sufficient to maintain the carbon dioxide in the reaction chamber in a supercritical state, and pyrolyzing the coal to obtain a char and supercritical carbon dioxide containing dissolved pyrolysis products. The method further comprises: flowing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide and dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a third recuperator/collector stage to obtain a third stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a third stage pyrolysis product condensate; flowing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide and dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a fourth recuperator/collector stage to obtain a fourth stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a fourth stage pyrolysis product condensate; and flowing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide and dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a fifth recuperator/collector stage to obtain a fifth stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a fifth stage pyrolysis product condensate. The method further comprises: flowing at least a portion of the carbon dioxide and dissolved pyrolysis product stream through a fourth recuperator/collector stage to obtain a fourth stage carbon dioxide effluent stream and a fourth stage pyrolysis product condensate.Preferred Conditions: The inlet carbon dioxide stream's temperature is 300-600degreesC and pressure is 7-30 MPa. The first, second, third, fourth and fifth recuperator/collector stages all operate at different temperatures and the method further comprises: controlling the different temperatures of the recuperator/collector stages by setting a position of one or more bypass valves. The first, second, and third recuperator/collector stages operate at different temperatures. The first, second, third, and fourth recuperator/collector stages operate at different temperatures.

   ACP SYSTEMS AG (ACPS-Non-standard)
GA 202106115Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding liquid carbon dioxide into an expansion duct (8) through an inlet opening (24). The liquid carbon dioxide in the expansion channel is expanded, in such a way that liquid carbon dioxide is converted into a carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture (10). The CARBON carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture emerged from an outlet opening (28) of the expansion channel is outputted. A carrier gas is used to generate a jacket jet nozzle (14). A pulsating carbon dioxide snow jet (4) is generated. A carbon dioxide valve unit (32) releasing or blocking the supply of liquid CARBON DIOXIDE into the expansion duct is opened for an opening period (34) and closed for a closing period (36). The opening duration and the closing duration are selected from one another and the opening duration and the closing duration are utilized to control the carbon dioxide valve unit.
   USE - Method for generating carbon dioxide snow jet used in gentle cleaning of surface e.g. workpiece surfaces.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide snow blasting enables continuous operation for automation. The cleaning effect of CARBON DIOXIDE snow blasting is cleaned. Economical mode of operation is achieved. Reliable and quick opening or closing is enabled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carrier gas valve unit (52) is provided for optionally releasing or blocking the supply of the carrier gas into the jacket jet nozzle which is periodically opened for an opening period (54) and closed for a closing period (56). The opening duration and the closing duration are selected separately from one another and the opening duration and the closing duration are used to control the carrier gas valve unit. The opening period and the closing period of the carbon dioxide valve unit and the opening period and the closing period of the carrier gas valve unit are provided with specific value. The opening period and the closing period of the carbon dioxide valve unit and the opening period and the closing period of the carrier gas valve unit are selected. The opening time of the carbon dioxide valve unit is set as 120 ms and that the closing time of the carbon dioxide valve unit is set as 80 ms. An opening switching time (44) and a closing switching time (46) of the carbon dioxide valve unit are selected and used to control the carbon dioxide valve unit and an opening switching time (58) and a closing switching time (60) of the carrier gas valve unit selected and used to control the carrier gas valve unit. The carbon dioxide valve unit and the carrier gas valve unit or the common valve unit are opened and closed. The carbon dioxide valve unit and the carrier gas valve unit or the common valve unit is controlled by an electrical or pneumatic control signal. A cleaning effect is adapted in the course of a cleaning process by the carbon dioxide snow jet. The opening duration or the closing duration of the carbon dioxide valve unit and the opening duration or the closing duration of the carrier gas valve unit is changed. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device (2) for generating carbon dioxide snow jet, has a blasting tool comprising expansion channel for generating a carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture based on liquid carbon dioxide and an expansion channel. The expansion duct has an inlet opening for supplying liquid carbon dioxide and an outlet opening for discharging the carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture. The jacket jet nozzle is designed in such a way that the jacket jet surrounds the carbon dioxide gas/carbon dioxide snow mixture that is discharged from the outlet opening of the expansion duct. A carbon dioxide valve unit interacts with a supply device for liquid CARBON DIOXIDE to release a supply of liquid carbon dioxide into the expansion channel in an open state and to block in a closed state. A carbon dioxide control device (48) controls the carbon dioxide valve unit. A first selection device selects an opening duration and closing duration of the carbon dioxide valve unit. The common valve unit comprises a valve piston and a valve needle. The valve piston is slidably mounted in a blasting tool housing between a piston open position and a piston closed position. The valve needle is mounted displaceably between a needle open position and a needle closed position. The valve piston is coupled to the valve needle. The valve needle is transferred from the needle open position to the needle closed position, when the valve piston is moved from the piston open position into the piston closed position. The valve piston is moved from the piston open position into the piston closed position, when the valve needle is moved from the needle open position into the needle closed position.

TI Power generation method for producing electricity or shaft power and for capturing carbon dioxide, involves capturing carbon dioxide, contained in cooled carbon-dioxide-rich exhaust gas, in carbon dioxide capture unit.
AU KOEIJER G M D
   RORTVEIT G J
AE EQUINOR ENERGY AS (DENO-C)
GA 201974486F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves combusting a first fuel in a reciprocating engine (2) to generate electricity or shaft power and an exhaust gas (4). The exhaust gas is then divided into a first portion (6) and a second portion (5). The first portion of the exhaust gas is recycled into the reciprocating engine. A second fuel is oxidized in the presence of the second portion of the exhaust gas to produce a carbon-dioxide-rich exhaust gas (7). The carbon-dioxide-rich exhaust gas is then cooled. The carbon dioxide contained in the carbon-dioxide-rich exhaust gas is then captured in a carbon dioxide capture unit.
   USE - Power generation method for producing electricity or shaft power and for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a power generation method that uses a more compact and efficient system for producing electricity or shaft power and for capturing carbon dioxide. Improves space and energy efficiency by utilizing a reciprocating engine and a catalytic oxidation unit to increase the carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust gas, from where the carbon dioxide is ultimately captured. Ensures increased carbon dioxide concentration in the exhaust, and increased power output.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a power generation system for producing electricity or shaft power and for capturing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the power generation system for producing electricity or shaft power and for capturing carbon dioxide.
   Reciprocating engine (2)
   Exhaust gas (4)
   Second portion of exhaust gas (5)
   First portion of exhaust gas (6)
   Carbon-dioxide-rich exhaust gas (7)

PN US2022325649-A1
TI Bottoming cycle power system i.e. inverted Brayton            bottoming cycle power system, for use in combined power            system for delivering e.g. electrical power, in e.g.            mobile applications, has flow path operated to receive            flow of gas from turbo-compressor after gas is            compressed by turbo-compressor.
AU SCUDERI S C
AE SCUDERI GROUP INC (SCUD-C)
GA 2022C9109P
AB    NOVELTY - The system (104) has a turbine-generator                comprising a turbo-expander (122) and a                turbo-compressor (124) that are arranged on a                turbo-crankshaft (126). The turbo-expander is                operated to rotate the turbo-crankshaft as a flow                of cooled exhaust gas (116) from combustion process                passes through the turbo-expander. The                turbo-compressor is operated to compress the flow                of exhaust gas after the exhaust gas passes through                the turbo-expander. An exhaust gas heat exchanger                (106) comprises first flow path (130) and second                flow path (132) that are operated to exchange heat                between the first and second flow paths. The first                flow path is operated to receive the flow of gas                from the turbo-expander to the gas being compressed                by the turbo-compressor. The second flow path is                operated to receive the flow of gas from the                turbo-compressor after the exhaust gas is                compressed by the turbo-compressor. A capture tank                contains carbon dioxide absorbent material to                absorb carbon dioxide from the gas.
   USE - Bottoming cycle power system i.e. inverted                Brayton bottoming cycle power system, for use in a                combined power system (claimed) delivering power                such as mechanical or electrical power in                stationary and/or mobile applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The power system efficiently reduces specific                volume of flow of exhaust gas after exiting the                turbo-expander and prior to entering the                turbo-compressor. The system reduces work required                of the turbo-compressor to increase working                efficiency of the power system. The system reduces                energy required in a carbon dioxide capture system                to remove the carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas                so as to maintain working efficiency of an internal                combustion engine associated with the power system.                The power system efficiently reduces volume and                mass of water vapor in a flow of the exhaust gas                prior to entering the turbo-compressor. The power                system makes near ambient temperature of the                exhaust gas to enable the turbo-compressor to                efficiently pump the exhaust gas back to                atmospheric pressure or greater with a reduced work                burden on the turbo-expander. The power system                reduces the energy needed from external sources                i.e. electric heaters, to regenerate the carbon                dioxide by using heat of compression of the carbon                dioxide compressor to regenerate the carbon dioxide                in a capture tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                combined power system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                combined power system with a primary power system                and a bottoming cycle power system.

AB    NOVELTY - Controlling of invasion of stratum carbon dioxide into wellbore involves e.g. (i) real-time monitoring the presence of carbon dioxide in the stratum at the bottom of the wellbore during the drilling, through downhole hydrocarbon detection tools, (ii) monitoring the downhole leakage if the stratum carbon dioxide is not detected, (iii) maintaining the bottom hole pressure constant if the downhole leakage cannot be monitored, (iv) adjusting the bottom hole pressure if downhole leakage is detected, (v) performing monitoring whether there is stratum carbon dioxide intrusion into the wellbore at the bottom of the well until the downhole leakage cannot be monitored, and until there is no stratum carbon dioxide in the wellbore or lost circulation, (vi) adjusting bottom hole pressure if stratum carbon dioxide is detected, (vii) monitoring the downhole leakage until no carbon dioxide from the stratum enters the wellbore, and (vii) discharging carbon dioxide invaded into the wellbore.
   USE - Controlling of invasion of stratum carbon dioxide into wellbore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling of invasion of stratum carbon dioxide into wellbore by finding out whether there is stratum carbon dioxide entering the wellbore at the first time, quickly changing the bottom hole pressure through surface control equipment, and simultaneously forcing carbon dioxide to stop entering the wellbore, and discharging carbon dioxide that is entered into the wellbore to achieve safe and efficient drilling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling of invasion of stratum carbon dioxide into wellbore involves (i) real-time monitoring the presence of carbon dioxide in the stratum at the bottom of the wellbore during the drilling process, through downhole hydrocarbon detection tools, (ii) monitoring the downhole leakage if the stratum carbon dioxide is not detected, (iii) maintaining the bottom hole pressure constant if the downhole leakage cannot be monitored, (iv) adjusting the bottom hole pressure if downhole leakage is detected, (v) performing the steps of monitoring whether there is stratum carbon dioxide intrusion into the wellbore at the bottom of the well until the downhole leakage cannot be monitored, and until there is no stratum carbon dioxide in the wellbore or lost circulation, (vi) adjusting bottom hole pressure if stratum carbon dioxide is detected, (vii) monitoring the downhole leakage until no carbon dioxide from the stratum enters the wellbore, (vii) discharging carbon dioxide invaded into the wellbore if the downhole leakage cannot be monitored, (viii) adjusting the bottom hole pressure if downhole leakage is detected, (ix) performing the steps of monitoring whether there is stratum carbon dioxide intrusion into the wellbore at the bottom of the well, and (x) discharging the carbon dioxide invaded into the wellbore until there is no stratum carbon dioxide in the wellbore or lost circulation.

AU CHEN H
   XU S

GA 2021983845
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and purification of high-purity liquid carbon dioxide comprises vaporizing liquid carbon dioxide to make it gas, storing liquid carbon dioxide in steel cylinder, passing through pressure-increasing valve to vaporize into gas and becomes carbon dioxide, passing carbon dioxide raw material gas into spray tower, spraying, filtering to remove dust, passing raw material gas into dehydration device, adding concentrated sulfuric acid to dry carbon dioxide gas, sending dehydrated dioxide to interior of desulfurization tower to remove hydrogen sulfide, adding desulfurization agent and adsorbent, passing carbon dioxide into first purification tower with molecular sieves and activated carbon to absorb impurities in gas and treat odors and organic matter, passing gas into second purification tower equipped with high-efficiency particulate air filter, and passing carbon dioxide gas through filter to effectively filter small solid particles.
   USE - The method is used for separating and purifying high-purity liquid carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that carbon dioxide contains moisture inside, and existing purification method cannot remove moisture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: When carbon dioxide gas is compressed, it is controlled below 60 MPa. The water temperature in spray tower is controlled to 25-35 degrees C. The filtration value of high-efficiency particulate air filter used is 0.1-0.3 mu m. Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is artificially synthesized screening molecules with strong adsorption, molecular sieving, ion exchange, and catalysis, it is also called natural zeolite and is also natural aluminosilicate because of its appearance and structure, where there are multiple pores with uniform pore diameter and neatly arranged pores, and molecular sieves with different pore diameters will be separated to separate molecules of different sizes. The molecular sieve is 2A-4A molecular sieve. Preferred Composition: The added amount of desulfurization agent is 10-20 pts. wt. and amount of adsorbent is 15-20 pts. wt. The added amount of activated carbon is 13-19 pts. wt.

AU HE D
   ZHAO Y

AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical carbon dioxide device comprises a high-purity carbon dioxide cylinder (H), carbon dioxide cooler (C), carbon dioxide pump (B), a pressure gauge (P) and valve (K1). The entrainer container (CS) and the entrainer pump (U) generate high pressure fluid. Supercritical carbon dioxide of No.1 reaction kettle (A1), a pressure gauge (P1) No.1, a reaction kettle, a temperature gauge (T1), the inlet valve (K2) and an outlet valve (K4) are included. A pressure gauge (P2) balances the supercritical carbon dioxide pressure of the No.2 reaction kettle (A2). Temperature gauge (T2), inlet valve (K5) and outlet valve (K3) are included. A connection pipeline is provided with a flowmeter (L). A separator (S) is connected with the top part of the No.2 reaction kettle. Medlar nutritious ingredients are extracted through the separator under the discharge port (E3). A small amount of carbon dioxide is discharged through outlet (E2).
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide device used for extracting medlar nutrient content.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple operation, strong stability, and high production efficiency.

TI System for use in beverage dispensing unit and            dispense kit for dispensing chilled beverage, comprises            secondary regulators for feeding regulated pressure            nitrogen gas to liquid/gas contactor unit and beverage            pump, and beverage faucet attached downstream to            liquid/gas contactor unit.
AU KLEINRICHERT C
AE AUTOMATIC BAR CONTROLS INC (AUTO-Non-standard)
GA 2022C9452Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a liquid/gas contactor                unit (12), a beverage pump (10), a beverage tank                (14) in liquid communication with the liquid/gas                contactor unit through the beverage pump, a cooling                or refrigeration system configured to cool the                beverage tank, a controlled pressurized supply of a                regulated pressure nitrogen gas (4) comprising at                least 50 wt.% nitrogen (N2), one or more secondary                regulators for feeding the regulated pressure                nitrogen gas to the liquid/gas contactor unit and                the beverage pump, and a beverage faucet (3)                attached downstream to the liquid/gas contactor                unit configured to dispense the cooled                nitrogen-infused noncarbonated beverage at a                controlled rate. The beverage tank is pressurized.                The beverage pump is configured to transfer a                cooled noncarbonated beverage from the beverage                tank to the liquid/gas contactor unit.
   USE - System for use in beverage dispensing unit and                dispense kit for dispensing chilled dispensed                nitrogen or nitrogen/carbon dioxide infused                beverage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables to infuse a chilled                beverage with N2 or a mixed gas and then dispense                that gas infused chilled beverage into a receiver                such as a glass or mug so that the dispensed                chilled beverage has a flavor, odor and appearance                enhanced by the gas infusion and beverage                dispensation process. The system is also cost                effective and user friendly for utilization and                maintenance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system comprises a liquid/gas contactor                unit (12), a beverage pump (10), a beverage tank                (14) in liquid communication with the liquid/gas                contactor unit through the beverage pump, a cooling                or refrigeration system configured to cool the                beverage tank, a controlled pressurized supply of a                regulated pressure nitrogen gas (4) comprising at                least 50 wt.% nitrogen (N2), one or more secondary                regulators for feeding the regulated pressure                nitrogen gas to the liquid/gas contactor unit and                the beverage pump, and a beverage faucet (3)                attached downstream to the liquid/gas contactor                unit configured to dispense the cooled                nitrogen-infused noncarbonated beverage at a                controlled rate. The beverage tank is pressurized.                The beverage pump is configured to transfer a                cooled noncarbonated beverage from the beverage                tank to the liquid/gas contactor unit. The cooled                noncarbonated beverage contacts the regulated                pressure nitrogen gas in the liquid/gas contactor                unit to dissolve the regulated pressure nitrogen                gas in the cooled noncarbonated beverage to form a                cooled nitrogen-infused noncarbonated                beverage.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: i. a                beverage dispensing unit; ii. a dispense kit; iii.                a chilled dispensed N2 or N2/carbon dioxide (CO2)                infused beverage; and iv. system for dispensing a                cooled beverage, which comprises a bag-in-box                beverage container in liquid communication with the                liquid/gas contactor unit through the beverage                pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system having a beverage tank.
   1Dispense tower
   3Beverage faucet
   4Controlled pressurized supply of a regulated                pressure nitrogen gas

   14Beverage tank

PN EP4056664-A1; WO2022189288-A1
TI Nitride-free solid composition used for preparing            e.g. refrigerant, comprises water, alkylene glycol,            alkylene glycol monoalkyl ether and glycerol, inhibitor            e.g. benzoic acid, organic dicarboxylic acid and            triazole compound, optionally inorganic base, and            component e.g. hard water stabilizer.
AU DIETL H
   NITZSCHKE U
   SANDOR M
   SCHMITZ R
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2022B64436
AB    NOVELTY - A nitride-free solid composition comprises 10                wt.% or less, preferably 5 wt.% or less water (a),                10 wt.% or less, preferably 5 wt.% or less alkylene                glycol, alkylene glycol monoalkyl ether and                glycerol (b), an inhibitor (c) comprising inorganic                compound(s) (c1) chosen from silicate, borate,                nitrate, molybdate and phosphate, optionally                benzoic acid (c2) as aromatic monocarboxylic acid,                4-20C organic dicarboxylic acid(s) (c3) and                azole(s) compound (c4), preferably triazole                compound(s), optionally inorganic base(s) (d), and                component(s) (e) chosen from hard water stabilizer,                defoamer, dye and bitter substance. The composition                comprises 90-95 wt.% components (c)-(e) in total, 1                wt.% or less aliphatic monocarboxylic acid and 0.25                wt.% or less nitrite.
   USE - Nitride-free solid composition is used for                preparing concentrate, super-concentrate and                refrigerant for reducing emissions e.g. nitrogen                oxide emissions, sulfur oxide emissions and carbon                dioxide emissions (all claimed) from mobile or                stationary internal combustion engine of electric                vehicle, or combination of vehicle with electric                motor and internal combustion engine, and liquid                antifreeze agent for anticorrosion cooling                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitride-free solid composition is stable,                and does not corrode ferrous materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (i) preparation of the solid                composition, which involves mixing the components                (a)-(e) in suitable apparatuses at 10-100&#176;                C for 5 minutes to 10 hours until a homogeneous                mixture is formed, optionally screening of the                homogeneous mixture, and subsequently processing                into molded product; (ii) preparation of a                super-concentrate, which involves mixing the                components (a)-(e). The super-concentrate comprises                15 wt.% or less, preferably 5 wt.% or less                component (a), 0.5-30 wt.%, preferably 2-10 wt.%                components (c)-(e) in total, 0.6 wt.% or less                aliphatic monocarboxylic acid, 0.2 wt.% or less                nitrite, and remainder of component (b); (iii)                preparation of a concentrate, which involves adding                the component (b) to the solid composition to                obtain super-concentrate, and adding the component                (a). The concentrate comprises 15 wt.% or less,                preferably 5 wt.% or less component (a), 0.1-10                wt.%, preferably 1-5 wt.% components (c)-(e) in                total, 0.4 wt.% or less aliphatic monocarboxylic                acid, 0.1 wt.% or less nitrite, and remainder of                component (b); (iv) preparation of refrigerant,                which involves mixing the components (a)-(e). The                refrigerant comprises 40 wt.% or more component                (a), 30 wt.% or more component (b), up to 10 wt.%,                preferably up to 5 wt.% components (c)-(e) in                total, 0.2 wt.% or less aliphatic monocarboxylic                acid and 0.05 wt.% or less nitrite; and (v) method                for reducing emissions e.g. nitrogen oxide                emissions, sulfur oxide emissions and carbon                dioxide emissions, preferably determined as carbon                footprint, life cycle assessment or in accordance                with , , here in particular issue 2019-02, , here                in particular the issue 2006+A1:2018 and/or , here                in particular the issue 2009-11, which involves                producing the solid composition at a location (l1)                and transporting the location (l1) to a location                (l2), and producing super-concentrate and/or                concentrate using the solid composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The component (b) is                  preferably monoethylene glycol. The component                  (c1) is preferably silicate, borate, nitrate or                  phosphate. The composition does not contain                  aromatic monocarboxylic acid. The component (c3)                  is 6-12C linear alkanedicarboxylic acid,                  preferably chosen from succinic acid, maleic                  acid, fumaric acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid,                  pimelic acid (heptanedioic acid), azelaic acid                  (nonanedioic acid), sebacic acid (decanedioic                  acid), undecanedioic acid, dodecanedioic acid,                  alkyl- and alkenyl-succinic acid and glutaric                  acid, such as 2-methylbutanedioic acid,                  2-ethyl-3-methylbutanedioic acid,                  2-ethylpentanedioic acid, 2-dodecylbutanedioic                  acid, 2-dodecenylbutanedioic acid,                  2-phenylbutanedioic acid,                  2-(p-methylphenyl)butanedioic acid,                  2,2-dimethylbutanedioic acid,                  2,3,4-trimethylpentanedioic acid,                  2,2,3-trimethylpentanedioic acid, glutaconic acid                  (pent-2-enedioic acid), itaconic acid,                  hex-2-enedioic acid, hex-3-enedioic acid,                  5-methyl-hex-2-enedioic acid and                  2,3-dimethyl-pent-2-enedioic acid. The component                  (c4) is preferably chosen from benzotriazole,                  tolutriazole, (2-benzothiazylthio)acetic acid,                  3-(2-benzothiazylthio)propionic acid and                  2-mercaptobenzothiazole. The refrigerant                  comprises 15 wt.% or less, preferably 5 wt.% or                  less component (a), 0.05-30 wt.%, preferably 2-10                  wt.% components (c)-(e) in total, 0.6 wt.% or                  less aliphatic monocarboxylic acid, 0.2 wt.% or                  less nitrite and remainder of component (b), or                  comprises 15 wt.% or less, preferably 5 wt.% or                  less component (a), 0.01-10 wt.%, preferably 1-5                  wt.% components (c)-(e) in total, 0.4 wt.% or                  less aliphatic monocarboxylic acid, 0.1 wt.% or                  less nitrite and remainder of component (b).                  Preferred Process: The preparation of                  super-concentrate, concentrate and refrigerant                  involves adding the components (a)-(e) of the                  solid composition and mixing application of                  shearing energy.

PN WO2022176709-A1; JP2022125620-A
TI Recovery of valuable metals e.g. lithium, involves            e.g. extracting battery slag from lithium ion battery            waste, acid-leaching, adding sulfur compound,            filtering, adding sulfur compound to filtrate,            filtering, and adding calcium hydroxide to            filtrate.
AU KENTA K
   ATSUSHI M
   HIROKI M
   KURAMOCHI K
   MIYAZAKI A
   MURAOKA H
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
   MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2022A8549M
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of valuable metals involves                extracting (S3) battery slag (M) from lithium ion                battery waste, acid-leaching (S4) to form a                leachate (E), adding (S5) a sulfur compound to the                leachate to produce a processed product (P1),                filtering (S6) the product (P1) to separate into a                processed filtrate (F1) and a processed residue                (R1), adding (S7) a sulfur compound to the filtrate                (F1) to produce a processed product (P2), filtering                (S8) the product (P2) to separate it into a                processed filtrate (F2) and a processed residue                (R2), adding (S9) calcium hydroxide to the filtrate                (F2) to produce a processed product (P3), filtering                (S10) the product (P3) to separate it into a                processed filtrate (F3) and a processed residue                (R3), adding (S11) sodium carbonate to the filtrate                (F3) to produce a processed product (P4), filtering                (S12) the product (P4) to separate it into a                processed filtrate (F4) and a residue (R4), adding                (S14) carbon dioxide gas (G) or carbonate salt to                produce, and filtering.
   USE - Recovery of valuable metals e.g. lithium,                nickel and cobalt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently separates lithium                component contained in the lithium ion battery by                separating sodium component and calcium component                contained in the additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of valuable metals involves                extracting (S3) battery slag (M) from lithium ion                battery waste, acid-leaching (S4) by adding acid                (S) to the battery slag to form a leachate (E),                adding (S5) a sulfur compound containing sodium as                an additive (A1) to the leachate to produce a                processed product (P1), filtering (S6) the product                (P1) to separate into a processed filtrate (F1) and                a processed residue (R1) containing copper, adding                (S7) a sulfur compound containing sodium as an                additive (A2) to the filtrate (F1) to produce a                processed product (P2), filtering (S8) the product                (P2) to separate it into a processed filtrate (F2)                and a processed residue (R2) containing cobalt                and/or nickel, adding (S9) calcium hydroxide as an                additive (A3) to the filtrate (F2) to produce a                processed product (P3), filtering (S10) the product                (P3) to separate it into a processed filtrate (F3)                containing lithium and a processed residue (R3),                adding (S11) sodium carbonate as an additive (A4)                to the filtrate (F3) to produce a processed product                (P4), filtering (S12) the product (P4) to separate                it into a processed filtrate (F4) and a processed                residue (R4) containing calcium, heating (S13) the                filtrate (F4), adding (S14) carbon dioxide gas (G)                into the heated filtrate or adding a carbonate salt                to produce a processed product (P5), and filtering                (S15) the product (P5) to separate it into a                processed filtrate (P5) containing sodium and a                processed residue (R5) containing lithium. The pH                of the product (P2) is higher than the pH of the                product (P1). The pH of the product (P3) is higher                than the pH of the product (P2). An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for valuable metal recovery                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of valuable                metal recovery method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   S3Extraction process
   S4Acid leaching process
   S5,S7,S9,S11,S14Adding processes
   S6,S8,S10,S12,S15Filtering processes
   S13Heating process
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid (S) is                  sulfuric acid (preferred) and hydrogen peroxide.                  The battery slag contains a positive electrode                  active material containing nickel and/or cobalt,                  and a negative electrode active material                  containing graphite.

PN DE102021104444-A1
TI Beverage useful as energy drink for e.g.            increasing blood sugar level, comprises water and            isomaltulose, polyphenols, L-theanine, procyanidin,            caffeine, steviol glycoside, glucuronolactone, carbonic            acid and/or citric acid.
AU VOLKMANN A P
AE BETTERGY GMBH (BETT-Non-standard)
GA 2022A8325M
AB    NOVELTY - Beverage comprises water and additionally                five, six, seven, eight or nine ingredients chosen                from isomaltulose, polyphenols, L-theanine,                procyanidin, caffeine, steviol glycoside,                glucuronolactone, carbonic acid and citric                acid.
   USE - The beverage is useful as energy drink which                is used for increasing the blood sugar level,                caffeine content in the blood and the level of                L-theanine in the blood (all claimed) and as sports                drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage is tooth-friendly, comprises only                natural flavorings and is free from synthetic                sweeteners and/or substantially free of sucrose.                The blood sugar level, caffeine content in the                blood and level of L-theanine in the blood 60                minutes after administration is increased to more                than 50% of the value present 30 minutes after                administration, while administering 2-5 ml drink                per 1 kg body weight, preferably 3.571 ml drink per                1 kg body weight. The beverage has a long-lasting                invigorating effect which does not occur with the                standard formulations and has a glycemic index of                25-37, preferably 32, a glycemic load of 2-3,                preferably 2.4 to 2.5, a calorific value of 120-150                kJ/100 ml, preferably 129-132 kJ/100 ml and an                osmolality of 300-350 mOsm/kg, preferably 310-320                mOsm/kg which supports performance in endurance                sports and/or weight training.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   use of (a) citric acid in a beverage                comprising water and additionally five, six, seven,                eight or nine ingredients chosen from isomaltulose,                polyphenols, L-theanine, procyanidin, caffeine,                steviol glycoside, glucuronolactone, carbonic acid,                citric acid for masking, the taste of steviol                glycoside and the taste of green tea extract;
   use of green tea extract as a source of                caffeine and L-theanine in a beverage;
   use of guarana extract as a source of                caffeine, preferably as a source of caffeine and                procyanidin in a beverage; and
   process for producing a beverage involves (a)                producing or providing (i) isomaltulose, preferably                as a powder comprising isomaltulose, (ii)                polyphenols, preferably as a powder comprising                L-theanine, (iii) procyanidin, preferably as a                powder comprising caffeine, (iv) steviol glycoside,                preferably as a powder comprising stevioside and                rebaudioside A, (v) citric acid, preferably as a                powder comprising citric acid, (vi)                glucuronolactone, preferably as a powder comprising                glucuronolactone, (vi) carbon dioxide, preferably                liquefied carbon dioxide in a pressurized gas                container and (vii) water, preferably liquid water,                (viii) polyphenols and L-theanine, preferably                polyphenols and L-theanine and at least a                proportion of caffeine as components of a green tea                extract and/or (ix) procyanidin, preferably                procyanidin and at least a proportion of caffeine                as a component of guarana extract, (b) contacting                and mixing together in one or more sub-steps the                ingredients prepared or provided in step (a),                preferably powdered ingredients, isomaltulose,                polyphenols, L-theanine, procyanidin, caffeine,                steviol glycoside, citric acid and glucuronolactone                such that a mixture, preferably a powder mixture,                where the contact-mixing involves contact-mixing of                powdered green tea extract and powdered guarana                extract, (c) contacting and mixing the mixture of                step (b), preferably the powder mixture of step                (b), in liquid water, to obtain a liquid mixture,                and (d) carbonating the liquid mixture of step (c)                to obtain in a carbonated drink.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The beverage is an                  energy drink in accordance with . The beverage                  comprises caffeine and L-theanine in a mass ratio                  of 2:0.8-2:1.5, preferably 2:1. The beverage                  comprises (a) isomaltulose and caffeine, and                  preferably L-theanine, (b) isomaltulose and                  steviol glycoside, and preferably citric acid,                  (c) 5-35 mg/100 ml, preferably 25 mg/100 ml                  caffeine, (d) 5-35 mg/100 ml, preferably 12.5                  mg/100 ml L-theanine, (e) 0.01-0.02 wt.%,                  preferably 0.015 wt.% steviol glycoside, (f)                  0.03-0.2 wt.%, preferably 0.1 wt.%                  glucuronolactone, (g) 4-12 wt.%, preferably 6-7.9                  wt.% isomaltulose, and/or (h) 0.1-1 wt.% citric                  acid. The ratio of the total mass of steviol                  glycoside to the total mass of isomaltulose is                  1:500-1:520, preferably 1:506.66 and/or the total                  mass of citric acid to the total mass of                  isomaltulose is 1:10-1:25, preferably 1:19 and/or                  the total mass of steviol glycoside to the total                  mass of citric acid is 1:20-1:30, preferably                  1:26.66. The beverage additionally comprises one,                  two or more ingredients chosen from (a) proteins,                  preferably enzymes, (b) amino acids, preferably                  one, two, three or more amino acids chosen from                  glutamic acid, tryptophan, glycine, serine,                  aspartic acid, tyrosine, valine, leucine,                  threonine, arginine and lysine, (c)                  carbohydrates, preferably chosen from cellulose,                  pectins, glucose, fructose and sucrose, (d) trace                  elements, preferably chosen from calcium,                  magnesium, chromium, manganese, iron, copper,                  zinc, molybdenum, selenium, sodium, phosphorus,                  cobalt, sodium, strontium, nickel, potassium,                  fluorine and aluminum, (e) lipids, preferably                  linoleic acid, (f) sterols, preferably                  stigmasterol, (g) vitamins, preferably one or                  more vitamins chosen from vitamin B, vitamin C                  and vitamin E, (h) theophylline, (i) chlorophyll,                  (j) carotenoids, (k) tannins, (i) catechins,                  preferably one, two or more catechins chosen from                  epicatechin (EC), epicatechin gallate (ECG),                  epigallocatechin (EGC) and epigallocatechin                  gallate (EGCG) and (l) theobromine. Preferred                  Method: The production or provision of caffeine                  involves extracting coffee beans, preferably                  green coffee beans of the Robusta type.
   ACTIVITY - Hyperglycemic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Components comprising 0.005 wt.% caffeine from                coffee beans, 0.015 wt.% steviol glycoside, 0.5                wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.4 wt.% citric acid, 0.1                weight/weight% glucuronolactone, 0.1 wt.% guarana                extract, 0.06 wt.% green tea extract, 7.6 wt.%                isomaltulose and remainder of water were mixed                together to obtain 100 ml drink comprising 25 mg                caffeine and 12.5 mg theanine.
