PT P
PN WO2022168059-A1; US2022251453-A1
TI Preparing bituminous material for transport by            accessing non-volatile bituminous material, heating            bituminous material until suitably viscous for casting            and introducing suitably viscous bituminous material to            molds and filling mold cavity.
AU GIANNELIA P
AE PHILERGOS GROUP FOUND (PHIL-Non-standard)
   PHILERGOS GROUP FOUND (PHIL-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1552E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M2) bituminous material for                transport comprises: (a) accessing non-volatile                bituminous material; (b) heating the bituminous                material until it is suitably viscous for casting;                (c) introducing the suitably viscous bituminous                material to molds, where each mold defines a mold                cavity configured to define a brick of irregular                shape and to receive the suitably viscous                bituminous material; (d) filling each mold cavity                of the molds with the suitably viscous bituminous                material; (e) solidifying the suitably viscous                heavy oil in the molds until a substantially solid                bricks are formed; and (f) removing the bricks from                the molds.
   USE - The methods are useful for preparing                bituminous material and non-volatile bituminous                material for transport.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides transporting bituminous                materials as irregular solid bricks that to                provides several benefits over traditional methods                where continuous heat, added diluent, or both                necessary to move bituminous materials from one                location to another in a cost-effective way; is by                substantially removing diluent and any other                harmful additives, the resulting bituminous                material is non-volatile and unlikely to burn given                higher flash- and fire- points, as result, it can                travel more readily by vehicle, which reduces                reliance on pipelines, and the threat to the                environment is reduced or eliminated, preferably                should any bituminous spill during transport, by                further enhancing bricks of bituminous material                with customized skeletons or other buoyant                features, bricks are unlikely to sink if they are                spilled into marine environments and deliverable to                customers with preferred rather than excess amounts                of polymer or other additives; and reduces                significantly carbon dioxide emissions, when                traditional vehicles and shipping containers are                replaced with low-emissions or zero-emissions                vehicles carrying transport chambers incorporating                passive environmental control systems and features,                carbon dioxide emissions are significantly                reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing (M1) non-volatile bituminous material for                transport comprising (a1) heating bituminous                material until it is suitably viscous for casting,                (b1) retaining the suitably viscous bituminous                material in a vessel at a filling station, where                the filling station is positioned along a conveyor,                (c1) accessing molds, where each mold defines an                irregular mold cavity when assembled and comprises:                (i) a lower mold part and (ii) an upper mold part                removably attachable to the lower mold part, where                each upper mold part defines a channel extending                from an upper surface of the mold to the mold                cavity when the molds are assembled, (d) arranging                the lower mold parts on the conveyor, (e) attaching                the upper mold parts to the lower mold parts, (f1)                moving the molds with the conveyor to the filling                station and dispensing the bituminous material from                the vessel to mold cavities defined by the molds                through the mold channels defined by the upper mold                parts of the molds, (g) moving the molds with the                conveyor to a solidifying station and solidifying                the bituminous material in each mold until a                substantially solid brick is formed, (h) moving the                molds with the conveyor to a mold-disassembling                station and removing the upper mold parts from the                molds and (i) removing the bricks from the                molds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M2) further                  comprises: applying a coating to the bricks after                  removing them from the molds; preparing the molds                  before introducing the suitably viscous                  bituminous material, where preparing the molds                  comprises positioning a skeleton in each mold                  cavity; before introducing the suitably viscous                  bituminous material to the molds (a) obtaining a                  desired polymer specification from a customer,                  (b) preparing according to the desired polymer                  specification skeletons, where each skeleton                  comprises buoyant features and (c) disposing one                  skeleton in the mold cavity of each mold; (a2)                  using molds where each of the molds defines a                  channel extending from an upper surface of the                  mold to the mold cavity, (b2) collecting the                  suitably viscous bituminous material in a vessel,                  and (c2) dispensing the suitably viscous                  bituminous material from the vessel to the molds                  through retractable conduits in fluid                  communication with the vessel, where each                  retractable conduit is configured to fit within                  the channels defined by each of the molds, be in                  fluid communication with the mold cavities                  defined by each of the molds; moving the                  plurality of bricks to a coating station and                  applying a coating to the bricks after removing                  the bricks from the molds; and accessing                  skeletons and placing a skeleton in each of the                  lower mold parts before attaching the upper mold                  parts, where each skeleton comprises a polymer                  matrix. The method (M2) further comprises (a3)                  obtaining a desired polymer specification from a                  customer before assembling the molds and (b3)                  configuring each to satisfy the desired polymer                  specification provided by the customer. The brick                  of irregular shape comprises a brick body defined                  by an irregular outer surface, the outer surface                  comprising (a) of non-planar face surfaces and                  (b) a non-planar top surface integrally connected                  to the faces. The outer surface comprises three                  non-planar face surfaces, where each of the                  non-planar face surfaces connects to adjacent                  non-planar face surfaces at angles of less than                  ninety degrees and to the top surface at an angle                  of less than ninety degrees. The skeleton                  comprises polymer arranged in a matrix and                  buoyant features supported by the polymers at                  regular intervals throughout the matrix. The                  irregular mold cavity is configured to define a                  brick defined by an irregular outer surface, the                  outer surface comprising: (a3) three non-planar                  face surfaces, where each non-planar face                  surfaces connects to the other non-planar face                  surfaces at angles of less than ninety degrees                  and (b3) a non-planar top surface integrally                  connected to each of the non-planar face surfaces                  at angles of less than ninety degrees. The                  bituminous material is dispensed from the vessel                  with retractable conduits in fluid communication                  with the vessel, where each of the retractable                  conduits is configured to fit within the channels                  defined by the upper mold parts of the molds. The                  skeleton further comprises buoyant features                  positioned at regular intervals throughout the                  polymer matrix.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN US2022120655-A1; TW202217205-A
TI Preventing and handling indoor air pollution caused by air pollutant e.g. carbon dioxide, bacteria and virus, involves providing portable gas detection device and gas exchangers implemented in indoor space, disposing gas exchangers within indoor space and remotely controlling gas exchangers.
AU LEE W
   TSAI C
   HAN Y
   HUANG C
   WU C
   LIN C
   MOU H
AE MICROJET TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MIJT-C)
   MICROJET TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MIJT-C)
GA 202252356E
AB    NOVELTY - Preventing and handling indoor air pollution for an indoor space, involves providing a portable gas detection device and many gas exchangers provided for implementing in the indoor space, where the gas exchangers are allowed to inhale outdoor gas, purify and filter the inhaled gas and introduce the inhaled gas into the indoor space and an air pollutant in the indoor space is exchanged and exported out to the outdoor environment, disposing 1-75 gas exchangers within the indoor space such that the gas exchangers have an exported airflow rate of 200-1600 clean air output ration (CADR), and remotely controlling the gas exchangers to enable filtration, purification and gas exchange procedure by the portable gas detection device, as the portable gas detection device detects an air pollutant in the indoor space in real time, to reduce the air pollutant in the gas of indoor space to a safe detection value.
   USE - Method for preventing and handling indoor air pollution for an indoor space, where the air pollutant in the indoor space is particulate matter (PM)1, PM2.5, PM10, carbon dioxide, total volatile organic compounds (TVOC), formaldehyde, bacteria and/or virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to utilize portable gas detection device to detect an air pollutant in the indoor space in real time, to reduce the air pollutant in the gas in the indoor space to a safe detection value within 1 minute, so that the air pollutant in the indoor space is exchanged to achieve a clean and safe and breathable condition, to prevent the people from breathing harmful gases in the indoors environment and to monitor the indoor air quality in real time anytime and anywhere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preventing and handling indoor air pollution for an indoor space, involves providing a portable gas detection device and many gas exchangers provided for implementing in the indoor space, where the gas exchangers are allowed to inhale outdoor gas, purify and filter the inhaled gas and introduce the inhaled gas into the indoor space and an air pollutant in the indoor space is exchanged and exported out to the outdoor environment, disposing 1-75 gas exchangers within the indoor space such that the gas exchangers have an exported airflow rate of 200-1600 clean air output ration (CADR) and the indoor space has a volume of 16.5-247.5 m3, and remotely controlling the gas exchangers to enable filtration, purification and gas exchange procedure by the portable gas detection device, as the portable gas detection device detects an air pollutant in the indoor space in real time, to reduce the air pollutant in the gas in the indoor space to a safe detection value within 1 minute, so that the air pollutant in the indoor space is exchanged to obtain a clean, safe and breathable gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a gas flowing paths of the gas detection device.
   Particulate sensor (135)
   Outer cover (136)
   Gas-inlet groove (1314)
   Gas-inlet (1341a)
   Inlet opening (1361a)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gas exchanger comprises gas-inlet channel, gas-outlet channel, purification unit and remote-control driving device. The purification unit is a high efficiency particulate air filter screen coated with a cleansing factor containing chlorine dioxide to inhibit viruses and bacteria in the gas, a herbal protective layer extracted from ginkgo and Japanese Rhus chinensis to form a herbal protective anti-allergic filter, to resist allergy effectively and destroy a surface protein of influenza virus in the gas passing through the high efficiency particulate air filter screen, or coated with a silver ion to inhibit viruses and bacteria contained in the gas introduced into the main body.

PN CN113754175-A
TI Treating grease in rubbish liquid e.g. taking rubbish liquid collected by waste treatment plant, combining activated carbon into evaporator, obtaining organic impurities in rubbish liquid, placing activated carbon obtained by filtering, and placing mixture in combustion furnace and burning.
AU ZHOU T
   LUO Y
AE HUNAN TIANWEI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202206198M
AB    NOVELTY - Treating grease in rubbish liquid comprises (1) taking rubbish liquid collected by waste treatment plant and placing it in separation tank, allowing to stand, and separating out grease and waste water, (2) filtering after full stirring to obtain grease and activated carbon for adsorbing organic impurities for one treatment, (3) carrying out step for once-treated grease, and collecting and storing obtained grease, (4) combining activated carbon into evaporator, heating under reflux, and filtering, (5) repeating operation twice with the activated carbon filtered, (6) obtaining organic impurities in rubbish liquid, (7) placing waste water in evaporator and concentrated to obtain concentrated solution, (8) placing concentrated solution in heating tank to be heated, and filtering after continuous stirring, and (9) placing the activated carbon obtained by filtering in the evaporator, and placing mixture in combustion furnace and burning into carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for treating grease in rubbish liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem that the landfill leachate containing benzopyrene cannot be effectively treated, and the water source and soil are polluted; and enters into vegetables, fruits, grains, aquatic products and meat and other foods that human beings depend on for survival, which poses huge threat to human health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating grease in rubbish liquid comprises (1) taking the rubbish liquid collected by the waste treatment plant and placing it in separation tank, allowing to stand, and separating out the grease and waste water, (2) placing the grease in heating tank to be heated, adding activated carbon in the heating tank, and filtering after full stirring to obtain the grease and the activated carbon for adsorbing organic impurities for one treatment, (3) carrying out step 1-3 times of the step (2) for the once-treated grease, and collecting and storing the obtained grease, (4) combining the activated carbon obtained by the step (2) and step (3) into the evaporator, adding organic solvent, continuously stirring, heating under reflux for 30-50 minutes, and filtering while hot, (5) repeating the operation of the step (4) twice with the activated carbon filtered by the step (4), (6) steaming the filtrate obtained by the step (4) and (5), and obtaining the organic impurities in the landfill leachate grease, where the organic impurity comprises benzopyrene, (7) placing the waste water obtained in the step (1) in the evaporator and concentrated to obtain concentrated solution, (8) placing the concentrated solution placed in heating tank to be heated, adding activated carbon to the heating tank, and filtering after continuous stirring, and (9) placing the activated carbon obtained by filtering the step (8) in the evaporator, rotary evaporating the organic solvent after repeating the operation of the step (4) twice, mixing the residue obtained by rotary evaporation with the organic impurities obtained by rotary evaporation of the step (6) to obtain mixture, and placing the mixture in combustion furnace and burning into carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (4), the organic solvent is acetone, ether, dichloromethane and/or ethyl acetate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3)); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PN CN113457210-A; CN113457210-B
TI Supercritical extraction of active substance from fruit and vegetable involves placing to-be-extracted active substance in extraction device, fixing pressure value, and simultaneously separating pesticides by changing temperature.
AU WU J
   SHEN L
AE JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8077K
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extraction of active substance from fruit and vegetable involves (i) maintaining to-be-extracted fruit and vegetable active substance at room temperature (35-40degrees Celsius), (ii) placing the active substance in an extraction device, adjusting pressure value in the extraction device to fix, and simultaneously separating pesticides by changing temperature, and (iii) fixing temperature value again, and separating active substances from vegetables by reducing pressure to complete extraction of active substances from whole fruits and vegetables. The extraction device comprises a storage tank, an extractor provided on the right side of the storage tank, and an electric heater provided outside the extractor. A pressurizer is provided on the right side of the storage tank. The pressurizer is located between the storage tank and the extractor. The inner wall of the extractor is provided with a carbon dioxide flow control device.
   USE - Supercritical extraction of active substance containing pesticide from fruit and vegetable.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces raw material granularity, increases solid and solvent of contact, improves extraction speed, and prevents blocking of sieve so as to avoid extractor outlet filter screen of blockage, thus improving working efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical extraction of active substance from fruit and vegetable involves (i) maintaining to-be-extracted fruit and vegetable active substance at room temperature (35-40degrees Celsius), (ii) placing the active substance in an extraction device, adjusting pressure value in the extraction device to fix, and simultaneously separating pesticides by changing temperature, and (iii) fixing temperature value again, and separating active substances from vegetables by reducing pressure to complete extraction of active substances from whole fruits and vegetables. The extraction device comprises a storage tank, an extractor provided on the right side of the storage tank, and an electric heater provided outside the extractor. A pressurizer is provided on the right side of the storage tank. The pressurizer is located between the storage tank and the extractor. A condensation tank is installed at the upper end of the pressurizer. A solenoid valve is installed at the upper end of the condensation tank. The extractor is provided with an original liquid tank on the right side. An extraction plug is fixedly connected to the upper end of the extractor. A connecting pipe is fixedly connected between the extraction plug and the original liquid tank. A separation platform is fixedly connected between the inner walls of the extractor. Two grooves are excavated at the upper end of the separation platform. Evenly distributed separation beads are slidably connected between the inner walls of the groove. The inner bottom end of the groove is fixedly connected with a deformable air bag. The deformable airbag is located on the lower side of the separating ball. The inner cavity of the deformable airbag is filled with insulating powders in contact with each other. A magnet block is fixedly connected between the inner walls of the deformable airbag. Evenly distributed screening holes are excavated at the upper end of the separation platform. The inner wall of the extractor is provided with a carbon dioxide flow control device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The insulating powder is made of iron-nickel alloy material comprising 80% nickel.

TI Cultivation bag assembly for cultivation of microbes, fungi and other organisms has inner bag having first and second walls made of layer of water-vapor permeable material, and outer bag with walls having opening with gas filter patch.

   ADVANTAGE - The improved cultivation bag assembly can reduce the steps in preparation and manufacture of the agents and can reduce risks of competing or contaminating matter introduced into to the process. PN CN112646845-A
TI Extracting and purifying Staphylococcus aureus 5-type capsular polysaccharide useful in immunization, comprises e.g. inoculating Staphylococcus aureus seed liquid, filtering and separating complex solution by cellulose column chromatography.
AU UNANNOUNCED I
AE CANGZHOU SHUOJIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CANG-Non-standard)
GA 202143634J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and purifying Staphylococcus aureus 5-type capsular polysaccharide comprises e.g. (i) adding 1.8-2.2 wt.% sodium chloride (NaCl) in the Colombia blood agar base culture medium, after high pressure sterilizing, adjusting the temperature to 40-50 degrees C, adding 8-12 wt.% new born calf serum to prepare agar culture plate, inoculating the Staphylococcus aureus seed liquid identified as 5 type capsular polysaccharide to the culture medium according to the inoculum size of 0.8-1.2 vol.%, culturing the bacteria in the biochemical culture box of carbon dioxide (CO2) of 35-40 degrees C and not less than 24 hours, (ii) adding 5-10 times volume of high pressure sterilized phosphate buffered saline buffer solution (pH = 7.2-7.5) in the culture medium to obtain the bacteria suspension, suspending the bacteria suspension in distilled water with 1.8-2.5 mg/ml, adding 0.5-1.2 wt.% phenolic acid, and processing for 1-1.5 hours at 80-120 degrees C.
   USE - The polysaccharide is useful as an antigen directly without further modification, or coupled with a protein for immunization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polysaccharide reduces the use of phenol, chloroform, pollution caused by acetone and harm to the human body, is safe and environmentally friendly, high yield and high purity, and has low protein and nucleic acid content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and purifying Staphylococcus aureus 5-type capsular polysaccharide comprises (i) adding 1.8-2.2 wt.% sodium chloride (NaCl) in the Colombia blood agar base culture medium, after high pressure sterilizing, adjusting the temperature to 40-50 degrees C, adding 8-12 wt.% new born calf serum to prepare agar culture plate, inoculating the Staphylococcus aureus seed liquid identified as 5 type capsular polysaccharide to the culture medium according to the inoculum size of 0.8-1.2 vol.%, culturing the bacteria in the biochemical culture box of carbon dioxide (CO2) of 35-40 degrees C and not less than 24 hours, (ii) adding 5-10 times volume of high pressure sterilized phosphate buffered saline buffer solution (pH = 7.2-7.5) in the culture medium to obtain the bacteria suspension, suspending the bacteria suspension in distilled water with 1.8-2.5 mg/ml, adding 0.5-1.2 wt.% phenolic acid, processing for 1-1.5 hours at 80-120 degrees C, high speed bead grinding the bacteria suspension after processing the phenolic acid for 2-5 minutes, using 0.2 mu m of fiber film for filtering to remove thalli impurity, and taking the filter liquid, (iii) adding quaternary ammonium salt cationic surfactant into the filtering liquid, adding while stirring, allowing to stand overnight precipitate polysaccharide at 0-4 degrees C, taking the precipitate to dissociate with 1-1.5 mol/l NaCl solution, adding final concentration of 70-95 vol.% ethanol, collecting precipitate, using de-ionized water to dissolve, separating the complex solution by cellulose column chromatography, where the mass ratio is 7-9: 2-2.5:1 of ethanol, acetic acid, water mixture as eluent, the flow rate is 0.8- 1.4 ml/minute, differential detector online detection, collecting according to the eluting peak, vacuum extracting and concentrating and mixing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Staphylococcus aureus 5-type capsular polysaccharide prepared according to above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the phenolic acid is p-hydroxybenzoic acid, protocatechuic acid, and gallic acid. In the step (ii), the high-speed bead milling process, 0.3-0.5 g grinding balls are added to each 1 ml of bacterial suspension. The grinding balls are mixed in three different sizes, 0.1 mm, 0.2 mm, 0.4 mm, and three sizes of weight ratio of the grinding balls is 10:5-8:2-5. In the step (iii), the quaternary ammonium salt cationic surfactant is (dodecyldimethyl-2-hydroxypropyl)-ammonium acetate chloride or (dodecyldimethyl-2-hydroxypropyl)-salicylic acid ammonium chloride. The halogenated hydrocarbon is epoxy chloropropane, epoxy bromopropane or epoxy iodopropane. In the step (iii), the eluate contains 0.5-1 wt.% cetylpyridinium chloride. In the step (iii), the cellulose column filler is dextran cellulose or hyskon cellulose. Preferred Method: The quaternary ammonium salt cationic surfactant is prepared by: mixing the carboxylic acid, dodecyl dimethyl tertiary amine, halogenated hydrocarbon according to 1: 1.5-3.5: 2-2.5 mol ratio, stirring and heating to 60-80 degrees C, reacting for 8-12 hours, dissolving the product with sufficient acetone, cooling to form paste, separating two phases, removing solvent from the product, vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C for 10-24 hours.

PN CN112462060-A
TI Detecting classical swine fever virus indirect immunofluorescence neutralizing antibody comprises e.g. digesting PK15 cells, centrifuging to prepare cell suspension containing cells, spreading on 96-well plate and culturing.
AU LI P
   LI H
   WANG X
   WANG L
   LIU X
   LIU C
   LIU M
   SUN G
   YUE F
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV XINXIANG (UNXX-C)
GA 202125727D
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting classical swine fever virus (CSFV) indirect immunofluorescence (IFA) neutralizing antibody comprises (i) digesting the PK15 cells, centrifuging to prepare a cell suspension containing 1-105 cells/ml, spreading 100 mu l per well on a 96-well plate, placing it in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 12 hours, attaching the cells fully, incubating an equal volume of CSFV virus liquid and clinical serum, inoculating the PK15 cells in a 96-well plate, culturing, taking out the 96-well plate after cells are full and setting up a control hole for normal toxin cells; (ii) fixing with pre-chilled absolute ethanol, adding pre-chilled absolute ethanol 50 mu l per well, storing, discarding absolute ethanol, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), pat drying; (iii) adding 5% skimmed milk powder to seal, placing in a 37 degrees C incubator for 2 hours, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) and pat drying.
   USE - The method id useful for detecting classical swine fever virus indirect immunofluorescence neutralizing antibody.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares the cell reaction plate in advance which can be stored for a long time at 20 degrees C; produces the results of the reaction which can be observed with a fluorescence microscope and IFA detection with strong specificity, high sensitivity and simple operation, where detection IFA method uses live virus inoculation, using whole virus, the antigen structure is more complete and provides accurate result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting classical swine fever virus (CSFV) indirect immunofluorescence (IFA) neutralizing antibody comprises (i) digesting the PK15 cells, centrifuging to prepare a cell suspension containing 1-105 cells/ml, spreading 100 mu l per well on a 96-well plate, placing it in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 12 hours, attaching the cells fully, incubating an equal volume of CSFV virus liquid and clinical serum at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, inoculating the PK15 cells in a 96-well plate, culturing for 48 hours, taking out the 96-well plate after the cells are full and setting up a control hole for normal toxin cells; (ii) fixing with pre-chilled absolute ethanol, adding pre-chilled absolute ethanol 50 mu l per well, storing at -20 degrees C for 30 minutes, discarding the absolute ethanol, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), pat drying; (iii) adding 5% skimmed milk powder to seal, 200 mu l per well, placing in a 37 degrees C incubator for 2 hours, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), pat drying; (iv) adding CSFV positive antibody, diluting with phosphate buffer saline according to 1:400, adding 50 mu l to each well, placing in a 37 degrees C incubator for 1 hour, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) and pat drying; and (v) adding goat anti-pig IgG-FITC, diluting with 1:100 with phosphate buffer saline, adding 50 mu l to each well, placing in a 37 degrees C incubator for 1 hour, washing with phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), adding 50 mu l phosphate buffered saline with Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) into each well and observing in an inverted fluorescence microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation of CSFV virus liquid comprises grinding the clinically collected disease material tissue, adding 1 ml phosphate buffered saline to dissolve 1 mg tissue sample, freeze-thawing repeatedly for 3 times, taking the supernatant, centrifuging at 12000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes and filtering with 0.22 mu m filter to obtain CSFV virus liquid. The preparation method of the clinical serum comprises centrifuging the collected blood and collecting the serum for later clinical serum detection.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CN112062274-A; CN112062274-B
TI Exploring the impact of papermaking wastewater treatment aeration on various greenhouse gas emission sources involves establishing greenhouse gas emission mechanism model, establishing indirect greenhouse gas emission model and comparing.
AU SHEN W
   LI W
   HUANG F
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020C65095
AB    NOVELTY - Exploring the impact of papermaking wastewater treatment aeration on various greenhouse gas emission sources involves establishing greenhouse gas emission mechanism model for papermaking wastewater treatment process where greenhouse gas emission mechanism model includes direct greenhouse gas emission model and indirect greenhouse gas emission model, setting reactors 1, 2, 3, and 4 of activated sludge model 1 model as anaerobic reactors, setting reactor 5 as aerobic reactor, establishing indirect greenhouse gas emission model where process of indirect greenhouse gas emission model is carried out through sludge treatment, power consumption and chemical addition, collecting on-site sewage data, dividing influent chemical oxygen demand and total nitrogen components, and obtaining variables in activated sludge model 1 model.
   USE - The method is used for exploring the impact of papermaking wastewater treatment aeration on various greenhouse gas emission sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can evaluate the degree of influence of aeration on various greenhouse gas emission sources, explore the influence path of dissolved oxygen change on greenhouse gas emission, and provide theoretical basis for feasibility study of realizing greenhouse gas emission reduction through dissolved oxygen control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Exploring the impact of papermaking wastewater treatment aeration on various greenhouse gas emission sources comprises:
   (A) establishing greenhouse gas emission mechanism model for papermaking wastewater treatment process where greenhouse gas emission mechanism model includes direct greenhouse gas emission model and indirect greenhouse gas emission model, setting reactors 1, 2, 3, and 4 of activated sludge model 1 model as anaerobic reactors, setting reactor 5 as aerobic reactor where each reactor contains 13 reaction components: fast and fast biodegradable matrix, soluble non-degradable organic matter, slow biodegradable matrix, granular non-biodegradable organic matter, active heterotrophic microorganism, active autotrophic microorganism, particulate biodegradable organic nitrogen, inert particulate matter produced by microbial attenuation, dissolved oxygen, nitrate nitrogen or nitrite nitrogen, soluble biodegradable organic nitrogen, alkali and 8 reaction processes: aerobic growth of heterotrophic bacteria, hypoxic growth of heterotrophic bacteria, aerobic growth of autotrophic bacteria, attenuation of heterotrophic bacteria, attenuation of autotrophic bacteria, ammoniated soluble degradable organic nitrogen, slow hydrolysis of fast degradable organic matter, and hydrolysis of slow degradable organic nitrogen;
   (B) establishing indirect greenhouse gas emission model where process of indirect greenhouse gas emission model is carried out through sludge treatment, power consumption and chemical addition;
   (C) collecting on-site sewage data, dividing influent chemical oxygen demand and total nitrogen components, and obtaining variables in activated sludge model 1 model;
   (D) establishing greenhouse gas emission experimental model to verify the accuracy of greenhouse gas emission mechanism model;
   (E) establishing experimental model of greenhouse gas emissions, using calculated carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide emissions as real values, comparing with carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide emissions calculated by direct greenhouse gas emission model, and verifying greenhouse gas emission mechanism model of papermaking wastewater treatment process accuracy;
   (F) setting different amounts of aeration as input of greenhouse gas emission mechanism model, and conducting simulation experiments on papermaking wastewater treatment process to obtain the greenhouse gas emissions of each emission source; and
   (G) using Pearson correlation coefficient method, calculating correlation coefficient between aeration volume and greenhouse gas emissions of each emission source, and finding emission source that has greatest impact on greenhouse gas.

PN DE102019207654-A1; CN111977057-A; EP3753875-A1; CN111977057-B
TI Device for introducing protective gas into bottle,            has transport system between receiving and delivery            points which is designed with long stator linear motor            drive for individually controllable movement of            transport elements for containers.
AU KNIELING E
   CONELIN E
AE KRONES AG (KROE-C)
   KRONES AG (KROE-C)
GA 2020B6243Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a transport system (100) which                is provided with a closed transport path, which is                designed to transport the containers (2) from a                receiving point to a delivery point. An injection                device (150) is arranged and designed on the                transport path such that the protective gas is                introduced into the containers during transport of                the containers from the receiving point to the                delivery point. The transport system between the                receiving point and the delivery point is designed                with a long stator linear motor drive for                individually controllable movement of several                transport elements for the containers along the                transport path. The protective gas comprises                nitrogen or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Device for introducing protective gas such as                liquid nitrogen and carbon dioxide into head space                of container e.g. can, polyethylene terephthalate                (PET) plastic bottle or glass bottle used in                container treatment system (claimed) for liquid                food such as beer, fruit juices, smoothies, water,                iced tea and milk used in beverage processing                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The high throughput of containers is enabled                and the consumption of protective gas and water is                reduced. The icing on machine components is avoided                by the protective gas. The production costs in the                beverage processing industry are lowered and the                productivity of the associated container treatment                systems is increased. The long stator of the long                stator linear motor is designed as a synchronous                linear motor, since slip in the synchronous linear                motor is avoided, so that the control and                regulating unit moves a transport element with a                predetermined movement profile more easily. The                volume flow of protective gas emitted by the                injection nozzle is reduced with the same                requirement for the amount of protective gas to be                introduced into the container. The losses of                protective gas with continuous delivery are                reduced, so that any possible icing of machine                components is reduced. The water used to de-ice the                machine components is saved. The consumption of                protective gas is reduced. The unnecessary rejects                due to containers that are not correctly loaded                with protective gas are avoided. The closed                containers show a improved preservation of the                filled liquid food. The format changes are carried                out in a simple and uncomplicated manner. The                injection devices and transport systems shown use                the flexibility of a long-stator linear motor drive                so that the dwell time of the filled containers at                the injection device is extended and the throughput                of containers is increased. The loss of protective                gas is reduced due to protective gas entering                between the containers, so that water consumption                to prevent machine components from icing up is                reduced. The lower media consumption in the                container treatment system and to reduced operating                costs is achieved. The use of the long stator                linear motor drive allows gaps to be brought                together and a flexible change of division, for                example when changing formats.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for introducing a protective gas into                bottles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                container treatment plant with a device for                introducing protective gas.
   Container (2)
   Container treatment system (15)
   Outlet star (20)
   Transport system (100)
   Injection device (150)

PN CN111592927-A
TI Mixing device used for extracting Ganoderma lucidum spore oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises storage cylinder containing storage cavity for mixing material, where sidewall of storage cylinder is set with row of notches.
AU JIANG Q
   YAO S
   LI Z
   GUO C
   YANG Z
AE ANHUI JINZHAI QIAOKANG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020857621
AB    NOVELTY - Mixing device comprises storage cylinder (6) containing storage cavity (7) for mixing material, where sidewall of the storage cylinder is set with a row(s) of notches along the axial direction, each of the notches is rotatably mounted with a spherical connector, which is provided with an air inlet hole having inner end connected with the inner part of the storage cavity. The outer port of the air inlet hole is fixed with a connecting pipe (8), and the outer side of the storage cylinder is movably installed with an annular clamping plate clamped at the outer side of each connecting pipe. The clamping plate is uniformly distributed with multiple locating plates to form jacks, and the outer side of the storage cylinder is further provided with a driving mechanism used for driving the clamping plate to move to adjust the position of the connector.
   USE - The mixing device is used for extracting Ganoderma lucidum spore oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has simple structure and adjustable airflow inlet direction, realizes convenient mixing in the storage cavity of the storage cylinder, stirs the material by the air flow, and achieves uniform and fast mixing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mixing device comprises storage cylinder (6) containing storage cavity (7) for mixing material, where sidewall of the storage cylinder is provided with a row(s) of notches along the axial direction, each of the notches is rotatably mounted with a spherical connector, which is provided with an air inlet hole having inner end connected with the inner part of the storage cavity. The outer port of the air inlet hole is fixed with a connecting pipe (8). The outer side of the storage cylinder is movably installed with an annular clamping plate clamped at the outer side of each connecting pipe. The clamping plate is uniformly distributed with multiple locating plates to form jacks. The tail end of each connecting pipe respectively movably passes through the different jacks. The outer side of the storage cylinder is further provided with a driving mechanism used for driving the clamping plate to move to adjust the position of the connector. The driving mechanism comprises a movable plate fixed on one side of the clamping plate, strip-shaped hole opened on the movable plate and motor installed on the outer wall of the storage cylinder. The output shaft of the motor drives the connecting turntable. The plate surface of the turntable is fixed with a guide rod. The tail end of the guide rod movably passes through the strip-shaped hole. The motor drives the turntable to rotate. The guide rod extrudes the inner wall of the strip-shaped hole, so that the movable plate drives the clamping plate to move, so as to drive the connector to rotate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the mixing device.
   Electric telescopic rod (2)
   Side plate (5)
   Storage cylinder (6)
   Storage cavity (7)
   Connecting pipe (8)

PN CN111592017-A
TI Preparing battery-grade lithium chloride by spodumene pressure leaching comprises e.g. adding spodumene into high temperature roasting, depressurizing, separating gas and liquid, pressurizing and filtering.
AU LI L
   CHEN C
   PENG Q
   WANG B
   YU X
   FU X
   GAO G
   ZHOU F
   CHEN L
   WANG C
   XIE L
   QIAN P
   HE W
   LI J
   LI Z
   AI G
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202085781T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery-grade lithium chloride by spodumene pressure leaching comprises e.g. adding spodumene into high temperature roasting to transform spodumene from alpha -type to active beta -type spodumene, depressurizing high-temperature and high-pressure pressure immersion fluid into flash tank, separating gas and liquid, pressurizing and filtering liquid components obtained by flash evaporation, washing and filtering to obtain spodumene slag and washing water part, adding soda ash into crude lithium chloride solution to form calcium carbonate precipitation and filtering to remove calcium impurities, magnesium and iron impurities in solution, acidifying, evaporating and concentrating processed solution, cooling, precipitating some sodium chloride crystals, filtering, evaporating and crystallizing the impurity-removed lithium chloride solution, and filtering and separating the crystal, drying by disc type drier and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing battery-grade lithium chloride by spodumene pressure leaching.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to remove sodium and economical; and has low impurity content, low energy consumption and stable product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing battery-grade lithium chloride by spodumene pressure leaching comprises (a) adding spodumene into a high temperature of 1000-1100 degrees C for roasting to transform the spodumene from alpha -type to active beta -type spodumene, (b) mixing the transformed beta -spodumene with calcium chloride solution and a small amount of calcium hydroxide, placing in an autoclave, sealing, heating and stirring, (c) depressurizing the high-temperature and high-pressure pressure immersion fluid into flash tank, and separating the gas and liquid after the fluid is vaporized, where the water vapor can be liquefied to obtain condensed water, and the condensed water can be recycled and used for washing and filtering and preparing calcium chloride solution for recycling, (d) pressurizing and filtering the liquid components obtained by flash evaporation to obtain filter press residue and lithium chloride crude liquid, (e) washing the filter press residue with condensed water and filtering to obtain spodumene slag and washing water part, where the washing water part can be used to prepare calcium chloride solution, (f) adding soda ash into the crude lithium chloride solution and mixing uniformly to form calcium carbonate precipitation and filtering to remove calcium impurities, and remove magnesium and iron impurities in the solution, (g) using excessive hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH value of the solution to be 2-3, so as to acidify the solution and removing the generated carbon dioxide by a steam stripping method, the carbon dioxide generated by the reaction is collected by the stripping method, which can reduce equipment corrosion and prevent gas from entering the device causing excessive device pressure, (h) evaporating and concentrating the processed solution to the mass percentage concentration of lithium chloride of 35-45%, (i) cooling the concentrated solution to room temperature, and precipitating some sodium chloride crystals, filtering and separating to remove sodium chloride solid to obtain lithium chloride solution with low sodium content, (j) using ion sieve type sodium ion adsorbent to absorb sodium, (k) evaporating and crystallizing the impurity-removed lithium chloride solution, and (l) using a centrifuge to filter and separate the crystal, drying by a disc type drier and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the heating temperature is controlled at 160-200 degrees C, the pressure is set at 1.2-1.8 MPa, the stirring speed is 300-500 revolutions/minute and the pressure immersion time is 0.5-3 hours. In the step (f), the sodium carbonate is generated after adding pure alkali into the lithium chloride crude solution, the pH value of the solution is adjusted to be 9-10 and the content of calcium ions in solution after removing the impurity is less than 0.0001% and the calcium carbonate generated can be recycled and used to prepare calcium chloride solution by hydrochloric acid to be used circularly in step (a). In the step (j), the preparation of sodium ion adsorbent comprises mixing sodium salt and manganese-containing substance and calcining to obtain precursor sodium manganese oxides, mixing the precursor with hydrochloric acid, where the mixing ratio is n(NaxMnO2): n(HCl)=(1:0.1-0.01), washing, filtering and drying.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC E33-G
IP C01D-015/04

PN CN111570972-A
TI Efficient mold repair device has splints that are symmetrically threaded between outer circular surfaces of screws which are rotatably connected with placement plate that is fixedly connected between cavity inner walls at rotating plate top.
AU DENG J
   GUO Y
   CHONG R
AE CHUZHOU XIANDAI MOULD CO LTD (CHUZ-Non-standard)
GA 202084855J
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a base (1) whose top is provided with an adjustment mechanism (2) through sliding grooves and sliding blocks. A carbon dioxide gas tank (3) is fixedly connected to the top of the base. Two vertical plates (4) are fixedly connected to the top of the base symmetrically, and a top plate (5) is fixedly connected between the tops of the two vertical plates. The output end of the first motor (6) is fixedly connected with a lead screw (7). A placement plate is fixedly connected between the inner walls of the cavity at the top of the rotating plate. The left and right sides of the rotating plate are symmetrically rotatably connected with two sets of screws. The two sets of screws have close ends respectively penetrate the left and right sides of the rotating plate and are rotatably connected with the left and right sides of the placement plate. Two splints are symmetrically threaded between the outer circular surfaces of the two sets of screws.
   USE - Efficient mold repair device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device accurately changes the up and down position of the mold. The surfacing repair of the mold is realized without dead corners in all directions, entire mold is repaired by clamping and fixing once, which greatly improves the efficiency of mold repair, and effectively reduces the labor intensity of the staff. The moving trajectory of the welding wire becomes simple. The stability of the welding wire conveying speed is ensured, so that the welding wire conveying speed is consistent with the welding wire consumption rate for a long time, which greatly improves the quality of mold repair and ensures the stability of the repair quality, thus effectively improves the device performance reliability. The stability of the adjustment mechanism is greatly improved, the safety of the mold during the repair process is guaranteed, and the safety of nearby workers is guaranteed, which greatly improves the safety and reliability of the device. The device improves the accuracy of adjusting the rotation angle of the rotating plate, thus improves the accuracy of mold position adjustment during mold repair, more accurately controls the size and range of the molten tank area, reduces the area of the heat affected zone, and effectively improves the quality of mold repair. The stability of the operation of the adjustment mechanism is ensured, and thus the stability of the mold during the position adjustment process is improved. The control of the mold position accuracy are effectively improved, improves the quality of mold repair, and greatly improves the practicability and reliability of the device. The phenomenon of slack and slipping of the placed welding wire makes the control of the welding wire conveying speed more stable and ensures the quality and reliability of mold repair.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an efficient mold repair device.

PN CN110961867-A; CN110961867-B
TI Preparation of three-layer aluminum alloy composite material involves bonding brazing layer ingot and base layer ingot, tightening, hot-rolling-heating, cooling, hot-rough-rolling, cold-rolling obtained coils, annealing, and cleaning.
AU DONG Y
   ZHANG Y
   YANG Z
   REN G
   QIN J
   ZHANG G
   ZU L
AE TIANJIN ZHONGWANG ALUMINIUM IND CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN ZHONGWANG ALUMINIUM IND CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202028996N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of three-layer aluminum alloy composite material involves melt-casting brazing layer raw material and base layer raw material, respectively, obtaining a brazing layer ingot and a base layer ingot, respectively, sawing the brazing layer ingot and the base layer ingot head and end, milling, hot-rolling brazing layer ingot, polishing the brazing layer ingot, removing surface dust on the upper and lower surfaces of the base layer ingot, spraying aviation kerosene, then wiping with oil-absorbing paper or oil-absorbing cloth, bonding the polished brazing layer ingot and the wiped base layer ingot by carbon dioxide gas shielded welding process, tightening the combined composite material using several steel strips, hot-rolling-heating the composite material, cooling, removing the steel strips, hot-rough-rolling obtained composite material, cutting, curling, cold-rolling obtained hot-rolled coils, annealing, and alkali-cleaning.
   USE - Preparation of three-layer aluminum alloy composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of three-layer aluminum alloy composite material with high strength, excellent mechanical property and corrosion resistance, improved sag resistance after brazing at high temperature, and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of three-layer aluminum alloy composite material involves melt-casting brazing layer raw material and base layer raw material by semi-solid casting method, respectively, obtaining a brazing layer ingot and a base layer ingot, respectively, refining brazing layer ingot, adding residue to aluminum-strontium master alloy for modification treatment, sawing the brazing layer ingot and the base layer ingot head and end to a predetermined length, milling with main milling amount of 15 mm or more, and side milling amount of 10 mm or more, heating the brazing layer ingot at 480 degrees C for 3 hours, rolling to the target thickness (h) with rolling pass of 17-25, polishing the brazing layer ingot, removing surface dust on the upper and lower surfaces of the base layer ingot using an air gun, spraying aviation kerosene, then wiping with oil-absorbing paper or oil-absorbing cloth, bonding the polished brazing layer ingot and the wiped base layer ingot by carbon dioxide gas shielded welding process, tightening the combined composite material using several steel strips, hot-rolling-heating the composite material at 520 degrees C for 6-8 hours, cooling, removing the steel strips, hot-rough-rolling obtained composite material at 480 degrees C and rolling speed of 1 m/second with hot rolling pass of 25-30, such that the gap between the brazing layer and the base layer is fully compressed and exhausted, further hot-rough-rolling obtained intermediate billet at 330 plus minus 10 degrees C, cutting, curling, cold-rolling obtained hot-rolled coils, annealing, and alkali-cleaning. The total processing volume of cold rolling is 30% or more. The thickness of the base layer ingot after milling is set to H. The base layer ingot thickness (H) and the brazing layer ingot thickness (h) satisfy the relation: h/(h+H)x 100%. The thickness of hot-rough-rolling intermediate billet is 40-50 mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite material is alkali-cleaned using 15-25% sodium hydroxide solution, then rinsed with water, and dried.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The raw material composition of the brazing layer comprises 7.5-7.9 wt.% silica, 0.3 wt.% or less iron, 0.02 wt.% or less magnesium, 0.1-0.2 wt.% copper, 0.1 wt.% or less manganese, 0.05 wt.% or less chromium, 0.1-0.2 wt.% zinc, 0.03 wt.% or less titanium, 0.03 wt.% or less strontium, 0.05 wt.% or less single impurity, 0.15 wt.% or less total impurities, and remaining of aluminum. The raw material composition of the base layer comprises 1.3-1.4 wt.% manganese, 0.9-1 wt.% zinc, 0.3-0.4 wt.% silicon, 0.25-0.35 wt.% iron, 0.1-0.2 wt.% copper, 0.05 wt.% or less magnesium, 0.05 wt.% or less chromium, 0.03 wt.% or less titanium, 0.03 wt.% or less strontium, 0.05 wt.% or less single impurity, 0.15 wt.% or less total impurities, and remaining of aluminum. Excluding the addition of magnesium and zinc in the form of alloy ingots, all other elements are added in the form of intermediate alloys. The brazing layer ingot is refined using aluminum-titanium-boron alloy (Al-5Ti-1B) refining agent.
DC P56 (Machine tools (B23P,Q).)

PT P
PN CN110735146-A
TI Treating pressure rollers comprises ultrasonically cleaning forming pressure roller, ultrasonically polishing, quenching, heating, insulating, incubating, carburizing, cooling, secondary quenching, tempering, heating, and surface coating.
AU ZHANG Y
AE ANHUI XINYU MACHINERY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202011276X
AB    NOVELTY - Treating pressure rollers comprises (i) placing a forming pressure roller in sodium bicarbonate solution, ultrasonically cleaning, taking out, and washing with water to obtain cleaning member,(ii) ultrasonically polishing the cleaning member to obtain polished member, (iii) quenching by placing the polished member in a vacuum oven, heating, insulating, then placing in quenching liquid, incubating, and then naturally cooling to obtain quenched member, (iv) carburizing by placing the quenched part in a vacuum furnace, heating, introducing carbon dioxide, insulating, and cooling to obtain carburized member, (v) secondary quenching by placing the carburized member in a vacuum furnace, heating, and then placing in quenching liquid and cooling to room temperature to obtain secondary quenched member, (vi) tempering by heating the secondary quenched member, insulating, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain tempered member, and (vii) heating the tempered member, and surface coating.
   USE - The method is useful for treating pressure rollers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, easy, suitable for large-scale production; improves anticorrosive performance of the pressure roller; avoids the phenomenon of corrosion and rust caused by long-term sticking of the dough; and can extends the life of the pressure roller. The pressure rollers: has high strength, strong abrasion resistance, anti-corrosion property; can maintain good fatigue resistance during continuous rotation; avoids wear on the pressure roller; improves sanitation in the noodle production process, and avoids influence to the health of human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating pressure rollers comprises (i) placing a forming pressure roller in sodium bicarbonate solution, ultrasonically cleaning for 20-30 minutes, taking out, and washing with water to obtain cleaning member,(ii) ultrasonically polishing the cleaning member for 45-55 minute to obtain polished member, (iii) quenching by placing the polished member in a vacuum oven, heating at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minutes to 470-510 degrees c, insulating for 30-40 minutes, continuously heating to 760-820 degrees C for 70-90 minutes, then placing in quenching liquid, incubating for 40-50 minutes when the temperature reduced to 540-580 degrees C, and then naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain quenched member, (iv) carburizing by placing the quenched part in a vacuum furnace, heating at a rate of 3-4 degrees C/minute to 670-710 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, continuously heating to 910-940 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide, insulating for 160-180 minutes, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain carburized member, (v) secondary quenching by placing the carburized member in a vacuum furnace, heating at a rate of 4-5 degrees C/minute to 640-680 degrees C for 35-45 minutes, continuously heating to 890-950 degrees C for 70-90 minutes, and then placing in quenching liquid and cooling to room temperature to obtain secondary quenched member, (vi) tempering by heating the secondary quenched member to 710-740 degrees C at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minute, insulating for 60-80 minutes, naturally cooling to 340-380 degrees C for 45-55 minutes, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain tempered member, and (vii) heating the tempered member to 90-100 degrees C, and surface coating. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing wear-resistant and anti-corrosion surface roller by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonic cleaning in the step (i) is carried out at ultrasonic frequency of 27-29 kHz and temperature of 45-55 degrees C. The ultrasonic polishing in the step (ii) is carried out at ultrasonic frequency of 26-28 kHz, where the polishing liquid is nano-diamond polishing liquid with a roughness of 0.1-0.2 mu m. Preferred Components: The amount of sodium bicarbonate solution in the step (i) is 3-4 wt.%. The quenching liquid in the step (iii) is titanium dioxide-polyalkylene glycol quenching liquid, where the titanium dioxide-polyalkylene glycol quenching liquid is prepared by adding nano-titanium dioxide to polyalkylene glycol quenching solution, and stirring at a rate of 90-100 revolutions/minute for 40-50 minutes. The nano-titanium dioxide is anatase-type nano-titanium dioxide, and the particle size is 35-45 nm. The carbon dioxide in the step (iv) has a flow rate of 6.7-6.9 m3/hour at 1-60 minutes, and flow rate of 5.2- 5.4 m3/hour at 61-100 minutes, and flow rate of 3.6-3.8 m3/hour at remaining time. The thickness of the coating in the step (vii) is 15-20 mu m.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); M12 (Chemical cleaning and degreasing - including cleaning and pickling.); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).); P61 (Grinding, polishing (B24).)

TI Double-sided double-wire gas shielded welding of low alloy high strength steel fillet welds with shop priming comprises removing oil stains and moisture from low-alloy high-strength steel longitudinal, assembling, welding and fine tuning.
AU ZHANG J
   LEI B
   SHAO D
   CHEN L
   YANG T
   YANG C
   ZENG F
AE GUANGZHOU HUANGCHUAN OCEANOGRAPHIC ENG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202004112G
AB    NOVELTY - Double-sided double-wire gas shielded welding of low alloy high strength steel fillet welds with shop primingcomprises (i) removing oil stains and moisture from low-alloy high-strength steel longitudinal and welding area of the panel, polishing the welds by using mechanical sanding, (ii) assembling the longitudinal bone on the panel, performing welding of the positioning welds by using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method, (iii) selecting a 1.4 mm diameter metal powder cored wire as L pole wire, selecting a semi-metal powder cored wire with a diameter of 1.4mm as T pole wire for welding, (iv) adjusting the welding gun angle and welding gun spacing, and (v) setting the welding speed, and performing welding by gas shielded welding according to L and T pole welding parameters comprising L pole having a welding current and welding voltage, and T pole having a welding current, and welding voltage, and fine tuning.
   USE - The method is useful for double-sided double-wire gas shielded welding of low alloy high strength steel fillet welds with shop priming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the efficiency of profile fillet welding; reduces welding pore sensitivity; improves welding quality; reduces post-weld rework; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Double-sided double-wire gas shielded welding of low alloy high strength steel fillet welds with shop priming comprises (i) removing oil stains and moisture from low-alloy high-strength steel longitudinal and welding area of the panel, polishing the welds by using mechanical sanding, and removing oxides, rust and other impurities in the weld area, (ii) assembling the longitudinal bone on the panel, performing welding of the positioning welds by using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method, tack weld length of 50-60 mm, and tack welds distance of 500-600 mm, (iii) selecting a 1.4 mm diameter metal powder cored wire as L pole wire, selecting a semi-metal powder cored wire with a diameter of 1.4mm as T pole wire for welding, where the L-pole welding wire meets the requirements of E70C-G type in AWS A5.18 standard, and T-pole welding wire meets the requirements of E70T-1C model in AWS A5.20 standard, (iv) adjusting the welding gun angle and welding gun spacing, where the angle between the welding gun and the panel is 45-50 degrees , L pole forward angle of 5-7 degrees , T pole backward angle of 7-10 degrees , ensuring length of welding wire between L-pole contact tip and welding workpiece to 15-20 mm, length of the welding wire between the T pole tip and the welding workpiece is 20-25mm, and welding wire tip spacing of 25-30 mm, and (v) setting the welding speed to 70-90 cm/minute, and performing welding by gas shielded welding according to L and T pole welding parameters comprising L pole having a welding current of 280-360 A and welding voltage of 30-34 V, and T pole having a welding current of 250-320 A, and welding voltage of 30-36 V, and fine tuning the welding parameters with the above parameters according to the welding size.

PN CN110590036-A
TI Reusing waste acid resource by using water pump for pumping industrial discharged mixed acid waste liquid, filtering, generating mixture of nitric acid gas and water gas, using vacuum pump to pump mixed gas and decomposing.
AU CHEN Y
AE SICHUAN LANMOF ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019A86161
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reusing waste acid resource, by using water pump for pumping the industrial discharged mixed acid waste liquid into a filtering system, and filtering, pumping the mixed acid waste liquid filtered out into the evaporation system, generating the mixture of nitric acid gas and water gas at the top of the evaporation system, using the first vacuum pump to pump the mixed gas into the first condensation system, condensing to obtain mixed acid solution composed of acetic acid and nitric acid, mixing the acetic acid decomposition system and decomposing the acetic acid in the mixed acid solution into carbon dioxide and water, using water pump to pump the remaining solution into the nitric acid decomposition system, decomposing the nitric acid decomposition system with nitric acid in the solution to nitrogen dioxide, oxygen and water and using the second vacuum pump to pump the high-purity phosphoric acid to the second condensation system.
   USE - Method for reusing waste acid resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the beneficial effects of reducing environmental pollution, reuses phosphoric acid, and is economical and simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for reusing waste acid resource, involves (i) using water pump for pumping the industrial discharged mixed acid waste liquid into a filtering system, and filtering the particulate impurities in the mixed acid waste liquid through cloth bag in the filtering system, (ii) pumping the mixed acid waste liquid filtered out of particle impurities in the step (i) into the evaporation system, setting the temperature in the evaporation system at 130-170 degrees C, the vacuum degree to -0.08 to -0.09 MPa, and the evaporation time to 1-6 hours, during negative pressure evaporation, generating the mixture of nitric acid gas, acetic acid gas and water gas at the top of the evaporation system, where the concentration at the bottom of the evaporation system is greater than or equal to 80% high purity phosphoric acid, (iii) using the first vacuum pump to pump the mixed gas in the step (ii) into the first condensation system, condensing the mixed gas, and after a period of condensation, obtaining mixed acid solution composed of acetic acid and nitric acid, (iv) using the water pump to pump the mixed acid solution in the step (iii) into the acetic acid decomposition system, setting the temperature in the acetic acid decomposition system at 20-50 degrees C, the decomposition time for 1-6 hours, mixing the acetic acid decomposition system and decomposing the acetic acid in the mixed acid solution into carbon dioxide and water, (v) using water pump to pump the remaining solution in the step (iv) into the nitric acid decomposition system, and setting the time in the nitric acid decomposition system for 1-6 hours, decomposing the nitric acid decomposition system with nitric acid in the solution to nitrogen dioxide, oxygen, and water to obtain reclaimed water that meets customer discharge standards, and (vi) using the second vacuum pump to pump the high-purity phosphoric acid in step (ii) to the second condensation system, where the second condensation system condenses the phosphoric acid, after a period of condensation, high-purity phosphoric acid product with a concentration of greater than or equal to 80% is obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The step (iv) involves, absorbing carbon dioxide using exhaust gas treatment system. The step (v) involves absorbing nitrogen dioxide using exhaust gas treatment system.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Preparing olive oil paste fermented species useful e.g. as emulsifier, comprises e.g. preparing material using olive oil, flours, yeast and water, mixing and kneading olive oil, flour and yeast for first time, sealing dough and fermenting.
AU KWON S M
AE KWON S M (KWON-Individual)
GA 2019A39128
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing olive oil paste fermented species, comprises preparing material using 100 pts. wt. olive oil, 83-84 pts. wt. flour A, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt. yeast A, 66-67 pts. wt. flour B, 0.3-0.4 pts. wt. yeast B and 33-34 pts. wt. water; mixing and kneading olive oil, flour A and yeast A for first time; sealing dough A and fermenting for 3 hours for first time in an environment having a temperature of 25-35 degrees C and humidity of 30-40%; mixing and kneading the flour B, yeast B, and water for second time; fermenting dough B for 4 hours in an environment having a temperature of 30-45 degrees C and humidity of 30-40%; mixing and kneading dough A and dough B subjected to first fermentation process and second fermentation process; sealing mixed dough subjected to primary fermentation process at 25-35 degrees C and humidity of 30-40% to ferment; supplying oxygen and uniformly mixing the whole dough after unsealing the mixing dough and discharging carbon dioxide generated during fermentation; and stirring mixed dough.
   USE - The fermented species is useful as emulsifier and moisture supplying agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The fermented species: softens the dough of bread, improves the taste and aroma of savory, supplies enough fat to flour, suppresses sugar intake of yeast under acidic and proper fermentation; and has the taste, function and flavor of ordinary bread without using egg, butter, milk, salt and sugar which can cause allergy during the production of bread.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing olive oil paste fermented species, comprises preparing material using 100 pts. wt. olive oil, 83-84 pts. wt. flour A, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt. yeast A, 66-67 pts. wt. flour B, 0.3-0.4 pts. wt. yeast B and 33-34 pts. wt. water; mixing and kneading olive oil, flour A and yeast A for first time; sealing dough A and fermenting for 3 hours for first time in an environment having a temperature of 25-35 degrees C and humidity of 30-40%; mixing and kneading the flour B, yeast B, and water for second time; fermenting dough B for 4 hours in an environment having a temperature of 30-45 degrees C and humidity of 30-40%; mixing and kneading dough A and dough B subjected to first fermentation process and second fermentation process; sealing mixed dough subjected to primary fermentation process at 25-35 degrees C and humidity of 30-40% to ferment for 6 hours; supplying oxygen and uniformly mixing the whole dough after unsealing the mixing dough and discharging carbon dioxide generated during fermentation; and stirring mixed dough for 6 hours in an environment of temperature 25-35 degrees C and humidity of 30-40%, repeating the stirring process at 2-6 degrees C, storing in an environment with humidity of 25-40%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for olive oil paste fermented species, prepared as mentioned above.

AU SUN P

   ZHOU H
AE UNIV SHANDONG TRADITIONAL CHINESE MEDICI (SDTC-C)
GA 2019968295
AB    NOVELTY - Applying ginseng extract GQR1 comprises (i) using sampling equipment to extract peripheral blood of tumor patients, adding heparin sodium anticoagulation and uniformly mixing, (ii) centrifuging sample by centrifugal device, collecting the upper layer plasma and inactivating, (iii) diluting and mixing the resulting cell pellet with physiological saline, adding to the lymphocyte separation liquid, centrifuging at room temperature, discarding the supernatant, re-suspending and mixing physiological saline, and centrifuging at room temperature to obtain cell pellet, (iv) labeling the tumor cells in which cell pellets are with tumor cell marker, (v) adding cell pellet using ginseng extract GQR1 induction medium, diluting the count with white blood cell dilution, incubating in carbon dioxide incubator with saturated humidity,expanding the liquid at specific time, and (vi) using chromogenic solution developing the color of labeled tumor cells.
   USE - The extract GQR1 is useful for tumor cells (claimed), producing anti-tumor effect and anti-tumor body's immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and convenient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Applying ginseng extract GQR1 comprises (i) using sampling equipment to extract peripheral blood of tumor patients, adding heparin sodium anticoagulation and uniformly mixing, (ii) centrifuging sample by centrifugal device, collecting the upper layer plasma and inactivating, (iii) diluting and mixing the resulting cell pellet with physiological saline, adding to the lymphocyte separation liquid, centrifuging at room temperature, discarding the supernatant, re-suspending and mixing physiological saline, and centrifuging at room temperature to obtain cell pellet, (iv) labeling the tumor cells in which cell pellets are with tumor cell marker, (v) adding cell pellet using ginseng extract GQR1 induction medium, diluting the count with white blood cell dilution, incubating in carbon dioxide incubator with saturated humidity, during the period, according to the growth state of the cells, expanding the liquid at specific time, and (vi) using chromogenic solution developing the color of labeled tumor cells An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for operating system for applying ginseng extract GQR1 comprising sampling module, centrifugation module, cell separation module, cell labeling module, induction culture module, color rendering module, sampling module, connected to the centrifugation module, for extracting peripheral blood of the tumor patient by sampling device. Centrifugation module connected to the sampling module and the cell separation module for centrifuging the sample by a centrifugal device. Cell separation module which is connected to the centrifugation module and the cell labeling module, and is used for separating and separating by using physiological saline to obtain a cell sediment. Cell labeling module coupled to the cell separation module, the induction culture module, and the tumor cell for labeling the cell pellet by the tumor cell marker solution. The induction culture module is connected to the cell labeling module and the color development module, and is used for inducing and culturing the cells by adding a cell sediment to the GQR1 induction medium containing the ginseng extract. Color developing module coupled to the induction culture module for coloring the labeled tumor cells by the color developing solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the tumor cell marker solution contains 20 mu g/ml horseradish peroxidase-labeled alpha -fetoprotein/ alpha -fetoprotein heterogene and 4.0 mmol/l calcium chloride in 0.01 mol/l pH 7.4 phosphate buffered saline solution. The horseradish peroxidase-labeled alpha-fetoprotein/alpha-fetoprotein heterogeneous body comprises (i) dissolving 6 mg horseradish peroxidase with 1 ml 0.4 mol/l pH 5 sodium acetate buffer to prepare horseradish peroxidase solution, (ii) adding 0.4-0.1 mol/l sodium periodate solution to the horseradish peroxidase solution, and reacting in the dark for 50 minutes at room temperature, (iii) eluting the resultant reaction solution by G25 gel column chromatography, 0.4 mol/l pH9 carbonate buffer, and collecting the activated horseradish peroxidase, (iv) adding 5 mg alpha-fetoprotein or alpha-fetoprotein heterogeneous body to the activated horseradish peroxidase solution, and reacting in the dark at room temperature overnight, (v) adding 100 mu l freshly prepared 0.1 mol/l sodium borohydride solution, and reacting at room temperature for 30 minutes in the dark, (vi) subjecting the resultant to G25 gel column chromatography to collecting protein elution peak, and (viii) concentrating the resultant, purifying by G200 column. In the step (v), the ginseng extract is prepared by (i) ginseng is extracted by boiling water in 6-9 times water at 92-98 degrees C, and extracted 4-6 times, each time 1.5-2.0 hours, (ii) remove the precipitate from the obtained heated extract with ethanol having a volume concentration of 60-90% to obtain crude extract of ginseng, (iii) crude ginseng extract is freeze-dried to obtain ginseng crude extract lyophilized powder, and (iv) ginseng crude extract lyophilized powder was used as test material, and the crude ginseng extract was purified by adsorption column packed with macroporous resin. In the step (vi), the color developing solution is horseradish peroxidase chromogenic substrate, o-phenylenediamine, tetramethylbenzidine, 5-aminosalicylic acid, and/or o-dianisidine.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Cytostatic.
