PN CN110367060-A
TI Method for cultivating mizuna vegetables, involves            treating seeds using top sprouts cultivation device,            spreading on wet medium, covering with cling film,            germinating followed by cultivating, planting, and            harvesting.
AU LI J
   LAI H
   ZHANG X
   YAN W
AE FUJIAN SINO-SCI BIOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201990399C
AB    NOVELTY - A mizuna vegetables cultivating method                involves treating seeds using a top sprouts                cultivation device that automatically supplements                the nutrient solution for cultivation on the lower                layer, and a seeding medium on the upper layer for                soaking at 55&#176; C for 10-15 minutes after                drying, spreading on wet medium, spraying water,                covering with cling film, germinating. The liquid                level is contacted the root system, and the                automatic rehydration box is filled, nutrient                solution temperature is maintained at                18-22&#176; C, carbon dioxide concentration is                300-1200 ppm, and light time is 12-16 hour/ day,                followed by cultivating under the conditions,                planting at a density of 30-200 plants/m2, the                nutrient solution level is reduced to 1/2 at the                seedling stage, when the plant grows to 6-15 cm and                the leaves grow to more than 20 pieces, entering                into the mature period and extend the light time to                12-18 hour/day, and harvesting.
   USE - Method for cultivating mizuna                vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating mizuna                vegetables without stem base browning to avoid                later rot diseases, with high seedling growth in                simple, stable, safe an cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mizuna vegetables cultivating method                involves treating seeds using a top sprouts                cultivation device that automatically supplements                the nutrient solution for cultivation on the lower                layer, and a seeding medium on the upper layer for                soaking at 55&#176; C for 10-15 minutes after                drying, spreading on wet medium at a density of                2400-4800 grains/m2, spraying water, covering with                cling film, germinating at 18-23&#176; C,                humidity of 80-100%, raising young sprouts. After                the seeds are exposed, moved to light to raise                seedlings, the cling film is removed at next day,                nutrient solution is added to the bottom of the                first layer of seedling trays, and nutrient                solution is added to the automatic rehydration box,                device is automatically refill, after the root                system grows 1-3 cm, move the upper hole seedling                tray to the circulating nutrient solution                cultivation bed to continue growing, light                intensity is increased and level of the nutrient                solution is lower than the hole seedling tray,                which effectively avoids algae and wait for the                seedlings to grow, when it is 10-20 cm, cut and                harvested neatly. The liquid level contacts the                bottom of the medium, after the root system grows,                after the cotyledons are expanded, the water is                replaced with nutrient solution. The liquid level                is contacted the root system, and the automatic                rehydration box is filled, nutrient solution                temperature is maintained at 18-22&#176; C,                carbon dioxide concentration is 300-1200 ppm, and                light time is 12-16 hour/ day, followed by                cultivating under the conditions, planting at a                density of 30-200 plants/m2, the nutrient solution                level is reduced to 1/2 at the seedling stage, when                the plant grows to 6-15 cm and the leaves grow to                more than 20 pieces, entering into the mature                period and extend the light time to 12-18 hour/day,                and harvesting.

TI Stem cell injection used for treating            osteoarthritis cartilage defect, comprises fifth            generation human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell,            and liquid solvent e.g. normal saline and mixed liquid            of normal saline and human serum albumin.
AU RAO W
   ZHANG Q

   SHI L

   WU D
AE WUHAN HAMILTON BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY COR (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201982193L
AB    NOVELTY - A stem cell injection comprises fifth                generation human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                cell and liquid solvent. The fifth generation human                umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell is suspended                in liquid solvent. The concentration is                2.0-3.0asterisk107 cells/4.5-5.0 ml liquid solvent.                The liquid solvent is normal saline, autologous                platelet-rich plasma, mixed liquid of normal saline                and human serum albumin, or mixed liquid of normal                saline and autologous platelet-rich plasma. The                liquid solvent is mixed liquid of normal saline and                human serum albumin, or mixed liquid of normal                saline and autologous platelet-rich plasma.
   USE - The stem cell injection is used for treating                osteoarthritis cartilage defect (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the stem cell injection                comprising:
   (A) collecting umbilical cord after fetal                delivery, clamping umbilical cord using hemostatic                forceps, cutting umbilical cord using surgical                cutting, ligation processing other end of umbilical                cord with silk, washing umbilical cord, and placing                in sterilized collecting bottle;
   (B) transferring umbilical cord to sterile                culture dish in sterile environment, measuring                umbilical cord length, recording, disinfecting and                washing, cutting and removing umbilical cord                vessel, tearing between the amniotic membrane and                blood vessel of Wharton's jelly into sterile                culture dish, and adding 5-10 ml phosphate buffer                solution to wash colloid;
   (C) transferring Wharton's jelly to centrifuge                tube, repeatedly cutting to 1-4 mm3 Wharton's jelly                tissue homogenate block, adding 10-20 ml umbilical                cord mesenchymal stem cell complete culture medium,                lightly shaking, centrifuging at normal temperature                for 6 minutes at speed of 1900 revolutions/minute                (rpm), removing remaining supernatant, and weighing                record;
   (D) adding 0.5 g/ml umbilical cord mesenchymal                stem cell complete culture medium, repeatedly                blowing colloidal homogenate block by electric                pipette, 0.5g/5-10ml in the homogenate, adding                umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell complete                culture medium to homogenate at ratio of 0.5 g/5-10                ml, uniformly mixing, moving to culture bottle                according to 15-20 ml/bottle, uniformly                distributing tissue homogenate blocks on whole                bottom flask, putting into constant-temperature and                constant-humidity culturing box with 5 plus minus                0.2% carbon dioxide content at 37 plus minus 0.5                degrees C, digesting and centrifuging for                harvesting, collecting residual tissue block wall                that are not firm, and secondary culturing residual                tissue block;
   (E) adding 10-20 ml fresh umbilical cord                mesenchymal stem cell complete culture medium to                flask, blowing and mixing harvested residual tissue                pieces, inoculating on new culture bottle,                secondary culturing in constant-temperature and                constant-humidity culturing box with 5 plus minus                0.2% carbon dioxide content at 37 plus minus 0.5                degrees C for 5-6 days until cell fusion reaches                bottle bottom area of 85-90%, digesting, and                centrifuging and collecting secondary to primary                cell culture; and
   (F) inoculating collected primary cells into                new culture vessel at density of 5000-6000                cells/cm2 of medium, subculturing, culturing until                the cell fusion degree is 75-85%, harvesting first                generation (P1) stem cells, passing to same                density, repeating amplification culture to fifth                generation, and suspending cultured fifth                generation human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                cells in liquid vehicle at 2.0-3.0asterisk107                cells/4.5-5 ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The injection is                  optionally added with dexamethasone at adding                  amount of 1 mg/per ml injection. The first fifth                  generation human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                  cell is umbilical cord tissue by high purity                  separation derived from abdominal surgery in                  infants mesenchymal stem cell placed in complete                  culture medium performing culture and passage                  amplification to fifth generation of pluripotent                  mesenchymal stem cells. The complete culture                  medium comprises basic culture medium and                  serum-free culture medium additive. The basic                  culture medium comprises serum-free LONZA                  UltraCULTURE medium, DMEM/F12 culture medium                  and/or HyClone DMEM sugar liquid culture medium.                  The culture medium additive is fibroblast growth                  factor L-Glutamine and PALL UltroserG serum                  substitute, and has L-Glutamine adding volume of                  1-2% and PALL Ultroser G adding volume of 2-5%.                  The autologous platelet-rich plasma is                  platelet-rich plasma from patients of autologous                  blood collection and separation. Preferred                  Method: The autologous platelet-rich plasma is                  prepared by collecting autologous peripheral                  blood of patients with knee arthritis under                  aseptic operation with blood collection tube                  preloaded with anticoagulant, dispensing into                  sterile 50 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging for                  10 minutes at speed of 3000 rpm, pipetting upper                  layer of plasma into another new 50 ml centrifuge                  tube, centrifuging for 10 minutes at speed of                  3000 rpm, discarding upper layer of plasma,                  retaining required plasma dose, shaking                  centrifuge tube, standing, and resuspending                  bottom of tube in remaining plasma.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration is done by intravenous                injection and/or local injection into bone and                joint cavity (all claimed). The intravenous                injection has injection amount of 22.5-25 ml/time                and amount of cells injected of 1.0-1.5asterisk108                cells/time. The joint cavity injection has                injection amount of 4.5-5.0 ml/time/single knee and                amount of cells injected of 2.0-3.0asterisk107                cells/time/one knee.

PN US2019168192-A1; US10421062-B2
TI Converting mixed waste plastic into low molecular weight organic compound, comprises e.g. feeding particles of mixed waste plastic and solid catalyst component into fluidized bed reactor, heating, and withdrawing first and second streams.
AU GAFFNEY A M
AE AMG CHEM & CATALYSIS CONSULTING LLC (AMGC-Non-standard)
   AMG CHEM & CATALYSIS CONSULTING LLC (AMGC-Non-standard)
GA 201949383P
AB    NOVELTY - Converting mixed waste plastic into low molecular weight organic compound, comprises: (a) feeding particles of mixed waste plastic and solid catalyst component into fluidized bed reactor; (b) heating the particles of the mixed waste plastic and solid catalyst component at temperature to produce coarse filler, inorganic components, coke, volatile organic component, and spent catalyst component; (c) withdrawing first stream comprising the volatile organic component from the reactor; (d) withdrawing second stream comprising the spent catalyst component, the coke, the coarse filler and the inorganic components from the reactor; (e) heating the second stream in regenerator in the presence of oxygen, air, or blend of oxygen with inert gas at temperature to convert the coke to mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, and to regenerate the solid catalyst component; and (f) separating the regenerated solid catalyst component from the coarse filler and the inorganic components.
   USE - The method is useful for converting mixed waste plastic into low molecular weight organic compound (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting a mixed waste plastic into a low molecular weight organic compound, comprises: (a) feeding particles of the mixed waste plastic and a solid catalyst component into a fluidized bed reactor, the solid catalyst component comprising (i) a modified zeolite and (ii) alloyed metals comprising at least one noble metal alloyed with at least one transition metal, where the zeolite comprises chabazite, erionite, faujasite, ferrierite, mordenite, offretite, TEA-mordenite, zeolite A, zeolite beta , zeolite boron beta , zeolite L, zeolite X, zeolite Y, zeolite ZK-5, Breck-6, H(zeolite socony mobil)-5, calcined (siliceous mobil composition of matter (MCM)-22) (ITQ)-1, ITQ-21, MCM-22, MCM-36, MCM-39, MCM-41, MCM-48, PSH-3, zeolite framework type SZR-4, zeolite framework type EUO, silicoaluminophosphate (SAPO)-5, SAPO-11, SAPO-34, (S)AIPO-31, SSZ-23, SSZ-32, TUD-1, VPI-5, ZSM-4, ZSM-5, ZSM-8, ZSM-11, ZSM-12, ZSM-20, ZSM-21, ZSM-22, ZSM-23, ZSM-34, ZSM-35, ZSM-38, ZSM-48, ZSM-50 and/or ZSM-57, and the modified zeolite is modified with a modifier comprising (1) phosphorus, (2) boron, (3) phosphorus and boron and/or (4) an additive comprising gallium, zinc, zirconium, niobium and/or tantalum; (b) heating the particles of the mixed waste plastic and the solid catalyst component at a temperature effective to produce a coarse filler, inorganic components, coke, a volatile organic component, and a spent catalyst component; (c) withdrawing a first stream comprising the volatile organic component from the reactor; (d) withdrawing a second stream comprising the spent catalyst component, the coke, the coarse filler and the inorganic components from the reactor; (e) heating the second stream in a regenerator in the presence of oxygen, air, or a blend of oxygen with an inert gas at a temperature effective to convert the coke to a mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, and to regenerate the solid catalyst component; and (f) separating the regenerated solid catalyst component from the coarse filler and the inorganic components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas of step (e) is nitrogen and/or steam. The volatile organic component comprises a light 2-6C olefinic product stream. The high molecular weight organic compound is benzene, toluene and/or xylene. Preferred Process: The process further comprises (g) recycling the light 2-6C olefinic product stream back to the fluidized bed reactor to produce a high molecular weight organic compound. The hydrogen is added into the step (a), where a hydrogen source is added into the step (a). The particles of the mixed waste plastic in step (a) have an average diameter of less than 1 cm. The temperature of step (b) is of 450-750 degrees C. The before the step (a), the particles of the mixed waste plastic are fed into a thermal reactor and undergo pyrolysis. The pyrolysis is performed using a two-step temperature program comprising (i) raising the temperature to 300-330 degrees C for at least two hours, and (ii) raising the temperature to 400-450 degrees C. The pyrolysis produces mixed waste plastic having less than 15 ppm halogenated hydrocarbons.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE XISHUANGBANNA HUAKUN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (XISH-Non-standard)
GA 2019474883
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is prepared by (i) performing mechanical extrusion by drying fresh rubber tree seed and shelling, heating and subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic spiral extrusion to obtain the product, (ii) performing solvent leaching by drying rubber tree seeds, shelling, puffing, and crushing to obtain processed material, placing in a sealed leaching container, adding a solvent, stirring the material and the solvent or ultrasonically processing the material to sufficiently contact with the solvent, leaving still and precipitating, leaching and volatilizing the solvent to obtain the product, or (iii) performing supercritical extraction by drying rubber tree seeds and shelling, puffing, crushing, and placing in a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction container, adding carbon dioxide, stirring and mixing the material (C) and the solvent, or ultrasonically processing, precipitating, leaching solvent and volatilizing to obtain the product.
   USE - The rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is useful for preparing a medicament, a health care food or a food for improving human immunity (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rubber tree seed fat-soluble part is prepared by (i) performing mechanical extrusion method by drying fresh rubber tree seed and shelling to obtain kernel material (A), heating material (A) at 80-100 degrees C and subjecting to static hydraulic extrusion or dynamic spiral extrusion to obtain the final product, (ii) performing solvent leaching method by drying fresh rubber tree seeds, shelling to obtain kernel material (B), puffing, granulating or crushing the material (B) to obtain a mixture, placing the mixture in a sealed leaching container, adding a solvent, where the solvent comprises a solvent oil No.6, petroleum ether having a boiling point of 30-90 degrees C, n-hexane, diethyl ether, ethyl acetate and/or n-butanol, stirring and mixing the material and the solvent at a temperature lower than the boiling point of the solvent and under normal pressure, or ultrasonically processing the material to sufficiently contact with the solvent, leaving still and precipitating, leaching the solvent and volatilizing the solvent at a temperature above the boiling point of the solvent and under vacuum to obtain a remaining liquid substance as a final product, or (iii) performing supercritical extraction by drying fresh rubber tree seeds and shelling to obtain kernel material (C), puffing, granulating or crushing, and placing the crushed material (C) in a sealed pressure-resistant supercritical extraction container, adding carbon dioxide as a solvent, stirring and mixing the material (C) and the solvent under the condition that the critical temperature and the critical pressure of the solvent are exceeded, or ultrasonically processing to enable the material (C) to be in full contact with the solvent, leaving still and precipitating mixture, leaching the solvent and volatilizing the solvent at room temperature under reduced pressure to obtain a remaining liquid substance as a final product. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a pharmaceutical composition comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier;
   (2) a health care food comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier commonly used for health care food; and
   (3) a food comprising the rubber tree seed fat-soluble part and a carrier commonly used for food.

TI Extracting rubidium by flash leaching of silicate            involves preparing extract solution, filtering,            leaching washing liquid, extracting regenerated organic            phase, baking volatile tin chloride and calcining            rubidium chloride stannate.
AU HUANG X
   LU K
   SHI Z
   LUO G
   LI Y
   LIN H
AE MINERALS APPL RES INST GUANGDONG PROVINC (MINE-Non-standard)
GA 201948240H
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting rubidium by flash leaching of                silicate involves adding silicate ore with calcium                chloride, adding calcined slag with water and                leaching solution with sodium carbonate, filtering,                subjecting precipitate to calcination, feeding                calcium oxide to chlorine gas absorption tower,                concentrating, separating concentrate by adding                sodium hydroxide, extracting solution by adding                t-BAMBP (4-tert-butyl-2-( alpha                -methylbenzyl)phenol) and xylene as extractant,                washing organic phase with sodium hydroxide,                leaching washing liquid, performing back                extraction, extracting regenerated organic phase,                washing solution formed by adding tin salt to                water, subjecting washing liquid to evaporative                crystallization and volatile tin chloride treatment                together with precipitated mother liquid, baking                volatile tin chloride, calcining washed rubidium                chloride stannate and decomposing and subjecting                tin tetrachloride gas to adsorption                treatment.
   USE - Extracting rubidium by flash leaching of                silicate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process effectively increases leaching                rate, improves extraction efficiency and reduces                loss rate thereby improving technical index of                rubidium, effectively reduces waste of mineral                resources. The extraction process is simple and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting rubidium by flash leaching of                silicate involves adding silicate ore raw material                with calcium chloride to obtain calcined slag and                generating chlorine gas, feeding chlorine gas into                chlorine gas absorption tower for treatment, adding                calcined slag with water for flash circulation,                leaching and dehydration to obtain leachate and                leach residue, where each leaching time is less                than 5 minutes, using leaching liquid more than 8                times, sieving 85-90% leaching slag and                dehydrating, dewatering remaining 10-15% slag by                pressure filtration, adding leaching solution with                sodium carbonate to carry out precipitation                reaction, filtering to obtain filtrate and calcium                carbonate precipitate, subjecting calcium carbonate                precipitate to calcination treatment to decompose                and generate carbon dioxide and calcium oxide,                returning carbon dioxide to leaching solution and                precipitation of sodium carbonate, feeding calcium                oxide to chlorine gas absorption tower to form                calcium chloride, returning to calcination                operation, concentrating filtrate by evaporation to                concentration ratio of 1/7-1/10 to increase                concentration of hydrazine to obtain concentrated                liquid, salting out potassium chloride, sodium                chloride and distilled water, separating                concentrate prepared by adding 30-40 g/L sodium                hydroxide to obtain extract liquid, extracting                solution by adding t-BAMBP ( 4-tert-butyl-2-( alpha                -methylbenzyl)phenol)and xylene as extractant to                obtain organic phase and raffinate, washing organic                phase with sodium hydroxide at concentration of                1.5N until washing liquid changes from black to                yellow to colorless, leaching washing liquid,                adding washed organic phase with hydrochloric acid                having concentration of 4N, performing back                extraction to obtain stripping solution and                regenerated organic phase, concentrating stripping                solution by evaporation to obtain concentrated                reaction solution, extracting regenerated organic                phase, adding concentrated reaction solution with                tin tetrachloride pentahydrate for precipitation                reaction, filtering to obtain rubidium chloride                stannate and mother liquor, the bismuth                chlorostannate is made of hydrochloric acid,                washing solution formed by adding tin salt to water                as washing solution, subjecting washed washing                liquid to evaporative crystallization and volatile                tin chloride treatment together with precipitated                mother liquid to obtain potassium chloride and                sodium chloride, baking volatile tin chloride where                potassium chloride and sodium chloride, feeding                volatile tin chloride into adsorption bottle,                calcining washed rubidium chloride ostannate and                decomposing to obtain ruthenium chloride product                and subjecting produced tin tetrachloride gas to                adsorption treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The calcined slag is                  added to water in weight ratio of 1:8-12. The                  leaching rate is not greater than 85%. The                  concentration of t-BAMBP (4-Tert-butyl-2-( alpha                  -methylbenzyl) phenol) in extractant is 1.0                  mol/L. The extraction is performed compared with                  O/A (organic/ aqueous)of 1-3. The stripping                  solution is concentrated by evaporation to                  acidity of 6-8 N. The concentration of                  hydrochloric acid and tin salt in washing                  solution is 4N and 0.1N. The amount of washing                  solution is 8-15 mL/g/time.

PN CN109694448-A
TI Reticulated graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores has average specific surface area, graphene-angstrom both nanoscale and microscale pores are exist in aerogel material.
AU LI H
   FU Y
   LIU H
   WEI D
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2019419502
AB    NOVELTY - Reticulated graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores has average specific surface area of the graphene-allotite composite aerogel material is 601-605 square meter per gram, graphene-angstrom both nanoscale and microscale pores exist in aerogel material. The nanoscale pores average 10-11 nm, and the micrometer pores average 20-21 mu m. The reticulated graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores includes adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and uniformly disperse ultrasonically to obtain a halloysite dispersion, and 0.6-12 pts. wt. benzene. Sodium vinyl sulfonate, 0.06-1.8 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. initiator, 0.08-9 pts. wt. decabromodiphenyl ether added to 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol in the mixed solution, after stirring.
   USE - Reticulated graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores.
   ADVANTAGE - The reticulated graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores is helps to combines the flame retardant of different to realize synergistic flameretardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - , the above solution is added to the halloysite dispersion, the ultrasonic dispersion is uniform, the vacuum is maintained, the vacuum is maintained, and then the pressure is returned to normal pressure. The vacuuming step is repeated three times, the product is washed and dispersed in 100 pts. wt. water, the water bath is heated at 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, the polymerization time is 50 hours, and the dispersion is washed and dispersed to 100 pts. wt. water to obtain the dispersion. The polydivinylsiloxane has a number average molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, and a vinyl content mole percentage. The ratio of the number of vinyl moles to the number of moles of 0.1-5 wt.% amino mono-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or an amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane, 0.8-8 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.3-12 pts. wt. graphite powder are mixed, 0.6-12 pts. wt. potassium permanganate is added and the mixture is placed in an ice water bath and stirred for 12 hours, and then stirred. The 360-520 pts. wt. water and 9-52 pts. wt. peroxide are added to the above solution, stirred uniformly, and the product is washed until near neutral, and 100 pts. wt. water is added to the product, and the ultrasonic dispersion is uniform. The dispersions includes adding 0.01-1.5 pts. wt. the initiator, 0.03-6 pts. wt. ammonium polyphosphate, stirring uniformly, and then raising the temperature to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization. The polymerization time is 60 hours, 0.7-12 pts. wt. ascorbic acid is added to the above reaction solution and continued the reaction for 10-40 hours. The product is placed in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction apparatus, using carbon dioxide as a medium at a temperature of 31-56. The mesh-graphene-allotite composite aerogel material having mesopores and micropores can be obtained by supercritical drying at 31-56 degrees C and a pressure of 6-12 mega Pascal for 1 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide (BPO) or azobisisobutyronitrile (ABIN). Preferred Process: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide (BPO) or azobisisobutyronitrile (ABIN). The method further involves 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-5 pts. wt. ammonium polyphosphate is added to the mixed dispersion, stirred for 1-10 hours, and then prepolymerized in a water bath at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours, then sequentially polymerizing in 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, 100 degrees C constant temperature water bath for 2-8 hours, and then placing at 80-200 degrees C, after reacting in water kettle for 1-10 hours. The 1-10 pts. wt. ascorbic acid is added, reaction temperature is controlled at 20-100 degrees C, and the supercritical drying condition is drying at a temperature of 33-55 degrees C and a pressure of 7-10 mega Pascal or 2-4 hours.

PN CN109047285-A; CN109047285-B
TI Converting sodium and fluorine-containing            compounds in waste cathode carbon block of aluminum            electrolytic cell by adding waste cathode carbon powder            to conversion mill, adding water and grinding aid, gel            breaking, aging, and calcining.
AU LIU E
   SHAO X
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201901064G
AB    NOVELTY - Converting sodium and fluorine-containing                compounds in waste cathode carbon block of aluminum                electrolytic cell comprises crushing, grinding,                sieving and homogenizing waste cathode carbon block                in aluminum electrolytic cell, adding grinding                body, adding waste cathode carbon powder to                conversion mill, and adding water and grinding aid;                sampling slurry, analyzing in batches, and stopping                conversion mill; transferring slurry to stirred                reactor for gel breaking and aging treatment;                subjecting slurry to solid-liquid separation by                filtration or centrifugal separation, placing                aqueous solid phase in reforming mill or stirred                reactor, washing with water, and combining                separation solution; drying aqueous solid phase,                pulverizing, calcining, oxidizing and burning;                concentrating dilute solution of mixture or                crystallizing; converting cyanide to nitrogen or                ammonia; and subjecting ammonia, carbon dioxide,                and water gasses to absorption conversion and                drying, and collecting.
   USE - Method for converting sodium and                fluorine-containing compounds in waste cathode                carbon block of aluminum electrolytic cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low production                cost and no pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting sodium and fluorine-containing                compounds in waste cathode carbon block of aluminum                electrolytic cell comprises:
   (A) crushing, grinding, sieving and                homogenizing waste cathode carbon block in aluminum                electrolytic cell to obtain waste cathode carbon                powder with particle size of less than or equal to                200 mu m, analyzing and determining mole or mass of                sodium and fluorine in waste cathode carbon powder,                adding grinding body to conversion mill, subjecting                to air evacuation or nitrogen replacement, adding                waste cathode carbon powder to conversion mill to                control mass ratio of waste cathode carbon powder                to grinding body at 1:0.2-10, adding water to                conversion mill to control solid-liquid mass ratio                of waste cathode carbon powder to water to 1:1-10,                adding conversion agent to conversion mill, and                adding grinding aid to conversion mill;
   (B) controlling rotation speed of conversion                mill to 10-1000 revolutions/minute with conversion                temperature of 10-110 degrees C, sampling slurry,                analyzing in batches until sodium ion content in                slurry solid phase meets enterprise standard and                soluble fluorine-ion content meets national                emission standard, and stopping conversion                mill;
   (C) transferring slurry to stirred reactor for                gel breaking and aging treatment at 60-150 degrees                C for 0.5-5 hours;
   (D) subjecting slurry to solid-liquid                separation by filtration or centrifugal separation,                placing aqueous solid phase in reforming mill or                stirred reactor, washing with water, and combining                separation solution;
   (E) drying aqueous solid phase at 80-300                degrees C for 1-10 hours, pulverizing, calcining at                700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, oxidizing and                burning;
   (F) concentrating dilute solution of mixture                or crystallizing to obtain concentrated solution or                solid phase mixture;
   (G) converting cyanide to nitrogen or ammonia;                and
   (H) subjecting ammonia, carbon dioxide, and                water gasses to absorption conversion and drying,                and collecting generated reducing gas or directly                oxidizing.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for waste                cathode carbon block aluminum electrolytic                cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The waste cathode                  carbon block comprises carbon, sodium, fluorine,                  aluminum, carbon, oxygen, nitrogen, lithium,                  magnesium, silicon, potassium, and/or iron. The                  sodium-containing compound comprises sodium                  fluoride, sodium aluminum fluoride (Na3AlF6),                  sodium cyanide, and/or sodium ferrocyanide. The                  fluorine-containing compound comprises sodium                  fluoride, Na3AlF6, aluminum fluoride, lithium                  fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride,                  and/or potassium fluoride. The                  fluorine-containing compound transforming agent                  comprises calcium, aluminum, magnesium,                  strontium, barium, lanthanum, and/or carbon. The                  cyanide transforming agent comprises hydrated                  sodium carbonate (Na2CO3-1.5H2O2), hydrated                  potassium carbonate (K2CO3-1.5H2O2), sodium                  peroxide, potassium peroxide, calcium oxide,                  strontium oxide, barium oxide, hydrogen peroxide,                  ammonium sulfate, sodium sulfate, potassium                  sulfate, potassium manganate and/or oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The grinding aid                  comprises polymeric polyalcohol, polymeric                  alkylol amine, triisopropanolamine, glycol,                  propanediol, propanetriol, diethylene glycol,                  triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid, oleic acid,                  sodium hexametaphosphate, and/or sodium                  stearate.

PN CN108840629-A
TI Preparation of concrete involves preparing modified peanut shell powder, preparing polyallylamine-modified glucose mixture, mixing with sodium borohydride, dialyzing, mixing with modified peanut shell powder and ultrasonically dispersing.
AU QIU Y
   LIU X
   ZHU D
AE FOSHAN LINGCHAO NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201894348U
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of concrete involves preparing modified peanut shell powder, preparing modified glucose mixture, preparing polyallylamine-modified glucose mixture, mixing polyallylamine-modified glucose mixture and sodium borohydride and stirring to obtain gel mixture and dialyzing the gel mixture to obtain gel, mixing the gel and water to obtain gel dispersion, mixing the gel dispersion and modified peanut shell powder, ultrasonically dispersing, adding 0.1-0.3 times of calcium chloride to the gel dispersion, mixing, filtering and drying to obtain additives, taking 55-75 pts. wt. ordinary Portland cement, 80-100 pts. wt. sand, 30-40 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-12 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 15-20 pts. wt. residue powder, 12-20 pts. wt. additives and 30-40 pts. wt. water, mixing ordinary Portland cement with water, adding sand in sequence under carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding fly ash, residue powder, additive and water reducing agent and mixing.
   USE - Preparation of concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of concrete ensuring excellent crack resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of concrete involves (1) crushing the peanut shell, sieve and drying to obtain peanut shell powder, mixing peanut shell powder and sodium hydroxide solution at mass ratio of 1:15-1:20, stirring, filtering and drying to obtain pretreated peanut shell powder, (2) mixing pretreated peanut shell powder and citric acid solution at mass ratio of 1:10-1:20, stirring, filtering and drying to obtain modified peanut shell powder, (3) mixing glucose and potassium iodate at mass ratio of 8:1-10:1, and adding 15-20 times of glucose, stirring and mixing to obtain glucose mixture, mixing glucose mixture with cerium chloride solution at mass ratio of 10:1-15:1 and filtering to obtain filtrate, where the mass ratio of filtrate and sodium sulfate solution is 15:1-18:1, mixing, stirring and filtering to obtain modified glucose mixture, (4) mixing polyallylamine and water at mass ratio of 1:100-1:110 and adjusting pH to 9.8-10.0 to obtain polyallylamine solution, stirring vigorously, mixing modified glucose mixture and polyallylamine solution at mass ratio of 1:8-1:10 and stirring to obtain polyallylamine-modified glucose mixture, (5) mixing polyallylamine-modified glucose mixture and sodium borohydride at mass ratio of 200:1-250:1 and stirring to obtain gel mixture and dialyzing the gel mixture to obtain gel, mixing the gel and water at mass ratio of 1:10-1:12 to obtain gel dispersion, (6) mixing the gel dispersion and modified peanut shell powder at mass ratio of 3:1-5:1, ultrasonically dispersing, adding 0.1-0.3 times of calcium chloride to the gel dispersion, mixing, filtering and drying to obtain additives, (7) taking 55-75 pts. wt. ordinary Portland cement, 80-100 pts. wt. sand, 30-40 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-12 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 15-20 pts. wt. residue powder, 12-20 pts. wt. additives and 30-40 pts. wt. water, mixing ordinary Portland cement with water, adding sand in sequence under carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding fly ash, residue powder, additive and water reducing agent and mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The sand comprises mixture of sand with particle size of 100 mesh and sand with particle size of 20 mesh at mass ratio of 1:2-1:3. Preferred Composition: The water reducing agent is sodium lignosulfonate or melamine water reducing agent.

PN CN108837834-A; CN108837834-B
TI Catalyst useful for directly producing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG J
   WANG X
   ZHAO T
   GAO X
   MA Q
   FAN S
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2018944101
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is a multi-layered ABCOx compolex system structure, where the A is an alkaline auxiliary agent, which is a lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium or cesium element and the amount is 0.1-10% of the total amount of catalyst, the B is iron, zinc and/or zirconium elements, the C is titanium element, the O is oxygen element, the x is the number of oxygen atoms, the BCOx is the total amount of catalyst 5-90%, the BCOx provides catalytic active sites for the catalyst, and having a regular octahedral structure, the elements in B and the elements in C are located at the center of the regular octahedron and the oxygen atoms are located at the apex of the regular octahedron, the BCOx is layered and negatively charged.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for directly producing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst for directly producing low-carbon olefin by hydrogenation of CO2, where the catalyst is a multi-layered ABCOx complex system structure, where the A is an alkaline auxiliary agent, which is a lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium or cesium element and the amount is 0.1-10% of the total amount of catalyst, the B is iron, zinc and/or zirconium elements, the C is titanium element, the O is oxygen element, the x is the number of oxygen atoms, the BCOx is the total amount of catalyst 5-90%, the BCOx provides catalytic active sites for the catalyst, and having a regular octahedral structure, the elements in B and the elements in C are located at the center of the regular octahedron and the oxygen atoms are located at the apex of the regular octahedron, the BCOx is layered and negatively charged, the element in A is ionic, positively charged and is filled in the BCOx layered sequence in a balanced charge manner A and BCOx interact to balance the charge and increase the basic position, the catalyst layer and the layer spacing are 0.2-10 nm, preferably 0.5-3 nm. The catalyst is obtained by taking the elements in A and B are derived from the compound of the A and B elements, and the elements in the C are derived from the compound of the C element in the molar ratio is A: B: C = 0.6 to 11:1.-48:1-17, mixing and grinding at 120 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain the ABCOx precursor before calcinations, (ii) calcining at 500 degrees C for 1-3 hours and homogenizing, (iii) taking the powder sample obtained after calcinations in an atmosphere of nitrogen, syngas or oxygen at a gas flow rate is |/ 50 ml/minute, preferably air, performing segmental calcination in a tube furnace, heating to 500 degrees C at 10-30 degrees C/minute for 0.5 hours, heating to 800 degrees C at 5-10 degrees C/minute for 0.5-2 hours, heating to 1000 degrees C at a heating rate of 10 degrees C/minutes rises for 0.5-2 hours, heating to 1100 degrees C at 2-10 degrees C/minute for 3-12 hours, cooling naturally and grinding uniformaly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the catalyst is obtained by grinding the compound containing the A element to more than 100 mesh, grinding the compound added with the C element to more than 150 mesh, grinding compound added with the B element to more than 100 mesh and drying at 120 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a pre-calcined ABCOx precursor. The catalyst is a layered structure or a block morphology.

TI Solar heat insulating material comprises e.g. sodium silicate, sodium fluosilicate, modified expanded perlite, hydrogen peroxide, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, additive, calcium bicarbonate and modified expanded perlite.
AU LIU J
   LIU X
   ZHU H
AE FOSHAN TENGLI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (FOSH-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG TESHENG BUILDING MATERIALS CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201870288R
AB    NOVELTY - Solar heat insulating material comprises 50-60 pts. wt. sodium silicate, 7-12 pts. wt. sodium fluosilicate, 10-18 pts. wt. modified expanded perlite, 3-5 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, 1-2 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, 10-15 pts. wt. additive, 2-6 pts. wt. calcium bicarbonate, the modified expanded perlite is prepared by mixing (N-amidino)-dodecyl acrylamide and polyethylene glycol derivatives with ratio 4:1-5:1, and adding the polyethylene glycol derivative is 0.1-0.2 times of the mass of p-dichlorobenzene, polyethylene glycol derivative is 0.08-0.12 times of the mass of the ferrocene and glycol derivative is 10-15 times of the mass of tetrahydrofuran, stirring and mixing to obtain the treating agent, the treating agent is mixed with expanded perlite according to the mass ratio of 5:1-5:2, distilling and concentrating, allowing to stand under a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the modified expanded perlite.
   USE - Used as solar heat insulating material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solar heat insulating material comprises 50-60 pts. wt. sodium silicate, 7-12 pts. wt. sodium fluosilicate, 10-18 pts. wt. modified expanded perlite, 3-5 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, 1-2 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, 10-15 pts. wt. additive, 2-6 pts. wt. calcium bicarbonate, the modified expanded perlite is prepared by mixing (N-amidino) - dodecyl acrylamide and polyethylene glycol derivatives with ratio 4:1-5:1, and adding the polyethylene glycol derivative is 0.1-0.2 times of the mass of p-dichlorobenzene, polyethylene glycol derivative is 0.08-0.12 times of the mass of the ferrocene and glycol derivative is 10-15 times of the mass of tetrahydrofuran, stirring and mixing to obtain the treating agent, the treating agent is mixed with expanded perlite according to the mass ratio of 5:1-5:2, distilling and concentrating, allowing to stand under a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the modified expanded perlite, the additive is prepared by melamine and formaldehyde mixed with mass ratio of 1:2-1:3, adding melamine 8-10 times of the mass of water, stirring and mixing, adjusting pH value to 8-9, continuously stirring and reacting to obtain prepolymer solution, the styrene-maleic anhydride copolymer and sodium hydroxide mass ratio is 5:1-6:1 and adding the styrene-maleic anhydride copolymer by 8-9 times of water, adjusting the pH value to 4.2-4.5, stirring and mixing to obtain the styrene-maleic anhydride copolymer latex, the styrene-maleic anhydride copolymer emulsion and the paraffin mixed according to mass ratio of 5:1-6:1, stirring and mixing to obtain the paraffin emulsion, mixing ratio of 1:2 by volume to the graphene oxide dispersing liquid and paraffin emulsion, and adding oxidized graphene dispersion liquid whose volume is 1-2 times of the polymer solution, stirring and reacting, filtering, washing and vacuum drying to obtain the additive. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing solar heat insulating material comprising weighing sodium silicate and sodium fluosilicate with water, adding methylcellulose, stirring and mixing to obtain the soluble glass solution, mixing modified perlite with additive, adding calcium bicarbonate and sodium silicate solution, stirring and mixing to obtain the mixed material, mixing mixed material with hydrogen peroxide, allowing to stand, injection molding, drying and demolding to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of sodium silicate and water is 1:3-1:4. The polyethylene glycol derivative is polyethylene glycol-p-toluene sulfonate or glycol salicylate. The expansion perlite grain diameter is 80 meshes. The paraffin whose carbon atom number is 24-32 paraffin mixture. The dispersion of graphene oxide to graphene oxide and water mixing mass ratio is 1:100-1:120 and carrying out ultrasonically dispersing to obtain oxidized graphene dispersion liquid.

PN CN108440916-A
TI Preparation of artificial culture stone, involves reacting sodium aluminate, styrene, vinyltrimethoxysilane and benzotriazole to obtain cultural stone blank, dewatering, adding fly ash, ball milling, filtering, molding, insulating, cooling.
AU SHEN C
   HE W
   ZHANG J
AE FOSHAN JIUMO TECHNOLOGY INFORMATION CONS (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201868190Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing artificial culture stone, involves adding sodium aluminate solution to plastic tank, passing carbon dioxide gas, leaving still, removing filtrate, heating and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide powder, placing the powder in high-pressure air flow machine, adding sodium silicate solution, dispersing, mixing polystyrene and vinyl acetate resin to obtain mixed resin, adding styrene, adding E-51 epoxy resin pellets, standard bisphenol A epoxy vinyl resin pellets and dispersion glue, heating, adding vinyltrimethoxysilane and benzotriazole to obtain cultural stone blank, dewatering, heating, drying to obtain dewatered sludge, adding fly ash and smectite clay, ball milling, screening to obtain mixed powder, adding deionized water to mixed powder, mixing, filtering, mixing cultural stone blank, pouring into bulk molding compound metal mold, insulating, naturally cooling to room temperature and demolding to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing artificial culture stone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of lightweight high-strength artificial culture stone with improved interface bonding force between the artificial culture stone inorganic particles and polymer resin, reduced curing shrinkage rate and increased strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing artificial culture stone, involves (1) adding 700-800 mL sodium aluminate solution to plastic tank, passing carbon dioxide gas into the plastic tank, leaving still, after the ventilation is completed, obtaining settling mixture, placing the settled mixture in flat plate filter, removing filtrate by filtration to obtain filter residue, placing the filter residue in oven, heating and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide powder, (2) placing the aluminum hydroxide powder in high-pressure air flow machine to obtain ultrafine aluminum hydroxide powder, adding 200-220 g ultrafine aluminum hydroxide powder to 500-550 mL sodium silicate solution, obtaining gelatinous solution, dispersing the colloidal liquid in high-speed dispersing machine to obtain dispersing glue, (3) mixing 60-70 pts. wt. polystyrene and 30-40 pts. wt. vinyl acetate resin to obtain mixed resin, pouring the mixed resin into three-necked flask-containing 60-70 pts. wt. styrene, heating, mixing, dispersing, stirring, discharging and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain low-shrinkage foam particles, (4) adding 20-25 pts. wt. E-51 epoxy resin pellets and 50-60 pts. wt. standard bisphenol A epoxy vinyl resin pellets, mixing to obtain cultural stone base, placing cultural stone base and 40-50 pts. wt. dispersion glue in metal tank, stirring, mixing, heating, insulating, adding 10-12 pts. wt. vinyltrimethoxysilane and 15-18 pts. wt. benzotriazole to metal tank, continuously reacting to obtain cultural stone blank, (5) dewatering the secondary sedimentation sludge in sludge dewatering machine, placing in an oven, heating, drying to obtain dewatered sludge, adding dewatered sludge, fly ash and smectite clay, placing in a ball mill, ball milling, screening to obtain mixed powder, adding 700-800 mL deionized water to 2-3 kg mixed powder, stirring, mixing, filtering to remove the filtrate, obtaining raw material ball, (6) mixing the cultural stone blank with raw material ball, pouring into bulk molding compound metal mold, curing, removing the pressure, insulating, naturally cooling to room temperature and demolding to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (1), the mass fraction of sodium aluminate solution is 20%. In step (2), the mass fraction of sodium silicate solution is 30 %mass. In step (5), the mass ratio of dewatered sludge, fly ash and montmorillonite clay is 5:1:2. The mass ratio of culture stone blank to raw material ball is 4:1. Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the aeration rate of carbon dioxide gas is 20-30 mL/minute. The ventilation time in the plastic tank is 40-45 minutes. After the ventilation is completed, the static settling time is 3-4 hours. The heating process is carried out at 90-100 degrees C and drying process is carried out for 4-5 hours. In step (2), the pulverization process is carried out for 10-15 minutes and the dispersing process is carried out at 3000-3300 rpm. The step (3) comprises heating at 80-90 degrees C and stirring at 300-350 rpm for 4-5 hours. The step (4) comprises heating at 100-110 degrees C, leaving still for 4-5 hours and continuing the reaction for 10-20 minutes. The step (5) comprises dehydrating for 30-40 minutes, heating at 70-80 degrees C and drying for 4-5 hours. The step (5) comprises ball milling for 3-4 hours. The step (6) involves molding at temperatures of 115-120 degrees C and 130-135 degrees C, pressure of 11-15 MPa for 45-50 seconds and the holding time is 4-5 hours. Preferred Properties: In step (2), the particle size of aluminum hydroxide is 50-80 mu m. In step (5), the sieve size is 200 mesh sieve and particle size is 2-3 mm. The size of bulk molding compound metal mold is 80 mmx 60 mmx 10 mm.

TI Dropping pills comprises e.g. Ganoderma lucidum            spore oil, Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract ultrafine            powder, Ganoderma lucidum water extract ultrafine            powder and matrix and its functional ingredient index            is Ganoderma lucidum.
AU LI Y
   ZHOU Y
   WANG Q
   HUANG Y
   YAO W
   WU C
AE FUJIAN XIANZHILOU BIOLOGICAL CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
   GANOHERB TECHNOLOGY FUJIAN CORP (GANO-Non-standard)
GA 201859359S
AB    NOVELTY - Dropping pills comprises e.g. Ganoderma                lucidum spore oil, Ganoderma lucidum alcohol                extract ultrafine powder, Ganoderma lucidum water                extract ultrafine powder and matrix, where its                functional ingredient index is Ganoderma lucidum                and Ganoderma lucidum polysaccharides, where the                Ganoderma lucidum spore oil is obtained by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction from                Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and Ganoderma                lucidum triterpenoids, where Ganoderma lucidum                alcohol extract ultrafine powder is prepared by                ultra-finely pulverizing Ganoderma lucidum fruit                body, treating by ethanol, extracting, centrifuging                to collect filtrate, concentrating, purifying with                water, further centrifuging, freeze-drying,                performing ultra-fine pulverization and Ganoderma                lucidum, and Ganoderma lucidum water extract                ultrafine powder is prepared by ultra-finely                pulverizing by Ganoderma lucidum fruit body.
   USE - Used as dropping pills.
   ADVANTAGE - The dropping pills are rich in functional                ingredients of Ganoderma lucidum, ensures clear                form of ingredients, has stable quality, rapid                disintegration speed, and has high bioavailability,                is convenient to carrying, simple taking mode and                good mouth-feel efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dropping pills comprises Ganoderma lucidum                spore oil, Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract                ultrafine powder, Ganoderma lucidum water extract                ultrafine powder and matrix at the weight ratio of                1:2-3:3-2:6, where its functional ingredient index                is Ganoderma lucidum and Ganoderma lucidum                polysaccharides, where the Ganoderma lucidum spore                oil is obtained by supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction from Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and                main functional ingredient is 30-31 wt.% Ganoderma                lucidum triterpenoids, where Ganoderma lucidum                alcohol extract ultrafine powder is prepared by                ultra-finely pulverizing Ganoderma lucidum fruit                body and treating by 95% ethanol, extracting, and                centrifuging to take the filtrate, concentrating,                purifying with water, further centrifuging,                freeze-drying, performing ultra-fine pulverization,                where the main functional ingredient is 20-21%                Ganoderma lucidum, where Ganoderma lucidum water                extract ultrafine powder is prepared by                ultra-finely pulverizing by Ganoderma lucidum fruit                body and extracting with purified water,                centrifuging the filtrate, concentrating, and aging                with food grade 95% ethanol, precipitating by                centrifugation, freeze-drying and ultra-finely                pulverizing and having the main functional                ingredients including Ganoderma lucidum                polysaccharide content: 24-25%, where Ganoderma                lucidum spore oil extract has a dropping weight of                300 plus minus 15 mg, of which Ganoderma lucidum                triterpenoid content is 5.5-7.5%, and Ganoderma                lucidum polysaccharide content is 4-6%. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing                dropping pill, comprising mixing ultrafine powder                of Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract and the                ultrafine powder of Ganoderma lucidum water extract                are according to different weight ratios of                2:3-3:2, grinding the mixed powder and Ganoderma                lucidum spore oil and mixing at a weight ratio of                5:1, and suspending the obtained mixture and                substrate at a weight ratio of 1:1, and uniformly                dispersing, when the mixture is mixed with the                substrate, the temperature of the solution is kept                at 70-75 degrees C, where the final suspended                solution is dropped into the condensing agent                through the cooling column at a rate of 20-30                drops/minute, and the condensation temperature is 3                degrees C, dropping distance is 5-9 cm, taking out                the dropping pills, removing the surface condensing                agent and drying to obtain Ganoderma lucidum spore                oil extract dropping pills, where the weight of                dropping pills is 300 plus minus 15 mg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The Ganoderma lucidum                  spore oil is produced by granulating Ganoderma                  lucidum spore powder with water, drying to below                  3% moisture, sieving the whole grain with 10-100                  meshes, where Ganoderma lucidum spore powder                  particles having 10-100 meshes which are                  dynamically extracting by supercritical carbon                  dioxide equipment at the extraction temperature                  of 40 degrees C, where the extraction pressure is                  30 Mpa, extraction time is 1 hour at a flow rate                  of 200-210 l/hour, and isolating the Ganoderma                  lucidum spore oil from the separation kettle. The                  ganoderma lucidum fruit body ultrafine powder is                  prepared by sieving the Ganoderma lucidum fruit                  body to remove impurities, and pulverizing to 20                  meshes to obtain a coarse powder, crushing by                  ultrafine, and drying the coarse powder of                  Ganoderma lucidum fruit body to a moisture                  content of less than 3%, ultrafinely pulverizing                  for 1 hour, and sieving through 400 meshes. The                  Ganoderma lucidum alcohol extract ultrafine                  powder is prepared by adding a food grade of                  10-12 times the weight of the raw material of the                  Ganoderma lucidum fruit body ultrafine powder by                  95% ethanol extraction, where extraction                  temperature is 85-90 degrees C, extraction time                  is 2-3 hours, centrifuging the extract with a                  horizontal and butterfly centrifuge, where the                  drum speed of the horizontal centrifuge was 3200                  revolutions/minute (rpm), the separation factor                  is 2400 g, and the turbidity of the separated                  turbid liquid reaches less than 210 nut or the                  rotation speed of the butterfly centrifuge is                  6000 rpm, where the separation factor is 10000 g,                  and the turbidity of the separated supernatant is                  less than 20 nut, clearing the solution to a                  relative density of 1.20 (60 degrees C) and                  adding 4 times the weight of purified water to                  allow to cool to room temperature and overnight                  at 4 degrees C, centrifuging by using a butterfly                  centrifuge, where the disc centrifuge rotates at                  a speed of 7200 rpm, and the separation factor is                  12000 g, taking out the precipitate and                  freeze-drying to obtain the solid of the extract,                  and pulverizing to 400 mesh to obtain the extract                  of Ganoderma lucidum ultra-fine powder, and                  sealing in an environment for not greater than 4                  degrees C. The Ganoderma lucidum water extract                  ultrafine powder is prepared by adding 10-12                  times of purified water of the Ganoderma lucidum                  fruit body ultrafine powder to the raw material                  extraction, extracting at 98-120 degrees C for                  2-3 hours, centrifuging the extract with a                  horizontal and butterfly centrifuge, where the                  drum speed of the horizontal centrifuge is 3800                  rpm, and the separation factor is 2800 g and the                  turbidity of the separated turbid liquid reaches                  to below 210 nut and the rotation speed of the                  butterfly centrifuge is 7200 rpm, and the                  separation factor is 12000 g and the separated                  turbidity is below 20 nut, taking out the                  supernatant and concentrating to a relative                  density of 1.05 (85 degrees C), adding 4 times                  the weight of the food grade 95% ethanol alcohol                  precipitation, allowing to cool to room                  temperature under 4 degrees C environment under                  overnight, using a butterfly centrifuge to                  centrifuge, and the butterfly centrifuge drum                  speed is 7200 rpm, where the separation factor is                  12000 g, taking out the precipitate,                  freeze-drying to obtain the extract solid, and                  immediately pulverizing through 400 meshes,                  obtaining the ultrafine powder of the Ganoderma                  lucidum alcohol extract, sealing and storing in                  an environment of not greater than 4 degrees                  C.

PN CN108315153-A
TI Preparation of wine involves soaking sticky rice and red yeast rice, controlling temperature, repeatedly washing to absorb water and expand, mixing sticky rice, Mengshan small molecular mineral water and red yeast rice, and decomposing.
AU XING Z
   TAO S
AE XING Z (XING-Individual)
   TAO S (TAOS-Individual)
GA 201863574D
AB    NOVELTY - A wine is prepared by soaking sticky rice and red yeast rice, controlling temperature, repeatedly washing to absorb water and expand, taking sticky rice, Mengshan small molecular mineral water and red yeast rice, putting rice into jar, uniformly mixing, and decomposing to produce amino acid and other nutrient substance; heating starch in sticky rice and red yeast rice by steamed rice, saccharifying and fermenting, removing steamed sticky rice and red yeast rice from pot, pouring, spreading for cooling, and airing; and putting into cylinder, uniformly stirring, uniformly stirring sticky rice and red yeast rice into fermentation tank, continuously stirring, adding yeast, Aspergillus, Rhizopus and red yeast, binding and interacting, saccharifying and fermenting to obtain mash, making fermentation components uniform, reducing content of carbon dioxide, supplementing oxygen, and inhibiting bacteria invasion propagation.
   USE - Method for preparing wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has closely matched process steps. The wine is mellow, and has synergistic effect, amber color, moderate wine degree, long aftertaste, natural health, high grade and sweet and mellow taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A wine is prepared by: (A) selecting sticky rice having high-quality, full grain, hard texture, innocent color, bright and fragrant, selecting red yeast rice having natural appearance and color, safe and non-toxic and strong tinting power to protein, and selecting Mengshan small molecular mineral water; (B) soaking sticky rice and red yeast rice for more than 12 hours, controlling temperature at 18-22 degrees C, repeatedly washing to absorb water and expand, taking sticky rice, Mengshan small molecular mineral water and red yeast rice at weight ratio of 10:15:1.5, putting rice into jar, uniformly mixing, and decomposing to produce amino acid and other nutrient substance; (C) heating starch in sticky rice and red yeast rice by steamed rice, saccharifying and fermenting, removing steamed sticky rice and red yeast rice from pot, pouring, spreading for cooling, and airing at 25-30 degrees C; (D) putting into cylinder, uniformly stirring, uniformly stirring sticky rice and red yeast rice into fermentation tank, continuously stirring, adding yeast, Aspergillus, Rhizopus and red yeast, binding and interacting, saccharifying and fermenting to obtain mash, making fermentation components uniform, reducing content of carbon dioxide, supplementing oxygen, and inhibiting bacteria invasion propagation; (E) reducing contact area with air, alcohol volatilizing, controlling mixed bacteria propagation, improving wine quality, controlling general fermentation room temperature to 18-22 degrees C, fermenting for 48-52 days, distilling, and cooling to obtain clear and transparent, mellow and delicate fragrance faint scent type white wine; (F) dipping sticky rice and red yeast rice for 12 hours, repeatedly washing, putting into steamer to steam, taking out, taking sticky rice, Mengshan small molecular mineral water, faint scent type white wine and red yeast rice at weight ratio of 10:5:20:1,5, stirring sticky rice, Mengshan small molecular mineral water and red yeast, putting into fermentation tank, naturally fermenting for 3-5 days, pouring faint scent type white wine, and secondary saccharifying and fermenting; and (G) taking put secondary fermented wine, placing in glass bottle, and clarifying at low temperature for 1 week.

PN CN108293582-A
TI Culturing a molded Ganoderma lucidum comprises taking sawdust, wheat husk, chicken manure, potato powder, tea slag, bean curd, phosphate fertilizer, sucrose and gypsum powder; mixing, adding water, stirring, filing, sterilizing and stacking.
AU YU Y
AE FEIDONG FENGBAO LINGZHI BREEDING CO LTD (FEID-Non-standard)
GA 2018583324
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing a molded Ganoderma lucidum comprises (i) taking sawdust, wheat husk, chicken manure, potato powder, tea slag, bean curd, phosphate fertilizer, sucrose and gypsum powder; (ii) mixing the above mentioned raw materials, adding water and stirring, then filing the bag and sterilizing; (iii) stacking the bacteria bag on the sterilization stove, sterilizing and maintaining; (iv) transferring the sterilized bacteria bag to the sterile room for cooling and inoculating the bacteria bag by using the eye-inoculation method; (v) placing the inoculated bacteria in a sterile room and culturing; (vi) transporting the bacteria-coated bacteria into the planting greenhouse; (vii) covering the top of the bag; (viii) adjusting the plastic wrap and the aluminum film of the sunshade and trimming to obtain directional cultivation of Ganoderma lucidum; and (ix) air-packing the Ganoderma lucidum, controlling carbon dioxide concentration, humidity, temperature and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing a molded Ganoderma lucidum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses simple equipment, easy to mold the Ganoderma lucidum and has appearance, high output and the shape of the Ganoderma lucidum is good for production of the bonsai, vivid and natural.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing a molded Ganoderma lucidum comprises (i) taking 37 wt.% sawdust, 22 wt.% wheat husk, 16 wt.% chicken manure, 12 wt.% potato powder, 7 wt.% tea slag, 3 wt.% bean curd, 1 wt.% phosphate fertilizer, 1 wt.% sucrose and 1 wt.% gypsum powder; (ii) mixing the above mentioned raw materials, adding water and uniformly stirring at 200-400 rotations/minute until the moisture content of the raw materials reaches 58-64%, then filing the bag with the flat length of 63cmx 32 cm flat width and sterilizing the bacteria bag by ethylene oxide; (iii) randomly stacking the bacteria bag on the sterilization stove and sterilizing by Co60 and maintaining for 1-2 hours; (iv) transferring the sterilized bacteria bag to the sterile room for cooling, when the package temperature drops to 25 degrees C inoculating the bacteria bag by using the eye-inoculation method including adding ten rows of equidistant eye holes on the round surface of the bacteria bag using a sterilized tapered iron rod, five bag per row, one per hole and then inoculating the bacteria into the hole with a sterile inoculating loop; (v) placing the inoculated bacteria in a sterile room, culturing the bacteria at 37-45 degrees C with the humidity of the bacteria to 60-80% and the time to not greater than one week; (vi) transporting the bacteria-coated bacteria into the planting greenhouse, and the angle between the bacteria bag and the planting frame in the greenhouse is placed at an oblique angle of 75 degrees ; (vii) covering the plastic wrap and the aluminum film on the top of the bag; (viii) adjusting the plastic wrap and the aluminum film of the sunshade according to the biological characteristics of Ganoderma lucidum and trimming to obtain directional cultivation of Ganoderma lucidum; and (ix) air-packing the Ganoderma lucidum, controlling the carbon dioxide concentration to 12-20%, humidity to 25-35%, temperature to 16-18 degrees C and storing in the dark environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In step (ii) the moisture content of the raw materials reaches 61.5%. Preferred Method: In step (ii) the stirring speed is 300 rotations/minute. The step (iii) is maintained for 1.5 hours. In step (v) the bacteria culturing temperature is 40.7 degrees C and the humidity of the bacteria is 70%. In step (ix) the carbon dioxide concentration is controlled at 16%, the humidity is controlled at 30.4% and the temperature is controlled at 17 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).);

PN CN107996284-A
TI Producing mushroom, by inoculating mushroom strain into culture medium, culturing, injecting fungal strains, culturing, providing light source after formation of mushroom buds, growing into young mushroom, and culturing product.
AU CHANG M
   CHANG Z
   DENG Z
   LIU M
   CHANG G
   LI Z
   ZHENG C
   WANG B
   DING Y
   GUO H
AE SHANDONG CHANGSHENGYUAN FLORA CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG ZOULU AGRIC MICROBIOLOGY TECHNOLOGY RES (SHAN-Non-standard)
   JINING CHANGFENG EDIBLE FUNGUS CO LTD (JINI-Non-standard)
GA 2018379409
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing mushroom, involves (a) inoculating mushroom strains into culture medium under sterile conditions, (b) controlling culture chamber at 20-24 degrees C with relative humidity of 60-70%, providing fresh air, culturing and providing light for 40-45 days, until the hypha get matured, (c) removing old strains and old hypha layer of culture material, injecting fungal strains and 5-10 ml clear water into strainer and draining the remaining water for 1 hour, (d) culturing, until the flesh of the bottle neck becomes flock-like and the color becomes gray, (e) providing light source after formation of mushroom at 12-14 degrees C after mushroom bud grows to 1 cm, removing the cover, and (f) shaping and growing buds into small young mushroom, opening bag, rolling up close to material surface, increasing ventilation and culturing product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product with prolonged shelf-life of 8-10 days without browning and increases crude fiber content to 2.01 g and reduces fat content to 0.04 g.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing mushroom, involves (a) inoculating mushroom strains into culture medium under sterile conditions, (b) controlling culture chamber at 20-24 degrees C with relative humidity of 60-70%, providing fresh air, culturing for 35-40 days until the bottle is full of mycelium, cooking, raising temperature to 23-25 degrees C and maintaining relative humidity to 60-70%, providing fresh air and protecting light for 40-45 days, until the hypha get matured, (c) removing old strains with surface thickness of 5-6 mm and old hypha layer of culture material, injecting fungal strains and 5-10 ml clear water into strainer and draining the remaining water for 1 hour, (d) placing bottles at 14-16 degrees C with relative humidity 90-95% and less than 0.1% carbon dioxide, culturing under darkness for 5-7 days, until the flesh of the bottle neck becomes flock-like and the color becomes gray, reducing temperature of mushroom to 12-14 degrees C for 7-10 days, until the needle-like taupe mushroom bud appears on the material surface, (e) providing light source at 100-200 lx after formation of mushroom at 12-14 degrees C with relative humidity 90-95% after mushroom bud grows to 1 cm, removing the cover, where the mushroom is maintained at room temperature of 14-16 degrees C and relative humidity of 85-95%, carbon dioxide content of below 0.1 volume/volume%, light intensity of 500-1000 lx for 5-7 days and (f) shaping and growing buds into small young mushroom, opening bag, rolling up close to material surface, increasing ventilation, where the light intensity is 150-300 lx, temperature is controlled at 10-20 degrees C with relative humidity of 85-90%, culturing for 7-10 days, flattening mushroom cap, where the middle is swollen or concave, the surface is shiny and the pleats are clearly stretched and harvesting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The culture medium comprises 70 wt.% broad-leaved sawdust, 20 wt.% or 18 wt.% wheat bran, 72 wt.% corncob powder, 4 wt.% rice bran, 4 wt.% soybean powder, 4 wt.% corn powder, 1 wt.% calcium sulfate and 1 wt.% calcium carbonate. The culture medium has moisture content of 63-65 wt.% and pH of 7.5-8. The culture medium undergoes a fermentation process at 65 degrees C for 4-5 days, turning after 24 hours for twice, cooling, exhausting gas, reducing moisture content to 60-65% and pH to 6.8-7.2.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
