PN CN107961199-A
TI Skin care cream used for e.g. removing acne, comprises traditional Chinese medicinal composition containing Codonopsis pilosula, plant extracts containing lavender essential oil and Melaleuca alternifolia leaf oil, and solvent.
AU ZHANG H
AE QINGDAO MINGFANGLIAN COMMERCE & TRADE CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201837916D
AB    NOVELTY - Skin care cream comprises traditional Chinese medicinal composition, plant extracts and a proper amount of solvent. The traditional Chinese medicinal composition comprises Codonopsis pilosula, mung bean, rhubarb, Chinese yam, dandelion, almond, lotus plumule and menthol. The plant extract comprises lavender essential oil, Aloe leaf extract, honeysuckle flower extract, Houttuynia cordata extract, Melaleuca alternifolia leaf oil and jojoba seed oil.
   USE - The skin care cream is useful for beautifying skin, removing acne and diminishing inflammation.
   ADVANTAGE - The skin care cream has excellent synergistic effect, and is free of side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Skin care cream comprises traditional Chinese medicinal composition, plant extracts and a proper amount of solvent. The traditional Chinese medicinal composition comprises 0.2-0.5 part mass Codonopsis pilosula, 0.1-0.3 part mass mung bean, 0.2-0.4 part mass rhubarb, 0.1-0.2 part mass Chinese yam, 0.05-0.15 part mass dandelion, 0.1-0.3 part mass almond, 0.1-0.3 part mass lotus plumule and 0.01-0.05 part mass menthol. The plant extract comprises 1-2 parts mass lavender essential oil, 1.5-3.5 parts mass Aloe leaf extract, 2-3 parts mass honeysuckle flower extract, 0.8-1.2 parts mass Houttuynia cordata extract, 1.5-2.5 parts mass Melaleuca alternifolia leaf oil and 3-5 parts mass jojoba seed oil. The method of extracting M.alternifolia leaf oil involves crushing and drying the leaves of M.alternifolia, then adding the mixture to an extraction tank, carrying out supercritical extraction using carbon dioxide as an extractant, introducing the carbon dioxide from the extraction tank into a heater for heating and then introducing into a rectification tower for rectifying to obtain the product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the skin care cream, which involves: (a) cleaning and drying the traditional Chinese medicinal composition until the water content is less than 7%, crushing, sieving to obtain a 200-mesh fine powder, and sterilizing the fine powder using UV rays for 10-15 minutes; (b) adding propylene glycol, benzyl alcohol, ethylhexylglycerin and purified water to a solvent, uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture (I), heating at 80 degrees C, and heat preserving; (c) adding caprylic acid/capric triglyceride, polydimethylsiloxane, sodium acrylate/sodium acryloyldimethyl taurate copolymer, isohexadecane and jojoba oil to a solvent, heating at 84 degrees C to completely melt, stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture (II); (d) pumping the mixture (I) obtained in step (a) and the mixture (II) obtained in step (c) into an emulsifying tank, adding polysorbate-80 and uniformly mixing and emulsifying; (e) cooling the temperature of emulsifying tank to 45 degrees C, adding the fine powder, stirring and carrying out ultrasonic treatment for 15-20 minutes, and uniformly mixing; and (f) adding lavender essential oil, Aloe leaf extract, honeysuckle flower extract, H.cordata extract and M.alternifolia leaf oil, fully and uniformly mixing, and discharging the mixture to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent includes propylene glycol, benzyl alcohol, caprylic/capric triglyceride, polydimethylsiloxane, cyclic pentamethicone, sodium acrylate/sodium acryloyldimethyl taurate copolymer, isohexadecane, ethylhexylglycerin, polysorbate-80 and/or purified water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Condition: The extracting step is carried out at a temperature of 40-55 degrees C under pressure of 12-20 MPa. Preferred Components: The traditional Chinese medicinal composition comprises 0.2 part mass Codonopsis pilosula, 0.3 part mass mung bean, 0.3 part mass rhubarb, 0.1 part mass Chinese yam, 0.08 part mass dandelion, 0.25 part mass almond, 0.15 part mass lotus plumule and 0.03 part mass menthol. The plant extract comprises 1.5 parts mass lavender essential oil, 2 parts mass Aloe leaf extract, 2.5 parts mass honeysuckle flower extract, 1.1 parts mass H.cordata extract, 2.2 parts mass M.alternifolia leaf oil and 4 parts mass jojoba seed oil.
   ACTIVITY - Antiseborrheic; Dermatological; Antiinflammatory.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01C1; B04-C03; B05-B01B; B10-E04; B10-G02; B10-J02; B12-M02B; B14-C03; B14-N17; B14-S09; B14-S18; D08-B09A1A; A12-V01; A12-V04C

PN CN107714569-A
TI Preparing mouthwash comprises e.g. crushing mint, honeysuckle, thyme into powder, placing in microwave extraction device, distilling, condensing to obtain aromatic plant extract containing essential oil and preparing Chrysanthemum extract.
AU GAO R
AE WEIHAI VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (WEIH-Non-standard)
GA 201817331U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mouthwash comprises e.g. crushing mint, honeysuckle and thyme into powder, adding purified water to aromatic plant powder, placing in microwave extraction device, controlling microwave extraction power, transferring extract to distillation pot, heating distillation pot, distilling aromatic plant oil-water mixture, passing through condensation tube, separating by an oil-water separator to obtain aromatic plant extract containing essential oils, preparing Chrysanthemum extract and Panax notoginseng extract, mixing raw materials, stirring, cooling and packaging.
   USE - The mouthwash is useful for relieving sore throat and cough.
   ADVANTAGE - The mouthwash has good mouth feel, is safe, non-toxic and is suitable for long-term use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mouthwash comprises (i) crushing mint, honeysuckle and thyme into powder, where ratio of peppermint powder, honeysuckle powder and thyme powder is 1:0.8-1.6:4-8, adding 5 times the volume of purified water to aromatic plant powder, placing in microwave extraction device, controlling microwave extraction power of 350-500 W for 15-25 minutes, transferring extract to distillation pot, heating distillation pot at 55-69 degrees C with pressure of 2-3 Kpa, continuously distilling for 1-1.5 hours, heating to 150-160 degrees C, distilling aromatic plant oil-water mixture, passing through condensation tube, maintaining inlet temperature of condenser at 150 degrees C and outlet temperature at 70 degrees C and separating by an oil-water separator to obtain aromatic plant extract containing essential oils; (ii) washing Chrysanthemum stem and drying, crushing into Chrysanthemum powder, passing through 20 mesh sieve, mixing Chrysanthemum powder with 95% ethanol cosolvent to prepare an extraction solution, introducing extraction solution in an extraction kettle and feeding extraction kettle with super critical carbon dioxide fluid, setting extractor pressure at 24-28 MPa, extracting at 35-55 degrees C for 2-4 hours, dissolving supercritical carbon dioxide fluid with extract in separator kettle I and separator kettle II, setting pressure of 6-8 MPa in separation kettle I and extracting at 44-48 degrees C and collecting extract I, maintaining pressure of 4-5 MPa in separation vessel II, extracting at 32-34 degrees C to obtain extract II and combining extract I and extract II to obtain Chrysanthemum extract; (iii) washing Panax notoginseng, vacuum drying at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours, crushing, sieving with 60-80 mesh sieve, sieving and refluxing Panax notoginseng powder in distilled water and extracting, where ratio of extracting liquor is 1:10-1:20, extracting at 80-95 degrees C for 60-90 minutes, filtering, centrifuging filtrate for 15-20 minutes at 10000-12100 g, filtering using filter paper, condensing filtrate to 40-50% of the original volume and concentrating filtrate Panax notoginseng extracting solution; and (iv) preparing aromatic mouthwash comprises 4-5 pts. wt. aromatic plant extracts, 6-8 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum extraction liquid, 3-5 pts. wt. Panax notoginseng extract, 2-3 pts. wt. Tween 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80), 0.05-2 pts. wt. fructooligosaccharide, 4-6 pts. wt. glycerol, 0.01-0.2 pts. wt. p-hydroxybenzoic acid, 3.5-5.5 pts. wt. tea tree oil and 600-800 pts. wt. purified water, mixing raw materials and stirring uniformly at 121 degrees C for 20 minutes, cooling to room temperature and packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) mixing aromatic plant powder, mint powder, honeysuckle powder and thyme powder in the ratio 1:1.2:6.3, using microwave extraction power of 450 W and extracting for 20 minutes. In the step (i) distilling at 62 degrees C for 1 hours. In the step (ii) maintaining extraction kettle pressure of 26 MPa and extracting at 45 degrees C for 3 hours. In the step (ii) maintaining pressure of separating kettle I at 7 MPa, extracting at 46 degrees C, maintaining pressure of separating kettle II at 4.5 MPa and extracting at 33 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The ratio of Chrysanthemum powder and ethanol cosolvent in the step (ii) is 1:4-6.

AU DAI H
   LIANG Y
   LIU Y
   WANG Y
   DENG J
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 201940789A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of titania/palladium tungstate catalyst comprises preparing lead tungstate through solvothermal synthesis, soaking nano-titania carrier, and calcining.
   USE - Method for preparation of titania/palladium tungstate catalyst used for oxidizing benzene to carbon dioxide and water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The loading amount palladium is 0.2-1%, preferably 0.67%. The loading amount of tungsten is 0.5-1.5%, preferably 0.93%. Preferred Method: The preparation of catalyst is done by adding 0.002 mmol acetylacetone palladium, 1-10 mL, preferably 5 mL toluene and 2-4 mL. preferably 3.5 mL oleylamine to magnetic stirrer, magnetically stirring at room temperature for 20 minutes, adding tungsten carbonate to obtain mixed precursor solution, stirring at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, transferring to reaction kettle, oven heating to 130-200 degrees C, preferably 160 degrees C for 3-6 hours, preferably 4 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding to centrifuge tube, adding ethanol, sonicating, centrifuging, repeatedly washing for 3-4 times to obtain ultra-thin palladium tungstate bimetallic nanosheet, dispersing in cyclohexane, and storing; calculating amount of nanosheet solution required, adding titania carrier, stirring for 12 hours, placing to porcelain boat, putting to muffle furnace, calcining to 320-380 degrees C, preferably 350 degrees C at rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours, preferably 2 hours, and cooling.

PN CN114391576-A
TI Preparing cherry fresh-keeping folium artemisiae argyi-based antibacterial spray agent used in fruit industry, comprises e.g. carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on ultrafinely pulverized folium artemisiae argyi leaf powder, extracting, concentrating the extract and emulsifying.
AU JIN B
   LONG L
   CHEN Y
   HU H
   LIAO Z
AE JINGPAI CHIZHENGTANG PHARM CO LTD (JING-Non-standard)
GA 202262988K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cherry fresh-keeping folium artemisiae argyi-based antibacterial spray agent comprises (1) using an ultra-fine pulverizer to pulverize the dried folium artemisiae argyi leaves, and collecting the undersize, (2) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on ultrafinely pulverized folium artemisiae argyi leaf powder, and collecting essential oil of folium artemisiae argyi leaves and remaining folium artemisiae argyi powder in the extraction bag, (3) using ethanol for the remaining argyi leaf powder, extracting, and combining extracts, (4) concentrating the extract to paste, dissolving with pure water, collecting supernatant liquid after allowing to stand, centrifuging and collecting supernatant liquid, (5) passing the supernatant after centrifugation through a ceramic membrane, (6) emulsifying argyi leaf essential oil and ethanol, and spray drying, and (7) preparing chitosan oligosaccharide, microcapsule powder C and ceramic membrane permeate A,
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cherry fresh-keeping folium artemisiae argyi-based antibacterial spray agent, which is used in fruit industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the synergistic bacteriostasis effect between efficient bacteriostatic components in the leaves and the chitosan oligosaccharide. The antibacterial spray agent: is naturally available and edible; has stronger antibacterial and fresh-keeping effect and strong stability, and excellent fresh-keeping effect; inhibits the growth of pathogenic bacteria and the dehydration of materials; and prolongs the shelf life of the food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cherry fresh-keeping folium artemisiae argyi-based antibacterial spray agent comprises comprises (1) using an ultra-fine pulverizer to pulverize the dried folium artemisiae argyi leaves, passing through a 20-100 mesh pharmacopoeia sieve, and collecting the undersize, (2) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the ultrafinely pulverized folium artemisiae argyi leaf powder at extraction temperature of 45-55degrees Celsius and extraction pressure of 25-35MPa, carbon dioxide flow of 25-35 l/hour for 0.5-1.5 hours, and collecting the essential oil of folium artemisiae argyi leaves and the remaining folium artemisiae argyi powder in the extraction bag respectively, (3) using 20-40 times the amount of 50-95% ethanol for the remaining argyi leaf powder, extracting 1-2 times under the condition of 40-80 degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours each time, and combining the obtained extracts, (4) concentrating the extract to paste, dissolving with 1-4 times the amount of pure water, collecting the supernatant liquid after allowing to stand for 24-48 hours, centrifuging the supernatant liquid and collecting the supernatant liquid, (5) passing the supernatant after centrifugation through a ceramic membrane with a pore size of 20-200 nm at a flow rate of 40-80 gallons/minute, and collecting the ceramic membrane permeate A, (6) emulsifying argyi leaf essential oil and 40-70% ethanol in a mass ratio of 1:7-10 to obtain the emulsion B, using a homogenizer to homogenize the inulin and the emulsion B according to the mass ratio of 1:5-10, spray drying under the conditions of inlet air temperature of 155-170degreesC and outlet air temperature of 80-95degreesC to obtain microcapsule powder C, and (7) preparing chitosan oligosaccharide, microcapsule powder C and ceramic membrane permeate A, where the composition includes 20-40 g/l chitosan oligosaccharide, and : 1-5 g/l microcapsule powder C, and ceramic membrane permeate A (balance)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (7), the composition of the fresh-keeping spray is 25g/l chitosan oligosaccharide, 3 g/l microcapsule powder C, and Ceramic membrane permeate A (balance)Preferred Components: In the step (1), the mesh number of the pharmacopoeia sieve used is 80 meshes. In the step (2), the preferred supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process is that the extraction temperature is 50degrees Celsius, the extraction pressure is 35 MPa, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 30 l/h, and the extraction time is 1.5 hours. In the step (3), the mass ratio of artemisiae argyi to ethanol is 1:25. The concentration of ethanol used is 65%. The extraction temperature is 60degrees Celsius, the extraction time is 3 hours, and the extraction times are 2 times. In the step (4), the mass ratio of the extract to pure water is 1:3, and the time for allowing to stand is 24 hours. In the step (5), the flow rate of the supernatant through the ceramic membrane is 45 gallons/minute. The pore size of the ceramic membrane is 100 nm. In the step (6), the mass fraction of ethanol is 60%, and the mass ratio of argyi leaf essential oil and ethanol is 1:8. The mass ratio of inulin and emulsion B is 1:8, the inlet air temperature is 165degrees Celsius, and the outlet air temperature is 80degrees Celsius. The average molecular mass of the chitosan oligosaccharides used was 1000-1500 Da.

PN CN114314616-A
TI Extraction of potassium carbonate and aluminum oxide from potassium-rich slate involves crushing potassium feldspar, performing magnetic separation-reverse flotation to obtain concentrate, mixing with sodium hydroxide, leaching, adding leaching residue to sulfuric acid, filtering, and drying.
AU BAI J
   LV X
   PENG Y
   HOU W
   JIANG F
   LIU F
   LI M
AE INNER MONGOLIA BOYAN ZHICHENG METAL MINE (INNE-Non-standard)
   BAOTOU STEEL GROUP MINING RES INST CO (BTSU-C)
GA 202256291B
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of potassium carbonate and aluminum oxide from potassium-rich slate involves: (1) crushing potassium feldspar, mixing and grinding to obtain a test sample; (2) performing magnetic separation-reverse flotation on the sample to obtain concentrate; (3) mixing the concentrate and sodium hydroxide, calcining, leaching and filtering; (4) adjusting the pH of filtrate to 8-10, and removing aluminum and silicon in the filtrate; (5) adding the leaching residue obtained in step (3) to sulfuric acid, and filtering; (6) preparing W-type molecular sieve from acidified filter residue obtained in step (5); (7) adjusting the pH of leaching liquid obtained in step (5) to 14, filtering, removing metal cations, filtering, and drying to obtain aluminum oxide; (8) evaporating and crystallizing the filtrate to produce sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate; and (9) introducing carbon dioxide filter residues obtained in step (4) to prepare W-type molecular sieve.
   USE - Extraction of potassium carbonate and aluminum oxide from potassium-rich slate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the problems of high utilization cost of potassium-rich slate, low output value of by-products, and various defects in development and utilization technology; uses beneficiation method to reduce content of sulfur in the feedstock and reduce pollution during calcining; improves the content of potassium oxide; reduces the cost; uses additive, which is wide in source and low in cost, and high efficiency non-water-soluble potassium resource; does not generate waste; and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of potassium carbonate and aluminum oxide from potassium-rich slate involves: (1) crushing potassium feldspar to -3 mm, then uniformly mixing and dividing the crushed product several times, and grinding 81% crushed product to -200 mesh to prepare a test sample; (2) performing magnetic separation-reverse flotation on the sample to obtain potassium feldspar concentrate; (3) uniformly mixing the concentrate and sodium hydroxide as an additive in a ratio of 1:(0.5-1.5), calcining at 350-500degrees Celsius for 20-90 minutes, leaching at 60-95degrees Celsius for 1-4.5 hours, and filtering until potassium leaching rate reaches 90% or more; (4) adjusting the pH of filtrate containing small amount of silicon and aluminum to 8-10 with carbon dioxide, and removing 99.9% or more aluminum and silicon in the filtrate, where the ratio of potassium oxide/sodium oxide is (1:7)-(1:10); (5) adding the leaching residue obtained in step (3) to 10-25% hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid in a liquid-solid ratio of 1:(2-10), heat-preserving at 70-150degrees Celsius for 40-240 minutes, and filtering; (6) preparing W-type molecular sieve from the acidified filter residue obtained in step (5); (7) adjusting the pH of leaching liquid obtained in step (5) to 14 with sodium hydroxide, filtering, removing metal cations, adding 10-25% hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid solution to the filtrate to adjust the pH to 8.35-11, filtering and drying to obtain 98.5% aluminum oxide; (8) evaporating and crystallizing the filtrate to produce more than 98.5% sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate by ion exchange method after carbon dioxide is introduced in step (4); and (9) introducing carbon dioxide filter residues obtained in step (4) to prepare W-type molecular sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (2), the grade of potassium oxide in the potassium feldspar concentrate is greater than 12.02%, the yield is greater than 86.86%, and the grade of sulfur is less than 0.2%.

   ZHANG Q
   LI X
   YANG Y
   LU C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202221544J
AB    NOVELTY - Use of carbon material-coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst in the synthesis of                m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid by sodium                m-nitrobenzenesulfonate hydrogenation. The                preparation of carbon material-coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst comprises (1) taking nickel                salts and organic ligands, pouring them into an                alcohol solvent, dissolving them in an alcohol                solvent, stirring for 3-24 hours to obtain a mixed                solution, (2) adding lye dropwise in batches, and                adjusting the pH, (c) adjusting the pH to 8.5-9.5,                keeping for 1-3 hours, (3) sealing the slurry and                placing on a vibration platform for                crystallization, precipitation, aging, and filter                washing, vacuum filtering, (4) placing the                precursor in the agate for agate mortar, crushing                and grinding in a mortar, calcining, grinding to                obtain a carbon material-coated nickel nanoparticle                catalyst, where the calcining atmosphere is an                inert atmosphere or an inert atmosphere containing                carbon dioxide or a carbon dioxide                atmosphere.
   USE - The carbon material-coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst is useful in the synthesis                of m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid by sodium                m-nitrobenzenesulfonate hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst : has high conversion rate,                high selectivity, good stability and long service                life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of carbon material-coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst in the synthesis of                m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid by sodium                m-nitrobenzenesulfonate hydrogenation. The                preparation of carbon material-coated nickel                nano-particle catalyst comprises (1) taking nickel                salts and organic ligands, pouring them into an                alcohol solvent, and dissolving them in an alcohol                solvent, stirring for 3-24 hours to obtain a mixed                solution, (2) adding lye dropwise in batches, and                adjusting the pH value of the solution in three                stages, (a) using 0.1-5 mol/l sodium hydroxide, the                pH of the aqueous solution is 4.0-5.5, and keeping                for 0.5-3 hours, (b) adjusting the pH to 6.5-7.5                with 80-99% triethanolamine aqueous solution, (c)                adjusting the pH to 8.5-9.5 by 25-28% ammonia                water, keeping for 1-3 hours, (3) sealing the                slurry and placing on a vibration platform for                crystallization, precipitation, aging, and filter                washing, vacuum filtering, where the metal organic                framework precursor is obtained by drying, and the                vibration program is set as: (a) vibration                frequency 10-20Hz, banner vibration is 1-5 minutes,                (b) vibration frequency is 20-40 Hz, banner                vibration is 1-5 minutes, (c) vibration frequency                is 40-55 Hz, banner vibration for 1-5 minutes, (d)                performing entire crystallization, precipitation,                and aging process for 10-20 hours, and a vibration                cycle is 1-2 hour intervals, (4) placing the                precursor in the agate for agate mortar, crushing                and grinding in a mortar, calcining at                300-800degreesC for 5-24 hours, grinding to obtain                a carbon material-coated nickel nanoparticle                catalyst, where the calcining atmosphere is an                inert atmosphere or an inert atmosphere containing                carbon dioxide or a carbon dioxide atmosphere                .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process, (a) adding                  water to sodium m-nitrobenzene sulfonate, heating                  and dissolving, adding activated carbon to boil,                  performing hot filtration, and adjusting the pH                  value of the obtained filtrate to 7.5-8.5 with                  sodium hydroxide to obtain a treatment passed                  sodium m-nitrobenzenesulfonate solution, (b)                  adding the treated sodium m-nitrobenzene                  sulfonate solution and the carbon material                  coating nickel nanoparticle catalyst in the                  autoclave, sealing the reactor, replacing the air                  with nitrogen, replacing the nitrogen with                  hydrogen, heating the reactor , start stirring,                  reacting at 50-80 degrees Celsius, hydrogen                  pressure of 0.5-1.5 MPa until hydrogen                  consumption is no longer, stop stirring, after                  the temperature drops to room temperature,                  filtering, adjusting the pH value of the obtained                  filtrate with sulfuric acid to 1.5-2.5 , the                  precipitation of white precipitate is                  m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid. In the step (1), the                  stirring temperature is 0 -50degrees Celsius,                  preferably 20-50degrees Celsius, the stirring                  time is 3-24 hours, preferably 6-15 hours. In the                  step (3), precipitating and aging, filtering and                  washing by ethanol, placing into a vacuum oven at                  50-120degrees Celsius and drying for 2-15 hours                  and taking out. In the step (4), the roasting                  temperature is 300-800degrees Celsius, preferably                  400 -600degrees Celsius, and the calcining time                  is 5-24 hours, preferably 5-10 hours. In the step                  (4), the inert atmosphere is nitrogen or argon                  gas, the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the                  roasting atmosphere is not less than 10 %, the                  most preferably roasting atmosphere is carbon                  dioxide atmosphere, the total flow rate of the                  gas is 5- 50 ml/minute.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  carbon material coated nickel nanoparticle                  catalyst and m-nitro-benzene sulfonate is 1:                  10-50. The alcohol solvent                  methanol or absolute ethanol has a volume                  concentration of more than 95%. In the step (1), the molar                  ratio of nickel in the nickel salt to the organic                  ligand is 1:1 -1:8, preferably 1:2-1:6, the mass                  of nickel in the nickel salt to the alcohol                  solvent is 1:50-1:1000, preferably                  1:100-1:500.

PN CN113135582-A
TI Producing combined composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride by sodium chloride comprises evaporating and concentrating high-salt waste water, performing ammoniation treatment, calcining, chemically sublimating, and recycling.
AU ZHU X
   WU H
   LU X
   GU H
   SHI Q
   LIU M
   WANG W
   ZHANG Y
   PEI M
   CHEN X
   LIU J
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2021863385
AB    NOVELTY - Producing combined composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride by sodium chloride comprises (i) evaporating and concentrating high-salt waste water, ultrasonically dissolving to obtain supersaturated sodium chloride aqueous solution, performing ammoniation treatment, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the carbonization tower, performing carbonization treatment to obtain mixed solution containing bicarbonate, (ii) crystallizing, filtering, washing to obtain sodium bicarbonate crude product, calcining, (iii) filtering the mother liquid to obtain aqueous solution containing ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, performing low temperature crystallization, chemical sublimation, refining, collecting ammonium chloride crystal and the ammonia gas released after the chemical sublimation, recycling, dissolving the residual liquid after low temperature crystallization and recrystallization with sodium chloride, aqueous solution of ammonium chloride, recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for producing combined composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride by sodium chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves single-pass conversion rate of sodium chloride to more than 80%; ensures the total conversion rate of the sodium chloride more than 95%; ensures the total conversion rate of ammonia more than 98 %; ensures the yield of sodium carbonate more than 90 %; ensures the yield of ammonium chloride more than 90%; ensures the circulating material reduced more than 80%; reduces the energy consumption; improves the economic benefit; is environmentally-friendly; produces the product sodium carbonate, which has good quality of the ammonium chloride; and has high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing combined composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride by sodium chloride comprises (i) evaporating and concentrating high-salt waste water, ultrasonically dissolving by high-purity water to obtain supersaturated sodium chloride aqueous solution, then conveying the saturated state of sodium chloride aqueous solution to an ammonifying tower and continuously introducing ammonia in the ammonifying tower for heterogeneous absorption, performing ammoniation treatment, converting the absorption liquid into ammonium high salt waste water, ammonium salt high salt waste water and then conveying to the carbonization tower, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the carbonization tower, performing carbonization treatment to obtain mixed solution containing bicarbonate, (ii) taking mixed solution containing bicarbonate, crystallizing, filtering, washing to obtain sodium bicarbonate crude product, calcining at high temperature to obtain sodium carbonate product, collecting the carbon dioxide gas after calcining the sodium carbonate and recycling to the carbonization tower, (iii) filtering the mother liquid to obtain aqueous solution containing ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, performing low temperature crystallization, chemical sublimation, separating out ammonium chloride crude product, refining into ammonium chloride product by recrystallization, reducing the sodium chloride impurity content to less than 2%, collecting ammonium chloride crystal and the ammonia gas released after the chemical sublimation, recycling to the ammonifying tower, dissolving the residual liquid after low temperature crystallization and recrystallization with sodium chloride, aqueous solution of ammonium chloride, recycling to the carbonization tower, where the ammonification treatment conditions comprising controlling the mass concentration of sodium chloride aqueous solution at 30-50%, mol ratio of ammonia and sodium chloride is 1.0-2.0:1, ammoniation reaction temperature at 30-50 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours at a pressure of 0.1- 0.5 MPa, where the carbonization treatment conditions comprising mol ratio of the carbon dioxide gas and the sodium chloride at 1.0-1.5:1, carbonization reaction temperature of 20-45 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours and a pressure of 0.1-0.5 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises controlling crystallization temperature at 55-85 degrees C, calcining sodium carbonate at 200-280 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours. The step (ii) comprises controlling the crystallization temperature of 65-70 degrees C. The step (iii) comprises controlling the temperature of crystallization at 10-30 degrees C. The step (iii) comprises step performing the low-temperature crystallization at 15-20 degrees C.

PN CN112250324-A; CN112250324-B
TI Two-step preparation of sintered magnesia from            powdered granular magnesite involves preheating            powdered magnesite, performing light burning, heavy            burning, heat recovery, separation and purification and            flue gas purification treatment.
AU FU L
   BAI H
   BAI D
   XU G
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 202111189N
AB    NOVELTY - Two-step preparation of sintered magnesia from                powdered granular magnesite involves preheating                powdered magnesite, performing light burning, heavy                burning, heat recovery, separation and purification                and flue gas purification treatment to produce                sintered magnesia products. The raw material is                obtained by crushing natural magnesite powder or                massive magnesite. The preheating involves                preheating the magnesite raw materials using                high-temperature flue gas after light burning and                reburning of magnesite. The light burning uses a                fluidized bed/moving bed to lightly burn the                preheated powdered magnesite to decompose magnesium                carbonate and to discharge carbon dioxide to obtain                high-purity, porous active magnesium oxide, which                can be used as a separate product or as the raw                materials for the process. The reburning uses a                fluidized bed/moving bed to reburn the lightly                burned magnesium oxide to transform its crystal                form to prepare high-purity sintered                magnesia.
   USE - Two-step preparation of sintered magnesia e.g.                active magnesium oxide and sintered magnesium                sand.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has faster heat transfer and carbon                dioxide emission from small particle size                magnesite, solves the difficulty of heat transfer                due to the large particles of magnesite used in                reverberatory furnace light burning and shaft kiln                reburning, improved the utilization rate of                magnesite resources due to low activity of light                burned magnesia and low density of magnesia after                reburning, solves the problems of long calcination                time and high recalcination temperature, reduces                the sintering temperature and shortens the                sintering time to improve the processing capacity                of the system and reduces the production cost of                sintered magnesia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Two-step preparation of sintered magnesia from                powdered granular magnesite involves preheating                powdered magnesite, performing light burning, heavy                burning, heat recovery, separation and purification                and flue gas purification treatment to produce                sintered magnesia products. The raw material is                obtained by crushing natural magnesite powder or                massive magnesite. The preheating involves                preheating the magnesite raw materials using                high-temperature flue gas after light burning and                reburning of magnesite. The light burning uses a                fluidized bed/moving bed to lightly burn the                preheated powdered magnesite to decompose magnesium                carbonate and to discharge carbon dioxide to obtain                high-purity, porous active magnesium oxide, which                can be used as a separate product or as the raw                materials for the process. The reburning uses a                fluidized bed/moving bed to reburn the lightly                burned magnesium oxide to transform its crystal                form to prepare high-purity sintered magnesia. The                heat recovery uses air to cool the sintered                magnesia in a high-temperature state, and the                heated air is used as the fluidizing gas and                combustion-supporting gas of the fluidized                bed/moving bed. The separation and purification                unit that when coal powder is used as fuel in the                calcination device, the coal ash mixed in the                sintered magnesia is removed by particle size                classification or gravity separation depending on                the difference in particle size and density between                the sintered magnesia and magnesia. The flue gas                purification is an environmental protection method                for desulfurization, denitrification and dust                removal of the flue gas generated by the combustion                of the fuel in the reburning process, so that the                gas discharged into the atmosphere meets the                environmental protection requirements. The carbon                dioxide is produced during calcination of magnesite                is collected as a byproduct of the system based on                concentration and market demand.

TI Constructing epigenetic drug apoptosis induction model involves selecting epigenetics-related tumor cells, culturing, synchronizing cell growth, culturing with epigenetic drugs, collecting cells, detecting and measuring cell apoptosis.
AU YANG Z
   ZHANG Q
   YI Z
   SHI Q
   SAI B
   NI Y
AE KUNMING MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED HOSPITA (UKMM-C)
GA 202109157G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing an epigenetic drug apoptosis induction model involves (a) selecting epigenetics-related tumor cells and using cell culture medium for in vitro culture, (b) after the tumor cells grow to the logarithmic growth phase, transferring the tumor cells to a 4 degrees C environment to synchronize cell growth, (c) dividing the cells that have completed synchronized growth in step (b) into not less than 5 groups, adding epigenetic drugs and placing in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator for culture, (d) replacing the cells with the cell culture medium without epigenetics drugs and continuously culturing, (e) collecting the cells, extracting DNA, recording the results and measuring the process of cell apoptosis, (f) collecting the cells, observing the cell morphology and measuring the process of cell apoptosis, and (g) collecting the cells, detecting the cell growth cycle and measuring the process of apoptosis by proportion of cells in the G1 phase, S phase cells, and G2 phase cells detected.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing epigenetic drug apoptosis induction model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method constructs the epigenetic drug apoptosis induction model with simple operation and accurate results and is beneficial to guide the application of epigenetic drugs in tumor treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing an epigenetic drug apoptosis induction model involves (a) selecting epigenetics-related tumor cells and using cell culture medium for in vitro culture, where the tumor cells are myelodysplastic syndrome tumor cells, skin T cell lymphoma cells, non-small cell lung cancer cells, breast cancer cells and prostate cancer cells, the cell culture medium comprises Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI)-1640 culture fluid containing 10% fetal bovine serum and the in vitro culture includes culture in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator, (b) after the tumor cells grow to the logarithmic growth phase, transferring the tumor cells to a 4 degrees C environment to synchronize cell growth, (c) dividing the cells that have completed synchronized growth in step (b) into not less than 5 groups, adding gradient concentrations of epigenetic drugs and placing in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator for culture, where the epigenetic drugs include DNA methyltransferase inhibitors, histone deacetylase inhibitors and combination agents and the combination agent is prepared by mixing a DNA methyltransferase inhibitor and a histone deacetylase inhibitor, (d) replacing the cells cultured in step (c) with the cell culture medium without epigenetics drugs and continuously culturing in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator, where the epigenetic drug-free cell culture medium includes RPMI-1640 culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, (e) collecting the cells obtained in step (d), extracting DNA for agarose gel electrophoresis, recording the results of DNA agarose gel electrophoresis and measuring the process of cell apoptosis through the results of DNA agarose gel electrophoresis, (f) collecting the cells obtained in step (d), observing the cell morphology by transmission electron microscope and measuring the process of cell apoptosis through the cell morphology results, and (g) collecting the cells obtained in step (d), using flow cytometry to detect the cell growth cycle and measuring the process of apoptosis by the proportion of cells in the G1 phase, S phase cells, and G2 phase cells detected.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The culture time in the step (c) is 1-2 hours. The culture time in the step (d) is 6-10 hours. The method involves after the cells in the step (f) are fixed with glutaraldehyde solution, dehydrating with acetone, performing electron staining and then observing by transmission electron microscope.

TI Planting auspicious grass involves selecting land with quiet environment, no industrial pollution, sandy soil, and fresh air as planting land, plowing planting land, applying decomposed farm manure and compound fertilizer per acre.
AU YU Y
   YU X
AE ZIYUN HUINONG PLANTING BREEDING FARMERS (ZIYU-Non-standard)
GA 202101571A
AB    NOVELTY - Planting auspicious grass involves selecting land with a quiet environment, no industrial pollution, sandy soil, and fresh air as planting land, plowing planting land, applying 3000-3200 kg decomposed farm manure and 200-300 kg compound fertilizer per acre, 60-80 kg superphosphate, 40-60 kg urea, and then raking and leveling the planting land after fertilization. A large shed is built on the planting ground, adjusted a temperature control system, spray system, lighting system, carbon dioxide supply system, and humidity control system in the greenhouse. The borders are formed on the raked and leveled planting land in the greenhouse, where the border height is 5-15 cm, width is 10-15 cm, and distance between the borders is 10-20 cm. The auspicious grass seedling plants are selected with dark green leaves, vigorous growth, and disease-free pests for transplanting.
   USE - Method for planting auspicious grass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides environmental conditions suitable for fast growth and high yield of the auspicious grass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Planting auspicious grass involves selecting land with a quiet environment, no industrial pollution, sandy soil, and fresh air as planting land, plowing planting land, applying 3000-3200 kg decomposed farm manure and 200-300 kg compound fertilizer per acre, 60-80 kg superphosphate, 40-60 kg urea, and then raking and leveling the planting land after fertilization. A large shed is built on the planting ground, adjusted a temperature control system, spray system, lighting system, carbon dioxide supply system, and humidity control system in the greenhouse. The borders are formed on the raked and leveled planting land in the greenhouse, where the border height is 5-15 cm, width is 10-15 cm, and distance between the borders is 10-20 cm. The auspicious grass seedling plants are selected with dark green leaves, vigorous growth, and disease-free pests for transplanting. The roots of the auspicious grass seedling plants are left with their own soil, where the auspicious grass seedling plants are planted on the border of the planting ground in the greenhouse. The roots of the auspicious grass seedlings are covered with soil tightly, and watered until the roots are soaked at 21-23 degrees C for 5-7 days. The light is provided for the auspicious grass seedlings in the greenhouse through the illumination system from 8:00-9:00 every night with an illumination intensity of 2-3000 Lux. The greenhouse temperature is adjusted at 23-26 degrees C after surviving the auspicious grass seedlings, where potassium dihydrogen phosphate and water is sprayed through a spraying system. The quicklime is sprinkled among the vacant land of auspicious grass plants. The auspicious grass is topdressed every six months with 2000-3000 kg decomposed farmyard manure, 80-100 kg compound fertilizer, and 40-50 kg urea per mu, where carbon dioxide is added to the greenhouse through the carbon dioxide supply system. The spray system foliar fertilizer is sprayed to ensure vigorous growth of auspicious grass. The alcohol is sprayed through the spray system every one month for disinfection. The charcoal/wheat is sprayed to kill insects through the spray system every 12-14 days. The auspicious grass is picked when matured.

TI Production of syngas involves charging oxidant and feedstock composition comprising densified textile aggregates to gasification zone within gasifier, gasifying composition with oxidant, and discharging portion(s) of syngas from gasifier.
AU TRAPP W L
   MURPHY J W
   WEST N M
AE EASTMAN CHEM CO (EACH-C)
   EASTMAN CHEM CO (EACH-C)
   EASTMAN CHEM CO (EACH-C)
GA 2020980872
AB    NOVELTY - Production of syngas involves charging (i) an oxidant and a feedstock composition (70) comprising densified textile aggregates to a gasification zone (54) within a gasifier, gasifying (ii) the feedstock composition with the oxidant in the gasification zone to produce a syngas composition, and discharging (iii) portion(s) of the syngas composition from the gasifier.
   USE - Production of syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables stable and energy-efficient production of syngas having desired quality and consistent rate, using feedstock composition having excellent stability and pumpability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) syngas composition, which comprises a syngas stream optionally comprising less than 4 wt.% tar based on the weight of all condensable solids in the syngas composition;
   (2) production of syngas stream, which involves gasifying the feedstock composition in the gasifier. The syngas stream has a compositional variability of 5% or less measured over a time period that is the lesser of 12 days or the time period the feedstock fed to the gasifier. The syngas compositional variability is satisfied against at least one of the gaseous compounds (in mol) comprising carbon monoxide, or hydrogen, or carbon dioxide, or methane, or hydrogen sulfide, or carbonyl sulfide, or carbon monoxide and hydrogen, or carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, or carbon monoxide, hydrogen and methane, or carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and methane, or hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide, or carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide;
   (3) production of syngas composition or syngas composition stream, which involves gasifying the feedstock composition in the gasifier. The syngas composition has a switching variability that is negative, zero, or 15% or less, where the switching frequency is at least 1 time every two years. The switching variability is the ratio of difference between syngas compositional variability (A1) of gaseous compound(s) measured using the feedstock composition comprising densified textile aggregates and fossil fuel source and syngas compositional variability (A2) of gaseous compound(s) measured using the feedstock composition comprising same fossil fuel source and different densified textile aggregates with respect to syngas compositional variability (A2);
   (4) feedstock composition, which comprises a slurry comprising the densified textile aggregates, a solid fossil fuel and/or a hydrocarbon liquid that is liquid at 25 degrees C and 1 atm. The densified textile aggregates have a particle size of 2 mm or less. The solid fossil fuel in the feedstock composition has a particle size of less than 2 mm. The solid content in the slurry is 62 wt.% or more. The slurry is stable as determined by having an initial viscosity of 100000 cP or less, or pumpable as determined by having a viscosity of less than 30000 cP; and
   (5) use of densified textile aggregates for producing syngas by gasifying the densified textile aggregates in a gasifier. A portion of or all portion of the densified textile aggregates are obtained from textiles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a plant for gasifying the feedstock composition.
   Injector (51)
   Gasification zone (54)
   Feedstock Composition (70)
   Quench Chamber (71)
   Refractory valve (75)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The total amount of hydrogen and carbon monoxide relative to the total amount of syngas discharged from the gasifier is more than 70 mol%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Composition: The feedstock composition comprises 90 wt.% or more densified textile aggregates. The densified textile aggregate is obtained from a source of textiles separated from a municipal solid waste stream, or a source of textiles comprising 5 wt.% or more thermoplastic polymer. The ratio (wt.%) of total hydrogen to total carbon in the densified textile aggregate is 0.075 or more. The amount of densified textile aggregate fed to the gasifier is 10 wt.% or less based on the weight of all fuel fed to the gasifier.

PN CN111484985-A
TI Culturing high-yield varicella virus comprises e.g. resuscitating and sub culturing cells from ATCC working cell bank, culturing, cells grow into monolayer, and washing cell surface twice with phosphate buffered solution.
AU YANG J
   YU J
   LI F
   TANG Y
   WANG S
   YANG W
   ZHAO Y
   ZHU S
AE JIANGSU GDK BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202075810F
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing high-yield varicella virus comprises e.g. (i) resuscitating and sub culturing the MRC-5 cells from working cell bank of ATCC, where the cell growth medium is M199-801 culture medium containing 10-20% neonatal bovine serum, culturing at 37 degrees C for 3-4 days and 5% carbon dioxide, the cells grow into monolayer and is ready to pass on to the next generation, and (ii) washing the cell surface twice with 0.01 mol/L phosphate buffered solution, adding pre-heated ethylene diamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA)-Trypsin digestion solution, and when there is gap on the cell surface observed by naked eyes, discarding the digestive juice, adding small amount of cell culture solution, blowing to make the cells disperse evenly, and sub-culturing in ratio of 1:4-1:8.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing high-yield varicella virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method relates to the technical field of biological medicine, increases production output by 100%, and reduces production cost by 40-50%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing high-yield varicella virus comprises (i) resuscitating and sub culturing the MRC-5 cells from working cell bank of ATCC, where the cell growth medium is M199-801 culture medium containing 10-20% neonatal bovine serum, culturing at 37 degrees C for 3-4 days and 5% carbon dioxide, the cells grow into monolayer and is ready to pass on to the next generation, (ii) washing the cell surface twice with 0.01 mol/L phosphate buffered solution, adding pre-heated ethylene diamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA)-Trypsin digestion solution, and when there is gap on the cell surface observed by naked eyes, discarding the digestive juice, adding small amount of cell culture solution, blowing to make the cells disperse evenly, and sub-culturing in ratio of 1:4-1:8, sub culturing the cells into Corning HYPERStack (RTM: Closed system for high yield cell growth introducing the next generation in Corning's high yield Performance (HYPER) platform) culture room, supplementing with 0.2-0.3 ml/cm2 of 5-10% newborn bovine serum M199-801 culture medium, and incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, (iii) When the cell monolayer fusion degree is 80-90%, removing the varicella working seed batch for infection, and the multiplicity of infection (MOI) is 0.001-0.01, (iv) adding the cells into 2-5% neonatal bovine serum at pH of 7.2-7.4, M199-801 culture medium as maintenance medium, where the maintenance liquid medium volume is 0.2-0.3 ml/cm2, and culturing the cells at 35 degrees C, and 5% carbon dioxide, and (v) harvesting cells when CPE reaches greater than or equal to 50%, washing the cell surface twice with 0.01 mol/L phosphate buffered saline solution, adding 0.05% EDTA digestion solution at 0.1 ml/cm2, when there is gap in the cell surface observed by naked eyes, use a pipette to suck the cell liquid into the centrifuge bottle, sucking the cell liquid into the centrifuge bottle, adding freeze-dried protective agent into the 0.01 ml/cm2 culture area, using straw for gently blowing, and adding into the freezing bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the volume fraction of carbon dioxide is 5 %, and the environment temperature is 37 degrees C. In the step (ii), passing the cells in ratio of 1:4-1:8, and passing the cells into the Corning HYPERStack (RTM: Closed system for high yield cell growth introducing the next generation in Corning's high yield Performance (HYPER) platform) culture room. In the step (iv), the maintenance liquid is 2-5% newborn bovine serum having pH of 7.2- 7.4 M199-801 culture solution with maintenance amount of 0.2-0.3 ml/cm2, and incubating at 35 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide. In the step (v), the rotation speed in the centrifugation by centrifuge is 1000 revolutions/minute, and carrying out the centrifugation for 5 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - An inverter type carbon dioxide gas shielded welding machine comprises a main unit, a shock absorber, four universal wheels, a connecting rod, a telescopic device, a pulley, and a sliding device. The top end of the support column is fixedly connected to the lower surface of the main unit. The connecting rod is fixedly connected to the left and right sides of the support column. The bottom end of the connecting rod is provided with a groove. The telescopic device is fixedly connected to the upper surface of the inner wall of the groove. The pulley is fixedly connected to the bottom end of the telescopic device. The bottom end of the pulley is slidingly connected with a chute. The two chutes are opened on the left and right sides of an inner wall of a housing.
   USE - The inverter type carbon dioxide gas shielded welding machine is useful for electrical components.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine guarantees the service life of the electrical components. The vibration amplitude of the main unit of the machine is avoided to exceed the use range of the electrical components.

The bottom end of the connecting rod is provided with a groove. The telescopic device is fixedly connected to the upper surface of the inner wall of the groove. The pulley is fixedly connected to the bottom end of the telescopic device. The bottom end of the pulley is slidingly connected with a chute. The two chutes are opened on the left and right sides of an inner wall of a housing. The housing is clamped on the upper surface of a bottom plate. The lower surface of the bottom plate is fixedly connected to the top of the four universal wheels. The upper surface of the bottom plate is fixedly connected with four support boxes. The sliding device is clamped on the upper surface of the support box. Both ends of the sliding device are fixedly connected to the lower surface of the main unit and an upper surface of a movable plate. The upper surface of the movable plate is fixedly connected to the upper surface of the inner wall of the support box through two second springs.

TI Preparing bee pollen crystals comprises e.g. taking Camellia powder, medlar pollen, motherwort pollen, rose pollen and milk powder, removing impurities , sterilizing, drying and controlling carbon dioxide in the extraction device.
AU ZHAO H
   ZHANG B
   JIANG Y
   PENG Z
   DU Y
AE UNIV SHANXI AGRIC (USXA-C)
GA 201955068N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bee pollen crystals comprises (a) taking 28-45 pts. wt.Camellia powder, 5-15 pts. wt. medlar pollen, 8-14 pts. wt. motherwort pollen, 2-3 pts. wt. rose pollen, 10-18 pts. wt. milk powder, 8-15 pts. wt. pumpkin powder, 5-12 pts. wt. cocoa powder, 14-25 pts. wt. hydrophilic colloid, 40-60 pts. wt. liquid component and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. additive; (b)removing impurities in theCamellia powder, medlar pollen, motherwort pollen and rose pollen, sterilizing; (c) collecting broken wall pollen and separately extracting with an ethanol solution to obtain an ethanol extract, and then enriching ethanol extract with a non-polar macroporous resin column; (d) crushing milk powder, the pumpkin powder and the cocoa powder into ultrafine powders; and (e) mixing the mixed extract A and the mixture B, adding the above hydrophilic colloid, liquid component, additive and stirring uniformly, using a granulator to prepare particles to 20-25 mesh and freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bee pollen crystals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves nutritional value, and the nutrient component; and promotes convenient carrying and drinking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing bee pollen crystals comprises (a) taking 28-45 pts. wt. Camellia powder, 5-15 pts. wt. medlar pollen, 8-14 pts. wt. motherwort pollen, 2-3 pts. wt. rose pollen, 10-18 pts. wt. milk powder, 8-15 pts. wt. pumpkin powder, 5-12 pts. wt. cocoa powder, 14-25 pts. wt. hydrophilic colloid, 40-60 pts. wt. liquid component and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. additive, where the hydrophilic colloid is locust bean gum, carrageenan, gum arabic, guar gum, pectin and agar and the liquid component is determined by water, milk and syrup and syrup is high fructose corn syrup, beet syrup, isomerized corn syrup or type 50 syrup; (b)removing impurities in theCamellia powder, medlar pollen, motherwort pollen and rose pollen, sterilizing, drying to a moisture content of less than 3%, and then addingthe dried pollen into supercritical carbon dioxide. in the extraction device to wall breaking treatment, controlling carbon dioxide in the extraction device at 25-36 degrees C, the pressure under 40-75 MPa for 15-30 minutes, and then evacuatingthe carbon dioxide in the extraction device to collect the broken wall pollen; (c) collecting broken wall pollen and separately extracting with an ethanol solution to obtain an ethanol extract, and then enriching ethanol extract with a non-polar macroporous resin column, and eluting with ethanol, collecting ethanol elution site, concentrating, drying, and mixing to obtain a mixed extract A which is reserved; (d) crushing milk powder, the pumpkin powder and the cocoa powder into ultrafine powders having a particle size of less than 10 mu m by a supersonic jet mill, and uniformly mixing by a vertical multi-screw continuous mixing device to obtain a mixture B; and (e) mixing the mixed extract A and the mixture B, adding the above hydrophilic colloid, liquid component, additive and stirring uniformly, using a granulator to prepare particles to 20-25 mesh at -10 to-50 degrees C and freeze-drying for 120-150 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The additive is cannabidiol (CBD). Preferred Method: In the step (b) the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device is added with 0.03-0.06 pts. wt. cell wall enzyme, where the cell wall enzyme is glycosidase, protease and peptidase. In the step (b) when the broken wall pollen is extracted by the ethanol solution, the broken wall pollen is added 9-20 times amount of the volume fraction is 45-60% in an aqueous solution of ethanol, immersing at 18-42 degrees C for 3-8 days, and filtering to obtain an ethanol extract. In the step (c) enriching the active ingredients in the broken wall pollen by the non-polar macroporous resin column comprises eluting impurities with water after the ethanol extract is applied to the non-polar macroporous resin column and then using the volume fraction, eluting non-polar macroporous resin column with 30-85% aqueous ethanol solution, concentrating, drying, and mixing to obtain a mixed extract A. In the step (e) granulation process comprises adding 0.15-0.2 pts. wt., preferably 0.25-0.40 pts. wt. binding agent to the granulator and mixing with anhydrous ethanol and purified water in a ratio of 1:3, preferably 1:3.

PN BR102017025335-A2
TI Separation and recovery process of water and cellulose pulp generated in cellulose industry, comprises e.g. transferring wastewater to tanks, transferring carbon dioxide to side pipes, agitating water tank and flocculating carbon dioxide.
AU DA SILVA M N
AE DA SILVA M N (DSIL-Individual)
GA 2019585583
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and recovery process of water and cellulose pulp generated in the cellulose industry, comprises: e.g. transfer of wastewater from the dewaterers, cleaners and channels, which follow to the primary tanks for a lung tank; transfer of carbon dioxide (CO2) from the CO2 emission chimneys of the foramina or from the recovery boilers of the factory, to the side pipes of the lung tank, cylindrical, 100 m carbonator type; water is agitated in the tank, with agitation of 5 m/s of peripheral speed on the mixing rotor shaft, while simultaneously injecting CO2 through the pipes, where CO2 is injected with the aid of a low pressure, high flow blower, it captures the smoke from the chimneys and blows inside the carbonator lung tank; flocculation of CO2 upon contact with the wastewater lowers the pH of the same to a pH of 5.5-6.5 at the same time as the cellulose fiber particles will flocculate, when lowering the pH of the in water, the fibers come together, increasing in flake size.
   USE - The process is useful for separation and recovery process of water and cellulose pulp.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes the system that has very high efficiency and excellence; does not use polymers in their process, whose use would be detrimental to the reuse of fibers; provides high added value in recovered pulp; is cost-effective and lower consumption of clean water in the system with the treatment of recovered water and 100% sustainability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and recovery process of water and cellulose pulp generated in the cellulose industry, comprises: transfer of wastewater from the dewaterers, cleaners and channels, which follow to the primary tanks for a lung tank; transfer of carbon dioxide (CO2) from the CO2 emission chimneys of the foramina or from the recovery boilers of the factory, to the side pipes of the lung tank, cylindrical, 100 m carbonator type; water is agitated in the tank, with agitation of 5 m/s of peripheral speed on the mixing rotor shaft, while simultaneously injecting CO2 through the pipes, where CO2 is injected with the aid of a low pressure, high flow blower, it captures the smoke from the chimneys and blows inside the carbonator lung tank; flocculation of CO2 upon contact with the wastewater lowers the pH of the same to a pH of 5.5-6.5 at the same time as the cellulose fiber particles will flocculate, when lowering the pH of the in water, the fibers come together, increasing in flake size; transfer of flocculated water by flocculated cellulose still in the carbonator water is transferred to a rotary centrifuge with internal filtration system with rhyme mesh of 0.2-0.4 mm, where the transfer is made by a pump 50 m3/h flow centrifuge upon entering the centrifuge system the flocculated cellulose is retained in the centrifuge rhyme screen and exits from the centrifuge solids side, while clean water exits through the centrifuge disposal, in the centrifuge the cellulose fibers are completely retained and the water in turn separated from the fibers, the water that comes out of the centrifuge disposal is a clean water that can be used as reuse water in industrial process and the retained cellulose is directed to the front of the centrifuge by discharging solids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The tank is a 100 m carbonate cylindrical lung. The CO2 injection pipes have a flow rate of 5000 nm/ min. The working speed of the blower is equivalent to the height of the tank column to break it, where trilobal blower. The rotary centrifuge is of the CBX type. The working speed of the centrifuge is 1200 rpm at its central axis and the system is continuous, where the flow the working temperature is the same as that of the feed pump, i.e. 50 m / per hour of water entering with flocculated pulp.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) in exhaust gas of vehicle, comprises delivering exhaust gas to cooled section of rotary contactor, adsorbing CO2 of exhaust gas with sorbent of cooled section of contactor, heating contactor and cooling.
AU HAMAD E Z
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201936894R
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing (M1) carbon dioxide (CO2) in exhaust gas of a vehicle, comprising: (a) delivering the exhaust gas of the vehicle to a cooled section of a rotary contactor (24), the rotary contactor including openings that extend from a first side of the rotary contactor to a second side of the rotary contactor opposite the first side; (b) adsorbing the CO2 of the exhaust gas with a sorbent of the cooled section of the rotary contactor, where non-CO2 components of the exhaust gas pass through the openings; (c) heating the cooled section of the rotary contactor with a hot fluid of the vehicle to release the adsorbed CO2 and converting the cooled section of the rotary contactor to a heated section of the rotary contactor; and (d) cooling the heated section of the rotary contactor to convert the heated section of the rotary contactor back to the cooled section of the rotary contactor.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of a vehicle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) capturing (M2) CO2 in exhaust gas of a vehicle, comprising the step (a) as above per se, adsorbing the CO2 of the exhaust gas with a sorbent of the cooled section of the rotary contactor, rotating the rotary contactor so that the cooled section of the rotary contactor is in a path of a hot fluid of the vehicle and heating the cooled section of the rotary contactor to release the adsorbed CO2, converting the cooled section of the rotary contactor to a heated section of the rotary contactor, and rotating the rotary contactor so that the heated section of the rotary contactor is in a path of the exhaust gas and cooling the heated section of the rotary contactor with the exhaust gas to convert the heated section of the rotary contactor back to the cooled section of the rotary contactor, where the exhaust gas passes through an energy recovery device (16), the energy recovery device generating energy for compressing the released CO2, and the rotary contactor and the energy recovery device are located onboard the vehicle; and
   (2) a system (10) for capturing CO2 in exhaust gas of a vehicle (12), comprising a rotary contactor having a cooled section operable to receive delivery of the exhaust gas of the vehicle, the rotary contactor including openings that extend from a first side of the rotary contactor to a second side of the rotary contactor opposite the first side, a sorbent located on the cooled section of the rotary contactor operable to adsorb the CO2 of the exhaust gas, and a hot fluid directed at the cooled section of the rotary contactor operable to release the adsorbed CO2 and convert the cooled section of the rotary contactor to a heated section of the rotary contactor, where the rotary contactor is rotatable so that the heated section of the rotary contactor is cooled to convert the heated section of the rotary contactor back to the cooled section of the rotary contactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of an onboard carbon dioxide capture system.
   System (10)
   Vehicle (12)
   Energy recovery device (16)
   Compressor (20)
   Rotary contactor (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further includes: before delivering the exhaust gas of the vehicle to the cooled section of the rotary contactor, passing the exhaust gas through an energy recovery device, the energy recovery device generating energy for compressing the released CO2; after delivering the exhaust gas of the vehicle to the cooled section of the rotary contactor, passing the exhaust gas through an energy recovery device, the energy recovery device generating energy for compressing the released CO2, where the hot fluid of the vehicle is a working fluid of the organic Rankine cycle energy recovery device; and compressing and storing the released CO2 onboard the vehicle. The system further includes: an energy recovery device operable to generate energy for compressing the released CO2; and a compressor (20) operable to compress the released CO2 and a storage unit for storing the compressed CO2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109384236-A
TI Preparing silicon by using coal gangue or oil shale slag comprises taking coal or oil shale slag, catalyst, sulfuric acid, sodium hydroxide and carbon dioxide, specifically crushing coal gangue or oil shale slag and crushing.
AU LIU S
   WANG L
   YANG C
   TIAN X
   ZHU F
AE FUSHUN SHENGDA IND PROD COMPREHENSIVE (FUSH-Non-standard)
GA 2019212970
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon by using coal gangue or oil shale slag comprises taking coal or oil shale slag, catalyst, sulfuric acid, sodium hydroxide and carbon dioxide, specifically crushing coal gangue or oil shale slag, crushing to 80-100 meshes, (2) under the temperature of 600- 650 degrees C by boilin, calcining furnace, boiling and can use the carbon in coal gangue, affixing a little combustion catalyst, roasting method to heat activation, carbon in the raw material can be completely combusted, (3) removing organic substance, the raw material sintering comprises sintering powder of active use of the pozzolanic properties, (4) with 25-30 % concentration of sulfuric acid, the solid-liquid ratio is 1:3-4, the temperature is 90-95 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 3-4 hours until the pH value is 3-4 as acid leaching reaction terminal, (5) supplementing sulfuric acid to 25-30%, continuously reacting, repeating 3 times leaching operation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon by using coal gangue or oil shale slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes waste and reduces environment pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing silicon by using coal gangue or oil shale slag comprises taking coal or oil shale slag, catalyst, sulfuric acid, sodium hydroxide and carbon dioxide, specifically crushing coal gangue or oil shale slag, crushing to 80-100 meshes, (2) under the temperature of 600- 650 degrees C by boilin, calcining furnace, boiling and can use the carbon in coal gangue, affixing a little combustion catalyst, roasting method to heat activation, carbon in the raw material can be completely combusted, (3) removing organic substance, the raw material sintering comprises sintering powder of active use of the pozzolanic properties, (4) with 25-30 % concentration of sulfuric acid, the solid-liquid ratio is 1:3-4, the temperature is 90-95 degrees C, stirring and reacting for 3-4 hours until the pH value is 3-4 as acid leaching reaction terminal, (5) supplementing sulfuric acid to 25-30%, continuously reacting, repeating 3 times leaching operation, (6) separating solid-liquid, where residue is content not less than 90% silicon dioxide crude product, sodium hydroxide solution with concentration of 25-30 %, solid-to-liquid ratio of 1: 3-4, stirring at 95-100 degrees C for 2-3 hours to prepare the qualified modulus sodium silicate solution, (7) the qualified sodium silicate obtained by aging process can prepare sodium silicate product, (8) using about 30-40 % concentration of carbon dioxide gas, and qualifying the modulus of the sodium silicate solution and adjusting for carbonization reaction, a multi-stage counter-current absorption makes carbonization rate reaches more than 90%, controlling carbonized liquid PH value to 9-10, adding anhydrous ethanol as the carbide which has white colloidal precipitate in the solution, i.e. hydrated silicon dioxide precipitate, (9) separating solid from liquid and solid after washing with sulfuric acid to adjust the pH value, drying to obtain the silicon dioxide i.e. white carbon black product made of silicon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas with the lime calcining process and generated in the process of burning coal gangue gas purified by the gas purification, processing to obtain a concentration of about 30-40 % of the carbon dioxide gas. The carbonization process comprises adjusting the adding amount of the absolute ethyl alcohol and controlling the carbonization temperature and speed can produce nano-white carbon black product series.

PN US2019001256-A1; WO2019005716-A1; IN201917053217-A; CN110997879-A; EP3645672-A1; US10710020-B2; EP3645672-A4; IN355636-B; CN110997879-B
TI Purification of gas for separating contaminants e.g. carbon dioxide, involves contacting impure feed gas stream and solvent stream in absorber and concentrator/reabsorber to obtain overhead gas stream and solvent effluent bottom stream.

   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201900179L
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of gas involves contacting impure feed gas stream and a solvent stream in a counter-current absorber (4) to provide a overhead gas stream (46) and a solvent effluent bottom stream (23), passing the stream (23) to a concentrator/reabsorber (24) and contacting the stream (23) with a gas stream from a downstream rich solvent flash drum (10) to provide a overhead gas stream (116) and a solvent effluent bottom stream (42), splitting the stream (42) into streams (s1) and (s2), heat-exchanging the stream (s1) with a solvent stream from a downstream regenerator (64), heat-exchanging the stream (s2) with a portion of a solvent stream from a drum (40), combining streams to produce a combined stream, passing the solvent stream from the drum (10) to the drum (40), passing a remaining solvent stream from the drum (40) to the regenerator to provide an overhead gas stream and a bottom solvent effluent stream, and passing the bottom solvent effluent stream back to a top of the absorber.
   USE - Purification of gas used for separating contaminants e.g. carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and other sulfur compounds e.g. carbonyl sulfide and mercaptans. Can also be used for separating ammonia, hydrogen cyanide, and metal carbonyls.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and efficiently removes the acid gases from hydrocarbons with reduced amount of solvent and recovers significant portions of the desired components in the impure feed gas components, as a purified product gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of gas involves contacting an impure feed gas stream comprising hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and sulfur compounds and a solvent stream in a counter-current absorber to provide a overhead gas stream (46) and a solvent effluent bottom stream (23) comprising absorbed hydrogen sulfide, absorbed carbon dioxide and absorbed other sulfur compounds, passing the stream (23) to a concentrator/reabsorber and contacting the stream (23) with a gas stream from a downstream rich solvent flash drum (10) or an inert gas stream in the concentrator/reabsorber to provide a overhead gas stream (116) and a solvent effluent bottom stream (42), splitting the stream (42) into a stream (s1) and a stream (s2), heat-exchanging the stream (s1) with a solvent stream from a downstream regenerator, heat-exchanging the stream (s2) with a portion of a solvent stream from a downstream rich solvent flash drum (40), after the heat-exchanges, combining streams (s1) and (s2) to produce a combined stream and passing the combined stream to the downstream rich solvent flash drum (10), passing the solvent stream from the drum (10) to the drum (40), passing partial of the solvent stream from the drum (40) back to the counter-current absorber, passing a remaining portion of the solvent stream from the drum (40) to the downstream regenerator to provide an overhead gas stream and a bottom solvent effluent stream, and passing the bottom solvent effluent stream from the downstream regenerator back to a top of the counter-current absorber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the process for purifying gas.
   Counter-current absorber (4)
   Rich solvent flash drums (10,40)
   Solvent effluent bottom streams (23,42)
   Concentrator/reabsorber (24)
   Overhead streams (46,116)
   Regenerator (64)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The overhead gas stream (46) comprises a purified product gas stream. The overhead gas stream (116) comprises carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is chosen from alcohol, glycol ether, lactam, sulfolane, N-alkylated pyrrolidone, N-alkylated piperidine, cyclotetramethylene sulfone, N-alkylformamide, N-alkylacetamide, ether-ketone, propylene carbonate, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, N-formylmorpholine and alkyl phosphate, and comprises dimethyl ether of polyethylene glycol.
