PN CN108796548-A; CN108796548-B
TI Preparing formic acid and acetic acid by electro catalytic reduction comprises e.g. adding acetonitrile into a buffered aqueous solution, carrying out pre-electrolysis, electrolyzing, separating catholyte and separating liquid phase product.
AU LI C
   ZHA B
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201892680F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing formic acid and acetic acid by electro catalytic reduction comprises e.g. (i) adding acetonitrile into a buffered aqueous solution, mixing, adding a heteropoly acid anion room temperature ionic liquid into the acetonitrile-buffered aqueous solution, and mixing; (ii) adding acetonitrile into a buffered aqueous solution and mixing, carrying out pre-electrolysis of the catholyte at -0.5 to -2V for 0.5-1 hours, introducing carbon dioxide into the catholyte until the carbon dioxide in the catholyte is saturated and electrolyzing at a constant voltage of -0.5 to -2 V for 100-140 hours to obtain a catholyte after electrolysis; and (iv) separating catholyte after electrolysis by chromatography on a silica gel column to obtain a liquid phase product, and separating liquid phase product by batch distillation to obtain formic acid and acetic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing formic acid and acetic acid by electro catalytic reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces formic acid with Faraday effect of 20-90%, and acetic acid Faraday effect of 5-70%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing formic acid and acetic acid by electro catalytic reduction comprises (i) adding acetonitrile into a buffered aqueous solution, mixing to obtain an acetonitrile-buffered aqueous solution, adding a heteropoly acid anion room temperature ionic liquid to the acetonitrile-buffered aqueous solution, and mixing to obtain a heteropolyacid anion-acetonitrile-water ternary electrolyte solution, where pH of the buffered aqueous solution is 3.3-4.3, volume ratio of the acetonitrile and buffered aqueous solution is 3:2, where concentration of the heteropoly acid anion in the heteropoly acid anion-acetonitrile-water ternary electrolyte solution is from 1.517-2.275 mmol/liter; (ii) adding acetonitrile into a buffered aqueous solution and mixing to obtain an acetonitrile-buffered aqueous solution having a pH of 3.3-4.3, where volume ratio of the acetonitrile and buffered aqueous solution is 3:2, separating sodium type double-chamber electrochemical cell into a cathode chamber and an anode chamber by using a Nafion117 cation exchange membrane, using heteropoly acid anion-acetonitrile-water ternary electrolyte solution as a catholyte, and an acetonitrile-buffered aqueous solution as an anolyte, adding anolyte and the catholyte respectively into the anode chamber and the cathode chamber of the H-type double-chamber electrochemical cell, using indium sheet as the working electrode, the reference electrode is the silver/silver chloride electrode, and the counter electrode is the platinum sheet(anode), carrying out pre-electrolysis of the catholyte at -0.5 to -2V for 0.5-1 hours, introducing carbon dioxide into the catholyte until the carbon dioxide in the catholyte is saturated and electrolyzing at a constant voltage of -0.5 to -2 V for 100-140 hours to obtain a catholyte after electrolysis; and (iv) separating catholyte after electrolysis by chromatography on a silica gel column to obtain a liquid phase product, and separating liquid phase product by batch distillation to obtain formic acid and acetic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The room temperature ionic liquid of the heteropoly acid anion in step (i) comprises tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide cation and heteropoly acid anion at molar ratio is 7:1, the tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide cation chemical formula is (C32H68N)+, and the heteropolyacid anion chemical formula is (SiW9V3O40)7- or room temperature ionic liquid of heteropoly acid anion comprises dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation and heteropoly acid anion at molar ratio 4:1, where dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation has the chemical formula (C16H31N2)+, and the heteropolyacid anion has the chemical formula ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4- or room temperature ionic liquid of heteropoly acid anion consisting of tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide cation and heteropoly acid anion at molar ratio is 5:1, where tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide cation has the chemical formula (C32H68N)+, and the heteropoly acid anion has the chemical formula ( alpha -SiMnIIIW11(H2O)O39)5-. The solvent in the step (2) is acetone. Preferred Method: The method comprises preparing room temperature ionic liquid of the heteropolyacid anion comprising tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide cation and heteropoly acid anion comprises (a) dissolving sodium metasilicate nonahydrate in deionized water, adding sodium tungstate dihydrate under stirring, adding 6 mol/liter hydrochloric acid solution, stirring continuosly for 10 minutes, filtering with glass sand, setting the filtrate at 4 degrees C, allowing to stand for 2-3 days, precipitating white crystals, filtering by glass sand, and drying at room temperature for 1-2 days to obtain an intermediate product, where volume ratio of the mass of the sodium metasilicate nonahydrate and deionized water is 60g:500 ml, mass ratio of the sodium metasilicate nonahydrate and sodium tungstate dihydrate is 60:362 and volume ratio of the 6 mol/liter hydrochloric acid solution and deionized water is 200:500, dissolving sodium metavanadate in hot water at a temperature of 85 degrees C, cooling to 20 degrees C, adding 6 mol/liter hydrochloric acid solution during the cooling process, adjusting the pH to less than 1.5, stirring for 15 minutes, adding intermediate product, stirring for 15 minutes, adding potassium chloride, stirring, adding methanol into precipitate orange-red crystals, filtering through a glass sand and drying at room temperature for 12-24 hours to obtain a crude product, where the mass of the sodium metavanadate and the hot water of 85 degrees C is 6.4 g: 900 ml, where mass ratio of sodium metavanadate and intermediate product is 6.4:48, mass ratio of sodium metavanadate and potassium chloride is 6.4:60 and volume ratio of the mass of sodium metavanadate and methanol is 6.4 g: 900 ml and mixing hydrochloric acid with 0.03 mol/liter methanol to obtain a mixed solution, heating to 55 degrees C, adding crude product, dissolving crude product completely, cooling at 2-3 degrees C/minute, cooling mixture to 4 degrees C, and recrystallizing at 4 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain a polyoxometallate which is a polyoxometallate K6H (SiW9V3O40 containing (SiW9V3O40)7-anion).3H2O, where volume ratio of hydrochloric acid and 0.03 mol/liter methanol is 100:50 and volume ratio of the mass of the crude product and mixed solution is 30g:150;(b) dissolving tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide in ethanol to obtain a tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide solution, where mass ratio of tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide solution and ethanol is 3.85 g: 4 ml; and (c) dissolving the polyoxometallate in deionized water, adding the tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide solution drop wise, allowing to stand for 1-2 days to obtain the ionic liquid of the cherry red molten state which is based on heteropoly a room temperature ionic liquid of an acid anion, the mass ratio of polyoxometallate and deionized water is 2.78 g: 20 ml and molar ratio of (C32H68N)+ to (SiW9V3O40)7- in the room temperature ionic liquid based on heteropoly acid anion is 7:1. The addition rate of 6 mol/liter hydrochloric acid in the step (a) is 100-200 ml/minute. The preparation of room temperature ionic liquid of the heteropolyacid anion comprising dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation and heteropolyacid anion comprises (1) dissolving sodium molybdate in deionized water, adding 18mol/liter concentrated sulfuric acid under stirring, cooling to room temperature, adding acetonitrile, condensing, refluxing for 1-2 hours, transferring into a separating funnel, allowing to stand for layering and collecting an upper layer solution to obtain mixed organic solution containing ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4-anion, where volume ratio of the mass of the sodium molybdate and is 6.23 g: 20 ml, volume ratio of the mass of the sodium molybdate and 18 mol/liter concentrated sulfuric acid is 6.23 g: 4.96 ml and the volume ratio of sodium molybdate and acetonitrile is 6.23 g: 100 ml; (2) adding 1-methylimidazole and 1-iodododecane into the solvent, heating in a hydrothermal synthesis reaction kettle at 100 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain pale yellow solution, distilling and concentrating to remove the solvent in the pale yellow solution to obtain a dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide solution, where dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide chemical formula is C16H31IN2, where concentration of dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation in the dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodine solution is from 0.75-1.5 mol/liter, where the dodecyl 1-methyl group chemical formula of the imidazolium cation is (C16H31N2)+, mass ratio of 1-methylimidazole and 1-iodododecane is 1.23165:4.44345 and the mass ratio of 1-methylimidazole and solvent is 1.23165 g: 30 ml; and (3) adding a solution of dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide into the mixed organic solution containing ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4-anion at room temperature, allowing to stand for 1 to 2 days to obtain a blue-black solid solution ionic liquid, where molar ratio of room temperature ionic liquid based on the heteropoly acid anion of (C16H31N2)+ and ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4- in the room temperature ionic liquid based on the heteropolyacid anion is 4:1. The preparation of room temperature ionic liquid of the heteropolyacid anion comprising dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation and heteropolyacid anion comprises (1) dissolving sodium molybdate in deionized water, adding 18 mol/liter concentrated sulfuric acid under stirring, cooling to room temperature, adding acetonitrile, condensing, refluxing for 1-2 hours, transferring into a separating funnel, allowing to stand for layering and collecting an upper layer solution to obtain mixed organic solution containing ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4-anion, where volume ratio of the mass of the sodium molybdate and is 6.23 g: 20 ml, volume ratio of the mass of the sodium molybdate and 18 mol/liter concentrated sulfuric acid is 6.23 g: 4.96 ml and the volume ratio of sodium molybdate and acetonitrile is 6.23 g: 100 ml; (2) adding 1-methylimidazole and 1-iodododecane into the solvent, heating in a hydrothermal synthesis reaction kettle at 100 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain pale yellow solution, distilling and concentrating to remove the solvent in the pale yellow solution to obtain a dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide solution, where dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide chemical formula is C16H31IN2, where concentration of dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium cation in the dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodine solution is from 0.75-1.5 mol/liter, where the dodecyl 1-methyl group chemical formula of the imidazolium cation is (C16H31N2)+, mass ratio of 1-methylimidazole and 1-iodododecane is 1.23165:4.44345 and the mass ratio of 1-methylimidazole and solvent is 1.23165 g: 30 ml; and (3) adding a solution of dodecyl 1-methylimidazolium iodide into the mixed organic solution containing ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4-anion at room temperature, allowing to stand for 1 to 2 days to obtain a blue-black solid solution ionic liquid, where molar ratio of room temperature ionic liquid based on the heteropoly acid anion of (C16H31N2)+ and ( alpha -S2Mo18O62)4- in the room temperature ionic liquid based on the heteropolyacid anion is 4:1.

PT P
PN CN108605648-A
TI Industrialized clinker cultivation method of organic Agaricus blazei, involves adding medium to gas permeable bag, autoclaving bag in turnover basket, inoculating culture bag with Agaricus blazei, culturing and harvesting Agaricus blazei.
AU WEI L
   ZHOU X
   LI X
AE BEIJING IEDA PROTECTED HORTICULTURE CO L (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018805130
AB    NOVELTY - Industrialized clinker cultivation method of organic Agaricus blazei, involves preparing medium, adding medium to gas permeable bag and then placing bag in a turnover basket, autoclaving the assembled culture bag together with the turnover basket, cooling content, inoculating cooled culture bag with A.blazei var. cultivar in a sterile inoculation chamber by a fully automatic inoculation machine, and automatically sealing with a sealing machine, transferring the inoculated culture bag together with the turnover basket from the inoculation chamber to the culture rack of the culture chamber, setting the culture temperature and, air humidity and culturing content in dark environment, transferring the culture bag together with the turnover basket to the germination chamber, continue culturing, performing management and harvesting A.blazei.
   USE - The industrialized clinker cultivation method is useful for organic A.blazei.
   ADVANTAGE - The industrialized clinker cultivation method increases A.blazei (2-3.5) kg to (5-6) kg/square m, and ensures that cultivated A.blazei is pesticide-free, mercury-free and cadmium-free, and provides nutritious healthy food for humans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Industrialized clinker cultivation method of organic Agaricus blazei, involves (a) preparing medium by adding fresh, mold-free straw, corn cob, wheat bran, cornmeal and lime to the mixer and uniformly mixing the content, adding water to the water content of 60-65 wt.%, and adjusting the pH to 7.5- 8.5, (b) adding medium to gas permeable bag and then placing bag in a turnover basket, (c) adding the assembled culture bag together with the turnover basket into an autoclave for high temperature autoclaving, (d) cooling content, (e) inoculating cooled culture bag with A.blazei var. cultivar in a sterile inoculation chamber by a fully automatic inoculation machine, and automatically sealing with a sealing machine, (f) transferring the inoculated culture bag together with the turnover basket from the inoculation chamber to the culture rack of the culture chamber, setting the culture temperature to 23-25 degrees C and air humidity to 60-65%, the concentration of carbon dioxide to 2000-2500 ppm, and culturing content in a dark environment for 55-60 days, (g) covering soil 2-3 cm, controlling the temperature at 23 degrees C, controlling the humidity at more than 90%, and continue cultivating in dark light to make the hyphae climb the soil layer, (h) transferring the culture bag together with the turnover basket to the germination chamber, the temperature control is 16-18 degrees C, the humidity is more than 90%, the carbon dioxide concentration is 1300-1500 ppm, and the illumination is 150-300 lx, thus the A.blazei is neat and concentrated, (i) further controlling temperature to 22-23 degrees C, the humidity to more than 90%, the carbon dioxide concentration to 1300-1500 ppm, and the light is 100-150 lx, thus A.blazei grows quickly and neatly and (j) performing management and harvesting A.blazei by harvesting when the A.blazei is fully grown and the membrane is not broken, after harvesting, remove the residues from the mushroon bed, adding 150-200 ml water per bag, then cooling content to 16-18 degrees C, humidity to 93-95%, the concentration of carbon dioxide to 1300-1500 ppm, and the illumination to 300 lx, and performing A.blazei fruiting body, and performing fruiting management.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the medium comprises 30-50% corn cob, 5-49.5% straw, 15-35% wheat bran, 5-15% corn flour, and 0.5-1% lime. In step (b), placing medium in the bag using a fully automatic bagging machine, a conveyor belt, and a robot. The size of the bag is 20 x 12.5 x 44.5 cm. In step (c), the high temperature and high pressure sterilization pressure is 1.2-1.3 kg/cm2 , the temperature is 120 degrees C, and the holding pressure is 1-1.5 hours. In step (d), reducing the temperature of the culture bag to less than 22 degrees C using the refrigeration facility. In step (e), the weight of the medium in each culture bag is 1.7-1.8 kg, and the inoculum amount of A.blazei is 25-30 g. In step (f), the inoculated culture bag and the turnover basket is transferred from the inoculation chamber to the culture chamber of the culture chamber by a forklift and a racking machine. In step (g), the covering soil is river soil, grass soil or vegetable garden soil below 20 cm. In step (i), controlling temperature and humidity for germination and mushroom management with air conditioning, humidifiers and fresh air equipment.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-A08; C04-A10; C04-A98; C05-A01B; C05-C04; C14-S18; C14-T03; P13-B01; P13-E01; P13-E02; P13-E03

PN WO2018072021-A1; US2018112142-A1; US9969949-B1; US2018251694-A1; US10487282-B2
TI Provision of upgraded biogas e.g. renewable            natural gas used for transportation applications,            involves enriching obtained tail gas with specified            amount of natural gas, and feeding portion of obtained            upgraded biogas to distribution system.
AU FOODY B
   FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 201832198F
AB    NOVELTY - Provision of upgraded biogas (22) involves                feeding biogas (15) into a biogas upgrading system,                enriching (30) the obtained tail gas (24) with                natural gas withdrawn from a distribution system,                providing at least a portion of the enriched tail                gas to a combustion system configured to produce                heat and/or electricity, and feeding (60) at least                a portion of the upgraded biogas to the                distribution system. The content of the natural gas                withdrawn from the distribution system is at least                50%. The content of the natural gas and the                upgraded biogas is measured as energy delivered                over a period of time.
   USE - Provision of upgraded biogas e.g. renewable                natural gas used for transportation applications                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and                environmentally-friendly by the use of electricity                generated from enriched tail gas and energy                captured from pressure letdown resulting from                withdrawing natural gas, and efficiently produces                upgraded biogas. The enriched tail gas has low                methane content and reduced heating value. The use                of natural gas as sweep gas reduces number of                membranes and power required for upgrading biogas,                and enhances the removal of carbon dioxide from                biogas. The obtained renewable natural gas                efficiently replaces fossil transportation fuels.                The injection of upgraded biogas into distribution                system reduces emissions with respect to transport                and provide diversity to transportation                sector.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Provision of upgraded biogas involves (a)                feeding biogas into a biogas upgrading system, (b)                enriching tail gas produced by the biogas upgrading                system with natural gas by feeding natural gas                withdrawn from a distribution system into biogas                upgrading system and/or the tail gas to provide an                enriched tail gas, (c) providing at least a portion                of the enriched tail gas to a combustion system                configured to produce heat and/or electricity, and                (d) feeding at least a portion of the upgraded                biogas to the distribution system. The content of                natural gas withdrawn from the distribution system                is at least 50%, based on the amount of upgraded                biogas fed into the distribution system. The                content of the natural gas and the upgraded biogas                is measured as energy delivered over a period of                time. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for biogas                upgrading system, which consists of an inlet for                receiving biogas, a separating system configured to                remove carbon dioxide from the biogas, a natural                gas inlet configured to provide natural, an outlet                (o1) configured to provide a portion of the                upgraded biogas to the distribution system, and an                outlet (o2) configured to provide a portion of the                enriched tail gas to the combustion system for                combusting (40) the enriched tail gas stream                comprising gas comprising at least 75% methane,                based on methane content of the biogas. The                separating system produces upgraded biogas and tail                gas comprising at least a portion of the removed                carbon dioxide. The natural gas inlet is arranged                such that a stream of natural gas is fed to the                separating system and/or the tail gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram of                provision of the upgraded biogas.
   Biogas (15)
   Removal of carbon dioxide from biogas                (20)
   Upgraded biogas (22)
   Tail gas (24)
   Enrichment of tail gas with natural gas                (30)
   Combustion of enriched tail gas (40)
   Process of feeding portion of upgraded biogas                into distribution system (60)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The biogas upgrading                  system further consists of a membrane configured                  to separate carbon dioxide from methane, a                  pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit configured                  to separate nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide from                  methane, and a turboexpander configured to reduce                  the pressure of the natural gas withdrawn from                  the distribution system and to increase the                  pressure of the biogas fed to the upgrading                  system and the upgraded biogas. The turboexpander                  generates energy for compressing the upgraded                  biogas from a pressure letdown resulting from                  withdrawing the natural gas from the distribution                  system. The membrane has a retentate side and a                  permeate side. The PSA unit consists of at least                  one vessel having an adsorbent for adsorbing                  carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen and passing                  methane, and purge gas inlet(s) for providing a                  purge gas to desorb carbon dioxide and/or                  nitrogen from the adsorbent. The purge gas inlet                  is fluidly connected to the distribution system                  for providing purge gas comprising a portion of                  the natural gas. The distribution system consists                  of a transmission pipeline and a distribution                  pipeline. The separating system consists of an                  inlet (i1) configured to receive the biogas, an                  inlet (i2) configured to receive natural gas, and                  the outlets (o1) and (o2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  removing (20) carbon dioxide from a stream of                  biogas to produce a stream of methane-rich gas                  and a stream of methane-depleted gas, producing                  the enriched tail gas comprising gas from the                  stream of methane-depleted gas, feeding the                  renewable natural gas comprising gas from the                  methane-rich gas stream to the distribution                  system, and generating a fuel credit associated                  with transportation use of the renewable natural                  gas. The content of the natural gas and the                  renewable natural gas is measured as MMBTU/hour.                  The step (b) involves mixing the natural gas with                  the tail gas, after the tail gas exits the                  upgrading system. In step (b), the natural gas is                  preferably fed into the biogas upgrading system                  as a sweep gas, which flows on the permeate side                  of the membrane. The method further involves (e)                  partially removing siloxane from the biogas such                  that the content of siloxane in the upgraded                  biogas and in the enriched tail gas is less than                  50%, based on the amount of siloxane originally                  present in the biogas, or partially removing                  siloxane from the tail gas prior to enrichment,                  or removing siloxane from the biogas and                  disposing 50% or more removed siloxane in a                  stream that is not fed to the distribution system                  and not combusted in the combustion system, and                  (f) treating the biogas to reduce hydrogen                  sulfide concentrations prior to feeding the                  biogas to the separating system. The step (d)                  involves feeding a specified amount of upgraded                  biogas associated with environmental attributes                  (e1) to the distribution pipeline. The method                  further involves (g) withdrawing a specified                  amount of methane associated with environmental                  attributes (e2), based on the amount of the                  upgraded biogas, from the distribution system,                  (h) generating the fuel credit associated with                  the content of methane withdrawn from the                  distribution system in dependence upon the                  methane content and the injection of the content                  of upgraded biogas into the distribution system                  or upon a quantity of upgraded biogas fed to the                  distribution system, and (i) withdrawing a                  specified amount of natural gas from the                  distribution system for the fuel credit. The fuel                  credit is dependent on the environmental                  attributes (e1). The step (b) involves selecting                  a flow of the stream of natural gas to provide                  the enriched tail gas having a heating value of                  at least 75% or within 25%, based on the heating                  value of the biogas fed to the upgrading system.                  The method involves feeding the stream of natural                  gas into the separating system such that the tail                  gas is enriched with natural gas. The stream of                  natural gas is conveyed with the carbon dioxide                  removed from the biogas to the outlet (o2). The                  method involves obtaining biogas from a biogas                  source that produces raw biogas comprising carbon                  dioxide and methane. For every BTU/day of                  upgraded biogas fed to the distribution system,                  at least half BTU/day of natural gas is withdrawn                  from the distribution system for enriching. The                  method involves feeding the natural gas having                  reduced pressure into the biogas upgrading                  system, mixing the natural gas having reduced                  pressure and the methane-depleted tail gas,                  feeding a portion of the methane-rich upgraded                  biogas to a compressor for compressing the                  methane-rich upgraded biogas to a pressure                  suitable for injection into the distribution                  system. The compressor is at least partially                  driven by the energy provided by the                  turboexpander. Preferred Composition: The content                  of natural gas withdrawn from the distribution                  system is preferably at least 80%, based on the                  content of upgraded biogas fed into the                  distribution system. The content of methane in                  the enriched tail gas is alternately at least                  35%, preferably at least 40%, based on the                  methane content of the biogas fed to the                  upgrading system. Preferred Properties: The                  enriched tail gas has a heating value of at least                  350 BTU/ft3, preferably 350-600 BTU/ft3. The fuel                  credit has a renewable identification number, and                  a low carbon fuel standard credit. The tail gas                  has a heating value of less than about 125                  BTU/ft3 without enrichment. The heating value of                  the enriched tail gas is more than the minimum                  heating value of the combustion unit by at least                  50 BTU/ft3. The flow rate of the sweep gas is                  selected such that the rate of energy delivered                  of natural gas is equal to at least half of the                  rate of energy delivered of the portion of                  upgraded biogas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2022B5270X
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrate membrane device comprises gas cylinder                (1), gas buffer tank (4), first temperature sensor                (7), first three-way valve (8), first pressure                sensor (9), second shut-off valve (10), second                three-way valve (11), second pressure sensor (12),                second temperature sensor (13), hydrate membrane                separator (14), first product gas tank (17), second                product gas tank (19), circulating water bath (20),                computer (21) and data acquisition instrument (22).                The gas cylinder is connected to the gas buffer                tank through a pipeline. The gas buffer tank is                provided with a first temperature sensor. The top                of the gas buffer tank is connected with the air                inlet of the hydrate membrane separator through a                pipeline. The first three-way valve, second                shut-off valve, and second three-way valve are                arranged on pipeline between gas buffer tank and                hydrate membrane separator. The top of the hydrate                membrane separator is provided with a second                temperature sensor.
   USE - Hydrate membrane device used for separating                function of methane-hydrogen, methane-carbon                dioxide, methane-nitrogen, carbon dioxide-nitrogen                mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrate membrane device realizes the                continuous gas separation operation, improves the                production efficiency, and has high separation                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hydrate                membrane method for gas separation, which involves:                (a) placing hydrate film tube in a drying                container, heating hydrate film tube, vacuuming at                25-150&#8451; for 0.5-24 hours to make it meet                the required vacuum degree requirement;
   (b) injecting deionized water in a vacuum                state, cooling, transferring the hydrate membrane                tube from the deionized water to the prepared                additive solution for 0.5-48 hours;
   (c) placing the hydrate membrane tube after                soaking in the solution in a -30-3&#8451;                environment for 1-48 hours to form a hydrate                membrane;
   (d) placing hydrate membrane tube in an                environment of 0-15&#8451; for 1-48 hours to                melt the ice that may be formed, converting into                hydrate;
   (e) putting the hydrate membrane tube in the                environment of -30-3&#8451; to solidify stably                for 0.5-24 hours to ensure that all the aqueous                solution in the pores of the hydrate membrane tube                forms hydrate;
   (f)assembling hydrate membrane tube into the                hydrate membrane separator;
   (g) opening the circulating water bath,                stabilizing the temperature of the gas buffer tank                and the membrane module cooling jacket (15) at -20-                15&#8451;;
   (h) keeping the vent valve in a closed state,                opening the first shut-off valve, passing the mixed                gas into the buffer tank;
   (i) opening the second shut-off valve to carry                out gas separation for a period of time;
   (j) opening the third shut-off valve, using                the first product gas tank to collect the permeate,                opening the fourth shut-off valve; and (k) using                the second product gas tank to collect the                retentate gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                hydrate membrane device.

PN CN114317411-A
TI Culturing human glomerular endothelial cell by sterilizing microfluidic cell chip and chip cover, rinsing microfluidic cell chip, adding human glomerular endothelial cell solution and serum-containing medium and culturing cells.
AU GONG Z
   ZHUO Q
   WANG L
   WANG J
   PIAO W
   HUANG J
   YIN J
   CHEN D
   HUO J
   QIN W
   YANG Z
AE NUTRITION & HEALTH INST CHINESE CENT DIS (CCDC-C)
GA 202256780F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing human glomerular endothelial cell involves sterilizing the microfluidic cell chip and chip cover, perfusing with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer, aspirating and discarding the PBS buffer, again adding PBS buffer to wells, sealing, allowing to stand, covering the microfluidic cell chip, then rinsing the microfluidic cell chip, then allowing to stand, discarding the liquid, adding human glomerular endothelial cell solution and serum-containing medium, pressurizing the microfluidic cell chip and culturing the cells in microfluidic cell chip under carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method for culturing human glomerular endothelial cell and evaluating its toxicity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method precisely regulates temperature and gas composition of the cell growth area, hence eliminates need for an external incubator, achieves long-term continuous observation of cells under the microscope, hence the microfluidic cell model improves simulation of the human microenvironment, and accurately performs drug evaluation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing human glomerular endothelial cell based on microfluidic chip involves (a) sterilizing the microfluidic cell chip and chip cover, preparing a rubber pad for cleaning, sterilization, ultra-static drying, then placing on the microfluidic cell chip, where the microfluidic cell chip is provided with 1-6 columns of liquid inlet wells and 7 columns and 8 columns of drainage wells, sucking out the moisturizing liquid in the wells, perfusing the phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer into the wells 1-8, aspirating and discarding the PBS buffer in wells 1-8, again adding 300-350 mul PBS buffer to wells 1-6, sealing, allowing to stand at room temperature for 30-40 minutes, covering the microfluidic cell chip, then rinsing the microfluidic cell chip and then allowing to stand, (b) discarding the liquid in well 6 of the microfluidic cell chip, then adding 10-15 mul human glomerular endothelial cell solution to the well 6 at a concentration of 1-5x106 cells/ml, adding 350 mul serum-containing medium into well 1 and pressurizing the microfluidic cell chip at 0.25 psi for 8 seconds to complete the injection of cells into the culture chamber and (c) culturing the cells in microfluidic cell chip at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide gas concentration, where the well is naturally perfused with the medium by gravity, and the cells are adhered to the wall for 24 hours.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for evaluating toxicity using the human glomerular endothelial cells, involves (a) blotting the liquid in wells 1 and 2 dry, adding 350 mul test substance to well 2 for intervention, setting the injection pressure of well 2 to 0.5-1 psi, culturing at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide for 48 hours and then detecting toxicity index, (b) collecting the cell culture solution obtained in step (a), adding the cell culture solution into a lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) kit, detecting the LDH concentration, and recording the detection results and (c) adding CCK-8 detection working solution, incubating for 1-4 hours at 3 psi and 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide, transferring the product into a cell culture plate, placing the cell culture plate on a microplate reader for detection, setting the absorbance of the microplate reader to 450 nm, detecting and recording the detection results.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves adding 2x106 cells/ml into the well 6. The step (b) involves adding 15 mul human glomerular endothelial cell solution. The step (b) involves centrifuging or filtering the test substance and then adding to the well 2. The step (c) involves incubating for 4 hours. The method (M2) further involves adding live and dead cell dye to the cells obtained in step (a), adjusting the pressure of the microfluidic chip to 2-3 psi, performing fluorescence observation after 30 minutes of staining, and recording the observation results. The method (M2) further involves adding 4% formaldehyde to the cells obtained in step (a) to fix the cells, blocking, adding primary antibodies and secondary antibodies, then incubating for fluorescence observation, and recording the observation results, where the primary antibody concentration is 1:50.

PN EP3939690-A1
TI Abatement of sulfur dioxide in flue gas stream of internal combustion engines that use high sulfur content fuel in vehicle, watercraft, power generation machinery or system or exhaust gases from industrial processes by catalytic converters.
AU YAKOUMIS I
   SAKKAS K M
   MOSCHOVI A M
AE MONOLITHOS CATALYSTS & RECYCLING LTD (MONO-Non-standard)
GA 202212377Y
AB    NOVELTY - Abatement of sulfur dioxide in flue gas stream of internal combustion engines that use high sulfur content fuel in vehicle, watercraft, power generation machinery or system or exhaust gases from industrial processes, using catalytic converters in contact flow with the flue gas stream, which contains at least 0.0001-1 vol.% sulfur dioxide and 0.0001-1 vol.% carbon monoxide and 0.0001-1vol.% hydrocarbons and 0.0001-1 vol.% nitric oxide, of which part or all of the sulfur dioxide is converted to liquid elemental sulfur after chemical reaction of it with carbon monoxide on catalytic converters in temperature values higher than 200 degrees C to the production of carbon dioxide or/and with hydrocarbons to the production of carbon dioxide and water, or/and with hydrocarbons and nitric oxide to the producing carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for abatement of sulfur dioxide in flue gas stream of internal combustion engines that use high sulfur content fuel in vehicle, watercraft, power generation machinery or system or exhaust gases from industrial processes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves efficiency of sulfur dioxide reduction; provides procedure that to involves installation of device at engine's exhaust stream at appropriate temperature, in series with microparticle filter, to effectively treat all toxic gaseous pollutants; imposes drastic reduction of sulfur dioxide emissions in all ships up to 85%; utilizes catalytic conversion method of sulfur dioxide emissions from internal combustion engines to converts sulfur dioxide to elemental sulfur in flue gas stream, using catalytic converters similar to the ones used extensively in automotive industry for treatment of gasoline engines exhaust gases; and leads to development and evolution of sulfur dioxide emissions handling technologies and allows for using same fuel in internal combustion engines, thus avoiding large fixed cost for necessary conversions to the internal combustion engines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) device for the abatement of the sulfur dioxide in flue gas stream of internal combustion engines, which is monolithic catalysts installed in a row and hermetically sealed inside a metallic structure for the exhaust flow handling, with exhaust inlet stream, contains the sulfur dioxide and carbon monoxide and the hydrocarbons and the nitric oxide, and exhaust outlet stream which contains sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons and nitric oxide in concentration ranges smaller than the concentration ranges of the exhaust inlet stream or in zero amounts, and part of draining and flowing of the produced liquid elemental sulfur; and
   (2) procedure for the abatement of the sulfur dioxide in flue gas stream of internal combustion engines, to which the device for the abatement of the sulfur dioxide is installed on the flue gas pipeline with exhaust inlet stream where the sulfur dioxide is converted to elemental sulfur when it comes in contact with the sulfur dioxide abatement device to the production of exhaust outlet stream with concentration ranges of sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide, hydrocarbons and nitric oxide less than the concentration and elemental sulfur stream flowing from the output.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalytic converter is at least two catalytic monoliths installed in a row and hermetically sealed inside a metallic structure for the exhaust flow handling, and each catalytic monolith is heterogeneous catalyst deposited on an non-flammable monolithic ceramic matrix made of oxides comprising oxide of magnesium, aluminum, silicon, iron, zirconia, cerium, yttrium, lanthanum, titanium, gadolinium, manganese, and niobium or metallic matrix made of materials comprising steel, stainless steel, titanium or other similar material, and the heterogeneous catalyst is the metallic phase at a rate of 0.5-20 wt.%, at least one metal or/and sulfate form of it comprising copper, iron, molybdenum, cobalt, rhodium, iridium, ruthenium, palladium, nickel, tungsten, vanadium, tin, zinc, antimony or manganese, which is immobilized on the surface of porous ceramic phase ceric oxide, enhanced, stabilized, combined or mixed with oxides of yttrium, praseodymium, strontium, scandium, lanthanum, titanium, zirconium, gadolinium, niobium, vanadium, samarium, neodymium or/and terbium. The device is installed on the flue gas pipeline and is in contact flow with a vessel for the collection, cooling and storing of the produced elemental sulfur. The procedure contains an additional carbon nanoparticle filter, self-regenerating or not, which is placed at the flue gas pipeline in upstream or downstream contact flow to the sulfur dioxide abatement device, which is possible to combine an oxidative catalyst with the carbon nanoparticle filter. The procedure contains an additional oxygen removal system installed on the flue gas pipeline in upstream contact flow to the sulfur dioxide abatement device and is possible to reduce the oxygen concentration in the exhaust gasses. The procedure contains an additional cyclone-type centrifugal system with a solids collection vessel, for the removal of leaked sulfur, installed on the flue gas pipeline in upstream contact flow to the sulfur dioxide abatement device.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Co-producing ethylene glycol by synthesizing            dimethyl carbonate coupling-carbon dioxide comprises            crushing and sieving dried raw material coal, carrying            out gasification reaction, separating crude synthesis,            rectifying and separating methanol and dimethyl            carbonate, and recycling obtained methanol.
AU CHU G
   ZHANG D
   ZHANG J
   YANG Q
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 2021B61222
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing ethylene glycol by synthesizing                dimethyl carbonate coupling-carbon dioxide (CO2),                comprises crushing and sieving the dried raw                material coal, and carrying out gasification                reaction to obtain crude synthesis gas mainly                containing hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO) and                CO2, performing water gas shift to a portion of the                crude synthesis gas, reacting CO and H2O to                generateH2 and CO2, converging the crude synthesis                gas after the water gas conversion with the                residual crude synthesis gas without water gas                conversion to obtain the crude synthesis gas,                deacidifyingthe crude synthesis gas, separating the                crude synthesis by lowtemperature methanol washing                process, rectifying and separating the mixed gas of                dimethyl carbonate and ethylene glycol,                respectively pure dimethyl carbonate and ethylene                glycol, and rectifying and separating methanol and                dimethyl carbonate, respectively ethylene glycol,                dimethyl carbonate and methanol, and recycling the                obtained methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing ethylene                glycol by synthesizing dimethyl carbonate                coupling-carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recycles CO2, reduces the discharge                of CO2, improves the carbonutilization efficiency                of the system, energy efficiency and                economic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing ethylene glycol by synthesizing                dimethyl carbonate coupling-carbon dioxide (CO2),                comprises (i) crushing and sieving the dried raw                material coal, and carrying out gasification                reaction to obtain crude synthesis gas mainly                containing hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO) and                CO2, (ii) performing water gas shift to a portion                of the crude synthesis gas, reacting CO and H2O to                generateH2 and CO2, converging the crude synthesis                gas after the water gas conversion with the                residual crude synthesis gas without water gas                conversion to obtain the crude synthesis gas after                adjusting the hydrogen-carbon ratio, the mol ratio                of H2 to CO in the crude synthesis gas after                regulating and controlling the hydrogen-carbon                ratio is 1.6-2.4, (iii) deacidifyingthe crude                synthesis gas, separating the crude synthesis by                lowtemperature methanol washing process, purifying                to remove the acidic gas in the synthesis gas to                obtain purified synthesis gas, separating the                purified synthesis gas, respectively CO2 and                synthesis gasmainly containing H2 and CO, dividing                the synthesis gas mainly containing H2 and CO into                two partsof synthesis gas I and synthesis gas II,                (iv) taking the synthesis gas I for synthesizing                methanol by methanol synthesis, (v) reacting the                CO2 with themethanol, generating dimethylcarbonate                and water, reacting water with theexternal ethylene                oxide to generate ethylene glycol, (vi) reacting                the synthesisgas II with methyl nitrite to generate                dimethyl oxalate and nitric oxide, generating                dimethyl carbonate and nitric oxide by side                reaction, dimethyl oxalate reacts with H2 to                generate methylglycolate and methanol, reacitng                methyl glycolate with H2 to generate ethylene                glycol and methanol, partially reacitng methanol                with nitric oxide, and the oxygengenerates methyl                nitrite, (vii) rectifying and separating the mixed                gas of dimethyl carbonate and ethylene glycol,                respectively pure dimethyl carbonate and ethylene                glycol, and (viii) rectifying and separating                methanol and dimethyl carbonate, respectively                ethylene glycol, dimethyl carbonate and methanol,                and recycling the obtained methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the raw material                  consumption of the method, the ratio of the molar                  amount of ethylene oxide to the mass of raw coal                  is 1-10 kmol/t. In the step (ii), the mole                  percentage of the crude synthesis gas subjected                  to the water gas shift to the total crude                  synthesis gas is 20-80%. In the step (iii), the                  molar ratio of synthesis gas I to synthesis gas                  II is 0.5-2:1. the yield ratio of ethylene glycol                  and dimethyl carbonate is 0.7-1.5t:1t.

TI Performing exhaust gas measurement calibration method involves using real-time exhaust gas measurement device, and measuring concentration of soot contained in exhaust gas of driving vehicle by signal received from ultraviolet receiver.
AU HWANG B H
   YANG J Y
   JUNG C H
AE KENTEC CO LTD (KENT-Non-standard)
GA 2021990156
AB    NOVELTY - Performing exhaust gas measurement calibration method involves using real-time exhaust gas measurement device, measuring concentration of soot contained in exhaust gas of driving vehicle by using signal received from ultraviolet receiver of light receiving unit. The running vehicle has soot calibration element for correcting magnitude of signal of exhaust gas measured by infrared receiver using concentration of soot measured by ultraviolet receiver in, irradiated ultraviolet, white light and infrared rays at same time or at set time intervals from light transmitting unit. The light irradiated in light irradiation step passes through exhaust gas emission region of running vehicle and passes through reflection unit to measure signal in infrared receiver and ultraviolet receiver.
   USE - Method for performing exhaust gas measurement calibration method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to have high absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing exhaust gas measurement calibration method involves using real-time exhaust gas measurement device, measuring concentration of soot contained in exhaust gas of driving vehicle by using signal received from ultraviolet receiver of light receiving unit. The running vehicle has soot calibration element for correcting magnitude of signal of exhaust gas measured by infrared receiver using concentration of soot measured by ultraviolet receiver in, irradiated ultraviolet, white light and infrared rays at same time or at set time intervals from light transmitting unit. The light irradiated in light irradiation step passes through exhaust gas emission region of running vehicle and passes through reflection unit to measure signal in infrared receiver and ultraviolet receiver. The measurement result of infrared band is measured by infrared receiver in measurement step, 4,25 micrometer signal for carbon dioxide (CO2) detection, 4.7 micrometer signal for carbon monoxide (CO) detection, 3.34 micrometer signal for HC detection, and influence of three gasses infrared reaction gas concentration calculation step of calculating concentration of three gasses by using 3.80 micrometer signal that does not receive. The concentrations of nitrogen oxide NO and soot are calculated by using embedded spectrometer to measure concentrations of NO and soot as result of measurement in UV band measured by UV receiver in measuring step, and when measurement result calculated in infrared reaction gas concentration calculation step is low in soot concentration calculated in ultraviolet reaction gas concentration calculation step, effect on measurement results of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbon, and nitrogen monoxide is small, but measured When soot concentration increases, measured concentrations of these gasses appear high in case of exhaust gasses containing same concentrations of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons and nitrogen monoxide, because higher soot concentration, smaller size of signal measured by infrared receiver because has effect of blocking ultraviolet light. The concentration of gas measured in infrared reaction gas concentration calculation step using soot concentration measured due to phenomenon in which absorbance is measured to be high for each gas as natural log value of concentration of soot carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas by dividing concentrations of carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons, and nitrogen monoxide to correct concentration of gas contained in exhaust gas.
TF EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Breathing machine for mechanical ventilation patient, has air flow meter connected with air suction pipeline or exhaling pipeline through air flow meter collecting air pipe, where air flow meter is configured to be capable of detecting and sending air flow signal.
AU LI J
AE GUANGZHOU WOMEN & CHILDRENS HEALTHCARE (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU WOMEN & CHILDRENS MEDICAL CENT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021453969
AB    (CN213031568-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a respirator, relating to the technical field of medical appliance, comprising an oxygen mixing device, an air suction pipeline and an expiration pipeline; it further comprises a gas sensor and an airflow meter; the air inlet of the gas sensor is connected with a first gas collecting pipeline and a second gas collecting pipeline; one end of the first gas collecting pipeline far away from the gas sensor is connected with the air suction pipeline; one end of the second gas collecting pipeline far away from the gas sensor is connected with the exhaling pipeline; and the gas sensor is configured to detect and emit the oxygen concentration signal of the gas and the carbon dioxide concentration signal; the air flow meter is connected with the air suction pipeline or the exhalation pipeline through the air flow meter collecting air pipe; and the air flow meter is configured to detect and send out air flow signal; the utility model at least relieves the existing technology in measuring VO2 by Douglas bag collecting method; without considering the difference between the suction amount and expiratory amount and cannot be suitable for mechanical ventilation patient; the clinical application is not reliable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of ventilator use in as a medical appliance during cardiac surgery of congenital heart disease.
   Oxygen mixing device (1)
   Air suction pipeline (2)
   Gas sensor (4)
   Air flow meter (5)
   Oxygen inlet (11)
   Air inlet (12)
   Air outlet (13)
   Electromagnetic valves (411,421)
EA    NOVELTY - The ventilator has an oxygen mixing device (1) provided with an oxygen inlet (11), an air inlet (12) and an air outlet (13). An end of an air suction pipeline (2) is connected with the air outlet of the device. A gas sensor (4) detects and emits an oxygen concentration signal of gas and a carbon dioxide concentration signal. An air flow meter (5) is connected to the suction or exhaling pipeline through an air flowmeter collecting air pipe, and detects and sends the air flow signal. Two electromagnetic valves (411,421) are installed in a machine shell of the respirator, and are configured to alternately open or close.
   USE - Ventilator use in as a medical appliance during cardiac surgery of congenital heart disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine provides enough nutrition to the critical patient, especially the baby and the infant, which can reduce the death rate and improve the prognosis. The respiratory quotient RQ is the ratio of the volume of carbon dioxide released by the exhalation in the same time and the volume O2 sucked by the inhalation which can provide the utilization of the carbohydrate, fat and protein three nutrient elements. The oxygen consumption VO2 is an important factor leading to critical oxygen transportation balance damage. The proper energy supply of critical patients is required to meet EE. The excessive estimation EE may cause excessive nutrition, so as to increase VCO2 and prolong the mechanical ventilation time.

   ADVANTAGE - The composition improves taste, smell,                addition of functional effect, therapeutic effects,                or effect increase of the lipophilic compound or                compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition of lipophilic compounds by                extraction using liquid or supercritical carbon                dioxide as extractant is produced by providing an                amount of greater than or equal to 1 1st matrix                comprising a mixture of lipophilic compounds for                composition of lipophilic compounds; mixing the                amount of greater than or equal to 1 1st matrix and                amount of greater than or equal to 1 2nd matrix for                providing the extraction mixture; extracting the                extraction mixture using the extractant, at                selected extraction pressure and extraction                temperature, providing an extract in the                extractant; and separating the extract from                extractant at defined separation pressure and                defined separation temperature for providing the                composition of lipophilic compounds with a                functional amount of greater than or equal to 1                terpene and/or greater than or equal to 1 flavonoid                compound in composition of lipophilic                compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functional amount                  is defined by greater than or equal to 1 terpene                  and/or greater than or equal to 1 flavonoid                  compound in composition of lipophilic compounds,                  where the functional amount is further defined by                  a quantity of greater than or equal to 1 terpene                  and/or greater than or equal to 1 flavonoid                  compound in the composition of lipophilic                  compounds, more preferably by a ratio of greater                  than or equal to 1 terpene and/or greater than or                  equal to 1 flavonoid compound in composition of                  lipophilic compounds. PN KR2020009913-A; KR2102379-B1
TI Mixed absorbent for separating acid gas including carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide and carbonyl sulfide, comprises mixture comprising aminobutanol compound having one alcoholic hydroxyl group and amine group.
AU SHIM J G
   KWAK N S
   UK L D
   HYUN L J
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2020102218
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed absorbent comprises a mixture comprising 3-aminobutan-1-ol compound (I) having one alcoholic hydroxyl group and one amine group, and another mixture comprising 2-(methylamino) ethan-1-ol compound (II) having one alcoholic hydroxyl group and one secondary amine group. A third mixture comprising a heterocyclic compound (III) is also included with an amine in a ring or an amine compound (IV) having two or multiple amines. A fourth mixture comprising a trimethylamine compound (V) and piperazine compound (VI) is also provided with an alcoholic hydroxyl group and tertiary amine or a heterocyclic compound having two tertiary amines in a ring.
   USE - Mixed absorbent for separating acid gas including carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide and carbonyl sulfide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed absorbent has a high binding force and a fast reaction rate with acid gas including carbon dioxide, and has an effect of reducing energy consumption used for removing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mixed absorbent comprises a mixture comprising 3-aminobutan-1-ol compound of general formula (I) having one alcoholic hydroxyl group and one amine group, and another mixture comprising 2-(methylamino) ethan-1-ol compound of general formula (II) having one alcoholic hydroxyl group and one secondary amine group. A third mixture comprising a heterocyclic compound of formula (III) is also included with an amine in a ring or an amine compound of formula (IV) having two or multiple amines. A fourth mixture comprising a trimethylamine compound of formula (V) and piperazine compound of formula (VI) is also provided with an alcoholic hydroxyl group and tertiary amine or a heterocyclic compound having two tertiary amines in a ring. A fifth mixture comprising a compound of formula (R21)2- or (R22)2-S comprising an oxygen or sulfur component in the molecule.
   a=0-3C;
   b=1-4C;
   c=0-2C;
   d=0-1C;
   e=0-3C;
   f=0-3C;
   g=0-6;

   R4=-(CH2)i-H;
   R5=-(CH2)j-CH3, -CH-(CH3)2 or -C-(CH3)3;
   R6, R7, R8 and R9=same or different H, 1-4C alkyl group substituted by 1-4C amine or alkyl group substituted by alcoholic hydroxyl group;
   R10 and R11=-(CH2)k-H, -(CH2)k-NH2, -CH-(CH3)2 or -C-(CH3)3;
   R12, R13 and R14=same or different H, 1-4C alkyl group substituted by amine or 1-4C alkyl group substituted by alcoholic hydroxyl group
   R15, R16, R17 and R18=same or different H, 1-4C alkyl group substituted with 1-4C amine or alkyl group substituted with alcoholic hydroxyl group;

PN CN109888423-A
TI Recycling scrap ternary lithium battery, comprises            disassembling scrapped ternary lithium battery into            lithium battery cell, discharging disassembled lithium            battery cell, crushing and extracting resultant battery            crushed slurry.
AU ZHAO Y
   LUO J
   SHI P
   LIU Y
   YANG X
AE SHANGHAI EASON ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954737D
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling scrap ternary lithium battery,                comprises disassembling the scrapped ternary                lithium battery into a lithium battery cell,                discharging the disassembled lithium battery cell,                crushing, adding battery crushed slurry to an                extraction tank, and subjecting battery crushed                slurry to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                treatment in the extraction tank, and extracting                mixture of the mobile phase and the residual solid,                subjecting extracted mobile phase to reduced                pressure treatment on the one hand, separating the                extracted mobile phase into carbon dioxide gas and                electrolyte, and then separating the separated                electrolyte solution, storing the residual solid                mixture by wind sorting method, and sorting                residual solid mixture into a plastic separator                material and metal solid mixture composed of a                metal material, an electrode material and a small                amount of lithium salt.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling scrap                ternary lithium battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has recovery rate can reach more                than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling scrap ternary lithium battery,                comprises (i) disassembling the scrapped ternary                lithium battery into a lithium battery cell, and                discharging the disassembled lithium battery cell,                (ii) crushing lithium battery cell after the                discharge treatment under the condition inert gas                as the protective gas for protection to forming                battery crushed slurry, (iii) adding battery                crushed slurry to an extraction tank, and                subjecting battery crushed slurry to supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction treatment in the                extraction tank, and extracting a mixture of the                mobile phase and the residual solid, (iv)                subjecting extracted mobile phase to a reduced                pressure treatment on the one hand, separating the                extracted mobile phase into carbon dioxide gas and                electrolyte, and then separating the separated                electrolyte solution, storing the residual solid                mixture by wind sorting method, and sorting                residual solid mixture into a plastic separator                material and metal solid mixture composed of a                metal material, an electrode material and a small                amount of lithium salt, and the sorting plastic                separator material is recycled, (v) adding a                solution of a mixture of sulfuric acid and hydrogen                peroxide to the metal solid mixture and dissolving                the metal solid mixture to form an acidic                solid-liquid mixture, (vi) filtering acidic                solid-liquid mixture and then separating into an                acidic mixed solution and a graphite cathode                powder, and recovering and reusing the graphite                cathode powder, (vii) adjusting the pH of the                acidic mixed solution to 7-7.5 using a sodium                hydroxide solution, so that copper, iron, and                aluminum in the acidic mixed solution are                precipitated in the form of hydroxide, and                filtering obtain a metal mixture obtained by mixing                copper hydroxide, iron hydroxide and aluminum                hydroxide, and a mixed solution containing a                ternary metal component, then recycling and reusing                the metal mixture, (viii) slowly adjusting the pH                of the mixed solution containing the ternary metal                component to 11-14 using a sodium hydroxide                solution until the precipitation is completed, and                filtering to obtain nickel hydroxide to obtain                metal mixture obtained by mixing cobalt hydroxide                and manganese hydroxide, and a solution containing                small amount of a lithium salt, and recovering and                recycling the metal mixture obtained by mixing                nickel hydroxide, cobalt hydroxide and manganese                hydroxide, and (ix) adding sufficient amount of                sodium carbonate solution to the solution                containing a small amount of lithium salt, and                filtering to obtain lithium carbonate and                salt-containing waste liquid, and recovering and                recycling lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  subjecting disassembled lithium battery cell to                  discharge treatment by salt water soaking, and                  cleaning the lithium battery cell after the                  discharge treatment to remove the soaking liquid                  adhered on the surface of the lithium battery                  cell. In the step (iv), the carbon dioxide gas                  formed by the separation of the extracted mobile                  phase is compressed and then circulated to the                  extraction tank. Preferred Components: In the                  step (ii), the inert gas is nitrogen. In the step                  (v), the mass ratio between sulfuric acid and                  hydrogen peroxide is 7:3-9:1. In the step (vii),                  the concentration of the sodium hydroxide                  solution is 1.5-10 wt.%. In the step (viii), the                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is                  3-15 wt.%. In the step (ix), the concentration of                  the sodium carbonate solution is 5-15 wt.%.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN CN109759148-A; CN109759148-B
TI Regenerating aromatization catalyst comprises switching reactor, carrying out nitrogen circulation heating with oil, first stage scorching, second stage scorching, third stage microporous carbon and nitrogen replacement oxygenation.
AU GUO Y
   CUI Q
AE DONGFANG AOLI PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
   DONGFANG AOLI PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201946862R
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating aromatization catalyst comprises (i) switching reactor (ii) carrying out nitrogen circulation heating with oil; (iii) carrying out the first stage scorching; (iv) carrying out the second stage scorching; (v) carrying out the third stage microporous carbon; (vi) carrying out nitrogen replacement oxygenation; and (vii) production operation in the next cycle, the third stage microporous carbon comprises changing the recycle gas completely to nitrogen when no water is formed in the reactor to be regenerated and the amount of carbon dioxide and the temperature of the bed have a downward trend, completely changing the cycle gas to hydrogen when the nitrogen content in the reactor to be regenerated is constant, slowly raising the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated to 350-400 degrees C and maintaining for 1.5-2.5 hours when the pressure and flow rate of nitrogen in the reactor to be regenerated are constant to obtain regenerated reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating aromatization catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates carbon covered on the acid sites, improves the regeneration level of catalyst and eliminates the catalyst of microporous carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating aromatization catalyst comprises (i) switching reactor (ii) carrying out nitrogen circulation heating with oil; (iii) carrying out the first stage scorching; (iv) carrying out the second stage scorching; (v) carrying out the third stage microporous carbon; (vi) carrying out nitrogen replacement oxygenation; and (vii) production operation in the next cycle, where the first stage scorching comprises adding a small amount of air into the circulating gas when it is observed that the combustible gas in the reactor to be regenerated is exhausted, continuously increasing the amount of air when the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated is stable, controlling the temperature of the bed below 450 degrees C and raising the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated to 480-525 degrees C when the carbon dioxide content does not change and no water is formed and the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated, the second stage scorching comprises changing the recycle gas completely to air when the carbon dioxide content in the reactor to be regenerated is constant and the temperature is decreasing, maintaining the system running for 7-9 hours and controlling the bed temperature below 500 degrees C, the third stage microporous carbon comprises changing the recycle gas completely to nitrogen when no water is formed in the reactor to be regenerated and the amount of carbon dioxide and the temperature of the bed have a downward trend, completely changing the cycle gas to hydrogen when the nitrogen content in the reactor to be regenerated is constant, slowly raising the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated to 350-400 degrees C and maintaining for 1.5-2.5 hours when the pressure and flow rate of nitrogen in the reactor to be regenerated are constant to obtain regenerated reactor and the regenerated reactor is depressurized to 0.02-0.03 MpaG and held under pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the switching reactor comprises gradually reducing the feed load of the reactor to be regenerated to 30%, slowly reducing the temperature inside the reactor, cooling at a rate of not greater than 30 degrees C/hour, reducing the reactor feed load to 20% when the temperature inside the reactor drops to 300 degrees C, stopping the reactor feed when the temperature inside the reactor drops below 250 degrees C and the furnace temperature of the furnace is below 300 degrees C, stopping heating the reactor, switching the reactor to be regenerated to another warmed reactor to the operating temperature and isolating the reactor to be regenerated. In the step (ii), the nitrogen circulation heating oil comprises turning on the nitrogen cycle, slowly raising the temperature in the reactor to be regenerated to 300-350 degrees C at a heating rate of 10-20 degrees C/hour when the pressure and flow rate of nitrogen are stable. In the step (vi), the nitrogen replacement oxygenation comprises pressurizing the regenerated reactor to 0.28-0.3 MpaG with nitrogen, stabilizing the pressure, reducing the pressure to 0.02-0.03 MpaG, carrying out repeatedly charging and pressure relief operations until the oxygen content in the regenerated reactor is less than 0.1% and maintaining the regenerated reactor under pressure of 0.1-0.12 MPaG with nitrogen.

PN CN108309877-A; CN108309877-B
TI Skin-protecting oil composition useful e.g. for moisturizing skin, comprises e.g. golden jojoba oil, ginseng root extract comprising terpene substance and ginsenoside, rose oil, ginseng seed oil, Camellia seed oil and myrrh oil.
AU XU M
AE GUANGZHOU ZHONGCAOJI COSMETICS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201859893J
AB    NOVELTY - Skin-protecting oil composition comprises golden jojoba oil, ginseng root extract, rose oil, ginseng seed oil, Camellia seed oil, myrrh oil, and Dendrobium nobile extract. The ginseng root extract is prepared by e.g. (i) cleaning ginseng, placing in drying hot air current drier for drying, and quickly using cold air to reduce temperature, (ii) sending ginseng to hot air baking furnace, heating, and maintaining temperature, (iii) quickly radiating and cooling, and pressing into grains, (iv) steaming in frying cooker, discharging temperature, screening using mesh screen, and selecting powder, (v) extracting oil, and (vi) cooling the crude oil using chilled salt water, insulating, filtering using filter press, and controlling pressure to obtain oil-ginseng root extract, the ginseng seed oil is prepared by taking ginseng seeds, cleaning, drying, crushing, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, reducing pressure, separating, sterilizing and filling, and extracting.
   USE - The composition is useful for moisturizing skin, relieving dry skin, increasing skin elasticity, and brightening skin color.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is light and not greasy; has bright color; and enhances the beauty of skin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Skin-protecting oil composition comprises 10-95 pts. wt. golden jojoba oil, 2-5 pts. wt. ginseng root extract, 2-5 pts. wt. rose oil, 0.5-5 pts. wt. ginseng seed oil, 0.5-5 pts. wt. Camellia seed oil, 0.1-5 pts. wt. myrrh oil, and 1-5 pts. wt. Dendrobium nobile extract, where the ginseng root extract comprises at least 30% terpene substance, 4.5% ginsenoside. The ginseng root extract is prepared by (i) cleaning ginseng, placing in a drying hot air current drier for drying until moisture is controlled at 5-6%, and quickly using cold air to reduce the temperature to below 40 degrees C, (ii) sending ginseng to hot air baking furnace, heating to 180-200 degrees C, and maintaining temperature for 10 minutes, (iii) fastly radiating and cooling to below 40 degrees C, and pressing into grains by a toothed roller crusher, (iv) steaming in a frying cooker, discharging temperature to 108 degrees C until moisture content is 5-6%, screening using mesh screen, and selecting powder of less than 35-50 meshes as product, (v) extracting oil using 200 type spiral oil press to press under 20-25 kpa pressure for 20-30 minutes, and (vi) cooling the crude oil to 10-15 degrees C using chilled salt water at low speed stirring, insulating for 48 hours, filtering using filter press thrice, and controlling the pressure to 25-30 kpa to obtain oil-ginseng root extract, the ginseng seed oil is prepared by taking ginseng seeds, cleaning and removing impurities, drying, crushing, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, reducing pressure, separating, sterilizing and filling, where the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction specifically comprises adding 40-100 pts. wt. organic solvent in 1 pts. wt. ginseng seed or ginseng seed powder, adding the mixture of ginseng seed or ginseng seed powder with organic solvent into a supercritical extraction tank, supplying supercritical fluid carbon dioxide under 350-450 bar pressure, and extracting at room temperature for 2.5-3.5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: Skin-protecting oil composition comprises 65 pts. wt. golden jojoba oil, 2.5 pts. wt. ginseng root extract, 2.5 pts. wt. rose oil, 0.5 pts. wt. ginseng seed oil, 0.5 pts. wt. Camellia seed oil, 0.5 pts. wt. myrrh oil, and 3 pts. wt. Dendrobium nobile extract.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
