PN US2021245092-A1
TI System used for capturing carbon dioxide from flue            gas, consists of compressor adapted to produce            compressed flue gas stream, in which compressed flue            gas stream is processed in parallel by membrane-based            carbon dioxide separation unit and aqueous-based carbon            dioxide capture unit.
AU LIU K
AE UNIV KENTUCKY RES FOUND (KENT-C)
GA 2021932284
AB    NOVELTY - A hybrid post-combustion carbon dioxide                capture system (10) consists of a compressor (12)                adapted to produce a compressed flue gas stream                with various pressure determined by grid demand, a                membrane-based carbon dioxide separation unit (14)                configured to receive a portion (p1) of the                compressed flue gas stream from the compressor and                an aqueous-based carbon dioxide capture unit (16)                configured to receive a portion (p2) of the                compressed flue gas stream from the compressor. The                compressed flue gas stream is processed in parallel                by the membrane-based carbon dioxide separation                unit and the aqueous-based carbon dioxide capture                unit.
   USE - Hybrid post-combustion carbon dioxide capture                system used for capturing carbon dioxide from flue                gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid post-combustion carbon dioxide                capture system avoids plant efficiency loss due to                rapid cycle operation at high variable renewable                energy. The pressure and thermal swing operation                for aqueous capture lowers the solvent regeneration                temperature for same lean solvent and increases the                local liquid/gas volumetric ratio to reduce solvent                degradation and increases absorber volumetric                effectiveness and effect in-situ short-term carbon                dioxide storage in the solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for capturing                of carbon dioxide from flue gas, which involves                compressing the flue gas into a compressed flue gas                stream under various pressure determined by grid                demand, delivering portion (p1) of the flue gas                stream to a membrane-based carbon dioxide                separation unit, delivering portion (p2) of the                compressed flue gas stream to aqueous-based carbon                dioxide capture unit and processing the compressed                flue gas stream in parallel in the membrane-based                carbon dioxide separation unit and the                aqueous-based carbon dioxide capture unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                hybrid post-combustion carbon dioxide capture                system.
   Hybrid post-combustion carbon dioxide capture                system (10)
   Compressor (12)
   Membrane-based carbon dioxide separation unit                (14)
   Aqueous-based carbon dioxide capture unit                (16)
   Reactor (18)
   Inlet (26)
   Outlets (28, 30)
   Absorber (32)
   Stripper (34)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system further                  consists of a flow control valve for directing                  the portion (p1) of the compressed flue gas                  stream to the membrane-based carbon dioxide                  separation unit. The membrane-based carbon                  dioxide separation unit includes a reactor (18)                  and a membrane held in the reactor, which is                  adapted to allow the passage of carbon dioxide                  through the membrane. The reactor includes an                  inlet (26) adapted for receiving the portion (p1)                  of the compressed flue gas stream from the                  compressor, and an outlet (28) adapted to                  discharge a filtered flue gas stream from the                  reactor, on an upstream side of the membrane and                  an outlet (30) on a downstream side of the                  membrane adapted to discharge a recovered carbon                  dioxide stream from the reactor. The                  aqueous-based carbon dioxide capture unit                  operated at sliding-pressure upon the grid demand                  includes an absorber (32) for capture of carbon                  dioxide by a carbon capture solvent in                  countercurrent flow to the portion (p2) of the                  compressed flue gas stream moving though the                  absorber. The aqueous-based carbon dioxide                  capture unit includes a stripper (34) connected                  to the absorber and adapted to receive a carbon                  dioxide-rich carbon capture solvent from the                  absorber and return a carbon dioxide-lean carbon                  capture solvent to the absorber. The portion (p1)                  of the compressed flue gas is 10-30% of the                  compressed flue gas. Th portion (p1) of the                  compressed flue gas is 20% of the compressed flue                  gas. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves recovering carbon dioxide from the                  portion (p1) of the compressed flue gas stream on                  a downstream side of a membrane adapted to                  recover the carbon dioxide and recovering the                  carbon dioxide from the portion (p2) of the                  compressed flue gas stream in an absorber using a                  countercurrent flow of a carbon dioxide-lean                  carbon capture solvent thereby generating a                  carbon dioxide-rich carbon capture solvent and                  releasing and recovering the captured carbon                  dioxide from the carbon dioxide-rich carbon                  capture solvent and regenerating the carbon                  dioxide-lean carbon capture solvent, compressing                  the compressed flue gas to a pressure of 2-4 bar                  determined by grid demand, and providing 10-30%                  compressed flue gas in the portion (p1) and                  providing 20% of the compressed flue gas in the                  portion (p1).

AU SONG H
   JEONG H
   KIM J
   SONG H J
   KIM J H
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 2018A3839M
AB    NOVELTY - An absorbent comprises an amino acid salt, a piperazine derivative, and water.
   USE - Absorbent is used for apparatus for separating carbon dioxide (all claimed) in greenhouse gas, purifying natural gas, and biogas recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent has improved surface tension and carbon dioxide absorption capacity. The absorbent prevents air-hole wetting in the membrane contactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for separating carbon dioxide from a mixed gas comprising carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the absorbent with the mixed gas to obtain a final absorbent in which the carbon dioxide has been absorbed, separating portion or all of the carbon dioxide from the final absorbent to obtain a recycled absorbent, contacting the recycled absorbent with a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to obtain a saturated absorbent in which carbon dioxide is reabsorbed, and separating the carbon dioxide from the saturated absorbent and regenerating the saturated absorbent; and
   (2) apparatus for separating carbon dioxide, which comprises an absorbent treatment tower for contacting carbon dioxide with the absorbent to produce a final absorbent comprising absorbed carbon dioxide, and regenerator receiving the final absorbent from the absorbent treatment tower and separating portion or all of the carbon dioxide from the final absorbent or receiving a saturated absorbent from the absorption tower to separate the carbon dioxide and regenerating the saturated absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The salt of the amino acid is chosen from potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide and lithium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The piperazine derivative comprises a steric hindrance structure. The piperazine derivative is chosen from 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine, (1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine), N-(2-aminoethyl)piperazine, and cis-2,6-dimethylpiperazine. The piperazine derivative is a compound of formula (I). The amino acid is chosen from glycine, alanine, serine, cysteine, taurine, gamma -aminobutyric acid, arginine and histidine, preferably serine. The molar ratio of piperazine derivative (I) with respect to amino acid salt is 1:0.01-1:10.R1,R2 = 1-6C alkyl.Preferred Properties: The absorbent has a surface tension of more than 70 mN/m at 25 degrees C. Preferred Process: The step of contacting the absorbent with the mixed gas involves contacting carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and an inert gas with the absorbent to absorb carbon dioxide to obtain a pretreated absorbent, and separating a portion of the carbon dioxide from the pretreated absorbent to obtain the final absorbent.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The biomass during                  gasification is subjected to hydrogen in presence                  of FE-CR-based catalysts. Hydrogen sulfide and                  carbon dioxide are removed using an acid gas                  removal unit. Carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen are converted into methane using a                  two-pass methanation reactor. The amine system                  removes up to 99.8% of the carbon dioxide.

   XU H
   HUANG W
   QU Z
   YAN N
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202210863J
AB    NOVELTY - Low-carbon reduction lead-smelting coupling carbon enrichment method involves adding reducing agent comprising metal sulfide or metal sulfide and sulfur to pre-reduction section connected with oxygen-enriched smelting section, reacting hydrogen sulfide gas mixture obtained by the reduction and hydrolysis of sulfur oxide generated in pre-reduction section and metal sulfate obtained by the sulfuric acid leaching of the metal oxide obtained in fuming furnace section, reacting, passing solid product to pre-reduction section as the reducing agent of the pre-reduction section, and enriching the residual gas and recovering.
   USE - Low-carbon reduction lead-smelting coupling carbon enrichment method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes zinc sulfide instead of pulverized coal as the reducing agent of lead oxide, which can reduce the direct consumption of carbon-containing fuel in the reduction furnace and effectively reduce the emission of low-concentration carbon dioxide, and uses artificially prepared metal sulfide with better reducing activity than natural sulfide ore, which improves the product purity, and enriches carbon dioxide in cycle, so that to realize low-carbon lead smelting compared with existing method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal sulfide is one or more of artificially synthesized zinc sulfide, iron sulfide and copper sulfide. The sulfur dioxide generated in pre-reduction section is carried out under action of 2-15 vol.% reducing gas and a catalyst. The reducing gas is one or more of coal gas, natural gas, hydrogen, methane and coke. The active component of the catalyst is one or more of cobalt, nickel, molybdenum, zirconium and titanium supported on alumina. The metal oxide is one or more of zinc oxide, iron oxide and copper oxide. The sulfuric acid is sulfuric acid solution with concentration of 2-20 wt.%.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of reducing agent to the lead oxide entering the pre-reduction section is 1:2.5-3.0. The solid product comprises 85-95 wt.% and balance sulfur. The recovered gas comprises 85-100 vol.% carbon dioxide and balance nitrogen.

PN CN113562760-A
TI Controllably preparing cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phases used as photo-reduction carbon dioxide reaction catalyst comprises e.g. preparing cobalt-metal-organic framework nanospheres as precursor by vulcanizing reaction, and carrying out ion exchange reaction.
AU ZHANG H
   HU R
   WANG Q
   REN J
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 2021C79137
AB    NOVELTY - Controllably preparing cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phases comprises firstly preparing cobalt-metal-organic framework (MOF) nanospheres as the precursor by vulcanizing reaction to obtain cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres, and carrying out ion exchange reaction between the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres and cadmium salt in a solvent, when the cadmium salt comprises organic acid cadmium salt, and the solvent comprising polyhydric alcohol, the cubic phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared, and when the cadmium salt comprises inorganic acid cadmium salt, and the solvent comprising deionized water, the hexagonal phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared.
   USE - The method is useful for controllably preparing cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phases, which is used as photo-reduction carbon dioxide reaction catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: controls the type of cadmium salt and solvent in the ion exchange reaction; achieves the synthesis of two cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phases using the same cobalt-MOF nanosphere as the precursor; and improves efficiency of synthesizing cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phase states using MOF as the precursor. The nanomaterial: exhibits their own differences in different photocatalytic reaction processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the cadmium salt comprises cadmium acetate and the solvent comprising ethylene glycol, the cubic phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared. When the cadmium salt comprises cadmium chloride and the solvent comprising deionized water, the hexagonal phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared. The cubic phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterials comprises cadmium sulfide hollow nanospheres composed of nanoparticles. The size of the cadmium sulfide hollow nanospheres is 180-360 nm. The size of the nanoparticles constituting the hollow cadmium sulfide nanospheres is 3-20 nm. The hexagonal phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterials comprises cadmium sulfide solid nanoflowers composed of nanoparticles. The size of the cadmium sulfide solid nanoflowers is 150-1200 nm. The size of the nanoparticles constituting the cadmium sulfide solid nanoflowers is 20-300 nm. In the step (ii), the vulcanizing reagent comprises thioacetamide, cysteine or thiourea. The mass of the vulcanizing reagent is 6 times the mass of the cobalt-MOF nanospheres. In the step (iii), the cadmium acetate comprises cadmium acetate dihydrate. The mass of the cadmium acetate is 4 times the mass of the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres. In the step (iv), the cadmium chloride comprises cadmium chloride hemipentahydrate, and the mass of the cadmium chloride is 4 times the mass of the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres. Preferred Method: The cobalt-MOF nanosphere is prepared by adding equimolar amounts of cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate and isophthalic acid into a clean reaction vessel, pouring in N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring and mixing carrying out the reaction in a constant temperature oil bath at 120degreesC for 2 hours to obtain the mixture A, lowering the mixture A to room temperature, centrifuging to collect precipitation B of mixture A, washing the precipitate B with N,N-dimethylformamide and absolute ethanol for multiple times and performing vacuum drying. The cadmium sulfide nanomaterials with different phases is controllably prepared by (i) preparing cobalt-MOF nanospheres including adding equimolar amounts of cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate and isophthalic acid into a clean reaction container, pouring N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring and mixing, carrying out the reaction in a constant temperature oil bath at 120degreesC for 2 hours to prepare mixture A, cooling the mixture A to room temperature, collecting the precipitate B of the mixture A by centrifuging, washing the precipitate B with N,N-dimethylformamide and absolute ethanol for multiple times and performing vacuum drying, (ii) preparing cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres including ultrasonically dispersing the cobalt-MOF nanospheres and the vulcanizing reagent described in step (i) in anhydrous ethanol, where the mass of the vulcanizing reagent is 2-8 times the mass of the cobalt-MOF nanospheres, stirring and mixing to obtain mixture C, vulcanizing the mixture C at 120degreesC for 80 minutes to obtain mixture D is prepared, cooling the mixture D, collecting the precipitate E by centrifuging, washing the precipitate E with absolute ethanol and performing vacuum drying, (iii) preparing cubic phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterials including ultrasonically dispersing the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres described in step (ii) in ethylene glycol, adding cadmium acetate, stirring and mixing to obtain a mixture F, where the mass of the cadmium acetate is 1.5-6 times the mass of the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres, subjecting the mixture F to an ion exchange reaction at a reaction temperature of 130-170degreesC for 4-10 hours to obtain mixture G, cooling the mixture G, collecting the precipitate H by centrifuging, washing precipitated H with absolute ethanol and performing vacuum drying, and (iv) preparing hexagonal phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterials including ultrasonically dispersing the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres described in the step (ii) in deionized water, adding cadmium chloride to stir and mix to obtain mixture I, where the mass of the cadmium chloride is 1.5-6 times the mass of the cobalt-sulfurx hollow nanospheres, subjecting the mixture I to ion exchange reaction at a reaction temperature of 130-170degreesC for 4-10 hours to obtain a mixture J, cooling the mixture J, collecting the precipitate K by centrifuging, washing precipitate K with absolute ethanol and performing vacuum drying, when the method includes step (i), step (ii) and step (iii), the cubic phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared, and when the method includes step (i), step (ii) and step (iv), the hexagonal phase cadmium sulfide nanomaterial is prepared.    LEI C
   LIU Y
   HE J
   DU Y
   SONG M
AE NORTHWEST INST NON-FERROUS METALS (BEJW-C)
GA 2021C4059C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flexible bulk amine modified                three-dimensional graphene mesoporous material,                involves (1) ultrasonically dispersing the graphene                oxide aqueous solution, and then adjusting the pH                value with the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to                obtain a graphene oxide dispersion, (2) mixing the                high amine aqueous solution with the graphene oxide                dispersion obtained in step (1), stirring and                ultrasonically processing, to obtain a mixture, (3)                putting the mixture obtained in step (2) into a                mold and placing it in an incubator for heat                preservation and leaving still to form a hydrogel,                and (4) removing the hydrogel formed in step (3),                soaking in deionized water and then                freeze-drying.
   USE - Preparation of flexible bulk amine modified                three-dimensional graphene mesoporous material for                carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the regulation of the                internal morphology and pore structure of the                mesoporous material by controlling the temperature                and time of the interaction between the graphene                oxide sheet layer and the high amine molecule, and                improves the amine loading and adsorption                performance of the mesoporous material. The                preparation method combines with freeze-drying                method to maintain a complete and highly resilient                block structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the graphene oxide aqueous solution in step (1)                  is 2-10 mg/mL, the concentration of the sodium                  hydroxide aqueous solution is 4 mg/mL, and the                  adjusted pH is 8.0.Preferred Process: In step (3), the mixture                  is poured into the mold, covered with plastic                  wrap and pierced with small holes in the plastic                  wrap, and then placed in an incubator at                  20-90&#8451; for 12-24 hours. Preferred                  Conditions: The deionized water immersion time in                  step (4) is 1 hour. The freeze-drying temperature                  does not exceed -4&#8451;, the pressure does                  not exceed 20 Pa, and the time is 48 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amines in the high                  amine aqueous solution in step (2) are one or                  more of diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine,                  tetraethylenepentamine, pentaethylenehexaamine                  and polyethyleneimine, preferably                  polyethyleneimine.Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  polyethyleneimine aqueous solution is 10-30                  mg/mL. The mass ratio of polyethyleneimine in the                  polyethyleneimine aqueous solution to graphene                  oxide in the graphene oxide dispersion is                  6:1-1:6.

TI Preparation of high-purity calcium hydroxide            involves stirring microemulsion containing calcium            chloride and Gemini quaternary ammonium salt            surfactant, microemulsion containing sodium hydroxide            and cyclohexane, and carbon dioxide fixing agent,            hydrothermally reacting, washing and vacuum            drying.
AU DING Q
AE HUNAN CHANGLE NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202199493W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-purity calcium hydroxide                involves dissolving calcium chloride in water to                obtain solution (A), dissolving sodium hydroxide in                water to obtain solution (B), mixing Gemini                quaternary ammonium salt surfactant, alcohol                additive and cyclohexane, dividing into two equal                portion, recording them as solution (C) and                solution (D), adding solution (C) to solution (A)                and mixing to obtain microemulsion (A), adding                solution (D) to solution (B) and mixing to obtain                microemulsion (B), mixing microemulsion (A),                microemulsion (B) and carbon dioxide fixing agent,                stirring and reacting for 30-50 minutes, adding to                hydrothermal reactor, hydrothermally reacting at                160-200 degrees C for 10-15 hours, lowering                temperature to room temperature, washing with water                and ethanol and vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C                for 8-15 hours.
   USE - Preparation of high-purity calcium                hydroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method produces product                without secondary purification step and with no                agglomeration of particles, and is suitable for                large-scale industrial production. The calcium                hydroxide has high purity of greater than or equal                to 99%, whiteness of greater than or equal to 99%,                high uniformity, narrow particle size distribution,                regular morphology and excellent                dispersibility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of divalent                  calcium ion in solution (A) to hydroxide ion in                  solution (B) is 1:1-1.4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Gemini quaternary                  ammonium salt is of formula (I). The alcohol                  additive is one of n-propanol, n-butanol,                  n-hexanol, and n-pentanol. The carbon dioxide                  fixing agent comprises                  hydroxyethylethylenediamine, ethanolamine, and                  diethylaminoethanol in mass ratio of                  1:2-4:1.m = 1-14.Preferred Process: The Gemini quaternary                  ammonium salt surfactant is prepared by adding                  tertiary-amine compound of formula (II) and                  concentrated hydrochloric acid to n-propanol,                  stirring until the white smoke disappears,                  dissolving epoxy compound of formula (III) in                  n-propanol, dripping in reaction mixture,                  reacting at 30-35 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours,                  adding compound (II), raising temperature to                  40-45 degrees C, reacting for 5-8 hours,                  concentrating reaction solution under reduced                  pressure to obtain crude product, washing crude                  product with sodium hydroxide solution and water,                  vacuum drying, dissolving in hot acetone,                  concentrating under reduced pressure until white                  crystals are precipitated, leaving still at 0-5                  degrees C for crystallization for 5-10 hours,                  suction filtering, purifying and vacuum drying.                  Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon                  dioxide fixing agent used is 5-10 %mass of the                  total mass of microemulsion (A) and microemulsion                  (B).

PN CN113143955-A
TI Preparing modified polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener used for preparing varicose vein drugs, by adding additive preparation and raw materials of polycinnamyl alcohol, collecting modified polycinnamyl alcohol stock solution, mixing with active ingredient and performing foaming treatment.
AU LIU G
   DAI Q
   CHEN H
   CHU C
   MAO J
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202191484P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener comprises (i) adding an additive preparation and clinical raw materials of polycinnamyl alcohol into a supercritical carbon dioxide reactor in a mass ratio of 1:(5-10), pressurizing to 10-20 MPa, stirring for 1-2 hours once the temperature and pressure are stable, decompressing and collecting modified polycinnamyl alcohol stock solution of the additive preparation from the nozzle, and (ii) mixing the polycinnamyl alcohol stock solution and an active ingredient, performing foaming treatment to obtain a modified polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener with the function of the active ingredient.
   USE - The method is used for preparing modified polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener used for preparing varicose vein drugs or tumor ablation drugs (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener is administered through minimally invasive methods, and combined with sclerotherapy and other therapies to achieve excellent therapeutic effect on varicose veins and tumors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a modified cinnamyl alcohol foam hardener prepared by the method comprising raw materials of clinical polycinnamyl alcohol, additive preparation, active ingredient and clean gases, where the clean gas is clean air or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises (i) dissolving the active ingredient in a 5% ethanol aqueous solution, avoiding light and performing ultrasound treatment until it dissolves, (ii) adding the polycinnamyl alcohol stock solution to obtain a polycinnamyl alcohol-active ingredient solution, where the amount of polycinnamyl alcohol is based on the mass-volume ratio, accounting for 1-2% of the total solution, and (iii) using two 10 mL syringes to draw 2 mL prepared polycinnamyl alcohol-active ingredient solution and 8 mL air or carbon dioxide sterilized by a 0.22 mu m air disinfection filter, connecting through a medical three-way valve, holding one side of the syringe, giving additional pressure, performing rapid suction 5 times, removing the additional pressure and continuing to repeat rapid suction 5-15 times, and obtaining a modified polycinnamyl alcohol foam hardener with the function of active ingredients. Preferred Components: The additive preparation comprises gelatin, agar powder, chitosan, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and/or polylactic acid polyglycolic acid copolymer. The active ingredient comprises a photosensitive agent, a photothermal agent, a nuclear magnetic resonance imaging agent, a chemotherapeutic drug, and/or a nuclide. The photosensitive agent comprises indocyanine green, sodium porphyrin, 5-aminolevulinic acid, and/or temoporfin. The nuclear magnetic resonance imaging agent comprises gadopentetate meglumine, gadodiamide, gadoterol, gadolinium meglumine and/or gadobutrol. The chemotherapeutic drug comprises doxorubicin hydrochloride, epirubicin, nimustine, erlotinib, loplatin, oxaliplatin, fluorouracil, irinotecan, and/or gemcitabine. The nuclide comprises lutetium 177, copper 64, zirconium 89, and/or fluorine 18.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic; Cytostatic.

TI Preparing magnesium single atom catalyst useful for high-efficiency carbon dioxide reduction reaction to generate carbon monoxide comprises e.g. grinding dicyandiamide, potassium chloride and sodium chloride, placing in crucible.
AU LIU M

   JIANG L
   LI H
   FU J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202184110Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium single atom catalyst comprises (i) mixing and grinding dicyanodiamine, potassium chloride and sodium chloride according to molar ratio of 1:2:2, placing in crucible of the muffle furnace, heating to 670 degrees C and calcining under the air atmosphere, and maintaining for 45 minutes, (2) grinding solid product after calcining at the same time adding mixed solution of water and ethanol, washing, and drying to obtain pure carbon nitrogen nano sheet, (3) adding 20 mg carbon nitride into water for dispersing, then adding micro magnesium salt and 8 mg carbon nano tube, stirring, mixing, freezing and drying, and (4) transferring sample after freezing and drying into the quartz boat, heating to 600 degrees C and carbonizing under the argon atmosphere, and maintaining for 45 minutes to obtain magnesium single atom catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnesium single atom catalyst, which is useful for high-efficiency carbon dioxide reduction reaction to generate carbon monoxide with a Faraday conversion efficiency of 90-100% (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is uniformly dispersed on the electrode material, exhibits good catalytic activity for carbon monoxide production and maximum atom utilization efficiency.

PN CN112275252-A
TI Chabazite zeolite made from high-kaolin clay for ion exchanger production, is prepared by roasting kaolin ore powder, adding seed crystals and potassium hydroxide solution, hydrothermally reacting, and adding sodium chloride solution.
AU LONG Y
   LI H
   PU P
   CAO Y
AE FUYU ZHANGJIAGANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (FUYU-Non-standard)
GA 202113456L
AB    NOVELTY - Chabazite zeolite made from high-kaolin clay is prepared by taking kaolin ore powder as raw material for high-temperature roasting to obtain amorphous ore powder, adding an appropriate amount of seed crystals and potassium hydroxide solution to the amorphous ore powder for uniform mixing to obtain a reaction mixture, performing hydrothermal reaction on the reaction mixture to obtain chabazite zeolite as product, and adding to sodium chloride solution for cation exchange to obtain a cationic zeolite.
   USE - The chabazite zeolite is useful for carbon dioxide and methane enrichment and for adsorption separation and removal of toxic and harmful gases in exhaust gas (claimed), preferably for the production of ion exchangers and small molecules, such as carbon dioxide and methane enrichment, and for adsorption separation and removal of carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen cyanide, cobalt sulfide, ammonia, nitrogen oxide (NOx) and other toxic and harmful gases in exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The chabazite zeolite shows the ion exchange and adsorption properties of the small pore zeolite. The method has low raw material price, simple synthesis process, no environmental pollution and easy industrialization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding sodium chloride solution to the product for cation exchange at 950 degrees C for 2 hours for 3 times, washing 3 times with deionized water, filtering and subjecting filter cake to the next ion exchange, where the ratio of potassium-chabazite zeolite and sodium chloride solution is 1:10. Preferred Conditions: The roasting is carried out at 650-800 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 130-150 degrees C for 24-48 hours. The cationic zeolite is used for carbon dioxide and methane enrichment, and adsorption separation and removal of toxic and harmful gases in exhaust gas. Preferred Composition: The selected concentration of the potassium hydroxide solution in the synthesis of chabazite zeolite is 1 mol/l. The ratio of the amorphous powder and the potassium hydroxide solution is 1:9. The weight ratio of the seed crystal and the amorphous powder is (1-10):(50-500). Preferred Properties: The fineness of the kaolin ore powder is 320 mesh.

PT P
PN CN111909078-A; CN111909078-B
TI Preparing (2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl)methanol, comprises e.g. reacting diisopropylamine with tetrahydrofuran and 2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridine, heating, then adding borane dimethylsulfide and reacting.
AU XU Y
   DAI H
AE ALI BIOLOGICAL NEW MATERIALS CHANGZHOU (ALIB-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3850E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl)methanol (I), comprises (i) adding diisopropylamine and tetrahydrofuran into a reactor, stirring and protecting with nitrogen, cooling to -80 to -75 degrees C, adding hexane solution of n-butyllithium, stirring, adding 2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridine (II), continuously stirring, passing carbon dioxide, heating, and reacting to obtain 2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)nicotinic acid (III), and (ii) mixing (III) with tetrahydrofuran evenly, protecting with nitrogen, cooling, adding borane dimethylsulfide dropwise, increasing the temperature, and reacting to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing (2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl)methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation, and short route; and is easy to control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl)methanol of formula (I), comprises (i) adding diisopropylamine and tetrahydrofuran into a reactor, stirring and protecting with nitrogen, cooling to -80 to -75 degrees C, adding hexane solution of n-butyllithium, stirring, adding 2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)pyridine of formula (II), continuously stirring, passing carbon dioxide, heating, and reacting to obtain 2-fluoro-6-(trifluoromethyl)nicotinic acid of formula (III), and (ii) mixing (III) and tetrahydrofuran evenly, protecting with nitrogen, cooling, dropwise adding borane dimethylsulfide, increasing the temperature, and reacting to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio between (II) and diisopropylamine is 5:3-5. The solid-liquid ratio between (II) and tetrahydrofuran is 1:14 (g/ml), and the volume ratio between tetrahydrofuran and hexane solution of n-butyllithium is 5:1. In step (ii), the solid-to-liquid ratio between (III) and tetrahydrofuran is 1:20 (g/ml), and the volume ratio between borane dimethylsulfide and tetrahydrofuran is 1:13-15.

PT P
PN CN111820394-A
TI Preparing sustained-release Torreya grandis shell flavor for preparing microcapsule flavor powder, by drying washing Torreya grandis, crushing, extracting in supercritical carbon dioxide kettle, separating solute and centrifuging.
AU CHI X
   CHEN L
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV LUDONG (UNLD-C)
GA 2020A7476E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sustained-release Torreya grandis shell flavor by (a) washing T.grandis with water to remove impurities, drying through microwave drying tunnel, controlling the temperature to 70-80 degrees C and passing time for 1-2 hours to obtain dried T.grandis, (b) crushing dried T.grandis shell into powder to particle size of 100-200 mesh with high-speed crusher, (c) placing the T.grandis powder into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle for extraction, where the extraction pressure is 10-20 MPa, the temperature is 40-45 degrees C, the time is 1-3 hours, and the solvent flow rate is 10-15 ml/minute, separating solute to obtain the essential oil, and (d) mixing 400 pts. wt. essential oil, 10-20 pts. wt. porous microsphere powder and 10-20 pts. wt. fixative, stirring at 35-45 degrees C for 1-2 hours to make the porous microsphere powder fully absorb the essential oil, centrifuging to obtain product, and reusing the flavor and essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microcapsule flavor powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The microcapsule flavor powder is coated with various natural or synthetic polymer compounds to effectively improve the effect of fragrance retention.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The starch used is cross-linked with sodium trimetaphosphate, where cross-linked starch is copolymerized and modified with sodium methacrylic acid sulfonate, dimyrcenol allylamine, and 2,4-nonadienal to obtain a modified starch copolymer. The magnetic powder comprises ferrosoferric oxide and maghemite. In step (d), the flavor fixer comprises ethyl maltol, thiothiazole, 2,4-nonadienal, triethyl citrate, oleic acid, vanillin and/or furanone. Preferred Method: In step (d), the porous microspheres is prepared by (i) introducing nitrogen into the high-pressure reactor, adding and mixing 400-500 pts. wt. dried starch, 4-12 pts. wt. acetic acid, and 2000-2500 pts. wt. deionized water, controlling the temperature at 40-50 degrees C, continuously reacting for 4-8 hours, using 5-10% sodium hydroxide solution to neutralize the reaction solution to pH to 7, gelatinizing at 80-90 degrees C for 60-150 minutes, adding 11-16 pts. wt. sodium trimetaphosphate, 1-5 pts. wt. magnetic powder with particle size of 10-50 nm and 3-6 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate, uniformly stirring and mixing, reacting at 50-60 degrees C for 60-150 minutes, and (ii) adding 80-120 pts. wt. 10-16% sodium methacrylic acid sodium methacrylate aqueous solution, 0.5-2.4 pts. wt. myrcenol, and 0.5-2.4 pts. wt. 2,4-nonadienal to the high pressure reaction kettle under the protection of nitrogen, adding 2-6 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate, stirring at 150-200 rpm for 30-100 minutes, ultrasonically treating for 60-120 minutes, heating at 58-65 degrees C under pressure of 1.2-1.5 MPa for 3-6 hours, discharging the material, and spray drying.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN111747387-A; CN111747387-B
TI Yellow phosphate rock powder forming binder prepared by mixing sulfuric acid solution and aluminum hydroxide.
AU YU S
   LIU F
AE TRIVINE CHEM CO LTD (TRIV-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1232X
AB    NOVELTY - Yellow phosphate rock powder forming binder, prepared by mixing 50% sulfuric acid solution and 1 wt.% aluminum hydroxide is claimed.
   USE - Used as yellow phosphate rock powder forming binder.
   ADVANTAGE - The powder: is economical; can prepare high compressive strength mineral powder pellets, which can reach more than 100 kg at room temperature, having good thermal stability, does not burst after 1000 degrees C high temperature; and meets the requirements of yellow phosphorus production. The method can take away carbon dioxide in the phosphate rock powder; reduces the power consumption of yellow phosphorus production; and saves costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing mineral powder pellet comprising (i) providing the phosphate rock powder with a water content of 1-15% and adding water to make the water content of the phosphate rock powder 15%, adding 4-6 pts. wt. binder, mixing uniformly ingredients by a double-roll mixer, during the mixing process, reacting the binder with the phosphate rock powder, and removing carbon dioxide and organic matter in the carbonate after the reaction, (ii) transporting the evenly mixed powder to the ball press through a belt for ball pressing, and (iii) drying and solidifying the obtained sphere at 300 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), transporting the uniformly mixed powder to the ball press through the belt to press the ball under the pressure of 20-50 tons. Preferred Components: The diameter of the mineral powder pellets is 35-38 mm.

PN CN111705264-A; CN111705264-B
TI Preparation of rare earth steel by weighing raw            materials, melting chromium, tungsten, tantalum and            iron, deoxidizing, adding molten silicon and manganese,            adding yttrium, dissolving, homogenizing, and pouring            carbon dioxide and argon gas.
AU QIU G
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
   UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 202096813S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of rare earth steel comprises                weighing raw materials comprising 0.01-0.05 wt.%                carbon, 0.15-0.3 wt.% silicon, 0.4-0.5 wt.%                manganese, 8-10 wt.% chromium, 2-2.5 wt.% tungsten,                0.2-0.25 wt.% vanadium, 0.05-0.1 wt.% tantalum,                0.001-0.01 wt.% zirconium, 0.3-0.3 wt.% yttrium                oxide, and balance iron; melting chromium,                tungsten, tantalum and iron, deoxidizing to                1650-1700 degrees C until oxygen mass fraction is                below 10 ppm, adjusting temperature to 1550-1600                degrees C, adding molten silicon and manganese,                adding yttrium, dissolving, and homogenizing; and                pouring mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon to                oxidize yttrium, adding molten zirconium, vanadium                and carbon, casting in protective atmosphere to                obtain ingot, purifying by electroslag remelting,                austenitizing, pressure processing into steel plate                at 850-1000 degrees C, water cooling, and heating                to precipitate yttrium-rich nano-precipitated                phase.
   USE - The method is for preparation of rare earth                steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can increase the output and improve                high temperature mechanical properties of                reduced-activation ferritic-martensitic                steel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for rare                earth steel.

PN CN109225229-A
TI Nickel silicon dioxide core-shell catalyst useful for methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction, comprises silica-based spherical structure catalyst with ultrafine nano-particle nickel as core.
AU WANG F
   ZHANG L
   HAN B
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2019098979
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel silicon dioxide core-shell catalyst comprises silica-based spherical structure catalyst with ultrafine nano-particle nickel as core. The nickel particles have mass percentage of 3-6 wt.% and particle size of 1-4 nm. The silica has mass percentage of 97-94 wt.% and particle size of 25-35 nm.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is low cost. The method is simple and easy in industrial production with good development prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing nickel silicon dioxide core-shell catalyst, comprising (i) mixing the nickel source solution, the surfactant and cyclohexane to obtain mixed solution A; (ii) placing the mixed solution in a water bath, and adding hydrazine hydrate for first stirring, adding silicon source, and performing second stirring to obtain mixed solution B; (iii) dropping the mixed solution B into the L-lysine solution, hydrolyzing in a water bath to control the stirring rate, drying the hydrolyzed product in an oven and calcining in a muffle furnace under air to obtain gray powder; and (iv) subjecting the gray powder into hydrogen reduction to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the nickel source is a nickel nitrate solution, and the concentration is 0.5-1 mol/l, the volume ratio of cyclohexane to nickel nitrate solution is 15:0.5, the surfactant is polyoxyethylene cetyl ether, n = 10, 15 or 20, and the concentration of the surfactant in the mixed solution a is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. In the step (ii), the mass percentage of hydrazine hydrate is 80%, and the molar ratio of the added amount to the amount of nickel nitrate added in the nickel source is 20:1, and the silicon source is tetraethyl orthosilicate, and the molar ratio of the added amount to the amount of hydrazine hydrate added is 94:100. In the step (iii), the pH of the lysine solution is 9-10. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of the added amount to the amount of the silicon source added is 0.02:1, when the mixed solution b is added into the L-lysine solution, the control drip acceleration rate is 0.1-0.5 ml/minute, and the water bath temperature is 60 degrees C, the mixing rate is 500-1000 revolutions/minute, maintaining constant speed and the mixing time is 3-4 hours. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the temperature control of the water bath is constant at 45-55 degrees C, the first stirring time is 20-30 minutes, the second stirring time is 40-60 minutes, and the dropping process is kept at constant speed. In the step (iii), the mixture is taken out and dried in an oven at 90-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and the muffle furnace is calcined at 500-700 degrees C for 2 hours, and the heating rate is 1-2 degrees C per minute. In the step (iv), the gray powder is reduced by hydrogen at 750 degrees C, the reduction time is 30 minutes, and the heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute.

PT P
PN WO2019011659-A1; AU2018299756-A1; CA3069493-A1; IN201917052606-A; CN110831892-A; KR2020028905-A; EP3652107-A1; US2020180952-A1; VN70286-A; BR112020000497-A2; US11286159-B2
TI Producing ammonia syngas involves steam reforming            hydrocarbon-containing feedstock and treating formed            syngas by shifting carbon monoxide using zinc oxide and            zinc aluminum spinel based catalysts, and subsequently            removing carbon dioxide.
AU KROLL JENSEN A E
   SPETH C H
   ROSTRUP-NIELSEN T
   KROLL J A E
   KROLLJENSEN A E
   ROSTRUPNIELSEN T
   JENSEN A E K
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2019070402
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia synthesis gas (syngas) from                a hydrocarbon-containing feedstock, comprises:                steam reforming the feedstock, thus obtaining a                syngas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide (CO)                and carbon dioxide (CO2); and treating the obtained                syngas including shift of CO and subsequent removal                of CO2, where the shift of the syngas comprises two                shift steps, and in both shift steps, stable                catalysts based on zinc oxide and zinc aluminum                spinel are used, and the formation of hazardous                by-products is avoided or at least reduced to an                acceptable low level.
   USE - The process is useful for producing ammonia                syngas from a hydrocarbon-containing                feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes stable catalysts in both                shift steps, and thus avoids the formation of                hazardous by-products or at least reduces the                formation of hazardous by-products to an acceptable                low level (claimed), preferably utilizes iron-free,                hexavalent chromium-free, HTS catalyst which has                the unique ability to operate at any steam/carbon                ratio, to obtain optimal plant efficiency in                ammonia production, prevents the formation of                unwanted iron carbides that reduce the catalyst                strength and eliminates a serious risk to plant                personnel safety and also to the environment, as                compared to conventional iron-based HTS                catalysts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The two shift steps: both                  are high temperature shift (HTS) steps; are HTS                  and low temperature shift (LTS); or are HTS and                  medium temperature shift (MTS). In process,                  hazardous by-products are removed in a downstream                  process, preferably by a water wash, where the                  water is of ambient temperature or chilled as                  required. Preferred Components: The catalyst used                  in the MTS step and in the LTS step is based on                  copper. The carrier for the copper-based catalyst                  is zinc oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The two shift reactors                  are: combined to a single reactor provided with                  suitable inter-bed cooling; and combined to a                  single isothermal reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Two-stage condensation industrial residual heat recovery system, has working substance generator connected to evaporator, and absorption type heat pump and carbon dioxide heat pump connected to heat user for heating.
AU WANG Y
AE DAQING YUANSHI ELECTROMECHANICAL TECHNOL (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 2019092301
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a working substance generator connected to an evaporator. The working substance generator is connected to an absorption type heat pump to form a generation main circulation. The absorption type heat pump high-temperature-hot-outlet is connected electrically to driven a heat pump. The absorption heat pump low- temperature heat source outlet is connected to a working substance pump. The absorption type heat pump and a carbon dioxide heat pump are connected to a heat user for heating. A waste heat recycling device is utilized as a heat recovery condenser.
   USE - Two-stage condensation industrial residual heat recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is couple with an absorption heat pump and an electric drive heat pump by utilizing low-temperature waste heat cycle power generation, and drives the absorption heat pump by industrial waste heat to cool the working medium so as to utilize industrial waste heat as a power source for power generation and the working medium after power generation enters an absorption heat pump, thus obtaining high temperature heat for heating and an electricity utilize for production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a two-stage condensation industrial residual heat recovery system.

AB    NOVELTY - Chrome-ferritic stainless steel welding and post-weld annealing process in cold continuous rolling line comprises welding chrome-ferritic stainless steel in cold continuous rolling production line by carbon dioxide laser welding using nickel-based welding wire as filling metal to welded strip thickness of 0.6-6 mm with maximum parallelism tolerance of welded steel plate of less than or equal to 0.1 mm and wrong margin of less than or equal to 0.05 mm at laser power of 8-12 kW, welding speed of 12.0-3.4 m/minute, weld gap of 0.10-0.33 mm, and wire feeding speed of 1.13-10.58 m/minute, and annealing at 22.1-40.0 kW; and post weld annealing by air cooling tail of previous roll of steel strip and head of next roll of steel for 2 seconds, compressing by compressed air or water mist, post-weld annealing and cooling.
   USE - Method for chrome-ferritic stainless steel welding and post-weld annealing process in cold continuous rolling line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes rapid annealing, reduces high temperature embrittlement phenomenon, ensures plastic toughness, and avoids occurrence of breakage accident.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves integrating wastewater                treatment in a reactor. An anode electrode (4) is                arranged in the anode anaerobic microbial reaction                chamber, and is filled with a mixture of granular                activated carbon and anaerobic electrogenic                bacteria (10). The cathode advanced oxidation                reaction chamber is provided with multiple cathode                plates. The wastewater to be treated is entered                into the anode from the bottom of the reactor. A                solid catalyst packing zone (6) repolarized by the                multi-stage electric field is sequentially passed.                The opening and breaking of macromolecular                pollutants in water is accelerated and finally                decomposed into water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                other small molecules to remove refractory organic                pollutants in wastewater.
   USE - Integrated method for advanced oxidation                treatment of wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The multi-stage cathode plate microporous                aeration or hollow fiber membrane gas supply can be                used to improve oxidant utilization and energy                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                integrated apparatus for advanced oxidation                treatment wastewater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view                of an integrated device for advanced oxidation                treatment wastewater.
   Inlet (1)
   Outlet (2)
   Anode electrode (4)
   Solid catalyst packing zone (6)
   Granular activated carbon and anaerobic                electrogenic bacteria mixture (10)

The cathode                  plate is made of a common carbon material, a                  modified carbon material or a composite catalyst                  electrode material of metal and carbon.

PT P
PN CN108624554-A; CN108624554-B
TI Non-genetically regulating proliferation of mesenchymal stem cells using modified light manipulation by culturing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, passaging, irradiating passage cells and subjecting to proliferation culture.
AU LI X
   YANG K
   LIU Q
   LIU J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 201880202J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for non-genetically regulating proliferation of mesenchymal stem cells using modified light manipulation involves (a) inoculating the isolated human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells into the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell proliferation medium and culturing at 36-38 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 3-4 days until the cell density reaches 80-90% to obtain primary cultured cells, (b) washing the primary cultured cells to remove residual medium, performing cell digestion using trypsin solution, suspending the cells, then transferring to the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell proliferation medium and culturing at 36-38 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 3-4 days until the cell density reaches 80-90% to obtain passage cells, (c) irradiating the passage cells for 85-95 minutes under light wavelength of 400-480 nm and light intensity of 95-105 mu w/cm2 to obtain irradiated cells and (d) subjecting the irradiated cells to proliferation culture.
   USE - The method is useful for non-genetically regulating proliferation of mesenchymal stem cells using modified light manipulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not involve transfer of foreign genes into stem cells or require viral transfection vectors and additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In steps (a), (b) and (d), the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell proliferation medium comprises 50 ml alpha -minimum essential medium (MEM), 20 mu g 20 mg/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), 5 mu l 20 mg/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF), 5 mu l 20 mg/ml vascular EGF (VEGF), 5 mu l 20 mg/ml platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF)-BB, 50 mu l 100000 U/ml penicillin and 50 mu l 100000 U/ml streptomycin. The step (b) involves blowing the cells by pipetting, adding to the equal volume of cell proliferation medium, mixing, centrifuging at 700-1000 revolutions per minute (rpm), preferably 800 rpm for 3-8 minutes, preferably 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 1-3 ml, preferably 1 ml umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell proliferation medium, resuspending the cells and mixing by pipetting. The step (b) involves collecting the original medium in the culture dish using vacuum pump and rinsing the residual medium 2-5 times with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS). The step (c) involves irradiating the passage cells obtained in the step (b) for 90 minutes under light wavelength of 445 nm and light intensity of 100 mu w/cm2 to obtain irradiated cells. The step (d) involves subjecting the irradiated cells to proliferation culture at 36-38 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 3-4 days.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108128791-A; CN108128791-B
TI Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate used in e.g. ink, involves mixing treated calcium carbide slag and citrate solution, centrifuging, separating supernatant when calcium ion is at preset range, purging carbon dioxide and processing.
AU WANG Y
   YE B
   CEN B
   LIN S
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201846452U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate involves placing calcium carbide slag in a mortar, grinding, screening using screen having size of 80-100 mesh, mixing citrate with water, obtaining a citrate solution having concentration of 0.02-0.1 mmol/L, mixing pretreated calcium carbide slag with the citrate solution as a leaching agent, stirring, dissolving, centrifuging, separating supernatant, detecting calcium ion content, removing the supernatant when the content of calcium ion is 0.06-0.1 mol/L, purging carbon dioxide into the supernatant, adjusting pH to 10.5-11.5, stirring for 10 minutes, centrifuging, washing the precipitate with pure water and ethanol, drying, obtaining calcined phase nano-calcium carbonate, adding citrate to the clear solution obtained by centrifuging, adding pretreated calcium carbide slag, stirring, dissolving, centrifuging, removing supernatant when content of calcium ion is 0.06-0.1 mol/L and separating calcite phase nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate used in plastic, coating, papermaking, printing ink and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical industrial preparation of nano-calcium carbonate with reduced energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The calcite phase nano-calcium carbonate has shape chosen from dumbbell, rod, spherical and needle. The particles size of calcite phase nano-calcium carbonate is 20-200 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The citrate is chosen from sodium citrate, potassium citrate, calcium citrate and magnesium citrate.

GA 2018315250

   USE - Method for extracting tea polyphenols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract tea polyphenols that has purity, high extraction yield, and biological activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tea polyphenols involves selecting tea leaves to clean, cleaning and draining water, then slicing machine for cutting, carrying out vacuum freezing and drying Camellia chrysantha leaf sheet, using screw extrusion swelling, airflow swelling and microwave puffing technology. The Camellia nitidissima is swelled, then tea leaves are crushed in the bulking to 25-40 meshes. The supercritical carbon dioxide is used as solvent for extracting the tea polyphenol solution, decompressed, distilled and tea polyphenol solution is concentrated, and then spray dried to obtain tea polyphenol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The Camellia nitidissima tea leaves is dried by vacuum freezing-drying method until the water is controlled below 20 wt.%. The leaves of the Camellia sinensis are cut into lwidth of 0.5-1.0 cm. The swollen Camellia nitidissima are crushed to 30 meshes. The Camellia nitidissima tea leaves are soaked for 2-4 hours, then injected in the extracting kettle with high pressure pump, a continuous cycle for extraction in the extraction loop kettle-separator-extracting, and subjected to ultrasound to obtain tea polyphenol solution. The supercritical extraction entrainer is 60-70% edible alcohol. Preferred Condition: The screw extrusion bulking treatment parameters are rotation speed of the screw is 190-210 revolution per minute. The extrusion temperature is 120-145 degrees C. The feeding speed is 18-23 revolution per minute of airflow. The swelling process parameters are operation temperature is 65-78 degrees C. The puffing pressure difference is 220-380 kilopascal. The swelling time is 60-78 minutes. The microwave puffing power is 1000-2000 watts and processing time is 6-8 minutes. The supercritical extraction parameters are supercritical operation pressure is 25-40 megapascal. The temperature is 35-55 degrees C. The extraction time is 3 hours. The ultrasonic power density is 150-250 W/L. The ultrasonic frequency is 20-25 kilohertz. The ultrasonic radiation mode irradiation time 6-8 seconds. The intermittent time is 6-8 seconds.

PN CN115072749-A
TI Extracting lithium from spodumene without slag,            involves mixing crushed and grinded alpha-spodumen            concentrate with an alkaline substance to obtain mixed            material, roasting to obtain roasted product, and            uniformly mixing roasted product with water to obtain            the mixed slurry.
AU WANG M
   ZHUANG L
   TIAN B
   HU H
   LI Y
   LIU M
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
GA 2022C3577F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from spodumene without                slag, involves (i) mixing the crushed and grinded                alpha-spodumen concentrate with an alkaline                substance to obtain a mixed material, (ii) roasting                to obtain roasted product, (iii) uniformly mixing                the roasted product with water to obtain the mixed                slurry, heating and reacting, obtaining a water                immersion liquid and nepheline after filtering,                (iv) adding calcium oxide to the water immersion                solution, (v) aging the slurry after aging,                carrying out hydrothermal reaction, and filtering                to obtain lithium-rich filtrate, and (vi)                introducing carbon dioxide gas into the lithium                filtrates, and (vii) filtering, washing and drying                to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for extracting lithium from spodumene                without slag used in lithium battery and lithium                battery cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively utilizes silicon                aluminum component in spodumene to produce high                value-added product nano kaolinite and hydrated                calcium silicate at the same time of preparing                lithium carbonate, realizes whole process without                slag process, which is a set of green and                eco-friendly and high economic benefit, and                effectively solves the problem that the large                amount of smelting slag in the lithium extracting                technique of our country is difficult to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The                  &#945;-spodumene concentrate and the alkaline                  substance are mixed in mass ratio of 1: 0.3-0.8                  at 700-850&#8451; and roasted for 1-4 hours.                  The &#945; spodumene is spodumene                  concentrate, and the lithium oxide content is                  &#8805;3 wt.%. The &#945; spodumene                  concentrate and alkaline substance undergoes                  roasting, carried out co-grinding and mixed                  uniformly with particle size of (-)200 mesh                  &#8805;90%. The alkaline substance is sodium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide                  and potassium hydroxide. The calcined product and                  water are mixed in a mass ratio of 1: 3-12, and                  reacted at 80-120&#8451; for 0.5-2 hours. The                  calcium-silicon ratio is 1: 1-1.2, calcium oxide                  is added to the water immersion solution, and the                  slurry is aged for 0.5-1 hours, then reacted at                  80-90&#8451; for 1-2 hours.

PN WO2022171074-A1; CN114906831-A
TI Preparing lithium iron phosphate material useful            in positive pole piece of lithium ion battery,            comprises sintering iron source, lithium source,            phosphorus source and reducing carbon source under mild            oxidizing gas atmosphere.
AU YUE H
   GUO H
   YANG C
   XI X
   HUANG Y
   HE X
AE BTR TIANJIN NANO MATERIAL MFR CO LTD (BTRT-Non-standard)
   BTR TIANJIN NANO MATERIAL MFG CO LTD (BTRT-Non-standard)
GA 2022A6610D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium iron phosphate material,                comprises sintering dry materials of iron source,                lithium source, phosphorus source and reducing                carbon source at 800-900&#176; C under mild                oxidizing gas atmosphere including carbon dioxide                to obtain the lithium iron phosphate                material.
   USE - The lithium iron phosphate material is useful                in positive pole piece of lithium ion battery                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can ensure purity, crystallinity                and electrochemical performance of lithium iron                phosphate, can easily changes mild oxidizing gas in                the sintering atmosphere, adjusts the redox                situation of the system, uses carbon source which                have more selectivity and flexibility, inhibits                generation of magnetic material, compensate reduced                reduction tendency by raising the temperature, and                can ensure the purity, crystallinity, and                electrochemical properties of lithium iron                phosphate. The lithium iron phosphate material has                better crystallinity and improved compaction                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   lithium iron phosphate material produced by                the above method; and
   lithium ion battery comprising a positive pole                piece, a negative pole piece and a separator                arranged between the positive pole piece and the                negative pole piece, where the positive pole piece                includes the lithium iron phosphate material                produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the total                  gas flow rate in the sintering atmosphere is 3-10                  l/minutes, and the holding time of the sintering                  is 2-20 hours. The preparation of the dry                  material comprises: mixing and grinding                  phosphorus source, iron source, lithium source,                  reducing carbon source and solvent to obtain a                  slurry, and drying the slurry to obtain the dried                  material. The mixed grinding method includes ball                  milling and/or sand milling, where the median                  particle size (D50) of the solid particles in the                  slurry is 0.1-3 &#956; m, the solvent                  includes water and/or ethylene glycol, and the                  solid content in the slurry is the total mass of                  the slurry 5-80%. The drying method includes                  spray drying and/or evaporative drying, where the                  drying temperature is 50-500&#176; C; and the                  drying time is 0.1-10 hours. Preferred                  Components: The iron source includes iron                  phosphate, metallic iron, iron nitrate, at least                  one of iron oxide, ferric chloride and/or ferrous                  oxalate. The phosphorus source includes iron                  phosphate, ammonium monohydrogen phosphate,                  ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate                  and/or ferrous monohydrogen phosphate. The                  lithium source includes lithium carbonate,                  lithium phosphate, lithium hydroxide and/or                  lithium chloride. The mass ratio of the iron                  source, the lithium source, the phosphorus source                  and the carbon source is                  (95-105):(90-110):(95-105):(0.1-10). The lithium                  iron phosphate material has olivine structure,                  powder compaction density of 2.1-3.3 g/cm3,                  average particle size of 0.1-5 &#956; m, and                  specific surface area of 1-50 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mild oxidizing gas                  further comprises protective gas containing                  nitrogen, argon, helium and/or neon, where the                  volume ratio of the carbon dioxide to the                  protective gas is (1:99)-(99:1). The reducing                  carbon source includes sucrose, glucose and/or                  polyethylene glycol.
   EXAMPLE - 4000 g Iron phosphate, 1000 g lithium                carbonate, 150 g sucrose, 250 g polyethylene glycol                and 15.5 l deionized water were mixed to obtain                slurry, 0.525 l slurry was subjected for ball                milling, mixed and finely grind to obtain slurry                having D50 particles size of 0.2 &#956; m and                solid content of 40%. After that, the slurry was                spray dried at 240&#176; C, outlet temperature                of 80&#176; C, gas flow rate of 14 m3/hour, and                peristaltic pump flow rate of 5 l/hour to obtain                spray material. Then, the spray material was sinter                in a rotary resistance furnace at 800&#176; C                for 10 hours, and the total gas flow rate in the                sintering atmosphere was adjusted to 5 l/minutes to                obtain a lithium iron phosphate material.

PN CN216644285-U
TI Red mud furnace for use in table, has bracket set            in furnace body, electric heater placed on bracket, and            lead set in through hole, where end of lead is            connected with electric heater and another end is            provided with plug.
AU DENG Z
AE HE R (HERR-Individual)
GA 202283089C
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a red mud furnace and                a table surface, relating to the field of cooking                appliance. the red mud furnace comprises a furnace                body, a bracket, an electric heater and a lead; the                side wall of the furnace body is provided with a                through hole; the bracket is set in the furnace                body; the electric heater is placed on the bracket;                the lead is set in the through hole, one end of the                lead is connected with the electric heater, the                other end is provided with a plug. The electric                heater is set up in the furnace body through the                bracket, replacing carbon fire as the heating When                using, power is supplied to the electric heater                through the plug and the wire, the electric heater                converts the electric energy into internal energy,                it is used for boiling hot pot, rice cooker or soup                and so on. in the process, it will not generate                carbon dioxide, avoiding the indoor air turbidity,                so as to improve the eating experience of the                food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a table                surface.
DC X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC X25-C01; X25-B01E1
IP F24C-007/06

PN CN216480940-U
TI Furnace with convenient lifting combustion efficiency, has carbon dioxide and water are discharged into atmosphere from smoke outlet in heat taking chamber.
AU ZHANG D
AE ZHANG D (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2022683752
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model relates to a stove, especially a stove capable of conveniently improving combustion efficiency, wherein a smoke distributing plate is set between the hearth and the smoke outlet; the smoke distributing plate is located right above the hearth, the upper end of the smoke distributing plate is provided with a surrounding smoke collecting plate, the surrounding smoke collecting plate and the top of the furnace main body form a heat taking chamber; two sides respectively the furnace chamber is provided with a furnace door and a hearth wall, wherein a part of the hearth wall is divided into an inner and outer layer, the lower port of the inner and outer layer is a second air inlet, the upper port of the inner and outer layer is an air supplement heating passage outlet, an air supplement heating passage is formed between the second air inlet and the outlet of the air supplement heating passage; the smoke channel is interval around the smoke distributing plate and the air complementing heating channel outlet and the smoke collecting plate to form the smoke chamber. The utility model claims a stove with a structure simple of conveniently lifting the combustion efficiency with a high combustion efficiency.

PT P
PN CN114428106-A
TI Preparing carbon monoxide gas sensor, involves            preparing self-assembled polypeptide, adding            self-assembled polymer into mixed solution, adding            self-assembled polypeptide-carrying tin oxide composite            material in water, where mixed solution is            polyvinylpyrrolidone-K30, sodium hydroxide.
AU HU Y
   ZHU C
   WEN F
   WANG C
   LI L
AE UNIV HANGZHOU DIANZI (UYHH-C)
GA 202266245K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide (CO) gas sensor                involves dissolving polypeptide in proper amount of                dissolving agent, deionized water, keeping at room                temperature to obtain self- assembled polypeptide.                The self-assembled polypeptide is added into the                mixed solution with the same quality, stirred                uniformly, subjected to hydrothermal reaction after                finishing reaction, washed and centrifuged to                obtain precipitate, dried, crushed and annealed to                obtain self-assembled polypeptide carrying tin                oxide composite material. The mixed solution is                polyvinyl pyrrolidone-K30, sodium hydroxide and                stannic chloride pentahydrate as solutes. The                deionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol is used                as solvent. The self-assembled polypeptide carrying                tin oxide composite material is dispersed in                deionized water to obtain suspension, the                suspension is uniformly coated on the electrode                sheet and dried to obtain CO gas sensor.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon monoxide (CO) gas                sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses self-assembled polypeptide                mediated inorganic ion deposition, control                composite material shape structure to obtain                beautiful appearance, uniform structure, and                prepared CO gas sensor can realize fast and                effectively detecting carbon monoxide at room                temperature, the whole performance is greatly                improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dissolving agent                  is dimethyl sulfoxide. The polypeptide is                  diphenylalanine dipeptide.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC A12-E13; A04-D05A; E11-Q03J; E31-N05B1; E33-A03; E35-H; J04-C04A; J04-C02; S03-E14P; S03-E02A
