PN WO2022087054-A1; US2022127721-A1; CN114901858-A
TI Depositing diamond layer on substrate, comprises            generating pulsed plasma in gas mixture in substrate            processing chamber, and depositing nanocrystalline            diamond layer having thickness, roughness, hardness and            modulus.
AU SAHMUGANATHAN V
   CHEN Z
   JITENG G
   VENKATASUBRAMANIAN E
   KIAN PING L
   MALLICK A B
   SUDIJONO J
   KIAN P L
   LUO J
   GU J
AE APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 202257095X
AB    NOVELTY - Depositing a diamond layer on a substrate                comprises generating a pulsed plasma in a gas                mixture in a substrate processing chamber (200),                and depositing a nanocrystalline diamond layer on                the substrate. The gas mixture comprises a first                gas comprising hydrogen, a second gas comprising                carbon dioxide, a third gas consisting of methane,                carbon hexahydrogen, and a fourth gas comprising an                inert gas. The diamond layer has a thickness,                roughness, hardness, and modulus. The inert gas is                helium, nitrogen, neon and/or argon.
   USE - The method is useful for depositing a                nanocrystalline diamond layer on a substrate during                manufacture of electronic devices i.e. integrated                circuits.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables depositing the                nanocrystalline diamond layer on the substrate with                high durability and high hardness, thus reducing                processing time and cost, and increasing chip                density in 3-dimensional-NAND by adding more                layers, and hence reducing cost per bit per                bit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                non-transitory computer readable medium including                instructions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flow diagram of depositing                the nanocrystalline diamond layer on a                substrate.
   Substrate processing chamber (200)
   Processing chamber interior volume                (202)
   Process cage input (204)
   Plasma power source (206)
   Substrate support (210)
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); ; T01 (Digital Computers); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)

The heat exchange tube is vertical heat exchange tube arranged along vertical direction. The heat exchange medium outlet (3) of the vertical heat exchange tube is arranged above the heat exchange medium inlet (9).

   ADVANTAGE - The exchanger realizes high parameter power circulating working medium supercritical carbon dioxide and heat collecting working medium solid particles of stable and efficient heat exchange, thus reducing the retention of particles, improving the convection heat exchange efficiency, and greatly reducing the collision of particles and heat exchange tube bundle, and reducing the abrasion of the heat transfer tube bundle.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The surfactant is prepared                  by (i) performing acyl chlorination reaction on                  brominated acid compound of formula                  (Br(CH2)n(C=O)OH) (II) and acyl chlorination                  reagent to obtain brominated acyl chloride-based                  polymer of formula (III), (ii) performing                  amidation reaction on (III) and dimethylamine                  hydrochloride to obtain brominated                  N,N-dimethylacetamide-based polymer of formula                  (IV), (iii) performing Williamson ether synthesis                  reaction on (IV) and hydroquinone to obtain                  1,4-dimethoxybenzene-based N,N-dimethylacetamide                  polymer of formula (V), performing reduction                  reaction on (V) and lithium aluminum hydride                  system to obtain 1,4-dimethoxybenzene-based                  N,N-dimethylethanamine polymer of formula (VI),                  and (v) reacting (VI) with brominated alkane in                  solvent to obtain final product, where the molar                  ratio between (VI) and brominated alkane is                  2:0.5-1.2. The step (i) further comprises adding                  catalyst, and the catalyst is                  N,N-dimethylformamide. The step (ii) further                  comprises adding acid binding agent to carry out                  amidation reaction and the acid binding agent is                  triethylamine. The step (iii) further comprises                  adding alkali to carry out Williamson ether                  synthesis reaction and the alkali is anhydrous                  potassium carbonate.Preferred Components: The amount of the                  hydrophilic aluminum(III) oxide particles                  relative to the aqueous phase is 0.01-0.5 wt.%.                  The concentration of surfactant relative to the                  aqueous phase is 0.003-0.1 mmol/l. The oil phase                  includes n-decane, toluene and/or                  tricaprylin.

The sites that promote activity are Cu0-CuO, Cu0-Cu2O, Cu0-CuO-CuO2, Cu0-Cu2O-ZnO, Cu0-Cu2O-ZnO-ZrO2, and Cu0-CuO-Cu2O-ZnO-ZrO2.

PT P
PN CN113200845-A
TI Preparing columnar structure zinc glutarate catalyst for polypropylene carbonate production by dissolving sodium glutarate aqueous solution with cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, pouring into zinc chloride solution dissolved in aqueous solution of acetic acid, reacting, filtering, washing and drying.
AU LI J
AE LI J (LIJJ-Individual)
GA 2021926075
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing columnar structure zinc glutarate catalyst for polypropylene carbonate production involves (1) dissolving a sodium glutarate aqueous solution with cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in a zinc chloride solution dissolved in an aqueous solution of acetic acid and reacting under a slow-speed horizontal circular-flow stirring, (2) filtering the obtained reaction solution with a single-layer flat filter to obtain a fixed filter layer of the zinc glutarate crystal, (3) sequentially washing the crystal filter layer using hot sodium chloride brine, desalted water and ethers that are miscible with water, where the crystal filter layer can azeotrope with water and is azeotropic to form a relatively high water content and (4) drying the washed zinc glutarate catalyst under vacuum vibration.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing columnar structure zinc glutarate catalyst for polypropylene carbonate production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the columnar structure zinc glutarate catalyst which can catalyze the copolymerization of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide, adopts the reaction time of 8 hours and has a catalytic efficiency that can reach 63.94 g polymer/g catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves adopting zinc chloride and sodium glutarate in a molar ratio of (1.20-1.35):1, where the mass ratio of sodium glutarate and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide is (125-250):1, the mass concentration of sodium glutarate is 40-60%, preferably 50%, the mass concentration of the zinc chloride solution is 40-60%, preferably 50%, the concentration of acetic acid used to dissolve zinc chloride is calculated based on the total water volume of the reaction system, and the pH value of the amount of desalinated water can reach 3-3.5, the pH value of the sodium glutarate solution is 6.2-6.6 and when the reaction is stopped, the pH value of the reaction system is not greater than 3.7, the reaction temperature is 10-40 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C and the reaction time is 1.5-2 minutes and the reaction is carried out under slow horizontal circular flow stirring. The step (2) involves adopting a filtration method using a single-layer plate filter, pressure filtration or vacuum suction filtration, so that the zinc glutarate crystals form a stable filter layer. The step (3) involves adopting the sodium chloride brine with a concentration of 15-20%, where the brine temperature is 80-100 degrees C, the dosage is 3-5 times the volume of the catalyst, the number of washings is 2-3 times, the amount of desalinated water is 3-5 times the volume of the catalyst, the number of washes is 2-3 times, the ethers comprises 1,4-dioxane and ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, ethylene glycol dimethyl ether is preferred due to its low boiling point, the dosage is 3-5 times the volume of the catalyst and the number of washes is 2-3 times. The step (4) involves drying using a vacuum vibration dryer with a vibration of 3-5 mm, where the drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C, the drying vacuum degree is -0.97 atm to -0.99 atm and the drying time is 8-12 hours.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC A02-A; A05-E06A; E05-L03D; E10-C02D2; E11-F11
IP C07C-051/41; C07C-051/42; C07C-055/12; C08G-064/34
PD CN113200845-A   03 Aug 2021   C07C-051/41   202176   Pages: 8   Chinese

GA 202183080X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbonated apple cider involves:                sulfitation of freshly squeezed apple juice; adding                beet sugar syrup and pure culture of Saccharomyces                cerevisiae Lalvin 1116 dry wine yeast; removing the                wort from the yeast sediment; storing the wine                material for up to 3 years at a temperature of up                to 10 degrees C; treating the wine material with                bentonite in combination with gelatin, blending,                and adding a preservative, preferably potassium                sorbate and water to obtain cider with ethyl                alcohol volume fraction of 5.7 vol.%; and                correcting total content of sulfur dioxide to a                mass concentration of 200 mg/dm3 and the free                content of sulfur dioxide to a mass concentration                of 20 mg/dm3, biologically filtering, cooling and                saturating with carbon dioxide under a pressure of                up to 200 kPa to obtain pearl cider, or under a                pressure of 200-400 kPa to obtain effervescent                cider, then feeding to an isobaric thermos tank for                assimilation and temporary storage before filling                into consumer containers.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonated                apple cider.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases shelf life of wine                materials, stability, and quality of apple                ciders.

   LIU W

   ZHANG M

AE JIANGSU PROVINCIAL ACAD ENVIRONMENTAL SC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202166979V
AB    NOVELTY - Ceramic fiber catalytic filter tube includes ceramic fiber tube (101), catalyst coating (102) and honeycomb carrier (103). The ceramic fiber tube is a tube structure with an open top. The honeycomb carrier is filled in the top opening of the ceramic fiber tube. The catalyst coating is coated on the inner wall of the ceramic fiber tube and the honeycomb carrier.
   USE - Ceramic fiber catalytic filter tube capable of catalyzing and oxidizing organic pollutants in a dust collector (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic fiber catalytic filter tube has aluminum oxide (Al2O3), silicon oxide (SiO2) and titanium oxide (TiO2), which is beneficial to adsorb organic pollutants in the exhaust gas. The catalyst coating is respectively coated on the ceramic fiber tube and the V2O5 carrier, which can effectively catalyze and oxidize the organic pollutants in the exhaust gas to generate harmless gases carbon dioxide (CO2), water (H2O), etc., so as to meet the requirements of emission regulations.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of ceramic fiber catalytic filter tube.
   Ceramic fiber catalytic filter tube (1)
   Ceramic fiber tube (101)
   Catalyst coating (102)
   Honeycomb carrier (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The catalyst coating involves weighing 20-60 pts. wt. copper sulfate heptahydrate (CuSO4.7H2O), 10-50 pts. wt. lead nitrate (Pb(NO3)2) and 20-40 pts. wt. zirconium nitrate pentahydrate (Zr(NO3)4.5H2O) to mix, and adding 150-200 pts. wt. citric acid and stirring to obtain a mixed solution, slowly adding 15-25 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol or polyglycerol, and stirring thoroughly, keeping, taking the solid powder and drying after suction filtration, and calcinate at 350-500 degrees C to obtain coapper-lead-zirconium (Cu-Pb-Zr) composite oxide. The weight ratio of nano titanium oxide (TiO2), Cu-Pb-Zr composite oxide and zirconium oxide (ZrO2) sol is (0.2-0.5):(0.5-0.8):(0.02-0.05), and added deionized water to obtain a mixed solution, and after grinding the mixed liquid to a particle size D90 of 10-30 micrometer. The platinum nitrate (Pt(NO3)2) and ethanol solution to the mixture to get it. The preparation method of the ceramic fiber tube involves mixing the mass percentage of 35-50 wt.% aluminum oxide (Al2O3), 50-60 wt.% silicon oxide (SiO2) and 0-10 wt.% titanium oxide (TiO2), and after being formed, calcining at a temperature of 1600-2200 degrees C to form a ceramic fiber tube with a porosity greater than 80%. Preferred Components: The honeycomb carrier is a honeycomb-shaped vanadium oxide (V2O5) carrier. Preferred Conditions: The catalyst coating is coated on both sides of the honeycomb carrier according to a coating amount of 100-300 kg/m3, and after the coating is completed, it is calcined at 450-550 degrees C for 1-5 hours.

GA 202160696M

   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis gas (claimed) which is useful for increasing hydrogen concentration in the feed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes: the renewable power/energy and provides the synthesis gas, thus reducing emission of greenhouse gases to a minimum, hence eco-friendly; and lesser amounts of electricity for heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The electrolysis of water and/or steam in step (b) is powered at least in part by renewable energy, where the step of separating air is powered at least in part by renewable energy. The electrical heated reformer is powered at least in part by renewable energy. The electrolysis is operated so that all the hydrogen produced by the electrolysis is added to the reformed gas downstream step (d) to provide a module (M) is a difference of hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the synthesis gas withdrawn from step (f) is 1.9-2.2, preferably 2-2.1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: a step of separating air into a separate stream containing oxygen and into a separate stream containing nitrogen and introducing at least a part of the separate stream containing oxygen into the autothermal reformer; a step of introducing substantially pure carbon dioxide upstream step (c), and/or upstream of step (d), and/or downstream step (d); a step of steam reforming a part of the hydrocarbon feed stock in indirect heat transfer relationship with the process stream leaving the autothermal reforming step (d) and mixing the heat exchange steam reformed process gas stream with autothermal reformed process gas stream up stream step (e); and a step of heat exchange steam reforming the hydrocarbon feed stock in indirect heat transfer relationship with the process stream leaving the autothermal reforming step (d) and passing the heat exchanged steam reformed hydrocarbon feed stock to step (d). In the method, a part of the hydrocarbon feed stock from step (a) is bypassed to the steam reforming in step (c) and introduced to the autothermal reformer in step (d). The synthesis gas withdrawn in step (f) is in a further step converted to a methanol product. In the method, where off-gas generated in process steps is burnt to pre heat the hydrocarbon feedstock and/or steam generation. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon feed stock comprises natural gas, methane, liquefied natural gas (LNG), naphtha and/or their mixtures either as such or pre-reformed and/or desulfurized.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 2021515445

The preset humidity is 50-80%, and real-time acquisition of test parameters through the detection unit. The test parameter is the real-time concentration of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the gas component in the in-situ reaction unit. The output rate of trace gas phase products during the sample test process is calculated according to the test parameters. The stability of the test sample of the polyamide-based material is evaluated according to the output rate of the trace gas phase product.

   CHEN Y
   TANG J
   CAI Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANCAI YUNJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021510159

   USE - The method is useful for preparing methyl chloride using the tail gas of chlorine-based chemical vapor deposition process grown by silicon-silicon carbide crystal film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces tail gas emission and make up the blank of chlorine-based chemical vapor deposition process tail gas processing technology.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for processing data (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises pre-processing the raw material gas into a preprocessing unit composed of a dust remover, a particle removing filter and an oil mist catcher; removing the dust, particle, oil mist and partial high chlorine silane under the operation condition of 0.2-0.3 MPa pressure and normal temperature; perchloric alkane and high hydrocarbon impurity; the purified raw material gas enters the next process, chlorosilane spray absorption, spraying and absorbing the chlorosilane; the purified raw material gas from the pre-treatment process is pressurized to 0.6-1 MPa, and after cold-heat exchange to 80-160 degrees C, entering from the bottom of the spray tower, using chlorosilane and anhydrous hydrogen chloride liquid as absorbent; spraying from the top of the spraying absorption tower and carrying out reverse mass transfer exchange with the purified raw material gas; flowing the absorption liquid enriched with chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride from the bottom of the chlorosilane spraying absorption tower; entering the next process: multi-stage vaporation/compression/condensation process; small amount of residual particles and perchloric silane flowing out from the bottom of the tower; perchloric alkane and high hydrocarbon impurity output for environment-friendly treatment; from the top of chlorosilane spraying absorption tower, flowing out the non-condensable gas rich in hydrogen; after adsorption treatment, or directly as the fuel gas for heating the subsequent chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction process; or outputting the raw material gas as extracting hydrogen gas, multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation; the absorption liquid from the chlorosilane spray absorption process enters the multi-stage evaporation; and directly or vacuum extracting to 0.6-1 MPa and then entering the condenser; obtaining the gas phase crude hydrogen chloride gas; the crude hydrogen chloride-liquid formed by condensing enters into the next procedure, hydrogen chloride refining; flowing crude chlorosilane liquid from the condenser; entering into the subsequent chlorosilane and shallow cooling rectifying, crude hydrogen chloride liquid from the multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process enters the hydrogen chloride refining process composed of hydrogen chloride rectifying tower and vacuum rectifying tower, where the rectifying tower operation pressure is 0.3-1 MPa; the operation temperature is 60-120 degrees C, the operation pressure of the vacuum tower is -0.08 to -0.1 MPa; the operation temperature is 60-120 degrees C; distilling the hydrogen chloride gas whose purity is more than 99.9% from the top of the rectifying tower; entering the next step-chlorosilane hydrochlorination reaction; and entering the vacuum tower from the distillate at the bottom of the tower; the tower top gas flowing out from the tower top, mainly containing a little of methane chloride; entering the subsequent chloromethane rectifying process, further recycling chloromethane, heavy component flowing out from the bottom of the vacuum tower, or returning to the multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, or returning to the subsequent shallow cold rectification process in chlorosilane, chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction; hydrogen chloride gas from the hydrogen chloride refining process; after heat, mixing with the methanol steam of the external transportation according to ratio into the hydrogen chlorination reactor for hydrochlorination reaction; the reaction temperature is 230-300 degrees C; the reaction pressure is atmospheric pressure or less than 0.3 MPa; the solid catalyst is ferric oxide or zinc oxide; the crude chloromethane gas generated by reaction is flowed out from the reactor; after quenching and condensing, entering the next process, washing and absorbing, washing and absorbing; the crude chloromethane gas after quenching and condensing from the chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction process enters a water washing absorption tower for separating organic matter and inorganic matter; the washing absorption operation condition is room temperature and atmospheric pressure or micro positive pressure; the by-product hydrogen chloride output from the water washing absorption tower flows out; the non-condensable gas flows into the alkali neutralizing tower and the washing tower, further removing the residual hydrogen chloride and methanol, purifying chloromethane gas enters into the next working procedure, chloromethane rectifying, refining chloromethane; the purified chloromethane gas from the washing and absorbing process is mixed with the top gas of the vacuum tower from the hydrogen chloride refining process; entering the chloromethane rectifying tower; distilling the chloromethane product with purity more than 99.9% from the rectifying tower top; the rectifying tower bottom distillate is processed to produce the polychloromethane product, shallow cooling rectifying in chlorosilane; rough chlorosilane liquid from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process; shallow cold rectifying process in the chlorosilane; the operation temperature is -35 degrees C to 10 degrees C; the operation pressure is 0.6-2 MPa, the non-condensable gas flowing out from the rectifying tower top, or directly used as fuel gas, or returning to chlorosilane spraying absorbing process, and discharging chlorosilane liquid from the rectifying tower bottom as absorbent to return to chlorosilane spraying absorbing process for recycling. The chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process comprises the step of purifying the hydrogen chloride contained in the raw material gas, chlorosilane concentration higher working condition, adding secondary intermediate temperature chlorosilane absorption process, namely, non-condensable gas from chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process, compressing; after condensing and gas-liquid separation, forming fresh non-condensable gas directly or pressurizing to 0.2-1 MPa, and cold-heat exchanging at 60-120 degrees C, from the added dihypochlorite spraying absorption tower bottom enters; using containing chlorosilane/hydrogen chloride mixed liquid as absorbent; spraying from the top of the second hypochlorous silane spray absorption tower to perform reverse mass transfer exchange with the fresh non-condensable gas; flowing out the absorption liquid 2 enriched with chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride from the bottom of the absorption tower; mixing with the absorption liquid 1 of the primary chlorosilane spray absorption process, and then entering the subsequent multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process; returning the liquid obtained from the gas-liquid separation to the shallow cold rectifying process in the chlorosilane; flowing out the non-condensable gas from the top of the absorbing tower; after the adsorption treatment, or directly as the fuel gas; or outputting the raw material gas as the extracting hydrogen gas. The purified chloromethane gas from the washing and absorbing process contains more polychloromethane and water under the working condition of water separation, purifying the chloromethane gas, dehydrating and drying by sulfuric acid, then processing by chloromethane rectifying process; distilling tower top gas from the rectifying tower top and separating by condensing and gas-liquid; returning the liquid to the rectifying tower as reflux; then entering into an adsorption drying process to obtain the chloromethane product gas; the purity is greater than or equal to 99.99%; and rectifying tower bottom distillate is containing polychloromethane, sending into the boundary region to rectify and separate to obtain the respective product. The chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction uses liquid phase reaction, and anhydrous hydrogen chloride gas from the hydrogen chloride refining process; mixing with a specific ratio of vaporized methanol steam into the chloromethane hydrogen chloride liquid phase reaction process, taking 75-85% of zinc chloride aqueous solution as liquid catalyst, heating to 120 degrees C, the reaction temperature is 120-160 degrees C; the reaction pressure is atmospheric pressure or micro positive pressure condition; performing bubble-type gas-liquid contact generate chloromethane hydrogen chloride liquid phase reaction; the crude chloromethane gas generated by reaction; after quenching, condensing, washing and absorbing; sulfuric acid drying and chloromethane rectifying, or after absorbing and drying to obtain the chloromethane product; the purity is greater than or equal to 99.9-99.99%. The rectifying tower bottom distillate of the chloromethane rectifying process enters into a chlorination reactor, where the chlorine is introduced according to a specific ratio, the tower bottom distillate is further chloridized; the reaction mixed gas generated by this enters a carbon tetrachloride rectifying tower; the carbon tetrachloride product is distilled from the tower top; the tower bottom distillate is returned to the chloromethane rectifying tower; the distilled water from the tower top is chloromethane product; the purity is greater than or equal to 99.99%; the yield is more than 98%; the tower bottom distillate enters into the dichloromethane rectifying tower; distilling from the tower top is dichloromethane product, tower bottom distillate into the trichloromethane rectifying tower, distilling from the tower top is trichloromethane product, discharging the remaining heavy component impurities at the tower bottom, comprising a small amount of chlorosilane, multi-carbon (C2+) and water, sending into the incinerator to reach the standard processing and discharging. The purifying raw material gas in hydrogen chloride content is less than 5% and the hydrogen content is more than 80% working condition, adding chlorination reaction step, the dry crude methyl chloride gas after drying by sulfuric acid and non-condensable gas (hydrogen-rich gas) from chlorosilane spray absorption process, chlorine according to a specific ratio into the chlorination reactor for hydrochlorination reaction; the formed reaction gas after quenching and rectifying tower of the hydrogen chloride rectifying process, crude hydrogen chloride distilled from the rectifying tower top directly enters the chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction process after cooling, a part or then enters the hydrogen chloride refining vacuum tower, or directly as the absorbing agent of chlorosilane spraying absorption process; directly entering the chloromethane hydrochlorination reaction process from the fraction flowing out from the bottom of the rectifying tower; obtaining the crude chloromethane gas; then quenching and condensing; washing and absorbing and drying with sulfuric acid, then entering a carbon tetrachloride rectifying tower; distilling from the tower top is carbon tetrachloride product; the tower bottom distillate enters into the chloromethane rectifying tower; the distilled from the tower top is chloromethane product; the purity is greater than or equal to 99.99%; the tower bottom distillate enters into the dichloromethane rectifying tower; distilling from the tower top is dichloromethane product, tower bottom distillate into the trichloromethane rectifying tower, distilling from the tower top is trichloromethane product, discharging the rest heavy component impurities at the tower bottom, comprising a small amount of chlorosilane, multi-carbon and water, sending into the incinerator to reach the standard processing and discharging. The content of polychloromethane and water in the purified chloromethane gas in the washing and absorbing process is less than 1 to the power of 1%, can directly enter into a temperature change adsorption drying composed of at least two adsorption towers; one tower adsorption, another tower or multiple tower regeneration, adsorption and regeneration alternating operation, realizing continuous production; the adsorption tower in the adsorption state of the purity is greater than or equal to 99.9-99.99% of the chloromethane product; there is no need of chloromethane rectifying process; the regenerated carrier gas is from non-condensable gas flowing out from chlorosilane spraying and absorbing process; and the regeneration temperature is greater than 80-120 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN112624244-A; CN214829685-U
TI Removing volatile organic matter efficiently in            petrochemical wastewater involves sealing and isolating            waste gas containing volatile organic matter dispersed            by wastewater, collecting waste gas in closed            environment.
AU GAO Y
   LIU H
   ZHONG Q
   DENG L
   CHEN H
   JIN Q
   ZHANG R
   ZHOU M
   PENG X
AE CHINA CHENGDA ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 202140280J
AB    NOVELTY - Removing volatile organic matter efficiently                in petrochemical wastewater involves sealing and                isolating the waste gas containing volatile organic                matter dispersed by the wastewater. The waste gas                in the closed environment is collected by                micro-negative pressure and then sent to the                pre-processing unit to remove the particle impurity                in the waste gas. The waste gas-removing particle                impurities are sent into the adsorption unit, the                volatile organic matter in the waste gas is                absorbed. The gas is directly discharging with                standard adsorption. When the adsorption is                saturated, the desorption regeneration is carried                out. The desorbed gas enters the combustion unit                for oxidation combustion. The volatile organic                matter in the waste gas is completely decomposed                into carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O).
   USE - Method for removing volatile organic matter                efficiently in petrochemical wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low operation cost, small                investment, fast effect, which can completely                realize automatic control, processing by the                method, volatile organic matter (VOCs) removing                efficiency reaches 95-99 %, exhaust meets the                national and local VOCs emission concentration and                emission rate in emission limit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flow diagram of the                petroleum chemical industry wastewater volatile                organic matter (VOCs) efficient removal of process.                The Drawing includes non-English language                text.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: The adsorption unit uses                  zeolite molecular sieve adsorber, the zeolite                  molecular sieve is regenerated by desorption of                  hot air, cooling air is introduced and cooled,                  and the adsorption efficiency is ensured. The                  combustion exhaust gas generated by the                  combustion unit is discharged after entering the                  heat exchanger to recycle heat. The cold air                  enters the zeolite molecular sieve absorber                  through a blower, and then enters the heat                  exchanger and the combustion exhaust gas to                  exchange heat, after heating, returned to the                  zeolite molecular sieve absorber as hot air to                  carry out desorption regeneration. The desorption                  regeneration high boiling point organic matter                  and self-polymer are controlled, the zeolite                  molecular sieve adsorber is desorbed by the                  program-controlled heating method. The desorption                  temperature is gradually operated at 230 degrees                  C, 260 degrees C, 280 degrees C, 300 degrees C                  for a period of time to remove the self-polymer.                  The pre-processing unit removes the particle                  impurities in the waste gas by using the high                  molecular filter fiber material. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature of the regenerated                  hot air is 200-220 degrees C. The concentration                  of the volatile organic compounds in the desorbed                  gas is not greater than 10 times the                  concentration of volatile organic compounds in                  the waste gas. Preferred Parameters: The air                  quantity of the regenerated hot air is controlled                  at (1 divide by 10)-(1 divide by 15) of the air                  quantity of the processed organic waste gas. The                  combustion unit is a heat storage type oxidation                  furnace, the oxidation combustion reaction                  temperature is 800-820 degrees C, and the                  residence time of the oxidation chamber is not                  less than 1 second.

PN CN112495151-A
TI Eliminating blue plume from coal-fired boiler chimney involves storing coarse particle sesquicine in storage hopper, grinding coarse particle sesquicine alkali into fine particles, and conveying fine particle sesquicine to spray gun.
AU CHENG H
   LIU L
AE ZHANGJIAGANG AIR ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202128275G
AB    NOVELTY - Eliminating blue plume from a coal-fired boiler chimney involves storing coarse particle sesquicine in the storage hopper, grinding the coarse particle sesquicine alkali into fine particles, and conveying the fine particle sesquicine to spray gun through a conveying fan. The fine particle sesquicine alkali is sprayed to flue between outlet end of SCR reactor and air pre-heater through a special spray gun. The fine particle sesquicine is uniformly mixed with the flue gas in the flue and releases water vapor and carbon dioxide to form porous sodium carbonate at a temperature, where specific surface area of decomposition is 10 square meters/g. The porous sodium carbonate reacts with sulfur trioxide and hydrogen chloride in flue gas to remove sulfur trioxide and hydrogen chloride in the flue gas.
   USE - Method for eliminating blue plume from coal-fired boiler chimney.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to eliminate blue plume from coal-fired boiler chimney, which reduces aerosol substance in flue gas, effectively eliminates blue plume of chimney, effectively solves problem of blockage and corrosion of air pre-heater, and prolongs service life of filter bag of bag-type dust collector.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The storage hopper is a stainless steel storage hopper. Preferred Component: The sesquicine is sodium carbonate-sodium bicarbonate-dihydrate.

PN CN112315915-A
TI Preparing coenzyme nanoparticles by supercritical fluid granulation technology involves using one or more of absolute ethanol, methanol, and acetone as a solvent, and dissolving coenzyme, maltodextrin and Tween-80 in the solvent.
AU HU Y
AE HU Y (HUYY-Individual)
GA 2021151774
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coenzyme Q10 nanoparticles by supercritical fluid granulation technology involves using one or more of absolute ethanol, methanol, and acetone as a solvent, and dissolving coenzyme Q10, maltodextrin and Tween (RTM: non-ionic surfactant)-80 in the solvent to prepare a coenzyme Q10 solution, and when the temperature of the emulsification kettle reaches the set temperature, turning on the carbon dioxide (CO2) cylinder. The CO2 gas is cooled into a liquid by the refrigerator. A high-pressure pump is used to send the coenzyme Q10 solution into the emulsification kettle, and turned on the magnetic stirrer to form a supercritical microemulsion with high-speed stirring. The CO2 is poured into crystallization kettle, adjusted the pressure and temperature in the crystallization kettle and keep it constant, and injected the supercritical coenzyme Q10 microemulsion into crystallization kettle through the nozzle, and obtained coenzyme Q10 particles with uniform particle size.
   USE - Method for preparing coenzyme Q10 nanoparticles by supercritical fluid granulation technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses maltodextrin to clathrate coenzyme Q10 that can better protect the activity of coenzyme Q10, improves the solubility of coenzyme Q10 in aqueous solution, have high dissolution rate, and improves the bioavailability of coenzyme Q10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the supercritical emulsification kettle is 10-30 megapascal. The flow rate of the supercritical microemulsion into the crystallization kettle is 0.5-1.5 mL/minute. The particle size distribution of the sample prepared under the optimal conditions is narrow and uniform, and the average particle size is below 100 nanometer.

PN CN112062123-A
TI Preparation of water hyacinth based            honeycomb-shaped heteroatom porous carbon used e.g. as            cathode material of lithium ion battery, by washing            waste water hyacinth, drying stem leaf, soaking debris            into alkaline solution, and carbonizing.
AU LI F
   CHEN X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020C6386B
AB    NOVELTY - A water hyacinth based honeycomb-shaped                heteroatom porous carbon is prepared by collecting                waste water hyacinth floating in river, using tap                water to wash away substance attached on surface of                biomass, removing root, naturally drying stem leaf,                crushing air-dried biomass by crusher, sieving,                adding debris into alkaline solution, stirring and                soaking at normal temperature, filtering to obtain                material filter cake, drying in vacuum drying box                at 105.0 degrees C overnight, pyrolyzing and                carbonizing, cooling to room temperature to obtain                water hyacinth-based bio-carbon material, adding                into acidic solution, refluxing and removing                potassium hydroxide and ash substance contained in                material, cooling to room temperature, filtering to                obtain material filter cake, washing by ionized                water, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing water                hyacinth based honeycomb-shaped heteroatom porous                carbon as cathode material of lithium ion battery                (claimed) and for water resource purification and                carbon dioxide curing.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good stability, high specific                surface and rich multiple heteroatoms. The method                is environment-friendly, has rich raw material and                simple synthesis method, realizes easy industrial                production, and converts waste water hyacinth                biomass into porous biological carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution                  is 3.0-5.0 mol/L potassium hydroxide solution.                  The acidic solution is 10-30 wt.% nitric acid                  solution. The carbon has impurity element mass                  content of 2.94-3.42 wt.% nitrogen, 13.33-20.82                  wt.% oxygen and 0.81-0.83 wt.% sulfur.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Soaking is done for                  12.0-24.0 hours. Pyrolyzing and carbonizing is                  done in nitrogen atmosphere at 500.0-700.0                  degrees C for 3.0-5.0 hours at nitrogen gas flow                  rate of 20.0-100.0 ml/mol and heating speed of                  10.0-15.0 degrees C/minute. Refluxing is done at                  60.0-80.0 degrees C for 6.0-12.0 hours. Drying                  involves vacuum drying washed material filter                  cake at 95.0-1056 degrees C for 24.0-48.0                  hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. AB    NOVELTY - The tin has a rotating cavity vertically provided with two symmetrical rotating rods (17). Sliding plates (10) are provided with a through opening. The rotating rods and the rotating cavity are fixedly connected with gears (20) that are engaged with a rack.

PN CN111974752-A
TI Supercritical fluid cleaning method, involves            separating contaminants from surface of workpiece, and            discharging pollutants separated from workpiece with            supercritical fluid to complete cleaning of            workpiece.
AU YANG J
   LI W
   YANG F
AE LUOYANG MAITUOFENG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9115J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves continuously passing                supercritical fluid into a cleaning chamber.                Pressure in the cleaning chamber or temperature of                a workpiece is alternately changed such that the                supercritical fluid repeatedly forms cavities or                bubbles in the contaminants on a surface of the                workpiece. The contaminants are separated from a                surface of the workpiece. Pollutants separated from                the workpiece are discharged with the supercritical                fluid to complete the cleaning of the workpiece,                where the supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide,                nitrogen, water, air or inert gas. The                supercritical fluid is passed into the cleaning                chambers with different cross-sectional areas.                Surface temperature of the work piece is changed by                heating.
   USE - Supercritical fluid cleaning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables forming cavities or bubbles                repeatedly in contaminants by alternately changing                the pressure of the cleaning chamber or the                temperature of the workpiece, repeating cycle                continuously to separate the pollutants from the                surface of the workpiece to achieve the purpose of                cleaning. The high diffusion coefficient of the                supercritical fluid is conducive to the mass                transfer between the pollutants and the fluid,                which greatly improves the cleaning rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                supercritical fluid cleaning device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram                of a supercritical fluid cleaning device.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC P43-B01A
IP B08B-013/00; B08B-007/00

PN WO2020223059-A1; CN113748150-A; EP3962976-A1; US2022220249-A1; JP2022531217-W
TI Isocyanate-reactive composition for foam,            comprises blend of saccharide-initiated polyether            polyol, amine-initiated polyether polyol and            alkanolamine-initiated polyol, blowing agent            composition and tertiary amine catalyst            composition.
AU RIDER S G
   PARKS B W
   MILLER C C
   REED S G
AE COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO LLC (CVST-C)
GA 2020A7097E
AB    NOVELTY - An isocyanate-reactive composition comprises a                polyol blend comprising 50-98 wt.%                saccharide-initiated polyether polyol having                hydroxyl number of 200-600 mg potassium hydroxide/g                and functionality of 4-6, 1-20 wt.% aliphatic                amine-initiated polyether polyol having hydroxyl                number of 20-120 mg potassium hydroxide/g and                functionality of 3-6, and 1-20 wt.%                alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol having                hydroxyl number of 500 mg potassium hydroxide/g or                more and functionality of 2.5-4, a blowing agent                composition comprising hydrochlorofluoroolefin and                a carbon dioxide generating chemical blowing agent,                and a tertiary amine catalyst composition.
   USE - Isocyanate-reactive composition for producing                rigid polyurethane foam for composite article used                for refrigeration apparatus including refrigerated                trailors (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides foam having excellent                rigidity, thermal insulation properties and low                shrinkage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) rigid polyurethane foam-forming                composition comprising a polyisocyanate and the                isocyanate-reactive composition;
   (2) process for producing a rigid polyurethane                foam, which involves mixing organic polyisocyanate                with the isocyanate-reactive composition at an                isocyanate index of 90-140 or 110-130; and
   (3) composite article comprising the rigid                polyurethane foam sandwiched between one or more                facer substrates made of plastic, paper, wood, or                metal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The saccharide                  comprises sucrose, sorbitol, or maltitol. The                  saccharide-initiated polyether polyol is the                  reaction product of at least one alkylene oxide                  comprising propylene oxide and/or ethylene oxide,                  with initiator compounds in the presence of a                  catalyst. The initiator compound comprises one or                  more saccharides and co-initiators comprising                  water, propylene glycol, glycerin, ethylene                  glycol, an ethanol amine and/or diethylene                  glycol. The aliphatic amine-initiated polyether                  polyol is the reaction product of at least one                  alkylene oxide comprising propylene oxide and/or                  ethylene oxide, with suitable initiator compound                  in the presence of catalyst. The initiator                  compound comprises ammonia, ethylene diamine,                  hexamethylene diamine, methyl amine,                  diaminodiphenyl methane, aniline and/or                  tetrahydroxyl ethyl ethylenediamine. The                  alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol is the                  reaction product of at least one alkylene oxide                  comprising propylene oxide, butylene oxide,                  styrene oxide and epichlorohydrin, with one or                  more initiator compound comprising alkanolamine                  of formula: NH2-Z-OH. The alkanolamine comprises                  monoethanolamine, l-amino-2-propanol,                  2-amino-l-propanol, 3-amino-l-propanol,                  l-(2-aminoethoxy) ethanol, l-amino-2-butanol,                  2-amino-3-butanol, 2-amino-2-methylpropanol,                  5-aminopentanol, 3-amino-2,2-dimethyl propanol                  and 4-aminocyclohexanol. The isocyanate-reactive                  composition further contains an aliphatic                  triol-initiated polyether polyol having hydroxyl                  number of 100-500 mg potassium hydroxide/g,                  preferably 233-243 mg potassium hydroxide/g,                  and/or functionality of more than 2.0-4, 2.5-3.5,                  or 3.0. The HCFO comprises                  l-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene (HCFO-1233zd, E                  and/or Z isomer), 2-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene                  (HCFO-1233xf), HCF01223,                  l,2-dichloro-l,2-difluoroethene (E and/or Z                  isomer), 3,3-dichloro-3-fluoropropene,                  2-chloro-l,l,l,4,4,4-hexafluorobutene-2 (E and/or                  Z isomer) and/or                  2-chloro-l,l,l,3,4,4,4-heptafluorobutene-2 (E                  and/or Z isomer). The composition further                  contains one or more other halogenated blowing                  agents and/or one or more hydrocarbon blowing                  agents. The carbon dioxide generating chemical                  blowing agent comprises water and/or a                  formate-blocked amine. The composition further                  contains a polysiloxane-polyalkyene-block                  copolymer, a polyethylene glycol ether of a long                  chain alcohol, tertiary amine or an alkanolamine                  salt of a long chain alkyl acid sulfate ester, an                  alkylsulfonic ester, or an alkylarylsulfonic                  acid. The polyisocyanate comprises an aromatic,                  aliphatic, or cycloaliphatic polyisocyanate,                  methylene-bridged polyphenyl polyisocyanate                  and/or a prepolymer of methylene-bridged                  polyphenyl polyisocyanate having an average                  functionality of 1.8-3.5, or 2.0-3.1, isocyanate                  moieties per molecule, and isocyanate content of                  25-32 wt.%. The plastic facer substrate comprises                  a polypropylene resin reinforced with continuous                  bi-directional glass fibers or a fiberglass                  reinforced polyester copolymer, paper, wood, or                  metal.Z = divalent radical which is 2-6C linear or                  branched alkylene, 4-6C cycloalkylene radical or                  4-6C dialkylene ether radical of formula:                  -R-O-R-;andR = 2-3C hydrocarbyl.Preferred Properties: The                  saccharide-initiated polyether polyol has                  hydroxyl number of 300-550 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g, preferably 398-422 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g, and/or functionality of 4-5,                  4.0-4.5, or 4.2-4.3. The aliphatic                  amine-initiated polyether polyol has hydroxyl                  number of 40-80 mg potassium hydroxide/g,                  preferably 57-63 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and                  functionality of 3-5, preferably 4. The                  alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol has                  hydroxyl number of 500-900 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g, preferably 680-720 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g, and/or functionality of 2.5-3.5. The                  polyol blend has weighted average functionality                  of 3-5 or 3.8-4.2, and/or weighted average                  hydroxyl number of 300-500 mg potassium                  hydroxide/g or 350-400 mg potassium hydroxide/g,                  and/or weighted average molecular weight of                  800-1200 g/mol, 850-1200 g/mol, or 900-1000                  g/mol. HCFO has boiling point at atmospheric                  pressure of -25 degrees C or more, preferably -19                  degrees C to 33 degrees C. Preferred Composition:                  The composition contains 60-98 wt.%, preferably                  70-85 wt.% saccharide-initiated polyether polyol,                  5-15 wt.% aliphatic amine-initiated polyether                  polyol, 1-10 wt.%, preferably 4-8 wt.%                  alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol, and 1-20                  wt.%, preferably 10-20 wt.% aliphatic                  triol-initiated polyether polyol. The weight                  ratio of saccharide-initiated polyether polyol                  and the aliphatic amine-initiated polyether                  polyol is 3:1 or 6:1-8:1. The weight ratio of                  saccharide-initiated polyether polyol and                  alkanolamine-initiated polyether polyol is 5:1,                  5:1-20:1, 5:1-15:1, or 10:1-15:1. The weight                  ratio of aliphatic amine-initiated polyether                  polyol and the alkanolamine-initiated polyether                  polyol is 0.8:1, 0.8:1-4:1, 1:1-2:1, or                  1:1-1.5:1. The content of HCFO is 10 wt.% or                  more, preferably 10-20 wt.%. The composition                  contains 0.5-5 wt.%, preferably 2-3 wt.% carbon                  dioxide generating chemical blowing agent. The                  total content of HCFO and the carbon dioxide                  generating chemical blowing agent is 90 wt.% or                  more, preferably 99 wt.% or more. The weight                  ratio of HCFO and carbon dioxide generating                  chemical blowing agent is 2:1, preferably                  6:1-10:1. The tertiary amine catalyst composition                  comprises 80-99 wt.%, preferably 97-98 wt.%                  morpholine and/or 1-20 wt.%, preferably 2-3 wt.%                  imidazole. The morpholine catalyst comprises                  dimorpholinodiethylether,                  dimorpholinodimethylether N-ethylmorpholine                  and/or N-methylmorpholine. The imidazole catalyst                  comprises imidazole, n-methylimidazole and/or                  1,2-dimethylimidazole. The morpholine catalyst                  comprises a mixture of 30-80 wt.%, preferably                  40-80 wt.% dimorpholinodiethylether and 20-70                  wt.%, preferably 20-60 wt.%                  N-methylmorpholine.

AU WANG Y
   MA W
   DING Y

   MA B

   LIU Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE LANZHOU VETERINARY RES INST CHINA AGRIC (CAGS-C)
GA 2020A3195C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing recombinant baculovirus rBacNT1L2B based on the simulation results of computer software comprises embedding SAT2 foot-and-mouth disease virus (FMDV) B cell epitope (VYTKAAAAIRGDRAALAAKYADTNHTLPPTFNFGYVTVDK) in Loop2 region of porcine parvovirus PPV VP2 protein, N-terminal embedded T cell epitope (NVQEGRRKHTDVAFLLDRST), according to the optimization of insect cell tropism, synthesizing the chimeric gene GS1923OP, cloning into the vector pFastBacTMDual, transforming competent cells DH10Bac, obtaining the recombinant bacmid rBacmidNT1L2B, transfect sf9 cells, and harvesting the recombinant bacmid rBacNT1L2B.
   USE - The porcine parvovirus-like particles displaying the B cell epitope and T cell epitope of South Africa type 2 foot-and-mouth disease virus is useful in preparing of SAT2 foot-and-mouth disease virus (FMDV) vaccine, and preparing novel PPV vaccines (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using the strain rBacNT1L2B to infect HF cells to express the recombinant protein NT1L2B culturing at 27 degrees C without carbon dioxide in a cell shaker at 125 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 72 hours, collecting cells by centrifugation, and obtaining recombinant protein by sonication and centrifuging using sucrose density gradient to collect the sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) amplification of NT1L2B gene and construction of recombinant donor plasmid according to insect cell tropism, optimizing and synthesizing the plasmid GS1923OP containing chimeric sequence, and designing primers P1/P2 using the bioinformatics software Primer 5 to amplify the chimeric gene NT1L2B The target band NT1L2B was recovered and purifying, digesting the target fragment and pFastBacTMDual vector with BamH I and Pst I respectively, cleaning and ligating, chemically transferring to DH5 alpha competent cells, screening positive clones, and the recombinant donor plasmid pFastN, extracting T1L2B, carrying out restriction enzyme digestion and sequencing identification, primer sequence and restriction digestion site as given in the table 1, (ii) constructing and identifying of NT1L2B recombinant shuttle plasmid, transforming the pFastNT1L2B donor plasmid into DH10Bac competent cells, which contains gentamicin (7 mu g/ml), kanamycin (50 mu g/ml), tetracycline (10 mu g/ml), IPTG (40 mu g/ml) and X-gal (100 mu g/ml) on solid Luria broth (LB)plates, inverted culture at 37 degrees C for 48 hours for blue-white screening, pick white colonies and placing shake bacterium in liquid medium containing three antibiotics, extracting DNA, performing PCR detection with M13F/R (Table 1) vector universal primers, positive clone bacmids were screened, named rBacmidNT1L2B, and extracting rBacmidNT1L2B recombinant bacmids for Cell transfection, and (iii) obtaining and identification of NT1L2B recombinant baculovirus, referring to the instructions of the liposome transfection kit Cellfectin-II Reagent, transfecting the rBacmidNT1L2B bacmid into well-growing sf9 cells, and culture in a constant temperature incubator at 28 degrees C for 36-72 hours, appearing the cytopathic condition, collecting the supernatant to obtain the positive recombinant baculovirus, named rBacNT1L2B. Use the strain rBacNT1L2B to infect HF cells to express the recombinant protein NT1L2B, and culture at 27 degrees C without carbon dioxide in a cell shaker at 125 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 72 hours. The cells were collected by centrifugation, and the recombinant protein was obtained by sonication and centrifugation using sucrose density gradient to collect the sample.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.

   ADVANTAGE - The baffle rotates under the action of the ejection airflow and covers the ejection tube and completes emergency closure. The flow rate of the jet airflow is suddenly reduced to zero, without affecting the tail flow of the engine and the effect of carbon dioxide gas fire extinguishing is ensured.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device structure in a normal test drive state. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Hinge (2)
   Support frame (3)
   Tail nozzle (4)

PN US2020282363-A1; WO2020180353-A1; US11135556-B2; CN113613763-A; EP3934796-A1
TI Polymer membrane for gas separation comprises crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer.
AU HARRIGAN D J
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020880861
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer membrane (1) comprises crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer (I) (10).
   USE - A polymer membrane for gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer membrane has a balance of suitable levels of thermal stability, pressure stability, permeability and selectivity in pure gas streams, and permeability and selectivity in mixed gas streams. Moreover, the polymer membrane can be produced at moderate temperatures (i.e., less than or equal to 50 degrees C), without the use of a catalyst, and with minimal or no amounts of solvent. Since carbon dioxide (CO2) has a favorable affinity to the ether bonds in the crosslinked polyethylene glycol (PEG) backbone, increasing the PEG chain length leads to higher permeability and also increases the likelihood of the interactions with CO2, eliciting improved selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polymer membrane comprises crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer of formula (I).
   l, m, n=1-50.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) gas separation which involves flowing a gas stream through a polymer membrane comprising the crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer (I); and
   (2) production of polymer membrane which involves reacting polyethylene glycol oligomer and methylidynetri-p-phenylene triisocyanate to produce polymer (I) and casting polymer (I) onto a filtration membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional diagram of a polymer membrane.
   Polymer membrane (1)
   Crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer (10)
   Filtration membrane (20)
   Polyester support layer (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Gas stream comprises methane (CH4), carbon dioxide (CO2), and/or hydrogen sulfide (H2S). Gas stream comprises greater than or equal to 4 parts per million by volume (ppmv) H2S. Preferred Properties: Polymer membrane has CO2/CH4 selectivity of greater than or equal to 10. Polymer membrane has H2S/CH4 selectivity of greater than or equal to 30.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Polymer membrane further comprises filtration membrane, which acts as a support for crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer. Filtration membrane comprises polyacrylonitrile. Filtration membrane further comprises a polyester support layer. Preferred Properties: Filtration membrane has molecular weight cut-off of 100000-200000 Daltons. Crosslinked polyethylene glycol network polymer has a thickness of 10-100 mu m. Preferred Condition: Polyethylene glycol oligomer and methylidynetri-p-phenylene triisocyanate are reacted for less than or equal to 100 minutes. Preferred Processes: Casting comprises knife casting. Production method further comprises dissolving the polyethylene glycol oligomer in an amount of an aprotic solvent before combining the polyethylene glycol oligomer and the methylidynetri-p-phenylene triisocyanate.
   Preferred Definitions:
   l, m, n=13-23.

PN CN111528268-A; CN111528268-B
TI Preserving meat based on gas protection by pretreating fresh meat blocks, spraying preservative containing olive leaf extract, performing pressure-holding treatment, vacuumizing, filling with mixed gas, refrigerating and preserving product.
AU XU J
   WU J
   WU C
AE UNIV BENGBU (UBBU-C)
   UNIV BENGBU (UBBU-C)
GA 202080090U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving meat based on gas protection involves (a) pretreating by precooling fresh meat blocks at 0-4 degrees C for 12-24 hours, (b) performing surface treatment by spraying a 0.02-0.06 ml/g preservative evenly on the surface of fresh meat at 2-5 degrees C under pressure of 0.5-1.5 MPa and performing pressure-holding treatment for 15-30 minutes, where the preservative comprises 2.5-8 %mass olive leaf extract, 1.6-6 %mass orange peel extract, 5.5-10 %mass epsilon -polylysine, 0.4-1 %mass sodium alginate and remainder water, and (c) preserving in modified atmosphere by placing the processed meat in a modified atmosphere packaging box, vacuumizing, filling with a mixed gas comprising 45-60% carbon dioxide, 30-40% nitrogen and 10-15% oxygen to a pressure of 0.12-0.5 MPa, refrigerating and preserving at 0-4 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving meat based on gas protection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves fresh-keeping effect and quality of the meat, reduces juice loss rate, provides obvious bacteriostatic effects and prolongs shelf life of meat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Component: The meat blocks are one of poultry meat block, livestock meat block and fish meat block. Preferred Method: The olive leaf extract is prepared by grinding the fresh olive leaves, adding 60-85% ethanol solution in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g/10 ml, performing ultrasonic-assisted extraction for 6 hours, centrifuging, adding 40-50% ethanol solution in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g/10 ml, performing reflux extraction at 80 degrees C for 3 hours, combining obtained extracts, purifying by S-8 type macroporous adsorption resin chromatography, eluting using 90% ethanol solution, performing rotary evaporation and vacuum-drying obtained product. The orange peel extract is prepared by grinding the fresh orange peel, adding 60-85% ethanol solution in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g/10 ml, performing ultrasonic-assisted extraction for 6 hours, centrifuging, adding 40-50% ethanol solution in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g/10 ml, performing reflux extraction at 80 degrees C for 3 hours, combining obtained extracts, purifying by S-8 type macroporous adsorption resin chromatography, eluting using 90% ethanol solution, performing rotary evaporation and vacuum-drying obtained product.

PN CN211233965-U
TI Silicon carbide grinding wheel sintering high temperature resistant shed plate, has channel extending from circumferential side of locating hole to periphery of plate main body, where upper and lower surfaces of main body are provided with through holes.
AU WANG T
AE LIAONING CHENGRUI GRINDING WHEEL CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 202078947T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a silicon carbide grinding wheel sintering special high temperature resistant shed plate, relating to the technical field of silicon carbide firing. The utility model comprises a shed plate main body; the surface of the shed plate main body is provided with a locating hole; the upper surface of the shed plate main body is provided with a channel extending from the peripheral side of the locating hole to the periphery of the shed plate main body; between the upper and lower surfaces of the shed plate main body is provided with uniformly staggered ventilating through holes. The special high temperature resistant shed plate of the utility model, the channel on the plate and the vent hole make the grinding wheel fully contact with the oxygen in the kiln, the carbon in the blank reacts with the oxygen to generate carbon dioxide discharge blank, so that the silicon carbide grinding wheel apparent quality is improved, at the same time, the vent hole reduces the weight of the shed plate itself, It greatly reduces the labor intensity of workers.

TI Supported alkali catalyst useful in preparing carbon stable isotope-labeled compound, comprises two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix and small molecule catalyst supported on two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix.
AU XIN Z
   HUANG Y
AE JIANGSU XINGNUO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202076577E
AB    NOVELTY - Supported alkali catalyst comprises two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix and small molecule catalyst supported on two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix, where the small molecule catalyst is located between two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix layer in a supramolecular intercalation manner and the two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix is hydrotalcite, hydrotalcite-type or montmorillonite.
   USE - The supported alkali catalyst is useful in preparing carbon stable isotope-labeled compound in stable carbon isotope-labeled carbon dioxide gas by molecular direct insertion method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported alkali catalyst: does not easy to fall off from the carrier; can be reusable; is suitable for recycling organic alkali catalyst; improves repetitive utilization rate of organic alkali catalyst; and reduces synthesis step and thus reduces reaction cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing supported alkali catalyst, comprising preparing precursor solution, adding column supporting layering agent into precursor solution to obtain two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix solution, adding organic alkali catalyst into two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix solution and performing supramolecular intercalation to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The small molecule catalyst is at least one organic alkali catalyst. The divalent soluble salt is soluble salt of zinc, cobalt, iron, magnesium, nickel and/or manganese, the trivalent soluble salt is soluble salt of aluminum and/or chromium, and the alkaline substance is aqueous ammonia, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, urea or hexamethylenetetramine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The column supporting layering agent is hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and/or sodium dodecyl sulfate. The organic alkali catalyst is 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 1,5-diazabicyclo (4.3.0)non-5-ene, 1,8-diazabicyclo (5.4.0)undec-7-ene, 2,6-lutidine, 4-(dimethylamino)pyridine and/or N,N,N',N'-tetramethylnaphthalene-1,8-diamine. Preferred Method: The method comprises when two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix is hydrotalcite, then mixing soluble salt of divalent metal and soluble salt of trivalent metal with alkaline substance to obtain mixed solution having a pH of 7-13, and reacting at 80-160 degrees C for 8-48 hours to obtain catalyst precursor solution having a pH of 7-13, adding column supporting layering agent into precursor solution to obtain column supporting layer hydrotalcite solution, adding organic alkali catalyst into column supporting layer hydrotalcite solution and performing supramolecular intercalation to obtain final product. The method comprises when two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix is hydrotalcite-type, then mixing soluble salt of divalent metal and soluble salt of trivalent metal with alkaline substance to obtain mixed solution having a pH of 7-13, and reacting at 80-160 degrees C for 8-48 hours to obtain catalyst precursor solution having a pH of 7-13, adding column supporting layering agent into precursor solution to obtain column supporting layer hydrotalcite-type solution, adding organic alkali catalyst into column supporting layer hydrotalcite-type solution and performing supramolecular intercalation to obtain final product. The method comprises when two-dimensional inorganic clay nano-material matrix is montmorillonite, then preparing precursor solution, adding column supporting layering agent into precursor solution to obtain column supporting layer montmorillonite solution, adding organic alkali catalyst into column supporting layer montmorillonite solution and performing supramolecular intercalation to obtain final product, where the precursor solution is montmorillonite solution.

The decomposition rate of the nickel-zion complex in the first step is more than 80%.

PN CN111318276-A
TI Preparing gallium oxide photocatalytic material comprises taking gallium oxide and potassium carbonate, fully grinding and mixing uniformly, cooling to room temperature, stirring, heating, centrifuging, rinsing and cycling.
AU YAN S
   HUANG L
   LIU J
   WU C
   ZOU Z
AE UNIV NANJING KUNSHAN INNOVATION INST (UNAJ-C)
GA 202059664V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gallium oxide photocatalytic material comprises (a) taking gallium oxide and potassium carbonate in a mass ratio of (0.3-1):(1:0.3), (b) fully grinding and mixing uniformly in a mortar, (c) placing in a muffle furnace and heating at 750-900 degrees C for 2-12 hours, (d) cooling to room temperature to obtain solid phase sintered powder, (e) taking 0.1-5 g solid phase sintered powder dropwise into 50 ml deionized water and stirring for 10 minutes to obtain colloidal solution, (f) adding 0.1-0.5 g glacial acetic acid dropwise to the colloidal solution, (g) stirring and heating in 80 degrees C water bath condition, (h) centrifuging for 10 minutes to precipitate solids, rinsing with deionized water, and cycling for 3-5 times, (i) placing into a crucible in 60-80 degrees C oven and drying for 3-5 hours to obtain gallium oxide nanosheet material, and(j) placing the gallium oxide nanosheet material in a tube furnace for 3 hours and annealing with oxygen.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing gallium oxide photocatalytic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficiency of photo catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (j), the method comprises annealing at 550-750 degrees C. In the step (g), stirring and heating in 80 degrees C water bath for 3-12 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The medicament                  contains an effective dose of the derivative                  represented by (I). The medicine is an oral                  preparation or an injection preparation, and the                  oral preparation is selected from one of dripping                  pills, tablets, capsules, granules, or oral                  liquids. The injection preparation is selected                  from injections or powder injections. The carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction conditions are                  pressure of the extraction kettle is 20-30                  megapascal (MPa), temperature of the extraction                  kettle is 35-60 degrees C. The pressure of the                  separation kettle I is 8-11 MPa, temperature of                  the separation kettle I is 35-65 degrees C. The                  pressure of the separation kettle II is 3-6 MPa,                  temperature of the separation kettle II is 30-40                  degrees C. The amount of ethanol used in ethanol                  extraction is 15-25% of the inflorescence weight                  of hemp plants, and the extraction time is 30-60                  minutes. The crude extract is fully dissolved                  with petroleum ether. The normal-phase silica gel                  column chromatography accordingly uses 100%                  petroleum ether, petroleum ether: ethyl acetate                  is 100: 1, petroleum ether: ethyl acetate is 10:                  1, ethyl acetate 100% as the eluent for gradient                  elution. The first-stage reverse-phase silica gel                  column chromatography uses methanol/ solution as                  the eluent for gradient elution, preferably                  70-100% (v/v) methanol/ aqueous solution. The                  second-stage reverse phase silica gel column                  chromatography uses methanol/aqueous solution as                  the eluent for gradient elution, preferably                  75-100% (v/v) methanol/ aqueous solution.

The sealed wear-resistant steel tank is wrapped tightly with insulation cotton to insert a thermometer between outer wall of the ball mill tank and the insulation cotton to monitor the temperature of the tank body. AU SAKUMA Y
   NAKAZAWA J
AE ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
GA 202013974T
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of lithium from a lithium-ion battery involves forming an opening portion in the housing in salt water, baking the housing in which opening is formed in housing at a temperature of 650-720 degrees C for a predetermined time in an inert atmosphere, crushing the lithium-ion battery which is baked at 650-720 degrees C and sieving to obtain crude lithium carbonate, adding a metal hydroxide solution capable of producing a hardly soluble carbonate to the crude lithium carbonate to obtain a lithium hydroxide solution, and recovering the precipitated lithium carbonate by supplying carbon dioxide to the lithium ion battery.
