AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing piceatannol involves (i)                selecting passion fruit, grape and blueberry,                cleaning with sterile ionized water, taking the                pulp, crushing, filtering to obtain juice and                residue, (ii) adding 60-80% acetone to the filtered                juice, performing microwave extraction for 6-10                minutes, cooling to room temperature to obtain an                extract, (iii) adding 80-100% acetone to the filter                residue, performing microwave extraction for 4-6                minutes, cooling to room temperature, and filtering                to obtain a crude extract, (iv) combining the                extract and crude extract, and concentrating under                reduced pressure to obtain concentrated solution,                (v) passing the concentrated solution through                silica gel column and eluting with anhydrous                acetone to obtain an eluent and (vi) freeze-drying                the eluate to obtain piceatannol powder or                subjecting the eluate to carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction to obtain piceatannol                liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing piceatannol                powder used in anti-leukemia drug and preparing                piceatannol liquid used in health products or                cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method short in time, low in energy                consumption, and suitable for industrial                production, greatly improves the product yield and                extraction amount of piceatannol reaches 2200                mg/kg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the                  weight ratio of passion fruit, grape and                  blueberry is (6-8):(1-2):(1-2). In step (ii), the                  weight ratio of filtered juice to 60-80% acetone                  is 1:(6-8). In step (iii), the weight ratio of                  filter residue to 80-100% acetone is 1:(4-6). In                  step (iv), the volume of the concentrated                  solution is 1/20-1/15 the volume of combined                  solution of extract and crude extract. Preferred                  Conditions: In steps (ii) and (iii), the power of                  the microwave is 200-250 W.

PN CN110721690-A; CN110721690-B
TI Nickel iron bimetal multifunctional catalyst used for hydrogen production by steam reforming of bio oil and water includes nickel oxide, iron oxide and composite calcium zirconium alumina carrier.
AU SUN L
   WANG Z
   HUA D
   CHEN L
   ZHAO B
   YANG S
   SI H
   XIE X
AE ENERGY RES INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 2020106101
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel iron bimetal multifunctional catalyst comprises composite calcium zirconium alumina carrier, nickel oxide as main active component, iron oxide as additive active component and calcium oxide-zirconium oxide modified aluminum oxide Al2O3 carrier. The calcium oxide-zirconium oxide modified aluminum oxide carrier comprises 5-20 wt.% nickel oxide, 5-20 wt.% iron oxide and 60-90 wt.% composite calcium zirconium alumina support. The molar ratio of calcium, zirconium and aluminum in composite carrier is 1:1:1-5:10:10. The composite calcium zirconium alumina support is obtained by impregnation. The composite calcium zirconium alumina support is prepared by impregnation of nickel oxide and iron oxide.
   USE - The nickel iron bimetal multifunctional catalyst is used for hydrogen production by steam reforming of bio oil and water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves anti sintering and anti carbon deposition performance of the active component of the catalyst. The interaction between the active components is enhanced by forming nickel iron alloy, improves dispersion of active component nickel, inhibits agglomeration and sintering of nickel in high temperature reaction. The iron promotes transformation of carbon monoxide by-product. The calcium oxide-zirconium oxide improves thermal stability and carbon dioxide absorption of aluminum oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of calcium, zirconium and aluminum in composite calcium zirconium alumina carrier is 5:5:10. Preferred Method: The preparation of multifunctional catalyst comprises dissolving nitrate of soluble calcium salt and zirconium salt in deionized water, mixing with alumina powder, impregnating, drying to remove water, calcining, activating in muffle furnace, grinding, sieving, cooling, mixing composite calcium zirconium alumina support with nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate solution, impregnating, drying, dehydrating, calcining, activating in muffle furnace, grinding, sieving, cooling, mixing sample with iron (III) nitrate nonahydrate solution, impregnating, drying, calcining, activating in muffle furnace, cooling, grinding and sieving.

AB    NOVELTY - Performing pyrolytic carbon black modification method, where carbon nanotubes are supported on the surface of pyrolytic carbon black, comprisesv weighing the carbon nanotubes and pyrolytic carbon black, and pouring the carbon nanotubes and pyrolytic carbon black into the reaction kettle respectively in order. Tighten the lid of the reactor and place it in the heating chamber of the heating and stirring controller. After raising the temperature to the set temperature, fill in carbon dioxide to the set pressure, then set the stirring parameters and turn on the stirring. After stirring, let stand for 5 minutes, release the pressure and open the kettle to obtain a carbon nanotube / pyrolysis carbon black composite material.

PN KR2063170-B1
TI Producing environmentally friendly film used in plastic bag, comprises mixing recycled resin pellet and shellfish master batch with polyethylene pellets, melting, and extruding mixed composition.
AU KIMMISOON
AE KIMMISOON (KIMM-Individual)
GA 202004787G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing environmentally friendly film comprises (a) producing recycled resin pellets, (b) producing shellfish master batch by mixing 1-2 mu m sized crushed shellfish shells with lubricant, where the shellfish master batch is polyethylene resin, (c) mixing 40-50 pts. wt. recycled resin pellet and 4-5 pts. wt. shellfish master batch with 100 pts. wt. polyethylene pellets to produce mixed composition, and (d) melting and extruding the mixed composition to produce film. The lubricant includes zinc, stearic acid, zinc acid and/or magnesium in form of wax. The step (c) comprise mixing 0.2-2 pts. wt. phenyl- beta -naphthylamine, 1-3 pts. wt. liquid paraffin oil, 2-4 pts. wt. ethylene vinyl acetate copolymer resin, 12-21 pts. wt. vinyl acetate and 3-5 pts. wt. alkali agent.
   USE - The method is useful for producing environmentally friendly film (all claimed) which is used in processed product i.e. plastic bag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces amount of carbon dioxide generated, provides film having gloss effect, can be easily and quickly introduced by existing film manufacturers, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for environmentally film prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (a) further comprises (a-1) grinding waste vinyl scrap to size of 1.5-2.5 cm, (a-2) melting the pulverized waste vinyl scrap at 250-270 degrees C to produce recycled resin, (a-3) extruding the produced recycled resin with extruder, and (a-4) cooling the extruded recycled resin and cutting into size of 0.4-0.6 cm. The step (b) further comprises (b-1) pulverizing shellfish shell to size of 1-2 mu m, (b-2) mixing 11.5-23 pts. wt. crushed shellfish shell, 3-4 pts. wt. lubricant and 100 pts. wt. polyethylene resin to produce shellfish composition, (b-3) melting and extruding the resulting shellfish composition at 150-180 degrees C, and (b-4)cooling and cutting the extruded shellfish composition. The step (d) further comprises (d-1) supplying composition in which the recycled resin pellets and the shellfish master batch are mixed into polyethylene pellets in mixer into cylinder, (d-2) heating at 170-180 degrees C using heater provided outside the cylinder to melt the mixed composition inside the cylinder, and supplying the melted composition inside the cylinder to die using transfer screw, (d-3) extruding the molten composition into tubular film in the die, (d-4) cooling the outer surface of the extruded tubular film using cooling air ring, (d-5) guiding narrowed diameter of the cooled tubular film using guide, (d-6) pressing the tubular film passed through the guide into plate-shaped film using primary roller, and (d-7) pressing the plate-shaped film using secondary roller and winding the film by using winding machine. Preferred Components: The mixer includes dehumidifier for removing the water contained in the recycled resin pellets, the shellfish master batch and the polyethylene pellets.
   EXAMPLE - Typical environmentally friendly film comprised of 100 kg polyethylene resin, 45 kg recycled resin pellets and 4.5 kg shellfish master batch.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the elastomer rubber comprising weighing raw materials; melting thermoplastic polyurethane at 180-220 degrees C, adding ethylene propylene diene monomer thermoplastic elastomer and hydrogenated carboxybutyronitrile, and mixing into rubber compound; adding additives, stirring for 10-25 minutes, adding dioctyl phthalate, dibutyl formate, dioctyl adipate, o-hydroxybenzophenone, and UV absorber, stirring, adding reinforcing filler, and continue stirring for 5-8 minutes; adding silane coupling agent and calcium sulfide and stirring at 170-240 degrees C for 25-40 minutes; extrusion molding at 150-161 degrees C; and extruding to 165-170 degrees C, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide, blending, and vacuum extruding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reinforcing filler comprises 36 wt.% nano aluminum oxide, 15 wt.% nano titanium dioxide, 38 wt.% nano silicon dioxide, and 11 wt.% nano graphite powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The elastomer rubber comprises 66 wt.% thermoplastic polyurethane, 12 wt.% ethylene propylene diene monomer thermoplastic elastomer, 5 wt.% hydrogenated carboxybutyronitrile, 0.6 wt.% dioctyl phthalate, 0.4 wt.% dibutyl phthalate, 0.3 wt.% dioctyl adipate, 0.6 wt.% o-hydroxybenzophenone, 0.3 wt.% UV absorber, 8 wt.% reinforcing filler, 0.8 wt.% silane coupling agent, and 6 wt.% calcium sulfide. The ethylene propylene diene monomer thermoplastic elastomer comprises 70 wt.% ethylene propylene diene monomer and 30 wt.% polypropylene.

PN ID201901781-S
TI Raw material composition and binary catalyst useful for synthesis of diethyl carbonate, comprises butylene oxide and ethanol, and catalyst comprising potassium iodide and sodium ethoxide or zeolite.
AU PEBRIANI S
   ASYLA D
   ADIAN F
   GUNAWAN S
   WIBAWA G
   KUNIAWANSYAH F
   TETRISYANDA R
   WIGUNO A
AE PT PERTAMINA PERSERO (PERT-Non-standard)
   INST TEKNOLOGI SEPULUH NOPEMBER ITS (TEKN-Non-standard)
GA 202030313H
AB    NOVELTY - Raw material composition and binary catalyst comprises butylene oxide and ethanol in a mol ratio of (1:5)-(1:15), and catalyst comprising potassium iodide and sodium ethoxide or zeolite catalyst in a mass ratio of (1:1)-(1:5).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesis of diethyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves yield of diethyl carbonate, which improves exhaust emission of combustion in vehicles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for synthesizing diethyl carbonate involving (a) carrying out pre-ethanol dehydration treatment using zeolite, (b) preparing a binary catalyst mixture comprising potassium iodide catalyst and a sodium ethoxide or zeolite catalyst, (c) mixing the binary catalyst mixture with liquid reactant in a reactor equipped with a heater and stirrer, (d) introducing carbon dioxide gas reactant into the reactor at a reactor initial pressure of 3-4 MPa, (e) heating the reactor and maintaining at 150-170 degrees C for 1-7 hours, and (f) reducing the reactor temperature to room temperature and slowly reducing the reactor pressure to obtain the product; and
   (2) a by-product resulting from the synthesis of diethyl carbonate, comprising 1,2-butanediol, butylene carbonate and butylene glycol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of moles of butylene oxide to moles of ethanol is 1:15. The mass ratio of the potassium iodide catalyst and catalyst of sodium ethoxide or zeolite is 1:3. The binary catalyst for the total mass of liquid reactants is 1:90.5. Preferred Method: The step (e) involves heating the reactor at 170 degrees C and a reactor initial pressure of 3 MPa for 5 hours. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a potassium iodide-zeolite binary catalyst, or a potassium iodide-sodium ethoxide binary catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2019111539-A
TI Water gas shift reactor comprises e.g. reactor provided with heat exchanger including shell heat exchange member and multiple tubes, a water spray line located at lower portion of reactor and provided with gas header.
AU PYO H J
   IN O
   SUH D
   CHI J
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
   KOREA WESTERN POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
GA 201983948Y
AB    NOVELTY - Water gas shift reactor comprises a reactor (101) for producing gas composition comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and inflowing synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide from gas inlet line (10) and provided with heat exchanger including catalyst layer for water gas conversion, and a water spray line located at a lower portion of the reactor and having multiple spray nozzles for injecting water into the heat exchanger lower portion. The synthesis gas is in contact with the injected water to produce a first gas mixture. The first gas mixture flows into the lower portion of the heat exchanger and is heated by heat exchange and catalyzed to produce gas composition.
   USE - The water gas shift reactor is useful in hydrogen fuel cell type of power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The water gas shift reactor effectively reduces temperature of reactor through heat exchange, provides water gas conversion reaction efficiency, can be simplified and miniaturized and improves reaction rate and minimizes steam and power consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the water gas shift reactor.
   Gas inlet line (11)
   Thermometer (13)
   Flow meter (15)
   Gas discharge line (21)
   Reactor (101)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The water spray line is further provided with gas header formed along the spray nozzle. The gas header is connected to the gas inlet line to introduce the synthesis gas into the lower portion of the heat exchanger. The heat exchanger includes shell heat exchange member, multiple tubes provided inside the heat exchange member, and catalyst layer formed by filling a catalyst between the heat exchange member and the tube. The lower portion of the tube is opened to introduce the first gas mixture. The lower portion of the tube is opened to introduce the first gas mixture, and the first gas mixture moves to an upper portion of the tube. The heated first gas mixture inside the catalyst layer is heated through heat exchange and the heated first gas mixture flows into the upper portion of the catalyst layer and moves downward, and is heated by a catalytic reaction to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The heat exchanger includes a plate heat exchange member having multiple plates which are spaced apart, and a catalyst layer formed by filling a catalyst in the spaced space of the heat exchange member. The first gas mixture flows into lower portion of the heat exchange member and moves upwards, and heated through heat exchange with a heated first gas mixture moving the catalyst bed. The temperature of the catalyst layer is 270-450 degrees C. The temperature of the first gas mixture flowing into the catalyst layer is 200-350 degrees C. The reactor further includes water separator at its bottom portion of the reactor to remove water that is not in contact with the synthesis gas from the heat exchanger. The gas composition is discharged to the outside through a gas discharge line (21) provided under the catalyst layer of the heat exchanger. The water gas conversion reactor is provided with flow rate control valve provided in the water injection line to control the injection amount of the water, flow rate meter provided in the gas inlet line for measuring a flow rate of the syngas, and/or thermometer (13) provided at an upper portion of the heat exchanger. The first gas mixture flowing into the heat exchanger lowers water and carbon monoxide in a molar ratio of 1:1-3:1.

AE GANZHOU NONFERROUS METALLURGY INST (GANZ-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity rare earth oxide                comprises adding seed crystals to the reactor, then                subjecting rare earth chloride solution and a                composite precipitant solution to the reactor for                precipitation reaction, then aging, carrying out                liquid-solid separation and washing, obtaining rare                earth carbonate, burning the rare earth carbonate,                obtaining high purity rare earth oxide, where the                precipitating agent in the composite precipitant                solution comprises sodium hydrogencarbonate and                sodium carbonate, the mass ratio of sodium                hydrogencarbonate and sodium carbonate is 0.2-10:1,                and the quality content of REO in the high-purity                rare earth oxide is less than or equal to                99%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                rare earth oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the generation of carbon                dioxide during the precipitation of sodium                hydrogencarbonate, reduces the risk of bubble buoy,                and prevents the alkalinity of sodium carbonate                from being too strong; forms rare earth hydroxide                into a paste-type product; adopts the rare earth                carbonate having good crystallinity, stable crystal                form, narrow particle size distribution and low                impurity content; and obtains the high-purity rare                earth oxide after burning is superior to the                national standard.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The total concentration of                  sodium hydrogencarbonate and sodium carbonate in                  composite precipitant solution is 50-200 g/l. The                  concentration of REO in rare earth chloride                  liquid is 0.2-1.5 mol/l and the rare earth                  chloride liquid has a pH of 0.5-2.5. The seed                  crystal comprises rare earth carbonate and/or                  oxalic acid rare earth. The added amount of seed                  crystal is 2-20% of the total weight of rare                  earth, the mass content of total rare earth REO                  of rare earth chloride material in the liquid.                  The rare earth chloride liquid and composite                  precipitant solution are added, the pH of the                  mixed liquid in the reactor is controlled to be                  4-5.5. The final pH of the precipitation reaction                  is 6.5-7.5. The aging time is 2-24 hours. The                  burning temperature is 850-1000 degrees C, and                  the time is 1.5-3 hours.

PN CN110106362-A
TI Recycling lithium carbonate or cobalt oxide from cathode material of lithium cobalt oxide battery comprises e.g. crushing waste lithium ion battery to obtain lithium cobaltate powder, drying, and subjecting vacuum thermal decomposition.
AU TIAN Y
   WANG S
   YANG B
   XU B
   LIU D
   CHEN X
   WANG F
   DENG Y
   QU T
   LI Y
   JIANG W
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019706573
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling lithium carbonate or cobalt oxide from cathode material of lithium cobalt oxide battery comprises mechanically crushing the waste lithium ion battery to obtain lithium cobaltate powder, drying lithium cobaltate powder to obtain dried material, subjecting the dried material to vacuum thermal decomposition to obtain vacuum thermal decomposition product, mixing vacuum thermal decomposition product with water, then separating by solid-liquid separation to obtain cobalt oxide, and solution, introducing carbon dioxide into solution, and evaporating the crystals to obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling lithium carbonate or cobalt oxide from cathode material of lithium cobalt oxide battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides brand-new idea for recycling cobalt oxide and lithium carbonate from waste lithium cobalt oxide battery; has low temperature of vacuum thermal decomposition, and no problem of secondary oxidation of air under vacuum conditions; is clean and pollution-free; provides selection basis for thermal decomposition temperature and holding time of cobalt oxide and lithium carbonate recycled from waste lithium ion battery; and has short process flow, low reaction temperature, low process energy consumption, and basically no impact of the closed system on the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The vacuum thermal decomposition pressure is 1-100 Pa, temperature is 800-1200 degrees C, preferably 1000-1200 degrees C, and heat preservation time is 0.5-2.5 hours, preferably 1.5-2 hours. The drying temperature is 80-100 degrees C, and time is 0.5-2 hours. The temperature of the evaporating, and crystallizing is 25-50 degrees C, and time is 1-4 hours. The vacuum thermal decomposition further comprises process of cooling to room temperature with furnace. The temperature increase rate of the temperature at which the vacuum is decomposed is 5-20 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: The particle size of cobalt powder is 8-20 mu m.

TI Converter gas upgrading involves directly introducing preheated coke oven gas into converter flue, and then entering converter gas for mixing with coke oven gas to undergo different reforming in converter flue.
AU WANG J

   ZUO H
   SHE X
   WANG G
   LIU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201966978Y
AB    NOVELTY - Converter gas upgrading involves directly introducing preheated coke oven gas into converter flue, and then entering converter gas for mixing with coke oven gas to undergo different reforming in the converter flue. The obtained reaction product gas includes hydrogen and carbon monoxide main components to increase ratio in the converter gas. The high-temperature mixing reforming process of the two gasses occurs in the converter flue. The energy required for the reforming process is provided by the high-temperature converter gas for removing normal converter vaporization flue partially or completely. The heat consumption of the low-temperature coke oven gas is increased by the heat absorption and reforming process.
   USE - Method for upgrading converter gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides converter gas upgradation in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with full utilization of the physical heat and composition in the converter gas to realize efficient recycling of the residual heat of the converter gas, reduced carbon dioxide emissions, and improvement of the utilization value of the converter gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of the mixing and reforming of converter gas and coke oven gas. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The coke oven gas includes 20 vol.% methane, and 55-60 vol.% hydrogen. The mixed gas temperature in the reforming process is adjusted at 900 degrees C. The coke oven gas is introduced into the inlet portion of the converter flue. The amount of coke oven gas introduced into the converter flue is controlled according to a change in the amount, temperature and composition of the converter gas entering the converter flue.

   (1) a method for tri-reforming methane, which involves contacting methane, carbon dioxide, water, and oxygen with the yolk-shell structured catalyst; and
   (2) a method of fabricating yolk-shell catalysts by reverse microemulsion, which involves forming a yolk mixture by mixing a precursor nickel aqueous solution, a secondary material aqueous solution, and a surfactant in a non-aqueous solvent, adding a base to the yolk mixture, heating the yolk mixture, and allowing yolks to form within the yolk mixture, adding a silica precursor to the yolk mixture to form a yolk-shell mixture, drying the yolk-shell mixture and calcinating the yolk-shell mixture to produce the yolk-shell catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The primary material (M1) includes nickel (Ni) or nickel oxide. The secondary material includes cerium (Ce), palladium (Pd), or iron (Fe). The secondary material includes ceria (CeO2). The porous shell support includes silica (SiO2). The nickel aqueous solution is a nickel nitrate solution. The secondary material solution is a cerium nitrate solution. The surfactant includes cetrimonium bromide (CTAB). The non-aqueous solvent includes butanol and cyclohexane. The silica precursor includes tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS). Preferred Compositions: The catalyst comprises 0.1-30 wt.% nickel, 0.1-10 wt.% secondary material Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is produced using a reverse microemulsion process. The catalyst has pores ranging from about 5-30 nm in diameter. The catalyst is in the form of spherical particles. The spherical particles have an average diameter of from about 10-500 nm. The catalyst is in the form of tubular particles. The tubular particles have an average diameter of from about 10-300 nm. The tubular particles have an average length of from about 0.1-5 mu m. The tubular particles have an aspect ratio (length/diameter) of from about 2-50. The catalyst has a surface area of from about 30-600 m2/g. The multiple yolks exist within the shell and each of the yolks has a diameter ranging from 10-40 nm. Preferred Process: The fabrication of yolk-shell catalysts further involves adding a polymerizing agent to the yolk mixture and adding an ammonia solution to the yolk mixture.

The step (iii) comprises stirring in the high speed mixer at 30-40 degrees C at a rate of 960-1080 revolutions/minute. PN CN109701444-A; CN109701444-B
TI Complex gas hydrate nano promoter useful for synthesis gas hydrate, hydrate storage and transportation natural gas comprises obtained by oxidized carbon nanotubes as dispersion carrier and reducing metal cation in situ by reducing agent.
AU GUO R
   SONG Y
   LUO S
   WANG F
   GUO G
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 201942392E
AB    NOVELTY - Complex gas hydrate nano promoter obtained by taking oxidized carbon nano-tubes as a dispersion carrier, combining hydrophilic anionic carboxy group on the carrier with metal cation by electrostatic adsorption, and reducing metal cation in situ by reducing agent to obtain complex nano promoter.
   USE - The nano promoter is useful in a synthesis gas hydrate, in the fields of hydrate storage and transportation natural gas (methane, hydrogen, etc.), hydrate storage carbon dioxide and separation of mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to control and accelerate formation of hydrates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a complex gas hydrate nano-promoter comprising adding metal salt solution dropwise to the oxidized carbon nanotube fluid under the protection of nitrogen, mixing, stirring and adding a reducing agent dropwise to obtain a complex hydrate nano promoter, where the metal salt solution comprises: a silver nitrate solution, a copper chloride solution, ferric chloride solution, and aluminum chloride solution, and the reducing agent is hydrazine hydrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of preparation of complex nano promoter. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, firstly reacting oxidized carbon nanotubes with a mixed acid acidified carbon nano-tubes having a mass ratio of 100-400 for 4-10 hours, filtering, washing to obtain hydrophilic oxidized carbon nano-tubes. In the process, first acidifying oxidized carbon nanotubes with a mixed acid, reacting at 50-80 degrees C for 4-10 hours, filtering and washing to obtain hydrophilic oxidized carbon nano-tubes, where the mixed acid is concentrated sulfuric acid and concentrated nitric acid, and the volume ratio is 1-3. The complex gas hydrate nano-promoter specifically prepared by: mixing hydrophilic oxidized carbon nanotubes having a concentration 0.1-1 g/l, stirring metal cation solution at a speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute, adding 1-4 times the metal ion of hydrazine by dropwise at a rate of 1-3 drops/second, reducing action of hydrazine, stirring at a rate of 100-300 revolutions/minute at room temperature to reduce metal on the carbon nano-tube carrier, where the amount of the metal cation accounts for 10-100% of carboxy group of carbon nanotube. In the synthesis of gas hydrate comprises: diluting nano promoter with deionized water to 10-100 ppm, synthesized gas hydrate at 0-10 degrees C, and pressure 0-15 MPa, and reacting for 120-300 minutes.

TI Catalyst for selective conversion of hydrocarbons            comprises first component having group VIII noble            metals, second component having e.g. alkali metals, and            third component having e.g. tin, and lead, and support            forming catalyst particle.
AU SERBAN M
   COLE M C
   ARNOLD E
   ZHU G
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201937319N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises: a first component                comprising group VIII noble metals and their                mixtures, a second component comprising alkali                metals and/or alkaline-earth metals, and a third                component comprising tin, germanium, lead, indium,                gallium, and/or thallium; and a support forming a                catalyst particle comprising many pores, where at                least 15% of the pores have an average pore                diameter of 200-350 Angstrom , and the catalyst                particle has an apparent bulk density of 0.6-0.3                g/cc.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for selective                conversion of hydrocarbons (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has an oxygen effective                diffusivity at 480 degrees C of at least                1.5/asterisk10-7 m2/second, a porosity of at least                75%, and an effective carbon dioxide diffusivity at                10 degrees C of at least 1.6/asterisk10-6                m2/second; and utilizes lower density support that                has improved coke burn benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                selective conversion of hydrocarbons, comprising                contacting a hydrocarbon at selective conversion                conditions with a catalytic composite comprising                the first component, the second component, the                third component and support forming the catalyst                particle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The apparent bulk                  density is 0.6-0.5 g/cc, preferably 0.57 g/cc. At                  least 15% of the pores having an average pore                  diameter of 200-350 Angstrom and have an average                  pore diameter of 240-280 Angstrom . The catalyst                  has mono-modal porous distribution or a bi-modal                  porous distribution. The first component is                  platinum, the second component is potassium, and                  the third component is tin. The support comprises                  silica, alumina, silica-alumina, a zeolite, a                  non-zeolitic molecular sieve, titania, and/or                  zirconia. The support comprises theta -alumina,                  gamma -alumina, eta -alumina, and/or delta                  -alumina. The noble metal is in an amount of                  0.01-5 wt.% based on the total weight. The second                  component is in an amount of 0.7-1.5 wt.% based                  on the total weight. The third component is in an                  amount of 0.01-5 wt.% based on the total weight.                  The catalyst has a median diameter of 1.8-2.2 mm.                  The catalyst particle is spherical.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2019041725-A; KR2445835-B1
TI Battery module equipped with fire extinguisher            system, used in electric vehicle, has fire extinguisher            which is driven at abnormal operating temperature            higher than normal operating temperature range to            inject fire extinguishing gas.
AU CHOI J Y
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK ON CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 201937175B
AB    NOVELTY - The module (100) has multiple battery cells                (111,112) which are stacked and arranged in a                battery cell stack (110) so as to be adjacent to                each other in a lateral direction. A fire                extinguisher (130) is located adjacent to the                battery cells. The fire extinguisher is driven at                an abnormal operating temperature higher than a                normal operating temperature range to inject fire                extinguishing gas. A shape memory alloy is provided                with nickel-titanium alloy, copper zinc alloy,                copper zinc aluminum alloy, copper cadmium alloy,                nickel aluminum alloy, copper zinc aluminum alloy                and copper aluminum nickel alloy. A digestion gas                is carbon-dioxide gas.
   USE - Battery module equipped with fire extinguisher                system, used in electric vehicle (EV), diesel                vehicle, hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) and plug-in                hybrid electric vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire extinguisher is operated even when a                heat or a fire occurs in some of the battery cells                for preventing or minimizing a delay in the                phenomenon affecting other adjacent battery                cells.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the battery module.
   Battery module (100)
   Battery cell stack (110)
   Battery cells (111,112)
   Fire extinguisher (130)
   Electrode lead (141)

TI Fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping film base film has macromolecule co-extrusion composite membrane, gas permeation rate selective permeable layer, and microporous layer.
AU HAN H
   HAN Y
AE HAN Y (HANY-Individual)
GA 201939035S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping film is two layers or more than two layers of macromolecule co-extrusion composite membrane, the base film includes at least one layer of the gas permeation rate having selective permeable layer and at least one microporous layer with high permeability, wherein the total thickness of the base film is 10-200 m; the total thickness of the impermeable layer is 1 % the total thickness of the base film 50%. The utility model of fruit and vegetable preservative film base film with a small thickness and high permeability of permeable layer microporous layer structure film imparting high gas permeability, and can be combined with different materials and the mixture ratio to prepare series having different gas permeability ratio, to satisfy all kinds of different characteristics and demand for concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide. Furthermore, the preservative film base film can effectively eliminate the harmful gas in the internal environment, and has the function of automatic bidirectional adjustment for humidity. This kind of fruit and vegetable preservative film convenient to process from raw material to finished product in a blowing or casting production unit previous time.

   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 201935201U

A motor shaft (30) is rotated with the grinding vessel. A laser particle size analysis device (3) is provided with a lighting element (4) and a generating and feeding system (5). A data processing and control system (6) is connected with the device. A production device (8) generates the grinding media from solidified cryogenic gas liquefied gas (GL). An inlet device is filled with the liquefied gas and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Device for cryogenic grinding powders i.e. actinide powders.

PT P
PN CN109602943-A
TI Preparing fascia-derived I type collagen sponge            useful in field of medicine e.g. tissue repair            comprises e.g. removing fat from bovine Achilles            tendon, providing pre-treated substances, mincing,            acetic acid, adding pepsin and extracting.
AU PAN X
   PAN R
   WU Y
   DAI L
   WANG J
AE HANGZHOU S-EVANS BIOSCIENCES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201935949L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fascia-derived I type collagen                sponge comprises (i) removing fat from bovine                Achilles tendon, except for protein components,                providing 10 g pre-treated substances, mincing, 3%                acetic acid for 48 hours, adding 1% pepsin,                extracting for 48 hours, carrying out sodium                chloride salting out to obtain crude collagen                precipitate, re-dissolving with acetic acid,                dialyzing with 0.02 m disodium hydrogen phosphate,                1% acetic acid, 0.5% acetic acid, 0.1% acetic acid                and distilled water to obtain high-purity I-type                collagen, (ii) carrying out crosslinking treatment                on collagen by chemical crosslinking method, and                carrying out freeze drying to obtain collagen                sponge and (iii) carrying out pre-product packaging                on type I collagen sponge pre-product treated and                carrying out irradiation sterilization with carbon                dioxide to obtain collagen sponge product.
   USE - The preparing fascia-derived I type collagen                sponge is useful in the field of medicine e.g.                tissue repair.
   ADVANTAGE - The collagen sponge has mechanical strength,                has certain degree of moisture permeability and is                suitable for pore size and porosity and sterile                three-dimensional structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation                  fascia-derived I type collagen sponge comprises                  (1) acquiring fresh bovine Achilles tendon and                  removing fascia, cleaning, drying, cutting the                  slices with scalpel, and mincing by tissue                  homogenizer, (2) providing 60 g bovine Achilles                  tendon mincing substance, soaking for 4 hours                  with normal saline, filtering with gauze,                  stirring for 24 hours with sodium hydroxide with                  concentration of 0.1m in refrigerator at 4                  degrees C, and replacing primary liquid every 12                  hours, (3) filtering by gauze with gauze, washing                  with normal saline for multiple times, filtering                  until pH of cleaning fluid is neutral, adding 10%                  isopropyl alcohol, stirring for 24 hours at 4                  degrees C, and changing the liquid for one time                  every 12 hours, (4) reacting for 24 hours with                  0.05 mol/l tris-hydrochloride buffer solution                  containing 4.5 mol/l sodium chloride, (5)                  filtering with gauze and washing with normal                  saline for 6 times, (6) adding small amount of                  3%(0.5M) acetic acid solution into cleaned bovine                  Achilles tendon to obtain acetic acid solution,                  stirring the mixture into viscous mixture with                  tissue homogenizer, adding 1000 ml of 3% acetic                  acid solution, swelling for 24 hours at 4 degrees                  C, filtering by gauze, taking the precipitate,                  adding 1000 ml of acetic acid with concentration                  of 0.5 M, and continuously swelling for 24 hours,                  (7) filtering by gauze, taking precipitate,                  adding 1000 ml of 0.5 M acetic acid containing 1%                  pepsase, stirring for 24 hours at 4 degrees C,                  filtering, collecting supernatant swelling                  solution, taking precipitate and continuously                  adding enzymatic hydrolysate and performing                  enzymolysis for 24 hours, (8) providing enzymatic                  hydrolysate, filtering with enzymolysis mixed                  solution gauze, collecting the supernatant and                  mixing supernatant with upper batch of enzymatic                  hydrolysate, (9) adjusting pH of enzymatic                  hydrolysate to 7 by 1 M sodium hydroxide, (10)                  slowly adding sodium hydroxide fine particles                  with final concentration of 2.7 M into neutral                  swelling solution, and allowing to stand                  overnight at 4 degrees C, (11) filtering and                  removing supernatant with gauze to obtain white                  precipitate, (12) re-dissolving collagen                  precipitate by 0.5 M acetic acid, loading                  collagen precipitate into dialysis bag at 1%,                  0.5% and 0.01% of acetic acid, dialyzing for 24                  hours with dialysis external solution, carrying                  out dialysis for 24 hours, taking 0.02 M of                  disodium hydrogen phosphate as dialysis outer                  solution, carrying out dialysis for 72 hours by                  three distilled water, and changing the liquid                  every 12 hours, (13) adding glutaraldehyde with                  final concentration of 0.05% into dialyzed                  collagen solution, stirring for 10 minutes,                  pouring into cell culture dish with the diameter                  of 10 cm, allowing to stand for 4 hours at 4                  degrees C, transferring mixture to refrigerator                  at -20 degrees C, and freezing for 8-12 hours,                  (14) drying for 36-48 hours in vacuum freeze                  drier with the temperature of -58 degrees C and                  pre-cooling, (15) washing dried collagen sponge                  with three distilled water for 8-10 times, and                  each time is 10 minutes, (16) freezing the                  cleaned sponge at -20 degrees C for 4-6 hours,                  freezing and drying for 16-24 hours and (17)                  carrying out irradiation sterilization with                  cobalt-60 after packaging.

PN RD660051-A
TI Source for use in extreme UV lithography systems, has laser arranged such that laser is focused on stream of tin droplets through opening in collector mirror, where plasma is created to subsequently generate extreme UV radiation.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201932733H
AB    NOVELTY - The source has a laser arranged such that the laser is focused on stream of tin droplets through an opening in a collector mirror, where plasma is created to subsequently generate extreme UV (EUV) radiation when laser light hits tin droplets. The laser is formed as a carbon dioxide laser, where the laser light includes a double laser system arranged to create pre-pulse (PP) to transform the tin droplets into pancake shape part and main pulse (MP) to hit the tin pancake and generate the EUV-plasma and the EUV radiation is collected and focused using a collector mirror at or proximate to the opening in a source vessel of the EUV radiation source.
   USE - Source for use in EUV lithography systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The source can introduce an intercepting-cartridge that intercepts crawling tin, so that tin supply to the critical region of the interface is suppressed or eliminated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a source.
   Position provided at layer of tin particles (10)
   Vessel wall (11)
   Critical region (12)
   EUV cone (14)
   Hydrogen gas (16, 17)

PT P
PN EP3459917-A1; WO2019063290-A1; CA3077010-A1; EP3687950-A1; US2020262751-A1; EP3687950-B1; US11111177-B2; ES2884782-T3
TI Manufacturing binder with high beta-belite content, by providing starting material (e.g. quartz, sand, or gravel), hydrothermal treatment of starting material in autoclave, and annealing intermediate product in flash calciner.
AU BEN HAHA M
   ITUL A
   BEN H M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
GA 2019276748
AB    NOVELTY - Binder with high beta -belite content is manufactured by: (a) providing a starting material by selecting one raw material having calcium (Ca)/silicon (Si) molar ratio of 1.5-2.5 or by mixing raw materials to obtain a starting material with the Ca/Si molar ratio of 1.5-2.5; (b) hydrothermal treatment of the starting material in an autoclave at 100-300 degrees C and a retention time of 0.1-24 hours, where the water/solid ratio is 0.1-100 to provide an intermediate product; and (c) annealing the intermediate product in a flash calciner at 620-630 degrees C. The retention time is 1-30 s.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing a binder with high beta -belite content (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Method is provided for the production of binders, with which an increased level of reactivity of the binder that is based on a belite phase can be achieved, to produce high-performance cements having a high content of these phases. Thus, substantially lower carbon dioxide emissions can be achieved than with conventional Portland cements that have a high alite proportion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of Ca to Si in the starting material is 2. Preferred Components: Primary and/or secondary raw materials are used to provide the starting material, preferably quartz, sand, gravel, limestone, portlandite (Ca(OH)2), burnt lime, unburnt lime, silicious and calcareous fly ash, and grain fractions from the reprocessing of cement-containing binders in building materials such as old concretes and cements. The raw material(s) is(are) optimized with respect to particle size and particle size distribution by mechanical or thermal treatment. Preferred Compositions: The additional elements, also in the form of compounds like oxides, especially sodium, potassium, boron, sulfur, phosphorus or its combinations, are added in an amount of 0.1-30 wt.% to the raw material or during the mixing of the raw materials, for hydrothermal treatment or for annealing. The starting material is seeded with 0.01-30 wt.% seed nuclei containing calcium silicate hydrate like alpha -C2SH, afwillite, and calcio-chondrodite. Preferred Conditions: The raw materials or the starting material is burnt at 400-1400 degrees C, preferably 750-1100 degrees C, for 0.1-6 hours, preferably 1 hour. Hydrothermal treatment occurs at 150-250 degrees C, with a water/solids ratio of 2-20, and retention times of 1-16 hours. The intermediate product is annealed at 625 degrees C with a retention time of 1-3 s.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN109430394-A
TI Low-alcohol Lactobacillus beverage comprises water, kefir fermented dairy material e.g. white granulated sugar, stabilizer e.g. soluble soybean polysaccharide, sweet substance e.g. beet sugar, and acidity regulator e.g. citric acid.
AU LI H
   QIAO C
   LIU Z
   MEI F
AE BRIGHT DAIRY IND CO LTD (GMRY-C)
GA 201925079K
AB    NOVELTY - A low-alcohol Lactobacillus beverage comprises 17.6-50 wt.% kefir fermented dairy material, 0.25-0.6 wt.% stabilizer, 0.03-10 wt.% sweet substance, 0.05-0.25 wt.% acidity regulator and balance water.
   USE - Low-alcohol Lactobacillus beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage is suitable for most people due to low content of ethanol, has scent of mouth and ethanol, contains fermented flavor of ordinary yogurt, and produces small amounts of ethanol and carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the low-alcohol Lactobacillus beverage comprising pre-heating fresh cow milk to 50-60 degrees C, adding white granulated sugar and milk, uniformly mixing, homogenizing, sterilizing, first cooling, inoculating Kefir leaven Kefir-D, fermenting, second cooling to obtain kefir fermented dairy material, dissolving stabilizer and sweet substance in water at 50-70 degrees C, uniformly mixing, sterilizing, cooling to obtain aqueous sugar solution, uniformly mixing with kefir fermented dairy material, adding acidity regulator to 50-55 degrees C, homogenizing, cooling, and sterile filling. Sterilizing is done at 90-98 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. Cooling is done to 20-30 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Kefir leaven Kefir-D includes Lactococcus lactis subsp. lactis, Leuconostoc subspecies, Streptococcus thermophilus, Lactobacillus lactis, Kefir saccharomycetes and Kefir particles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The kefir fermented dairy material comprises 90.39-93.992 wt.% milk or 11-14 wt.% fresh milk, 5-8 wt.% white granulated sugar, 1-1.6 wt.% milk protein and 0.008-0.01 wt.% Kefir leaven Kefir-D. The stabilizer is high methoxyl pectin and/or soluble soybean polysaccharide. The sweet substance comprises white granulated sugar, beet sugar, maltose, glucose, fructose, lactose, high fructose syrup, xylitol, sucralose, neotame, acesulfame potassium, aspartame and/or sodium cyclamate. The acidity regulator comprises citric acid, lactic acid, succinic acid and/or malic acid. The milk is whole milk powder and/or skim milk powder. The milk protein is whey protein concentrate, whey protein isolate, milk protein concentrate, and/or sodium caseinate/calcium. Preferred Method: Homogenizing is done at 60-70 degrees C at pressure of 18-22 MPa. Sterilization is done at 90-98 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. The end temperature of first cooling is done to 22-25 degrees C. Fermentation is done at 22-25 degrees C for 18-24 hours. The end temperature of second cooling is 10-15 degrees C.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN109388771-A
TI Deep level thick oil foam oil viscosity reducing throughput well production parameter calculating method, involves determining liquid intensity based on liquid amount/thickness of oil layer using liquid producing intensity formula.
AU XU F
   ZHU D
   JIANG Y
   CHEN Y
   DONG R
   HAO X
   ZHAO M
   LIU Y
   LU Z
   WANG Z
   MA D
   NIU S
   XU C
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 201921211V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing oil foam generating model for simulating and reproducing bubbles in process oil under stratum condition. A porous medium is formed to produce stable foam oil using a fine mesh filling high-pressure device and using carbon dioxide as dissolved gas source. Lowest liquid amount gas is uniformly dissolved or precipitated in the liquid. Viscosity of the liquid is uniformly changed according to generation condition of the foam oil. Lowest liquid amount is calculated. Liquid intensity is determined based on liquid amount/thickness of an oil layer using liquid producing intensity formula.
   USE - Deep level thick oil foam oil viscosity reducing throughput well production parameter calculating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables continuously and stably performing production process of liquid, maintaining low viscosity state to achieve viscosity reduction and oil increasing effect, avoiding formation of foam rise process of oil changed into well gas, determining and analyzing position of stable foam oil under different production pressures so as to obtain viscosity reducing throughput well production parameters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic illustration of a deep level thick oil foam oil viscosity reducing throughput well production parameter calculating method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing the yield of microalgae oil using a walnut shell extract liquid involves (i) adding water to walnut shell, boiling for more than 4 hours until the mass ratio of the liquid to the walnut shell in the system is (9.5-10.5):1, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking liquid as a basic culture medium, adding sodium nitrate to the basic culture medium, adjusting the pH value of the culture medium to 6.8-7 using sodium hydroxide solution, and sterilizing to obtain microalgae culture medium, (ii) inoculating the microalgae into the obtained microalgae culture medium, introducing carbon dioxide-air mixed gas, and culturing at 24-26 degrees C and an illumination intensity of 6000-7000 lux, and (iii) determining the biomass of the microalgae under culture conditions, after the microalgae grows to reach a stable period, and extracting oil in the microalgae cells using an organic solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the yield of microalgae oil using a walnut shell extract liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the resource utilization of the walnut shell waste, and increases the yield of microalgae oil.

TI Preparation of zinc and sulfur involves crushing zinc concentrate, adding zinc leaching solution, leaching, performing electrochemical reduction, filtering, crystallizing, adding sulfur leaching solution to filter residue, and crystallizing.
AU LI J
   LI B
AE ZIBO GANDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECH (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2589N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zinc and sulfur involves crushing zinc concentrate, adding zinc leaching solution in a solid-liquid ratio 1-3:4-7 g:L, leaching at 80-95 degrees C for 2-3 hours, performing electrochemical reduction, mixing, filtering, obtaining filtrate (X1) and filter residue (Y1), crystallizing filtrate (X1) by evaporation to obtain zinc chloride, condensing and recycling evaporated steam to zinc leachate, adding sulfur leaching solution to filter residue (Y1) in a solid-liquid ratio of 1-3:5-10 g:L, leaching at 35-50 degrees C for 50-65 minutes, filtering, crystallizing filtrate by evaporation to obtain sulfur, and condensing and recycling evaporated vapor. The zinc leaching solution comprises 31.2-38.6 pts. wt. ferric chloride, 49.8-58.7 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 4.3-6.2 pts. wt. barium chloride, 4.2-6.7 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid and remainder of water. The sulfur leaching solution comprises 60.2-68.9 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 31.3-38.7 pts. wt. sodium and remainder of water.
   USE - Preparation of zinc and sulfur.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and environmentally-friendly preparation of zinc and sulfur with reduced energy consumption at low temperature without producing waste gas, waste residue and waste liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of zinc in the zinc concentrate is more than 40%. The sulfur leaching solution comprises 63.2-67.1 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 33.1-36.2 pts. wt. sodium and remainder of water. The solid-liquid ratio of zinc concentrate and zinc leaching solution is 2:5-6 g/L. The solid-liquid ratio of filter residue and sulfur leaching solution is 2:7-9 g/L. The concentration of zinc powder in the zinc concentrate is 0.5-1%.

PN IN201731018652-A
TI Green tackifier grafted diene rubbery macromolecule composition for diene rubber based green composite comprises diene rubber and green tackifier.
AU MANOHARAN P
   NASKAR K
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR (IIKH-C)
GA 201897581N
AB    NOVELTY - A green tackifier grafted diene rubbery macromolecule (GTDR) composition comprises 30-70 wt.% diene rubber and 1-15 wt.% green tackifier.
   USE - A green tackifier grafted diene rubbery macromolecule composition for diene rubber based green composite (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The GTDR composition when added to diene rubber in presence of inorganic filler results into a green composite having enhanced physico-mechanical properties of up to 20%, compared to conventional composites, including good retention of physico-mechanical properties, superior tire performance properties, such as low rolling resistance and ultra-high wet grip and better fuel economy, while minimizing carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) diene rubber based green composite free of silane comprising 30-70 wt.% green tackifier grafted diene rubbery macromolecule and 30-60 wt.% inorganic filler;
   (2) manufacture (M1) of green tackifier grafted diene rubbery macromolecule which involves using a selective diene rubber composition, and adding selective diene rubber composition to green tackifier in the presence of initiator; and
   (3) manufacture (M2) of green composite which involves using a selective diene rubber blend having desired attributes of good compatibility with inorganic filler and enhanced tire performance properties, adding inorganic filler to the obtained selective diene blend to obtain green composites, and adding curing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Inorganic filler is selected from silica or clay. Diene rubber based green composite comprises cure package in amounts of 2-6 wt.% including various basic ingredients, such as sulfur, accelerator, cure activator, and antioxidant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises initiator comprising organic peroxide, preferably Perkadox14 40BD (RTM: Di(2-tert.-butylperoxyisopropyl)) benzene (DTBPIB). The composition is substantially free of silane coupling agent and process oil and has enhanced physico-mechanical properties of up to 20% compared to conventional composites including improved mechanical properties of green compound ultra-low rolling resistance (up to 28% reduction) for low CO2 emission and better fuel economy, high wet grip (up to 30% increased), advanced dry handling (up to 32% increased), and super resistance to abrasion experiential in green compounds (up to 20% reduced).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Diene rubber comprises 30-70 wt.% butadiene rubber (BR); 30-70 wt.% styrene butadiene rubber (SBR); 30-70 wt.% natural rubber (NR); and 30-70 wt.% epoxidized natural rubber (ENR) with 25-50 mol.% epoxide groups. Green tackifier is selected from 5-9 carbon (5-9C), preferably 6C terpene phenolic resin. Diene rubber composition comprises selective blend of 10-90 wt.% butadiene rubber, 10-90 wt.% styrene butadiene rubber, 10-90 wt.% epoxidized natural rubber with 25-50 mol.% epoxide groups, and 10-90 wt.% natural rubber. Raw materials in method (M2) comprise 30-70 wt.% selective diene rubber blend; 30-60 wt.% inorganic filler; and 2-6 wt.% curing agent, preferably selective diene rubber blend that is 50 wt.% GTDR and 50 wt.% diene rubber, 60 wt.% inorganic filler, and 2-6 wt.% curing agent. Curing agents comprise polymerized 2,2,4-trimethyl 1,2 dihydroquinoline (TMQ) antioxidant, polymerized diphenylguanidine (DPG), and N-cyclohexyl-2-benzothiazole-sulfenamide (CBS)-accelerator. Preferred Processes: Method (M1) includes providing blend of diene rubber composition initially masticated for 0.5-1 minute, adding green tackifier and initiator mixed for 3-5 minutes, preferably 5 minutes, where all of the mixing steps are carried out at 110-140 degrees C, preferably 130 degrees C at a rotor speed of 50-60 rpm, preferably 60 rpm with mixer fill factor of 30-70%, preferably 70%.

PT P
PN CN108579790-A; CN108579790-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped multi-porous carbon catalyst comprises mixing coal asphalt powder, optionally L-alkali nitrogen source, and optional B-alkali nitrogen source, oxidative crosslinking, pre-carbonizing, heating and activating.
AU SONG J
   ZHANG T
   LV Y
   GUO J
   ZHANG X
   CHENG X
   WANG Y
   ZHU L
   LIN L
   LIU Y
   LI L
   LI Y
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201879791M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped multi-porous carbon catalyst comprises (i) mixing coal asphalt powder, optionally L-alkali nitrogen source, and the optional B-alkali nitrogen source uniformly, and performing an oxidative crosslinking reaction under an air atmosphere, (ii) filtering and drying the reaction solution to obtain a nitrogen-rich heavy organic substance, (iii) pre-carbonizing the nitrogen-rich heavy organic matter in a nitrogen atmosphere, heating and activating in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, heating the nitrogen-substituted system and activating in an ammonia atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrogen-doped multi-porous carbon catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers catalyst easily, has high conversion rate, high space-time yield, high value-added utilization of coal resources and no corrosion and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) nitrogen-doped porous carbon catalyst is prepared by above method; and
   (2) producing beta -isophorone, comprising subjecting alpha -isophorone to isomerization reaction under the catalysis of a nitrogen-doped porous carbon catalyst, where the catalyst is used in an amount of 0.01-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-1 wt.% based on the weight of the alpha -isophorone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-doped porous carbon has a pore diameter of 0.1-2 nm and a specific surface area of 2400-2600 m2/g. The nitrogen content is 9-10.5 wt.%, where the mass ratio of the L base to the B base in the nitrogen-doped porous carbon prepared is 0.2-7.0:1, preferably 1-2.0:1. In the step (1), the mass ratio of the coal asphalt powder, the L alkali nitrogen source and the B alkali nitrogen source is 1:0-200:0-200, and the amount of the L alkali nitrogen source and the B alkali nitrogen source cannot be 0 at the same time, preferably 1:20-50:20-50. In the step (i), the L alkali nitrogen source is quinoline, isoquinoline, benzoquinoline and benzisoquinoline. The B basic nitrogen source is aniline, p-phenylenediamine, o-phenylenediamine, and diphenylamine. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the pre-carbonization temperature is 200-300 degrees C, the time is 0.5-2.5 hours, and the temperature increase rate from room temperature to the pre-carbonization temperature is 0.5-2 degrees C/minute. In the step (iii), the activation temperature in the carbon dioxide atmosphere is 600-800 degrees C, and the time is 1-3 hours, and the temperature rise rate from the pre-carbonization temperature to the activation temperature in the carbon dioxide atmosphere is 2-5 degrees C/minutes. In the step (iii), the temperature of reactivation in an ammonia atmosphere is 1000-1200 degrees C, and the time is 1-2 hours, and the temperature rise rate from the temperature at which the activation in the carbon dioxide atmosphere is raised to the temperature at which the ammonia gas is reactivated is 0.5-2 degrees C/minutes.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC B04-C03; B05-C06; B06-D02; B06-D03; B06-D11; B06-D13; B10-B01A; B10-B04A; B10-F02; B14-Y; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N04-A; N05-D; N07-E05

PN CN108333166-A
TI Laser-induced surface enhanced Raman scattering substrate, has glass substrate matched with noble metal film layer, where substrate is arranged on laser-induced surface and layer is prepared by applying laser irradiation silver nanoparticle.
AU HONG R
   SUN W
   SHAO W
   DENG C
   TAO C
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV SHANGHAI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USHS-C)
GA 2018606544
AB    NOVELTY - The substrate has a glass substrate i.e. K9 glass, quartz or crystal, matched with a noble metal film layer. The glass substrate is arranged on a laser-induced surface. The noble metal film layer is made of silver material. The noble metal film layer is prepared by applying irradiation of laser i.e. Carbon dioxide laser or Yb-doped pulse fiber laser, into silver nanoparticle.
   USE - Laser-induced surface enhanced Raman scattering substrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The substrate utilizes acetone, alcohol and de-ionized water for realizing ultrasonic cleaning process to the glass substrate, forms the noble metal film layer on the glass substrate by using the laser irradiation process to detect the substrate so as to realize simple and high-efficient preparation process, thus shortening preparation period and reducing preparation cost. TI Preparing microorganism fermented red algae beverage comprises e. g. preparing red algae enzymes, rehydrating fresh red algae raw material or dried red algae, shearing, homogenizing with water, transferring and sterilizing.
AU HUANG K
   YIN H
   DONG J
   TIAN Y
   QIAN Z
   HUANG S
   YANG C
   CHEN H
   YAN P
   LIU X
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 201859446E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microorganism fermented red algae beverage comprises preparing red algae enzymes, rehydrating the fresh red algae raw material or dried red algae, shearing, homogenizing with water, transferring to fermenter, sterilizing, connecting the sterilized fermentation substrate to the suspension of Bacillus subtilis and the suspension of Lactobacillus plantarum, fermenting, obtaining the liquid red algae enzyme by solid-liquid separation of fermented seaweed enzyme, mixing hop pellets and the boiled pure water in a ratio of 1:100-150, boiling for 1 minutes, heating and cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain hop beverage, where the pretreated red algae beverage includes 0.5-5 wt.% liquid red algae enzyme, 1-10 wt.% hop tea, 2-5 wt.% syrup, 0.01-0.05 wt.% sour material and balance of water, stirring evenly, obtaining pretreated red algae beverage, carbonating the pretreated red algae beverage with carbon dioxide to obtain red algae beverage.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microorganism fermented red algae beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage is suitable for food processing field and has good drinking property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for microorganism fermented red algae beverage is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The amount of the liquid red algae enzyme is 0.5-2%, and the hop tea is 1-5%. The red algae beverage has carbon dioxide content of 0.35-0.65%. The syrup is fructose syrup or glucose syrup. The sour material is citric acid, malic acid or lactic acid. The hop is a fragrant hop or bitter fragrant hop of Qingdao heterophyllous, Tettanger, Saaz, Hallertauer MFR or Cascade or Citral. The red algae includes agar, laver, Asparagus, carrageen or bur carrageenan. Preferred Method: The sterilized fermentation substrate is aseptically introduced into the prepared Bacillus subtilis suspension by 1.5% inoculation amount, the fermentation is stirred at 40 degrees C for 4 days, stirring, and cooling to 30 degrees C, obtaining the prepared Lactobacillus plantarum suspension to the inoculation amount of 1%, stirred at 30 degrees C for 5 minutes, stirring, and allowing to stand for 2-3 days.

GA 201858392C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting provided gas into a selected well bore in fluid communication with a hydrocarbon-containing reservoir (100) that ceases the injection of the provided gas into the selected well bore. The provided gas and the gaseous hydrocarbons are gathered together from the hydrocarbon-containing reservoir by the selected well bore to form a gathered-gas mixture comprising the provided gas and the gaseous hydrocarbons from the hydrocarbon-containing reservoir. The gathered-gas mixture is produced through the selected well bore, where the hydrocarbon-containing reservoir producing the gathered-gas mixture comprises water-to-gas production ratio that is no more than another water-to-gas production ratio.
   USE - Method for recovering oil and gas e.g. methane, ethane, propane, nitrogen, butane, air, oxygen, argon, carbon dioxide and helium (all claimed) from a hydrocarbon-containing reservoir.

PN CN107963649-A
TI Preparation of ultra-fine activated calcium carbonate involves preparing lime, preparing calcium hydroxide slurry, coating agent solution, and additive, mixing slurry and additive, carbonizing, adding coating agent, heating, and drying.
AU LI T
   LI G
   CAO J
   ZHAN Z
AE ANHUI XINTAO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018344688
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultra-fine calcium carbonate comprises grinding quicklime to particle size of less than 10 mesh, adding water at weight ratio of 1:3-4, dissolving, sieving by 150-200 mesh sieve, stirring at 5-10 degrees C, and settling for 12-150 hours; pumping clear water in sedimentation tank at 20-30 m3/hour, transferring residue, and adding 15-22 wt.% calcium hydroxide; mixing coating agent and water at ratio of 1:3, dissolving, and adding water to prepare 5-20 wt.% coating agent solution; mixing organic and inorganic additives at mass ratio of 1:3-3:1 and dissolving with water to prepare 0.5-3 wt.% additive solution; transferring second and fourth steps slurry and additive solution into carbonization tower, introducing 85-99% carbon dioxide at 30-55 m3/(h.m3 lime milk suspension), and carbonizing; heating calcium carbonate slurry to 75-85 degrees C and adding 1 wt.% aqueous coating agent solution; and heating to 40-50 degrees C, filtering and drying at 70-80 degrees C to moisture content of less than 1.5%.
   USE - Method for preparing ultra-fine calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated calcium carbonate has improved dispersibility and reinforcing performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal is sodium or potassium salts. The soluble inorganic additives are soluble sulfates, nitrates and chlorides. The soluble sulfate is sodium sulfate, potassium sulfate, ammonium sulfate or magnesium sulfate. The soluble nitrate is calcium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, or sodium nitrate. The soluble chloride is calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, or hydrochloric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fatty acid is stearic acid and/or palmitic acid. The organic additive is glucose, sucrose and/or sodium citrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The coating agent is resin acid, resin acid metal salts, fatty acids, and/or fatty acid alkali metal or its salts. The resin acid is abietic acid.

PN CN110973427-A
TI Preparation of gas-containing soda water based on Wudalianchi natural spring water by mixing treated bicarbonate mineral water with natural soda water, cooling and then using gas-water mixer to fill mixed raw material water carbon dioxide.
AU WANG J
AE HEILONGJIANG ACAD SCI & TECHN VOLCANO MI (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 202031488J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gas-containing soda water based on Wudalianchi natural spring water involves: (1) treating Wudalianchi bicarbonate mineral water by (1a) passing raw water of Wudalianchi bicarbonate mineral water into an aeration tank, and then performing air aeration treatment to obtain bicarbonate mineral water, (1b) subjecting the bicarbonate mineral water after aeration treatment to natural sedimentation in the aeration tank to obtain bicarbonate mineral water with unstable mineral ions removed, (1c) passing the unstable mineral ions removed bicarbonate mineral water into an ozone oxidation tower for ozone oxidation treatment, and naturally settling in the ozone oxidation tower to obtain bicarbonate mineral water, (1d) using a manganese sand filter to filter the bicarbonate mineral water of after ozone oxidation treatment to obtain bicarbonate mineral water; (2) treating Wudalianchi natural soda water; (3) mixing; and (4) performing carbon dioxide filling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing gas-containing soda water based on Wudalianchi natural spring water.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared gas-containing soda water has rich mineral content, and no food additives such as soda or baking soda, and is rich in bubbles, fresh in taste and less in irritation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of gas-containing soda water based on Wudalianchi natural spring water involves: (1) treating Wudalianchi bicarbonate mineral water by (1a) passing raw water of Wudalianchi bicarbonate mineral water into an aeration tank, and then performing air aeration treatment to obtain bicarbonate mineral water after air aeration, (1b) subjecting the bicarbonate mineral water after aeration treatment to natural sedimentation in the aeration tank to obtain bicarbonate mineral water with unstable mineral ions removed, (1c) passing the unstable mineral ions removed bicarbonate mineral water into an ozone oxidation tower for ozone oxidation treatment, and naturally settling in the ozone oxidation tower to obtain bicarbonate mineral water after ozone oxidation treatment, (1d) using a manganese sand filter to filter the bicarbonate mineral water of after ozone oxidation treatment to obtain bicarbonate mineral water, (1e) taking a 0.6-1.2 mm fruit shell activated carbon as a filler, and using a fruit shell activated carbon filter for filtering the bicarbonate mineral water filtered by manganese sand filter to obtain bicarbonate mineral water filtered by the shell activated carbon filter and (1f) taking 1-4 mu m polypropylene as filter media, using a precision filter to filter the bicarbonate mineral water filtered by the fruit shell activated carbon filter; (2) treating Wudalianchi natural soda water by (2a) using a multi-media filter to filter raw water of Wudalianchi natural soda water under the water pressure of 0.75 MPa to obtain natural soda water filtered by the multi-media filter, where a filter medium of the multi-media filter in step (2a) is 0.1 m quartz sand with a particle size of 1-2 mm, 0.2 m quartz sand with a diameter of 8-10 mm and 0.5 m quartz sand with a diameter of 20-30 mm, (2b) taking a 0.6-1.2 mm fruit shell activated carbon as the filler, and using the fruit shell activated carbon filter for filtering the natural soda water after the multi-media filter filtration to obtain natural soda water filtered by the shell activated carbon filter, (2c) using 5-10 mu m polypropylene as the filter medium and using the precision filter for filtering the natural soda water filtered by the fruit shell activated carbon filter to obtain natural soda water after precision filter filtration, (2d) using a hollow fiber as a filter medium and an ultrafiltration device for filtering the natural soda water filtered by the precision filter to obtain natural soda water filtered by the ultrafiltration device, (2e) using an UV sterilization device to sterilize the natural soda water filtered by the ultrafiltration device for 5-10 seconds to obtain natural soda water after UV sterilization, and (2f) filtering the natural soda water after UV sterilization by using a terminal filter with a pore size of 0.22 mu m folded filter; (3) mixing the treated bicarbonate mineral water with the treated natural soda water in a mass ratio of (3-7):(3-7)to obtain a mixed raw material water; and (4) carbon dioxide filling by cooling the mixed raw material water to 4-6 degrees C by using a freezer, then using a gas-water mixer to fill the mixed raw material water with a temperature of 4-6 degrees with carbon dioxide, where the free carbon dioxide content in the gas-containing soda water based on the natural spring water is 1000-1800 mg/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The aeration amount of the air in the aeration treatment of step (1a) is 15-20 l/hour, and the aeration time is 2-4 seconds. The sedimentation time in step (1b) is 18-24 hours. The ozone dosage in the ozone oxidation treatment in step (1c) is 15-20 mg/l. The ozone oxidation treatment time is 0.5-1 minute. The natural sedimentation time in step (1c) is 10-12 hours. The filter medium of the manganese sand filter in step (d) is 0.1 m manganese dioxide and 0.10.4 m cobblestone from top to bottom. The height of the filler in step (1d) is 0.5-0.8 m. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the treated bicarbonate mineral water in step (3) and the treated natural soda water is 3:7 or 7:3. When the mass ratio of the treated bicarbonate mineral water and the treated natural soda water described in step (3) is 7:3, the free carbon dioxide content in the gas-containing soda water based on the natural spring water is 1800 mg/l, and the gas-containing soda water dissipates heat after exercise to supplement mineral ions. When the mass ratio of the treated bicarbonate mineral water and the treated natural soda water is 3:7, the free carbon dioxide content in the gas-containing soda water based on natural spring water is 1000 mg/l, and the gas-containing soda water is used for table water.
