PN CN108925732-A; CN108925732-B
TI Preparing red jujube hard candy by preparing concentrated red jujube juice, caramel red jujube viscous liquid, concentrated caramel jujube juice and jujube diced meat, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction.
AU LIANG X
   ZHANG B
   YAN T
   CHEN J
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 201899567V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing red jujube hard candy involves (a) taking dried red jujube, removing impurities, cleaning, drying water, adding red jujube and water, boiling, stirring, cooling, adding pulp enzyme and liquid acid cellulose, performing enzymolysis, cooling, centrifuging, filtering and concentrating red jujube juice, heating jujube juice, and stirring, cooling, and adding skim milk powder, (b) adding red jujube meat into sucrose aqueous solution, adding e.g. licorice into sugar liquid, taking fresh jujube, cutting, adding the material into prepared sugar liquid, boiling, stirring, soaking, drying, and cutting, (c) taking dried red jujube, cleaning, drying and crushing the material, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction and (d) heating and dissolving caramelized red jujube viscous liquid, uniformly stirring until the material dissolves completely, adding e.g. citric acid, uniformly stirring, and obtaining jujube hard candy using hard candy injection molding machine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing red jujube hard candy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high utilization rate and low quality requirements for red jujube, does not contain defoaming agent, thickening agent and other flavor and pigment, and the obtained product is rich in nutrients, and has strong jujube flavor, caramel flavor, bright color and good mouthfeel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing red jujube hard candy involves (a) taking dried red jujube, removing impurities, mildew and rot fruit, cleaning the material using water, drying water, adding red jujube and water to the weight ratio of 1:3-6, boiling, uniformly stirring at 95-100 degrees C until the red jujube skin breaks, cooling the material to 45-50 degrees C, adding pulp enzyme having a enzyme activity of 20000 U/ml and liquid acid cellulose having a enzyme activity of 140000 U/ml, where the total weight of pulp enzyme and liquid acid cellulase is 0.3-0.7 wt.%, and the weight ratio of pulp enzyme and liquid acid cellulase is 1:1-2:1, performing enzymolysis at 45-50 degrees C for 3-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging the residue using tri-pin centrifuge, filtering using plate and frame filter press to obtain red jujube juice, concentrating the red jujube juice using vacuum concentration pot at 0.009-0.0095 MPa and at 45-50 degrees C until the soluble solid content reaches 45-50% to obtain concentrated red jujube juice, heating the jujube juice at 140-150 degrees C using heat-conducting oil interlayer boiler, preserving heat for 40-60 minutes, and stirring until the soluble solid content reaches 60-65% to obtain concentrated caramel jujube juice, cooling the obtained juice to 105-110 degrees C, adding 5-7 wt.% skim milk powder while stirring, maintaining the temperature for 40-60 minutes to obtain caramel red jujube viscous liquid, (b) adding red jujube meat into sucrose aqueous solution containing 65% sugar, adding 1-3 wt.% licorice, 1-3 wt.% dried orange peel, 0.5-1.5 wt.% sodium chloride and 0.1-0.3 wt.% calcium chloride, and preparing into sugar liquid, taking fresh and immature jujube in milk white period, removing core, cutting the surface of jujube using grater, adding the material into prepared sugar liquid, boiling the material for 40-80 minutes, continuously stirring the sugar liquid during heat preservation process, soaking the material at room temperature for 12-36 hours, removing, drying the material in hot air drying oven at 60-65 degrees C for 4-5 hours, cutting the edge length for 1-3 mm in square to obtain jujube diced meat, (c) taking dried red jujube without mildew, cleaning, drying the material in hot air drying oven at 60-65 degrees C for 4-5 hours and crushing the material using hammer type crusher, adding the crushed jujube powder into carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device of material barrel, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at 35-45 degrees C and at a pressure of 35-45 MPa for 2-3 hours, carbon dioxide flow rate is 25-35 kg/hours and collecting golden yellow transparent jujube essential oil, and (d) heating and dissolving the caramelized red jujube viscous liquid obtained in step (a) in mixing pot, where the weight ratio of caramelized red jujube viscous liquid and sucrose is 2:1-3:2, uniformly stirring until the material dissolves completely, adding 0.2-0.4 wt.% citric acid, 0.2-0.4 wt.% malic acid, 0.8-1.2 wt.% refined rapeseed oil, 10-15 wt.% red jujube diced meat and 0.2-0.3 wt.% red jujube essential oil, uniformly stirring, and obtaining jujube hard candy using hard candy injection molding machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In step (a), the total amount of pectinase and liquid acid cellulase is 0.5 wt.%, and the ratio of pulp enzyme and cellulose is 3:2, heating the juice at 145 degrees C using heat conducting oil interlayer boiler, preserving heat for 50 minutes while stirring, and adding 6 wt.% skim milk powder. In step (b), the sucrose aqueous solution having a sugar content of 65% is added with 2 wt.% licorice, 2 wt.% dried orange peel, 1 wt.% sodium chloride and 0.2 wt.% calcium chloride, taking fresh, immature jujube in milk white period, removing core, cutting the surface of jujube using grater, adding the material into prepared sugar liquid, boiling the material for 60 minutes, continuously stirring the sugar liquid during heat preservation process, and soaking the material at room temperature for 24 hours. In step (c), the crushed jujube powder is subjected into barrel of carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction at 45 degrees C and at a pressure of 40 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate is 30 kg/hour. The step (d) involves adding 0.3 wt.% citric acid, 0.3 wt.% malic acid, 1 wt.% refined rapeseed oil, 11-12 wt.% red jujube diced meat, 0.2-0.25 wt.% red jujube essential oil.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D03-E10A; D03-H02B2; D03-P; D05-A02; D05-A04D

   USE - The method is useful for detecting cytotoxicity in electronic smoke aerosol water extract (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is accurate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting cytotoxicity of an aqueous aerosol extract of electronic cigarette comprises (i) using linear type smoking machine with the pumping capacity of 55.0 ml, pumping duration of 3 seconds, pumping frequency of 30 seconds, pumping the 100 electronic cigarette, adding to a collecting bottle containing FM cell culture medium for capturing electronic cigarette smoke with trapping concentration of 20/ml, collecting liquid using 0.22 mu m sterile filter and sterilizing to obtain electronic cigarette smoke aerosol water extract as the liquid to be tested and storing at -80 degrees C; (ii) adding recovered human lung fibroblasts HPF, inoculating into 25 cm2 cell culture bottle, adding into 10 ml FM cell culture medium, placing in 37 degrees C, adding into 5% carbon dioxide incubator, inverting in a microscope to observe the confluence of cultured cells and morphological conditions until the cells grow to 70- 90% confluency, removing the incubated FM cell culture medium in the flask twice with phosphate buffer and discarding the washed solution, adding 1 ml of 0.25% (w/v) trypsin solution and incubating for 1-2 minutes in a monolayer and suspending with FM cell culture medium to obtain a single cell suspension; (iii) calculating the blood cell concentration of the single cell suspension by the counting plate to obtain number of viable cells per ml of single cell suspension; (iv) diluting the counted single cell suspension with FM cell culture medium at a concentration of 2x 105/ ml, then inoculating 200 mu l/hole of single cell suspension with FM cell culture medium into 96-well of cell culture plate, and then placing the 96-well cell culture plate with 5% carbon dioxide in incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, removing the 96-well cell culture plates and dividing the cells in culture medium containing the test substance into four groups, i.e, blank group, cell control group, positive control group and test sample group, where the blank group is free of plant cells, then adding the FM cell culture medium, cell control group growing cells and the FM cell culture medium of dodecyl sodium sulfate, 200 mu g/ml of positive control group and adding 200 mu l test sample group, then adding the test sample group into the test solution, then controlling the final concentration to 10 non-zero concentration, 10% test solution, 20% test solution, 30% test solution, 40% test solution, 50% test solution, 60% test solution, 70% test solution, 80% test solution, 90% test solution and 100% test solution, supplementing the cell volume in each well of the cell culture plate to 200 mu l/well with FM cell culture medium to avoid the edge effect, removing the data from the edge of the 2 holes using 6 holes in the middle of the data; (vi) loading the 96-well cell culture plate to incubate in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours; (vii) adding 5 mg/ml MTT solution into dye incubation in blank group after incubation, adding cell control group, the positive control group and the detecting sample groups with 20 mu m/well, then adding in a culture box of 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C, incubating for 4 hours, removing the 96-hole cell culture plate of liquid, adding dimethyl sulfoxide solution and oscillating at 200 mu m/well in the micro-oscillator for 10 minutes; (viii) detecting the light absorption value at the wavelength of 490 nm by enzyme labeling after incubating of blank group of dye, cell control group, positive control group and test sample group; (ix) calculating cell viability according to the following formula as specified, where X-cell is cell viability, ODn is the detection average of porous light absorption value sample group, ODo is the porous blank group average light absorption value and ODc is the control group porous light absorbing value of cell average; and (x) determining the cytotoxicity of electronic cigarette smoke aerosol water-extract according to 10 non-zero dose and 0 dose cell survival corresponding to the drawn curve

Preferred                  Method: The step of preparing first polyol                  comprises polymerizing the polycarbonate polyol                  and epoxide. The modifying step is the film                  forming process (Reticulation) of the foam. The                  film forming process is to modify the foam of the                  closed cell to the foam of the open cell. The                  film forming process comprises the steps of                  putting the foam into a vacuum chamber, forming a                  vacuum inside the vacuum chamber, introducing                  hydrogen gas and oxygen gas into the vacuum                  chamber in which the vacuum is formed and                  removing the film through spark ignition.

PN CN114075587-A
TI Preparing anti-tight junction molecule claudin-18 isoform 2 antibody by adding Chinese hamster ovary cells to basal medium, centrifuging, resuspending cells in medium, culturing in carbon dioxide shaker, then culturing in bioreactor and harvesting CHO cells expressing CLDN18.2 antibodies.
AU WANG Y
   WEN Y
   SUN Z
   XU C
   GU C
   CHEN Q
AE JIANGSU AOSAIKANG PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202235340U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing anti-tight junction molecule claudin-18 isoform 2 (CLDN18.2) antibody by perfusion culture of Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cells involves (a) thawing the CHO cells, transferring into basal medium (M1), centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, resuspending the cells in basal medium (M1) and then culturing in a carbon dioxide shaker until the viable cell density reaches (3-6)x106 cells/ml, (b) inoculating the cells obtained in step (a) into basal medium (M1) and culturing until the viable cell density reaches (3-6)x106 cells/ml, (c) inoculating the cells obtained in step (a) into basal medium (M2), culturing in a WAVE reactor for 2-5 days, changing the medium and continuing culturing until the viable cell density reaches (80-120)x106 cells/ml and (d) inoculating the cells obtained in step (c) into a bioreactor to culture and harvesting the CHO cells capable of expressing anti-CLDN18.2 antibodies.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-CLDN18.2 antibody.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes high-density culture of seeds, saves equipment investment, improves protein yield and shortens production cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves centrifuging at 200-300 g for 3-8 minutes. In step (a), the basal medium (M1) comprises ActiPro (RTM: Cell culture medium to provide high yields of recombinant proteins). The step (a) involves culturing at 36.5plus minus1degrees Celsius and 105-125 revolutions per minute (rpm) under 5plus minus0.2% carbon dioxide and 78-82% humidity. The step (b) involves inoculating (0.4-0.6)x106 cells/ml. In step (c), the basal medium (M2) comprises 85-95% ActiPro (RTM: Cell culture medium to provide high yields of recombinant proteins), 1-8% feed (F1), 2-7% Cell boost 7a (RTM: Supplement to boost protein production) and 0.2-0.7% Cell boost 7b (RTM: Supplement to boost protein production). In step (c), the liquid exchange rate is 0.02-0.03 nl/cell/day. The step (c) involves culturing using the WAVE reactor at 36.5plus minus1degrees Celsius, pH of 6.7-7.3 and 14-18 revolutions per minute (rpm) under dissolved oxygen content of 30plus minus1%. The step (d) involves inoculating (4.5-5.5)x106 cells/ml. The step (d) involves harvesting the cells when the cell viability is 70-95%.

PN CN113896623-A
TI Extracting gingerol from ginger, comprises            providing montmorillonite, hydroxypropyl            methylcellulose as auxiliary materials, drying ginger,            crushing and collecting dry ginger powder, mixing dry            ginger powder with cosolvent, supercritical extraction            of carbon dioxide, post-extraction treatment.
AU LI H
   LI Z
   TANG J
   ZHANG W
   LIAO Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING ARTS & SCI (UYQA-C)
GA 2022137524
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting gingerol from ginger, comprises                taking ginger as raw material, montmorillonite,                hydroxypropyl methylcellulose as auxiliary                materials, drying, crushing and collecting dry                ginger powder, mixing dry ginger powder with                cosolvent, supercritical extraction of carbon                dioxide, post-extraction treatment; where the                cosolvent is at least one of methanol, ethanol,                acetone, ether, and normal hexane; the mass ratio                of the dry ginger powder and the cosolvent is                1:1-3.
   USE - Extracting gingerol from ginger. Gingerol is                used as food and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can greatly improve the extraction                transfer rate of the marked component gingerol; the                transfer rate of the marking component 6-gingerol                can reach 64.3%, the extraction post-treatment                process, because of the adsorption of                montmorillonite and the viscosity adjusting effect                of hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, the volatile                component loss of gingerol is less, the storage                process has good stability, the extreme environment                is stored for 30 days, the product trait is almost                non-change. The content of the marking component                6-gingerol is reduced; only 0.021%. The functional                additive of cosmetic is added directly, convenient                to use, worthy of market popularization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying and crushing is                  placing the ginger in a vacuum drying box, where                  the vacuum degree is -0.06 to -0.08 MPa, the                  drying temperature is 40-50degrees Celsius,                  drying until the water content is 5-9% to obtain                  the dried ginger, then placing the dried ginger                  in a universal crusher, crushing and sieving by                  30-60-mesh sieve, collecting the powder,                  obtaining dry ginger powder to obtain the                  product. The dry ginger powder is mixed with                  cosolvent to obtain the dry ginger powder,                  placing in a suitable stirring container, and                  stirring at speed of 10-15 revolutions/minute,                  under the condition of stirring, slowly adding                  cosolvent, after adding, continuously stirring                  for 5-10 minutes to obtain the mixed dry ginger                  powder. The carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction is to place the prepared mixed dry                  ginger powder in the extraction kettle of carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus,                  setting the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-20                  kg/h. The pressure is 20-40 MPa, the temperature                  is 45-50degrees Celsius, the extracting time is                  2-3 hours, the extracting is finished, collecting                  the extract. The cosolvent is preferably the                  mixed solution of ether and methanol, the volume                  ratio of the ether to methanol is 3:1, the mass                  ratio of the dry ginger powder and the mixed                  solution of ether and methanol preferably is                  1:1.2-1.7. The post-extraction treatment is                  carried out according to the following steps: (1)                  placing the extract in the device with stirring,                  setting the stirring speed of 20-30                  revolutions/minute, under the condition of                  stirring, adding montmorillonite, continuously                  stirring for 20-30 minutes to obtain intermediate                  1; the mass ratio of the extract to the                  montmorillonite is 100:3-8; (2) taking purified                  water, adding to 40-50degrees Celsius, stirring,                  adding hydroxypropyl methylcellulose,                  continuously stirring for 30-60 minutes to obtain                  intermediate 2; the stirring speed is 12-18                  revolutions/minute, the mass ratio of the                  purified water and hydroxypropyl methylcellulose                  is 100:7-11; (3) taking intermediate 2, under the                  condition of stirring, adding intermediate 1 into                  intermediate 2, continuously stirring for 30-40                  minutes, obtaining intermediate 3, for further                  use; the mass ratio of the intermediate 1 to the                  intermediate 2 is 1:1-3; (4) placing the                  intermediate 3 in the vacuum extracting and                  concentrating device, setting the vacuum degree                  at -0.06 to -0.08 MPa, the temperature is                  35-45degrees Celsius, vacuum extracting and                  concentrating for 1-2 hours, volatilizing the                  ether and methanol in the intermediate 3; the                  relative density of intermediate 3 is 1.16-1.23                  (25degrees Celsius), to obtain the target                  product. The grain diameter of the                  montmorillonite is 60-150 microm, and the grain                  diameter of the hydroxypropyl methylcellulose is                  120-180 microm.

   GUANGDONG SUNION HIGH-TECH CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B73735
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-cis, high-vinyl-terminated polybutadiene rubber comprises (i) reacting a bifunctional conjugated olefin with an alkali metal alkyl compound to obtain a bifunctional initiator; (ii) initiating polymerization of butadiene monomer using the bifunctional initiator to obtain living polybutadiene; (iii) adding epoxy compounds or carbonyl compounds to the living polybutadiene, reacting, and terminating the polymerization reaction; (iv) adding water to the reaction solution for hydrolysis to obtain basic hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene glue; and (v) introducing carbon dioxide into the basic hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene glue, neutralizing to obtain hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene, adding anti-aging agent and removing the solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-cis, high-vinyl-terminated polybutadiene rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene rubber has number-average molecular weight of 5000-10000 g/mol%, the cis content is 10-25%, the trans content is 40-60%, the 1,2-structure content is 30-45%, the 1,4-structure content is 60-75%, the molecular weight distribution is 1-1.2, and the functionality is 1.8-2.1.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for low-cis, high-vinyl hydroxyl terminated polybutadiene rubber, prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the bifunctional conjugated olefin is divinylbenzene and/or divinylnaphthalene. In step (i), the alkali metal alkyl compound is n-butyl lithium or sec-butyl lithium; preferably, the reaction is carried out under nitrogen protection; preferably, the solvent of the reaction is a non-polar solvent; preferably cyclohexane, cyclopentane, methylcyclopentane, n-heptane and toluene. In step (ii), the polymerization reaction system further comprises structure modifier; preferably, the structure modifier is a mixed liquid of tetrahydrofuran and N,N-dimethylformamide, a mixed liquid of tetrahydrofuran and tetramethylethylenediamine, tetrahydrofuran and 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuryl)propane or a mixture of 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuranyl)propane and tetrahydrofurfuryl alcohol ethyl ether; preferably, the mass ratio of tetrahydrofuran and N,N-dimethylformamide in the mixed solution of tetrahydrofuran and N,N-dimethylformamide is 1:1-2:1; preferably, the mass ratio of tetrahydrofuran and tetramethylethylenediamine in the mixed solution of tetrahydrofuran and tetramethylethylenediamine is 1:1-2:1; preferably, the mass ratio of tetrahydrofuran and 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuryl)propane in the mixture of tetrahydrofuran and 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuranyl)propane is 1:2-1:3; preferably, the mass ratio of 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuryl)propane and tetrahydrofurfuryl ethyl ether in the mixture of 2,2-bis(2-tetrahydrofuryl)propane and tetrahydrofurfuryl alcohol ethyl ether t is 1:2-1:3.The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide and/or propylene oxide; preferably, the carbonyl compound is acetone and/or methyl ethyl ketone. In step (iii), the molar ratio of the epoxy compound or carbonyl compound to the bifunctional initiator is 1:1.0-1.5. The antioxidant is hindered phenol antioxidants and phosphite antioxidants; where the hindered phenol antioxidant is tetrakis(beta-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionic acid) pentaerythritol ester, beta-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4 -Hydroxyphenyl) n-octadecyl propionate, 2-(1-(2-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-amylphenyl) ethyl)-4,6-di-tert-amyl phenyl acrylic acid ester, 2-tert-butyl-6-(3-tert-butyl-2-hydroxy-5-methylphenyl)-4-methylphenol acrylate; preferably, the phosphite antioxidant is tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite, bis((2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) propionic acid) pentaerythritol diphosphite ester and tris(tridecyl) phosphite; preferably, the mass ratio of hindered phenol antioxidant and phosphite antioxidant Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction is carried out at 45-60degreesC for 10-30 minutes. In step (ii), the initiation temperature in the polymerization reaction is 45-50degreesC, and the temperature peak is 95-105degreesC; preferably, the polymerization reaction time is 1-2 hours. In step (iii), the temperature of the reaction is 85-95degreesC; preferably, the epoxy compound or the carbonyl compound is added to the system in a dropwise manner, and the dropwise addition refers to 3-5 drops/seconds; preferably, the reaction time is 10-20 minutes. In step (iv), the amount of water added is 1.0-1.5 times, preferably 1.2 times, the molar amount of the active groups of the bifunctional initiator; preferably, the hydrolysis time is 15-20 minutes, preferably 20 minutes. In step (v), introducing carbon dioxide for duration of 20-40 minutes, preferably 30 minutes. In step (v) is a 1:1 combination; preferably, the addition amount of the antioxidant is 1.0-1.5 phr, preferably 1.3 phr. The number average molecular weight of the hydroxyl-terminated polybutadiene rubber is 5000-10000 g/mol, the cis content is 10-25%, the trans content is 40-60%, the 1,2-structure content is 30-45%, the 1,4-structure content is 60-75%, and the molecular weight distribution is 1.0-1.2. The degree is 1.8-2.1.

PN US2021277430-A1; CN113373183-A; WO2021183354-A1; TW202200792-A; IN202217029999-A
TI Producing lipid product from carbon dioxide and            hydrogen by passing portion of 1st fermentation broth            to 2nd bioreactor containing culture of 2nd            microorganism, e.g. Scenedesmus, in 2nd liquid nutrient            medium and fermenting acetate product.
AU CONRADO R J
   MIHALCEA C D
   SIMPSON S D
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2021A3574X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lipid product from carbon dioxide                (CO2) and hydrogen (H2), involves: (i) receiving a                gaseous substrate comprising at least CO2 and H2 in                a 1st bioreactor containing a culture of 1st                microorganism in a 1st liquid nutrient medium and                fermenting the gaseous substrate to produce acetate                product in a 1st fermentation broth; (ii) passing                portion of the 1st fermentation broth to a 2nd                bioreactor containing a culture of 2nd                microorganism in a 2nd liquid nutrient medium,                where the 2nd microorganism is not the same as the                1st microorganism and is selected from Scenedesmus,                Thraustochytrium, Japonochytrium, Aplanochytrium,                Elina, and Labyrinthula, and fermenting the acetate                product to produce lipid product in a 2nd                fermentation broth; (iii) obtaining a tail gas                comprising at least CO2 and O2 from the 2nd                bioreactor; and (iv) separating a portion of the O2                from the tail gas and recycling portion of the                reminder of the tail gas to the 1st                bioreactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lipid                product from CO2 and H2 (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides enhanced efficiency by                recycling unconsumed CO2 from the 2nd bioreactor                back to the 1st bioreactor while removing O2 from                the recycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves producing, from the at least one lipid                  product, at least one tertiary product selected                  from hydrogenation-derived renewable diesel,                  fatty acid methyl esters, fatty acid ethyl                  esters, and biodiesel. The method also involves                  limiting at least one nutrient in the 2nd liquid                  nutrient medium in the 2nd bioreactor to increase                  lipid production. Separating portion of the O2                  from the tail gas is accomplished using one or                  more stages of pressure swing adsorption,                  membrane separation, scrubbing with a basic                  solution, absorption using a solvent, or any                  combination. The method further involves                  recycling the O2 from the separation of portion                  of the O2 from the tail gas to the 2nd                  bioreactor; recovering a gaseous stream                  comprising CO2 and H2 from the 1st bioreactor and                  recycling the gaseous stream to the 1st                  bioreactor; and recycling portion of the 2nd                  fermentation broth to the 1st bioreactor. It also                  involves removing the 1st microorganism from the                  1st fermentation broth before passing at least a                  portion of the 1st fermentation broth to the 2nd                  bioreactor and recycling the 1st microorganism to                  the 1st bioreactor. It further involves removing                  the 2nd microorganism from the 2nd fermentation                  broth and recycling the 2nd microorganism to the                  2nd bioreactor. The method also involves passing,                  to the 1st bioreactor, the remainder of the 2nd                  fermentation broth after removing the 2nd                  microorganism; generating the H2 using an                  electrolyzer; and generating O2 using an                  electrolyzer and introducing the                  electrolyzer-generated O2 to the 2nd bioreactor,                  where the 2nd bioreactor is operated in an oxygen                  limited mode.Preferred Parameters: The production rate of                  acetate in the 1st bioreactor is at least 10                  g/L/day.Preferred Components: At least one of the                  1st microorganisms in the 1st bioreactor is                  selected from Acetobacterium, Moorella,                  Clostridium, Pyrococcus, Eubacterium,                  Desulfobacterium, Carboxydothermus Acetogenium,                  Acetoanaerobium, Butyribacterium,                  Peptostreptococcus, Ruminococcus, Oxobacter, and                  Methanosarcina. At least one of the 1st                  micoorganisms in the 1st bioreactor is                  Acetobacterium woodii. At least one of the 2nd                  microorganisms in the 2nd bioreactor is                  Thraustochytrium. The limited nutrient is                  nitrogen. The lipid product is a polyunsaturated                  fatty acid, where the polyunsaturated fatty acid                  is an omega-3 fatty acid. The omega-3 fatty acid                  is one or more of alpha-linolenic acid,                  eicosapentaenoic acid, and docosahexaenoic                  acid.

PN CN113307582-A; CN113307582-B
TI Preparing carbon capture concrete containing organic material net-structure, involves adjusting distance and net diameter of organic material net structure, and placing in natural environment through carbonization reaction of concrete.
AU YANG H
   WANG W
   JIANG M
   ZHANG G
   WANG D
   WANG Q
   SUN S
   SUN H
   YUAN W
AE UNIV SHIJIAZHUANG TIEDAO (USTD-C)
GA 2021A2398C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon capture concrete containing organic material (SAP) net-structure comprises placing cement, river sand and stone in concrete mixer to dry for 2 minutes; adding mixed water dissolved with high performance water reducing agent, and mixing concrete to uniform; preparing cement slurry with water gel, stirring, precipitating, placing supernatant as concrete hole solution in fine rod-shaped SAP in concrete hole solution to soak; pouring concrete to half of depth of test die, and placing fine rod-shaped SAP soaked by concrete hole solution on surface of concrete; binding fine rod-shaped SAP point position by thin nylon rope; pouring concrete on thin rod-shaped SAP network; absorbing SAP water and expanding in new mixed concrete; adjusting distance and net diameter of SAP net structure by adjusting expansion ability of SAP and arrangement mode of thin stick-shaped SAP and placing in natural environment through carbonization reaction of concrete for carbon dioxide capture.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing carbon capture concrete containing organic material net-structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; adjusted net distance of the SAP net structure; and inhibits climate deterioration caused by high carbon dioxide concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the water-reducing agent is a polycarboxylic acid high-performance water-reducing agent. In step (iii), the material of SAP is acrylic cross-linked acrylamide. In step (iii), the thin rod-shaped SAP must be soaked in the concrete hole solution.

PT P
PN CN113215401-A
TI Efficient washing impurity of cobalt hydroxide salt comprises e.g. taking cobalt hydroxide salt to prepare cobalt hydroxide salt slurry liquid, introducing carbon dioxide, stirring reacting, adding sodium hydroxide and controlling pH.
AU WANG B
   YIN J
   HONG T
AE QUZHOU HUAYOUGU NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (QUZH-Non-standard)
   HUAYOU COBALT CO LTD (HUAY-Non-standard)
GA 202193881D
AB    NOVELTY - Efficient washing impurity of cobalt hydroxide salt, comprises (i) taking cobalt hydroxide salt to prepare cobalt hydroxide salt slurry liquid; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into cobalt hydroxide salt slurry liquid under normal temperature and pressure, stirring reacting, continuously adding sodium hydroxide, controlling pH of slurry liquid to be stable at 8-14; (iii) finishing reaction, adding sodium carbonate to reaction liquid for stirring reaction; (iv) performing liquid-solid separation on reaction liquid to obtain primary filter residue and primary filtrate; (v) heating the primary filtrate to obtain reaction liquid containing calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate precipitate; (vi) performing liquid-solid separation on reaction liquid obtained in step (v) to obtain a secondary filter residue and secondary filtrate; and (vii) returning obtained secondary filtrate to production system for mixing the slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for efficient washing impurity of cobalt hydroxide salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy; obtains stable quality and high purity product; has low product cobalt loss, demagnesization rate and decalcification rate can reach more than 60%; and greatly reduces production water, and greatly reduces cost of subsequent treatment process consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (vii), obtained secondary filtrate contains cobalt ions, adding sodium sulfide to recover cobalt when cobalt ion is enriched to set concentration, and returning liquid to production system.

PN CN113151157-A; CN113151157-B
TI Passaging endoderm stem/progenitor cells by inoculating gastric myofibroblasts treated with mitomycin-C in well plate, drying, preparing passage medium, picking endoderm stem/progenitor cell clones, placing in passage medium and subculturing under carbon dioxide in incubator.
AU WANG Y
   WANG S
   ZHANG W
   QIN J
   WANG X
   CHANG M
   YAN F
   PEI X
AE FIELD OPERATION BLOOD TRANSFUSION INST P (JSYX-C)
GA 202191584F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for passaging endoderm stem/progenitor cells involves (a) inoculating gastric myofibroblasts treated with mitomycin-C as a trophoblast in the well plate or applying fibronectin (FN) glue or cell-TAK (RTM: a nonimmunogenic extracellular matrix protein preparation) (cell tissue adhesion) and spreading in the well plate and drying at room temperature, (b) preparing passage medium 1: advanced Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium: nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/DF12) and A83-01-Wnt3a-basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF) (AWF) or passage medium 2: advanced DMEM/DF12 and A83-01 and (c) manually picking the endoderm stem/progenitor cell clones, dividing into small pieces with a ratio of 1:3-4, placing in FN-passage medium 1, CT-passage medium 1, or trophoblast-passage medium 2 and subculturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator to obtain passaged endoderm stem/progenitor cells.
   USE - The method is useful for passaging endoderm stem/progenitor cells for inducing differentiation into liver cells, pancreatic beta cells and intestinal cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale subculture and good growth and maintain the original clonal state of the passage endoderm stem/progenitor cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the passage medium 1 comprises 0.5 mu M A83-01, 50 ng/ml Wnt3a and 10 ng/ml bFGF and the passage medium 2 comprises 0.5 mu M A83-01. The step (c) involves (i) using the primary isolated digestive tract-derived epithelial cells as starting cells to expand and culture the digestive tract-derived epithelial cells, (ii) treating the trophoblast cells with mitomycin-C, washing, and digesting the cells with enzymes, (iii) adding the trophoblast cells obtained in step (ii) to the digestive tract-derived epithelial cells obtained in step (i) and continuing co-culturing overnight and (iv) changing to the reprogramming medium on the second day, changing the medium once in every 2-3 days and culturing for 7-15 days to obtain induced endoderm stem/progenitor cell clones. In step (iv), the reprogramming medium is a mixture of a combination of small molecule compounds, a basal medium and a basic additive component for cell culture, where the small molecule compound combination is FBP, Bay K8644, Bix01294, SB431542, VPA, RG108, PD0325901 and PS48, combination of Bay K8644, Bix01294, A83-01, VPA, RG108, PD0325901 and PS48 in a molar ratio of 50:12500:12.5:1:12.5:50:125:87500, collectively referred to as 8M combination, combination of Bix01294, Bay K 8644, RG108 and SB431542 in a molar ratio of 12.5:12500:12.5:1:12.5:50:125:87500, referred to as BBRS combination or combination of A83-01, Bay K 8644, RG108 and Bix01294, referred to as BBRA combination. In step (i), the starting cells are epithelial cells derived from the digestive tract, including gastric epithelial cells and duodenal epithelial cells and the starting cells are cultured in Kubota medium at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator for 5 days. In step (ii), the trophoblast cells are stromal cells derived from the digestive tract, including gastric myofibroblasts or small intestinal myofibroblasts. The step (iii) involves adding the trophoblast cells obtained in step (ii) at a density of 1-3x 105 per cm2 to the epithelial cells from the digestive tract that are cultured in step (i) and culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator overnight or for 12-16 hours. In the 8M combination, the RG has concentration of 0.01-1 mu M. In the BBRS combination and BBRA combination, SB43152 has concentration of 1-10 mu M, A83 has concentration of 0.4-1 mu M, RG108 has concentration of 0.01-1 mu M, Bix01294 has concentration of 0.1-2 mu M, and Bay K 8644 has concentration of 1-4 mu M. The basic medium is advanced DMEM/F12, and the basic supplementary components for cell culture include 2 mM 1X glutamine and antibiotics, where the antibiotic is penicillin-streptomycin, the penicillin has concentration of 100 U/ml and streptomycin has concentration of 0.1 mg/ml.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing an you only                look once version-3 (YOLOv3) network based on                cavity convolution and characteristic graph fusion.                Characteristic information of an image is extracted                through the YLOv3 network. First target information                is obtained. Second target information is                determined according to carbon dioxide                concentration difference detected by a sensor by a                vectorization position. The first target                information and the second target information are                fused to obtain a final target position. Target                characteristic information of different scales of                an endoscope image is obtained by a residual                module.
   USE - Deep learning based air tube cannula                positioning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting air tube opening                in real time in a rapid manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a deep learning based air tube cannula                positioning device; and
   (2) a computer readable storage medium storing                a set of instructions for positioning an air tube                cannula based on deep learning; and
   (3) a computer device comprising a memory for                storing a set of instructions for positioning an                air tube cannula based on deep learning.

GA 202146217K
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide raw material gas supply device, comprising at least 2 raw material gas supply pipeline, and a buffer tank, a compressor and a gas storage tank; the raw material gas supply pipeline comprises a low pressure raw material gas supply pipeline and a high pressure raw material gas supply pipeline; the high pressure raw material gas supply pipeline is directly connected with the buffer tank; the low-pressure raw material gas supply pipeline is connected with the buffer tank through a booster fan; the outlet of the buffer tank is connected with the inlet of the compressor; the outlet of the compressor is connected with the inlet of the gas storage tank. the low pressure raw material gas supply pipeline is provided with a booster fan; the pressure is increased to be similar to the pressure in the high pressure raw material gas supply pipeline; then entering into the buffer tank; in the buffer tank, after mixing two pipeline gas sources, entering the compressor; In this way, it can reduce the work of the compressor, so as to greatly reduce the energy consumption.

PT P
PN RU2738977-C1
TI Method for incubation of poultry eggs, consists in keeping eggs under certain temperature and humidity conditions.
AU KONDRATOV S A
   POSTRIGAN S A
AE POSTRIGAN S A (POST-Individual)
GA 2020D0193D
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to methods of poultry reproduction. Method for incubation of poultry eggs consists in keeping eggs under certain temperature and humidity conditions. At that, parameters of incubation process are loaded into automatic system of automated incubation chamber proceeding from type and breed of agricultural birds according to recommended modes. During incubation with increase of temperature in incubation chamber with release of metabolic heat automatically opening flap, increasing air exchange and provoking air humidity drop. Then water is supplied directly to fan blades, and water, breaking down into fine mixture, lowers temperature. At that, fine mixture moves together with air flow, supplied through shutter, and promotes metabolic heat absorption directly at shellhumidity increases, turning into excess moisture, which is directly proportional to excessive metabolic heat and carbon dioxide emitted by embryos. Removal of excess moisture is controlled by increasing air exchange by opening dampers. More metabolic heat is released, the more it is converted into moisture, and that by a larger angle and for a longer period of time, dampers will be opened. At that, cooling radiators are used only in case of emergency, additional sensors of carbon dioxide and oxygen are not used, and heat sensor is used throughout the incubation period.
   USE - Agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides higher stability of temperature and humidity incubation conditions.
1 cl, 2 tbl

PN CN111751162-A
TI Method for detecting city water quality involves sampling, measuring pH and free carbon dioxide, shaking, measuring organic solution, solid element and microelement, processing average value and obtaining city water quality detecting report.
AU LI X
   FAN Y
   ZHENG X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG A & F (UZAF-C)
GA 2020A1153C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for detecting city water quality comprises (i) sampling, and collecting the urban water quality of at least five different areas using sampling test tubes; (ii) measuring pH and free carbon dioxide; (iii) placing 2 ml dilute hydrochloric acid in the No. 3 test tube and No. 4 test tube respectively, boiling the city water to 100 degrees C, reducing its alkalinity, cooling, dropping EDTA indicator agent into the No. 3 test tube and No. 4 test tube respectively to measure calcium ions, slowing down the titration speed when titrating to rose red, add one drop and shaking it to see if it turns purple, slowing down the titration rate when the solution drops to blue-violet when measuring magnesium ions, dropping one drop and shaking well to see if it turns blue; (iv) measuring organic solution, solid element and microelement; (v) processing the average value; and (vi) obtaining the city water quality detecting report based on the average processing result.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting city water quality.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is scientific and simple, makes the urban water quality qualified, and improves the quality of the urban water quality.

PN CN111547678-A; CN111547678-B
TI Preparation of methanol involves providing dried            and desulfurized biogas, mixing biogas with water vapor            to get mixed gas, performing wet-dry double reforming            on mixed gas in presence of catalyst to obtain            synthesis gas by reaction.
AU XIE J
   ZHANG Z
   GUO Y
   BI G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 2020819095
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol by thermal catalysis of all                biogas components involves providing dried and                desulfurized biogas, mixing the biogas with water                vapor to get a mixed gas, performing wet-dry double                reforming on the mixed gas in the presence of a                catalyst to obtain synthesis gas by reaction and                then preparing methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol by                thermal catalysis of all biogas components.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the new method for deep                utilization of biogas, which utilizes methane,                carbon dioxide and water vapor in the biogas to                react to produce biomethanol; uses renewable biogas                as raw material to directly produce                environmentally-friendly chemical product methanol,                so that methane, carbon dioxide and other                components in the biogas are all participated in                the reaction, which has significant carbon emission                reduction effect; has high energy efficiency; can                introduce water vapor to react with biogas to                prevent the incomplete reaction of methane from                polluting the environment and reduce energy                utilization; and can inhibit the formation of                carbon deposits in the reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for preparing methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume content of                  hydrogen sulfide in the biogas after                  desulfurization treatment is less than 1 ppm. The                  catalyst is a nickel-based catalyst, a                  copper-based catalyst and/or a copper-zinc                  catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: When the synthesis gas                  is prepared, the reaction temperature is 750-850                  degrees C. The synthesis gas is heated at 100-300                  degrees C to prepare methanol. Preferred                  Components: The volume ratio of methane in the                  biogas to the water vapor is 3:2. The methane                  content in the biogas is 50-70%, and the carbon                  dioxide content is 30-50%. When the volume ratio                  of carbon dioxide to methane in the biogas is                  less than 1/3, the carbon dioxide is added to the                  mixed gas until the volume ratio of carbon                  dioxide to methane is greater than or equal to                  1/3.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-E04E1; E11-W; J04-E01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N07-K

PN CN111530325-A; CN212396422-U
TI Production of stachyose by washing high-quality raw material, separating and filtering, introducing carbon dioxide gas, detecting pH change, centrifuging and extracting supernatant, introducing ozone into supernatant, and removing color.
AU CHEN C
   HAN B
   JIANG X
   CHEN N
   DENG H
   LIU D
   HAO J
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV ANHUI WEST (UYWX-C)
GA 202080100C
AB    NOVELTY - Production of stachyose involves weighing high-quality raw material, slicing, washing with clean water, drying, putting raw material into extracting groove, adding clean water into extracting groove, separating and filtering to obtain extracting liquid, boiling extracting liquid, cooling, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adding lime, detecting pH change of extracting liquid in real time, storing extracting liquid, filtering impurities including lime, stirring, centrifuging and extracting supernatant, introducing ozone into supernatant, sterilizing and removing color, introducing filtrate into nanofiltration membrane to purify to obtain purified liquid, introducing D301 anion exchange resin and D311 cation exchange resin into purified liquid to obtain stachyose solution, adding ethanol, heating and stirring, cooling to room temperature, standing to obtain crystalline particles, and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is used for producing stachyose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can make material stirring more fully, is convenient for subsequent stirring process, simplifies production process, reduces production cost, does not need separating and extracting process and not need to add filter device, and solves complex stachyose preparation technique, high production cost and low produced stachyose purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production device of stachyose.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material is Rehmannia, Stachys sieboldii, Salvia miltiorrhiza and natural plant bullion. Preferred Method: Separating and extracting is done by controlling water temperature at 60-80 degrees C and water bath time of 50-70 minutes. The pH value is adjusted to 7.4-8.0. Centrifugation is done at speed of 2000-3000 revolutions/minute (rpm). Drying is done at 45-50 degrees C.

AE CHINA ALUMINUM CORP LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 202045403S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for precipitating crystalline alkali involves (a) injecting carbon dioxide gas to carbon mother liquor, when the caustic alkali content in the solution reaches 0 or the solution pH to less than or equal to 11 and separating liquid and solid to obtain a solid phase aluminum hydroxide and dawsonite mixture, which is returned to sintering process, (b) continuing carbonizing the liquid phase to precipitate sodium bicarbonate crystals and carrying out liquid-solid separation to obtain sodium bicarbonate crystals, which are returned to sintering process, (c) evaporating and concentrating the liquid phase, and (d) subjecting the concentrated slurry to flash evaporation or heat exchanging to cool to precipitate crystalline alkali, replacing the obtained solution with a part of red mud washing liquid and subjecting into a production process of alumina by sintering method and returning the obtained crystalline alkali to the sintering batching.
   USE - The method is useful for precipitating crystalline alkali.
   ADVANTAGE - The method precipitates product at a rate of more than 70% under low temperature conditions, provides high quality product with coarse particle size, which is easy to filter and separate, reduces raw material moisture of sintering method, realizes dry sintering, and reduces energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), during carbonizing, the content of sodium bicarbonate in the solution is 5-100%, and the temperature is controlled at 40-100 degrees C to perform liquid-solid separation to obtain sodium bicarbonate crystalline alkali. In step (c), when the liquid phase is evaporated and concentrated, the Nt concentration of the solution is controlled to reach 120-330 g/l. The step (d) involves cooling the concentrated slurry to 40-100 degrees C and leaving still for 5-100 minutes before carrying out liquid-solid separation.

PN CN111087148-A
TI Anaerobic methane-producing bacteria used for inhibiting methane production, comprises culture medium added with sodium chloride and acetic acid.
AU ZENG J
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 202038833M
AB    NOVELTY - An anaerobic methane-producing bacteria comprises culture medium added with sodium chloride and acetic acid.
   USE - The anaerobic methane-producing bacteria is used for inhibiting methane production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good inhibitory effect and lasting inhibitory effect on methane production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the anaerobic methane-producing bacteria comprising taking sludge, using iron sieve, suspending in culture medium suspension, vibrating and uniformly mixing, centrifuging and removing supernatant, repeating operations, inoculating sludge in culture bottle, adding culture medium, sealing culture bottle, vacuumizing at vacuum degree of less than - 0.1 MPa, introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, putting in constant temperature culture box, culturing at 34-36 degrees C for 15-30 days at speed of 110-130 revolutions/minute (rpm) to enriched methane bacteria, inoculating 1-2 g/L enriched methane bacteria in vessel containing culture medium, aerating with nitrogen for 10-30 minutes, adding sodium chloride and acetic acid to culture medium, sealing container, vacuumizing at vacuum degree of less than or equal to - 0.1 MPa, introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and culturing at 34-36 degrees C for 2-7 days at speed of 110-130 rpm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The bacteria is thermophilic hydrogen methane-producing bacteria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in mixed gas is 75-85%:15-25%. The sodium chloride in culture medium is 1-15 wt.% sodium chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sludge is sewage anaerobic fermentation sludge. The culture medium is 450-550 mg/L ammonium chloride, 180-220 mg/L monopotassium phosphate, 45-55 mg/L sodium sulfate, 45-55 mg/L potassium chloride, 5-15 mg/L calcium chloride, 60-80 mg/L magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.5-1.0 mg/L manganese chloride tetrahydrate, 1-1.5mg/L cobalt(II) chlorate dihydrate, 3.0-4.0 mg/L ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.1-1.0 mg/L aluminum chloride, 0.05-0.15 mg/L sodium molybdate dihydrate, 0.1-0.3 mg/L boric acid, 0.4-0.6 mg/L nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate, 1.0-1.5 mg/L copper(II) chloride dihydrate, 3.0-3.5 mg/L zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 2.0-4.0 mg/L disodium EDTA, 1.0-3.0 mg/L biotin, 1.0-3.0 mg/L folic acid, 5-15 mg/L vitamin B6, 2.0-8.0 mg/L riboflavin, 2.0-6.0 mg/L vitamin B1, 3.0-8.0 mg/L niacin, 3.0-8.0 mg/L pantothenic acid, 0.05-0.15 mg/L vitamin B12, 2.0-8.0 mg/L 4-aminobenzoic acid and 2.0-8.0 mg/L lipoic acid. The acetic acid concentration in culture medium is 0.01-4 g/L. Preferred Method: The iron sieve is 200-mesh sieve. Centrifuging is done for 4-6 minutes at speed of 7500-8500 rpm.

AB    NOVELTY - Escherichia coli engineering strain fixed with                carbon dioxide comprises E. coli engineering strain                knocked out of the fumarate reductase gene (frdBC),                fumarase gene (fumB), lactate dehydrogenase gene                (ldhA) and alcohol dehydrogenase gene (adhE), and                overexpress formate dehydrogenase (FDH), acetyl-CoA                synthetase (ACS), acylated acetaldehyde                dehydrogenase (ACDH), formaldehyde lyase (FLS),                dihydroxyacetone kinase (DHAP), malic enzyme (ME)                and phosphite oxidoreductase (PTXD), is new.
   USE - The engineering strain is useful in the                fermentation of malic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The engineering strain reaches malic acid                yield to 39 g/l, and yield of 1.53 mol/mol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of E. coli engineering bacteria strain                comprising activating E.coli engineering bacteria,                performing aerobic incubation for 12-18 hours under                the conditions of temperature 30-37 degrees C,                rotation speed 700-800 revolutions/minute, oxygen                ventilation 0.8-1.2 vvm, and pH of 6.5-7, adjusting                the oxygen ventilation to 0 vvm and the speed to                180-200 revolutions/minute, introducing the                nitrogen at a rate of 1 vvm for 10-20 minutes,                fermenting for 60-80 hours under anaerobic                conditions and maintaining the pH to                neutrality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The E. coli                  engineering bacteria is based on E. coliMG1655 as                  a host. In the E. coliengineering strain, the                  formate dehydrogenase, acetyl-CoA synthetase,                  acylated acetaldehyde dehydrogenase, formaldehyde                  lyase and dihydroxyacetone kinase gradually                  ligated to the vector for over-expression by                  homo-tail enzyme assembly. The nucleotide                  sequence of the vector comprises sequence of 5351                  base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 5) as given in the                  specification. The malic enzyme gene and the                  phosphite oxidoreductase gene are ligated to the                  vector by homo-tail enzyme assembly for                  overexpression. The nucleotide sequence of the                  vector comprises sequence of 3958 base pairs (SEQ                  ID NO: 6) as given in the specification. The                  nucleotide sequence of the fumarate reductase                  gene is consistent with the gene of accession                  number NP418577.1 or NP418576.1 on NCBI. The                  nucleotide sequence of the fumarase gene is                  consistent with the accession number: NP418546.1                  gene on NCBI. The nucleotide sequence of the                  lactate dehydrogenase gene is consistent with the                  accession number: NP415898.1 gene on NCBI. The                  nucleotide sequence of the alcohol dehydrogenase                  gene is consistent with the accession number:                  NP415757.1 gene on NCBI. The nucleotide sequence                  of the formate dehydrogenase gene is consistent                  with the accession number: CP001666.1 gene on                  NCBI. The nucleotide sequence of the acetyl-CoA                  synthetase gene is consistent with the accession                  number: NP418493.1 gene on NCBI. The gene                  sequence of the acylated acetaldehyde                  dehydrogenase comprises sequence of 1383 base                  pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1) as given in the                  specification. The gene sequence of formaldehyde                  lyase comprises sequence of 1728 base pairs (SEQ                  ID NO: 2) as given in the specification. The gene                  sequence of dihydroxypyruvate kinase comprises                  sequence of 1755 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 5) as                  given in the specification. The nucleotide                  sequence of the malic enzyme gene is consistent                  with the accession number: NP418493.1 gene on                  NCBI. The nucleotide sequence of the phosphite                  oxidoreductase gene comprises sequence of 1011                  base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1) as given in the                  specification. The fermentation medium for                  fermentation comprises 40-50 g/l glucose, 20-50                  mM Sodium phosphite dibasic pentahydrate, 30-50                  mM potassium bicarbonate, 15.11 g/l sodium                  dihydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, 3 g/l                  potassium dihydrogenphosphate, 1 g/l ammonium                  chloride, 0.5 g/l sodium chloride, and each liter                  medium contains 1 ml trace element solution. The                  trace element solution comprises 2.4 g/l ferric                  chloride hexahydrate, 0.3 g/l cobalt(II) chloride                  hexahydrate, 0.15 g/l cupric chloride, 0.3 g/l                  zinc chloride tetrahydrate, 0.3 g/l sodium                  permanganate, 0.075 g/l boric Acid, 0.495 g/l                  manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, dissolved in                  0.1 M hydrochloric acid.

PN WO2020000121-A1
TI Method for generating low-carbon emissions fossil energy, involves oxygen-enhanced combustion increasing oxygen concentration of fossil fuel combustion atmosphere, so that combustion generates heat energy.
AU PENG S
AE PENG S (PENG-Individual)
GA 202001678X
AB    NOVELTY - The low-carbon emissions fossil energy generating method involves oxygen-enhanced combustion increasing the oxygen concentration of the fossil fuel combustion atmosphere, so that the combustion generates heat energy while increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in the combustion smoke. The combustion flue gas is washed with seawater to perform high-carbon decarbonization capture of the flue gas to produce clean decarbonized flue gas and dissolved carbon dioxide in the flue gas. The water quality restoration is diluted having acidic washing water. The fresh seawater restore the pH of the acidic washing water to the legally prescribed value allowed to be discharged to the ocean and become the washing discharge seawater. The carbon in marine waters has a storage that injects the washed and discharged seawater.
   USE - Method for generating low-carbon emissions fossil energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables achieving long-term safe and eco-friendly utilization of resources in a natural marine carbon sink, and generating low-cost clean energy which produces low-carbon emission to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for generating low-carbon emissions fossil energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of method for generating low-carbon emissions fossil energy.

PN CN110540631-A; CN110540631-B
TI Flame-retardant material used for fire-resistant core board or outer board, comprises norbornane diisocyanate oligomer, polyester polyol, starch copolymer, hydroxyphosphate, melamine cyanurate, cage polysilsesquioxane and antioxidant.
AU FU D
   CHEN Y
AE HUNAN ZHENQIU FIRE IND CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019A4377U
AB    NOVELTY - Flame-retardant material comprises 15-23 pts. wt. norbornane diisocyanate oligomer, 8-16 pts. wt. polyester polyol, 6-14 pts. wt. starch copolymer, 2-4 pts. wt. trimethylol phosphorus oxide, 1.5-3 pts. wt. hydroxyphosphate, 1.5-3 pts. wt. melamine cyanurate, 1-2.4 pts. wt. cage polysilsesquioxane and 1-2 pts. wt. antioxidant.
   USE - The material is foamed material, which is used for fire-resistant core board or outer board (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material improves flame retardancy and mechanical properties, effectively ensures uniform cells, has high foaming ratio, light in weight, high in material strength and excellent in cushioning performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the flame-retardant material involving (i) obtaining pre-foamed mixture by blending the raw materials with acetone, stirring the reaction, and filling nitrogen to expel gas, (ii) placing the pre-foamed mixture obtained in step (i) under high pressure to react, filling with carbon dioxide, heating, and after full reaction, releasing the pressure to release carbon dioxide to obtain the crude product, and (iii) quickly cooling the crude product obtained in step (ii), and placing at room temperature to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molecular weight of norbornane diisocyanate oligomer is 1800-12000, and the content of isocyanate groups is 15-25%. The amount of acetone added in step (i) is 10-20 %mass. Preferred Components: The hydroxyphosphate comprises bis (4-hydroxybutyl) phenyl phosphate and bis (4-hydroxyphenyl) phenyl phosphate. The antioxidant comprises butylated hydroxytoluene (Antioxidant BHT), pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) (antioxidant 1010), Octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate (antioxidant 1076) and/or 3, 4-Dihydro-6-hydroxy-2, 5, 7, 8-tetramethyl-2H-1-benzopyran-2-carboxylic (antioxidant 164). Preferred Conditions: The blending temperature in step (i) is carried out at 47-53 degrees C and 100-150 rpm for 3-5 minutes. The stirring reaction temperature in step (i) is 75-85 degrees C, reaction time is 1.5-2.5 hours, the pressure in the high pressure reaction in step (ii) is 18.5-24 MPa, the reaction temperature is 110-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-6 hours. The pressure relief speed in step (ii) is 0.8-1.2 mPa.second. The rapid cooling temperature in step (iii) is -30 to -40 degrees C, and the rapid cooling time is 0.5-1.5 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyester polyol comprises polycaprolactone polyol and polycarbonate diol. Preferred Composition: The molecular weight of the polyester polyol is 4000-12000. The starch copolymer comprises polyglycolic acid grafted starch copolymer, where the grafting rate of polyglycolic acid in the polyglycolic acid grafted starch copolymer is 24-40%. The cage polysilsesquioxane comprises octyl hydroxybutyl cage polysilsesquioxane and 9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphaphenanthrene-10-oxide grafted cage polysilsesquioxane.

AE VALDEZ O A (VALD-Individual)
   SANTOS B D U (SANT-Individual)
GA 202068961Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing microalgal biomass in crops under open field conditions using low-density polyethylene photobioreactor (PBR) installed in a metal structure, involves installing a metal structure supporting the low density polyethylene tubular FBRs and a connective aeration system for the reactors; installing the FBRs with polyethylene bags where the bags will fold in half and hang on hooks on the structure; installing a crop agitation system through continuous injection of pre-filtered atmospheric air; positioning the FBR at an angle of 10 degrees with respect to the solar light incident; carrying out a scaling under laboratory conditions with culture medium until reaching the stationary phase; culturing in bioreactors in outdoor conditions; and recovering microalgal biomass by flocculation.
   USE - The process is useful for producing microalgal biomass in crops under open field conditions using low-density polyethylene PBR installed in a metal structure (claimed).

The costs of capital are reduced to the take out the need of bubbling and the compressors for the cells and suspending the mixing of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The method further involves pre-filtering with a 0.45 mu m Whatman brand nylon cartridge, with an average flow of 50 cm3/s; and using culture medium comprising 75.0 g/L sodium nitrate (NaNO3), 5.0 g/L sodium dihydrogen phosphate monohydrate (NaH2PO4.H2O), 3.5 g iron(III) chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3.6H2O), 436 g Na2EDTA, and one of each component from the set of trace metals comprising 9.8 mg/ml copper sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO4.5H2O), 22 mg/ml zinc sulfate heptahydrate (ZnSO4.7H2O), 10 mg/ml cobalt chloride hexahydrate (CoCl2.6H2O), 180 mg/ml manganese chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2.4H2O), and 6.3 mg/ml sodium molybdate dihydrate (Na2MoO4.2H2O).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal structure is 2.5 meter high, 6 meter long and 2 meters in width, with hooks for hanging five FBRs.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameter: The polyethylene bags of 30 L have dimensions of width of 0.20 m by height of 0.1 m.

   USE - As one-step embryo culture medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the one-step embryo culture medium, which involves formulating sodium chloride, potassium chloride, magnesium sulfate, calcium chloride, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium bicarbonate, N-(2-hydroxyethyl) piperazine-N'-2-ethanesulfonic acid/4-hydroxyl ethylpiperazineethanesulfonic acid, 3-(N-morpholinyl) propanesulfonic acid sodium salt-sodium, EDTA, sodium pyruvate, alanylglutamine, taurine, glucose, D-sorbitol, alpha -lipoic acid, acetyl L-carnitine or acetyl L-carnitine hydrochloride and amino acid under a hundred-level conditions to ultrapure water, mixing the materials to obtain solution I, flushing protective gas in solution I, adding human blood albumin, insulin, transferrin and selenium after carbon dioxide protective gas is flushed, filtering the mixture through 0.22 mu m filter membrane, sterilizing the mixture in carbon dioxide incubator for 5-10 hours, and aseptically packaging equilibrated solution II in protective gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of the composition and the basic culture medium is 0.1-1%. TI Oil field waste heat utilization system has dilute solution remaining in generator is adiabatically throttled, and refrigerant in absorber is sprayed to absorb evaporated refrigerant to form absorption refrigeration system cycle.
AU XIE Y
   WANG Y
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201968087M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a refrigerant vapor exchanges heat with a supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid of a supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system in a condenser (3), and cools and condenses into a refrigerant solution. The refrigerant solution enters an expansion valve (4), and the pressure drops to the evaporation pressure. The refrigerant solution evaporates and absorbs heat to generate refrigerant vapor. The heat is exchanged with water in an evaporator (5) to produce cold water for air conditioning (6). The dilute solution remaining in a generator (2) is adiabatically throttled by a pressure reducing valve (9), and the pressure is lowered. The refrigerant in an absorber (7) is sprayed to absorb the evaporated refrigerant to form an absorption refrigeration system cycle.
   USE - Oil field waste heat utilization system based on absorption refrigeration.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves energy utilization efficiency, and has a compact system structure, and has important engineering significance.

PT P
PN CN109627156-A; CN109627156-B
TI Extracting and separating high-purity kaurenoic acid by naturally drying arrowhead corms, crushing and sieving to arrowhead corm powder and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and dynamic extraction to supercritical extract.
AU LIAO Z
   DU Y
   XU X
   HOU P
   FENG Y
   ZHAO S
   LU D
   LI J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201938601S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting and separating high-purity kaurenoic acid involves (a) slicing and naturally drying fresh arrowhead corms in the shade and crushing and sieving to obtain an arrowhead corm powder, (b) placing the arrowhead corm powder in a supercritical extraction kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, carrying out static extraction by using an ethyl acetate solution as an entrainer and performing dynamic extraction to obtain a supercritical extract, (c) placing the supercritical extract in a raw material cylinder of a molecular distillation device and collecting a light phase component and (d) concentrating and drying the obtained light phase component, adding an ethanol solution, recrystallizing and filtering and washing to obtain a crystal of kaurenoic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and separating high-purity kaurenoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the plant arrowhead which is widely planted and cost-effective, further provides a method for separating kaurenoic acid from natural plants, is simple and easy to operate, ensures high yield and high purity of up to 99%, does not use toxic and harmful solvents, ensures low environmental pollution degree and is suitable for industrial application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves crushing to more than 30-mesh and sieving to obtain the arrowhead corm powder. The step (b) involves carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 40-65 degrees C and the extraction pressure at 20-30 MPa, taking ethyl acetate as an entrainer, where the flow rate ratio of carbon dioxide to ethyl acetate is 100:10-15 and carrying out static extraction for 5-8 minutes and dynamic extraction for 2-3 hours. The step (c) involves loading the supercritical extract in the raw material cylinder of the molecular distillation device and pre-heating the molecular distillation device parameter, opening a vacuum pump to vacuumize, setting the molecular distillation temperature at 160-190 degrees C, rotating speed of the scraping film at 250-300 rpm and pressure at 0.2-0.5 Pa, opening the feeding valve of the raw material cylinder, adjusting the flow rate of the material to 1.5-2 ml/min and collecting the light phase component. The step (d) involves adding ethanol solution, preferably a hot ethanol solution at 40-60 degrees C.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

GA 2018A5402E
AB    NOVELTY - A desiccant preparation method involves adding 280-300 g iron oxide powder and 80-100 g graphite powder to ball mill, performing ball milling in presence of dry air to obtain nano mixed particles, adding the mixed particles and 20-35 g adhesive to ball mill, mixing at room temperature, molding to form modified electrode, placing the modified electrode in sealed atmosphere filled with argon, utilizing 220 V to excite arc between two poles, performing intermittent excitation, such that heat generated by the arc causes material temperature to reach 3500-3900 degrees C, graphite in the electrode turns into carbon vapor and iron oxide in the electrode turns into iron oxide vapor, at 3500-3900 degrees C, reducing the iron oxide vapor and carbon vapor at high temperature to form iron and carbon dioxide, such that carbon vapor and carbon dioxide form carbon nanotubes under action of electric arc and remaining iron oxide acts as catalyst and naturally cooling remaining gas on surface of iron oxide.
   USE - Method for preparing desiccant.

PN IN201711006041-A
TI Composition for performing in-vitro reprogramming of cells for generation of desirable cell types, comprise serine protease and Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium, and has no risk of integration of viral genome into host genome.
AU GUPTASARMA M L
   SHARMA M
   RAM J
AE POSTGRADUATE MEDICAL EDUCATION & RES INS (POST-Non-standard)
   EYE RES FOUND (EYER-Non-standard)
GA 201868484T
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises serine protease and Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium (DMEM).
   USE - Composition for performing in-vitro reprogramming of cells for generation of desirable cell types (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has no risk of integration of viral genome into the host genome, does not require expansion of stem cell clones/spheres/colonies, no risk of acquiring culture dependent mutations, results in induction of a self-sufficient niche of growth factors that induce and maintain stemness/pluripotency, and has the potential to evolve into a rapid, and direct approach for generating customized cell types, and is reproducible, simple and cost effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing in-vitro reprogramming of cells for generation of desirable cell types involves:
   (A) culturing the starting cell types in a media comprising 10 wt.% serum;
   (B) starving the culture obtained for 22-26 hours at 37 degrees C under 5 vol.% carbon dioxide and removing the serum; and
   (C) stimulating with serine protease for 24-96 hours at 37 degrees C under 5 vol.% carbon dioxide to obtain reprogrammed cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The serine protease is 1-10 mu g/ml. The reprogrammed cells obtained is further differentiated into adipocytes using adipocyte differentiation media and further differentiated into lineage specific cell types using specific differentiation conditions. Preferred Components: The starting cell types are selected from D407 cell lines, ARPE19 cell lines, Human Corneal Fibroblasts, dermal fibroblasts, NCI-H292 cancer cell lines, MCF-7 cancer cell lines or MDA-MB 231 cancer cell lines. The serine protease is trypsin. The media is selected from adipogenic differentiation media, neuronal differentiation media, cardiomyocyte differentiation media, chondrocyte differentiation media, osteogenic differentiation media, or hepatocyte differentiation media. Transcription factor or microRNA (miRNA) is used for differentiation of reprogrammed fibroblasts, where the transcription factor and miRNAs are selected from MyoD (muscle), Ascl1, Brn2, Myt1l (neurons), Gata4, Mef2c, Tbx5 (cardiomyocytes), Myocardin, miR-1, miR-133, GHMT (cardiomyocytes), Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, or c-MYC (short reprogramming for endothelial cells).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
