PN CN108359837-A; CN108359837-B
TI Preparation of oxygen-free high copper bar containing silver involves heating and roasting silver, zirconium, copper and chromium, melting in furnace, injecting carbon dioxide gas, adjusting amount of water and cooling.
AU XU F
AE CHONGQING GEPAI ELECTRICAL MATERIAL CO (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING GEPAI ELECTRICAL MATERIAL CO (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201862873W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of oxygen-free high copper bar containing silver involves heating and roasted 0.0085-0.2 wt.% silver, 0.015-0.05 wt.% zirconium, 0.015-0.03 wt.% chromium and balance copper, melting the roasted raw materials at 1138-1550 degrees C, covering the surface of the induction furnace and the transition chamber with graphite scales, and covering surface of the melting furnace with charcoal for oxygen barrier treatment, injecting carbon dioxide gas from top to bottom on the surface of the whole furnace to re-separate oxygen, adjusting amount of water in the cooling water of the upper power frequency furnace to keep the water pressure at 0.2-0.25 MPa, and maintaining the temperature of the cooling water at 50-55 degrees C. The upper induction frequency furnace is equipped with a copper crystallizer.
   USE - Preparation of oxygen-free high copper bar containing silver.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and is carried out economically with reduced energy consumption. The method produces high purity silver-containing copper with improved oxidation resistance, creep resistance, abrasion resistance, heat resistance and strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The silver has purity of 99.99% and copper is high purity cathode electrolytic copper.

PT P
PN CN108085337-A; CN108085337-B
TI Screening cell line of glutamine synthetase (GS) expression system, by transfecting expression vector containing target gene into cell, culturing, selecting positive cloned cells, processing and screening positive monoclonal cell strain.
AU ZHANG W
   MA Y
   RUAN K
   ZOU Y
   WANG M
   GE P
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN X
AE SINOBIOWAY BIOMEDICINE CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 201843381W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for screening a cell line of a glutamine synthetase (GS) expression system, involves (a) transfecting expression vector containing the target gene into host cell under pressurized condition and in presence of methionine sulfoximine, culturing the cells at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 3-4 weeks, detecting expression level of the fermentation broth, selecting positive cloned cells based on optical density (OD) value, and performing expansion culture using the obtained positive cloned cells until the cell viability reaches 85% or more, and (b) subjecting the positive cloned cells to monoclonal screening by limiting dilution method, adding a host cell having a density of 1 greater than or equal to 104-1x 105 cells/well to a 96-well plate, culturing the cells at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, after 3-5 weeks, observing the cell growth in the 96-well plate, and screening positive monoclonal cell strain to obtain GS expression system cell line.
   USE - The method is useful for screening a cell line of GS expression system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits cloning efficiency of 22.1% on average, and improved monoclonalization efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecules: The expression vector containing the target gene comprises a recombinant plasmid of GS synthase, cytomegalovirus (CMV) promoter, light chain, CMV promoter and heavy chain. The host cell is a CHOK1SV cell or knockout Chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cell comprising GS gene. Preferred Method: The GS expression system transfects an expression vector carrying the GS gene into a host cell. The antibody heavy and light chain genes are inserted downstream of the GS gene of the expression vector. The expression vector is eukaryotic expression vector containing GS gene. The method detects expression quantity of fermentation broth by HPLC method or enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) method.

TI Reduction of acid gas e.g. carbon dioxide, sulfur            dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, from streams e.g. natural            gas and post-combustion flue gas, involves contacting            stream with solvent system comprising glycerol            derivative.
AU BARA J E
   FLOWERS B
   MITTENTHAL M
AE UNIV ALABAMA (UABR-C)
   UNIV ALABAMA (UABR-C)
GA 201819408K
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of acid gas from a stream involves                contacting the stream with a solvent system                comprising glycerol derivative (I).
   USE - Reduction of acid gas e.g. carbon dioxide,                sulfur dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, from stream                e.g. natural gas for sweetening natural gas feed                stream, byproducts of chemical reactions, gaseous                products produced from water-gas shift reaction and                post-combustion flue gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently reduces acid gases from                the streams, and provides products with high purity                and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reduction of acid gas from a stream involves                contacting the stream with a solvent system                comprising glycerol derivative having a structure                of formula (I).
   R1-R3=optionally substituted 1-20C alkyl,                2-20C alkenyl, 2-20C alkynyl, cycloalkyl, aryl,                thio, alkoxy, aryloxy, 2-20C fluoroalkyl, 2-20C                trifluoromethyl-terminated alkyl, and/or 2-20C                nitrile-terminated alkyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) solvent system, which comprises the                derivative (I) and acid gas; and
   (2) method for sweetening natural gas feed                stream, which involves contacting the natural gas                feed stream comprising acid gases with the solvent                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                gas-solubility apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The process is carried                  out in a hollow fiber membrane contactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The derivative (I) has                  a viscosity of 5 cP or less, preferably 1.5 cP or                  less at 25 degrees C. Preferred Components: The                  derivative (I) is selected from                  1,2,3-trimethoxypropane, 1,2,3-triethoxypropane,                  1,2,3-tri(2-methoxyethyl)propane, and                  1,2,3-tris(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)propane,                  preferably 1,2,3-trimethoxypropane. Preferred                  Composition: The solvent system comprises at                  least 50 vol.% derivative (I). Preferred Process:                  The acid gas is removed from the stream through                  absorption into the derivative (I).

AE JIANGSU PROVINCE METALLURGICAL DESIGN (BJSW-C)
GA 2018183547
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of coal gangue involves mixing gangue, limestone and magnesite to obtain mixture, adding starch solution, pelletizing, drying, feeding to rotary hearth furnace to obtain calcined pellet, leaching, injecting carbon dioxide into silica-aluminum leaching solution, carrying out aluminizing treatment to obtain aluminum hydroxide and aluminum residual solution, concentrating precipitated aluminum residual solution, leaching with acid, aging, filtering, washing and drying to prepare silica.
   USE - Processing of coal gangue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and energy-efficient processing of coal gangue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing of coal gangue involves mixing gangue, limestone and magnesite to obtain mixture, adding starch solution, pelletizing to obtain mixed pellets, drying mixed pellets, feeding dried pellets to a rotary hearth furnace provided with pre-desiliconization zone and main reaction zone to obtain calcined pellet, leaching calcined pellets with sodium carbonate solution to obtain silicon-aluminum leaching solution and insoluble slag, injecting carbon dioxide into silica-aluminum leaching solution, carrying out aluminizing treatment to obtain aluminum hydroxide and aluminum residual solution, baking aluminum hydroxide to obtain aluminum oxide, concentrating precipitated aluminum residual solution, leaching with acid, adjusting pH, aging, filtering, washing filtrate with water and drying to prepare silica. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for processing of coal gangue.

AU LIU J
   ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201811770X
AB    NOVELTY - Automatic carbon dioxide recovery device comprises recycling device and automatic control device. The recovery device includes a defoamer, sealed cans, scrubbers, first separator, carbon dioxide compressor, cooler, second separator, filter, condenser and carbon dioxide storage tank. The gas inlet of the defoamer is connected with the gas outlet of the carbon dioxide of the fermenter. The gas outlet of the sealed tank is connected with the gas inlet of the scrubber. The gas outlet of the scrubber is connected to the inlet of the first water separator.
   USE - The device is useful for fermentation tank (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces carbon dioxide emission, protects environment and improves the economical and social benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Automatic carbon dioxide recovery device comprises recycling device and automatic control device. The recovery device includes a defoamer, sealed cans, scrubbers, first separator, carbon dioxide compressor, cooler, second separator, filter, condenser and carbon dioxide storage tank. The gas inlet of the defoamer is connected with the gas outlet of the carbon dioxide of the fermenter. The gas outlet of the sealed tank is connected with the gas inlet of the scrubber. The gas outlet of the scrubber is connected to the inlet of the first water separator. The gas outlet of the first water separator is connected with the inlet of the carbon dioxide compressor. The carbon dioxide compressor outlet is connected to the cooler. The outlet of the cooler is connected with the second steam separator. The second steam separator gas outlet and filter inlet even. The outlet of the filter is connected to the condenser. The condenser gas outlet is connected to carbon dioxide storage tank. The automatic control device includes a control system, a level gauge, compressor pressure sensor, carbon dioxide concentration detector and carbon dioxide storage tank to store pressure sensors and alarms. The level gauge is arranged in the demister to measure the foam level in the demister drainage unobstructed. The compressor pressure sensor is set on the carbon dioxide compressor and carbon dioxide compressor switch solenoid valve control connection. The carbon dioxide compressor gas source reaches the upper limit position carbon dioxide compressor switch solenoid valve automatically shut down with the reduction of gas volume, intake valve and automatically open and carbon dioxide compressor and the carbon dioxide concentration detector is installed in the condenser. The carbon dioxide concentration in the condenser was measured when the carbon dioxide concentration reaches the set concentration. The signal is transmitted to the control system, the control system signal controls the vent valve of the condenser to close, and vice versa opens the vent valve when the carbon dioxide storage tank gas pressure reaches the set value and the control system through the carbon dioxide storage tank pressure sensor control carbon dioxide storage tank switch solenoid valve closed to stop inflating. The control system for transmit signals to control the alarm remind staff for carbon dioxide storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the automatic carbon dioxide recovery device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A blower is connected between the sealed tank and the scrubber. The pressure of the carbon dioxide compressor is 1.6-1.8 MPa. The control system includes a display screen, which may be a level gauge, a compressor pressure sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration detector, carbon dioxide storage tank pressure sensor and alarm data storage for real-time understanding. The control system includes control module and a data transmission module. The control module is connected with a compressor pressure sensor, carbon dioxide concentration detector, carbon dioxide storage tank pressure sensor and alarm signal storage, data transmission module for data transmission storage. The control system includes a start switch. The alarm is an acoustooptic alarm.

PN JP2021187773-A
TI Hydrocarbon production system comprises recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide, reactor for producing hydrocarbons, and capture unit for capturing unreacted carbon dioxide discharged from reactor, where reactor produces hydrocarbons.
AU KAMADA H
   MIZUKAMI N
   ISHIDA Y
   YOSHINOYA T
   OKUNO S
AE IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
GA 2021E7354V
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon production system (1) comprises a recovery unit (30) for recovering carbon dioxide from a gas containing carbon dioxide, a reactor (60) for producing hydrocarbons, and a capture unit (80) for capturing unreacted carbon dioxide discharged from the reactor. The reactor produces the hydrocarbons from a raw material containing carbon dioxide recovered by the recovery unit, carbon dioxide captured by the capture unit, and hydrogen. The capture unit includes a second absorption tower that absorbs carbon dioxide with an absorbent material containing an alkaline solution. The recovery unit includes a first absorption tower that absorbs carbon dioxide with the alkaline solution and a dissipation tower that dissipates carbon dioxide absorbed by the first absorption tower. The alkaline solution in which carbon dioxide is absorbed in the second absorption tower is transferred to the dissipation tower of the recovery unit.
   USE - Hydrocarbon production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves utilization rate of carbon dioxide, and reduces amount of carbon dioxide supplied to separation device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hydrocarbon generation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the hydrocarbon production system.
   Hydrocarbon production system (1)
   Recovery unit (30)
   Reactor (60)
   Capture unit (80)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The device comprises capturing material comprising a porous body, an alkali metal, and/or an alkaline earth metal.

PN CN111420515-A
TI Improved low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide product gas boosting process by sending sulfur-free methanol to sulfur-free methanol flash tank, using pressure reducing valve to depressurize and sending to carbon dioxide flash tank.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHU Y
   SONG M
   ZHENG B
   YUAN W
   LIU Z
AE HUBEI YIHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020694851
AB    NOVELTY - Improved low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide product gas boosting process, involves (a) sending the sulfur-free methanol after the low-temperature methanol wash and absorb carbon dioxide to the sulfur-free methanol flash tank, (b) using the first pressure reducing valve to depressurize the sulfur-free methanol after flash evaporation in step (a), (c) sending the sulfur-free methanol evaporated in step (b) to a carbon dioxide flash tank, and (d) sending the carbon dioxide product gas flashed in step (c) to a carbon dioxide compressor inlet after heat exchange, pressurizing the carbon dioxide product gas by the carbon dioxide compressor, and sending the pressurized carbon dioxide product gas to a urea synthesis tower to synthesize urea, and using the second pressure reducing valve to depressurize the sulfur-free methanol solution flashed in step (c), and sending the methanol solution to the carbon dioxide desorption tower for desorption.
   USE - As improved low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide product gas boosting process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process increases the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas at the inlet of the compressor, increases the air pumping capacity of the compressor, reduces the power consumption of the compressor, and reduces the production cost of urea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improved low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide product gas boosting process, involves (a) sending the sulfur-free methanol after the low-temperature methanol wash and absorb carbon dioxide to the sulfur-free methanol flash tank for flash evaporation, (b) using the first pressure reducing valve to depressurize the sulfur-free methanol after flash evaporation in step (a), (c) sending the sulfur-free methanol evaporated in step (b) to a carbon dioxide flash tank for flash evaporation, and (d) sending the carbon dioxide product gas flashed in step (c) to a carbon dioxide compressor inlet after heat exchange, pressurizing the carbon dioxide product gas by the carbon dioxide compressor, and sending the pressurized carbon dioxide product gas to a urea synthesis tower to synthesize urea, and using the second pressure reducing valve to depressurize the sulfur-free methanol solution flashed in step (c), and sending the evaporated sulfur-free methanol solution to the carbon dioxide desorption tower for desorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: In step (a), the pressure of the sulfur-free methanol is 1.85 MPaA after flashing in the sulfur-free methanol flash tank. In step (b), after the pressure is reduced by the first pressure reducing valve, the pressure of the sulfur-free methanol is 0.60 MPaA. In step (c), after the carbon dioxide flash tank is flashed, the pressure of the flashed carbon dioxide product gas is 0.55 MPaA. In step (d), after the carbon dioxide compressor is pressurized, the pressure of the carbon dioxide product gas is 20 MPaA. In step (d), after the pressure is reduced by the second pressure reducing valve, the pressure of the sulfur-free methanol solution is 0.32 MPaA.

PN WO2022223606-A1
TI Operating electric arc furnace for steel mill,            involves injecting hydrogen into molten bath in            electric arc furnace by hydrogen injection devices such            that hydrogen acts as reducing agent for reducing iron            oxide in molten bath.
AU KRULL J
   PAPALIA K
AE WURTH SA PAUL (WURP-C)
GA 2022D4200U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for operating an electric arc furnace,                involves capturing a heated metallurgical gas                comprising water and carbon monoxide, from at least                one facility of a steel mill, conducting the                metallurgical gas to a reactor through a reactor                supply line, transforming the carbon monoxide and                water into hydrogen and carbon dioxide according to                a water-gas shift reaction, by a treatment of the                metallurgical gas within the reactor, and                subsequently separating the hydrogen by a                separation device. The method further comprises                providing an iron-bearing material, which comprises                iron mainly in the form of iron oxide, to the                electric arc furnace, at least partially melting                the iron-bearing material to obtain a molten bath,                conducting the hydrogen through a furnace supply                line to the electric arc furnace, which is arranged                downstream of the furnace supply line, and                injecting the hydrogen into the molten bath in the                electric arc furnace unit of hydrogen injection                devices.
   USE - The method is used for operating an electric                arc furnace, which is used in a steel mill                (claimed) for producing steel from a charging mix                typically made by scrap and hot metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The electric arc furnace is operated in a safe                and efficient manner. The method reduces carbon                dioxide emissions, and reduces the cost of hydrogen                production and transport and storage of large                quantities of hydrogen in a simple and                cost-effective manner, thus reducing the energy                consumption of the furnace. The furnace can be                operated in an industrial environment without the                need for an extensive and safe infrastructure for                transporting and storing the hydrogen, thus saving                valuable resources such as space and energy, in and                on the premises of the mill.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for operating an electric arc furnace,                involves capturing a heated metallurgical gas                comprising water and carbon monoxide, from at least                one facility of a steel mill, conducting the                metallurgical gas to a reactor through a reactor                supply line, transforming the carbon monoxide and                water into hydrogen and carbon dioxide according to                a water-gas shift reaction, by a treatment of the                metallurgical gas within the reactor, and                subsequently separating the hydrogen by a                separation device. The method further comprises                providing an iron-bearing material, which comprises                iron mainly in the form of iron oxide, to the                electric arc furnace, at least partially melting                the iron-bearing material to obtain a molten bath,                conducting the hydrogen through a furnace supply                line to the electric arc furnace, which is arranged                downstream of the furnace supply line, and                injecting the hydrogen into the molten bath in the                electric arc furnace unit of hydrogen injection                devices such that the hydrogen acts as a reducing                agent for reducing iron oxide in the molten bath                during a smelting operation of the electric arc                furnace. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                steel mill.

PN CN114163627-A
TI New polyaromatic-based bifunctional bimetallic            catalysts useful for copolymerization reactions of            alkylene oxides to prepare degradable polycarbonate,            degradable polyester and degradable            poly(ester-carbonate).
AU REN W
   LI Y
   LV X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202241771T
AB    NOVELTY - Polyaromatic-based bifunctional bimetallic                catalysts (A), which are (I-VII) are new. (A) is                two monometallic complexes containing quaternary                ammonium salt or quaternary phosphonium salt, which                are covalently bonded into one molecule through a                rigid bridging group to form bifunctional                bimetallic catalyst.
   USE - (A) is useful for copolymerization reactions                of alkylene oxides to prepare degradable                polycarbonate, degradable polyester and degradable                poly(ester-carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (A): has high activity and selectivity for the                preparation of polycarbonates, polyesters and                poly(ester carbonates); prepares multichiral                bifunctional bimetallic catalysts and the                corresponding chiral polymers can be obtained with                high activity and high enantioselectivity; utilizes                low dosage catalyst, is easy to control molecular                weight and polymer structure, has narrow                distribution and wide temperature range and is                suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyaromatic-based bifunctional bimetallic                catalysts (A), which are of formulae (I-VII) are                new. (A) is two monometallic complexes containing                quaternary ammonium salt or quaternary phosphonium                salt, which are covalently bonded into one molecule                through a rigid bridging group to form bifunctional                bimetallic catalyst.
   M = Fe3+, Co3+, Cr3+, Mn3+, Al3+, In3+ or                Ru3+;
   Z = nitrogen or phosphorus atom;
   n = 0, 1, 2 or 3;
   X = Cl-1, Br-1, NO3-1, CH3C(=O)O-1, ClO4-1,                BF4-1, BPh4-1, CF3C(=O)O-1, 3,5-difluorophenol                anion, pentafluorophenol anion, 2, 4-dinitrophenol                anion or p-nitrophenol anion;
   R1 = H, CH3, CH2CH3, CH(CH3)2, C(CH3)3, OCH3,                OCH2CH3, F, Cl, Br, I or NO2;
   R2 = CH3, CH2CH3, CH(CH3)2, CH2CH2CH2CH3,                cyclohexyl, phenyl or benzyl;
   R3 = H or CH3; and
   R4 = e.g. benzene-1,2-diyl,                cyclohexane-1,2-diyl, ethane-1,2-diyl,                butane-2,3-diyl or propane-1,2-diyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) Use of (A) for the copolymerization                reactions of alkylene oxides with carbon dioxide to                form degradable polycarbonates, for the reaction of                alkylene oxide and cyclic acid anhydride to form                degradable polyester, and in the reaction of                alkylene oxide, carbon dioxide and cyclic acid                anhydride to synthesize degradable                poly(ester-carbonate); and
   (2) Preparing bifunctional bimetallic                multichiral catalyst of formula (XVIII-XX),                comprising bridging framework of (A) and the two                diamine frameworks for constructing catalyst                ligands all use the same chirality to form                bifunctional bimetallic multichiral catalyst,                bridging group is rigid structure of axial                chirality, the two diamine skeletons of the                catalyst ligand are constructed using chiral                cyclohexanediamine and the whole ligand contains                one axial chirality and four carbon chiral centers                and the chiral structure is same i.e. (R,R,R,R,R)-                or (S,S,S,S,S)-configuration, which is used for                asymmetric copolymerization of meso alkylene oxide                with carbon dioxide or cyclic acid anhydride,                asymmetric telomerization to prepare chiral                polycarbonate, chiral polyester or chiral                poly(ester-carbonate) telomer.
   M = Co3+, Cr3+, Al3+ or In3+; and
   X = Cl-1, Br-1, NO3-1, CH3C(=O)O-1,                CF3C(=O)O-1, 3,5-difluorophenol anion,                pentafluorophenol anion, 2,4-dinitrophenol anion or                p-nitrophenol anion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The use method comprises                  reacting alkylene oxide of formulae (VIII-IX) and                  carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst (A) to                  form degradable polycarbonate of formulae (X-XI),                  where the molar ratio of (VIII-IX) to (A) is                  5000:1-1000000:1; the carbon dioxide pressure is                  0.1-5 MPa; the reaction temperature is                  25-120degrees Celsius; the reaction time is 1-36                  hours, the alkylene oxides are cyclohexane oxide,                  cyclopentane oxide, cycloheptane oxide,                  tetrahydrofuran oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, phenyl                  glycidyl ether, styrene oxide, ethylene oxide                  and/or propylene oxide. The use method comprises                  reacting alkylene oxide of formulae (XII-XIII)                  and cyclic acid anhydride of formula (XIV) in                  presence of catalyst (A) to form degradable                  polyester of formula (XV-XVI), where the molar                  ratio of (XII-XIII) and (A) is 2000:1-1000000:1;                  the molar ratio of (XII-XIII) to cyclic acid                  anhydride is 1:1-5:1; the reaction temperature is                  25-150degrees Celsius; the reaction time is 1-36                  hours and the cyclic acid anhydride is e.g.                  dihydrofuran-2,5-dione, furan-2,5-dione,                  3-methylenedihydrofuran-2,5-dione,                  3-oxabicyclo(3.1.0)hexane-2,4-dione or                  hexahydroisobenzofuran-1,3-dione. The use method                  comprises reacting alkylene oxide (XIII), carbon                  dioxide and cyclic acid anhydride to synthesize                  degradable poly(ester-carbonate) of formula                  (XVII), where the molar ratio of (XIII)to (A) is                  5000:1-1000000:1; the molar ratio of (XIII) to                  cyclic acid anhydride is 1:1-10:1; the carbon                  dioxide pressure is 0.1-5 MPa; the reaction                  temperature is 50-150degrees Celsius and the                  reaction time is 1-24 hours. The molecular weight                  of the polycarbonate, polyester or poly(ester                  carbonate) is 3000-1,000,000 g/mol and the                  molecular weight distribution is 1.05-2.50; the                  carbonate unit content in polycarbonate is                  greater than 95%; the content of ester units in                  polyester is greater than 95%; the content of                  carbonate units in the poly(ester-carbonate) is                  5-95%. In the synthesis of chiral polycarbonate                  system, the molar ratio of alkylene oxide to                  bifunctional bimetallic multichiral catalyst is                  5000:1-1000000:1; the carbon dioxide pressure is                  0.1-5 MPa; the reaction temperature is                  25-90degrees Celsius; the reaction time is 1-36                  hours; in the synthetic chiral polyester system,                  the molar ratio of alkylene oxide to cyclic acid                  anhydride is 2000:1-1000000:1; the molar ratio of                  alkylene oxide to cyclic acid anhydride is                  1:1-5:1; the reaction temperature is                  25-100degrees Celsius; the reaction time is 1-36                  hours; in the synthesis of the chiral                  poly(ester-carbonate) telomer system, the molar                  ratio of alkylene oxide to cyclic acid anhydride                  is 1:1-10:1; the carbon dioxide pressure is 0.1-5                  MPa; the reaction temperature is 50-90degrees                  Celsius and the reaction time is 1-24 hours. The                  carbonate units and ester units in the obtained                  telomer is 5-95%; the enantioselectivity of                  carbonate units and ester units in all polymers                  is 88-99%; the molecular weight is 3000-500000                  g/mol, and the molecular weight distribution is                  1.05-2.R = H, CH3, Ph or CH2OPh; andn = 1, 2, or 3.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen by purifying medium                temperature in hydrocarbon fuel reforming comprises                cooling the hydrogen-rich gas mainly hydrogen                generated by the reforming of hydrocarbon fuel to                the target intermediate temperature adsorption                temperature for rough removal, roughly removing the                hydrogen-rich gas by the first stage medium                temperature and pressure swing adsorption process                or the first stage medium temperature swing                pressure swing adsorption process to realize the                removal of most of carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide                and most of carbon monoxide, nitrogen, methane                impurities in hydrogen, obtaining the first stage                product hydrogen, and adjusting the temperature of                the first-stage product hydrogen to the target                adsorption temperature of fine desorption.

              normal-temperature-pressure-swing adsorption                process, the second-stage                room-temperature-pressure-swing adsorption process,                or the in                the first-stage product hydrogen, the fuel hydrogen                for fuel cells with hydrogen sulfide concentration                less-than 4ppb, carbon monoxide concentration PN CN113817644-A
TI Anaerobic digestion of marine product processing waste coupled with microalgae cultivation to co-produce gas-oil feed, comprises pulping the seafood processing waste into pretreatment waste and carrying out anaerobic fermentation reaction.
AU YANG T
   LI R
   XING W
   WANG X
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV SHENYANG AEROSPACE (UYAE-C)
GA 202206890N
AB    NOVELTY - Anaerobic digestion of marine product processing waste coupled with microalgae cultivation to co-produce gas-oil feed, comprises (i) pulping the seafood processing waste into pretreatment waste; (ii) passing the pretreated waste into the anaerobic fermentation reactor to carry out anaerobic fermentation reaction to generate biogas, biogas slurry and biogas residue; (iii) drying the biogas residue, and then granulating it into marine feed; (iv) passing the biogas into the desulfurization tower for purification and desulfurization, and then drying to form purified biogas; (v) diluting the biogas slurry to make pretreatment biogas slurry; (vi) passing the pretreated biogas slurry into the oil-rich microalgae cultivation system, carrying out oil-rich microalgae cultivation, and generating oil-rich microalgae; and (vii) squeezing out bio-oil from oil-rich microalgae, and carrying out anaerobic fermentation reaction together with pretreated sewage.
   USE - The method is useful for anaerobic digestion of marine product processing waste coupled with microalgae cultivation to co-produce gas-oil feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can integrate marine waste treatment, biogas purification and microalgae cultivation, and the high-quality bio-oil produced by microalgae realizes efficient operation and self-sufficient energy by controlling process conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anaerobic digestion of marine product processing waste coupled with microalgae cultivation to co-produce gas-oil feed, comprises (i) pulping the seafood processing waste into pretreatment waste; (ii) passing the pretreated waste into the anaerobic fermentation reactor to carry out anaerobic fermentation reaction to generate biogas, biogas slurry and biogas residue; (iii) drying the biogas residue, and then granulating it into marine feed; (iv) passing the biogas into the desulfurization tower for purification and desulfurization, and then drying to form purified biogas; where, part of the purified biogas is used for combustion and power generation, and the other part is passed into the separation system to separate carbon dioxide and biomethane; using carbon dioxide as gas fertilizer into oil-rich microalgae cultivation system; using carbon dioxide generated by combustion power generation as gas fertilizer into the oil-rich microalgae cultivation system; combustion power generation provides electricity to the light source, and the oil-rich microalgae cultivation system is illuminated by the light source; (v) diluting the biogas slurry with water to form a diluted biogas slurry; adding strong chloroquine into the diluted biogas slurry for sterilization treatment, then using an aeration system for aeration treatment with air, and then adding sodium carbonate solution to adjust the pH to 7-7.5, to make pretreatment biogas slurry; (vi) passing the pretreated biogas slurry into the oil-rich microalgae cultivation system, carrying out oil-rich microalgae cultivation under gas fertilizer and light source, and generating oil-rich microalgae; and (vii) squeezing out bio-oil from oil-rich microalgae, and returning the remaining algal residue to the anaerobic fermentation reactor, and carrying out anaerobic fermentation reaction together with pretreated sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the pulping pretreatment is using a pulping machines to form solid part pretreated waste with a mass content of 20-30%. In step (ii), the temperature of the anaerobic fermentation reaction is 30-40degrees Celsius, and the time is 4-8 hours. In step (iii), the temperature of the drying treatment is 60plus minus3degrees Celsius., and the drying treatment is until the mass content of water in the final material is less than 12.5%. In step (iii), the granulation is to prepare marine feed with a particle size of 3-4 mm. In step (v), the sterilization treatment time is at least 1-2 hours. In step (v), the aeration treatment time is 1-2 hours.Preferred Components: In step (iv), the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the biological methane is less than or equal to 2%, and the balance is methane. In step (iv), the purified biogas introduced into the separation system accounts for 50-65% of the total purified biogas. In step (v), the amount of water added is 4-10 times the mass of biogas slurry.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC D04-B10A; D04-B07D; D04-A02; D04-A01K2; D04-A01J; D05-A04A; D10-A01; H09-F03
IP A23K-010/12; A23K-050/80; B01D-053/22; B01D-053/26; B01D-053/52; B01D-053/78; C02F-009/14; C11B-001/06; C12N-001/12; C12N-001/20; C12P-007/64; C12R-001/01; C12R-001/89

PN CN113564109-A
TI Preparing menstrual blood mesenchymal stem cells,            by collecting menstrual blood samples of healthy            menstrual women, diluting blood, centrifuging,            collecting cell layer, inoculating cell pellet to cell            culture medium, centrifuging and incubating cells in            incubator.
AU LIU L
   DUAN S
   CUI L
   GAO X
   ZHAO H
AE ZHENGZHOU BEIYIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C6994C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing menstrual blood                mesenchymal stem cells, involves collecting                menstrual blood samples of healthy menstrual women,                adding the blood into menstrual blood collection                tubes containing antibiotic preservation solution,                adding EDTA or heparin to the collection tube for                anticoagulation, diluting the blood with phosphate                buffered saline (PBS), adding Ficoll-Plus                separation solution, spreading the diluted blood,                centrifuging, collecting cell layer above the                transparent separation layer, collecting the cell                layer into a clean centrifuge tube, adding PBS to                wash the cells, centrifuging, removing the                supernatant after the second centrifugation,                resuspending the cell pellet in culture medium,                inoculating cell pellet into cell culture medium,                sucking the cell suspension into a centrifuge tube,                centrifuging, adjusting the cell concentration with                complete culture solution for the precipitation,                inoculating into in a cell culture dish, and                incubating the cells in an incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing menstrual                blood mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform as cell source                is rich, and studied and cultured in large                quantities. The menstrual blood mesenchymal stem                cells are based on strong self-renewal ability,                easy to obtain, stable in vitro culture, and                autologous transplantation that reduces mental and                psychological pressure of patients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing menstrual blood                mesenchymal stem cells, involves collecting                menstrual blood samples from healthy menstrual                women, adding the blood into menstrual blood                collection tubes containing antibiotic preservation                solution, adding EDTA or heparin to the collection                tube for anticoagulation, diluting the blood with                phosphate buffered saline (PBS), adding Ficoll-Plus                separation solution to the centrifuge tube,                spreading the diluted blood on the surface of the                separation solution, centrifuging at 1000                revolutions per minute (rpm) with a horizontal                rotor at room temperature for 20 minutes, obtaining                cell layer between the plasma layer and the                separation layer, after centrifugation, removing                uppermost plasma layer, collecting cell layer above                the transparent separation layer, collecting the                cell layer into a clean centrifuge tube, adding 10                ml PBS to wash the cells, centrifuging at 300 rpm                for 10 minutes, removing the supernatant after the                second centrifugation, resuspending the cell pellet                in culture medium, inoculating cell pellet into                cell culture medium, placing in an incubator with                5% carbon dioxide, 95% humidity and 37degreesC,                removing the culture medium when the cells are                overgrown, washing with PBS, adding 0.25% trypsin,                placing in an incubator with 5% carbon dioxide, 95%                humidity and 37degreesC, increasing intercellular                space when the cytoplasm shrinks, immediately                adding culture solution to terminate the digestion,                repeatedly blowing the bottle wall to obtain cell                suspension, sucking the cell suspension into a                centrifuge tube, centrifuging, removing the                supernatant, adjusting the cell concentration with                complete culture solution for the precipitation,                inoculating into a cell culture dish, and                incubating the cells in an incubator with 5% carbon                dioxide, 95% humidity and 37degreesC.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Ficoll-Plus                  separation solution is a non-toxic, low endotoxin                  level density gradient separation solution, and                  the Ficoll-Plus separation solution density of                  separating solution is 1.077plus minus0.001 g/ml.                  When suitable amount of Ficoll-Plus separation                  solution is added, and the diluted blood is less                  than 3 ml, 3 ml Ficoll-Plus separation solution                  is added. When amount of Ficoll-Plus separation                  solution is greater than or equal to 3 ml, equal                  volume of Ficoll-Plus separation solution is                  added, and the total volume is two-thirds of the                  centrifuge tube. The Ficoll-Plus separation                  solution is maintained at room temperature of                  18-25degreesC. The menstrual blood samples                  collected from healthy menstrual women are fresh                  blood, and the blood samples are frozen or                  refrigerated in order to maintain cell viability.                  The blood is collected within 2 hours.Preferred Method: The method involves                  spreading the diluted blood on the liquid surface                  of the separation liquid, maintaining interface                  between the two liquid surfaces clear, sucking by                  a pipette, and carefully spreading blood on the                  separating solution. When the PBS dilutes blood                  or washes the cells, buffer solutions and culture                  solutions containing calcium and magnesium ions                  are not used. The centrifuge tube does not use                  plastic products to avoid cell wall hanging and                  affect the separation effect. The culture medium                  is Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM)-F12                  comprising 10% human serum or fetal bovine serum,                  and the culture medium is sterile. The DMEM-F12                  culture medium comprises 200 mM L-glutamine, 100                  U/ml penicillin and 100 microg/ml                  streptomycin.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN113373011-A
TI Preparing summer black dry red wine, comprises e.g. impregnating raw materials using carbon dioxide gas, adding sulfur dioxide, inserting activated high-activity yeast, fermenting, adding lactic acid bacteria, and sealing filtered raw wine.
AU CAI L
AE CAI L (CAIL-Individual)
GA 2021A8008S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing summer black dry red wine, comprises (i) impregnating the raw materials for 24 hours using carbon dioxide gas, (ii) manually threshing and crushing the raw materials, manually picking out the finely divided stalks, and adding sulfur dioxide for 24 hours disinfection treatment, (iii) inserting the activated high-activity yeast, where the insert amount is 35 g/HL grape mash, and pouring into the barrel, (iv) stirring the grape mash 6 times a day, controlling fermentation temperature, (v) adding lactic acid bacteria 1 g/HL to the original wine, fermenting with wine feet, (vi) sealing the filtered and sterilized raw wine, (vii) taking out the refrigerated raw wine and filtering twice with a polypropylene cotton filter element, and (viii) bottling and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing summer black dry red wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can also effectively extract the pigment of summer black grapes, making the wine appear ruby red; and through freezing and multiple filtrations, and reduces the content of sediment in the wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing summer black dry red wine, comprises (i) impregnating the raw materials for 24 hours using carbon dioxide gas, (ii) manually threshing and crushing the raw materials, manually picking out the finely divided stalks, and adding 30 mg/l sulfur dioxide for 24 hours disinfection treatment, (iii) inserting the activated high-activity yeast, where the insert amount is 35 g/HL grape mash, and pouring into the barrel, (iv) stirring the grape mash 6 times a day, measuring the temperature, controlling the fermentation temperature at 25-27 degrees C, and checking the density of the fermented grape juice every day, (v) when the density of fermented grape juice drops to 995 g/l, starting to detect the sugar concentration, when the total sugar concentration of the fermented grape juice is less than 4 g/l, terminating the fermentation, and separating the peel and dregs to obtain the original wine, (vi) adding lactic acid bacteria 1 g/HL to the original wine, fermenting with wine feet, and fermenting at 18-21 degrees C for 4 weeks, (vii) after the secondary fermentation is over, pouring the barrel, separating the wine feet, filtering once with a 5 mu m polypropylene cotton filter element, obtaining the filtered raw wine, and using sulfur dioxide for disinfection treatment, and the addition amount is 50 mg/l, (viii) sealing the filtered and sterilized raw wine and placing in a refrigerator at -5 degrees C for 15 days, (ix) taking out the refrigerated raw wine and filtering twice with a 1 mu m polypropylene cotton filter element, using a diatomaceous earth filter to filter, using sulfur dioxide for disinfection treatment, and the addition amount is 50 mg/l, and using a plate and frame filter for filtration, and (x) bottling and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The filter membrane used in the plate and frame filter is a 0.5 mu m filter membrane.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.)

PN CN113200535-A
TI Directional anchoring of palladium single atoms to rGO/graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface in organic catalysis, photocatalysis, lithium ion battery field, involves using environmentally friendly and pollution-free biomass.
AU WANG S
   XU R
   WANG L
   CHI K
   CHEN X
   DONG J
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2021925904
AB    NOVELTY - Directional anchoring of palladium single atoms to rGO/graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface involves using environmentally friendly and pollution-free biomass as raw materials, where the palladium single atom is directionally anchored to the rGO/g-graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface. The silkworm cocoons dried in advance and the iron-based or potassium-based catalyst aqueous solution are heated and stirred to fully react. The product is suction filtered and freeze-dried. The product is calcined at a high temperature of 600-900 degrees C for 1-10 hours under one or more mixed gasses of inert gas, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and ammonia, and then the temperature is increased to 900-2000 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, fully stirred the calcined product in an acid solution to remove iron-based or potassium-based compounds, subjected to suction filtration, acid solution washing, water washing, and freeze-dried to obtain biomass-derived graphene.
   USE - Method for directional anchoring of palladium single atoms to rGO/graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface used in organic catalysis, photocatalysis, electrochemical catalysis, supercapacitors, biosensors, lithium ion battery field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has wide application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Directional anchoring of palladium single atoms to rGO/graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface involves using environmentally friendly and pollution-free biomass as raw materials, where the palladium single atom is directionally anchored to the rGO/g-graphitic carbon nitride two-dimensional interface. The silkworm cocoons dried in advance and the iron-based or potassium-based catalyst aqueous solution are heated and stirred to fully react. The product is suction filtered and freeze-dried. The product is calcined at a high temperature of 600-900 degrees C for 1-10 hours under one or more mixed gasses of inert gas, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and ammonia, and then the temperature is increased to 900-2000 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, fully stirred the calcined product in an acid solution to remove iron-based or potassium-based compounds, subjected to suction filtration, acid solution washing, water washing, and freeze-dried to obtain biomass-derived graphene. The biomass graphene is heated and stirred in the dicyandiamine aqueous solution to fully react, transferred the solution to the reactor and reacted hydrothermally. The product is suction filtered, washed with water, and freeze-dried to obtain a biomass graphene/dicyandiamine composite material, placed the biomass graphene/dicyandiamine composite material in step 7 in a tube furnace, and perform high-temperature calcination under one or more mixed gasses of inert gas, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and ammonium to obtain a composite material, placed the rGO/g-graphitic carbon nitride in the palladium organic salt solution to fully react until the sample is completely evaporated to dryness. The product is suction filtered, washed with water and dried to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron-based or potassium-based catalyst is potassium ferrocyanide, iron chloride, potassium ferric hexacyanoferrate, potassium hydroxide, potassium hydrogen carbonate. The palladium organic salt is trimethyl palladium acetate (II), bis(dibenzylideneacetone) palladium, trifluoroacetate palladium (II), palladium acetate, bis(acetylacetone)palladium (II). Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the catalyst aqueous solution is 0.1-20 mole/L, heated the reaction, the temperature is 0-100 degrees C. The time is 1-100 hours. The gas flow rate is controlled to be 100-500 sccm under the premise of ensuring that the air is cleaned when calcining at high temperature, the heating rate is 1-20 degrees C/minutes. The acid is mixture of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, and nitric acid and the concentration of the acid solution is 0.1-10 mole/L, and the acid stirring time in the liquid is 1-200 hours. The mass percentage of dicyandiamine in the dicyandiamine aqueous solution is 0.1-50%, the heating reaction, the reaction temperature is 0-100 degrees C, the reaction time is 1-100 hours. The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 50-250 degrees C, and the time is 1-100 hours. The gas flow rate is controlled to be 100-500 sccm, the heating rate is 1-20 degrees C/minutes. The calcination temperature is 200-1000 degrees C and the holding time is 0.5-24 hours. The content of palladium organic salt is 0.1-20 mg/ml, the reaction temperature is 0-100 degrees C and the reaction time is 0.5-300 hours. The palladium single atom is oriented and anchored to rGO/graphitic carbon nitride, where the palladium loading is palladium rGO /g-graphitic carbon nitride composite material 20 wt.%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E05-U05C; E11-K03; E11-Q01C; E11-W; E31-N05D; J04-E04C; L03-B03A; L03-E03; L03-G09E; L03-J; N01-A01; N02-A; N02-F04; N04-A; N05-E01; N07-K; X16-B01F1
IP B01J-027/24; C01B-021/082; C01B-032/184; C01B-032/194; C07C-213/02; C07C-215/76

DN 71-0-0-0-K M P; 2809115-0-0-0-K M P; 104530-0-0-0-C K M; 104516-0-2-0-C K M; 874-0-0-0-C K M; 95071-0-0-0-C K M; 104525-0-0-0-C K M; 129414-0-0-0-C K M; 2646109-0-0-0-K S; 159-0-0-0-K S; 2010597-0-0-0-K S; 1564-0-0-0-K S; 721-0-0-0-K S; 3154-0-0-0-K S; 7-0-0-0-K U V; 80-0-0-0-K U V; 9-0-0-0-K U V

PN CN112169762-A; CN112169762-B
TI Pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction adsorbent comprises modified columnar activated carbon which contains silicon nitride.
AU XU Z
   WANG M
   LIU K
AE SHANDONG JINZHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021063967
AB    NOVELTY - Pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction adsorbent comprises modified columnar activated carbon with specific surface area of 1200-1500 m2/g, diameter of 0.8-2.0 nm and micropore volume of 0.2-0.3 ml/g, which contains 3-6 wt.% silicon nitride.
   USE - The adsorbent is used for pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high separation ability and compressive strength, wearable strength, columnar active carbon with apparent density, moderate production cost. It has higher unit filling volume separation ability, not easy to break property, high wear resistance, falling dust, long service life and low pollution of subsequent process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the pressure swing adsorption hydrogen extraction adsorbent comprising in kneader, mixing 65-80 pts. wt. anthracite powder and 30-35 pts. wt. coal tar powder, adding 20-30 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide solution with concentration of 40-50%, kneading uniformly, extruding strip, and controlling extruding temperature at 120-130 degrees C; drying at 150-250 pts. wt. for 1-3 hours, isolating carbon dioxide, heating to 600-700 degrees C, isolating oxygen, roasting, carbonizing for 1-3 hours, cooling, washing carbonized material with water, soaking in 5-10% dilute hydrochloric acid for 2-10 hours, washing with water, and drying at 100-130 degrees C until ash content of dry bar is 0.5% to obtain carbonized bar; soaking carbonized strip in water glass solution by equal volume immersion method, drying wet strip until moisture contained is removed by 20-40%, treating in carbon dioxide atmosphere to semi-wet strip section with pH 10, washing with water to remove alkali, drying at 100-130 degrees C, roasting at 400-500 degrees C for 1-3 hours, isolating oxygen, cooling down to obtain silica loaded carbon strips, and controlling silica content at 3.5-7%; and soaking carbon strips loaded with silica in equal volume of prepared resole phenolic resin aqueous solution, placing in closed place and homogenizing for 5-20 hours, drying, dehydrating, curing at 130-150 degrees C, treating with carbon monoxide and ammonia with carbon dioxide gas, heating to 900-950 degrees C, carrying out activation roasting treatment for 3-5 hours and cooling. The quality indicators of anthracite coal powder in first step include combustible base volatile content of 3.5-10%, ash content of 4-10%, and particle size of 200 mesh. The quality indicators of the coal pitch powder include softening point of 80-100 degrees C, coking value of greater than or equal to 45%, and particle size of 80 mesh. The aqueous resol resin solution is prepared by in stirred reactor, mixing formaldehyde and phenol at ratio of 1.5-2.0:1, heating to 60-65 degrees C, stirring for 5-20 minutes, adding dropwise 0.5-1 mol/L ferrous acetate solution, heating and reacting at 80-85 degrees C, maintaining temperature for 1-3 hours, vacuum distilling under vacuum at 80-85 degrees C until moisture is 30-40% and viscosity average molecular weight is 300-600..
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN WO2020165585-A1; GB2581771-A; GB2585998-A; GB2586421-A; GB2581771-B; GB2586421-B; GB2585998-B; EP3924947-A1; US2022120724-A1
TI Detector for determining occupancy of, e.g. refuse/recycling container, has housing sealed to restrict ingress of debris into housing and configured such that air can flow into housing and over first sensor of sensor arrangement.
AU SHARMAN P
   BLAND G
   HALE T
AE TOTAL WASTE SOLUTIONS LTD (TOTA-Non-standard)
   TOTAL WASTE SOLUTIONS LTD (TOTA-Non-standard)
   TOTAL WASTE SOLUTIONS LTD (TOTA-Non-standard)
GA 202079951T
AB    NOVELTY - The detector (110) has a housing (112) defining an internal volume. A mounting arrangement is provided for mounting the housing to a container (102). A sensor arrangement is provided with a first sensor arranged within the housing. The first sensor is configured to determine concentration of carbon dioxide within the internal space of the container. A control system is configured to receive input values from the first sensor, and to provide an output when the concentration of carbon dioxide exceeds a predetermined value. The housing is sealed for restricting the ingress of debris into the housing. The housing is configured such that air is able to flow into the housing and over the first sensor.
   USE - Detector used for determining occupancy of a container (claimed), as well as for monitoring level of carbon dioxide in container. Can be used for, e.g. refuse container, recycling container, shipping container, or trailer of road vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - Allows for existing containers to be retro-fitted with the detector. Includes a housing that is sealed and has an inlet configured to prevent dust ingress into the housing while enabling air to flow in the housing, thus enabling the detector to function, while preventing dirt, waste, and water from entering the housing to interfere with the detector. Minimizes ingress of water or debris into the housing that could damage the internal components of the detector. Ensures that the detector can be positioned on the external surface of the container, thus ensuring that the detector is highly visible to an operator approaching the container. Enables a more compact detector to be produced by extending through a wall of the container, as opposed to extending over an upper edge of a container wall. Ensures that the detector can be positioned away from the upper edge of the container wall to help avoid damage to the detector from impacts, e.g. from a container lid. Uses a mounting plate to provide a more secure mounting of the detector to the container. Reduces impact damage imparted on the components of the detector, thus increasing the service life of the detector. Performs comparative temperature monitoring to enable the control system of the detector to be used in different climates, or over different seasons, without requiring any reconfiguration. Ensures that the detector is a self-contained unit that is able to be mounted within a container without the need for connection to an external power source. Ensures that the detector may be arranged to allow for the occupancy of a container, or a series of containers, to be monitored remotely.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial schematic side view of the detector mounted to a container.
   Container (102)
   Detector (110)
   Housing (112)
   Fasteners (121)
   Sensor housing portion (174)

PT P
PN CN111548891-A
TI Process for impurity-removing and            fragrance-increasing of sweet potato wine by steaming            potato, adding water and amylase, adding partridge tea            bag and Garcinia cambogia fruit extract, extracting,            aging and adding natural clarifying agent.
AU BAI X
   SUN G
   QIAN X
   SI S
   CHEN X
AE HAINAN ARC NATURE IND CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202082382J
AB    NOVELTY - Process for impurity-removing and                fragrance-increasing of sweet potato wine, involves                (i) pre-treating sweet potatoes, cutting into small                blocks and steaming for 20-40 minutes using a 60-80                degrees C water bath, (ii) mashing the steamed                sweet potatoes, adding to a steaming pot, adding                water and amylase, maintaining temperature for 1-3                hours, cooling to 24-27 degrees C and transferring                to a rice koji fermentation tank for fermentation                for 20-30 days, (iii) adding a partridge tea bag to                the fermentation tank for impregnation, adding                Garcinia cambogia fruit extract to the fermentation                tank, adjusting the fermentation tank to 30-48                degrees C, maintaining the temperature and                naturally cooling, (iv) performing supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid extraction on the obtained                fermented wine liquid, obtaining the extracted wine                liquid and adopting UV sterilization, (v) aging the                obtained sterilized wine liquid and (vi) cooling,                adding 10-15 mg/l natural clarifying agent,                filtering and bottling while cold.
   USE - The process is useful for impurity-removing                and fragrance-increasing of sweet potato                wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The process adopts Hainan Qiaotou sweet                potato, which has extremely high selenium content,                sugar content and nutrition and ferments its own                sugars, controls fermentation temperature and time                and a series of process operations and provides the                sweet potato wine from the common sweet potato with                better impurity removal effect, high clarity, clear                color, no precipitation, multi-layer fragrance                characteristics and excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for impurity-removing and                fragrance-increasing of sweet potato wine, involves                (i) pre-treating sweet potatoes, cutting into small                blocks and steaming for 20-40 minutes using a 60-80                degrees C water bath, (ii) mashing the steamed                sweet potatoes, adding to a steaming pot, adding                water and amylase, maintaining temperature for 1-3                hours, cooling to 24-27 degrees C and transferring                to a rice koji fermentation tank for fermentation                for 20-30 days, (iii) adding a partridge tea bag to                the fermentation tank for impregnation for 5-8                days, taking out after the impregnation is                completed, adding Garcinia cambogia fruit extract                to the fermentation tank, adjusting the                fermentation tank to 30-48 degrees C, maintaining                the temperature for 40-60 minutes and naturally                cooling, (iv) performing supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid extraction on the obtained fermented                wine liquid, where the extraction pressure is 25-35                MPa, the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C,                the carbon dioxide flow rate is 28-35 l/hour and                the extraction time is 40-60 minutes, obtaining the                extracted wine liquid and adopting UV                sterilization, (v) aging the obtained sterilized                wine liquid at 25-32 degrees C for 3-6 months and                (vi) cooling to -5 degrees C to 0 degrees C, adding                10-15 mg/l natural clarifying agent, filtering and                bottling while cold to obtain the sweet potato                wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves                  adopting the wine liquid and the partridge tea in                  a ratio of 1:(0.01-0.05) V/W, where the ratio of                  the wine-liquid G.cambogia fruit extract is                  1-3:(0.3-0.5) V/W. The G.cambogia fruit extract                  is prepared by extracting peel of G.cambogia                  fruit, crushing the G.cambogia fruit to 70-90                  mesh, using distilled rice wine with an alcohol                  content of 50-58 vol.% as the absorption liquid,                  performing condensation reflux extraction, where                  the ratio of material to liquid (g:ml) is                  1:(20-40), where the extraction temperature is                  25-70 degrees C and the time is 0.5-2 hours,                  recovering the absorption liquid which is the                  G.cambogia fruit extract. The method involves                  adopting the UV sterilization using UV rays with                  a wavelength of 170-190 nm and irradiating for                  sterilization for 10-20 minutes. Preferred                  Composition: The sweet potato is Hainan Qiaotou                  sweet potato having a selenium-rich content of                  more than 0.07 mg/kg and a sugar content of less                  than 27.7 g/kg. The natural clarifying agent                  comprises egg white liquid, pectin and fish glue                  with a mass ratio of                  (0.01-0.9):(0.01-2.5):(0.3-4).
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02C; D05-E
IP C12G-003/026; C12G-003/055; C12G-003/06

PT P
PN US2020215137-A1; US11266702-B2
TI Obtaining extract of Cannabis, used to treat e.g.            epilepsy, involves contacting Cannabis with carbon            dioxide as sole supercritical fluid solvent, repeating            contacting step, removing supercritical fluid solvent,            and isolating cannabinoids.
AU SPEIER G J
AE CURE PHARM HOLDING CORP (CURE-Non-standard)
   CURE PHARM HOLDING CORP (CURE-Non-standard)
GA 202062053X
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining at least one extract of Cannabis,                comprises: (a) contacting Cannabis with carbon                dioxide as the sole supercritical fluid solvent, at                a pressure of at least 74 bar and at least 31                degrees C, for up to 8 hours to provide an extract                of Cannabis; (b) repeating step (a) at least one                time to obtain multiple extracts of Cannabis; (c)                removing the supercritical fluid solvent from the                multiple extracts of Cannabis; and (d) isolating                cannabinoids from one of the extracts of Cannabis                and isolating terpenes from another extract                ofCannabis, to yield an extract of Cannabis                enriched with cannabinoids and an extract of                Cannabis enriched with terpenes.
   USE - The process is useful for obtaining at least                one extract of Cannabis enriched with cannabinoids                or terpenes (all claimed), where the extract is                useful for treating a mammal afflicted with a                disease or disorder including neuroinflammation,                epilepsy, Alzheimer's disease, oxidative injury,                vomiting and nausea, anxiety, anorexia, arthritis,                schizophrenia, multiple sclerosis, joint                inflammation, joint pain, symptoms associated with                AIDS, chronic pain, acute pain, neuropathic pain                and spasticity, inflammatory bowel diseases (e.g.                Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis), tumor                neovascularization, tumor growth, cancer, glaucoma,                muscle spasms, severe pain, severe nausea, and                cachexia or dramatic weight loss or muscle atrophy                (wasting syndrome). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides the extract of Cannabis,                which: is enriched with cannabinoids that contains                at least 10%, preferably at least 90% higher                concentration of cannabinoids, relative to the                Cannabis; is enriched with terpenes contains at                least 10%, preferably at least 90% higher                concentration of terpenes, relative to the                Cannabis; exhibits better bioavailability and                results in fewer side effects; improves the onset                of action, lowers the dosing, and enhances safety                profile; is more stable, durable and quicker                dissolving than many other conventional dosage                forms; and is synergistic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Obtaining at least one extract of Cannabis,                comprises: (a) contacting Cannabis with carbon                dioxide as the sole supercritical fluid solvent, at                a pressure of at least 74 bar and at least 31                degrees C, for up to 8 hours to provide an extract                of Cannabis; (b) repeating step (a) at least one                time to obtain multiple extracts of Cannabis; (c)                removing the supercritical fluid solvent from the                multiple extracts of Cannabis; and (d) isolating                cannabinoids from one of the extracts of Cannabis                and isolating terpenes from another extract                ofCannabis, to yield an extract of Cannabis                enriched with cannabinoids and an extract of                Cannabis enriched with terpenes, where the                isolating the cannabinoids from one of the extracts                of Cannabis and the isolating the terpenes from                another extract of the Cannabis is carried out by                chromatography, adsorption, crystallization,                distillation, liquid-liquid extraction, filtration,                fractional distillation, precipitation,                recrystallization, lyophilization (freeze drying)                and/or sublimation. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for a product obtained from the process,                which is an extract of Cannabis enriched with                cannabinoids or terpenes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The step (a) is carried                  out at: a different temperature and/or a                  different pressure; a different temperature and                  the same pressure; or a different pressure and                  the same temperature.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Neuroprotective;                Anticonvulsant; Nootropic; Vulnerary; Antiemetic;                Tranquilizer; Anabolic; Eating-Disorders-Gen;                Antiarthritic; Neuroleptic; Anti-HIV; Analgesic;                Muscle relaxant; Gastrointestinal-Gen; Antiulcer;                Cytostatic; Ophthalmological; Muscular-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the dosage form is by oral                (e.g. buccal, sublabial, or sublingual),                parenteral, enteral, intradermal, topical,                intravenous, intramuscular, topical, transdermal,                ophthalmic, nasal, dermal, or subcutaneous route,                or via inhalation, infusion or implantation. No                dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.
