PT P
PN CN111215084-A
TI Preparation of copper-based catalyst for            hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, involves            adding zinc and aluminum nitrate solutions, regulator            and precipitant to reactor, aging, grinding, further            adding nitrate, aging and drying.
AU MA J
   HUANG Y
   LIANG B
   SHEN Z
   FAN S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202050524A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper-based catalyst involves                adding deionized water to a reaction container as a                precipitation tank, mixing a zinc nitrate solution                and an aluminum nitrate solution, adding the mixed                solution at a constant speed together with a                regulator and a precipitant, or adding the mixed                liquid of the regulator and the precipitant into                the sedimentation tank at uniform speed,                maintaining at 40-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to                7-10, continuously stirring, aging for 12-24 hours                at 40-90 degrees C, filtering, washing such that                sodium and potassium ions are not contained in the                filtrate, drying at 90-260 degrees C for 4-10                hours, grinding to size of less than 400 mesh,                ultrasonically dispersing in water, adding copper                nitrate solution and the precipitant containing                sodium carbonate solution and/or sodium hydroxide                solution to the resulting solution, reacting at                40-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to 7-10, aging for                4-12 hours at 40-90 degrees C, washing, drying,                calcining, grinding and tabletting.
   USE - Preparation of copper-based catalyst for                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst having high selectivity and                stability can be produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of copper-based catalyst involves                adding deionized water to a reaction container as a                precipitation tank, mixing a zinc nitrate solution                and an aluminum nitrate solution, adding the mixed                solution at a constant speed together with a                regulator and a precipitant, or adding the mixed                liquid of the regulator and the precipitant into                the sedimentation tank at uniform speed,                maintaining at 40-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to                7-10, continuously stirring, aging for 12-24 hours                at 40-90 degrees C, filtering, washing such that                sodium and potassium ions are not contained in the                filtrate, drying at 90-260 degrees C for 4-10                hours, grinding to size of less than 400 mesh,                ultrasonically dispersing in water, adding copper                nitrate solution and the precipitant containing                sodium carbonate solution and/or sodium hydroxide                solution to the resulting solution, reacting at                40-90 degrees C, adjusting pH to 7-10, aging for                4-12 hours at 40-90 degrees C, washing, drying at                40-90 degrees C for 4-10 hours, calcining at                300-500 degrees C for 4-12 hours, grinding and                tabletting. The regulator is sodium carbonate                and/or potassium carbonate. The precipitant is                sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The                concentration of the carbonate of the regulator is                0.5-3 times the charge concentration of the metal                cation, and the concentration of the hydroxide of                the precipitation agent is 1-1.5 times the charge                concentration of the metal cation. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalyst containing 30-60 mol% copper,                10-40 mol% zinc, and 10-30 mol% aluminum; and
   (2) use of the catalyst in the hydrogenation                of carbon dioxide to methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal ion                  concentration in the copper nitrate solution,                  zinc nitrate solution, and aluminum nitrate                  solution is 0.1-10 mol/L. The metal ion                  concentration in the mixed solution of zinc                  nitrate and aluminum nitrate is 0.1-10 mol/L. The                  concentration of metal ions in the regulator and                  precipitant is 0.1-10 mol/L. The concentration of                  metal ions in the mixed solution of regulator and                  precipitant is 0.1-10 mol/L. The precipitation                  agent is preferably a sodium hydroxide solution                  with concentration of 0.1-10 mol/L. The regulator                  is preferably a sodium carbonate solution with                  concentration of 0.1-10 mol/L. The active                  component zinc is stabilized in the alumina                  support. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to                  hydrogen is 2-5.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-E04E1; E11-D02; J04-E04B; J04-E06; N02-D01; N03-F01; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-B02

PT P
PN US2020009533-A1; US11344863-B2
TI Environmental control system has controller which is operative to determine air quality value based on signals from one or more air quality sensors and control two bypass valves in response to air quality value.
AU SPACE D R
   TRENT S M
AE BOEING CO (BOEI-C)
GA 202003700S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sorbent regeneration device comprising one regenerative sorbent material operative to remove gas substances from air. The one regenerative sorbent material is operatively coupled to receive hot air. Two bypass valves (42) are operative to selectively direct some or all of the hot air to the one regenerative sorbent material. A controller (30) is operatively connected to the sorbent regeneration device. The controller is operative to determine an air quality value based on signals from the one or more air quality sensors and control the two bypass valves in response to the air quality value.
   USE - Environmental control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The controller is operative to control an amount of air routed through the particulate filter in response to air quality determined from the air quality sensors to remove or reduce the amount of particulates in the circulating air. The particulate filter is operative to remove particulates from the circulating air. The system reduces the amount of particulates in the circulating air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The environmental control system has a controller which is further operative to selectively direct the hot air independently to one of the at least two regenerative sorbent materials. The controller is operative to control an amount of the hot air routed through the one regenerative sorbent material in response to the air quality value. The controller is further operative to apply vacuum pressure to the one regenerative sorbent material. The one or more air quality sensors (18) are arranged on opposite sides of the one regenerative sorbent material. The sorbent regeneration device is operatively coupled to one or more particulate filters. The system has volatile organic compound (VOC)/ozone converter and a carbon dioxide removal device. One of the two bypass valves is a three-way bypass valve. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of regenerating a sorbent material of an environmental control system, which involves monitoring air quality by receiving one or more signals from one or more air quality sensors. One of the two bypass valves is activated so as to selectively introduce hot air to a regenerative sorbent material. A portion of the regenerative sorbent material is heated for a time and a temperature capable of removing one substance. The portion of the regenerative sorbent material is regenerated. The vacuum pressure is applied to only one of the regenerative sorbent material. One of the two bypass valves are activated so as to selectively introduce hot air to a carbon dioxide removal device comprising carbon dioxide sorbent material. An anomaly in one of the two bypass valves is detected. A signal indicative of the anomaly is outputted to an alert device coupled to a human interface. The one bypass valve having the anomaly is isolated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an environmental control system.
   Aerospace vehicle (12)
   Interior volume (14)
   Quality sensor (18)
   Controller (30)
   Bypass valve (42)

PT P
PN CN110464875-A
TI Artificial intervertebral disc stent used for three-dimensionally printing electrostatic curve wire, contains chondrocyte, fetal bovine serum, bone marrow stem cell, connective tissue growth factor, biomaterial and biopolymer material.
AU QIAO Z
   HAN Y
   TANG J
   LIAN M
   SUN B
   DAI K
   ZHANG X
   SUN X
   ZHAO J
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAO TONG NINTH PEOPLES HO (USJT-C)
GA 2019990859
AB    NOVELTY - An artificial intervertebral disc stent and its preparation method are claimed. The stent comprises 10-16 pts. wt. chondrocyte, 3-6 pts. wt. fetal bovine serum, 8-10 pts. wt. bone marrow stem cell, 7-9 pts. wt. connective tissue growth factor, 5-6 pts. wt. ascorbic acid, 7-9 pts. wt. biological material, 7-9 pts. wt. organic polymer material and 7-9 pts. wt. biopolymer material.
   USE - The artificial intervertebral disc stent is useful for three-dimensionally printing electrostatic curve wire.
   ADVANTAGE - The stent has controllable porosity, large surface area, and excellent biological compatibility and mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the artificial intervertebral disc stent, which involves: culturing seed cells by preserving the chondrocytes and then culturing in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C by using a F12 culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum and preserving the bone marrow stem cell in advance and culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C by using an alpha -MEM medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, 25 ng/ml connective tissue growth factor and 25 mu M ascorbic acid; preparing printing inks by melting biomaterials and organic polymer materials or dissolving in organic solvents, or dissolving biomaterials and organic polymer materials in a suitable solvent and adjusting to the required density or mixing by heating and melting; preparing an artificial intervertebral disc by using a three-dimensional printing software to build a printing model, where an outer layer is a multilayer ring, and an inner layer is a multilayer spiral; measuring the appropriate amount of biopolymer material to sterilize, measuring the appropriate amount of natural material to sterilize, adding cells to obtain cell-containing gel; adjusting the process parameters of the biological three-dimensional printing equipment, where the diameter of the print head is 100-400 mu m, the printing temperature is adjusted according to the thermal properties of the material, the cylinder pressure is 0.05-1 MPa during printing, and the printing structure is controlled by the printing path; putting the polymer material and cell-containing gel into two barrels respectively, starting the equipment, and alternately printing the polymer material and natural material; then printing the vertebral plate area, and replacing the vertebral plate area with polycaprolactone and a cell-containing gel printing nucleus area after the printing is completed; after the preparation of the nucleus area is finished, changing the material in the material cylinder into polylactic acid-glycolic acid copolymer and sodium alginate, and printing the fiber ring; finally again printing the vertebral area by using the polylactic acid and gelatin to obtain a layered cell intervertebral disc scent; and then measuring the appropriate amount of medium, immersing the cell intervertebral disc scent, and continuously culturing in the carbon dioxide incubator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The preparation method further involves, when adjusting the parameters during printing, adjusting the path of the printing thread by controlling the voltage, printing height and other related parameters to control the degree of crosslinking of the stent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The biological material is an acellular matrix and/or a hydrogel. The organic polymer material is polycaprolactone, poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) and/or poly-L-lactic acid. The biopolymer material is polylactic acid, polycaprolactone, polylactic acid-glycolic acid copolymer, polylactic acid-caprolactone copolymer and/or poly-p-dioxane. The natural material is gelatin, hyaluronic acid, sodium alginate, chitosan, and collagen.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); P32 (Dentistry, bandages, veterinary, prosthesis (A61C, D, F).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography); T01 (Digital Computers); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC A08-S02; A09-D03; A11-B16; A11-C02D; A12-V02; A12-V03D; A12-W14; D09-C01D; D09-C01F; B04-B04D4; B04-C02E4; B04-C03; B04-H06; B04-N02; B11-C04A1; B14-N01; P32-A40A; P32-M; S06-G10; T01-J06A; X25-A08A; X25-A08C2; X25-A08U2

PT P
PN CN110252292-A; CN110252292-B
TI Preparing nano gold array having catalytic properties by rigid cross-linking useful e.g. as catalyst for carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction at normal pressure, comprises e.g. preparing gold nano solution and carrying out spin-coating.
AU LI Y
   XU Q
   CHANG H
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 2019916237
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano gold array having catalytic properties by rigid cross-linking, comprises e.g. mixing tetrachloroauric acid solution and sodium borohydride solution, adding acetone, normal ethane and dodecyl alcohol and statically placing to obtain gold nano solution; adding self-assembled solvent of toluene into nano-gold array, using a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethene) tape to allow it to evaporate in the atmosphere, stacking nano-gold arrays under ordered rules and heating the nano gold in a vacuum oven and cooling; soaking thermally annealed gold nano-array into different cross-linking agent solutions, carrying out spin-coating of poly-lactic acid acetone solution with nano-gold array, carrying out repetitive spin coating of composite film obtained for three times for 60 seconds, heating the composite film in vacuum, soaking in a mixed solution of ethanol and ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 10:1 to obtain a nano gold array, and processing in a plasma cleaner.
   USE - The nano gold array is useful as a catalyst for carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction at normal pressure and for designing heterogeneous catalysts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve the catalytic activity by the weaker electron donating effect and steric hindrance of the rigid group, where the rigid cross-linked nano gold array having a two-dimensional structure will provide a plurality of exposed gold catalytic sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano gold array having catalytic properties by rigid cross-linking, comprises (a) mixing 50 mu l of 50 mmol/l tetrachloroauric acid solution and 200 mu l of 50 mmol/l sodium borohydride solution, adding 1-100 g acetone, 1-100 g normal ethane and 0.01-1 mu l dodecyl alcohol and statically placing to obtain gold nano solution; (b) adding self-assembled solvent of 1-100 mu l toluene into nano-gold array, using a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethene) tape to allow it to evaporate in the atmosphere, stacking nano-gold arrays under ordered rules and heating the nano gold in a vacuum oven for 3 hours and cooling at room temperature; (c) soaking thermally annealed gold nano-array into different cross-linking agent solutions for 24 hours, carrying out spin-coating of poly-lactic acid acetone solution with nano-gold array at 3000 revolutions/minute, carrying out repetitive spin coating of composite film obtained for three times for 60 seconds, heating the composite film in vacuum at 60 degrees C for 10 minutes, soaking in a mixed solution of ethanol and ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 10:1 to obtain a nano gold array, and processing in a plasma cleaner; and (d) adding a reaction promoter, phenyl epoxy compound, absolute ethanol and carbon dioxide into a reaction flask, and continuously stirring for 30 hours, taking out nano gold array, washing with ethanol and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the heating condition in the thermal annealing is pressure of 70 Pa and heating at 80 degrees C for 3 hours. In the step (c), the cross-linking process is carried out by soaking thermally annealed nanogold array in the crosslinking agent solution for 24 hours. In the step (c), the condition under nano gold array is processed in the plasma cleaner is low level treatment carried out for 30 seconds at 6.8 W. In the step (d), the reaction promoter, namely 100 mg 1-butyl-4-aza-1-azaniabicyclo(2.2.2)octane bromide, 1-50 mg phenyl epoxy compound, 1-10 ml absolute ethanol and carbon dioxide are continuously added at a low speed of 1 l/minute. In the step (d), the purification treatment of the product is carried out by column chromatography. Preferred Components: In the step (c), the cross-linking agent used in thieno(3,2-b)thiophene, 2,2'-thiophene, 1,4-phenyldithiol or 4'4-dithiodiphenyl sulfide, and the solvent used for cross-linking agent is ethanol.

AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 201981751D
AB    NOVELTY - A spark carbon dioxide hydrate structure characteristic determining method involves synthesizing experimentally a sample of carbon dioxide hydrate and obtaining a graph of influence of temperature and pressure on structural characteristics of carbon dioxide hydrate. TI Membrane separation for capturing carbon dioxide by performing separation of passing gas mixture through membrane unit and performing separation of passing carbon-dioxide-enriched stream, withdrawn from permeate side of above membrane unit.
AU LEE J H
   YEO J
   MOON J
   OH W J
   LEE D
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202124885W
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane separation method for capturing carbon dioxide, in which a gas mixture is passed through a membrane unit to selectively separate the carbon dioxide, the membrane unit having a feed side, into which a carbon-dioxide-containing gas is provided, a retentate side, from which a carbon-dioxide-depleted stream is discharged, and a permeate side, from which a carbon-dioxide-enriched stream is produced, where the method involves performing a first separation step of passing the gas mixture through a first membrane unit; and performing a second separation step of passing the carbon-dioxide-enriched stream, withdrawn from the permeate side of the first membrane unit, through a second membrane unit, where the second separation step is performed at a temperature that is lower than a temperature at which the first separation step is performed.
   USE - As membrane separation method for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can capture carbon dioxide at a high concentration with improved efficiency and high recovery rate, in which the gas mixture is passed through the membrane unit to thus separate carbon dioxide, can limit the purity of carbon dioxide after passing through the membrane to 68% even though a low-temperature process is used to increase the purity of the separated carbon dioxide, reduces cooling energy and captures high-purity carbon dioxide at a high recovery rate when a low-temperature membrane process is applied in order to capture high-purity carbon dioxide and can recover carbon dioxide having relatively further increased purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, a selectivity of the carbon dioxide in the second membrane unit is higher than a selectivity of the carbon dioxide in the first membrane unit, where the temperature at which the first separation step is performed is determined by adjusting at least one of a temperature of the first membrane unit and a temperature of the gas mixture provided into the first membrane unit, and the temperature at which the second separation step is performed is determined by adjusting at least one of a temperature of the second membrane unit and a temperature of the gas which permeates the first membrane unit and is then provided into the second membrane unit. The method involves performing the second separation step at -40degrees Celsius to 0degrees Celsius or \--40degrees Celsius and less than -10degrees Celsius. The method involves performing the first separation step at -20degrees Celsius to 30degrees Celsius, preferably 0-30degrees Celsius, where the activation energy of carbon dioxide permeation is smaller than activation energy of nitrogen permeation in the second membrane unit and in the membrane of the second membrane unit, a difference between the permeance of carbon dioxide and the permeance of nitrogen is increased as a temperature is decreased.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202108652H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing gas flow to the constant volume sampling system. The predetermined amount of carbon dioxide is provided to the gas flow in a predetermined time. The carbon dioxide is provided from a carbon dioxide container (4). The carbon dioxide container is weighed before starting the provision of the carbon dioxide and after ending the carbon dioxide provision to obtain the weight of carbon dioxide is provided to the constant volume sampling system. The carbon dioxide container is heated to a predetermined temperature before the predetermined mass flow of carbon dioxide is started.
   USE - Method for calibrating constant volume sampling system for analyzing exhaust emissions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the system is not clogged or blocked ensuring that the total amount of carbon dioxide necessary for the calibration is completely delivered to the gas flow. The method allows to easily and efficiently control the temperature of the carbon dioxide container. The method allows to introduce a part of the energy that is released during expansion of the carbon dioxide during the expansion of the carbon dioxide so as to reduce the risk of blocking and clogging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:

   ZHANG P

GA 2022D8934P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating the                preparation. The model is constructed. The                calculation analysis is performed. The result is                evaluated. The mass and centroid of a three-way                catalytic converter are collected. The data of the                three-way catalytic converter support is refined. A                center of mass point equivalent to the three-way                catalytic converter is connected to the three-way                catalytic converter support. The constraints are                provided with fixed constraints, rigid constraints,                and elastic constraints.
   USE - Modal analysis method for three-way catalytic                converter support system of passenger vehicle                exhaust system used for converting carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide harmful gas                discharged by automobile tail gas into harmless                carbon dioxide through oxidation and reduction,                water and nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method makes the three-way catalytic                converter equivalent to a centroid point, and uses                the constraint connection to connect the center of                mass and the support, rationally simplifying the                non-key factors, emphatically projecting the                research object, effectively simplifying model                building work, improving the model building and                computer-aided engineering (CAE) calculation                efficiency, greatly reducing the analysis workload.                The method saves about 95% of calculation time,                greatly improves the working efficiency, and has                important guiding significance for the initial                stage selection design of the 3-way catalyst                support.

PN CN114790139-A
TI Method for synthesizing 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic            acid involves performing diazotization of            2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene, reacting obtained            2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene with cuprous cyanide, and            hydrolyzing obtained 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile            under acidic conditions.
AU ZHU W
   LUO W
   ZHANG P
   BU L
AE JIANGSU FLAG CHEM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202299810M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic acid (I) with                2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene (II) as raw material,                involves using 2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene (II) to                generate 2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene (III)                through diazotization reaction, reacting                2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene (III) with magnesium                to generate Grignard reagent and reacting with                carbon dioxide to generate 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic                acid (I) or reacting 2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene                (III) with cuprous cyanide to generate                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile (IV), and hydrolyzing                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile (IV) under acidic                conditions to generate 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic                acid (I) or hydrolyzing under alkaline conditions                to generate 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoate (V) and                acidifying.
   USE - Method for synthesizing                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic acid (I) with                2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene (II) as raw                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the use of expensive                precious metal catalysts and highly toxic reagents,                and has high yield. The used reagent is                environmentally-friendly, reduces cost and                simplifies process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for synthesizing                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic acid of formula (I) with                2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene of formula (II) as raw                material, involves using                2-chloro-4-aminobromobenzene (II) to generate                2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene of formula (III)                through diazotization reaction, reacting                2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene (III) with magnesium                to generate Grignard reagent and reacting with                carbon dioxide to generate 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic                acid (I) or reacting 2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene                (III) with cuprous cyanide to generate                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile of formula (IV), and                hydrolyzing 2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile (IV)                under acidic conditions to generate                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoic acid (I) or hydrolyzing                under alkaline conditions to generate                2-chloro-4-fluorobenzoate of formula (V) and                acidifying.
   M=Li, Na, K or Cs.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials of                  the diazotization reaction include                  2-chloro-4-aminobenzonitrile, hydrochloric acid,                  sodium nitrite and sodium tetrafluoroborate. The                  solvent used in the reaction of                  2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene and cuprous cyanide                  includes N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl                  sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone or benzonitrile.                  The acid used to form the acidic condition                  includes hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  phosphoric acid or acetic acid. The alkali used                  to form alkaline conditions includes sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium                  hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate,                  lithium carbonate or cesium carbonate. The acid                  used for acidification includes hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid or acetic                  acid. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the 2-chloro-4-aminobenzonitrile, hydrochloric                  acid, sodium nitrite and sodium tetrafluoroborate                  is 1:1-5:0.5-5:0.5-5, preferably 1:1-5:1-3:1-5.                  The molar ratio of 2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene                  to cuprous cyanide is 1:0.5-5. The molar ratio of                  2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene to magnesium is                  1:1-2. The molar ratio of                  2-chloro-4-fluorobromobenzene to carbon dioxide                  is 1:1-20. The molar ratio of                  2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile to acid is                  1:0.5-20. The molar ratio of                  2-chloro-4-fluorobenzonitrile to the base is                  1:0.5-20.

The method allows                a maintenance engineer to test the duct smoke                detectors without removing a duct housing cover and                measuring differential pressure between an inlet                and an outlet of the detector with a pressure                measuring apparatus positioned by the engineer,                thus avoiding damage to the seal that seals the                detector housing or replacement of the cover                correctly in order to create required firm seal,                and hence avoiding air leakage outside of a                detector system and monitoring failure using                existing maintenance processes in an efficient                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for self-testing duct environment detector                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                self-testing duct environment detector                system.

PN CN114316631-A
TI Preparing functionalized nano calcium carbonate            for use in rigid polyvinyl chloride, involves calcining            limestone to obtain quicklime, digesting, reacting            quicklime and water to obtain calcium hydroxide,            sieving, standing, and configuring into calcium            hydroxide suspension.
AU ZHAN Q
   LU Q
   LU C
   ZHU Y
   LI H
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022558778
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing functionalized nano calcium                carbonate involves calcining limestone at                980-1160degrees Celsius to obtain quicklime,                digesting, reacting quicklime and water to obtain                calcium hydroxide, sieving, standing, configuring                into a calcium hydroxide suspension with a solid                content of 6.0-14.0 wt.%, adding saccharide crystal                form control agent to calcium hydroxide suspension,                introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to                carry out carbonation reaction, when the reaction                reaches pHless than or equal to7 of suspension,                stopping feeding mixed gas, and adding a modified                solution consisting of sodium stearate.
   USE - Method for preparing functionalized nano                calcium carbonate for use in rigid polyvinyl                chloride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared functionalized nano calcium                carbonate has excellent mechanical property,                enhanced yellowing resistance, whiteness, luster                and processability of the hard polyvinyl chloride                (PVC) product, and can completely replace a PVC                stabilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing functionalized nano calcium                carbonate involves calcining limestone at                980-1160degrees Celsius to obtain quicklime,                digesting, reacting quicklime and water to obtain                calcium hydroxide, sieving, standing, configuring                into a calcium hydroxide suspension with a solid                content of 6.0-14.0 wt.%, adding saccharide crystal                form control agent to calcium hydroxide suspension,                introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to                carry out carbonation reaction, when the reaction                reaches pHless than or equal to7 of suspension,                stopping feeding mixed gas, adding a modified                solution consisting of sodium stearate, epoxy                linseed oil and sorbitan monostearate into the                suspension after the carbonation reaction to                perform primary surface modification, adding a                modified solution composed of barium salt and zinc                salt to the suspension for secondary surface                modification to obtain a modified calcium carbonate                suspension, subjecting the obtained modified                calcium carbonate suspension to pressure                filtration, drying, and sieving to obtain                functionalized nano-calcium carbonate filled with                rigid polyvinyl chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sugar is selected                  from sucrose, glucose, and maltose. The barium                  salt includes barium chloride and barium nitrate.                  The zinc salt solution is selected from zinc                  chloride, zinc nitrate, zinc sulfate and zinc                  acetate.Preferred Composition: The limestone                  comprises less than or equal to90 wt.% calcium                  carbonate. The modified liquid is 50-80 wt.%                  sodium stearate treatment agent, 10-30 wt.%                  epoxidized linseed oil, 10-30 wt.% sorbitan                  monostearate, 40-60 wt.% barium salt, and 40-60                  wt.% zinc salt.Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  digestion reaction quicklime and water is 1:8-12.                  The added amount of variety of mixture is 0.3-0.5                  wt.% based on the calcium carbonate dry basis                  mass in the suspension. The carbon dioxide                  concentration in the mixed gas volume is 23-43                  vol.%. The flow rate of the mixed gas is                  0.01-0.04 m2/hours. The carbonation reaction                  control temperature of is 23-33degrees Celsius,                  and the stirring speed is 900-1200 revolutions                  per minute. The mass concentration of the                  modified liquid is 40-60 wt.%, and the usage                  amount is 20-40 wt.% based on the dry basis mass                  of calcium carbonate in the suspension.The method for primary surface modification                  and secondary surface modification involves                  stirring at a temperature of 100-120degrees                  Celsius, and stirring at speed of 800-150                  revolutions per minute for 0.5-1.0 hours,                  respectively.

TI Full life cycle non-destructive testing method for concrete dam carbonization depth, involves calculating maturity, compressive strength, which is found on depth-compressive strength relation curve corresponding to depth.
AU ZHANG Q
   CAO P
   GAO L
   LUO Y
   FAN P
   WANG R
   ZHANG F
   LI Y
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 202215060E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing a concrete test piece which is the same as the component of the concrete dam to be measured and establishing the relation curve of the carbonization depth-compressive strength. The pre-embedded wireless concrete temperature sensor is arranged in the to-be-measured concrete dam. The temperature of concrete is detected. The concrete temperature is detected according to the concrete temperature. The maturity and compressive strength is calculated. The carbonization strength is obtained corresponding to the carbonized depth of the to be-measurement concrete dam is obtained.
   USE - Full life cycle non-destructive testing method for carbonization depth of concrete dam used in high-rise building foundation, large equipment foundation, water conservancy dam and tunnel foundation.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete dam carbonization depth full life cycle non-destructive detection method effectively judges the compressive strength in the concrete by temperature, not only can prevent the damage of the concrete surface structure, but also can quickly obtain the carbonization depths of concrete, can reach the purpose of non destructive detection, and avoids the damage caused by the stable seepage of carbon dioxide. The method solves the problem of large volume concrete damage and damage and the measuring result accuracy is poor. The concrete test pieces are prepared by using the raw materials with the same proportions as the components of concrete dam to be measured, and the concrete test piece is placed in the standard curing box for removing carbon dioxide for 28 days.

TI Gas dry circuit branch cooperative control and gas safety cooperative control system, has controller located to position of alarm generation at same time to execute response, and compressed gas control valve whose end is connected to compressed gas carbon dioxide storage tank through pipeline.
AU YU L
AE YU L (YULL-Individual)
GA 2021C9497H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a dry circuit controller that closes a dry circuit control valve (YV1) judging and corresponding strategy when valve protection control strategy is turned off and one key is off. The dry circuit controller closes a gas branch-control valve (YNn) through a gas inner pipeline and a sleeve. A pressure relief valve (YM5) is utilized for protecting exhaust and preventing pressure in the sleeve from abnormally heightening and damaging the pipeline. A front end of a gas dry circuit sleeve pressure relief electric valve is set. A negative pressure fan assembly is set at an outlet of the pressure relief electric valve. An end of a compressed gas control valve is connected to a compressed gas carbon dioxide storage tank through a pipeline.
   USE - Gas dry circuit branch cooperative control and gas safety cooperative control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces gas flame flow rate, prolongs a path area of the gas flame contact and improves flame temperature rate. The system reduces exhaust temperature, automatically controls temperature and automatic shutdown and improves heat conduction efficiency and safety of the gas appliance. The system adopts a multi-link making synergistic explosion suppression strategy for improving safety of the system operation and standby process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas dry circuit branch cooperative control and gas safety cooperative control system.
   Negative pressure fan (FM2)
   Communication module supply module (RP)
   Pressure relief valve (YM5)

TI ENERGY TECHNOLOGICAL COMPLEX FOR GENERATION OF THERMAL AND MECHANICAL ENERGY AND METHOD FOR OPERATION OF COMPLEX.
AU BORISOV YU A
   DATSENKO V V
   KOSOI A A
AE AS RUSSIA HIGH TEMP JOINT INST (ARUS-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1076H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to power engineering, in particular to environmentally friendly and economically viable methods and installations for generating thermal and mechanical energy. The power engineering complex for the generation of thermal and mechanical energy includes a power plant (1) consisting of a combustion chamber, a steam-gas turbine connected to an electric power generator, lines for supplying oxygen, natural gas, water and carbon dioxide to the combustion chamber, as well as a line for cooling exhaust gases, made with the possibility of condensation of water and carbon dioxide, a cryogenic air separation unit (2), a coal mine ventilation system (3), while the coal mine ventilation system (3) is connected by an air supply line (5) from the coal mine (3) to the unit (2) cryogenic air separation. The complex contains an additional power plant (6) connected by a power supply line to a coal mine (3) and connected by a liquefied natural gas supply line (7) and a power supply line (8) with a cryogenic air separation unit (2), configured to provide liquefied oxygen by means (9) for transferring oxygen to the power plant (1). At the same time, the additional power plant (6) contains a tank for storing liquid hydrocarbons and is configured to regulate the amount of liquefied natural gas used in the plant, supplied from the line (7) for supplying liquefied natural gas and liquid hydrocarbons from the tank for storing liquid hydrocarbons. Also, a method for operation of the power engineering complex is disclosed.
   USE - Power engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the efficiency of the complex at low concentrations of natural gas contained in the air from the mine, as well as ensuring the maintenance of a given level of heat and mechanical energy production, the production of the required level of liquefied gases, which is necessary at least for the operation of the complex.

PT P
PN DE102020106591-A1; US2021285779-A1; CN113392573-A
TI Method for adjusting vehicle powertrain, involves determining location of vehicle, determining emission limit of location and adjusting operating parameters of vehicle based on multiple outputs from trained neural network.
AU ASLANDERE T I
   KIRBACI O
   DE SMET F
   ROETTGER D
   ASLANDERE T
   SMET F D D
AE FORD GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (FORD-C)
   FORD GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (FORD-C)
GA 2021A6305N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a location of a vehicle, determining an emission limit of the location and adjusting the operating parameters of the vehicle based on multiple outputs from a trained neural network. The emission limit of the location is based on one or more of a place of interest and a time of day. The adjusting operating parameters include adjusting engine RPM thresholds, adjusting hybrid operation, adjusting fuel injection parameters, adjusting an air/fuel ratio, and adjusting an EGR flow rate. The operating parameters are adjusted in response to adjustments to other vehicles at the location. The adjusting operating parameters includes adjusting a nitrous oxide output or a carbon dioxide output in response to a current total nitrous oxide output and a current total carbon dioxide output.
   USE - Method for adjusting a vehicle powertrain.
   ADVANTAGE - The driving cycle of the vehicle and the powertrain components are simulated. The simulations are used to obtain virtual vehicle data and emission data. The simulation data is cost-effectively compared to actual measurement data. The forward-facing propagation and a back propagation with a loss function are performed during the training. The time-dependent data is processed efficiently since recurrent neural networks are characterized by higher prediction accuracy compared to deep neural networks. The trained artificial neural network is characterized by a high prediction accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for predicting emission data of a vehicle based on vehicle data;
   (2) a system for data processing; and
   (3) a computer program for adjusting a vehicle powertrain.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a training phase. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN WO2021183064-A1
TI Producing multi-layer carrying bag with increased strength by applying glue to one surface of oriented polypropylene (OPP), overlapping PP net and PP non-woven with surface of OPP film, fixing layered materials to each other, and winding.
AU AVCI A
AE NETPAK AMBALAJ SANAYI TICARET LS (NETP-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7425R
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-layer carrying bag with increased strength is produced by gluing process of placing oriented polypropylene (OPP) film in a 1st warp unit, transferring glue to one surface while passing through transfer roller, overlapping PP net woven from PP filaments located on 2nd and 3rd warp units of PP net and surfaces of PP non-woven, overlapping PP net and PP non-woven with the surface of OPP film on which glue is applied in such a way that PP net remains in between and passes through printing roller, and fixed and printing roller process of fixing greater than or equal to 3 layered materials to each other at the printing roller and winding on a winding unit in the form of coil.
   USE - Production of multi-layer carrying bag with increased strength.
   ADVANTAGE - Because all of the layers used comprise 3 or 4 layer materials, such as PP, AYP, YYP, or those suitable for ultrasonic source, recycling is ensured to be performed at a rate of 100%. Since stronger products can be obtained by using less raw materials, carbon dioxide release and resource consumption are reduced. A direct contribution is provided to the cyclical economy. Since all layers comprise the same synthetic material specifications, they are welded with ultrasonic method. Since it can be produced with 100% automation, workmanship costs and carbon dioxide release are mitigated, and it can be ensured that all of the products produced are at the same standard. The carrying bag covers less space compared to bag having same strength. Since the texture is softer, the carrying bag can easily be shaped by folding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrying bag may be made with PP net as a three-layer, or as well as a two-layer or more layers again with PP net, and a more layered configuration may also be created by using more PP net layers to further increase the strength. The method enables the use of similar materials other than the OPP film and PP non-woven materials used in the state of the art to avoid negatively affecting the recycling situation and the recycling status, where the carrying bag can be structured in the form of OPP+PP Net+PP Non-woven, and also be applied as paper+PP Net and PP non-woven to make suitable for ultrasound welding as the non-woven touches the non-woven at joint places as the paper remains on layers. The carrying bag can be constructed in the form of AYP (low density polyethylene) film+AYP (low density polyethylene) Net+AYP (low density polyethylene) film. The carrying bag can be constructed in the form of YYP (high density polyethylene) film+YYP (high density polyethylene) net+YYP (high density polyethylene) film.

PT P
PN CN113372737-A
TI Preparing waste tire pyrolysis carbon black            electrode material useful for supercapacitor, comprises            activating tire pyrolysis carbon black, cooling, adding            potassium hydroxide and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide            solution, heating and washing.
AU CHEN J
   WU Y
   HOU S
   QING L
   LI R
   HU M
AE UNIV SICHUAN SCI & ENGINEERING (USLG-C)
GA 2021A8378R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing waste tire pyrolysis carbon black                electrode material comprises (i) adding tire                pyrolysis carbon black in a tube furnace and                activating for the first time in a carbon dioxide                atmosphere, cooling to room temperature under the                protection of carbon dioxide gas and taking out the                first activation product; (ii) adding potassium                hydroxide into the first activated product and                mixing, adding cetyltrimethylammonium bromide                solution, heating to 50 degrees C and stirring to                make fully uniformly mixed, after the stirring is                completed, drying until there is no obvious                solution, performing second activation under a                nitrogen atmosphere, cooling to room temperature to                take out the second activation product, washing                with deionized water, filtering with suction to                remove the residual potassium hydroxide, and                drying; and (iii) adding 15 M nitric acid solution                into the obtained second activation product,                placing at 100-120 degrees C to acidify for 2-3                hours, washing, filtering and drying the acidified                product.
   USE - The electrode material is useful for                supercapacitor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode material retains the original                graphitization degree of the material, adds some                defects, has a certain pore structure to increase                the interface area between the electrode material                and the electrolyte, obtains a higher specific                capacitance and reaches the standard of electrode                materials for supercapacitors. The method realizes                the preparation of the electrode material for the                pyrolysis carbon black-based supercapacitor of                waste tires, improves the resource utilization of                waste tires, reduces the cost of electrode                materials for supercapacitors, meets the national                green development requirements, and has good                applications prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: an                electrode material prepared by the above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  first activation product and potassium hydroxide                  is 1:4. The concentration of the                  cetyltrimethylammonium bromide solution is 0.01                  M. Preferred Method: The flow rate of the carbon                  dioxide is 200-400 ml/minute. The first                  activation is carried out by heating to 1000-1200                  degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 2-3                  hours. The flow rate of the nitrogen is 100-200                  ml/minute. The second activation is carried out                  by heating to 850 degrees C at a rate of 5                  degrees C/minute for 1-2 hours. The drying                  temperature is 80-100 degrees C, and the drying                  time is 8-12 hours.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors)

TI High-strength fireproof cable has permeable layer that is arranged inside flame retardant cavity flame retardant that is filled between outer flexible permeable layer and inner hot melt layer that is filled with flame retardant that is provided with flame retardant liquid.
AU WANG L
AE NANTONG LONGCAI TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6265B
AB    NOVELTY - The cable has a cavity that is formed between two adjacent rope portion (3). The cavity is embedded with a heat-conducting reinforcing belt connected between two adjacent rope portion. A flame-retardant cavity is formed between an inner fiber flaps and an outer ring sleeve. A flame-retardant frame (5) is arranged in the flame-retardant cavity. The flame-retardant frame is provided with an outer flexible permeable layer and an inner hot melt layer arranged from the outside to the inside. The outer flexible permeable layer is arranged inside the flame retardant cavity, and a flame retardant is filled between the outer flexible permeable layer and the inner hot melt layer. The inner hot melt layer is filled with flame retardant, and the flame retardant is provided with a flame retardant liquid.
   USE - High-strength fireproof cable.
   ADVANTAGE - The cable melts and overflows the coolant when the flame retardant in the outer flexible permeable layer encounters high temperature. The cooling liquid reacts with the effervescent filler to produce large amount of carbon dioxide. The overflowing carbon dioxide is adsorbed in the gap of the cable which effectively improves flame retardancy. The inner hot melt layer gradually melts. The fire-retardant material on the inner side overflows, and the cooling liquid is overflowed on the rope which improves the fire-retardant and fire-retardant properties.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the high-strength fireproof cable.

PN WO2021013382-A1; EP3769928-A1
TI Method for producing molded component, involves introducing medium into cavity of tool via several injectors, and shutting off supply of fluid medium to respective injector by inlet valve which is assigned to respective injector.
AU HENINGER R
   PRALLER A
   SZYCH P
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2021114251
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a material (10) comprising a polymer, and heating an inner surface (11a) of a cavity (12) of a tool (11) by introducing a fluid medium (13) into the cavity, where the medium is carbon dioxide, nitrogen or argon. The molten material is injected into the cavity of the tool for forming the molded component after or during the introduction of the fluid medium into the cavity. The medium is introduced into the cavity of the tool via several injectors (I1-I5) which are arranged next to one another along a flow path of the material through the cavity. The supply of fluid medium to the respective injector is shut off by an inlet valve (V1-V5) assigned to the respective injector.
   USE - Method for producing molded component from polymer-containing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables heating the interior of the closed cavity with a hot fluid medium, preferably with hot gas, so that the best possible heating rate is achieved. The method enables lower energy consumption, since only comparatively thin wall section has to be heated and cooled. The complex shapes are heated at much lower cost for the manufacture of the mold and the mold inserts. Shorter heating times and lower costs are possible for the heating system. The heating of the tool with carbon dioxide or nitrogen with the parameters specified leads to minimal heating times with minimal gas losses/consumption. The return of the heating gas/medium is carried out in order to further reduce the gas consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for producing molded component.
   Material (10)
   Tool (11)
   Inner surface (11a)
   Cavity (12)
   Fluid medium (13)
   Injectors (I1-I5)
   Inlet valves (V1-V5)

PN GB2584714-A
TI Symbiotic system for sorting municipal and general wastes, and also for sewage processing that can be run/operated as separated systems so as to give mutual symbiotic relationship to enhance increased efficiency and outputs of products from alcohol fermentation stage.
AU JACKSON J
AE JACKSON J (JACK-Individual)
GA 202102304A
AB    NOVELTY - Symbiotic system for the sorting of municipal and general wastes, and also for sewage processing that can be run/operated as separated systems so arranged as to give a mutual symbiotic relationship to enhance increased efficiency and outputs of the products from an alcohol fermentation stage and of biodegestion, anaerobic methanogenesis processes. The system comprises apparatus, vessels, pipe work to carry products and process flows, control valves and motors/pumps, carbon dioxide percolation systems, mixers, water treatment units, steam/heat generators, receiving and holding vessels, macerators, distillation units, gas separation units and/or their multiplicities.
   USE - The system is useful for sorting of municipal and general wastes.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is able to share water hence enables a further symbiotic efficiency, where wastewater from the sewage process, when treated is used in the system, which will normally need its own supply of water. The municipal wastes and sewage processes are shown in a symbiotic relationship that they can operate independently using their respective substrates without affecting the operation of the other and in continuous process/operations, and the final watered inert digestate substances from both processes can be collectively gathered/combined to produce fuel for combustion uses and thus do not go to landfill.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic representation of a waste processing system to process suitable non sewage wastes.
   Cold/warm water processing (CW)
   Flow of carbon dioxide from another source (C4)
   Fermentation vessel/vessels (FM)
   Steam supply for HTST (ST)
   Stream of sorted waste (S1)
   Flow of waste from HX to FM (S5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The process of high heat treatment is used to kill any biological life/microorganisms present prior to subsequent biologicaI/microbiological processes. The process flow is then subjected to an alcohol fermentation stage. The collected oils and fats are fermented along with substrate to produce alcohols. The alcohol fermentation process also produces carbon dioxide gas, and the produced alcohols will also produce glycerol as a fermentation product, which are known to be utilized by methanogenic microbes and enhance biodegestion biogas production. The alcohols can change/breakdown certain substances/materials such as those found in general/municipal wastes. The process produces more methane per kg of substrate being bio digested. The alcohols so produced by fermentation may cause volatiles to chemically form and that when distilled off from the substrate.

PN CN111921534-A
TI Catalyst used for purifying exhaust gas and            converting sulfide and benzene into carbon dioxide and            water, comprises cerium-metal-oxide solid solution,            copper oxide, tricobalt tetroxide, aluminum oxide,            niobium, sodium hydroxide, and water.
AU ZHANG Y
   LIU L
   SONG C
   CHENG X
   LI G
   LI X
   REN H
AE UNIV HENAN URBAN CONSTR (UHUC-C)
GA 2020B54855
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises 10-30 pts. wt.                cerium-metal-oxide solid solution, 8-15 pts. wt.                copper oxide, 10-16 pts. wt. tricobalt tetroxide,                10-15 pts. wt. aluminum oxide, 1-3 pts. wt.                niobium, 3-4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, and 20-30                pts. wt. water.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for purifying exhaust                gas, and for converting sulfide and benzene into                carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst avoids harm to the human body,                improves the safety of people, and can be prepared                by a simple method in an economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the catalyst, which involves: (1)                configuring a colloid by mixing cerium-metal-oxide                solid solution, copper oxide, tricobalt tetroxide                and niobium with a preset amount of water to                prepare a slurry, and adding the slurry to aluminum                oxide; (2) stirring the filler by adding an aqueous                hydrogen test solution to the colloid to adjust the                pH value to 5-7, then stirring for 40-55 minutes,                putting into an oven, and heating at 50-80 degrees                C to perform aging; (3) coating a carrier by evenly                coating the treated colloid on a cordierite                honeycomb ceramic carrier; (4) grinding and                roasting by putting the coated carrier into a                spherical grinder for grinding, collecting the                qualified abrasive particles, then putting into the                oven for roasting, and increasing the temperature                inside the oven to 480-520 degrees C for 4.5-5.5                hours; (5) blowing out the slurry by using                high-pressure inert gas to blow out the excess                slurry inside the honeycomb ceramic carrier gap,                then heating at 120 degrees C and drying for 30                minutes; and (6) testing and storing by sampling a                portion of the prepared catalyst, and                storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The oven includes an oven                  main unit, a lifting groove, a motor, a screw, a                  fixed rotating shaft, a cutting fork, a moving                  rotating shaft, a sliding block, a threaded hole,                  a supporting plate, a box door, and a door                  handle. The groove is provided on both inner                  walls of the oven main unit. The motor is                  embedded on the inner wall of the bottom end of                  the groove. The screw is rotatably connected to                  one side of the inner wall of the groove. One end                  of the output shaft of the motor is fixedly                  connected to the outer wall of one end of the                  screw. The fixed rotating shaft is installed on                  both inner walls of the oven main unit. The                  cutting fork is symmetrically installed on one                  inner wall of the oven main unit. The other end                  of the fixed rotating shaft is rotatably                  connected to the inside of the fork. The sliding                  block is slidably connected to the inner wall of                  one side of the groove. The inner wall of the                  middle portion of the sliding block is provided                  with the threaded hole corresponding to the                  screw. The moving rotating shaft is fixedly                  installed on one outer wall of the block. The                  other end of the moving rotating shaft is                  rotatably connected to the inner wall of the                  middle portion of the fork. The supporting plates                  are distributed and welded on the outer wall of                  one side of the fork. The box door is                  symmetrically rotated on one side of the outer                  wall of the oven main unit through a hinge. The                  door handle is welded and fixed on one side of                  the outer wall of the box door.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst                  preferably comprises 30 pts. wt.                  cerium-metal-oxide solid solution, 15 pts. wt.                  copper oxide, 16 pts. wt. tricobalt tetroxide, 15                  pts. wt. aluminum oxide, 3 pts. wt. niobium, 4                  pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, and 30 pts. wt. water.                  Preferred Method: The step (4) involves after the                  roasting is completed, ensuring that the coating                  quality is 18-21 g/l. Preferred Condition: The                  aging time is 50 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a low-pressure shut-off valve (10) including a first valve inlet, a second valve inlet and valve outlet. The second valve outlet is connected to and in fluid communication with the first valve inlet of the low-pressure shut-off valve. The solenoid valve configured to direct a flow of a pressurized gas from the valve inlet into one of the first valve outlet and the second valve outlet. A gas monitor is electrically coupled to the solenoid valve. The gas monitor is configured to transmit one of a first signal and a second signal to the solenoid valve to control the flow of the pressurized gas through the solenoid valve. The gas monitor is configured to detect one of a normal amount of carbon dioxide in an area surrounding the gas monitor and an elevated amount of carbon dioxide in the area surrounding the gas monitor.

TI Gas delivery and monitoring apparatus for delivering oxygen and monitoring exhaled carbon dioxide in patient, in which bridge positions bite block from support element where support element and bite block are positioned on cheek of patient.

GA 2020A0273Y
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (610) has a support element (650) comprising a longitudinal axis. A bite block (670) is affixed to the support element sized to be inserted within a mouth of a patient. A first elongated conduit (620) defines an exhalation capture flow path extending from a capture inlet at a distal end (656) of the bite block. A second elongated conduit (630) defines a gas delivery flow path extending from an inlet port into the bite block and terminates in a gas delivery port (634) at the distal end of the bite block, such that both the gas delivery port and capture inlet are located in the mouth of the subject when the bite block is inserted in the mouth of the subject during use. A bridge (660) connects the bite block to the support element, and extends away from the support element. The bridge positions the bite block from the support element such that the support element and the bite block are simultaneously positionable on opposite sides of a cheek of the patient.
   USE - Gas delivery and monitoring apparatus for delivering oxygen and monitoring exhaled carbon dioxide in patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The conduits are coated or sheathed with other material to provide rigidity and deformability. The bridge is sized such that both the first elongated conduit and the second elongated conduit are extendable along the surface of the bridge to prevent the conduits from kinking when bending around the corner of the mouth of the patient.

PT P
PN CN111377813-A
TI Catalyst used for synthesizing phenylpropiolic acid, comprises hydrotalcite loading active metal salt, and ion-exchange type molecular sieve including acid and alkali treated Y-type, beta molecular sieve and Y molecular sieves.
AU ZHANG Z
   WANG T
   SUN X
   YIN Z
   LIU Q
   FANG X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 2020669046
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises hydrotalcite loading active metal salt and ion-exchange type molecular sieve. The active metal salt is silver iodide, silver bromide, silver chloride, copper(I) iodide, copper(I) bromide and/or copper(I) chloride. The ion-exchange type molecular sieve is cation exchange molecular sieve. The cation is lithium, sodium, potassium and/or cesium.
   USE - The catalyst is used for synthesizing phenylpropiolic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is not easy to loss, has high dispersion degree of active center, reduces content of impurities in product and difficulty of separating and refining process, and greatly reduces production waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising impregnating hydrotalcite using soluble metal salt solution containing active metal salt, drying and calcining, treating solid with soluble carbonate solution, washing, drying, calcining to obtain hydrotalcite loading active metal salt, treating molecular sieve with soluble salt solution containing metal cations, washing, drying, calcining to obtain ion-exchange type molecular sieve, and mixing hydrotalcite loading active metal salt and ion-exchange type molecular sieve; and
   (2) synthesis of phenylpropiolic acid comprising using catalyst comprising adding phenylacetylene and dimethyl formamide into reaction kettle, adding catalyst, carbon dioxide substitution reacting under air atmosphere, reacting in the presence of carbon dioxide at room temperature to 40 degrees C for 12-24 hours, cooling to room temperature, transferring reaction material to alkali metal carbonate solution, stirring at room temperature for 1-24 hours, filtering reaction product to separate out catalyst, extracting mixing liquid by non-polar organic solvent, acidifying extracted water layer to pH of less than or equal to 1, extracting using polar organic solvent, and removing organic solvent in extracted organic layer to obtain solid product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of hydrotalcite loading active metal salt to ion-exchange type molecular sieve is 1:0.5-10. The loading amount of active metal salt on hydrotalcite is 5-20 wt.%. The magnesium/aluminum molar ratio of hydrotalcite is 3-6. The soluble salt solution is sodium iodide, sodium bromide, sodium chloride, potassium iodide, potassium bromide, potassium chloride, cesium iodide, cesium bromide, cesium chloride, silver nitrate, silver acetate, silver tetrafluoroborate, copper(II) nitrate, cpper acetate and/or copper(II) tetrafluoroborate. The concentration of soluble salt solution is 0.5-3 mol/L. The metal is lithium, sodium, potassium and/or cesium. The soluble salt is halogenated salts, nitrates, acetates and/or phosphates of metal. The concentration of soluble salt solution is 0.1-1 mol/L in terms of metal cations. The pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.5-2 MPa. The acidification acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or phosphoric acid. The drying agent is anhydrous sodium sulfate, anhydrous cupric sulfate, anhydrous sodium carbonate and/or allochroic silica gel. Preferred Method: The method involves impregnating hydrotalcite using soluble metal salt solution containing active metal salt for 2-8 hours, drying, calcining to 100-200 degrees C for 1-24 hours, treating solid with soluble carbonate solution at 25-70 degrees C for 2-12 hours, washing, drying, calcining to 100-200 degrees C for 1-24 hours to obtain hydrotalcite loading active metal salt, treating molecular sieve with soluble salt solution containing metal cations at 25-80 degrees C for 2-12 hours, washing, drying, calcining to 400-600 degrees C for 1-12 hours to obtain ion-exchange type molecular sieve, and mixing hydrotalcite loading active metal salt and ion-exchange type molecular sieve. Synthesis further involves reusing separated calcined catalyst. The organic solvent is removed by distilling at normal pressure, and vacuum distilling.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is microporous molecular sieve and/or mesoporous molecular sieve. The microporous molecular sieve comprises Y-type molecular sieve, beta molecular sieve, ZSM-5 molecular sieve and Y molecular sieve, and/or acid and alkali treated Y-type molecular sieve, beta molecular sieve, ZSM-5 molecular sieve and Y molecular sieve. The mesoporous molecular sieve comprises MCM-41 molecular sieve and/or MCM-48 molecular sieve. The carbonate solution is potassium carbonate, lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or cesium carbonate solution. The concentration of carbonate solution is 0.1-1 mol/L counted by carbonate ions. The solid-liquid ratio of treatment is 1:5-1:10 g/L. The adding amount of catalyst is 5-10% of weight of phenylacetylene. The non-polar organic solvent comprises methylene dichloride, chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, dichloroethane, trichloroethane and/or tetrachloroethane. The polar organic solvents include diethyl ether, diisopropylether and/or ethyl acetate.

PN CN111322778-A; CN111322778-B
TI Marine generator cooling system, has controller for switching cooling of generator from air branch to emergency branch when current duration exceeds threshold value, where working medium in emergency branch is carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen.
AU MA Q
   LIU G
   GUO J
   XU H
   ZHANG S
   LIU X
   WANG L
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG JIAOTONG (SDJT-C)
GA 2020595719
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a generator air inlet temperature sensor installed at an air inlet of a main generator. A generator air outlet temperature sensor is installed on an air outlet of the main generator. A controller is connected with a compressor, an electric control valve, an emergency branch air inlet valve, an emergency branch air outlet valve, a current data generator, the generator air inlet temperature sensor and the generator air outlet temperature sensor. The controller receives outlet temperature of the main generator, air outlet temperature rising rate, a value of a transient current generator, transient current rising rate and transient current duration. The controller switches cooling of the generator from air branch to emergency branch when the transient current rising rate or the transient current duration exceeds a threshold value, where working medium in the emergency branch is carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen.
   USE - Marine generator cooling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The air branch and the emergency branch utilizes normal temperature air in atmosphere during normal and low loads so that the emergency branch uses a compression refrigeration cycle, which allows working medium to obtain low gas temperature so as to reduce generator cooling problem. The working medium is recovered and recycled after the emergency cooling so as to improve cooling effect of the emergency branch. The system utilizes a control system for monitoring current of the generator and pre-starting the emergency branch to achieve better cooling effect of the generator so as to effectively, efficiently, quickly and automatically prevent overheating condition of the main generator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for controlling a marine generator cooling system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a marine generator cooling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN KR2111503-B1; WO2020230979-A1; CN113811486-A; EP3971083-A1; SG11202112322-A; US2022185441-A1; JP2022528443-W
TI Eco-friendly ship fuel supply system, has            greenhouse gas analyzer for detecting concentration of            greenhouse gas containing carbon dioxide and            controlling supply ratio of first fuel and ammonia fuel            based on value detected by gas analyzer.
AU KWON H
   AN S K
   JEONG S W
   RYU M C
   GANG G J
   JIANG J
   RYU M
   JEONG S
   AN S
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
   DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
   DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 202053406Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a selective catalyst reduction                (SCR) device (40) installed on an exhaust gas                discharge line to discharge exhaust gas from an                engine to reduce nitrogen oxides in the exhaust                gas. A fuel storage tank (10) stored supplies fuel                supplied as fuel of the engine. An ammonia storage                tank (20) supplies ammonia as fuel for the engine.                A greenhouse gas analyzer (30) is installed on the                exhaust gas discharge line to detect greenhouse gas                contained in exhaust gas discharged from the                engine. The greenhouse gas analyzer detects                concentration of greenhouse gas containing carbon                dioxide and controls supply ratio of the first fuel                and the ammonia fuel based on value detected by the                greenhouse gas analyzer.
   USE - Eco-friendly ship fuel supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves reduction rate of                greenhouse gas emitted from the engine by                selectively or mixing existing fuel and ammonia                fuel as fuel for propulsion engine and power                generation engine of a ship so as to regulate                to-be-strengthened greenhouse gas of the ship in                the future. The system reduces insufficient amount                of greenhouse gas reduction by using ammonia fuel,                and avoids completely reorganize existing fuel                supply system. The system reduces investment cost                and installation space, realizes effect of reducing                CAPEX of an ammonia storage/supply system and                increasing space utilization of the ship by                integrating a system for storing and supplying                ammonia necessary for the SCR reaction and ammonia                supplied as the engine fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of an                eco-friendly ship fuel supply system.
   Fuel storage tank (10)
   Ammonia storage tank (20)
