AB    NOVELTY - The window has a transparent material. A chamber-type window portion (110) is provided in a box shape with both ends open to form a chamber area in which micro-algae is cultured inside. A window frame is provided with an upper frame coupled to an upper portion of the chamber-type window, one frame coupled to one side of the chamber-type window, another frame coupled to the other side of the chamber-type window, and a lower frame coupled to the lower portion of the chamber-type window. The window portion allows the sunlight that enters the indoor side to be shaded by the cultured micro-algae.
   USE - Window for algae cultivation for bioenergy production, used in curtain wall unit of curtain wall of bird-curing building (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The window is capable of awning sunlight entering the interior by cultured microalgae, fixing carbon dioxide and discharging oxygen to purify the air. The microalgae can be utilized as a biomass for bioenergy production by culturing the microalgae through the chamber region formed inside the chamber-type window. The effect of fixing carbon dioxide and purifying air by discharging oxygen is realized. The microalgae cultivated in the chamber area of the chamber-type window efficiently awns sunlight entering the interior of the building. The inside of the chamber-type window is provided with a culture solution and algae. Thus, the energy load of the building is reduced by increasing the heat insulation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a curtain wall unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the curtain wall unit equipped with the window for algae culture for bio energy production.

TI Sodium metal oxide material preparation involves            mixing precursor materials comprising sodium salt and            salt of the element manganese, copper, titanium, iron,            magnesium, nickel, vanadium, zinc, aluminum, lithium,            tin, and silicon.
AU FOLD VON BUELOW J
   DAHL S
   FOLD V B J
   FOLDVONBULOW J
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202027647R
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium metal oxide material preparation                involves mixing precursor materials comprising                sodium salt and salt of the element manganese,                copper, titanium, iron, magnesium, nickel,                vanadium, zinc, aluminum, lithium, tin, silicon,                gallium, germanium, antimony, tungsten, zirconium,                niobium, molybdenum and tantalum to a mixed                precursor comprising carbonate. The mixed precursor                is placed in an oven 800-1000 degrees C to provide                the sodium metal oxide material. The sodium metal                oxide material is cooled at room temperature in an                atmosphere with less than 100 ppm carbon dioxide.                The oven is heated at 900-1000 degrees C and then                cooled.
   USE - Method for preparing sodium metal oxide                material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the sodium metal oxide                material in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with optimal material powder                properties including density, flowability, and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sodium metal oxide material preparation                involves mixing precursor materials comprising                sodium salt and salt of the element manganese,                copper, titanium, iron, magnesium, nickel,                vanadium, zinc, aluminum, lithium, tin, silicon,                gallium, germanium, antimony, tungsten, zirconium,                niobium, molybdenum and tantalum to a mixed                precursor comprising carbonate. The mixed precursor                is placed in an oven 800-1000 degrees C to provide                the sodium metal oxide material. The sodium metal                oxide material is cooled at room temperature in an                atmosphere with less than 100 ppm carbon dioxide.                The oven is heated at 900-1000 degrees C and then                cooled. The sodium metal oxide material has formula                NaxMyCozO2- delta . The average length of primary                particles of the sodium metal oxide material is                2-10 pm.
   M=Mn, Cu, Ti, Fe, Mg, Ni, V, Zn, Al, Li, Sn,                Si, Ga, Ge, Sb, W, Zr, Nb, Mo or Ta;
   x=0.7-1.3;
   y=0.9-1.1;
   z=0-0.15; and
   d=0-0.2.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reforming and separating device (3) that includes a methanol water vapor inlet pipe, a hydrogen outlet pipe and a carbon dioxide mixed residual gas outlet pipe. An outlet of a water gas reforming device (5) is connected to the reforming mixed gas outlet pipe. An air pump (8) is provided on the reformed mixed gas outlet pipe for lifting the reformed mixed gas delivery pressure in the pipe. The methanol water vapor inlet pipe and the hydrogen outlet pipe are both connected to a three-phase heat exchange device (2). The methanol water vapor inlet pipe is connected to a liquid pump (1). AE AKAY FLAVOURS & AROMATICS PVT LTD (HNSN-C)
GA 202016109K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biologically active black cumin seed                extract (BCE) in free flowing powder comprises                pre-treating powdered or flaked black cumin seeds                with enzymes, performing supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction of the oil from the seeds to                obtain super critical extracted oil fraction which                comprises non-volatile fixed oils rich in alpha                -linoleic acid and volatile oils rich in                thymoquinone; extracting the residual black cumin                seed material with hydrophilic solvent,                spray-drying and dissolving obtained residual BCE                free flowing powder in water to obtain residual BCE                solution at 10-25% total dissolved solids level;                mixing the supercritical extracted oil with the                residual black cumin seed solution, homogenizing or                ultrasonicating to produce emulsion; and                spray-drying or freeze drying the emulsion to                obtain black cumin seed extracted oil composition                in free flowing powder form. The black cumin seed                extracted oil composition in free flowing powder                form comprises 0.1-8% thymoquinone.
   USE - The method is for preparing a biologically                active BCE in free flowing powder form used to                treat anxiety, stress and sleep disorders along                with significant memory enhancement at relatively                lower dosage.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition derived from black cumin seeds                comprises high concentrations of thymoquinone, and                is safe and convenient for oral delivery. The                method is green.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process                for preparing biologically active BCE in liquid oil                form using black cumin seeds, comprising                pre-treating the powdered or flaked black cumin                seeds with water to form a slurry and further                treating the slurry with at least one enzyme;                drying the slurry to the powder form with 3-6%                moisture level; and extracting the liquid oil by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction from the                dried powder, where the BCE in the liquid oil form                comprises 2-43% thymoquinone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The enzyme is                  pectinase, amylase, galactosidase or                  protease.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-treatment with at                  least one enzyme is performed for 1-8 hours. The                  supercritical extraction of black cumin seeds is                  performed using carbon dioxide at a pressure of                  100-220 bar and temperature of 35-50 degrees C.                  The step of extracting the residual black cumin                  seed material using a hydrophilic solvent is done                  by solvent extraction at 50-60 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: The hydrophilic solvent                  used for extraction is water or a mixture of                  water and ethanol.
   ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer; Hypnotic; Nootropic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor. BCE at a dose                of 10 mu g produced 41.82% inhibition of acetyl                cholinesterase which is equal to that of donepezil                (5 mu g) with 45.39% inhibition. The same condition                applies with BCE 20 mu g (64.47%) and donepezil 10                mu g (65.03%).
   ADMINISTRATION - Dose of 50-500 mg/day, preferably 50-250                mg/day is administered orally.
   EXAMPLE - Matured and dried black cumin seeds were                flaked with a flake thickness maintained at 0.2-0.5                mm, and pulverized to particles of 0.05-0.25 mm.                Then 5 kg of black cumin seed powder was charged in                to supercritical fluid extractor and extraction was                conducted at 100-220 bar using micro filtered                carbon dioxide. The extraction process was                conducted at 42 plus minus 2 degrees C and the                extract was collected. The mixture was collected in                a separation chamber which was maintained at 70                degrees C. The extract was passed through anhydrous                sodium sulfate for removing moisture. The obtained                moisture-free oil was analyzed and blended to                produce black cumin oil extract containing                thymoquinone.

PT P
PN CN110767884-A
TI Inert metal lithium powder having a core-shell structure, prepared by e.g. taking pure metal lithium powder, and placing into reactor filled with inert gas under oxygen to make surface of pure metal lithium powder react with oxygen.
AU WANG S
   QU Y
   GUAN J
AE QU Y (QUYY-Individual)
GA 2020135847
AB    NOVELTY - Inert metal lithium powder having a core-shell structure, prepared by (i) taking pure metal lithium powder with a particle size of 5-100 mu m, placing into a reactor filled with inert gas under oxygen to make surface of pure metal lithium powder react with oxygen to generate layer of lithium oxide shell, obtaining inert lithium powder A having lithium (Li)/lithium oxide (Li2O) core-shell structure; (ii) taking the reactor filled with inert gas, placing inert metal lithium powder A, and then passing in carbon dioxide, and increasing the temperature of the reactor to 100-160 degrees C and reacting carbon dioxide and lithium oxide to form lithium carbonate shell with inert metal lithium powder B with Li/lithium carbonate (Li2CO3) core-shell structure; and (iii) using polyaniline as polymer shell, performing secondary coating on prepared inert metal lithium powder B i.e. obtaining inert lithium powder C having three-layer core-shell structure of Li/Li2CO3/polyaniline (PANI) as product.
   USE - Used as inert metal lithium powder having a core-shell structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The inert metal lithium powder: is present in the air below 90 degrees C, has no reaction occurs in NMP solvents or other reagents containing a small amount of water, has good stability, can directly prepare slurry for coating in the subsequent rolling process, can crush the inert shell, which plays the role of metallic lithium; adopts case material, which does not affect the normal electrochemical process of the lithium battery and simultaneously as powder, can flexibly grasp the amount of lithium used, does not change existing production processes and combined systems; and is an ideal lithium battery cathode and pre-lithiated material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium content in the inert lithium powder B is 97-98%. The solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethylacetamide, Dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, carbon tetrachloride or chloroform. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the flow rate of oxygen is controlled to be 0.7 l/hour, so that the thickness of the generated Li2O shell is 100-500 nm. The lithium content in the inert lithium powder A is 98-99%. In the step (ii), the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is controlled to be 1 l/hour, so that the thickness of the finally generated Li2CO3 shell is 100-200 nm. In the step (iii), the specific method of polyaniline coating is: adding 10-100 g polyaniline to 0.9-0.99 kg solvent, then heating to 50 degrees C and stirring for 1-2 hours, where prepared PANI solution having a concentration of 1-wt.%, adding 100 g inert lithium powder B to the prepared PANI solution, and fully dispersing the inert lithium powder B in the PANI solution using a non-destructive laminar flow stirring method to obtain inert lithium powder B mixed liquid, atomizing and drying the prepared inert lithium powder B mixed solution by spray drying, volatilizing the solvent that dissolves PANI, allowing PANI on the surface of inert lithium powder B, and forming a 50-500 nm PANI coating layer on the surface obtaining inert lithium powder C having a Li/Li2CO3/PANI three-layer core-shell structure.

AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical cell (1) comprises a                substrate (2) comprising a metal material having an                opening portion that cannot pass the gas, a support                component (3) comprising a material arranged on the                substrate so to cover the opening portion that can                pass the gas, and an oxide layer (4) arranged on                the side (A) of the support component so as to                cover the support component. The support component                is a sintered component of metal fibers. The outer                peripheral (3e) edge side of the support component                is joined to the substrate by a junction portion                that cannot pass gas. The oxide layer covers all of                the support component, and at least a layer that                covers a boundary portion (a) that is a boundary                between the support component and the junction                portion.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) support component; and
   (2) manufacture of electrochemical cell.

TI Artificial intelligent door system for being installed in e.g. building, has fire suppression apparatus driven by control unit to emit carbon dioxide when fire suppression apparatus determines door arrived at breakdown threshold point.
AU KIM K S
AE SAS CO LTD (SASS-Non-standard)
   KIM K S (KIMK-Individual)
GA 202004784L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fire determination unit configured to determine whether a current fire is occurred by receiving temperature information and smoke information transmitted from heat and smoke detection sensors. A power supply and charging device is provided with a control unit for controlling evacuation direction signal and a display device. A power supply is configured to supply power to a central control device. A battery is charged by the power supply and charging device, and supplies the power to the central control device. A fire suppression apparatus is installed in a door (1) and driven by the control unit to emit carbon dioxide to a fire occurrence area when the fire suppression apparatus determines the door arrived at breakdown threshold point.
   USE - Artificial intelligent door system for being installed in a building or a public facility i.e. flat.
   ADVANTAGE - The system recognizes sensing elements at different situations in real-time so as to simultaneously control operation of the door according to current situation, so that user convenience and safety of the user can be improved. The system to analyze behavior of the user by detecting movement of passers so as to easily recognize an evacuation person in an evacuation passage and form a staircase on an opposite side of the door, so that safe evacuation can be facilitated. The system improves quality of the indoor air with smooth ventilation to help human health by manually and automatically opening and closing the door during summer so as to maximize cooling and heating effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an artificial intelligent door system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Door (1)
   Handle (1a)

PT P
PN CN110577690-A
TI Packaging film for cold storage of sweet cherries, comprises graphene, silver nitrate, modified pullulan polysaccharide and polymer resin-based film.
AU QIU J
   CHEN L
   ZHAO S
   ZHOU Q
   CHEN X
   SUN H
   LI L
   YANG J
AE SHANDONG ACAD AGRIC SCI AGRIC RESOURCES (CAGS-C)
GA 202001262U
AB    NOVELTY - A packaging film comprises 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. graphene, 0.01-0.03 pt. wt. silver nitrate, 0.1-1.0 pt. wt. modified pullulan polysaccharide and 100 pts. wt. polymer resin-based film.
   USE - Packaging film for cold storage of sweet cherries.
   ADVANTAGE - The packaging film has good antibacterial and gas-proof properties with high sterilization, oxygen and carbon dioxide barrier properties, water vapor transmission rate and base film strength, and effectively adjust the oxygen and carbon dioxide content in sweet cherries packaging bags at -0.5-4 degrees C, and delays the breathing rate of sweet cherries. The packaging film maintains proper moisture content in the packaging bag, suppresses mildew, browning, water rot and other deterioration of sweet cherries during cold storage after water pre-cooling, effectively extends the storage period of sweet cherries, and increases the freshness of sweet cherries out of the warehouse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of packaging film, which involves ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide in water for 1-3 hours to obtain graphene oxide dispersion having concentration of 1 mg/ml, adding silver nitrate to the dispersion, adding sodium borohydride, raising the temperature to 80-100 degrees C, reacting for 2-4 hours, filtering, washing, drying to obtain nano silver modified graphene, adding nano silver modified graphene to N,N-dimethylformamide solution, ultrasonically processing for 0.5 hour to obtain a solution having concentration of 1 mg/ml, adding pullulan polysaccharide or its derivatives to the solution, stirring at room temperature overnight, filtering the reaction solution, washing to obtain pullulan-modified nano silver-modified graphene, mixing the polymer resin base dried in an oven at 80 degrees C with the modified substance, homogenizing, heating, shearing, extruding through twin screw extruder and granulating to obtain a modified resin and blowing the modified resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The modified pullulan polysaccharide comprises one of etherified pullulan polysaccharide, esterified pullulan polysaccharide and graft copolymerized pullulan polysaccharide. The polymer resin-based film comprises at least one of polyethylene, polypropylene, and polyvinyl alcohol. Preferred Conditions: The twin-screw extruder process is carried out by undergoing seven heating zones from the inlet to the outlet. The heating temperature in zone (A) is 165 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (B) is 185 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (C) and (D) is 190 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (E) is 180 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (F) is 175 degrees C and the heating temperature in zone (G) is 170 degrees C. The blowing of modified resin is performed in a single screw blowing machine by undergoing four heating zones from the inlet to the outlet, where the heating temperature in zone (P) is 150 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (Q) is 185 degrees C, the heating temperature in zone (R) is 190 degrees C and the heating temperature in zone (S) is 198 degrees C. The speed of the single screw is 40-80 rpm.

PN CN110375330-A; CN110375330-B
TI Staged oxygen supply combustion chamber used for rich gas turbine includes main combustion area, secondary combustion area, mixing area and combustion chamber outer jacket.
AU LI S
   QIAN W
   ZHU M
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2019902052
AB    NOVELTY - Staged oxygen supply combustion chamber comprises combustion chamber outer jacket and flame tube. The flame tube is arranged in the combustion chamber outer jacket. The flame tube is cylinder structure with one end in an arc-shaped contraction shape. A working medium gas channel is arranged between the flame tube and outer shell of the combustion chamber. The flame tube is divided into the main combustion area, secondary combustion area and mixing area. The front end of the main combustion area is provided with main nozzle. The secondary nozzle is secondary oxygen nozzle and secondary carbon dioxide nozzle. The secondary carbon dioxide nozzle is connected with working medium gas channel. One end of the secondary nozzle close to the main combustion area is arranged around the secondary combustion area. The mixing holes are arranged on the wall surface of the mixing area. The mixing holes are arranged around the mixing area.
   USE - The staged oxygen supply combustion chamber is used for rich gas turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The chamber has wide operation load regulation range, high adjustable ratio and low carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for staged regulation comprises reducing load of the gas turbine, supplying fuel to the main combustion area through the main nozzle of the staged oxygen supply combustion chamber is gradually reduced, reducing total oxygen flow rate according to the chemical equivalence ratio of fuel and oxygen, reducing total oxygen volume according to the order of reducing the first level oxygen supply after the first reduction of two level oxygen supply when the total oxygen flow is greater than the maximum oxygen content in the main combustion zone and reducing primary oxygen level of the main combustion zone when the total oxygen flow is less than the maximum oxygen content of the main combustion zone.

TI Laboratory tempering device e.g. carbon dioxide incubator for temperature controlled storing of sample for growth of cell culture, has chamber which is spaced from housing through spacer elements that are formed using specific material.
AU FITZER J

GA 201986116X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a temperature-controllable chamber which is arranged in the housing and whose chamber interior portion is surrounded by chamber wall for receiving the laboratory samples and several spacer elements (30'). The spacer elements has a first connecting portion by which the spacer and the housing are connected and a second connecting portion that is spaced from the first connecting portion through which the spacer and the chamber are connected, so that the chamber is spaced from the housing through the spacer elements which are formed using a material with a thermal conductivity of less than 15 W/(mK).
   USE - Laboratory tempering device e.g. incubator such as carbon dioxide incubator (claimed) for temperature controlled storing and tempering of laboratory samples in temperature control cabinet for growth of cell cultures, used in biological and medical laboratories.

The mechanical load is optimally distributed between the chamber and the housing, and the relative movements between the chamber and the housing prevent thermally induced mechanical stresses. The position of the plate-shaped component is stabilized. The heat transfer between wall end portion of the housing front wall and the wall end portion of the chamber front wall is minimized through thermal radiation and the thermal decoupling between the chamber and the housing is improved. The energy consumption of the incubator is reduced and the measurement efficiency is improved.

TI Intelligent ecological planting cabinet based on planting fruits and vegetables, has circulation pipe whose one end penetrates surface of insulation layer whose inner surface is fixedly connected with temperature sensor.
AU YANG B
AE ANHUI BAOJING ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOG (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201996183C
AB    NOVELTY - The planting cabinet has a cabinet structure (1) and a controller (2). The bottom of the inner cavity of the cabinet structure is fixedly connected with the top of the controller. A fan (3) is fixedly connected to the top of the inner cavity of the cabinet structure. An outlet pipe (7) is connected to a bottom of the heater (6). The bottom of the inner cavity of the cabinet structure is fixedly connected with an insulation layer (8). The surfaces of the outlet pipe and the return pipe (4) are connected to a circulation pipe (9). One end of the circulation pipe penetrates the surface of the insulation layer and extends to the inner surface of the insulation layer. The inner surface of the insulating layer is fixedly connected with a temperature sensor (10).
   USE - Intelligent ecological planting cabinet based on planting fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The intelligent ecological planting cabinet based on the cultivation of fruits and vegetables is provided, the fan drives the air circulation inside the cabinet, the air is mixed into the carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide generator, and the temperature of the heater is raised to make the air reach the temperature suitable for the growth of the crop, and then the gas is discharged. The pipes are passed into the cabinet and cooperate with the fill light to promote the photosynthesis of the plants.

PT P
PN US2019249132-A1; WO2019157519-A1; CA3090386-A1; CN111684050-A; IN202017030621-A; KR2020110706-A; AU2019218392-A1; EP3752587-A1; BR112020014839-A2; SG11202007617-A1; JP2021512782-W; EP3752587-A4; IN393050-B; US11441116-B2
TI Producing fermentable gas stream from input gas            stream for removing oxygen, acetylene, and hydrogen            cyanide from input gas stream, in which input gas            stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,            hydrogen, or combination.
AU ROSIN R
   GREENE J
   SCHULZ T
   ROSSIN R
   SCHULTZ T
   GREEN J
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZA TECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 201969707B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing fermentable gas stream from an input                gas stream, in which the input gas stream comprises                carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2),                hydrogen (H2), or a combination. The method                involves passing the input gas stream to a                hydrolysis module, where one constituent of the gas                stream is removed and/or converted to provide a                post-hydrolysis gas stream, then passing the                post-hydrolysis gas stream to an acid gas removal                module, where one further constituent of the gas                stream is removed and/or converted to produce an                acid gas depleted stream, and passing the acid gas                depleted stream to a deoxygenation module, where                one further constituent is removed and/or converted                to produce a fermentable gas stream.
   USE - Method for producing fermentable gas stream                from input gas stream for removing oxygen,                acetylene, and hydrogen cyanide from input gas                stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce fermentable gas                stream from input gas stream, which allows to                remove and/or convert various constituents found in                the gas stream which has harmful effects on                downstream removal modules and/or inhibitory                effects on downstream gas fermenting microorganisms                by removal modules, and using a portion of the                fermentable gas stream, which is preferably capable                of passing to a bioreactor, which contains gas                fermenting microorganisms, without inhibiting the                fermentation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for removing oxygen, acetylene, and hydrogen                cyanide from an input gas stream, which                involves:
   (A) reducing the oxygen to less than 100 ppm,                reducing the acetylene to less than 1 ppm;                and
   (B) reducing the hydrogen cyanide to less than                1 ppm by contacting the input gas stream with a                catalyst, where the catalyst comprises copper                supported on alumina, silica, titania, ceria,                lanthana, carbon, silica-alumina, or zeolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The constituent                  removed and/or converted is a microbe inhibitor                  and/or catalyst inhibitor. The constituents                  removed and/or converted by the acid gas removal                  module is selected from carbon dioxide (CO2),                  hydrogen sulfide (H2S), or hydrogen cyanide                  (HCN). The constituents removed and/or converted                  by the deoxygenation module is oxygen (O2) and/or                  acetylene (C2H2). The fermentable gas stream                  comprises no more than one hundred portions per                  million (100 ppm) oxygen (O2), one portion per                  million (1 ppm) hydrogen cyanide (HCN), and one                  portion per million (1 ppm) acetylene (C2H2). The                  catalyst is sulfided catalyst. Preferred                  Conditions: The constituents removed and/or                  converted by the hydrolysis module is carbonyl                  sulfide (COS) and/or hydrogen cyanide (HCN). The                  hydrolysis module is bypassed, in whole or in                  portion, and the input gas stream is delivered to                  the acid gas removal module. The method involves                  passing the acid gas depleted stream to a                  catalytic hydrogenation module, prior to passing                  to the deoxygenation module, where one                  constituent from the acid gas depleted stream is                  removed and/or converted prior to pass to the                  deoxygenation module and one constituent removed                  and/or converted by the catalytic hydrogenation                  module is acetylene (C2H2). The method further                  involves using one or more monitoring devices,                  placed before and/or after each module, for                  measuring the level of constituents in the gas                  stream. The method is capable of bypassing one or                  more module as a function of the level of one or                  more constituent in the gas stream. The                  fermentable gas stream is passed to a hydrogen                  cyanide removal module, prior to pass to the                  bioreactor, for removing a portion of hydrogen                  cyanide from the fermentable gas stream. A                  portion of the fermentable gas stream is passed                  to a bioreactor, where the bioreactor contains a                  culture comprising a fermentation broth and one                  or more microorganisms. A portion of the input                  gas stream is a synthesis gas and/or a producer                  gas. The input gas stream comprises oxygen up to                  7000 ppm, acetylene up to 700 ppm, and hydrogen                  cyanide up to 60 ppm. The input gas stream                  comprises oxygen up to 10000 ppm, acetylene up to                  1500 ppm, and hydrogen cyanide up to 500 ppm. The                  method consumes less than 10 percent of the                  carbon monoxide in the input gas stream, and the                  method is carried out at a pressure of at least                  138 kPag.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN CN109280027-A
TI Synthesis of 5,6-dimethoxy-2-picolinic acid by reacting 2-bromo-3-hydroxypyridine and elemental iodine in aqueous solution of potassium carbonate at room temperature, and reacting resultant with potassium carbonate in N,N-dimethylformamide.
AU XU H
   YANG L
AE KANGHUA SHANGHAI NEW DRUG DISCOVERY CO (KANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019158837
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of 5,6-dimethoxy-2-picolinic acid (4) comprises reacting 2-bromo-3-hydroxypyridine and elemental iodine in aqueous solution of potassium carbonate at room temperature to obtain hydroxy-containing compound (1), directly using (1) for the next reaction without purification by reacting (1) with potassium carbonate in N,N-dimethylformamide, adding methyl iodide, stirring overnight to obtain methoxy-containing compound (2), reacting (2) and sodium methoxide in N,N-dimethylformamide, selectively substituting bromine by methoxy to obtain dimethoxy-containing compound (3), reacting (3) and n-butyllithium at low temperature in tetrahydrofuran, adding solid carbon dioxide, reacting at room temperature, acidifying by hydrochloric acid to obtain (4), purifying by recrystallizing in petroleum ether and ethyl acetate mixed solvent and obtaining pure product.
   USE - Synthesis of 5,6-dimethoxy-2-picolinic acid (4).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the prior art problem of high reaction temperature, difficult operation and high device requirements.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of 5,6-dimethoxy-2-picolinic acid of formula (4) comprises reacting 2-bromo-3-hydroxypyridine and elemental iodine in aqueous solution of potassium carbonate at room temperature to obtain hydroxy-containing compound of formula (1), directly using (1) for the next reaction without purification by reacting (1) with potassium carbonate in N,N-dimethylformamide, adding methyl iodide, stirring overnight to obtain methoxy-containing compound of formula (2), reacting (2) and sodium methoxide in N,N-dimethylformamide, selectively substituting bromine by methoxy to obtain dimethoxy-containing compound of formula (3), reacting (3) and n-butyllithium at low temperature in tetrahydrofuran, adding solid carbon dioxide, reacting at room temperature, acidifying by hydrochloric acid to obtain (4), purifying by recrystallizing in petroleum ether and ethyl acetate mixed solvent and obtaining pure product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The second reaction temperature is 20-30 degrees C. The third reaction temperature is 90-110 degrees C. The low temperature reaction is conducted at - 70 to - 85 degrees C. The acidification is conducted using 1 N hydrochloric acid.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing sweetener acesulfame or sucralose mother liquor comprising (i) adding quicklime in preparation tank containing demineralized water, stirring to obtain 20-30% lime solution, adding in mother liquor mixing tank containing the acesulfame or sucralose mother liquor by the transfer pump and stirring, (ii) opening lower air inlet of the bubbling decolorization tower, blowing carbon dioxide gas, simultaneously starting acesulfame or sucralose mother liquor transfer pump from the feed pipe into the top feed port of the bubbling decolorization tower, uniformly spraying through the distributor, contacting mixture of the acesulfame mother liquor or sucralose mother liquor and lime solution with the blasted carbon dioxide gas in the column to produce calcium carbonate particles, adsorbing, embedding impurities and pigments of the mother liquor, pumping mixed liquid into the plate and frame filter at the bottom of the bubbling decolorization tower, collecting filtered clear liquid into the filtrate tank, carrying out condensation and crystallization to obtain acesulfame or sucralose.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.

PT P
PN US2018339265-A1
TI Method for reducing emission of constituent in flue gases generated by electric power plant, involves deferring regeneration of stored portion of rich solvent regenerated during operation of power plant at lower power demand level.
AU ELLIOTT W R
   GULEN S C
AE BECHTEL INFRASTRUCTURE & POWER CORP (BECH-Non-standard)
GA 201894841S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves exposing lean solvent to flue gases (1) that result from the combustion, and absorbing from the flue gases a portion of the constituent thus producing rich solvent by absorption of the portion of the constituent. The rich solvent is effective upon application of energy to the rich solvent to release the gas portion and to regenerate the solvent in lean condition. The rich solvent is collected and applying to the rich solvent a portion of the energy that is produced by combustion in the power plant, thus releasing the gas portion and regenerating lean solvent for re-use. The gas portion released by the rich solvent is collected and arranged. The rich solvent produced during operation of the power plant is accumulated in storage at a relatively higher power demand level, and deferring regeneration of a stored portion of the rich solvent that is regenerated during operation of the power plant at a relatively lower power demand level.
   USE - Method for reducing emission of constituent in flue gases generated by electric power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The two taxing post-combustion carbon dioxide capture plant design challenges to minimize regeneration energy needed by selecting a solvent with a relatively low reaction energy, and to use the lowest possible energy steam extraction to provide the requisite energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for reducing emission of flue gas constituents.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide capture from flue gas.
   Flue gases (1)
   Cooled flue gas stream (2)
   Flue gas stream (3)
   Treated flue gas stream (4)
   Heat exchanger (122)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The solvent is selected from the group comprises of diethanolamine, monoethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, diisopropanolamine and aminoethoxyethanol (diglycolamine).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The power plant includes combustion of fossil fuel and the constituent is greenhouse gas such as carbon dioxide.

GA 201834945B
AB    NOVELTY - A nano ceramic paint comprises silica sol, silane coupling agent, surfactant, leveling agent, modified filler, nano ceramic powder, melamine, diisocyanate, 12-hydroxy stearic acid, ammonium sulfate, and deionized water. The modified filler is prepared by mixing zeolite powder, silicone oil, calcium carbonate, nano carbon dioxide and sodium glutamate, adding sodium lauryl sulfate, heating, adding acrylic acid, stirring, drying to obtain a solid mixture, adding the solid mixture to a solution of ( alpha )-olefin sodium sulfonate, vacuumizing, grinding, filtering and drying.
   USE - Nano ceramic paint.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano ceramic paint is environmentally-friendly, pollution-free, has high surface strength, excellent water resistance and corrosion resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nano ceramic paint comprises 78-82 pts. wt. silica sol, 1.5-2.5 pts. wt. silane coupling agent, 3-5 pts. wt. surfactant, 1.2-1.8 pts. wt. leveling agent, 32-40 pts. wt. modified filler, 3-5 pts. wt. nano ceramic powder, 2-4 pts. wt. melamine, 1-4 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. 12-hydroxy stearic acid, 0.02-0.04 pt. wt. ammonium sulfate, and 60-70 pts. wt. deionized water. The modified filler is prepared by mixing zeolite powder, silicone oil, calcium carbonate, nano carbon dioxide and sodium glutamate in the mass ratio of 20:1.2:6:3:0.5, adding 3.5 %mass sodium lauryl sulfate in an amount of 5 mass times the zeolite powder, heating the solution at 85 degrees C for 2 hours, adding 5 %mass acrylic acid, stirring at 500 rpm for 40 minutes, drying using rotary evaporator to obtain a solid mixture, adding the solid mixture to a solution of 2.5 %mass ( alpha )-olefin sodium sulfonate, vacuumizing, grinding the mixture for 2 hours, filtering and drying to constant weight.

TI Preparation of nano-cellulose microporous foam material, involves dripping concentrated sulfuric acid to aqueous solution of microcrystalline cellulose in ice-water bath, stirring, centrifuging and ultrasonically-reacting.
AU LI Z
AE SHAANXI JUJIEHAN CHEM CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201831475X
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nano-cellulose microporous foam material, involves (1) dripping concentrated sulfuric acid to aqueous solution of 10% microcrystalline cellulose in ice-water bath to concentration of 50%, stirring at 40 degrees C, centrifuging resulting suspension, ultrasonically-reacting in ice bath for 0.5 hours, adjusting pH to neutral to obtain nanocrystalline cellulose suspension, (2) adding nanocrystalline cellulose suspension to polyethylene glycol aqueous solution, stirring for 2 hours, evaporating water solvent in water bath at 90 degrees C and vacuum drying.
   USE - Preparation of nano-cellulose microporous foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nano-cellulose microporous foam material with uniform shape and size by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of nano-cellulose microporous foam material, involves (1) dripping concentrated sulfuric acid to aqueous solution of 10% microcrystalline cellulose in ice-water bath to concentration of 50%, stirring at 40 degrees C, centrifuging resulting suspension, ultrasonically-reacting in ice bath for 0.5 hours, adjusting pH to neutral to obtain nanocrystalline cellulose suspension, (2) adding nanocrystalline cellulose suspension to polyethylene glycol aqueous solution, stirring for 2 hours, evaporating water solvent in water bath at 90 degrees C and vacuum drying to obtain polyethylene glycol/nanocrystalline cellulose composite filler, (3) heating poly lactic acid and polyethylene glycol/nanocrystalline cellulose composite filler with stirring at 170 degrees C at 30 rpm and mixing for 5 minutes to obtain poly lactic acid/nanocrystalline cellulose composites, (4) taking poly lactic acid/nanocrystalline cellulose composite material and pure poly lactic acid material, placing on foam table in foaming chamber, removing air in chamber, purging with carbon dioxide, adjusting pressure in chamber to 14 MPa, immersing material in supercritical carbon dioxide and reducing pressure.

AU QIN G

   HU X
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)

AB    NOVELTY - 1,2-Di(o-salicylaldehyde-diimine-p-hydroxyphenyl)ethane zinc complex (I), is new.
   USE - New 1,2-di(o-salicylaldehyde-diimine-p-hydroxyphenyl)ethane zinc complex is used as Schiff base catalyst for preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The 1,2-di(o-salicylaldehyde-diimine-p-hydroxyphenyl)ethane zinc complex has excellent catalytic efficiency and steric hindrance, and enables preparation of poly(cyclohexene carbonate). The content of poly(cyclohexene carbonate) is 204 g per 1 g catalyst. The content of polycarbonate in the poly(cyclohexene carbonate) is more than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 1,2-Di(o-salicylaldehyde-diimine-p-hydroxyphenyl)ethane zinc complex of formula (I), is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I), which involves adding aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide to mixture of salicylaldehyde and 1,2-dibromoethane, heating for 5 hours, cooling to room temperature, separating organic phase, washing with 10 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, drying with anhydrous magnesium sulfate, cooling, filtering, drying, obtaining 1,2-di(o-salicylaldehyde)ethane, dissolving 1,2-di(o-salicylaldehyde)ethane and p-aminophenol in ethanol, stirring, reacting at reflux temperature for 6 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, drying, obtaining 1,2-bis(o-salicylaldehyde diimine-p-hydroxyphenyl)ethane ligand, adding the ligand to three-necked bottle in nitrogen atmosphere, adding toluene, dissolving, adding 10 wt.% diethylzinc toluene solution, cooling in an ice salt bath, and stirring for 1 hour; and #preparation of poly(cyclohexene carbonate), which involves adding cyclohexene oxide and the compound (I) to reactor, adding carbon dioxide, stirring, polymerizing at 80-150 degrees C for 6-36 hours, cooling with water, releasing remaining carbon dioxide, adding chloroform, stirring, adding resultant product to methanol, stirring, precipitating, and drying.

The molar ratio of 1,2-di(o-salicylaldehyde)ethane and p-aminophenol is 1:2. The molar ratio of the ligand and diethylzinc is 1:1. The mass ratio of the compound (I) and cyclohexene oxide is 1:100-500.

PT P
PN WO2018029613-A1; GB2555773-A; GB2555773-B; EP3497362-A1
TI Method of insulating tank for containing liquefied            gas, involves sequentially applying multiple layers to            outer surface of tank.
AU NIKOLAISEN P I
AE MGI THERMO PTE LTD (MGIT-Non-standard)
   ALBUTT A (ALBU-Individual)
   MGI THERMO PTE LTD (MGIT-Non-standard)
GA 201813222H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sequentially applying                multiple layers to the outer surface of the tank                (1). The sequential layers comprise a first foam                layer applied to the tank and a second impervious                coating applied to the first layer. Each of the                layers surrounds a preceding layer. The first layer                is a polyurethane foam layer (5) comprising                multiple individual polyurethane sub-layers. The                foam layer is a premix of polyol and isocyanate and                is formed of sub-layers, each layer is between 5mm                and 35mm in thickness.
   USE - Method of insulating tank for containing                liquefied gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The impervious barrier prevents damage to the                foam layer during use and prevents humidity from                entering into the open cavities or cells within the                foam layer itself which can damage and degrade the                foam. The impervious layer prevents the emission of                carbon dioxide and the ingress of air into the foam                cells which advantageously prevents ageing or                degradation of tile foam layer. A foam layer with                an impervious encapsulating layer significantly                minimizes the ageing of the thermal properties of                the insulation layer by both preventing the ingress                of ambient air into the cells within the foam and                the egress of carbon dioxide from inside the cells                of the foam. The insulating layer significantly                reduces polyurethane foam ageing thus improving the                operating life of insulation layers. The                combination of aluminum foil and polymeric backing                provides tile desired impervious quality in                combination with flexibility and ease of                application to the outer surface of the foam                layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                ship.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                liquefied natural gas (LNG) tank incorporating an                insulation system.
   Tank (1)
   Inner tank case (2)
   Support (3)
   Polyurethane foam layer (5)
   Outer layer (8)

PT P
PN CN107671113-A
TI Treating contaminated soil with several plants, by applying organic fertilizer to contaminated soil, setting greenhouse, sowing sod grass and wild amaranth seeds, maintaining, mowing, drying, extracting heavy metal, and remediating soil.
AU XIAO K
AE HUNAN KUANGCHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201814586D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating contaminated soil with several plants, involves applying organic fertilizer to to-be-recovered soil in farmland, ditching, setting a greenhouse, isochronically sowing sod grass seed in trench in the soil, sowing wild amaranth seeds on-demand on the blank ditch, controlling temperature, humidity, lighting time, radiant power and carbon dioxide concentration in the greenhouse, supplementing nitrogen, when the growth cycle ended and cleaned up, mowing, drying, extracting the heavy metal after incineration, repeating, and performing remediation.
   USE - The method is useful for treating contaminated soil with several plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for treating contaminated soil with several plants, involves applying organic fertilizer to to-be-recovered soil in farmland, ditching, setting a greenhouse above the to-be-recovered soil, isochronically sowing sod grass seed in trench in the soil, leaving at least one blank groove between each adjacent row of sowing sod grass-type ditches, sowing wild amaranth seeds on-demand on the blank ditch, controlling temperature, humidity, lighting time, radiant power and carbon dioxide concentration in the greenhouse, supplementing nitrogen, when the wild amaranth growth cycle ended and cleaned up, transplanting the seedlings of sorrel grass in the groove, when the sod grass grows to 50-80 cm, mowing portion of 5-8 cm aboveground the ground, drying the ground portions of the mowed area at a designated place, burning to extract heavy metals, when the wild amaranth grows to 30-50 cm, mowing the aboveground portion 3-6 cm above the ground, drying the mulched aboveground portion at a designated place, extracting the heavy metal after incineration, repeating the cutting, drying and extracting after the incineration, and performing remediation of heavy metal contaminated soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method involves forming a shed with a removable shade net, where 7-9 months each year the shade net is kept opened. When the sunlight intensity is lower than 15 lux, the fill light is on, where the fill light is a metal halide lamp, and the ratio of the red light of 600-700 nm wavelength and the blue light of 400-550 nm wavelength is 1:1.5-1:2.5, which is emitted from the lamp. When the illumination intensity is higher than the 40 lux, the greenhouse temperature is controlled to 25-30 degrees C, and when the illumination intensity is 15-40 lux, the greenhouse temperature is controlled to 15-20 degrees C. When sunlight intensity is higher than 40 lux or when fill light is on, the concentration of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse increases. The method involves applying nitrogen once in every 7-10 days, and phosphorous fertilizer once in every 30-40 days.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PN PL418011-A1; PL238484-B1
TI Preparing cyclic alkylene carbonates, comprises introducing alkene and alkylhydroperoxide and molybdenyl acetylacetonate catalyst into a reactor, mixing whole mixture and cooling, and adding immobilized catalyst.
AU JASIAK K
   SIEWNIAK A
   BAJ S
   KOTYRBA L
AE POLITECHNIKA SLASKA PSTROWSKI (PLSK-C)
GA 201830891S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic alkylene carbonates, comprises e.g.: introducing alkene and alkylhydroperoxide in content of 1-2 (preferably 1.1) moles of alkylhydroperoxide/mole of alkene, and molybdenyl acetylacetonate catalyst in content of 0.0005-0.005 (preferably 0.001) mole molybdenyl acetylacetonate/mole alkene, into a reactor; mixing whole mixture at 70-110 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C for at least 1 hour and cooling to 20 degrees C; and adding immobilized catalyst based on tetraalkonyonium halide immobilized on polymer support, to prepare reaction mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic alkylene carbonates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic alkylene carbonates, comprises: introducing an alkene and alkylhydroperoxide in a content of 1-2 (preferably 1.1) moles of alkylhydroperoxide/mole of alkene, and molybdenyl acetylacetonate catalyst in a content of 0.0005-0.005 (preferably 0.001) mole molybdenyl acetylacetonate/mole alkene, into a reactor; mixing the whole mixture at 70-110 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C for at least 1 hour and cooling to 20 degrees C; adding immobilized catalyst based on tetraalkonyonium halide immobilized on a polymer support, preferably tributylmethylammonium chloride immobilized on polystyrene crosslinked divinylbenzene in a content of 0.001-3.3 mole catalyst to prepare reaction mixture; adding 0.01 mol an alkene and an aqueous zinc bromide complex in a content of 0.001-0.1 (preferably 0.003)/1 mol of alkene, where the molar ratio of zinc(II) bromide to water is 10: 1-5:1, preferably 5:1, and carbon dioxide, so that the pressure of the carbon dioxide in the reactor is 0.5-3 (preferably 0.9) MPa; mixing the whole mixture again at 70-110 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C for 4-10 hours; filtrating immobilized catalyst; and extracting the filtrate with ethanol and filtrating the precipitated molybdenyl acetylacetonate.

PT P
PN CN111359638-A
TI Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst            used in carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction            reaction to convert carbon dioxide into methanol,            obtained by subjecting photocatalytic material with            nickel selenide/tungsten selenide.
AU GUO F
   ZHANG L
   LI X
   LIU R
   ZHANG K
   LIU K
   JIA W
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
GA 202063802C
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                catalyst is obtained by subjecting a photocatalytic                material with a nickel selenide/tungsten selenide                composite structure to high-temperature annealing                treatment in a tube furnace.
   USE - The photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                catalyst is used in a carbon dioxide photocatalytic                reduction reaction to convert carbon dioxide into                methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                catalyst has high crystal grain orientation, has                high efficiency, excellent selectivity and stable                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The photocatalytic                  material of the nickel selenide/tungsten selenide                  composite structure is obtained by hydrothermal                  reaction using sodium tungstate, nickel nitrate                  and selenium powder as raw materials. The                  photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst                  is obtained by using sodium tungstate, nickel                  nitrate, and selenium powder as raw materials,                  through hydrothermal treatment at 180-200 degrees                  C, forming photocatalytic material with a nickel                  selenide/tungsten selenide composite structure,                  and placing in a tube furnace at 500-550 degrees                  C. The method for preparing photocatalytic carbon                  dioxide reduction catalyst comprises (a) placing                  sodium tungstate and nickel nitrate in deionized                  water and stirring to completely dissolve to                  obtain solution (A), (b) adding the dispersant to                  deionized water, stirring to completely dissolve                  it to obtain solution (B), (c) adding selenium                  powder to hydrazine hydrate with a concentration                  of 40-80%, and heating under reflux to react for                  2-3 hours to obtain solution (C), (d) mixing                  solution (A) and (B), pouring into solution (C),                  and sonicating uniformly to obtain a mixed                  solution, (e) transferring the above mixed liquid                  into a hydrothermal reaction kettle and                  hydrothermally react at 180-200 degrees C for                  24-48 hours, (f) cooling the reaction, washing                  with deionized water 3-4 times, transferring it                  into sodium hydroxide solution, heating the                  reaction to remove unreacted selenium powder,                  washing with deionized water, placing in a 60                  degrees C oven to dry to obtain nickel                  selenide/tungsten selenide composite structure                  photocatalytic material, and (g) placing the                  nickel selenide/tungsten selenide photocatalytic                  material in a tube furnace in a nitrogen                  atmosphere, and reacting at 500-550 degrees C for                  2-3 hours to obtain a photocatalytic carbon                  dioxide reduction catalyst. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of sodium tungstate                  to dispersant is 1:(1.1-1.3). The molar ratio of                  tungsten ion to nickel ion is 1:(0.1-0.8).                  Preferred Property: The mass concentration of                  sodium hydroxide in the step (f) is 30-40%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Preferred Components:                  The dispersant is citric acid.

PN CN111111465-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separating membrane comprises carrying out carbonization treatment of polyvinyl chloride film or polyvinyl chloride powder in oxygen-free atmosphere to obtain product.
AU YU H
   XU B
   ZHAO X
   CHEN H
   DOU H
   DING J
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 202041514T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separating membrane comprises carrying out carbonization treatment of polyvinyl chloride film or polyvinyl chloride powder in an oxygen-free atmosphere to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separating membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes polyvinyl chloride as substrate with poor thermal stability, which has a wide range of raw materials and low price; has simple and easy operating process; has low or no adsorption capacity for nitrogen; adsorbs and separates carbon dioxide/nitrogen; and has high selectivity for carbon dioxide/nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation membrane prepared by the above mentioned method, preferably the carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation membrane adsorbs carbon dioxide from 0.00409-0.414 mmol/g, and the carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation coefficient is greater than 101.22;
   (2) gas separating method, comprising separating carbon dioxide and nitrogen in a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and nitrogen from each other by the carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation membrane; and
   (3) gas separating device, comprising carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the carbonization treatment is 300-800 degrees C and time is 60-180 minutes. The method specifically comprises in an oxygen-free atmosphere, heating the polyvinyl chloride film or polyvinyl chloride powder to 300-800 degrees C at the rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute for carbonization treatment, maintaining the temperature for 60-180 minutes, cooling to room temperature at the rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, and obtaining carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas separation membrane. Preferred Components: The oxygen-free atmosphere comprises nitrogen atmosphere and/or inert gas atmosphere. The organic solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide, cyclohexanone, dichloroethane, tetrachloroethane, and/or tetrahydrofuran.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymerization degree of the polyvinyl chloride is 850-1200. The polyvinyl chloride comprises polyvinyl chloride SG-4, polyvinyl chloride SG-5 and/or polyvinyl chloride SG-6. The concentration of the polyvinyl chloride solution is 10-15 wt.% and the heating temperature is 60-80 degrees C. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises dissolving polyvinyl chloride powder in an organic solvent, heating to form a polyvinyl chloride solution, then applying the polyvinyl chloride solution to the substrate, and removing the organic solvent to obtain a transparent and flat polyvinyl chloride film. The method specifically comprises casting the polyvinyl chloride solution on the substrate and drying at 60-80 degrees C to volatilize the organic solvent to obtain a transparent and flat polyvinyl chloride film.

GA 201911864Y
AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical carbon dioxide desizing method                involves winding a slurry through a reel machine,                where the slurry is soluble in carbon dioxide, and                putting the slurry after the winding into the                desizing kettle, opening the pressure pump, and                inserting an appropriate amount of carbon dioxide                into the supercritical carbon dioxide dynamic                desizing system, heating the desizing kettle to                make the carbon dioxide in the desizing kettle                reach the supercritical state, and simultaneously                turning on the circulation pump and the pressure                relief valve, adjusting the pressure pump and                pressure relief valve, keeping the process                condition stable in the whole supercritical carbon                dioxide dynamic desizing system, slowly circulating                the supercritical carbon dioxide in the entire                circulation system by the circulating pump, after                desizing, turning off the pressure pump, and                entering the carbon dioxide fluid into the recovery                separation vessel to separate and recover the                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for desizing supercritical carbon                dioxide.

PN CN108087071-A; CN108087071-B
TI Determination of diesel particulate filter carbon            load involves calculating soot emission quality,            calculating carbon dioxide emission quality,            calculating carbon consumption, and determining diesel            particulate filter carbon load.
AU TANG J
   CAO Q
   XU L
AE NANJING ECOCAT EMISSION TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201843338K
AB    NOVELTY - Soot transient emission is calculated, and the                soot emission quality is calculated based on the                soot transient emission. Carbon dioxide transient                emission is calculated, and the carbon dioxide                emission quality is calculated based on the carbon                dioxide transient emission. The carbon dioxide                emission quality value is compared with the                detected value of carbon dioxide sensor, to                determine the accuracy of the calculated carbon                dioxide emission quality value. If the carbon                dioxide emission quality value is consistent with                the detected value of the carbon dioxide sensor,                the carbon consumption is calculated using the                carbon dioxide emission quality value and carbon                element content in carbon dioxide. The soot                emission quality value is detected from the carbon                consumption, to derive diesel particulate filter                (DPF) carbon load. If the DPF carbon load exceeds                the set value, the DOC pre-injection diesel program                is started.
   USE - Determination of diesel particulate filter                carbon load.
   ADVANTAGE - The diesel particulate filter carbon load is                accurately calculated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - If the carbon dioxide emission quality value                does not meet the requirements of the carbon                dioxide sensor detection value, the DPF catalytic                carrier is opened for inspection.

The filtered gas is sent into a microbubble                generator, where an agitator and an ultrasonic                transducer of the reactor are started. The carbon                dioxide-containing microbubbles generated by the                microbubble generator is sent into a carbonation                reaction in a device. The fly ash carbonation                reaction is carried out, where the wet fly ash is                obtained after carbonation. The massive carbonation                fly ash alarm is obtained, where a tea picking                machine is provided with a semi-automatic cleaning                mechanism. PN KR2350961-B1
TI Manufacturing bio-sulfur for treating degenerative brain diseases e.g. Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Lou Gehrig's disease comprises spraying sulfur powder to rapid cooling device to collect sulfur powder and freeze-drying.
AU HYUN S
AE HYUN S (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2022118609
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing bio-sulfur comprises immersing sulfur powder in sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and stirring, placing sulfur powder in a fine sieve and dehydrating in a centrifugal separator, immersing dehydrated sulfur powder in acetic acid aqueous solution and stirring, placing sulfur powder stirred in the acetic acid solution in a fine sieve and dehydrating in a centrifuge, placing the dehydrated sulfur powder into a supercritical fluid extraction system chamber, filling carbon dioxide with nitrogen in 5-step supercritical extraction system chamber, fixing temperature of supercritical extraction system chamber and applying pressure with supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide fluid, spraying sulfur powder stirred with supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide to a rapid cooling device to collect the sulfur powder and drying the collected sulfur powder in a freeze-drying device.
   USE - The bio-sulfur is useful for treating degenerative brain diseases e.g. Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Lou Gehrig's disease (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces oxidative stress in HT22 cells to enhance cell viability and reduces nitrite, has the efficacy of fundamentally treating degenerative brain diseases without side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing bio-sulfur comprises (i) immersing 20 kg sulfur powder in 50 l sodium hydroxide (10%) aqueous solution and stirring for 2 hours (30 times/minute); (ii) placing stirred sulfur powder in a fine sieve and dehydrating in a centrifugal separator; (iii) immersing dehydrated sulfur powder in 40 l acetic acid (5%) aqueous solution and stirring for 1 hour (30 times/minute); (iv) placing sulfur powder stirred in the acetic acid solution in step (iii) in a fine sieve and dehydrating in a centrifuge; (v) placing the dehydrated sulfur powder into a supercritical fluid extraction system chamber (capacity: 60 l, pressure 500 bar, neodymium agitator shaft Dal phi 50 equipped); (vi) filling 10l carbon dioxide (CO2) with 0.5 l nitrogen (N2) in the 5-step supercritical extraction system chamber; (vii) fixing temperature of supercritical extraction system chamber at 80 degrees C and applying 300 bar pressure with supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide fluid for 30 minutes (20 times/minute) of stirring treatment; (viii) spraying sulfur powder stirred with supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide to a rapid cooling device at -20 degrees C to collect the sulfur powder; and (ix) drying the sulfur powder collected in step (viii) in a freeze-drying device (dry shelf temperature -10 to 40 degrees C).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sulfur powder is treated with a supercritical synthesis CO2 fluid obtained by adding nitrogen (N2) to carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective; Nootropic; Antiparkinsonian. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Immerse 20 kg sulfur powder in 50 l sodium hydroxide (10%) aqueous solution in a drum, and stirred for 2 hours (30 times/minute). Stirred sulfur powder is placed in a fine sieve and dehydrated in a centrifuge. The sulfur powder dehydrated is immersed in 40 l acetic acid (5%) aqueous solution and stirred for 1 hour (30 times/minute). The sulfur powder is placed in a fine sieve and dehydrated in a centrifuge. The sulfur powder obtained is transferred to a supercritical fluid extraction system and dehydrated in 10 l carbon dioxide and 0.5 l nitrogen (N2) by 6-step filling treatment with an ultra-high pressure pump (ultra-high pressure CO2 pump: Germant, Hydraulic Diaphragm pump Orlita specifications; MH-proMinent pressure: 400 bar). The temperature in supercritical fluid extraction system chamber is fixed at 80 degrees C, and it is created by applying a pressure of 300 bar with an ultra-high pressure booster pump and stirring treatment with supercritical synthetic carbon dioxide fluid is carried out for 30 minutes (15 times/minute). The sulfur powder is rapidly cooled to -20 degrees C to collect sulfur. The sulfur powder is freeze-dried.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

IP A61K-033/04; A61P-021/00; A61P-025/16; A61P-025/28; C01B-017/02

PT P
PN CN109607567-A; CN109607567-B
TI Producing synthetic ammonia, sodium bicarbonate, sodium formate and urea-based product, comprises e.g. mixing hydrogen and nitrogen, conveying to ammonia synthesis device, mixing carbon-containing tail gas pretreated and reacting.
AU LUO J
   QI L
   JIAN K
   LUO Y
   WANG F
AE NANJING NEW CENTURY JIANGNAN ENVIRONMENT (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201935834U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthetic ammonia, sodium bicarbonate, sodium formate and urea-based product, comprises (a) taking hydrogen-containing tail gas obtained by hydrogen recovery device to obtain hydrogen gas and hydrogen-extracting tail gas, mixing hydrogen and nitrogen, conveying to an ammonia synthesis device to obtain synthetic ammonia, and conveying ammonia-releasing gas to hydrogen recovery device; (b) mixing carbon-containing tail gas is pretreated, a part of the hydrogen-extracting tail gas, conveying to a carbonization device, and reacting with sodium carbonate to obtain sodium hydrogencarbonate and carbonized tail gas; (c) reacting carbonized tail gas with caustic soda to obtain sodium formate; (d) pretreating carbon-containing tail gas, conveying a part of the carbon-containing tail gas to the carbon dioxide recovery unit to obtain carbon dioxide and conveying tail gas to sodium formate device; and (e) reacting product carbon dioxide with synthetic ammonia to obtain urea.
   USE - The method is useful for producing synthetic ammonia, sodium bicarbonate, sodium formate and urea-based product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing synthetic ammonia, sodium bicarbonate, sodium formate and urea-based product, comprises (a) taking hydrogen-containing tail gas obtained by hydrogen recovery device to obtain hydrogen gas and hydrogen-extracting tail gas, mixing hydrogen and nitrogen, conveying to an ammonia synthesis device to obtain synthetic ammonia, and conveying ammonia-releasing gas to hydrogen recovery device; (b) mixing carbon-containing tail gas is pretreated, a part of the hydrogen-extracting tail gas, conveying to a carbonization device, and reacting with sodium carbonate to obtain sodium hydrogencarbonate and carbonized tail gas; (c) reacting carbonized tail gas with caustic soda to obtain sodium formate; (d) pretreating carbon-containing tail gas, conveying a part of the carbon-containing tail gas to the carbon dioxide recovery unit to obtain carbon dioxide and conveying tail gas to sodium formate device; and (e) reacting product carbon dioxide with synthetic ammonia to obtain urea, where hydrogen-containing tail gas includes methanol purge gas, ammonia synthesis, deflation, the chlor-alkali unit electrolyzes hydrogen, coal hydrogen tail gas or dry gas hydrogen extraction product hydrogen, and the carbon-containing tail gas includes calcium carbide furnace tail gas, limestone calcination tail gas or yellow phosphorus tail gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device utilized in the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sum of the contents of carbon-monoxide and carbon-dioxide in the product hydrogen is less than 10 parts/million, oxygeb content in the nitrogen is less than or equal to 5 parts/million, preferably less than or equal to 0.5 parts/million and molar ratio of product hydrogen to nitrogen is 2.8-3.2. The carbon feed to the carbonization unit is 30-75% of the total gas. The hydrogen recovery unit comprises membrane recovery, pressure swing adsorption or cryogenic rectification. The carbon-dioxide volume fraction of the product carbon-dioxide gas is not less than 97%, carbon dioxide recovery unit includes pressure swing adsorption decarburization, low temperature methanol washing, NHD decarburization, PC decarburization and water elution carbon. Preferred Method: The concentration of caustic soda is 30-40%, sodium chloride content in caustic soda is less than or equal to 0.01%, reaction pressure is 2.5-4 MPa, and reaction temperature is 200-280 degrees C.

TI Laser beam polishing comprises polishing or            grinding the fused silica optical glass element,            selecting continuous carbon dioxide laser source and            scanning path and annealing in high temperature            annealing furnace.
AU SHAO J
   HE T
   WEI C
   GUO M
   JIANG Z
AE CAS SHANGHAI INST OPTICS & FINE MECHANIC (CAOF-C)
GA 201935061H
AB    NOVELTY - Laser beam polishing comprises (i) polishing                or grinding the fused silica optical glass element                to fine grinding stage, cleaning the fused optical                glass element using an optical component cleaning                method, ensures the surface of the component clean                and free of impurities, and placing the fused                silica optical glass component on a clean polishing                platform; (ii) selecting continuous carbon dioxide                laser source and scanning path according to                requirement, adjusting the scanning speed to 1-1                m/second according to the laser energy and the spot                size, and adjusting the scanning pitch to smaller                than the radius of the laser beam (1/e2); (iii)                decreasing the laser energy of the continuous                carbon dioxide laser source when the temperature of                the area to be polished is more than the material                vaporization temperature or the preset temperature;                and (iv) placing laser-polished fused silica                optical glass in a high temperature annealing                furnace and annealing.
   USE - The method is useful for laser beam                polishing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates residual thermal                stresses caused by laser beam polishing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Laser beam polishing comprises (i) polishing                or grinding the fused silica optical glass element                to fine grinding stage, cleaning the fused optical                glass element using an optical component cleaning                method, ensures the surface of the component clean                and free of impurities, and placing the fused                silica optical glass component on a clean polishing                platform; (ii) selecting continuous carbon dioxide                laser source and scanning path according to                requirement, adjusting the scanning speed to 1-1                m/second according to the laser energy and the spot                size, and adjusting the scanning pitch to smaller                than the radius of the laser beam (1/e2); (iii)                decreasing the laser energy of the continuous                carbon dioxide laser source when the temperature of                the area to be polished is more than the material                vaporization temperature or the preset temperature,                increasing the laser energy of the continuous                carbon dioxide laser source when the temperature of                the area to be polished is less than the preset                temperature and ensures the process temperature of                the polished fused silica optical glass element is                within a constant temperature range; and (iv)                placing laser-polished fused silica optical glass                in a high temperature annealing furnace and                annealing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The light source used                  is a continuous carbon dioxide laser source and                  the light source is non-focused light. The                  scanning path includes raster scan path, one-way                  round-trip scan path, spiral scan path or random                  scan path. The process parameters are set                  according to the polishing temperature and the                  polishing temperature is adjusted close to the                  gasification temperature and less than the                  gasification temperature. The annealing treatment                  is divided into heating phase, heat preservation                  phase and cooling phase and the cooling phase is                  further divided into slow cooling and rapid                  cooling phase. The temperature of heating stage                  was heated from room temperature at 2 degrees                  C/minute-950 degrees C. The heat preservation                  stage temperature is 950 degrees C for 10 hours.                  The cooling stage cooled to 700 degrees C/hour at                  15 degrees C and the temperature was lowered to                  room temperature at 30 degrees C/hour.
