TI Carbon dioxide application device for supplying carbon dioxide in combustion exhaust gas into agricultural house, has rectification unit which is arranged in liquid storage tank and provided upstream in flow direction of exhaust gas.
AU KATO H
AE FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
GA 201926185S
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide application device has a liquid storage tank which is configured to store a liquid (L) containing a compound. A taking-in flow path is provided to take the combustion exhaust gas into the liquid storage tank. A supply flow path is configured to supply the combustion exhaust gas passed through the liquid to the downstream side of the liquid storage tank. The liquid storage tank is included with a purifier (C) for supplying the compound to the liquid. A taking-in flow path is provided with a taking-in piping (11A) for releasing the flue gas into the liquid in the liquid storage tank. The liquid storage tank is provided with a rectification unit arranged in the liquid. The rectification unit is arranged in the liquid storage tank and provided upstream in the flow direction of the combustion exhaust gas with respect to taking-in piping with respect to the storage position of the purifier, so as to disperse the combustion exhaust gas discharged from the taking-in piping.
   USE - Carbon dioxide application device for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas into agricultural house such as greenhouse for garden plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The purification effect of the combustion exhaust gas is enhanced easily and reliably, while suppressing the decomposition and rotation of the purifier reliably. The efficiency of the replacement operation of the purifier is enhanced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The rectification unit is configured to form the flow of the liquid toward the purifier by dispersing the flue gas. The taking-in piping is provided to discharge the combustion exhaust gas from the lower side to the upper side in the liquid. The liquid storage tank is served as the rectifying unit and is provided with a plate in which the flue gas discharged from the open end of the taking-in piping is collided. The liquid storage tank is included with a case for storing the purifier. The case is arranged on the plate and is provided with an opening upward. The purifier is held in the container, so that extraction is possible from liquid storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the liquid storage tank and taking-in piping of carbon-dioxide application apparatus.

TI Sparkling drink useful for softening blood vessels, contains fermented vinegar, vinegar flavor, edible sucrose, edible honey, honey flavor, potassium sorbate, grape seed extract, anthocyanins and sodium cyclamate.
AU YANG C
AE YANG C (YANG-Individual)
GA 201877745Y
AB    NOVELTY - Sparkling drink comprises 2.5-10 wt.% fermented vinegar, 0.01-0.02 wt.% vinegar flavor, 1-9 wt.% edible sucrose, 0.1-0.5 wt.% edible honey, 0.005-0.01 wt.% honey flavor, 0.01-0.015 wt.% potassium sorbate, 0.01-0.1 wt.% grape seed extract, 0.01-0.1 wt.% anthocyanins, 0.01-0.06 wt.% sodium cyclamate, 0.005-0.01 wt.% acesulfame potassium, 0.005-0.008 wt.% aspartame, 0.005-0.01 wt.% sucralose, 1.5-3.5 times edible carbon dioxide under standard conditions, and the remainder being water.
   USE - The sparkling drink is useful for softening blood vessels, beautifying and maintaining young.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared drink is fashionable, healthy, tasty and refreshing health drink and suitable for maintaining the health of middle and old age people.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the sparkling drink, which involves (a) removing stem from fresh grape, crushing, and performing sterilization by uniformly adding 150-250 mg/kg food grade sulfur dioxide for 5-10 hours, (b) adding 0.1-2% wine yeast and 0.1-2% mother of vinegar to the crushed grape and fermenting at 28-32 degrees C for 20-40 days, (c) separating the fermented solid-liquid mixture after separating skin residue, filtering and aging the material, (d) calculating the aging in accordance with finished product of 10-ton steam-containing grape fruit vinegar drink, adding 200 kg purified water to batching tank and heating at 65-70 degrees C, adding sucrose, dissolving uniformly, adding edible honey, honey flavor, potassium sorbate, sodium cyclamate, acesulfame potassium, aspartame, sucralose, and grape vinegar, stirring uniformly, filtering, performing ultra-high temperature (UHT) sterilization, pumping the mixture into sterile tank, and mixing uniformly with sterile pure water, (e) cooling the mixed material liquid obtained in step (d) by refrigerating at 4 plus minus 1 degrees C, adjusting the mixing pressure of washed carbon dioxide mixing unit to 0.3-0.7 MPa in accordance with product requirements, mixing carbon dioxide gas, testing carbon dioxide mixture gas capacity at any time so that the carbon dioxide mixture capacity meets the product requirements, and (f) directly connecting the material liquid obtained in step (e) to aseptic filling machine from carbon dioxide mixing unit and aseptic filling the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The water is sterile water. Preferred Composition: The grape fruit vinegar drink has a total acid content of 0.3-0.5%, solid content of 1-10%, sugar content of 1-10%, and ethanol content of less than 0.4%. Preferred Method: The wine yeast is formed by using 20-50 times 15% sucrose aqueous solution or glucose aqueous solution, and activating at 30-36 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The mother of vinegar is formed by using 20-50 times 15% sucrose aqueous solution or glucose aqueous solution and activating at 28-36 degrees C for 1-2 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic.

TI Fixation of carbon dioxide during manufacturing process, involves contacting mixture containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride with gas containing carbon dioxide by feeding gas into mixed solution.
AU KENJI S
   SORIMACHI K
AE SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
   KENJI S (KENJ-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
   SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
   SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
   SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
GA 2020B63484
AB    NOVELTY - Fixation of carbon dioxide involves contacting mixture containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride with gas containing carbon dioxide by feeding gas into mixed solution. The mixed solution comprises 0.01-0.2 N sodium hydroxide and 0.05-0.5 mol/L calcium chloride.
   USE - Fixation of carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas, during manufacturing process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fixation method of carbon dioxide enables excellent reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of a fixed carbon dioxide, which involves fixing carbon dioxide by fixation of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The fixation of carbon dioxide involves feeding gas is bubbled into the mixed solution gas, and cooling the mixed solution. Preferred Composition: The mixed solution comprises 0.05 N or more sodium hydroxide. AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide pressurizing system (1) connected with a carbon dioxide fracturing fluid preparation system (2). A front end of a carbon dioxide supporting agent repeatedly settling system (3) is connected with the carbon dioxide fracturing fluid preparation system. A rear end of the carbon dioxide supporting agent repeatedly settling system is connected with a data collecting and control system (4). The data collecting and controlling system shoots and stores a sedimentation process video of a supporting agent. A recording supporting agent sedimentation tank moves a piston displacement, sets and controls turning speed and frequency of an electromagnetic reversing control shaft, and adjusts the brightness of a light source.
   USE - Device for evaluating compatibility of carbon dioxide fracturing fluid by using carbon dioxide supporting agent used in oil and gas resource exploitation such as shale oil and shale gas reserves.

The device is simple in structure and convenient for operation, and provides technical guidance for carbon dioxide anhydrous fracturing technology optimization.

AB    NOVELTY - Polyoxometallate catalyst comprises zinc and                at least two heteropolyanions.
   USE - The polyoxometallate catalyst is useful for                producing methane by photoreduction method of                carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyoxometallate catalyst produces methane                with selectivity of greater than or equal to                90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing polyoxometallate catalyst                comprising (a) preparing a first compound                comprising arsenic, hydrogen, boron, phosphorus,                silicon, germanium, nickel, rhodium, tin, aluminum,                copper, iodine, bromine, fluorine, iron, cobalt,                chromium, zinc, tellurium, manganese and/or                selenium, (b) preparing a second compound                comprising molybdenum, vanadium, niobium, tungsten,                manganese, iron, cobalt, chromium, nickel, zinc,                rhodium, ruthenium, titanium, aluminum, gallium                and/or indium, (c) forming heteropolyanion of                formula ((X1M1mOy)(n-) (I) mixing the first                compound and the second compound, and (d) preparing                catalyst by mixing the heteropolyanion and compound                containing zinc; and
   (2) photoreduction method of carbon dioxide,                comprising (a1) preparing the polyoxometallate                catalyst, (b1) preparing solution by dissolving the                polyoxometallate catalyst in solvent, and (c1)                reducing carbon dioxide by irradiating the solution                with light.
   X1 = H, B, P, S, As, Si, Ge, Ni, Rh, Sn, Al,                Cu, I, Br, F, Fe, Co, Cr, Zn, Te, Mn and/or Se,                preferably P;
   M1 = Mo, V, Nb, W, Mn, Fe, Co, Cr, Ni, Zn, Rh,                Ru, Ti, Al, Ga and/or In, preferably W;
   m = 5-30;
   y = 15-110; and
   n = 5-20.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyoxometallate                  catalyst has sandwich-type keggin structure; and                  has average particle size of 0.80-3 nm, and                  average zeta potential in an aqueous solution of                  -35 mV to -12.5 mV. The heteropolyanion has                  lacunary keggin structure. The light is in the UV                  or visible region. The solution further comprises                  a light absorbent containing iridium. The                  solution further comprises sodium sulfite.                  Preferred Method: The photoreduction method of                  carbon dioxide further comprises (d1) saturating                  the solution with carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - 120 g Sodium tungstate dihydrate was dissolved                in 150 ml distilled water. Then, 8.17 g disodium                phosphate and 22 ml acetic acid were additionally                added. After about 1 minute, the formed white                sediment was obtained through vacuum filtration,                and heat-treated at 140 degrees C for 6 hours to                obtain polyoxotungstate ( beta -(PW9O34)(9-)) .                Then, 0.99 g zinc chloride dihydrate was dissolved                in 12 ml distilled water, and then 5.1 g                polyoxotungstate was mixed and stirred for about 10                minutes. Then, 1 g potassium chloride was added to                form white precipitate, which was then vacuum                filtered and dried to obtain polyoxotungstate                catalyst.

Preferred Parameters: The value obtained by dividing the specific gravity by specific gravity in the standard state of second fluid is set to 1.02 when the specific gravity in the standard state (0 degrees C and 1 atm) of gas of the hydrocarbon adsorbed on the zeolite is 1.02. The second introduction route is connected to upper portion of the zeolite storage portion so that the second fluid is introduced from the upper portion of the zeolite storage portion, or the specific gravity in the standard state of the gas of the hydrocarbon adsorbed to the zeolite is divided by the specific gravity in the standard state of the second fluid. Preferred Process: The first introduction route to the zeolite storage portion of the first fluid and the second introduction route to zeolite storage portion of the second fluid are constituted to be selectively communicated with the zeolite storage portion. The method includes removing the hydrocarbon adsorbed on the zeolite from zeolite by introducing a second fluid containing carbon dioxide gas into the zeolite storage portion and circulating. The method includes a step of discharging the hydrocarbon from which the impurities are separated from first fluid by the zeolite from the zeolite storage portion; and a step of sending the mixture of the hydrocarbon and the second fluid removed from the zeolite to a flare processing portion from the zeolite storage portion. The method includes a process for mixing the hydrocarbon removed from the zeolite storage portion by the distribution of the second fluid and then mixing the hydrocarbon with the first fluid before being introduced into the zeolite housing portion.

PN CN113351211-A; CN113351211-B
TI Mesoporous fibrous catalyst useful for methanation            of carbon dioxide and carbondioxide hydrogenation            conversion reactions comprises cerium dioxide and            nickel.
AU HU F
   ZHANG R
   FENG G
AE UNIV NANCHANG (NANU-C)
GA 2021A75098
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous fibrous catalyst comprises cerium                dioxide and nickel, where the nickel is a nano                particle with a size of 4-6 nm and the content in                the catalyst is 1-20%, the cerium dioxide is in the                shape of mesoporous nanofibers and the content in                the catalyst is 80-99%, the specific surface area                is 25-120 m2/g, the pore diameter is 5-50 nm, the                fiber length is 20 nm-2 mu m, and the diameter is                50-500 nm.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methanation of                carbon dioxide (claimed) and carbondioxide                hydrogenation conversion reactions.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good porosity, specific                surface area and reactive centers, enhances the                carrier's adsorption of carbondioxide; improves the                conversion rate; reduces optimal activity                temperature; has a controllable morphology, high                efficiency and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing catalyst comprising placing the                mesoporous nanofiber cerium dioxide containing                nickel particles prepared by the method in a                furnace tube, pretreating at 200 degrees C for 2                hours in an inert atmosphere, and finally calcining                at 500 degrees C for 2-4 hours in a muffle furnace                to obtain a mesoporous ceria fibrous catalyst                containing nickel particles; and
   (2) use of catalyst for methanation of carbon                dioxide comprising normal pressure, reaction                temperature of 225-425 degrees C, reaction space                velocity of 12000-36000/hours, where molar ratio of                ydrogen and carbon dioxide is 4:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nickel nanoparticles                  are prepared from nickel salt precursors using                  high-temperature pyrolysis, where the salt                  precursors of nickel include nickel nitrate,                  nickel acetate, and nickel acetylacetonate. The                  high-temperature pyrolysis method for preparing                  the nickel nanoparticles comprises (i) taking                  salt precursor of nickel and dissolving it in the                  mixed liquid of oleylamine and oleic acid; (ii)                  heating fully dissolved to 100 degrees C for 1-3                  hours, carrying out in an oxygen-free and                  anhydrous state, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone with                  2-10 times the molar mass of nickel, and                  continuing to keep warm and stirring for 1 hour,                  cooling to room temperature, where the cooling                  process still needs to maintain an oxygen-free                  and anhydrous state; and (iii) adding absolute                  ethanol to the stirred mixture for washing,                  centrifuging, and drying to obtain nickel                  nanoparticles of uniform size, and dissolving the                  nickel nanoparticles in n-hexane. The mesoporous                  nanofiber-shaped cerium oxide is prepared from                  cerium salt precursors using an electrostatic                  spinning method, where the cerium salt precursors                  include cerium nitrate and cerium acetate. The                  electrostatic spinning method to prepare the                  mesoporous nanofiber-shaped cerium oxide                  comprising (a) adding cerium salt precursor to                  N-N dimethylformamide, and stirring it with                  ultrasonic for 10 minutes to completely dissolve                  to obtain a uniform light yellow transparent                  solution; (b) adding polyacrylonitrile and the                  nickel-n-hexane solution prepared to the light                  yellow transparent solution and continuing to                  stir at 60 degrees C for 12 hours to obtaindrak                  light yellow transparent solution; and (c) adding                  darkened solution into the syringe, setting the                  voltage to 16kV, and collecting it with the                  aluminum foil of the electrospinning machine,                  vacuum drying collected at 60 degrees C for 12                  hours to obtain the mesopores containing nickel                  particles and cerium dioxide in the shape of                  nanofibers. Preferred Components: In the step                  (i), the weight ratio of the polyacrylonitrile                  and the salt precursor of cerium is 3:1.

A top unit of the lift main body is provided with a connecting port that is hermetically and rotatably connected with a liquid storage box (4). The liquid storage box is fixedly connected with a top wall of the lift main body. An outlet unit of a branch pipe is hermetically connected with a one-way valve. An end of an air suction pipe (12) at a top unit of the liquid storage box is connected with a one-way valve.
   USE - Power-off carbon dioxide circulation absorption system for construction site lift.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes an effect of absorbing the carbon dioxide concentration in the lift through the radiating energy of the human itself. The system improves the environment quality, and is convenient to wait for rescue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a power-off carbon dioxide circulation absorbing system for construction site lift.
   Lift main body (1)
   Lift door (2)
   Sliding rail (3)
   Liquid storage box (4)
   Air suction pipe (12)

PT P
PN CN211632827-U
TI Domestic air bubble water machine with adjustable air outlet quantity, has limiting rod whose detecting end orderly passes through side wall of power box and side wall of limiting pipe and is set in limiting pipe.
AU CHEN J
AE SUZHOU YIKADI SPORTS EQUIP CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020A07357
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a household air bubble water machine with adjustable air outlet quantity, comprising a bubble water machine, a first sleeve, a clapboard, a second sleeve, a protective tube, a U-shaped tube, a gas flow meter, an inner threaded pipe, a limiting ring, a limiting sleeve, a limiting pipe, a power box, an electric push rod, a first push block, a spring telescopic rod, a second pushing block and a limiting rod. The gas flow meter of the utility model can effectively detect the gas output of the carbon dioxide, and by controlling the control switch of the gas bubble water machine to adjust and control the gas amount of carbon dioxide, so as to adjust the carbon dioxide content in the bubble water, it is different from the disadvantage that the traditional domestic bubble water machine cannot or inject the carbon dioxide content in the water; the two electric push rods respectively drive the two limiting rods to move, so as to effectively prevent the water bottle from falling caused by the water bottle is not installed tightly when processing; the device has simple structure, convenient use and is suitable for popularization and use.
DC X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC X27-B09

PT P
PN US2020264149-A1; US11287409-B2
TI Infrared (IR) imaging system for detecting carbon            dioxide gas, has optical filter that is configured to            selectively transfer light within band of IR            wavelengths to optical detector array and selectively            pass light provided with wavelength.
AU KESTER R T
   HAGEN N A
AE REBELLION PHOTONICS INC (REBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020798519
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has an optical filter that                selectively transfers light within a band of IR                wavelengths to an optical detector array. A                convolution of the optical filter with an                absorption spectrum of carbon dioxide gas is                greater than a convolution of the optical filter                with an absorption spectrum of hydrogen sulfide, or                methane, or sulfur dioxide or water within the band                of IR wavelengths. The optical filter selectively                passes light that includes a wavelength in a range                of 1400 nm to 1500 nm while attenuating light at                wavelengths above 1500 nano meter (nm) and below                1400 nm. The convolution of the optical filter with                the absorption spectrum of carbon dioxide gas is in                a range of 50 to 10,000 times greater than the                convolution of the optical filter with the                absorption spectrum of hydrogen sulfide.
   USE - IR imaging system for detecting carbon dioxide                gas, and for imaging scene.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces noise from radiation in                spectral ranges to increase the accuracy of                detection. The signal to noise ratio provided by                cooled mid-wavelength IR (MWIR) focal plane array                (FPA) is increased.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                imaging system.
   IR imaging system (100)
   Object (110)
   Optical channel (120)
   Spectral filter (130)
   Processor (150)

PN KR2012659-B1
TI Removing astringency of Aronia by immersing Aronia in e.g. ethanol, dehydrating alcohol, immersing in water, loading air, increasing temperature of chamber, and removing carbon dioxide from desorption chamber.
AU JUN B S
AE JUN B S (JUNB-Individual)
GA 201990901R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing astringency of Aronia involves primary immersing Aronia (S10) in a primary immersion tank mixed with ethanol and demineralized water for 20-24 hours, dehydrating the alcohol (S20) by blowing air to Aronia, secondary immersing (S30) by dipping the dehydrated Aronia in a second immersion tank filled with deionized distilled water for 10-12 hours, loading air into the Aronia in the desorption chamber (S35), raising the supply temperature of carbon dioxide and lowering the pH in the degassing chamber, while maintaining the internal pressure at 0.7-0.9 kg/cm2, increasing the internal temperature of the desorption chamber (S50) to 60-65 degrees C for 40-48 hours, and removing the carbon dioxide from the desorption chamber (S60), by maintaining the internal temperature at 30-35 degrees C for 20-24 hours, where the ethanol and deionized water are mixed in the primary immersion tank in a volume ratio of 1:1 at 60-65 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for removing astringency of Aronia.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flowchart describing removal of astringency of Aronia. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Primary immersing of Aronia (S10)
   Dehydrating alcohol (S20)
   Secondary immersing (S30)
   Loading air into Aronia (S35)
   Increasing internal temperature of desorption chamber (S50)
   Removing carbon dioxide from desorption chamber (S60)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The desorption is carried out by filling deionized distilled water in the secondary immersion tank at 60-65 degrees C.

AE UNIV JILIN ZHUHAI COLLEGE (UYJI-Non-standard)
GA 201819223H
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting andrographolide in andrographis tablet comprises e.g. (i) taking andrographolide tablet, crushing, placing in enzymolysis tank, adding composite enzyme, vortex mixing for 1-5 minutes, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 30-50 degrees C for 1-2 hours, filtering to obtain enzymatic solution, (ii) rapidly freezing enzyme solution, thawing, aging starch in extract, collecting supernatant, centrifuging remaining part, combining with supernatant, spray drying to obtain powder, and (iii) subjecting resulting powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction and then extracting.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting andrographolide in andrographis tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The andrographolide has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting andrographolide in andrographis tablet comprises (i) taking andrographolide tablet, crushing, placing in enzymolysis tank, adding 1-2 wt.% composite enzyme, vortex mixing for 1-5 minutes, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 30-50 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and filtering to obtain enzymatic solution, (ii) rapidly freezing enzyme solution, naturally thawing, aging starch in extract, collecting supernatant, centrifuging remaining part, collecting centrifuged liquid and combining with supernatant, and spray drying to obtain powder, and (iii) subjecting resulting powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, extracting at 40-50 degrees C for 3-5 hours, where fluid extraction pressure is 20-30 MPa, and carbon dioxide fluid flow is 750-850 L/hour, then collecting andrographolide to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The composite enzyme is formed by mixing acidic cellulase and acidic xylanase. The method comprises (i) taking andrographolide tablet, crushing, placing in enzymolysis tank, adding 1-2 wt.% composite enzyme, vortex mixing for 3 minutes, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 35 degrees C for 1.5 hours, and filtering to obtain enzymatic solution, (ii) rapidly freezing enzyme solution, naturally thawing, aging starch in extract, collecting supernatant, centrifuging remaining part, collecting centrifuged liquid and combining with supernatant, and spray drying to obtain powder, and (iii) subjecting resulting powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, extracting at 45 degrees C for 4 hours, where fluid extraction pressure is 25 MPa, and carbon dioxide fluid flow is 800 L/hour, then collecting andrographolide to obtain product. Preferred Components: The mass ratio between acid cellulase and acidic xylanase in composite enzyme is 2:3. The particle size of andrographolide tablet after grinding is 40-60 mesh.

PT P
PN WO2018044263-A1; CA3035169-A1; CA3035169-C
TI Recovering hydrocarbons with only 3 multistage columns, involves receiving carbon dioxide recycle stream, separating carbon dioxide recycle stream in 1st multistage column, and separating light hydrocarbon stream in 2nd multistage column.
AU PRIM E
AE PILOT ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (PILO-Non-standard)
GA 201819584J
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbons are recovered with only 3 multistage columns, which involves receiving carbon dioxide recycle stream (110); separating carbon dioxide recycle stream in 1st multistage column to produce purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (114) and light hydrocarbon stream (116); separating light hydrocarbon stream in 2nd multistage column to produce 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (122) and natural gas liquids stream (124); and separating natural gas liquids stream in 3rd multistage column to produce C3 hydrocarbons product stream (128) and 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream (130).
   USE - The method, set of process equipment, or system is useful for recovering hydrocarbons with only three multistage columns (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve (e.g. reduce) the minimum miscibility pressure of the carbon dioxide recycle stream by removing contaminants from the carbon dioxide recycle stream. The set of process equipment that only has three columns (e.g. three multistage distillation columns) does not use any cryogenic conditions, membranes, carbon dioxide recovery solvents, or amine recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrocarbons are recovered with only 3 multistage columns, which involves receiving a carbon dioxide recycle stream (110), where the carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide and 1-8C hydrocarbons; separating the carbon dioxide recycle stream in a 1st multistage column to produce a purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (114) and a light hydrocarbon stream (116), where the purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 1-4C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of 5-8C hydrocarbons, and where the light hydrocarbon stream comprises carbon dioxide, 3-8C hydrocarbons, trace amounts of C2 hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons; separating the light hydrocarbon stream in a 2nd multistage column to produce a 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (122) and a natural gas liquids stream (124), where the 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 2-3C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons and 4-8C hydrocarbons, and where the natural gas liquids stream comprises 3-8C hydrocarbons and trace amounts of carbon dioxide and 1-2C hydrocarbons; and separating the natural gas liquids stream in a third multistage column to produce a C3 hydrocarbons product stream (128) and 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream (130). The C3 hydrocarbons product stream comprises C3 hydrocarbons, some C4 hydrocarbons, trace amounts of carbon dioxide and C2 hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons and 5-8C hydrocarbons. The 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream comprises 4-8C hydrocarbons and trace amounts of C3 hydrocarbons. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a set of process equipment for recovering hydrocarbons with only three columns, comprising a 1st column (112) that receives a carbon dioxide recycle stream and separates the carbon dioxide recycle stream into a purified carbon dioxide recycle stream and a light hydrocarbon stream, where the purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 1-4C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of 5-8C hydrocarbons, and where the light hydrocarbon stream comprises carbon dioxide, 3-8C hydrocarbons, trace amounts of C2 hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons; a 2nd column (120) that receives the light hydrocarbon stream and separates the light hydrocarbon stream into a 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream and a natural gas liquids stream, where the 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 2-3C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons and 4-8C hydrocarbons, and where the natural gas liquids stream comprises 3-8C hydrocarbons and trace amounts of carbon dioxide and 1-2C hydrocarbons; and a 3rd column (126) that receives the natural gas liquids stream and separates the natural gas liquids stream into a C3 hydrocarbons product stream and a 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream, where the C3 hydrocarbons product stream comprises C3 hydrocarbons, some C4 hydrocarbons, trace amounts of carbon dioxide and C2 hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of C1 hydrocarbons and 5-8C hydrocarbons, where the 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream comprises 4-8C hydrocarbons and trace amounts of C3 hydrocarbons, and where the 1st column, the 2nd column, and third column are the only three multistage columns in the set of process equipment; and
   (2) a system (100) for recovering hydrocarbons with only 3 distillation columns, comprising unit for receiving a carbon dioxide recycle stream; unit for cooling the carbon dioxide recycle stream through a 1st heat exchanger to produce a cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream; unit for feeding the cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream to a 1st distillation column; unit for separating the cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream in the 1st distillation column to produce a 1st overhead stream and a 1st bottoms stream; unit for heating the 1st overhead stream in the 1st heat exchanger to produce a purified carbon dioxide recycle stream; unit for cooling the 1st bottoms stream through an air cooler to produce a cooled 1st bottoms stream; unit for expanding the cooled 1st bottoms stream through an expander to produce an expanded cooled 1st bottoms stream; unit for feeding the expanded cooled 1st bottoms stream to a 2nd distillation column; unit for separating the expanded cooled 1st bottoms stream in the 2nd distillation column to produce a 2nd overhead stream and a 2nd bottoms stream; unit for compressing the 2nd overhead stream to produce a 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream; unit for combining the 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream with the carbon dioxide recycle stream to produce a combined carbon dioxide recycle stream; unit for injecting the combined carbon dioxide recycle stream into an injection well; unit for cooling the 2nd bottoms stream to produce a natural gas liquids stream; unit for feeding the natural gas liquids stream to a third distillation column; and unit for separating the natural gas liquids stream in the third distillation column to produce C3 hydrocarbon product stream and a 4-8C hydrocarbon product stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of system for recovering hydrocarbons using three columns.
   Carbon dioxide recycle stream (110)
   Purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (114)
   Light hydrocarbon stream (116)
   Second purified carbon dioxide recycle stream (122)
   Natural gas liquids stream (124)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: The carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises hydrogen sulfide, where the method involves sacrificial hydrogen sulfide recovery to remove the hydrogen sulfide, and where the sacrificial hydrogen sulfide recovery involves using iron sponge, sulfanol, or iron chelate processing. The 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream is used as fuel to provide energy to reboilers of the 1st multistage column, the 2nd multistage column, and the third multistage column. The method further involves mixing 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream with a heavy hydrocarbon stream (108) to produce a blended heavy hydrocarbon stream, where the blended heavy hydrocarbon stream meets energy requirements and vapor pressure requirements for transportation by truck or pipeline. The method does not comprise any amine recovery. It further involves: (a) combining the purified carbon dioxide recycle stream with the 2nd purified recycle stream to produce a combined stream; and injecting the combined stream into an injection well in an enhanced recovery process; (b) recovering C3 hydrocarbons product stream as saleable product, where the saleable product meets energy requirements and vapor pressure requirements for transportation by truck or pipeline; (c) recovering 4-8C hydrocarbon product stream as saleable product, where the saleable product meets energy requirements and vapor pressure requirements for transportation by truck or pipeline; and (d) receiving an inlet stream (104), where the inlet stream comprises carbon dioxide, 1-8C hydrocarbons, and C9+ hydrocarbons; separating the inlet stream in a separator (106) to produce the carbon dioxide recycle stream and a heavy hydrocarbons stream, where the heavy hydrocarbons stream comprises C9+ hydrocarbons; and blending the natural gas liquids stream with the heavy hydrocarbons stream. The 1st bottoms stream comprises 9-11 mol.% carbon dioxide, 89-90 mol.% 3-8C hydrocarbons, 0-1 mol.% C2 hydrocarbons, and about 0 mol.% C1 hydrocarbons and nitrogen. Preferred Composition: The C3 hydrocarbons product stream recovers 10-20 mol.% C3 hydrocarbons from the carbon dioxide recycle stream, and the 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream recovers 80-90 mol.% 4-8C hydrocarbons from the carbon dioxide recycle stream. The carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises 0-2 mol.% nitrogen, 93-96 mol.% carbon dioxide, and 3-5 mol.% 1-8C hydrocarbons. The purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises 0-2 mol.% nitrogen, 94-97 mol.% carbon dioxide, 2-4 mol.% 1-4C hydrocarbons, and 0 mol.% 5-8C hydrocarbons. Light hydrocarbon stream comprises 9-11 mol.% carbon dioxide, 89-90 mol.% 3-8C hydrocarbons, 0-1 mol.% C2 hydrocarbons, and 0 mol.% C1 hydrocarbons and nitrogen. The 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises 95-98 mol.% carbon dioxide, 2-5 mol.% 2-3C hydrocarbons, and 0 mol.% nitrogen, C1 hydrocarbons, and 4-8C hydrocarbons. The natural gas liquids stream comprises 99-100 mol.% 3-8C hydrocarbons, 0-0.5 mol.% carbon dioxide, 0-0.5 mol.% C2 hydrocarbons, and about 0 mol.% of nitrogen and C1 hydrocarbons. The C3 hydrocarbons product stream comprises 94-96 mol.% C3 hydrocarbons, 3-5 mol.% C4 hydrocarbons, 0-0.5 mol.% carbon dioxide, 0-0.5 mol.% C2 hydrocarbons, and about 0 mol.% C1 hydrocarbons and 5-8C hydrocarbons. The 4-8C hydrocarbons product stream comprises 99-100 mol.% 4-8C hydrocarbons, 0-1 mol.% C3 hydrocarbons, and 0 mol.% carbon dioxide and 1-2C hydrocarbons. Preferred Conditions: The 1st multistage column, the 2nd multistage column, and the 3rd multistage column are distillation columns and are the only three multistage columns used in recovering the hydrocarbons, where the 1st multistage column operates at a pressure of 400-600 pounds per a square inch absolute (psia), where the 2nd multistage column operates at a pressure of 200-400 psia, and where the 3rd multistage column operates at a pressure of 200-400 psia. The purified carbon dioxide recycle stream and the 2nd purified carbon dioxide recycle stream have lower minimum miscibility pressures than the carbon dioxide recycle stream. No cryogenic conditions, membranes, and carbon dioxide recovery solvents are used in the method.

TI Apparatus for production of liquid extract from            plant biomass, comprises carbon dioxide chiller that            chills carbon dioxide, that exits chiller as liquid            carbon dioxide, and extraction chamber established            downflow of carbon dioxide chiller, for containing the            plant biomass for extraction.
AU RAMSEY J
   ROSS D
AE ESSENTIAL EXTRACTION CORP (ESSE-Non-standard)
GA 2022E0544A
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a carbon dioxide chiller                (20) that chills carbon dioxide, that exits the                chiller as liquid carbon dioxide, and an extraction                chamber (8) established downflow of the carbon                dioxide chiller, for containing the plant biomass                for extraction. The liquid carbon dioxide contacts                the plant biomass to generate a liquid carbon                dioxide-liquid extract mixture. An extract                collection chamber (10) is established downflow of                the extraction chamber. The heater heats the liquid                carbon dioxide-liquid extract mixture in the                extract collection chamber to convert at least some                of the liquid carbon dioxide of the liquid carbon                dioxide-liquid extract mixture in the extract                collection chamber to gaseous carbon dioxide. The                fluid conveyance componentry (15) fluidically                couples the carbon dioxide chiller, the extraction                chamber and the extract collection chamber to                define a closed loop. The apparatus produces                extract from plant biomass via passive, pump-free,                closed loop processing.
   USE - Apparatus for production of liquid extract                from plant biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus helps to avoid saturation of the                extractant, achive gentler production process, to                achieve higher quality, more refined product than                known apparatus using significantly higher                temperatures and/or pressures.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an                apparatus for production of liquid extract from                plant biomass with front door open.
   8Extraction chamber
   10Extract collection chamber
   12Extract collection chamber inlet flow                valve
   15Fluid conveyance componentry
   20Carbon dioxide chiller

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2020047243-A1; AU2019330991-A1; CA3108943-A1; KR2021044865-A; CN112839728-A; EP3843876-A1; US2021262320-A1; JP2022502236-W; EP3843876-A4
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide from gaseous source of            carbon dioxide, by contacting aqueous ammonia capture            liquid with gaseous source to produce carbon dioxide            sequestering carbonate and aqueous ammonium salt, and            combining salt with geomass.
AU NGUYEN Q
   CONSTANTZ B R
   SCHNEIDER J
   KANG S
   KANG S H
AE BLUE PLANET LTD (BLUE-Non-standard)
   NGUYEN Q (NGUY-Individual)
   BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
   BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 202020045Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method of sequestering carbon dioxide from a                gaseous source of carbon dioxide, involves (a)                contacting an aqueous ammonia capture liquid with a                gaseous source of carbon dioxide under conditions                sufficient to produce a carbon dioxide sequestering                carbonate and an aqueous ammonium salt, and (b)                combining the aqueous ammonium salt with a geomass                to produce a regenerated aqueous ammonia capture                liquid to sequester carbon dioxide from the gaseous                source of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for sequestering carbon                dioxide from a gaseous source of carbon dioxide                comprising flue gas (claimed). The prepared                sequestering carbonate is used as aggregates of                internal curing concretes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces large volume of carbonate                products. The system has continuous reactors. The                method improves heating efficiency of the solution                and dissolution efficiency of the material. The                method reduces the need of heat source such as heat                from steam or heat from electrical power. The                carbonate aggregate improves performance of                concrete by increasing autogenous curing and                reducing chemical shrinkage, leading to reduced                cracking of the concrete body through the slow and                uniform release of water throughout the placed                concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for sequestering carbon dioxide from a gaseous                source of carbon dioxide, comprising (A) a carbon                dioxide gas/aqueous capture liquid module, and (B)                an aqueous capture liquid regeneration module                coupled to a carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate                production module.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The system further                  comprises a carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate                  production module operatively coupled to a carbon                  dioxide gas/aqueous capture liquid module. The                  system does not include a carbon dioxide                  sequestering carbonate production module separate                  from the carbon dioxide gas/aqueous capture                  liquid module.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  contacting the aqueous ammonia capture liquid                  with the gaseous source of carbon dioxide under                  conditions sufficient to produce an aqueous                  ammonium carbonate and then contacting the                  aqueous ammonium carbonate with a cation source                  to produce the carbon dioxide sequestering                  carbonate. The step (a) comprises contacting the                  aqueous ammonia capture liquid that includes a                  cation source with the gaseous source of carbon                  dioxide under conditions sufficient to produce                  the carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate and the                  aqueous ammonium salt. The production of the                  carbon dioxide sequestering carbonate produces                  carbon dioxide gas. The produced carbon dioxide                  gas is recovered by contacting with aqueous                  capture ammonia liquid. The method further                  comprises (c) purifying the regenerated aqueous                  ammonia, or does not comprise purification of the                  regenerated aqueous ammonia. The step (c)                  comprises stripping aqueous capture ammonia from                  the regenerated aqueous ammonia. The method                  produces a carbon dioxide sequestering aggregate.                  Preferred Components: The geomass comprises                  construction and demolition waste, coal                  combustion products and alkaline waste                  products.

TI Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide used for producing methane product stream, involves contacting gas stream with agent comprising formate solution to form methane product stream and waste stream.
AU WHITEMAN P A
   FEKETE D S
AE INTELLIGAS RENEWABLE FUELS LTD (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 201834726D
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream (100) comprising methane and carbon dioxide involves contacting the gas stream with an agent essentially comprising a formate salt solution to form a methane product stream (200) having a lower concentration of carbon dioxide than in the gas stream, and a waste stream containing the agent and carbon dioxide dissolved in the waste stream, and isolating the methane product stream from waste stream.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide used for producing methane product stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and energy-saving removal of carbon dioxide from gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide with high carbon dioxide removal rate with reduced usage amount of formate agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide, which comprises a separation chamber (30) having an inlet for receiving the gas stream, and adapted for contacting the gas stream with the agent. The separation chamber comprises an outlet (o1) for isolating methane product stream from the waste stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide.
   Apparatus (10)
   Pump (20)
   Recirculation loop (23)
   Separation chamber (30)
   Regeneration chamber (50)
   Desorption element (52)
   Compressor unit (70)
   Gas stream (100)
   Methane product stream (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises a regeneration chamber (50), a recirculation loop (23) in fluid communication with the separation chamber, and having a pump to facilitate recirculating the waste stream so as to be reused in contacting with the gas stream, a compressor unit (70) in fluid communication with the separation chamber for compressing the gas stream prior to being received by the separation chamber, a pump (20) for recycling the agent regenerated in the regeneration chamber back into the separation chamber. The regeneration loop includes a regeneration chamber adapted to regenerate the agent in the waste stream so as to remove at least a portion of dissolved carbon dioxide from the waste stream. The regeneration chamber further comprises an outlet (o2) for isolating carbon dioxide removed from the waste stream. The separation chamber is adapted to withstand pressure of 0.01-450 bar, preferably 1-50 bar or 50-450 bar, and is operated at pressure of 1-250 bar, preferably 20-100 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves compressing the gas stream prior to the contacting step, recirculating the waste stream so as to be reused in the contacting step (a), and regenerating the agent in the waste stream so as to remove the dissolved carbon dioxide at 1-70 bar and 10-30 degrees C, and/or contacting the waste stream with a desorption element (52), and recycling the regenerated agent from regenerating step into the contacting step. Preferred Condition: The contacting step is carried out at -40-50 degrees C and 50-450 bar. Preferred Composition: The formate salt solution is a saturated or near-saturated salt solution containing potassium formate, sodium formate, calcium formate, lithium formate, beryllium formate, magnesium formate, ammonium formate, nickel formate and/or cesium formate, preferably potassium formate. The gas stream comprises gas derived from a natural source, preferably comprises a natural gas and/or a biogas.

Preferred Properties: The particle size of the nano-silica is 5-100 nm. The lamellar degree of the wollastonite nanoparticles with a lamellar structure is 0.5-5 nm. AU PARK S W
   BAEK S W
   PARK S
   BAEK S
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202264738K
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of aerogel blanket containing organic solvent involves (S10) introducing a wet gel blanket into a supercritical extraction device, and drying the wet gel blanket using supercritical carbon dioxide, (S20) reducing the pressure of the supercritical extraction device, (S30) supplying gaseous carbon dioxide to the depressurized supercritical extraction device, and (S40) drying the wet gel blanket.
   USE - Manufacture of aerogel blanket containing organic solvent used in transparent insulation material, high-temperature insulation material, ultra-low dielectric thin film for high-integration device, catalyst and catalyst carrier, electrode for supercapacitor, and electrode material for seawater desalination.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved productivity and reduced residual organic solvent and overall drying time, and prevents deterioration of physical properties. The method provides blanket with low cost and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The wet gel blanket prepared in the step (S10) is silica wet gel blanket. Preferred Process: The method further involves (S1) preparing a precursor composition containing a silica precursor and organic solvent, (S2) preparing a catalyst composition containing organic solvent, a catalyst and a surface modifier, (S3) preparing the wet gel blanket by introducing the precursor composition and the catalyst composition into an impregnation tank, (S4) passing a substrate for the blanket through the tank, and (S5) adding surface modification solution containing the surface modifier to the wet gel blanket and (S6) aging. In step (S3), the precursor composition and catalyst composition are impregnated and gelled in the base material. In step (S20), the blank is dried and extracted the organic solvent at the same time, and recycled to the step (S10). In step (S50), the pressure of the extraction device is reduced to atmospheric pressure. Preferred Properties: In step (S30), the pressure of the supercritical extraction device is 10-70 bar. In step (S40), the gaseous carbon dioxide is supplied at pressure of 10-70 bar, and temperature of 60 degrees C or more. The recovery rate of the organic solvent is 98 wt.% or more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic solvent is alcohol.

GA 202223767P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vanadium pentoxide by using calcium vanadate involves subjecting slurry containing vanadium acid calcium and ammonia and carbon dioxide to carbon ammonium reaction, heating and dissolving vanadium, separating solid and liquid, obtaining ammonium metavanadate solution, the solid-liquid separation further obtains calcium carbonate, the carbon dioxide obtained by calcining calcium carbonate returns to the ammonium bicarbonate reaction, crystallizing the ammonium metavanadate solution to obtain ammonium metavanadate crystal and ammonium metavanadate crystal mother liquor, calcining the ammonium metavanadate crystal to obtain the vanadium pentoxide and ammonia, returning the ammonia to the ammonium carbonate reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing vanadium pentoxide by using calcium vanadate used in steel, chemical catalysis and energy storage, aerospace and other fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, simple and environment-friendly, and provides high conversion rate of the vanadium element.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium vanadate is calcium orthovanadate. Preferred Conditions: The slurrying liquid containing calcium vanadate is obtained by slurrying the calcium vanadate and the liquid phase medium. The liquid phase medium in the slurry liquid comprises water and/or recycled ammonium metavanadate crystal mother liquid, the mol ratio of calcium vanadate to liquid phase medium is 1:5-1:20. The temperature of the pulpifying is 15-40degrees Celsius. The temperature of the carbon ammonium reaction is 15-40degrees Celsius, the temperature of the carbonatation reaction is consistent with the temperature of the slurrying and the carbon ammoniation reaction time is 10-120 minutes. The mol ratio of the ammonia in the ammonia to the vanadium element in the slurrying liquid containing the calcium vanadate is 1-1.2. The mol ratio of the carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide to the calcium element in the slurrying liquid containing the calcium vanadate is 1-1.2. The pressure of the carbonation reaction is normal pressure. The temperature of the carbon ammonium reaction after heating and dissolving vanadium is 70-90degrees Celsius and the carbon ammonium reaction after heating and dissolving vanadium time is 15-30 minutes. The calcium carbonate is calcined to obtain calcium oxide. The ammonium metavanadate crystallization mother liquid returns to the pulping step as liquid phase medium. The calcium vanadate and liquid phase medium at 15-40degrees Celsius for pulping to obtain the slurry containing calcium vanadate, the slurry containing calcium vanadate and ammonia and carbon dioxide for 15-40degrees Celsius, 10-20 minutes of carbon ammonium reaction, carbon ammonium reaction, heating to 70-90degrees Celsius for 15-30 minutes of vanadium-dissolving reaction, solid-liquid separation to obtain ammonium metavanadate solution and calcium carbonate, the liquid phase medium comprises water and/or recycled ammonium metavanadate crystal mother liquid, returning the carbon dioxide obtained by calcining the calcium carbonate to the carbon ammonium reaction, crystallizing the ammonium metavanadate solution to obtain the ammonium metavanadate crystal and ammonium metavanadate crystal mother liquor, calcining the ammonium metavanadate crystal to obtain the vanadium pentoxide and ammonia, returning the ammonia to the ammonium bicarbonate to react, the molar ratio of the amount of the ammonia to the vanadium element in the slurrying liquid containing the calcium vanadate and the nitrogen element in the ammonia is 1-1.2, the dosage of the carbon dioxide is the mol ratio of the C element in the carbon dioxide and the calcium element in the slurrying liquid containing the calcium vanadate is 1-1.2.

TI Preserving aroma of fermentable beverage such as            wine or beer, involves fermenting beverage in            fermentation container to produce headspace fluid            mixture comprising carbon dioxide gas and aromatic            fluid in headspace.
AU JONES R L
AE AROMALOC INC (AROM-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8029S
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving aroma of fermentable beverage                involves fermenting the beverage in a fermentation                container to produce a headspace fluid mixture                comprising carbon dioxide gas and an aromatic fluid                in a headspace located above the beverage contained                in the fermentation container. An aromatic fluid                concentration is increased within the headspace                fluid mixture by removing a portion of the carbon                dioxide from the headspace fluid mixture, where the                headspace fluid mixture is gaseous or vapor and                interacts with the beverage. The increased aromatic                fluid concentration within the headspace fluid                mixture acts to slow the loss of aromatic fluid                from the fermenting beverage.
   USE - Method for preserving aroma of fermentable                beverage such as wine or beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method desirably affect the smell and/or                taste of wine and enjoyment associated with                drinking of fermented wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The step of removing                  involves pumping the headspace fluid mixture                  through a flow passage. The portion of the carbon                  dioxide is removed by exiting through a release                  valve. The method particularly involves                  fermenting the beverage in a fermentation                  container to produce a headspace fluid mixture                  comprising carbon dioxide gas and an aromatic                  fluid in a headspace located above the beverage                  contained in the fermentation container; and the                  headspace fluid mixture exiting out of the                  container into a flow passage; producing a                  modified fluid having a higher aromatic fluid                  concentration than the headspace fluid mixture by                  removing a portion of the carbon dioxide of the                  headspace fluid mixture that is in the flow                  passage; and returning the modified fluid from                  the flow passage to the container, and modified                  fluid mixing with the headspace fluid mixture to                  increase the aromatic fluid concentration within                  the headspace fluid mixture, where the headspace                  fluid mixture interacts with the beverage, and                  the increased aromatic fluid concentration within                  the headspace fluid mixture acts to slow the loss                  of aromatic fluid from the fermenting beverage.                  The portion of the carbon dioxide is allowed to                  exit through a release valve. The headspace fluid                  mixture is allowed to exit out of the container                  into the flow passage occurs during an entire                  duration of fermentation. The headspace fluid                  mixture is allowed to exit out of the container                  into the flow passage occurs during less than an                  entire duration of fermentation. Preferred                  Process: The method further involves using                  headspace fluid mixture interacting with a                  membrane; pumping the headspace fluid mixture                  through the flow passage, and headspace fluid                  mixture in the flow passage interacting with a                  membrane.

PT P
PN WO2021186459-A1; IN202011011197-A; CN114787114-A
TI Synthesis of acetic acid by hydrocarboxylation by            reacting methanol with carbon dioxide in presence of            carbon dioxide-philic solvent and photocatalyst and            irradiating reaction mixture under visible illumination            to obtain the acetic acid.
AU JAIN S L
   SAINI S
   KHATRI P K
   GHOSH I K
   RAY A
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2021B59878
AB    NOVELTY - Acetic acid is synthesized by reacting                methanol with carbon dioxide (CO2) in the presence                of a CO2-philic solvent and a photocatalyst to                obtain a reaction mixture and irradiating the                reaction mixture under visible illumination to                obtain the acetic acid.
   USE - An improved hydrocarboxylation process for                synthesis of acetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction occurs under ambient temperature                and pressure condition using a readily available                household LED lamp in the presence of a transition                metal based molecular photocatalyst, without adding                any external electron and proton donors. The method                is performed under mild conditions, and the                catalyst is low cost and contains non-precious                transition metals, such as nickel, cobalt, or                copper, more specifically cobalt and nickel. Yield                of acetic acid is 30-80%, and selectivity is                95-99%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Photocatalyst is                  immobilized on a photoactive semiconductor                  support selected from the group consisting of                  N-doped carbon, graphite carbon nitride,                  naturally occurring allotrope of sulfur (S8), and                  metal oxides of titanium, nickel, cobalt, iron,                  zinc, and bismuth preferably NiO and TiO2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: CO2-philic solvent                  is selected from N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF),                  N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMA), N-methylpyrrolidone                  (NMP), dimethyl imidazoline (DMI), water,                  tetrahydrofuran (THF), and hexane. Photocatalyst                  is a macrocyclic complex of transition metal                  selected from ruthenium, cobalt, nickel, and                  copper with a ligand selected from porphyrin,                  polypyridine, and phthalocyanine. Preferred                  Process: Photocatalyst is reused. Preferred                  Conditions: Methanol and CO2-philic solvent is in                  a ratio of 5:1-1:1, and ratio of methanol and CO2                  is 2:1. Reaction is carried out at 15-35 degrees                  C with a pressure of 1-2 bar. Visible                  illumination is performed with LED light of 15-30                  W having wavelength of 400-800 nm for 10-30                  hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

AU LAM L J
   YOO C
   PARK J Y
   RIM J W
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 2020A0100R
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst electrode comprises a base material made of a first metal, and a metal layer made of a second metal formed on the surface of the base material. The metal layer is patterned so that the base material is exposed so that the length of the interface formed between the metal layer and the base material is more than a predetermined value.
   USE - The catalyst electrode is useful for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst electrode remarkably improves reduction efficiency of reducing carbon monoxide from carbon dioxide compared to conventional catalyst electrode. The method efficiently and economically produces catalyst electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing catalyst electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing catalyst electrode (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base material and metal layer are made of iron, silver, gold, copper, chromium, tungsten, aluminum, molybdenum, zinc, nickel, platinum, cobalt, indium, manganese, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tin, lead, vanadium, ruthenium, iridium, zirconium, rhodium and/or magnesium. The base material is made of copper. The metal layer is made of tin. The metal layer is formed by electron beam deposition method, sputter deposition method, thermal deposition method, chemical vapor deposition method, and hydrothermal synthesis method.

PN EP3594289-A1; WO2020011983-A1; CA3101422-A1; CN112272688-A; BR112020023937-A2; IN202017056157-A; KR2021032996-A; US2021115261-A1; EP3820945-A1; JP2021531361-W; CN112272688-B
TI Surface-reacted calcium carbonate for stabilizing            mint oil comprises reaction product of natural ground            calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate            with carbon dioxide and hydronium ion donors.
AU SHARMA L
   BUDDE T
   BADER T
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202006483F
AB    NOVELTY - A surface-reacted calcium carbonate having a                volume median particle size (d50) of 0.1-90 mm and                in which mint oil is adsorbed onto and/or absorbed                into the surface of surface-reacted calcium                carbonate, comprises reaction product of natural                ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium                carbonate with carbon dioxide and greater than or                equal to 1 hydronium (H3O+) ion donors, where the                carbon dioxide is formed in situ by H3O+ ion donors                treatment and/or is supplied from external                source.
   USE - A surface-reacted calcium carbonate for                stabilizing mint oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The surface-reacted calcium carbonate for                stabilizing mint oil is easily available, non-toxic                and can be used in a great variety of applications.                Stabilizing the mint oil is improved if the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate is in form of a                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has a volume median particle                  size (d50) of 0.5-50 mu m, preferably 1.5-15 mu                  m. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a                  specific surface area of 15-200 m2/g, preferably                  25-160 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method. Preferred                  Components: The natural ground calcium carbonate                  is selected from marble, chalk, and/or limestone,                  or the precipitated calcium carbonate is selected                  from precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal                  form. The hydronium (H3O+) ion donor is selected                  from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous                  acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid,                  an acidic salt, acetic acid, and/or formic acid,                  preferably greater than or equal to 1 of                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, dihydrogen                  phosphate (H2PO4-), being partially neutralized                  by a cation selected from lithium ion (Li+),                  sodium ion (Na+) and/or potassium ion (K+),                  hydrogen phosphate (HPO42-), being partially                  neutralized by a cation selected from Li+, Na+,                  K+, magnesium ion (Mg2+), and/or calcium ion                  (Ca2+), more preferably phosphoric acid. The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate is in form of a                  powder. Surface-reacted calcium carbonate is in                  form of granules, preferably granules having a                  volume median particle size of 0.1-6 mm,                  preferably 1-2 mm determined by sieve                  fractioning.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Mint oil comprises                  greater than or equal to 2 compounds selected                  from menthol, isomenthone, menthone, limonene,                  menthylacetate, beta -pinene, alpha -pinene,                  alpha -terpineol, isopulegol, terpinen-4-ol,                  neoisopulegol, pulegone, piperitone and                  caryophyllene. Mint oil is selected from mint                  oil, spearmint oil, and/or peppermint oil.                  Compound of the mint oil is stabilized against                  chemical conversion. Compound is selected from                  beta -pinene, alpha -pinene, limonene,                  isopulegol, neoisopulegol, and/or pulegone.                  Preferred Process: The stabilization of mint oil                  relates to a recovery rate of greater than or                  equal to 96 wt.%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 99.5 wt.% within 93 days, preferably 30                  days as determined by gas chromatography mass                  spectrometry (GC-MS) after storing the                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate at room                  temperature. Stabilization of mint oil relates to                  a recovery rate of greater than or equal to 90                  wt.%, preferably greater than or equal to 96 wt.%                  within 93 days as determined by GC-MS after                  storing the surface-reacted calcium carbonate at                  40 plus minus 2 degrees C. Stabilization of mint                  oil relates to a recovery rate of greater than or                  equal to 94 wt.%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 98 wt.% within 30 days as determined by                  GC-MS after storing the surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate at 40 plus minus 2 degrees C.

PN CN110314542-A
TI Carbon dioxide photo catalytic reduction type photo-catalysis reaction system, has photo-catalysis reaction kettle provided with light generating device, and controller for controlling light source illumination intensity.
AU HOU X
   REN J
   LI F
   MA C
   ZHANG X
   FENG H
   LIU G
   SHEN H
   REN G
   LIU Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ELECTRIC POWER (UYSI-C)
GA 2019873874
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a combined gas cylinder provided with a gas bottle for performing photo-catalytic reduction of each component of a CO2 product. The gas bottle is connected with a gas blender by a pressure reducing valve and a flow meter. The pressure reducing valve and the flow meter are connected with a controller. The controller collects and processes gas components. A photo-catalysis reaction kettle is connected with the gas blender. The photo-catalysis reaction kettle is provided with a light generating device. The controller controls light source illumination intensity generated by the light generating device.
   USE - Carbon dioxide photo catalytic reduction type photo-catalysis reaction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system occupies small volume and realizes accurate adjustment of the parameters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide photo catalytic reduction type photo-catalysis reaction system.

PT P
PN CN109096099-A; CN109096099-B
TI Preparation of 3,5-di-tert-butylphenol-4-hydroxybenzoic acid by dividing to reflux reaction 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol, potassium hydroxide aqueous solution and toluene, introducing carbon dioxide, and Kolbe-electrolyzing-Schmidt reacting.
AU QIAN C
   PAN Q
   WANG K
   WU C
   ZHANG X
AE XIAOXIAN XINXIU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (XIAO-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201903464L
AB    NOVELTY - A 3,5-di-tert-butylphenol-4-hydroxybenzoic acid is prepared by adding 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol, potassium hydroxide aqueous solution and toluene to reaction kettle under nitrogen protection, dividing to reflux reaction for 0.5-5 hours, introducing carbon dioxide into reaction kettle to pressure of 0.1-3.0 MPa, Kolbe-electrolyzing-Schmidt reacting at 80-160 degrees C for 5-15 hours, cooling to room temperature, releasing carbon dioxide in reaction kettle, adding distilled water to dissolve product in reaction kettle, standing, and acidifying water phase by diluted acid to pH of 10.1 degrees C to obtain precipitate.
   USE - Method for preparing 3,5-di-tert-butylphenol-4-hydroxybenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short reaction time and high conversion rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium hydroxide aqueous solution is 20-60 wt.% potassium hydroxide aqueous solution. The diluted acid is 8-12 vol.% dilute sulfuric acid, dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute nitric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves filtering, washing, drying, and decoloring. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of toluene and 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol is 2-20:1.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E10-C03; E11-F02B

PN CN108977243-A
TI Preparation of liquefied natural gas involves removing inorganic sulfur from gas-buffered blue carbon tail gas by wet desulfurization, purifying, carrying out methanation, absorbing carbon dioxide, drying, and subjecting to liquefaction.
AU ZHANG B
   CHEN S
   FENG J
   ZHANG X
   ZHENG J
   MEI L
   MENG J
   HU W
AE CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)
GA 2018A2598Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of liquefied natural gas involves removing inorganic sulfur from gas-buffered blue carbon tail gas to less than 30 mg/Nm3 by wet desulfurization, subjecting to low-temperature water washing treatment, pressurizing to 2-2.5 MPaG, purifying, adjusting heat exchange temperature to adjust hydrogen/carbon ratio, carrying out methanation reaction under the action of catalyst, obtaining synthesis gas, absorbing carbon dioxide in synthesis gas by 3,4-methylenedioxy-N-ethylamphetamine (MDEA) solution, MDEA mixed solution or complex amine solution, reducing content of carbon dioxide to less than 50 ppmv, drying to moisture content of less than 1 ppmv, subjecting to liquefaction in cold box, and storing.
   USE - Preparation of liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, easy and efficient preparation of liquefied natural gas having high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The liquefied natural gas is prepared using cryogenically liquefied mixed refrigerant comprising nitrogen, methane, ethylene, propane or isopentane.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

GA 201890066V
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cooling tower for cooling oxygen-enriched combustion flue gas. A desulphurization and de-nitration device is connected with the cooling tower for removing sulfur dioxide and nitric oxide in the oxygen-enriched combustion flue gas. A pressure cooling device is connected with the desulphurization and de-nitration device for pressurizing and cooling the oxygen-enriched combustion flue gas. The desulphurization and de-nitration device comprises an electrolytic water tank and a high-pressure bubbling absorption tower. The high-pressure bubbling absorption tower removes nitric oxide in the oxygen-enriched combustion flue gas.
