PT P
PN CN108480656-A; CN108480656-B; US2019284663-A1; JP2019157265-A; JP6749983-B2; US11111561-B2
TI Preparing thickness-controllable bismuth nano sheet comprises taking bismuth salt as raw material, and ethylene glycol ether as a solvent and carrying out aqueous solution reduction.
AU XU W
   YANG F
   RUAN M
   ZHANG Y
   SONG P
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
   CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
   CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 201870732F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thickness-controllable bismuth nano sheet comprises taking bismuth salt as raw material, and ethylene glycol ether as a solvent and carrying out aqueous solution reduction.
   USE - The nano sheet is useful for electro catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares sheet which exhibits excellent catalytic reduction performance of carbon dioxide at an overpotential of 330 mV, where Faraday efficiency of catalytic formation of formic acid by carbon dioxide reaches to 98%, the initial overpotential is as low as 80 mV, and the stability is as long as 75 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing thickness controlled bismuth nano sheet alloy, comprising reducing the salt of the bismuth, salt compound of palladium, nickel, zinc, gold or copper as raw material and taking ethylene glycol diethyl ether as the solvent and carrying out aqueous solution reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of preparing thickness-controllable bismuth nano sheet preferably comprises adding bismuth salts into ethylene glycol ether, stirring mixture evenly until the solution becomes transparent under the protection of an inert gas, reacting by stirring at 25-120 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, cooling to normal temperature, adding reducing solution drop wise under inert gas atmosphere, reacting by stirring for 15-30 minutes, carrying out ultra sonication, filtering, washing using ethanol and water collecting and drying. The bismuth nano-sheet is loaded onto the carbon-based support, where support is GO oxidation, reduction GO, BP or XC-72. The method of preparing thickness controlled bismuth nano sheet alloy preferably comprises dissolving salt compound of palladium, nickel, zinc, gold or copper in ethylene glycol ether, carrying out ultrasonic stirring, reacting by stirring at 25-120 degrees C for 30-60 minutes under the protection of an inert gas, cooling to room temperature, adding the salt compound of bismuth, mixing and stirring uniformly, adding reducing solution drop wise in an inert gas atmosphere, reacting further stirring for 15-30 minutes, finishing reaction, filtering, washing using ethanol and water, collecting, drying, placing sample in a tube furnace, introducing hydrogen and calcining at 300-600 degrees C for 1-3 hours. Preferred Composition: The amount using bismuth salts is 0.5-5 mmol, and ethylene glycol ether is 200-300 ml and bismuth salt is bismuth chloride or cerium nitrate, and the reducing liquid used for the reduction is sodium borohydride or lithium borohydride, where amount is 20-40 mmol. The thickness of the bismuth nano sheet is 0.7-50 nm. The amount of the salt compound of palladium, nickel, zinc, gold or copper is 0.5-5mmol.
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)
MC M22-H01; M22-H02; P53-C01; P53-T20; P53-V04A; P53-V04F; P53-V06C; P53-V07; P53-V09

TI Preparation of calcium magnesium carbonate,            involves calcining medium-low grade phosphate rock,            mixing with ammonium nitrate solution or ammonium            chloride solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas and            ammonia gas, carbonizing, precipitating.
AU ZHANG Q
   ZAHNG F
   DENG Y
   CEN Z
   ZHANG J
   LU Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG L
   HAN Y
AE GUIZHOU SHENGYUAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU RES INST CHEM IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201863792J
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of calcium magnesium                carbonate, involves (1) calcining medium-low grade                phosphate rock to obtain pyrolysis product and                carbon dioxide gas, (2) mixing obtained pyrolysis                product with ammonium nitrate solution or ammonium                chloride solution to obtain calcined medium and low                grade phosphate rock ammonium salt leaching                solution and ammonia gas containing calcium                magnesium salt, (3) introducing carbon dioxide gas                and ammonia gas into calcined medium-grade                low-grade phosphate ore ammonium salt leaching                solution, carbonizing the calcined medium-grade                low-grade phosphate ore ammonium salt leaching                solution and precipitating calcium magnesium                carbonate.
   USE - Preparation of calcium carbonate                magnesium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of calcium                magnesium carbonate ensuring reduced particle size,                improved productivity and reduced exhaust emission                by simple, environmentally-friendly and economical                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (2), when the                  pyrolysis product is mixed with ammonium nitrate                  solution or ammonium chloride solution,                  controlling mixing temperature at 25-95 degrees                  C, collecting ammonia and adjusting pH of                  leachate to 6.5-9.5. In step (3), the reaction                  temperature is controlled at 25-80 degrees C and                  pH value is adjusted to 7.5-9.0. The step (4)                  involves filtering the mixture obtained in step                  (3), separating precipitate of calcium magnesium                  carbonate, the filtrate is ammonium nitrate                  solution, returning the filtrate to leaching                  system for recycling, washing calcium carbonate                  magnesium precipitate and filtering.

PT P
PN KR2022090746-A
TI Preparing bischlorosulfonylimide useful as ionic            liquid comprises heating chlorosulfuric acid and            bischlorosulfonylimide, drop-wisely adding unreacted            mixed solution into heated chlorosulfuric acid and            distilling.
AU LEE D H
   MOON C S
   BOKYUNG K
   RA S
AE JOONGWON NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (JOON-Non-standard)
   CHUN BO LTD (CHUN-Non-standard)
GA 202286466X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide comprises:                (i) preparing an unreacted mixed solution by mixing                urea and chlorosulfuric acid; (ii) heating                chlorosulfuric acid and/or                bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide; (iii) drop-wisely adding                the unreacted mixed solution into the heated                chlorosulfuric acid and/or bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide                and reacting; and (iv) distilling the reaction                solution under reduced pressure.
   USE - The bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide is useful as                ionic liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and suppress the                rapid generation of carbon dioxide gas, violent                exothermic reaction and prevents the formation of                byproducts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), mixing                  process is carried out at 0-30 degrees C. In the                  step (ii), the heating process is carried out at                  90-130 degrees C by adding                  bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide into the chlorosulfuric                  acid in prior. In the step (iii), the reaction                  temperature is 100-140 degrees C. In the step                  (iv), the distillation is carried out at 90-180                  degrees C with a pressure of 10-50 Torr.                  Preferred Components: In the step (i), the amount                  of chlorosulfuric acid is 2-10 equivalents based                  on the urea. In the step (ii), the amount of                  chlorosulfuric acid and/or                  bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide is 1-10 equivalents                  based on the urea.
   EXAMPLE - 388 g chlorosulfuric acid was added into 1 l                reaction vessel equipped with a stirrer and                thermometer, then cooled to less than or equal to                20 degrees C. 100 g urea was added to dissolve. 98                g chlorosulfuric acid was added into 3 l reaction                vessel equipped with a stirrer, a thermometer and a                reflux tube, then heated at 110 degrees C. Urea                dissolved in chlorosulfuric acid was slowly added                dropwise into the heated reaction vessel so as not                to exceed 120 degrees C and the reaction                temperature was maintained at 110-115 degrees C and                stirred. The terminated reaction solution was                distilled at 110-120 degrees C and 25-30 Torr to                obtain 169 g bis(chlorosulfonyl)imide.

PT P
PN KR2021094304-A; KR2309122-B1
TI Titanium porphyrin aerogel for photocatalyst comprises multiple of micro, meso and macro pores having hierarchically porosity and amorphous structure.
AU PARKJINHEE
   KOUM Y S
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 202189833V
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium porphyrin aerogel comprises a multiple of micro, meso and macro pores having hierarchically porosity and amorphous structure.
   USE - The titanium porphyrin aerogel is useful for photocatalyst (claimed) and gas adsorption, separation, and sensing.
   ADVANTAGE - The titanium porphyrin aerogel exhibits excellent photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing aerogel comprising preparing titanium porphyrin gels (TPGs) by mixing and heating titanium-oxo cluster and tetrakis (4-carboxyphenyl) porphyrin (TCP) in a solvent; and preparing titanium porphyrin aerogels (TPAs) by performing carbon-dioxide supercritical point drying (CPD) on the Ti-porphyrin gel prepared above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aerogel has an average diameter (average width) of the pores (pores) is 18-528 ~A, and has a BET specific surface area of 400-700 m2/g. The aerogel is titanium-oxo cluster and tetrakis (4-carboxyphenyl) porphyrin (TCPP) by the reaction with at least one ligands present in the titanium-oxo cluster are substituted with TCPP to form a solid state in coordination with titanium. The titanium-oxo cluster is a Ti6O6(OiPr)6(AB)6 cluster (OiPr of isopropoxide/AB=4-aminobenzoate), Ti8O8(t-bbz)16 cluster (bbz is butylbenzoate) ate), Ti8O8(OH)2(AB)14 cluster (AB is 4-aminobenzoate) or (Ti8O8(AB)12)(acetonitrile)0.5 cluster (AB is 4-aminobenzoate), preferably Ti6O6(OiPr)6(AB)6 cluster, Ti8O8(t-bbz)16 cluster or Ti8O8(OH)2(AB)14 cluster. Preferred Method: The titanium-oxo cluster and TCPP are mixed in a molar ratio of 5-1:1. The concentration of the solution in which the titanium-oxo cluster and TCPP are dissolved is 1-2 wt.% when the titanium-oxo cluster and tetrakis (4-carboxyphenyl) porphyrin are mixed in a solvent. The heating time in the step of preparing the porphyrin gel is 2-4 days and heating temperature is 110-130 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - 20 mg Ti6O6(OiPr)6(AB)6 cluster (or the same molar amount of Ti8O8(t-bbz)16, Ti8O8(OH)2(AB)), (Ti8O8(AB)12)(acetonitrile)0.5 or Ti(OiPr)4 cluster and 10 mg of TCPP each were completely dissolved in 1 ml N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF). The dissolved solutions were mixed in a 4 ml vial and heated to 120 degrees C for 3 days. After heating for 3 days was completed, when the temperature was lowered to room temperature, dark red titanium porphyrin gels (TPGs) were obtained.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN KR2020020475-A; KR2111465-B1
TI Electrode catalyst used for generating oxygen in e.g. electrolysis of water, comprises nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide.
AU KANG J K
   MOON B C
   CHOI W
   HAN K J G
   PARK D K
   JAE-WON C
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202018522Q
AB    NOVELTY - An electrode catalyst comprises nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide.
   USE - Electrode catalyst is used for generating oxygen in electrolysis of water and electrochemical reduction reaction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode catalyst has rapid charge transfer efficiency in oxygen-generation reaction, excellent solid stability, excellent catalytic activity, stable cycle performance and long-term durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of electrode catalyst, which involves injecting a precursor solution comprising a salt of nickel, a salt of iron, and a salt of molybdenum into a high-pressure reactor to perform a hydrothermal reaction to form nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide grown directly on a substrate electrode which is a porous substrate electrode comprising a metal foam or a mesh. The catalyst further comprises a nanoparticle domain of metallic nickel having a diameter of 2-8 nm, formed on a surface of the nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide. The atomic ratio of nickel, iron, molybdenum and oxide in nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide is 2-4:1:3-5:20. Molybdenum has an oxidation number of 6+ and 4+. Preferred Process: Production of electrode catalyst further involves plasma-treating the nickel-iron-molybdenum oxyhydroxide using plasma of nitrogen, hydrogen, or a mixed gas of nitrogen and hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor solution further comprises a linear or branched 1-10C alkylamine as a structure regulator.

AU JI X
   LIAO C
   XU H
   HAN J
   WANG J
   YANG C
AE UNIV YUNNAN NORMAL (UNYN-C)
GA 201968133Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method for degradation of organophosphate as photocatalyst involves mixing three dimensional nested barium tungstate powder with organophosphorus wastewater in stainless steel reactor, stirring, irradiating with neon light source, and explaining organic phosphorus to convert it into carbon dioxide, water and other inorganic substances.
   USE - Method for degradation of organophosphate as photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high degradation efficiency, is degraded on a large scale, does not cause secondary pollution, and has great significance in field of organic phosphorus degradation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic product is inorganic salt containing phosphate ion (PO43-), which is present in the wastewater in form of ions. Preferred Composition: The amount of barium tungstate nano-material added to the organic phosphorus wastewater is 1 g/L. Preferred properties: The diameter of three dimensional nested barium tungstate nano-material is 0.8-1.4 um and the specific surface area is 24.68 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organophosphorus wastewater has an active ingredient of phosphorothioate used to simulate organophosphorus wastewater. Preferred Composition: The purity of organophosphorus wastewater is greater than 96%. The organophosphorus wastewater is mixed with dichlorvos, monocrotophos, parathion, and phorate in a volume of 1:1:1:1.

PN KR2019060378-A; KR2463133-B1
TI Cosmetic composition used for improving skin            conditions e.g. skin moisturizing, skin irritation and            skin inflammation, comprises urea, triacetin,            alpha-hydroxy acid and alkali metal salt.
AU KIM S J
   JANG D H
   SHIN H Y
   PARK S I
   PAKSUNGIL
AE AMOREPACIFIC CORP (AMOR-C)
GA 201950745H
AB    NOVELTY - Cosmetic composition comprises urea,                triacetin, alpha -hydroxy acid and alkali metal                salt, where composition comprises urea and                triacetin in weight ratio of 4-12.5:0.3-4.5.
   USE - The composition is useful for improving skin                condition, preferably skin moisturizing, skin                irritation and skin inflammation (all claimed). No                biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition reduces skin irritation and                has excellent skin condition improving effect,                inhibits production of carbon dioxide and ammonia                gas, and maintains pH for long period of time due                to urea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 4-12.5 wt.% urea, 0.3-4.5 wt.%                  triacetin, 0.1-19 wt.% alpha -hydroxy acid and                  1.5-3.9 wt.% alkali metal salt. The composition                  comprises alpha -hydroxy acid and alkali metal                  salt in weight ratio of 0.1-1.9:1.5-3.9, and urea                  and triacetin in weight ratio of 8.8-10.0:2-4.                  Preferred Components: The composition has pH                  of4-6.5. The alpha -hydroxy acid includes lactic                  acid, glycolic acid, or mixture of lactic acid                  and glycolic acid. The alkali metal salt includes                  sodium lactate, sodium-glycolate, or mixture of                  sodium lactate and sodium-glycolate. The                  composition reduces increase in pH and gas                  generation due to urea.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of 9 wt.% urea,                3 wt.% triacetin, 0.5 wt.% lactic acid, 2.5 wt.%                sodium-lactate, 15 wt.% polyol, 8 wt.% surfactants                and waxes, 0.05 wt.% disodium iodide, 1 wt.%                incrementer, 10 wt.% oil, 0.2 wt.% antiseptic, 0.3                wt.% odorant, and balance amount of purified                water.

PT P
PN CN109054007-A
TI New bio-based furan dicarboxylic acid polyester compounds useful as barrier film in barrier bottle.
AU WANG J
   LIU X
   ZHU J
   ZHANG C
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 201900898R
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-based furan dicarboxylic acid polyester compounds (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful as barrier film in barrier bottle (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) have excellent oxygen and carbon dioxide barrier properties, which solves problem of poor oxygen barrier property of traditional polyethylene terephthalate polyester, polyethylene, polypropylene and other food packaging film, solves problem of short shelf life of food, and solves problem of utilizes preservative.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bio-based furan dicarboxylic acid polyester compounds of formula (I) are new.
   n = 30-300;
   R1 = H or 1-6C alkyl; and
   R2 = 1-6C alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polycondensation catalyst is antimony catalyst, germanium catalyst, tin catalyst, preferably antimony trioxide, ethylene glycol antimony, antimony acetate and/or dibutyltin oxide; the stabilizer is phosphoric acid, phosphorous acid, hypophosphorous acid, pyrophosphoric acid, ammonium phosphate, trimethyl phosphate, dimethyl phosphate, triphenyl phosphate, diphenyl phosphate, triphenyl phosphate, diphenyl phosphate, triphenyl phosphite, diphenyl phosphite, ammonium phosphite and/or ammonium dihydrogen phosphate; and the antioxidant is phenolic antioxidant, preferably pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) (antioxidant-1010), n-octadecyl- beta -(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert -butyl-phenyl)-propionate (antioxidant-1076) and/or tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite (antioxidant-168).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) mixing diol and dibasic acid with esterification catalyst, and then carrying out esterification reaction to obtain first intermediate product, (ii) carrying out pre-condensation of the first intermediate product in presence of polycondensation catalyst, stabilizer and antioxidant to obtain second intermediate product, and (iii) continuously reacting the second intermediate product under vacuum condition to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The molar ratio of diol, dibasic acid to esterification catalyst is 1:1.1-2:0.0005-0.005, and the trinsic viscosity of (I) is more than 0.6 dL/g. The esterification catalyst is anhydrous zinc acetate, anhydrous cobalt acetate, manganese acetate and/or dibutyl tin oxide, the dibasic acid is furan dicarboxylic and/or furan dicarboxylic esters, and the diol is 2-methyl-1,3-propanediol, 2,2-dimethyl-1,3-propanediol, 2-methyl-2-ethyl-1,3-propanediol 2-methyl-2-propyl-1,3-propanediol and 2-ethyl-2-pentyl-1,3-propanediol, and/or 2-ethyl-2-hexyl-1,3-propanediol. In step (ii), the molar amount of the first intermediate product polycondensation catalyst is 0.05-0.5% of the molar amount of dibasic acid, the molar amount of stabilizer is 0.05-0.5% of the molar amount of dibasic acid, and the antioxidant is 0.05-0.5% of the molar amount of diacid acid. Preferred Method: In step (i), the esterification reaction temperature is 160-200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours. In step (ii), the pre-condensation reaction is carried out under vacuum, where the vacuum degree is less than 2000 Pa, the pre-condensation reaction temperature is 180-240 degrees C and the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours; in step (iii), the vacuum degree is 5-200 Pa, and the reaction time is 0.5-10 hours.

PT P
PN CN108793246-A
TI Improved method of preparing zirconium            tetrachloride comprises e.g. taking zircon sand            contains metal oxide impurities to remove metal oxide            impurities in zircon sand to obtain purified zircon            sand and heating purified zircon sand.
AU WU Z
   FAN X
   YIN B
   HUANG B
   LIU X
   SUN Y
   HE L
AE XINTE ENERGY CO LTD (XINT-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG JINGSHUO ADVANCED MATERIALS CO (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201892752D
AB    NOVELTY - Improved method of preparing zirconium                tetrachloride comprises (1) zircon sand contains                metal oxide impurities to remove metal oxide                impurities in zircon sand to obtain purified zircon                sand, (2) heating purified zircon sand with                carbonaceous reducing agent and chlorine gas to                form zirconium tetrachloride, silicon                tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for improved method of                preparing zirconium tetrachloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                zirconium tetrachloride which is prepared by above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The metal oxide impurities                  comprise an oxide of aluminum or an oxide of                  iron. The specific method for removing metal                  oxide impurities in the zircon sand comprises                  mixing zircon sand is mixed with the reaction                  gas, the reaction gas is chlorine gas and                  hydrogen chloride, and the metal oxide impurities                  in the zircon sand react with the reaction gas to                  form a gaseous metal chloride to obtain purified                  zircon sand. In the step (1) heating temperature                  is lower than the heating temperature of the                  zircon sand purified in step (2) to form                  zirconium tetrachloride. The heating temperature                  in the step (1) is 300-500 degrees C. The metal                  oxide impurity in the zircon sand in the step (1)                  reacts with the reaction gas to form a gaseous                  metal chloride, to obtain first syngas, the first                  syngas comprising a gaseous metal chloride, the                  method further comprising the step of: passing                  the first syngas into the lye for absorption. The                  specific method for removing metal oxide                  impurities in the zircon sand in the step (1)                  comprises the zircon sand is acid-dissolved, and                  the soluble metal oxide impurities in the zircon                  sand react with the acid to form metal ions into                  the solution to obtain purified zircon sand. In                  the step (1), the zircon sand is acid-decomposed,                  and the soluble metal oxide impurities in the                  zircon sand react with the acid to form a metal,                  the ions enter the solution to obtain an acid                  hydrolysis solution, and the acid hydrolysis                  solution includes a metal chloride. The method                  further comprises introducing the acid hydrolysis                  solution into the alkali solution for absorption.                  In the step (2) heating at 1100-1250 degrees C                  and the pressure is 10-100 KPa. In the step (2),                  the reducing agent and the chlorine gas are                  heated to form zirconium tetrachloride, silicon                  tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide, and a second synthesis gas is further                  obtained, and the second synthesis gas comprises                  zirconium tetrachloride, silicon tetrachloride,                  carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and also                  includes the following steps by cooling the                  second synthesis gas to precipitate a solid of                  zirconium tetrachloride to obtain a third                  synthesis gas, cooling to precipitate solid                  impurities and liquid silicon tetrachloride. The                  specific method for cooling the second syngas to                  cool and precipitate the zirconium tetrachloride                  solid to obtain the third syngas includes                  carrying out heat-exchanging second syngas                  through the first heat exchanger, cooling second                  syngas to 450-500 degrees C, and cooling by                  liquid silicon tetrachloride, and rinsing                  temperature is 200-300 degrees C, and cooling                  precipitate, the zirconium chloride is solid to                  obtain a third synthesis gas. The specific method                  for cooling the third syngas to precipitate solid                  impurities and liquid silicon tetrachloride                  comprises carrying out heat-exchange through the                  second heat exchanger using third syngas, cooling                  third syngas to 120-150 degrees C, and cooling by                  liquid silicon tetrachloride, and rinsing                  temperature is 25-60 degrees C, and cooling                  precipitated solid impurities and liquid silicon                  tetrachloride. The method further comprises                  cooling the leaching tail gas to obtain liquid                  silicon tetrachloride, and cooling the leaching                  tail gas to obtain a liquid tetrachloride. The                  silicon is used as an eluent to rinse the second                  synthesis gas and/or the third synthesis tail                  gas.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PT P
PN CN107971046-A; CN107971046-B
TI Rapidly removing surface carbon from alkane dehydrogenation catalyst comprises carrying out online analysis of carbon oxide and oxygen concentrations in burnt tail gas.
AU JIN L
   ZHANG Y
   DU Z
   WANG G
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201835204H
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly removing surface carbon from alkane dehydrogenation catalyst comprises carrying out online analysis of carbon oxide and oxygen concentrations in burnt tail gas, where the content of air in charred atmosphere in the reactor is adjusted, and the temperature in the reactor is adjusted, and carbon oxide is carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly removing surface carbon from alkane dehydrogenation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the time required for scorching during the regeneration of the catalyst, and product has excellent stability and repeatability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The time required for scorching process is within 2 hours, preferably within 1 hour. The detection probe of infrared online analyzer is set at the gas outlet of the reactor. The online analysis is infrared online analysis using infrared online analyzer. During scorching process, the temperature of catalyst bed in the reactor furnace tube is controlled between 480 and 600 degrees C. The scorch parameters of the reaction furnace are adjusted during scorching process: (a) mass percentage concentration of carbon oxides in the burned tail gas is less than 1%, and when it continues to decrease, the proportion of air is increased and the proportion of nitrogen is reduced, and (b) mass percentage concentration of carbon oxides in the burnt tail gas is less than 1%, and when the concentration of carbon monoxide and the concentration of carbon dioxide in the burnt tail gas are stable, then ratio between air and nitrogen should be maintained and the temperature of the furnace tube should be gradually increased. Preferred Components: The concentration of oxygen in tail gas during scorching is 0-20%; adjustment is based on the concentration of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, where total mass percentage concentration of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide does not exceed 5%.

PN CN107647046-A
TI Preparing mulberry tea, involves spreading fresh mulberry leaves, air-conditioning by performing vacuum treatment, anaerobic treatment, or anaerobic or aerobic alternating treatment, microwave fixing, and drying to obtain product.
AU WANG B
   LIU J
   ZHANG X
   LIU Q
   HE J
   ZHOU C
   CHEN Z
   WANG J
AE HANGZHOU TEA RESEARCH INST CHINA SUPPLY (HANG-Non-standard)
   YANTAI NORTH TEA PROMOTION CENT (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 201811604D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing mulberry tea, involves spreading fresh mulberry leaves, performing air-conditioning treatment on the leaves by performing vacuum treatment, anaerobic treatment, or anaerobic/aerobic alternating treatment, microwave fixing, and drying to obtain product, where the anaerobic gas used during air-conditioning treatment is chosen from carbon dioxide (CO2) and nitrogen (N2).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mulberry tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares mulberry tea with improved content of gamma aminobutyric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The vacuum treatment is performed for 1-6 hours. The anaerobic air-conditioning treatment is performed by evacuating, then filling anaerobic gas and processing for 1-6 hours, where the anaerobic gas comprises 72 vol.% carbon dioxide and 28 vol.% nitrogen. The anaerobic/aerobic alternating treatment is performed by filling vacuum with the anaerobic gas for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours, introducing oxygen into the anaerobic gas for 0.5-1.5 hours, preferably 1 hour and repeating for 1-3 times. The vacuum degree of vacuum treatment is less than 100 Pa. The method involves performing air-conditioning treatment at 25-30 degrees C, fixing with microwave power of 0.2-0.4 kW for 1-3 minutes, and drying at 95-115 degrees C for 1-3 hours.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN WO2020157151-A1; EP3918111-A1; US2022106692-A1
TI Electrode used in electrochemical cell, comprises metallic nanostructure, and hydrophobic layer of compounds containing thiol-containing compound and polysiloxane compounds, and has hydrophobicity as determined by preset contact angle.
AU FONTECAVE M
   MOUGEL V
   WAKERLEY D
   LAMAISON S
AE PARIS SCI & LETTRES QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
GA 202075177U
AB    NOVELTY - An electrode comprises a metallic nanostructure comprising metal chosen from copper, zinc, nickel, iron and/or silver, and is a portion of metallic hierarchical structure containing both micro and nanostructuration, preferably comprising dendritic hierarchical structures comprising metals, and hydrophobic layer of compounds partially or totally covering the surface of hierarchical structure and are chemisorbed to surface. At least 0-50% of surface of hierarchical structure is devoid of hydrophobic layer. The hierarchical structure is porous. The compounds are chosen from a thiol-containing compound (I), an acetylacetone group comprising structure (II), sulfur-containing compound (III), and polysiloxane compounds chosen from the 1-100C polyalkylsiloxane or polyarylsiloxane. The electrode has hydrophobicity as determined by contact angle measurement of 130-175 degrees , and an electrochemically active surface area less than 10% of the geometric surface area of hierarchical structure.
   USE - Electrode used in electrochemical cell for converting carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide into hydrocarbon(s) and/or alcohol(s), and for reducing carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide in aqueous medium (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode reduces carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide into hydrocarbon(s) and/or alcohol(s), and reduces carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide without concomitant proton reduction to hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrode comprises a metallic nanostructure comprising metal chosen from copper, zinc, nickel, iron and/or silver, and is a portion of metallic hierarchical structure containing both micro and nanostructuration, preferably comprising dendritic hierarchical structures comprising metals, and a hydrophobic layer of compounds partially or totally covering the surface of metallic hierarchical structure. The compounds are chemisorbed to surface. At least 0-50% of surface of metallic hierarchical structure is devoid of hydrophobic layer. The metallic hierarchical structure is porous. The compounds are chosen from a thiol-containing compound of formula: R-A (I), an acetylacetone group comprising structure of formula (II), sulfur-containing compound of formula: R-A-A-R (III), and polysiloxane compounds chosen from the 1-100C polyalkylsiloxane or polyarylsiloxane. The polyalkylsiloxane and polyarylsiloxane is substituted by amines, -OR1, -CO2R2 and -CX3. The electrode has hydrophobicity as determined by contact angle measurement of 130-175 degrees , and an electrochemically active surface area less than 10% of the geometric surface area of metallic hierarchical structure.
   A = -SH, -P(O)(OH)2, -CO2H, -SeH, -TeH, -Si(OH)3, -SiX3, Se or S;
   R = 2-100C linear or branched alkyl, 2-100C linear or branched alkenyl, 2-100C linear or branched alkynyl, 2-100C linear or branched heteroalkyl, 2-100C linear or branched heteroalkenyl, 2-100C linear or branched heteroalkynyl substituted by amines, -OR1, halo, preferably fluoro, chloro or bromo, aryl, preferably pyridyl or imidazoyl, -CO2R2 and -CX3;
   R1, R2 = H or 1-20C alkyl, preferably 1-5C alkyl;and
   X = halo, preferably fluoro, chloro or bromo.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of an electrode, for reducing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide into hydrocarbon(s) and/or alcohol(s) in aqueous medium. The hydrocarbon is ethylene. The alcohol is ethanol and/or propanol;
   (2) preparation of the electrode, which involves preparing electrode comprising metallic hierarchical structure containing both micro and nanostructuration, coating of the surface of metallic hierarchical structure with compounds to obtain a metallic hierarchical structure coated with a hydrophobic layer of compounds, and optionally washing metallic hierarchical structure coated with a hydrophobic layer of compounds to obtain a hydrophobic monolayer (L1) of compounds;
   (3) reduction of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide into hydrocarbon(s) and/or alcohol(s) by an electrolysis reaction, which involves placing an electrode with an anode in an electrolyte solution, providing an external source of electricity to electrode, providing of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide gas to the electrolyte solution, and recovering hydrocarbon(s) and/or alcohol(s) formed during electrolysis;
   (4) reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide by an electrolysis reaction, which involves placing an electrode with an anode in an electrolyte solution, providing an external source of electricity to electrode, providing of carbon dioxide gas to the electrolyte solution, and recovering carbon monoxide formed during electrolysis; and
   (5) electrochemical cell, which comprises a container of an aqueous electrolyte solution, an electrode in contact with the electrolyte solution, an anode in contact with the electrolyte solution, unit for providing carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to the electrolyte solution, and unit for providing electricity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface of the metallic hierarchical structure is partially devoid of compound. The portions of the surface of the metallic hierarchical structure which are not covered by the compounds are regions of the structure which are portion of the electrochemically active surface area. At least 0-10% (excluding 0) of surface of metallic hierarchical structure is devoid of hydrophobic layer. The metal is chosen from copper, zinc, copper and/or silver, and zinc and/or silver. The electrode comprises gas bubbles trapped between the surface of metallic hierarchical structure and electrolyte solution. Preferred Properties: The hydrophobic layer covers at least 80% of surface of metallic hierarchical structure. The electrochemically active surface area is 0-1% (excluding 0) of the geometric surface area. The bubbles have a size of more than 300 mu m. The concomitant proton reduction to hydrogen is 20% of Faradaic efficiency.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound is chosen from thiol-containing compound (I), preferably 1-octadecanethiol or 1-dodecanethiol. Preferred Process: The preparation method involves contacting the surface of metallic hierarchical structure of electrode with a monomer and precursor of a polymer, polymerizing monomer on the surface of metallic hierarchical structure, creating a layer (L1), forming a multilayer of polymerized compounds, preferably polysiloxane compounds on layer (L1).

PT P
PN CN108771947-A; CN108771947-B
TI High-humidity flue gas deep waste heat recovery and carbon dioxide collecting integrated device comprises a dehumidification/depth heat recovery device and an adsorption device.
AU TAO L
   LIU X
   QU H
   XIA D
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2018900671
AB    NOVELTY - High-humidity flue gas deep waste heat recovery and carbon dioxide collecting integrated device comprises a dehumidification/depth heat recovery device and an adsorption device. The dehumidification/depth heat recovery device comprises a liquid storage tank (3), dehumidification heat exchange chamber and liquid collecting groove. A wet flue gas inlet (1) is connected to the dehumidification heat exchange chamber. The dehumidification heat exchange chamber is provided with a serpentine falling film tube (2) and flue gas passage. The liquid plate is provided with a liquid dispenser (4). The cooling water inlet, cooling water outlet, dehumidification solution inlet, dehumidification solution outlet, liquid outlet valve is provided with a collecting tank (5) on the outside of the solution regeneration device. The dehumidification and temperature-removed flue gas enters the double-bed adsorption device through the outlet. The double bed adsorption device is provided with adsorption bed A.
   USE - Used as high-humidity flue gas deep waste heat recovery and carbon dioxide collecting integrated device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has simple design, small floor area and high integration degree. The method realizes functions of flue gas dehumidification, waste heat recovery and deep purification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-humidity flue gas deep waste heat recovery and carbon dioxide collecting integrated device comprises a dehumidification/depth heat recovery device and an adsorption device. The dehumidification/depth heat recovery device comprises a liquid storage tank (3), dehumidification heat exchange chamber and liquid collecting groove. A wet flue gas inlet (1) is connected to the dehumidification heat exchange chamber. The dehumidification heat exchange chamber is provided with a serpentine falling film tube (2) and flue gas passage. The liquid plate is provided with a liquid dispenser (4). The cooling water inlet, cooling water outlet, dehumidification solution inlet, dehumidification solution outlet, liquid outlet valve is provided with a collecting tank (5) on the outside of the solution regeneration device. The dehumidification and temperature-removed flue gas enters the double-bed adsorption device through the outlet. The double bed adsorption device is provided with an adsorption bed A, adsorption bed B and buried tube. The gas storage tank further comprises air intake valve, air outlet valve, exhaust valve, water inlet valve and water outlet valve. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the method of using the integrated device, comprising providing dehumidification/heat recovery device before the adsorption device, where the dehumidification/heat recovery unit can simultaneously achieve flue gas dehumidification, waste heat recovery and deep purification functions, conveying the medium and low temperature wet saturated flue gas into the dehumidification heat exchange chamber, lowering the dehumidification solution in the liquid storage tank by the funnel-shaped liquid dispenser on the outer wall of the serpentine falling film tube to obtain liquid film, heating and transferring of the wet flue gas between the pipes and the liquid film, absorbing the wall film moisture from the flue gas completing the dehumidification process, providing the tube with cooling water and liquid film for indirect heat exchange and cooling the dehumidification solution to ensure the dehumidification effect, taking away the sensible heat of the flue gas and the latent heat of water vapor and realizing the waste heat recovery, during the dehumidification of the flue gas, separating some impurity dust from the flue gas, and flowing into the liquid collecting groove with the dilute solution for achieving the purpose of purifying the flue gas, conveying the dilute solution flowing out of the liquid collecting groove into the regeneration device and regenerating into a concentrated solution, and sending the concentrated solution to the liquid storage tank, conveying the medium temperature water flowing out of the serpentine falling film tube into the buried tube of the adsorption bed, providing energy for the desorption process and enhance the desorption of carbon dioxide and completing the heat exchange, flowing the water out of the buried pipe and conveying the collecting tank, the flue gas after the dehumidification purification and cooling entering into the adsorption device, recovering and collecting the carbon dioxide, improving the recovery rate of carbon dioxide, working the two adsorption beds of the adsorption device alternately, adjusting the valve until the adsorption and desorption of carbon dioxide carried out simultaneously in the two adsorption beds, and completing the capture of the carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the high-humidity flue gas deep waste heat recovery and carbon dioxide collecting integrated device.
   Wet flue gas inlet (1)
   Serpentine falling film tube (2)
   Liquid storage tank (3)
   Liquid dispenser (4)
   Collecting tank (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cooling water can be replaced with other gas and liquid cooling fluids. The dehumidification solution is a high-concentration low-temperature solution with strong hygroscopicity and contains more than one type of dehumidification medium. The temperature and concentration of the dehumidification solution are actual process requirements. The serpentine falling film tube has an outer diameter of 10-20 mm. The length of the tube is 0.3-0.5 m. The number, size and arrangement of the falling film tubes are set according to the actual flue gas treatment amount. The outer tube wall of the serpentine falling film tube is smooth, convex or concave or vertical corrugated. A multiple of concave liquid dispensers are disposed on the surface of the liquid plate. The liquid dispenser is in the shape of a funnel. The material of the liquid dispenser is the same as that of the liquid plate or flexible material. The angle between the upper part of the liquid distributor and the vertical direction is 30 degrees less than or equal to -( theta )- less than or equal to 60 degrees . The spacing between the lower part of the liquid distributor and the outer tube wall is less than 5 mm. The number of liquid dispensers, position, size matches the size and process requirements of the serpentine falling film tube and the liquid plate. The concentrated solution in the liquid storage tank must be kept at a certain height to ensure that the amount of the upper solution of the liquid dispenser is immersed in the minimum circumferential joint. The liquid collecting groove must maintain at specific height. The working condition height is 10-12 mm, which acts as liquid seal and prevents the flue gas from leaking.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids.    INST NISKICH TEMPERATUR I BADAN STRUKTUR (NISK-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol by the carbon dioxide                conversion route in a photocatalytic process,                comprises providing a base liquid in the form of                demineralized and carbon dioxide-saturated water to                a reaction tank (1), providing graphene material,                exposing the contents of the reaction tank to an                electromagnetic radiation beam with a wavelength in                the UV-visible (VIS)-far infrared (FIR) radiation                range that is generated by the emitter.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol by                the carbon dioxide conversion route in a                photocatalytic process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the emitter that operates                in a continuous or pulsed mode, thus emitting                electromagnetic waves with a wavelength of 400-1100                nm, preferably 650-1100 nm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                installation for producing methanol by the carbon                dioxide conversion route in the photocatalytic                process, comprising a reaction tank made of a                transparent material partially or completely                transmittable for the UV-VIS-FIR wavelength,                connected from the top to a base liquid tank (9)                provided with a programmable injection pump and                connected from the top to a vapor condenser                subsystem (13), where the vapor condenser subsystem                is connected in the upper part to a deaerator (14)                and in the lower part to an intermediate tank for                methanol, and further the intermediate tank through                a valve (16) is connected to the target tank for                methanol equipped with a programmable pump, the                reaction tank further contains a graphene                suspension and the reaction tank is connected from                the top by a carrier gas supply to a process                controller connected to the carrier gas                installation, in the part where the reaction tank                is made of transparent material partially or                completely transmittable for the UV-VIS-FIR                wavelengths, there is an optical system equipped                with a light sensor and connected by an optical                fiber to an electromagnetic radiation emitter,                where the reaction tank is embedded in the body by                means of a mounting and additionally in the lower                part of the reaction tank there is a temperature                sensor, and in the upper part of the reaction tank                there is a pressure sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a vertical cross-section of                the device for producing methanol in production                cycle.
   Reaction tank (1)
   Base liquid tank (9)
   Vapor condenser subsystem (13)
   Deaerator (14)
   Valve (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  carbon dioxide in the base liquid is 7 g/l. The                  graphene material is in the form of graphene                  oxide powder, porous graphene, graphene flakes,                  an aerogel or graphene dots of sizes of 0.1-100                  mu m. The concentration of the graphene material                  in the reaction tank is 0.1 mu g/1 g                  demineralized water. The light emitter can be a                  LED power matrix or a halogen lamp with a                  reflector.
   EXAMPLE - 0.1 mu g Graphene B in the form of fine                flakes, foams or an aerogel was placed in a                transparent capsule (or with a quartz window)                containing carbon dioxide-saturated water as the                base liquid A. Operating the light beam from the                laser C or led D source (or mixed) causes the                generation of methanol (1-18%) in water.                Concentration programming was done by exposure to                light or by the luminous flux intensity. The                capsule can then be subjected to a standard                distillation process in order to obtain                methanol.

PT P
PN CN113634245-A; CN113634245-B
TI Tin oxide nanomaterial doped with high-valent            metal ion used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon            dioxide or degradation of organic pollutant in water,            has high-valent metal ion is doped into portion            tinoxide crystal lattice.
AU LI Q
   GUAN H
   GAO S
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST JIAOTONG (UYSJ-C)
GA 2021D4115X
AB    NOVELTY - Tin oxide nanomaterial doped with high-valent                metal ion has high-valent metal ion is doped into                portion tinoxide crystal lattice, so that surface                of material has ability to activate carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Tin oxide nanomaterial doped with high-valent                metal ion used for photocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide or degradation of organic pollutant                in water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high-valent metal ion-doped tin oxide                nanomaterial has excellent photocatalytic                degradation performance, and the photocatalytic                reduction efficiency of carbon dioxide under light                condition is significantly better than that of the                un-doped tin oxide material, and it solves the                defect of poor photocatalytic performance of the                existing tin oxide nanomaterial, and can be                directly applied to the field of photocatalytic                carbon fixation and pollution degradation in the                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1)a                method for preparing tin oxide nanomaterial doped                with high-valent metal ion, which involves:                (a)preparing ammonium salt solution and mixed salt                solution respectively, where the mixed salt                solution is a solution containing high-valent metal                ion and tin ion, the concentration of ammonium                radical in the ammonium salt solution is 0.05-0.24                mol/L, and the total concentration of high-valent                metal ion and tin ion in the mixed salt solution is                0.01-0.8 mol/L;(b)dropping the ammonium salt                solution to the mixed salt solution under stirring                condition, and the reaction precursor is obtained                after sufficient stirring;(c)adjusting the obtained                reaction precursor to a suitable pH value, foaming                nitrogen for 30 minutes;(d)keeping at                140-230degrees Celsius for 20-48 hours to obtain a                suspension;(d)centrifuging the obtained                suspension;(e)washing the separated powder with                secondary deionized water 3-5 times;(f) drying the                washed powder at 50-80degrees Celsius for 8-24                hours to obtain the high-valent metal ion-doped tin                oxide nanomaterial; and
   (2)a application method of tin oxide                nanomaterial doped with high-valent metal ion,                which involves: (a)dispersing the tin oxide                nanomaterial doped with high-valent metal ion in an                aqueous solution containing carbon dioxide for 2-24                hours under light condition;(b)reducing carbon                dioxide to carbon-containing products such as                methanol, formaldehyde, formic acid, ethanol,                acetaldehyde and acetic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is alcohol                  and water. The alcohol is selected from ethanol,                  ethylene glycol and tert-butanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-valent metal                  ion is selected from niobium, tantalum, vanadium,                  tungsten and molybdenum element. The ammonium                  salt solution includes ammonium nitrate or                  ammonium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The                  volume ratio of the ammonium salt solution to the                  mixed salt solution is 1:2. The atomic percentage                  of high-valent metal ion in the nanomaterial is                  10-30%. The tinoxide nano material has a size of                  less than 1 micron and has a rutile phase                  structure.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN109135254-A
TI Preparation of composite foam tent material based on supercritical carbon dioxide flame retardant comprises e.g. mixing surface modified aluminum hydroxide with dried thermoplastic polyurethane elastomeric, flame retardant and synergist.
AU SHU L
AE ANHUI YUTANG RAIN GEAR CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201905601D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite foam tent material based on supercritical carbon dioxide flame retardant comprises e.g. mixing surface modified aluminum hydroxide with dried thermoplastic polyurethane elastomeric, flame retardant and synergist, adding hollow glass beads modified by coupling agent into a high-speed mixer, stirring, discharging, placing mixture into a mixing chamber of torque rheometer, carrying out melt blending, subjecting to underwater dicing to obtain blended flame-retardant particles, placing mixture into an electric heating constant temperature blast drying oven, placing blended flame-retardant particles in a non-woven fabric bundle, placing bag mouth in a high-pressure reaction kettle, discharging residual gas in kettle, turning on booster pump, opening preheater and refrigerating machine, introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction kettle, reaching preset temperature and pressure, closing all valves, maintaining pressure, and opening steam heating foaming device.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of composite foam tent material based on supercritical carbon dioxide flame retardant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of composite foam tent material based on supercritical carbon dioxide flame retardant comprises (i) mixing surface modified aluminum hydroxide with dried thermoplastic polyurethane elastomeric, flame retardant and synergist, adding the hollow glass beads modified by the coupling agent into a high-speed mixer, stirring for 10-15 minutes, discharging, placing mixture into a mixing chamber of a torque rheometer, carrying out melt blending at 130-140 degrees C for 5-7 minutes, subjecting to underwater dicing to obtain blended flame-retardant particles, placing the mixture into an electric heating constant temperature blast drying oven at 60-63 degrees C for 3-4 hours, (ii) placing blended flame-retardant particles into a non-woven fabric bundle, placing the bag mouth in a high-pressure reaction kettle, discharging residual gas in kettle, turning on booster pump, opening preheater and refrigerating machine, introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction kettle, reaching preset temperature and pressure, closing all the valves, maintaining pressure for 2-3 hours, and opening steam heating foaming device, achieving required foaming temperature, maintaining pressure time, quickly removing pressure in the reaction kettle, removing the blended flame-retardant particles, placing in hopper, turning on vibrator, and uniformly spreading blended flame-retardant particles on mesh conveyor belt, foaming is completing through the box of the rapid foaming device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) mixing ethanol with water, preparing an alcohol-water mixed solution, adding a silane coupling agent, magnetically stirring for 1-2 hours, adding 10-20 pts. wt. flame retardant aluminum hydroxide powder, mixing, uniformly stirring, allowing to stand for 10-12 hours, spreading the mixture on a glass plate, placing in drying oven at 120-122 degrees C for 3-4 hours, evaporating the solvent, and grinding to powder to obtain aluminum hydroxide treated by the surface of the silane coupling agent; adding 90-100 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer in an electric heating constant-temperature blast drying oven at 80-82 degrees C for 3-4 hours, mixing 8-9 pts. wt. flame retardant (ammonium polyphosphate, melamine cyanurate and other mass mixing) and 2-3 pts. wt. synergist nano calcium carbonate, drying at 90-92 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The modification of the hollow glass beads comprises sieving the hollow glass beads through a 300-400 meshe seive, drying at 115-125 degrees C for 3-4 hours, adding 0.5 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution to 3-4 pts. wt. hollow glass beads in a ratio of 1:35-45, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, heating, refluxing at 85-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours, washing, filtering until the pH is neutral, and drying at 70-73 degrees C to obtain alkali pretreated hollow glass beads; adding 46-50 pts. wt. cyclohexane to 3-4 pts. wt. silane coupling agent KH550 and 0.06-0.07 pts. wt. n-propylamine, forming a mixed solution, adding alkali pretreated hollow glass beads, heating and refluxing at 58-60 degrees C, for 1-2 hours, washing, filtering and drying at 80-82 degrees C to obtain hollow glass beads modified by the coupling agent. In the step (ii) the preset temperature is 31-33 degrees C, and pressure is 7-9 MPa; the foaming temperature is 125-135 degrees C for 2-3 minutes.

Testing.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.);

PN WO2021016515-A1; US2021024808-A1; US11319478-B2; US2022213376-A1
TI Treating kerogen in subterranean zone, by placing            composition comprising carbon dioxide and oxidizing gas            in subterranean zone, and treating kerogen in            subterranean zone with composition.
AU SCHIPPER D
   HULL K L
   SAINI R K
   ABOUSLEIMAN Y N
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021106405
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating kerogen in a subterranean                zone, involves placing a composition in the                subterranean zone, where the composition comprises                carbon dioxide, and an oxidizing gas, and treating                the kerogen in the subterranean zone with the                composition.
   USE - The method is used for treating kerogen in a                subterranean zone for enhancing hydrocarbon fluid                recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The oxidizing gas                  comprises at least one of chlorine dioxide,                  chlorine gas, bromine gas, fluorine gas, chlorine                  monoflouride, oxygen gas, ozone, nitrous oxide,                  nitric oxide, nitrogen dioxide, or their                  mixtures. The precursors comprise a chlorite and                  an oxidizing agent, or a chlorate and a reducing                  agent. The breaker comprises at least one of                  bromate, chlorate, chlorite, hypochlorite,                  persulfate, perborate, peroxide, percarbonate,                  nitrate, nitrite, or their combinations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of placing the                  composition comprises flowing the composition to                  a location in the subterranean zone. The method                  further comprises supplying the oxidizing gas by                  the oxidizing gas stream, and generating the                  oxidizing gas in situ. The step of generating the                  oxidizing gas in situ comprises providing                  precursors comprising a first precursor compound                  and a second precursor compound, injecting the                  first precursor compound into the composition,                  pumping a spacer amount of carbon dioxide into                  the composition, and injecting the second                  precursor compound into the composition, where                  the first and second precursor compounds react to                  generate the oxidizing gas. The method further                  comprises providing precursors comprising an                  acid, and adding at least one of a proppant, a                  viscosifier, a breaker, or a fracturing additive                  to the composition. The method further comprises                  pressurizing the composition to an injection                  pressure, injecting the composition into a                  wellbore formed in the subterranean zone,                  maintaining the injection pressure for 2-20                  hours, penetrating the composition into                  kerogen-containing source rock in the                  subterranean zone, inducing flowback of the                  composition, and scrubbing unspent                  oxidizer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The viscosifier                  comprises at least one of biopolymers, synthetic                  polymers, or fluoropolymers. The proppant                  comprises at least one of sand, resin-coated                  sand, ceramic, resin-coated ceramic, bauxite,                  glass, walnut hull, resin-coated walnut hull,                  microproppant or nanoproppant. The fracturing                  additive comprises at least one of scale                  inhibitors, corrosion inhibitors, clay swelling                  inhibitors, iron control agents, flowback aids,                  biocides, fluid loss additive, buffer, or                  diverter.

PN CN111732094-A
TI Preparation of graphene by ultrasonic assisted supercritical liquid phase stripping includes performing carbon dioxide supercritical treatment and ultrasonic treatment on graphite.
AU ZHANG X
   SHEN W
   CAO J
   CHEN Y
AE BEIJING GRAPHENE INST (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202099319V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene by ultrasonic assisted supercritical liquid phase stripping comprises taking graphite as raw material and performing carbon dioxide supercritical treatment and ultrasonic treatment on graphite.
   USE - The method is for preparation of graphene by ultrasonic assisted supercritical liquid phase stripping.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low cost, abundant raw materials and easy mass production. The graphene has high quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first solvent and first dispersant are added to graphite to fully mix to obtain first dispersion as raw material. The first solvent is water, N-methylpyrrolidone and/or ethanol. The first dispersant is fatty acid sodium, sodium amino acid, sodium ketosulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, lecithin, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol, sodium lignosulfonate, alkyl glucoside, fatty acid glyceride, sorbitan and/or polysorbate. The mass ratio of graphite to first solvent is 1:10-200. The mass ratio of graphite to first dispersant is 200-5:1. The second solvent and second dispersant are added to reaction product to obtain second dispersion, homogenizing second dispersion under 100-300 MPa and drying. The second solvent is water, N-methylpyrrolidone and/or ethanol. The second dispersant is fatty acid sodium, sodium amino acid, sodium ketosulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, lecithin, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol, sodium lignosulfonate, alkyl glucoside, fatty acid glyceride, sorbitan and/or polysorbate. The mass ratio of graphite to second solvent is 1:10-200. The mass ratio of graphite to second dispersant is 200-5:1. The graphite is natural graphite, artificial graphite and/or expanded graphite.

PN CN111299600-A
TI Preparing three-dimensional printing tool steel powder comprises taking mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen as an atomizing gas.
AU CHEN J
   WU S
AE SHENZHEN JINGLAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020581690
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing three-dimensional printing tool steel powder comprises taking mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen as an atomizing gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing three-dimensional printing tool steel powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces nitrogen increase of the powder; and improves the outlet kinetic energy of the atomizing airflow, which is conducive to the atomization of metal particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method utilizes tool steel rods as raw materials, where the raw material required to have nitrogen content of less than or equal to 70 parts-per notation and carbon content of 0.02-0.03%, the tool steel bar should be polished to remove the surface oxide rust layer and cut into small sections of 30-50 cm. The carbon dioxide and nitrogen are introduced after being thoroughly mixed in a gas mixing tank. The mixed gas pressure is increased to 2.5-5 MPa by a gas booster pump. The mixed gas pressure is controlled by an accessory program added on a computer control panel, and the pressurized gas sprays the crushed tool steel metal droplets through the spray head to obtain metal powder. The metal powder is collected, the primary yield of powder with particle size of less than 20 mu m is greater than or equal to 50%, and the sphericity is greater than or equal to 95%. Preferred Components: The mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen is used as atomizing and cooling gas, and the mixed gas contains 0-25% carbon dioxide, and nitrogen (balance amount).

PN PH2201850194-U
TI Production of nanozeolite pellets used as adsorbents for carbon dioxide capture systems, involves using naturally occurring nanozeolite materials containing clinoptilolite, and obtaining pellets having preset diameter and surface area.
AU CELORICO J R
   SALVADOR J B
   SAMSON L E
AE IND TECHNOLOGY DEV INST (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 201985519G

TI Preparing multi-component synergistic catalyst            useful for carbon dioxide conversion, comprises fixing            zinc on a silica carrier, and immobilizing ionic liquid            on the zinc-added silica carrier.
AU BAI S
   SHANG H
   SUN J
   WU X
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 201927281R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-component synergistic catalyst                comprises (i) fixing zinc on a silica carrier; and                (ii) immobilizing the ionic liquid on the                zinc-added silica carrier.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide                conversion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, and does not                need precision instrument, and saves production                cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), adding an                  alcohol solution in which a zinc salt is                  dissolved to the vacuum activated silica carrier,                  the silica carrier immobilized with zinc is                  obtained by stirring, rotary distillation,                  washing, and drying. The step (i) comprises                  adding 0.5 g silica carrier into 100 ml                  three-necked flask and vacuum-activating in an                  oil bath at 120 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling                  the mixture to room temperature under nitrogen                  atmosphere, adding solution of zinc salt in 50 ml                  anhydrous methanol, pouring into a three-necked                  flask and stirring at room temperature for 10                  hours, removing the alcohol by rotary evaporation                  at 40 degrees C, and washing repeatedly with                  methanol and deionized water three times, vacuum                  drying he mixture at 50 degrees C for 12 hours to                  obtain zinc supported silica carrier. The step                  (ii) comprises dehydrating and activating the                  sample obtained in the step (i), adding toluene                  and ionic liquid modified silane coupling agent,                  centrifuging the mixture, washing, and vacuum                  drying to obtain zinc and ionic liquid supported                  silica carrier. In the step (ii), the sample                  obtained is vacuum dehydrated and activated in                  100 degrees C oil bath for 3 hours, and heated to                  110 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere, add 30                  ml toluene and 200 ml ionic liquid modified                  silane coupling agent for 12 hours, removing the                  toluene by centrifugation, and washed with                  ethanol, the sample is placed in a vacuum oven                  and dried to obtain zinc and an ionic liquid                  supported silica carrier.Preferred Components: The silica carrier                  comprises SBA-15 porous silica, FDU series porous                  silica, M41S series porous silica, and spherical                  porous silica. The molar ratio of the silica                  support to zinc is 8:1-1:6, and the mass of zinc                  is 0.068-3.250 g. The zinc salt comprises zinc                  acetate, zinc chloride, zinc bromide, zinc iodide                  and/or zinc nitrate. The ionic liquid includes                  1-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl-3-methylimidazolium                  chloride,                  1-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bromide,                  1-(triethoxysilyl)propyl-3-methylimidazolium                  chloride and/or                  1-(triethoxysilyl)propyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bromide.

   USE - Polyether copolyimide film used for removing carbon dioxide in a gas containing carbon dioxide impurities, where carbon dioxide-containing impurity gas refers to one or more of hydrogen, methane, and nitrogen containing carbon dioxide, and applied to the separation of gas pairs such as carbon dioxide/hydrogen, carbon dioxide/methane, or carbon dioxide/nitrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether copolyimide film permeates carbon dioxide (for carbon dioxide and hydrogen), and has mild synthesis conditions and simple synthesis process, and manufactured gas separation flat membrane has low cost and is easy to be industrialized, and can be applied to the separation of mixed gas containing carbon dioxide system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyether copolyimide film represented by formula (I) is new.
   R=selected from 1,2,4,5-tetramethylbenzene or biphenyl;
   R'=one or more of -CH2-, -O-, -S-, -SO2-, -CO- or -C(CF3)2-;
   Ar'=is o-xylene or p-xylene;
   X=-(CH2)3O(C2H4O)z(CH2)3 or -(CH2)3O(C3H6O)w(CH2)3;
   z=20-50;
   w=20-50;
   m=0.1-0.9; and
   n=0.1-0.9, and m+n is 1.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polyether copolyimide film represented by formula (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The method for preparing a polyether copolyimide film of formula (I), which involves using polyether diamine and aromatic diamine (the molar ratio of the two diamines are 1:0.5-1:20) and the aromatic dianhydride molar ratio 1:1.0-1:1.3, and dissolving in a polar solvent to form a mass concentration 1-40 wt.% solution. A polyamic acid solution is prepared by polycondensation reaction, and then a polyether copolyimide gas separation membrane with a weight-average molecular weight of 30,000-350,000 is prepared through a film-making process. Preferred Components: The polar solvent comprises N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, phenol, m-cresol or p-chlorophenol. Preferred Process: The process steps of polyamic acid involves dissolving the polyether diamine monomer in the polar solvent under stirring under 0-25 degrees C, protected by nitrogen or argon, and after the polyether diamine is completely dissolved, adding the aromatic diamine to stir to prepare the complete solution, then adding the aromatic dianhydride, and stirring is continued for 2-24 hours to obtain a polyamic acid solution.
